PT P
PN CN110029226-A; CN110029226-B
TI Recovering valuable metals in cathode material of waste ternary lithium ion battery, involves oxidizing and roasting oxidative roasting waste ternary lithium ion battery cathode material under oxygen atmosphere and placing in pit furnace.
AU ZHANG D
   YANG T
   LIU W
   CHEN L
   WANG H
   REN G
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 201965116E
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering valuable metals involves oxidizing and roasting oxidative roasting waste ternary lithium ion battery cathode material under oxygen atmosphere. The waste ternary lithium ion battery powder is placed in a pit furnace at a rate of 100-500 milliliter per minute at normal temperature, and introduced oxygen into the well furnace for 10-30 minutes, then increase the temperature in the furnace to 500-700 degrees C, roast for 30-150 minutes, and the carbon in the waste ternary lithium ion battery powder enters the subsequent absorption bottle as carbon dioxide. The temperature is lowered. After the temperature in the furnace is lowered to normal temperature, the oxygen is turned off to obtain a calcined product. The obtained calcined product is added to a mixed solution of ammonia-ammonium chloride and hydrazine hydrate according to a liquid-solid ratio, and ratio of a liquid volume to a solid weight.
   USE - Method for recovering valuable metals in cathode material of waste ternary lithium ion battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to recover valuable metals is simple, cost-effective, significantly reduces process energy consumption and has high valuable metal recovery rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering valuable metals in a cathode material of a waste ternary lithium ion battery involves oxidizing and roasting oxidative roasting waste ternary lithium ion battery cathode material under oxygen atmosphere. The waste ternary lithium ion battery powder is placed in a pit furnace at a rate of 100-500 milliliter per minute at normal temperature, and introduced oxygen into the well furnace for 10-30 minutes, then increase the temperature in the furnace to 500-700 degrees C, roast for 30-150 minutes, and the carbon in the waste ternary lithium ion battery powder enters the subsequent absorption bottle as carbon dioxide. The temperature is lowered. After the temperature in the furnace is lowered to normal temperature, the oxygen is turned off to obtain a calcined product. The obtained calcined product is added to a mixed solution of ammonia-ammonium chloride and hydrazine hydrate according to a liquid-solid ratio, and ratio of a liquid volume to a solid weight, and concentration of ammonium chloride is mixed solution is 1.0-5.0 mole per liter, the concentration of ammonia in the mixed solution is 0.40-2.0 mole per liter, and the volume ratio of hydrazine hydrate in the mixed solution is 1.0-5.0 wt.%, and the controlled reaction temperature is 55-95 degrees C, the reaction is carried out for 30-150 minutes, and then filtered and washed to obtain leaching slag and a leaching solution containing lithium, nickel, cobalt, manganese, iron, aluminum and copper. The obtained leachate is adjusted by pH, the pH of the leachate is adjusted to 6-10, and the O/A ratio is added to the extractant at an O/A ratio of 0.5-2.5, and the volume fraction of Versatic 911 in the extractant is 5-25%. The volume fraction of sulfonated kerosene is 65-95%, the control temperature is 20-40 degrees C, and after 5-25 minutes reaction, the raffinate and the organic phase are separated, and the organic phase is subjected to stripping to obtain a cobalt chloride solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxygen, ammonium chloride, ammonia, hydrazine hydrate and extractant Versatic 911 are industrial grade reagents. Preferred Compositions: The mass content of the valuable metal includes 5.0-10.0 pts. wt. lithium, 25.0-35.0 pts. wt. nickel, 10.0-20.0 pts. wt. cobalt, 10.0-20.0 pts. wt. manganese, 0.1-1.0 pts. wt. iron, 0.1-1.0 pts. wt. aluminum, 0.01-1.0 pts. wt. copper, and 1.0-5.0 pts. wt. carbon.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC L03-E01B; L03-E06; L03-J01; M25-A01; M25-B; M25-E; M25-G04; M25-G17; M25-H; M25-J; X16-B01F1; X16-E01; X16-M

PT P
PN CN109793807-A
TI Rubber tree seed fat-soluble part used in medicine treating or assisting in treating Parkinson's disease, is prepared by drying rubber tree seed, shelling, heating, and subjecting to static hydraulic extrusion or dynamic spiral extrusion.
AU WANG R
   ZHU Y
   WANG D
   WANG Y
   FAN J
   YANG W
   CHEN Y
   HOU Z
   PAN W
AE XISHUANGBANNA HUAKUN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (XISH-Non-standard)
GA 201947487W
AB    NOVELTY - Rubber tree seed fat-soluble part is prepared by (i) performing mechanical extrusion by drying fresh rubber tree seed and shelling, heating and subjecting to static hydraulic extrusion or dynamic spiral extrusion to obtain the product, (ii) performing solvent leaching by drying rubber tree seeds, shelling, puffing, and crushing to obtain processed material, placing in a sealed leaching container, adding a solvent, stirring the material and the solvent or ultrasonically processing the material to sufficiently contact with the solvent, leaving still and precipitating, leaching and volatilizing the solvent to obtain the product, or (iii) performing supercritical extraction by drying rubber tree seeds and shelling, puffing, crushing, and placing in a sealed pressure-resistant supercritical extraction container, adding carbon dioxide, stirring and mixing the material (C) and the solvent, or ultrasonically processing to enable the material (C) to be in full contact with the solvent.
   USE - The rubber tree seed fat-soluble part is useful in a medicine, a health care food or a food for treating or assisting in treating Parkinson's disease (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Rubber tree seed fat-soluble part is prepared by (i) performing mechanical extrusion method by drying fresh rubber tree seed and shelling to obtain kernel material (A), heating material (A) at 80-100 degrees C and subjecting to static hydraulic extrusion or dynamic spiral extrusion to obtain the final product, (ii) performing solvent leaching method by drying fresh rubber tree seeds, shelling to obtain kernel material (B), puffing, granulating or crushing the material (B) to obtain processed material, placing in a sealed leaching container, adding a solvent, where the solvent comprises a solvent oil No.6, petroleum ether having a boiling point of 30-90 degrees C, n-hexane, diethyl ether, ethyl acetate and/or n-butanol, stirring and mixing the material and the solvent at a temperature lower than the boiling point of the solvent and under normal pressure, or ultrasonically processing the material to sufficiently contact with the solvent, leaving still and precipitating, leaching the solvent and volatilizing the solvent at a temperature above the boiling point of the solvent and under vacuum to obtain the final product, or (iii) performing supercritical extraction by drying fresh rubber tree seeds and shelling to obtain kernel material (C), puffing, granulating or crushing, and placing the crushed material (C) in a sealed pressure-resistant supercritical extraction container, adding carbon dioxide as a solvent, stirring and mixing the material (C) and the solvent under the condition that the critical temperature and the critical pressure of the solvent are exceeded, or ultrasonically processing to enable the material (C) to be in full contact with the solvent. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a pharmaceutical composition comprising the rubber tree seed fat-soluble part and a carrier;
   (2) a health care food comprising the rubber tree seed fat-soluble part and a carrier commonly used for health care food; and
   (3) a food comprising the rubber tree seed fat-soluble part and a carrier commonly used for food.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The step (iii) further involves leaving still and precipitating mixture, leaching the solvent and volatilizing the solvent at room temperature under reduced pressure to obtain the final product.
   ACTIVITY - Antiparkinsonian.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PT P
PN US2019144765-A1; WO2019099002-A1; AU2017440227-A1; CA3082774-A1; AR113510-A1; US10738247-B2; KR2020098526-A; CN111566188-A; EP3710562-A1; SG11202004476-A1; BR112020009731-A2; JP2021503034-W; RU2742984-C1; KR2325304-B1; KR2021136172-A; JP2022033893-A; IN400454-B; IN202248034518-A
TI Producing hydrocarbons containing four or more            carbons by reforming gaseous mixture of methane and            carbon dioxide to form synthesis gas product and            converting hydrogen and carbon monoxide to hydrocarbons            provided in Fischer-Tropsch product.
AU MARKER T
   LINCK M B
   WANGEROW J
   ORTIZ-TORAL P
   MARKER T L
AE GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
GA 201942210R
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrocarbons containing greater than or equal                to 4 carbons ( greater than or equal to 4C) are                produced (I) by, (a) in a reforming stage,                contacting gaseous mixture comprising methane and                carbon dioxide (CO2) with a reforming catalyst to                produce synthesis gas product; and (b) converting                hydrogen (H2) and carbon monoxide (CO) in the                synthesis gas product to hydrocarbons, including                greater than or equal to 4C hydrocarbons, provided                in a Fischer-Tropsch (FT) product, where the                greater than or equal to 4C hydrocarbons in the FT                product include a wax fraction comprising normal                greater than or equal to 20C hydrocarbons.
   USE - Production of greater than or equal to 4C                hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The ability of CO2-steam reforming processes                to produce synthesis gas products with favorable                molar H2:CO ratios, in a stable manner and with                tolerance to sulfur-bearing contaminants that are                often present in sources of methane (e.g., natural                gas) and other light hydrocarbons, provides                advantages in the use of these processes with                additional steps for producing liquid hydrocarbons,                for e.g., gasoline- and diesel boiling-range                hydrocarbon fractions. These advantages include                greater simplicity of overall liquid hydrocarbon                production processes, which may, for example,                require fewer addition, separation, and/or recycle                steps compared to conventional processes. This                results not only in cost savings, but also in the                possibility of providing such overall processes in                an easily transportable (e.g., skid mounted)                configuration, which may be brought to sources of                natural gas, or other sources of components of                gaseous mixtures, from which sources the transport                of such components to conventional brick and mortar                production facilities would otherwise be                problematic. Advantages also include increased                flexibility in terms of opportunities for                integration with a wide variety of processes that                generate CO2- and/or light hydrocarbon-containing                gas streams, including biomass conversion                processes, fermentation processes, and industrial                processes that generate CO2-containing waste                gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) production (II) of renewable hydrocarbon                fuel which involves hydropyrolyzing                biomass-containing feedstock or biomass-derived                feedstock with hydrogen-containing feed gas stream                in a hydropyrolysis section to generate (i)                hydropyrolysis gaseous mixture comprising methane                and CO2 and (ii) substantially fully deoxygenated                hydrocarbon liquid, and contacting hydropyrolysis                gaseous mixture with reforming catalyst comprising                noble metal on solid support comprising cerium                oxide to produce synthesis gas product comprising                H2 and CO; and
   (2) system for producing greater than or equal                to 4C hydrocarbons from methane comprising                reforming reactor configured to connect, via a                system input, to source of gaseous mixture                comprising methane and CO2, synthesis gas product                cooler configured to receive synthesis gas product                from reforming reactor, compressor configured to                receive cooled synthesis gas product from synthesis                gas product cooler, FT reactor configured to                receive FT feed from compressor, optional FT                product heater configured to receive FT product                from FT reactor, finishing reactor configured to                receive heated FT product from FT product heater,                and separator configured to receive finishing                product from finishing reactor and further                configured to provide, via a system vapor output                and a system liquid output, vapor and liquid                fractions, respectively, of the finishing                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Processes: Step (b) is carried out                  with an FT feed having a substantially same molar                  H2:CO molar ratio as in the synthesis gas                  product, produced in step (a). Before step (b),                  water is condensed from the synthesis gas                  product, produced in step (a). The method (I)                  further includes (c) converting greater than or                  equal to 75% of the normal greater than or equal                  to 20C hydrocarbons to normal or branched 4-19C                  hydrocarbons, provided in a                  hydroisomerization/hydrocracking product. Gaseous                  mixture further comprises H2O, and step (a) is                  carried out in a CO2-steam reforming reactor at                  677-788 degrees C, a gauge pressure of 0-517 kPa,                  and a weight hourly space velocity (WHSV) of                  0.1-2.5/hour. Step (c) is carried out in a                  finishing reactor comprising dewaxing catalyst                  having hydroisomerization and/or hydrocracking                  activity with respect to normal greater than or                  equal to 20C hydrocarbons. The method (II)                  further comprises recycling a portion of (i)                  synthesis gas product or (ii) its H2-enriched                  portion, to provide a portion of the                  hydrogen-containing feed gas stream. The method                  (II) further includes converting H2 and CO in the                  synthesis gas product to hydrocarbons, including                  greater than or equal to 4C hydrocarbons, in an                  FT product, to increase yield of greater than or                  equal to 4C hydrocarbons from the                  biomass-containing feedstock or biomass-derived                  feedstock, relative to a baseline yield in the                  absence of converting the H2 and CO in the                  synthesis gas product to hydrocarbons.                  Hydropyrolysis section includes stages of both                  (i) catalytic hydropyrolysis of the                  biomass-containing feedstock or biomass-derived                  feedstock in the presence of the                  hydrogen-containing feed gas stream and a                  deoxygenating catalyst to provide a                  hydropyrolysis reactor vapor, and (ii) catalytic                  hydroconversion of a portion of the                  hydropyrolysis reactor vapor in the presence of a                  hydroconversion catalyst to provide the                  hydropyrolysis gaseous mixture and the                  substantially fully deoxygenated hydrocarbon                  liquid. Preferred Compositions:                  Hydroisomerization/hydrocracking product                  comprises less than or equal to 1 wt.%                  hydrocarbons that are solid at room temperature.                  At least a portion of the gaseous mixture                  comprises a hydrocarbon-containing feedstock                  component selected from hydropyrolysis gaseous                  mixture, natural gas comprising CO2, biogas                  obtained from bacterial digestion of organic                  waste, hydrogen-depleted pressure swing                  adsorption gas, or gaseous effluent from a                  bacterial fermentation stage. Preferred System:                  The system further comprises a condenser                  configured to condense liquid water from the                  cooled synthesis gas product, where the                  compressor is configured for receiving the cooled                  synthesis gas product from the condenser. The                  system is transportable. Preferred Conditions:                  Finishing reactor is at a temperature of 232-399                  degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Dewaxing catalyst                  comprises dewaxing active metal deposited on a                  solid acidic support. Dewaxing active metal is                  selected from Group 13 or Group 14 elements.                  Dewaxing active metal is gallium. Acidic support                  is a zeolitic or non-zeolitic molecular sieve                  having greater than or equal to 15 mmol/gram of                  acid sites, measured by temperature programmed                  desorption (TPD) of ammonia. Acidic support is a                  zeolitic molecular sieve having silica-to-alumina                  molar framework ratio of less than or equal to                  50. Zeolitic molecular sieve is ZSM-5.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. PT P
PN CN109679377-A
TI Preparing calcium carbonate for deacidified silicone rubber comprises calcining limestone, preparing lime milk, calcium carbonate suspension and calcium carbonate suspension, transferring, heating, modifying, dehydrating, and pulverizing.
AU LIANG L
   ZHU Y
   SU Q
AE ANHUI XUANCHENG HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHN (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019411139
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium carbonate for deacidified silicone rubber comprises (i) calcining limestone at 1000-1300 degrees C for 300-500 minutes to obtain quicklime after the digestion reaction to obtain lime slurry, removing the waste residue through 100 mesh sieve, and adjusting the lime slurry solid to obtain lime milk; (ii) preparing calcium carbonate suspension; (iii) preparing composite calcium carbonate suspension; (iv) transferring the composite calcium carbonate suspension to the surface treatment barrel, starting the mechanical high-speed, heating the water vapor to 70-90 degrees C, and modifying the surface treatment by adding a composite treatment agent with a calcium carbonate dry basis mass of 2-5%, continuously stirring for 30-90 minutes to obtain modified calcium carbonate; and (v) dehydrating the modified calcium carbonate, drying, and pulverizing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium carbonate for deacidified silicone rubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate has good dispersibility, good thixotropy, high reinforcement, high adhesive property, good acid resistance after filling silicone gel excellent thixotropic property, stronger reinforcing property and adhesive property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing composite calcium carbonate for deacidified silicone rubber comprises (i) calcining limestone at 1000-1300 degrees C for 300-500 minutes to obtain quicklime after the digestion reaction to obtain lime slurry, removing the waste residue through 100 mesh sieve, and adjusting the lime slurry solid content to 10-13% to obtain lime milk; (ii) transferring the lime milk to the carbonation reaction tower, starting the refrigeration cycle, adjusting the temperature of the lime slurry to 18-25 degrees C, adding the citric acid having a weight of 0.10-0.50% milk of the lime and starting the carbonation reaction by introducing the tail gas of the lime kiln to when the pH of the reaction system is 7.5, stopping the aeration, and terminating carbonation reaction to obtain a calcium carbonate suspension; (iii) adding a water-soluble silicate to the calcium carbonate suspension, where the water-soluble silicate should be continuously added in an amount of 60-120 minutes, and the temperature of the suspension is required to be maintained at 40-80 degrees C, and simultaneously starting the stirring and opening, where the carbon dioxide or carbonized tail gas begins to react, and maintaining the pH value at 8-10 during the reaction after adding water-soluble silicate, introducing continuously the carbon dioxide for 20-60 minutes, and stopping the reaction to obtain composite calcium carbonate suspension; (iv) transferring the composite calcium carbonate suspension to the surface treatment barrel, starting the mechanical high-speed, heating the water vapor to 70-90 degrees C, and modifying the surface treatment by adding a composite treatment agent with a calcium carbonate dry basis mass of 2-5%, where the composite surface treatment agent is a borate coupling agent and a sodium fatty acid compound, and the surface treatment is completed by continuously stirring for 30-90 minutes to obtain modified calcium carbonate; and (v) dehydrating the modified calcium carbonate, drying at 105-125 degrees C for 24-48 hours, and pulverizing to obtain a composite calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The quicklime in the digestion reaction is carried out at ash/water mass ratio of 1:7-13. In the step (ii), the carbon dioxide volume concentration in the lime kiln tail gas in the carbonation reaction is 30-40%.The water-soluble silicate is added in an amount of from 1-4 wt% based on the dry weight of the calcium carbonate in the calcium carbonate suspension. The water soluble silicate comprises sodium silicate or potassium silicate. The ratio of the borate coupling agent to the sodium fatty acid in the composite treatment agent is 1:1-4. The sodium fatty acid includes sodium stearate, sodium laurate and sodium palmitate. The high speed agitation is stirred at a rotational speed of 2000-3000 revolutions/minute.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).)
MC E05-C02; E10-C04L2; E31-P05C; E34-D03A; A06-A00B; A06-A00E; A08-R

TI Device for controlling culture environment of Flammulina velutipes cultivar, comprises reaction tank, control valve dispersed at outlet end of reaction tank, sterilizing device connected to outside of reaction tank, and compressor.
AU LIN Q
   CHEN F
   HUANG E
AE SHANDONG YOUHE MUSHROOM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019377509
AB    NOVELTY - A device comprises a reaction tank, a control valve dispersed at the outlet end of the reaction tank, a sterilizing device connected to the outside of the reaction tank, and a compressor for supplying aerodynamic force, where air outputted by the compressor is sterilized by the sterilizing device, and input into the reaction tank through the control of the control valve to continue the reaction, the tank port of the reaction tank is provided with a pressure sensor for detecting the pressure inside the tank, multiple of UV lamp groups for illumination are arranged in an upper region of the reaction tank, the side wall of the reaction tank is further provided with multiple of coils of heating wire, a humidity sensor is dispersed on the side wall of the reaction tank to detect the humidity in the tank, a carbon dioxide concentration detecting sensor is further dispersed on the inner wall of the reaction tank, the reaction tank is loaded with Flammulina velutipes culture solution.
   USE - Device for controlling culture environment of Flammulina velutipes cultivar (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device can accurately control the mushroom culture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A device comprises a reaction tank, a control valve dispersed at the outlet end of the reaction tank, a sterilizing device connected to the outside of the reaction tank, and a compressor for supplying aerodynamic force, where air outputted by the compressor is sterilized by the sterilizing device, and input into the reaction tank through the control of the control valve to continue the reaction, the tank port of the reaction tank is provided with a pressure sensor for detecting the pressure inside the tank, multiple of UV lamp groups for illumination are arranged in an upper region of the reaction tank, the side wall of the reaction tank is further provided with multiple of coils of heating wire, a humidity sensor is dispersed on the side wall of the reaction tank to detect the humidity in the tank, a carbon dioxide concentration detecting sensor is further dispersed on the inner wall of the reaction tank, the reaction tank is loaded with Flammulina velutipes culture solution comprising 10-15 parts lemon peel, 5-8 parts sugar cane, 5-10 parts Sassafras, 3-6 parts millet powder, 3-6 parts pumpkin, 0.5-1 part sodium chloride, 2-3 parts silkworm cocoon powder, 0.3-0.5 part yeast powder, 0.5-1 part tomato extract, 0.3-0.5 part paclobutrazol powder, 0.2-0.4 part anti-dropping powder, 3-5 parts chitosan, 0.5-2.5 parts magnesium carbonate, 20-25 parts trehalose, 1.3-9.5 parts nano calcium carbonate and 0.2-0.8 parts sodium lauryl sulfonate, and the reaction tank is pressure-controlled according to the relationship between the reaction tank pressure and the heating temperature by using the expressions as given in the specification. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for controlling culture environment of Flammulina velutipes cultivar.

PN CN109517788-A
TI Use of peroxiredoxin 4 protein e.g. for adjusting the balance between reactive oxygen and antioxidation of oocytes cultured, and adjusting effect in resisting apoptosis caused by granulosa cell oxidation stress.
AU MENG Y
   LIU J
   QIAN Y
   CUI Y
   YAN Z
AE UNIV NANJING MEDICAL FIRST AFFILIATED (UYNM-C)
GA 201931742H
AB    NOVELTY - Use of peroxiredoxin 4 protein for adjusting the balance between reactive oxygen and antioxidation of oocytes cultured in vitro.
   USE - The peroxiredoxin 4 protein is useful for adjusting the balance between reactive oxygen and antioxidation of oocytes cultured in vitro, adjusting effect in resisting apoptosis caused by granulosa cell oxidation stress, inhibiting the cumulus expansion index caused by hydrogen peroxide and reducing mature rate of oocyte MII, improving maturation of immature oocytes in vitro by the anti-oxidative stress mechanism in granule cells and oocytes themselves, delaying aging of oocyte by anti-oxidative stress mechanism in the granulosa cell and that of oocyte itself, used as compound component of medicine and medicine composition, in promoting oocyte maturation and delaying ovarian aging.(all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The peroxiredoxin 4 protein improves the maturation rate of the immature oocytes cultured in vitro; can delay aging of the oocytes, and improved the fertilization rate of oocytes and clinical pregnancy rate in the process of assisted reproduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) reagent for preparing control balance between reactive oxygen and antioxidant in follicle, comprising peroxiredoxin4 protein and auxiliary materials i.e., allowed to be added in reagent for culture in vitro;
   (2) regent for adjusting effect in resisting apoptosis caused by granulosa cell oxidation stress, comprising peroxiredoxin 4 protein;
   (3) reagent for inhibiting the cumulus expansion index caused by hydrogen peroxide and reducing mature rate of oocyte MII caused by exogenous reactive oxygen species in the culture of follicles in vitro, comprising peroxiredoxin 4 protein;
   (4) culture method for improving maturation rate of immature oocyte, comprising removing out the immature ovum from the species source body, the species source is human and mammal, mammals comprises mouse, rat, pig, cattle, sheep or primate, placing in the immature ovum culture solution containing peroxiredoxin 4 protein, the continuously culturing step is as follows: the culture condition is 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide and saturated humidity, continuous culturing time is 4-24 hours, the immature ovum of species source and the peroxiredoxin 4 protein species source can be same or different, peroxiredoxin 4 protein in the species source is not the same, two species generation should have the same or similar function; and
   (5) culture method for delaying aging of oocyte cultured in vitro, comprising removing out the mature ovum from species source, the species source is human and mammal, mammals comprises mouse, rat, pig, cattle, sheep or primate, placing in the mature ovum culture solution containing peroxiredoxin 4 protein, the continuously culturing is as follows: the culture condition is 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide and saturated humidity, should have the same or similar functional peroxiredoxin 4 protein, continuously culturing time is 3-6 hours until the mature ovum fertilization of species source and the peroxiredoxin 4 protein species source can be the same or not the same, and the peroxiredoxin 4 protein produced by the two species should have the same or similar function when the source of the species is different.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The reagent is an in vitro oocyte culture liquid and the immature ovum culture solution, comprising CZB culture solution and KSOM culture solution, Human Tubal Fluid (HTF) culture liquid or Minimum Essential Medium (MEM) culture liquid and used as the basic medium. The regent for adjusting effect in resisting apoptosis caused by granulosa cell oxidation stress is an in vitro immature ovum culture solution, comprising CZB culture solution, KSOM culture solution, HTF culture liquid, MEM culture liquid, and used as the basic medium. The reagent for inhibiting the cumulus expansion index caused by hydrogen peroxide and reducing mature rate of oocyte MII caused by exogenous reactive oxygen species in the culture of follicles in vitro is an in vitro oocyte culture liquid and immature ovum culture solution, comprising CZB culture solution, KSOM culture solution, HTF culture liquid or MEM culture liquid, and used as the basic medium. Preferred Method: The concentration of peroxiredoxin 4 in the in vitro culture of mature ovum and immature ovum is 15-25 mu g/ml.
   ACTIVITY - Apoptotic; Nootropic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. PT P
PN CN109248174-A
TI Human apical papillary stem cell microcapsule useful for preparing regenerative medicine and tissue engineering materials for bone diseases.
AU XU S
AE NANJING TAISHENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019108377
AB    NOVELTY - Human apical papillary stem cell microcapsule specifically enriched by cell surface markers is claimed. The stem cell is a human apical papillary stem cell, the human age is 19-29 years old, and the cell surface marker is of CD18, STRO-1, CD106/VCAM-1, CD146/MUC-18, HOP-26, or CD49A/integrin beta 1, SB-10/CD166. The microcapsule comprises a microsphere core and a shell, the microspheres having a diameter of less than 500 mm. The microcapsule material is collagen, alginate, chitosan, and hyaluronic acid.
   USE - The microcapsule is useful for preparing regenerative medicine and tissue engineering materials for bone diseases (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the human root apical papilla stem cell microcapsule, comprising (i) dissolving the alginate in phosphate buffered saline, formulating into a 1-3% alginate solution, filtering through a 0.22 m m filter, and storing, (ii) culturing the CD18-positive root tip nipple stem cells to the fourth generation were trypsinized, centrifuging at 900 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes, and washing the cells once with phosphate buffered saline, (iii) resuspending the CD18-positive root tip nipple stem cells with an alginate solution, adjusting the cell concentration to (1-2)x 106/ml, (iv) through the horizontal and vertical two-channel microfluidic device, the alginate solution containing the cells is dropped into the dish containing the 0.1-0.2 M calcium chloride solution through the channel to form cell microspheres, soybean oil flows laterally through the channel, intercepting the alginate solution and controlling the size of the cell microspheres, (v) incubating the microspheres at 37 degrees C for 15-45 minutes,(vi) coating the alginate cell microspheres in a (0.1-1)% chitosan solution for 15-45 minutes, (vii) placing in a 1-5 mg/ml aqueous solution of genipin at 37 degrees C for 1-10 hours to complete cross-linking to prepare cell microcapsules, (viii) washing and collecting the cell microcapsules with a -MEM, and inoculating 10-20% fetal bovine serum, 50-200 m M ascorbic acid 2-phosphate, 2 mM glutamine, 100U/ml penicillin, 100 m g/ml streptomycin in a -MEM medium, placing in an incubator at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide, and (ix) observing the shape and diameter of the formed cell microcapsules by an optical microscope; and
   (2) detecting dryness of a human apical papillary stem cell microcapsule, comprising (i) collecting cell microcapsules, and inoculation with 10-20% fetal calf serum, 50-200 m M ascorbic acid 2-phosphate, 2 mM glutamine, 100 U/ml penicillin, 100 m g/ml streptomycin in a-MEM medium, and placing in an incubator at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide, (ii) replacing with osteogenic induction medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum, 100 U/ml penicillin, 100 m g/ml streptomycin, 0.05-0.5 mM dexamethasone, 10-100 mg/ml vitamin C and 5-15 mM beta -phosphoglycerol in a-MEM medium, after 24-48 hours of culture, changing every 3-4 days, (iii) induction of culture for 4 weeks, fixing the cell microcapsules with 4% paraformaldehyde at room temperature for 30 minutes, (iv) placing in phosphate buffered saline for 15 minutes, (v) dehydration, transparent, paraffin embedding, slicing, dewaxing, rehydration, (vi) placing the cell microcapsule tissue section in a 2% alizarin red solution, staining at room temperature for 5 minutes, (vii) washing with tap water, counterstain with hematoxylin, and (viii) seal microscopy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The CD18 is used as a cell surface marker, and the CD18 is labeled with a fluorescent label or a magnetic bead. The microcapsule core material is alginate. The microcapsule shell material is chitosan membranes which are covalently crosslinked. The stromal stem cell source is teeth, fat, bone marrow, umbilical cord, placenta or cord blood. The transgenic technique is mediated by a retrovirus. Preferred Method: The CD18-positive root tip nipple stem cells cultured to the fourth generation are: (i) collecting normal adult plucked wisdom teeth, immediately placed at 4 degrees C pre-cooled containing 50-300 U/ml penicillin, 50-300 mu g/ml-streptomycin, and 1-5 mu g/ml amphotericin B in alpha -MEM medium, shipping to a clean laboratory at 2-8 degrees C, the age of the adult is 19-29 years old, the time interval from the start of extraction of the apical papilla stem cells is not greater than 72 hours, (ii) transferring to the biological safety cabinet, taking out the wisdom tooth to the culture dish, washing with 4 degrees C pre-cooled sterile phosphate buffer, washing the outer surface of the phosphate buffered saline, (iii) using a sterile scalpel, gently cut the apical nipple from the root of the tooth and chop, (iv) placing the apical papilla tissuein phosphate buffered saline containing 1-10 mg/ml type I collagenase and 1-10 mg/ml dispase, and digested at 37 degrees C for 30 minutes, (v) filtering the digestive juice through a 70 micron cell sieve to obtain a single cell suspension containing human apical papillary stem cells, centrifuging at 900 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes, and washing the cells once with phosphate buffered saline, (vi) resuspending the cells in phosphate buffered saline, adding CD18 monoclonal antibody, mixing, incubating at room temperature for 30 minutes in the dark, (vii) washing, centrifugation at 900 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes, and resuspending the cells with phosphate buffered saline, (viii) obtaining a CD18-positive stem cell subset by flow cytometry sorting or immunomagnetic bead sorting, and (x) changing the solution every 3-4 days, and after the cells are 90% confluent, the cells are passaged 1:3, and the cells used for preparing the microspheres are the fourth generation.
   ACTIVITY - Osteopathic.

PT P
PN CN109133113-A
TI Drying and dehydrating method for producing mirabilite in chloralkali industry by uniformly mixing anhydrous sodium sulfate and mirabilite, followed by introducing into cyclone dryer, crushing and then introducing into cyclone separator.
AU SUN Y
   YANG X
   TANG X
   SONG W
   DONG J
   LI Z
   HUANG M
   PEI B
   KE Y
AE XINJIANG ZHONGTAI CHEM FUKANG ENERGY CO (XINJ-Non-standard)
   XINJIANG ZHONGTAI CHEM CO LTD (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 201905533W
AB    NOVELTY - Drying and dehydrating method for producing mirabilite in a chloralkali industry involves: (1) uniformly mixing anhydrous sodium sulfate and mirabilite in a spiral feeder to obtain a water-containing material, and using a hot air flow for stably feeding the water-containing materials into an air pipe; (2) rapidly dispersing the water-containing materials under the action of hot air flow in the air pipe, sequentially passing the water-containing material and the hot air flow in the air pipe through pulse tubes (A)-(C) after the diameter of the air pipe is expanded, and discharging the pulse tube (C) to obtain a dried material; (3) introducing the dried material and the hot air flow through the air pipe behind the pulse tube (C) into a cyclone dryer, crushing the dried material in the cyclone dryer, and then introducing the crushed material together with the hot air flow into a cyclone separator; and (4) carrying out cyclone separation on the crushed material in the cyclone separator.
   USE - The drying and dehydrating method is useful for producing mirabilite in a chloralkali industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The drying and dehydrating method: is capable of simply producing the mirabilite in chloralkali industry; fully utilizes the waste heat in the exhaust gas and the carbon dioxide contained in the exhaust gas; reduces the environmental protection pressure in the chloralkali industry; and fully utilizes the waste to serve as a portion of production raw materials, thus reducing the production cost of the industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Drying and dehydrating method for producing mirabilite in a chloralkali industry involves: (1) uniformly mixing anhydrous sodium sulfate and mirabilite according to the ratio of 3:1 in a spiral feeder to obtain a water-containing material, and using a hot air flow for stably feeding the water-containing materials output by the spiral feeder into an air pipe; (2) rapidly dispersing the water-containing materials under the action of hot air flow in the air pipe, sequentially passing the water-containing material and the hot air flow in the air pipe through a pulse tube (A), a pulse tube (B) and a pulse tube (C) after the diameter of the air pipe is expanded, and discharging the pulse tube (C) to obtain a dried material; (3) introducing the dried material and the hot air flow through the air pipe behind the pulse tube (C) into a cyclone dryer, crushing the dried material in the cyclone dryer, and then introducing the crushed material together with the hot air flow into a cyclone separator; (4) carrying out cyclone separation on the crushed material and the hot air flow in the cyclone separator to obtain anhydrous sodium sulfate powder and mixed gas flow; and (5) discharging the anhydrous sodium sulfate powder through a bottom of the cyclone separator, removing a small amount of dust entrained in the mixed gas flow through a bag-type dust remover, then introducing into an alkali washing tower to absorb carbon dioxide in the mixed gas flow to obtain a mixed solution of sodium bicarbonate and sodium carbonate, and introducing the mixed solution into a downstream process to-be-used as a raw material of a fine preparation. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for carrying out drying and dehydrating method for producing mirabilite in a chloralkali industry.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: An absorption liquid in the alkali washing tower is a sodium hydroxide solution. In the method, the aqueous material has a water content of 10-15%, and the dried material has a water content of 1%. Preferred Properties: The hot air flow in the air pipe has a temperature of 150-170 degrees C, and a flow rate of 10-15 m/second.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); Q76 (Drying (F26))
MC E11-Q01B; E33-C; E33-D; Q76-T04

PN CN109078219-A
TI Preparing facial line engraving autologous collagen tissue filler comprises e.g. obtaining autologous fibroblast tissue, washing with D-Hanks buffer to remove residual blood and tissue debris residue and cutting autologous fibroblast tissue.
AU BAI J
AE BAI J (BAIJ-Individual)
GA 201901671D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing facial line engraving autologous collagen tissue filler comprises e.g. (1) obtaining autologous fibroblast tissue, washing with D-Hanks buffer to remove residual blood and tissue debris residue, cutting the autologous fibroblast tissue into small pieces, adding digestive juice, digesting in constant temperature shaking box, allowing to stand and layering, absorbing the upper fibroblast solution, transferring it to DMEM culture medium containing fetal bovine serum, sealing and centrifuging, removing the supernatant, adding DMEM medium containing fetal bovine serum, blowing the cells to make fibroblast suspension, inoculating into the culture flask, incubating at constant temperature in the presence of carbon dioxide, periodically changing the culture medium until the fibroblasts are spread over bottom of flask; (2) removing the fibroblasts obtained in step (1) from the culture medium, adding D-Hanks buffer to clean, adding enzyme solution to digest.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing facial line engraving autologous collagen tissue filler.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing facial line engraving autologous collagen tissue filler comprises (1) obtaining autologous fibroblast tissue, washing with D-Hanks buffer to remove residual blood and tissue debris residue, cutting the autologous fibroblast tissue into small pieces, adding digestive juice, digesting in constant temperature shaking box at 37 degrees C for 20-60 minutes, allowing to stand and layering, absorbing the upper fibroblast solution, transferring it to DMEM culture medium containing fetal bovine serum, sealing and centrifuging, removing the supernatant, adding DMEM medium containing fetal bovine serum, blowing the cells to make fibroblast suspension, inoculating into the culture flask, incubating at constant temperature 37 degrees C in the presence of 5% carbon dioxide, periodically changing the culture medium until the fibroblasts are spread over bottom of flask; (2) removing the fibroblasts obtained in step (1) from the culture medium, adding D-Hanks buffer to clean, adding enzyme solution to digest, sub-culturing after fibroblasts grow to 80-90% of fusion; (3) placing fibroblasts in serum-free medium after fibroblasts are grown for three generations, culturing in serum-free medium at 37 degrees C to secrete collagen from fibroblasts; (4) separating fibroblasts from collagen by taking out the serum-free medium in the step (3), filtering through 50k ultrafiltration membrane, and filtering through 0.22 mu m filter to obtain collagen solution; (5) dissolving sodium hyaluronate in sodium hydroxide solution, where mass concentration of sodium hyaluronate in the solution is 3-10%, stirring until fully dissolved, adding the cross-linking agent, stirring evenly, where the mass concentration of the cross-linking agent in the solution is 2-5 wt.%, reaction temperature is 35-50 degrees C, reaction time is 2-10 hours, carrying filtration to remove hydrochloric acid solution, adding to D-Hanks buffer, and washing for 24-48 hours to obtain hyaluronic acid gel; and (6) stirring and mixing the collagen solution obtained in step (4) and hyaluronic acid gel obtained in step (5), adding Ringer's solution and glucose solution to obtain autologous collagen tissue filler.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the digestive juice is mixture of pancreatin and type I collagenase in volume ratio of 1:1, where the mass fraction of pancreatin is 0.25 wt.%, and the mass fraction of type I collagenase is 0.1 wt.%. In the step (2), the enzyme solution is 0.25 wt.% pancreatin. In the step (5), the crosslinking agent is ethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, diethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, polyethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, polypropylene glycol diglycidyl ether, and/or 1,6-hexanediol diglycidyl ether. In the step (6), the volume ratio of the collagen solution, the hyaluronic acid gel, the Ringer's solution, and the glucose solution is 1:2-10:1-2:1-2. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the centrifugal separation speed is 800-1800 revolutions/minute, and the centrifugation time is 5-15 minutes. In step three, culturing fibroblasts in serum-free medium for 12-60 hours. In the step (6), further adding fibroblast growth factor; where the fibroblast growth factor is epidermal growth factor, fibroblast growth factor, keratinocyte growth factor, nerve growth factor, hepatocyte growth factor, transforming growth factor- alpha , and/or vascular endothelial growth factor. In the step (6), adding 1-5 mg fibroblast growth factor into each 10 ml of autologous collagen tissue filling.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

PN CN109022137-A
TI Preparing edible pepper seed oil by cleaning pepper seeds, removing impurities and shelling pepper seed kernels, followed by pressing, extracting, degumming, freezing and deacidifying, and then adding plant extract and vitamin material.
AU TANG S
   HUANG D
   WANG D
   MAO X
   MA S
   XUE Y
AE SHAANXI WEIKANG FOOD TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2018A51535
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing edible pepper seed oil involves: (1) processing a raw material by cleaning pepper seeds, removing impurities and shelling pepper seed kernels; (2) pressing the pepper seed kernels to obtain a pepper seed kernel cake; (3) putting the pepper seed cake into a charging tank, extracting to get a pepper seed oil; (4) adding degumming agent to the pepper seed oil obtained in the step (3), stirring and degumming; (5) adding the degummed crude oil obtained in the step (4) to a freezing device, controlling the temperature at -3 degrees C to -1 degrees C, leaving still, filtering and removing impurities; (6) adding the crude oil obtained in the step (5) to a heating device, adding alkaline buffer solution, heating, stirring and deacidifying; (7) adding plant extract to the crude oil obtained in the step (6), stirring, heating and removing impurities to obtain pepper seed crude oil; and (8) adding vitamin material to the pepper seed crude oil obtained in the step (7), and stirring.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing edible pepper seed oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables rapid preparation of the edible pepper seed oil with rich nutrient content and excellent storage stability, and without peculiar smell. The prepared edible pepper seed oil does not deteriorate easily.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing edible pepper seed oil involves: (1) processing a raw material by cleaning pepper seeds, removing impurities and shelling pepper seed kernels; (2) pressing the pepper seed kernels obtained in the step (1) to obtain a pepper seed kernel cake; (3) putting the pepper seed cake obtained in the step (2) into a charging tank, extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide at 32-50 degrees C under a pressure of 8-30 MPa for 2-4 hours to get a pepper seed oil, where the carbon dioxide flow rate in the pipeline is 1-9 m3/hour; (4) adding 1-3 %mass degumming agent to the pepper seed oil obtained in the step (3), stirring and degumming at 10-15 degrees C for 2.5-3 hours; (5) adding the degummed crude oil obtained in the step (4) to a freezing device, controlling the temperature at -3 degrees C to -1 degrees C, leaving still for 18-30 hours, filtering and removing impurities; (6) adding the crude oil obtained in the step (5) to a heating device equipped with a stirring device, adding 3-5 %mass alkaline buffer solution, heating, maintaining the temperature at 45-65 degrees C, stirring and deacidifying for 2-3 hours, and then leaving still for 5-7 hours to remove the precipitated crude oil; (7) adding 4-6 %mass plant extract to the crude oil obtained in the step (6), stirring and heating at 20-25 degrees C for 3-4 hours, leaving still for 3-5 hours, filtering and removing impurities to obtain pepper seed crude oil; and (8) adding 1-3 %mass vitamin material to the pepper seed crude oil obtained in the step (7), and uniformly stirring the mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The plant extract in the step (7) is a mixture of grape seed extract and pomegranate seed extract in a mass ratio of 5:3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline buffer solution in the step (6) is a mixture of sodium carbonate and sodium hydroxide in a mass ratio of 3:5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The degumming agent in the step (4) comprises sodium bicarbonate, sodium citrate and sodium oxalate in a mass ratio of 1:2:1. In the step (8), the vitamin is vitamin C, vitamin E and vitamin B in a mass ratio of 3:1:1-7:3:3.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC D03-C01; D10-A01; D10-A02
IP C11B-001/04; C11B-001/10; C11B-003/00; C11B-003/06; A23D-009/007; A23D-009/04

PN CN108816180-A
TI Preparing microporous adsorption material used to capture carbon dioxide includes grinding coal ash, mixing with sodium hydroxide, calcining, neutralizing, ultrasonifying, adding e.g. tetraethyl orthosilicate, and hydrothermally treating.
AU LIU Y
   YU X
   SU F
   WANG X
AE UNIV FUJIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYFI-C)
GA 201892036F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing microporous adsorption material comprises grinding and sieving coal ash from the coal-fired power plant, mixing with sodium hydroxide, calcining, crushing, dissolving in deionized water, neutralizing by adding hydrochloric acid, stirring, ultrasonifying to obtain solution, dissolving the template agent with the obtained solution, adding concentrated hydrochloric acid, stirring, dissolving, adding n-butanol and tetraethyl orthosilicate, sonicating, hydrothermally treating, drying to obtain a white powder, and calcining to remove the template and to obtain a carrier, adding amine into the carrier, adding cyclohexane, adding (3-chloropropyl)trimethoxysilane with the carrier, sonicating, suction filtering and vacuum drying to obtain a sample, adding the amine compound with the carrier to absolute ethanol, ultrasonifying, adding the sample after uniform dispersion, ultrasonifying, filtering, washing with ethanol and vacuum drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing microporous adsorption material for capturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method shortens the preparation time. The adsorption material: has synergistic effect; accelerates the chemical bonding of amino compound and silicon hydroxy; has large surface area (about 700 m2 g); has high physical adsorption rate at room temperature, has high adsorption capacity, preferably chemical adsorption; and introduces amine rich groups in the pore canal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing microporous adsorption material comprises (a) grinding and sieving coal ash from the coal-fired power plant to obtain particles having diameter of 20-50 mu m, mixing with sodium hydroxide in a mass ratio of 1:1-1:4, calcining at 500 degrees C for 60 minutes in a furnace, taking out, crushing, dissolving 1 g fly ash/100 ml deionized water, neutralizing by adding hydrochloric acid, stirring at 65 degrees C for 2 hours, ultrasonically treating for 3 hours to obtain solution, dissolving the templating agent having 2-3 times the mass of fly ash in the obtained solution, adding 35 wt.% concentrated hydrochloric acid (4 times) the mass of fly ash, stirring, dissolving at 40 degrees C, adding n-butanol twice with a mass of fly ash, continuing stirring at 40 degrees C for 1 hours, adding tetraethyl orthosilicate with (0.5-1 times) fly ash, sonicating for 30 minutes, continuing stirring at 40 degrees C, hydrothermally treating the mixed solution after an hour at 100 degrees C for 24 hours, drying at 100 degrees C to obtain a white powder, and calcining the white powder in air at 550 degrees C to remove the template to obtain a carrier, and (b) adding the amine into a carrier, adding to cyclohexane at a concentration of 1 g/10 ml, stirring at room temperature for 30 minutes, adding (3-chloropropyl)trimethoxysilane (2-3 times) with the carrier, stirring for 20 minutes, ultrasonically processing for 3 hours, suction filtering and vacuum drying for 16 hours to obtain a resulting sample, adding (2-4 times) the amine compound with the carrier to absolute ethanol at a concentration of 1 g/30 ml, ultrasonically treating for 1 hour, adding the sample after the uniform dispersion, ultrasonically processing for 2 hours, filtering the solidified product and washing twice with 100 ml ethanol, vacuum drying at 80 degrees C for 16 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The templating agent is P123 (RTM: Pluronic P123) or F127 (RTM: Pluronic F127). The amine compound is tetraethylenepentamine, pentaethylenehexamine or polyethylenimine.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN108419977-A
TI Preparing solid beverage involves weighing milk thistle, Prinsepia utilis, Dendrobium officinale, raisin tree seed, Ophiopogon root, green tea, medlar, longan slurry, ebony, sugar or aspartame, and chitosan.
AU HUANG S
   HUANG Y
AE HEILONGJIANG JUNMEN MODERN AGRIC DEV CO (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 201866937X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing solid beverage involves weighing 100-150 pts. wt. milk thistle, 20-50 pts. wt. Prinsepia utilis, 100-120 pts. wt. Dendrobium officinale, 80-100 pts. wt. raisin tree seed, 50-70 pts. wt. Ophiopogon root, 80-100 pts. wt. green tea, 20-40 pts. wt. medlar, 20-40 pts. wt. longan slurry, 20-40 pts. wt. ebony, 0.5-5 pts. wt. sugar or aspartame and 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. chitosan, cleaning the milk thistle and Prinsepia utilis, drying, putting into ultra-micro crusher, crushing, granulating into 50-100 meshes granularity, processing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and then introducing 0.5-1 wt.% acetone as entrainer, extracting, separating into the separation kettle, rectifying to obtain composite oil, collecting the remaining mixture, and weighing Dendrobium officinale, medlar,Ophiopogon root and green tea.
   USE - Method used for preparing solid beverage (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method offers product with less than 20 mg/100 ml reduced alcohol level after 20 minutes of intake to ensures that the drinking population can return to normal life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing solid beverage involves weighing 100-150 pts. wt. milk thistle, 20-50 pts. wt. Prinsepia utilis, 100-120 pts. wt. Dendrobium officinale, 80-100 pts. wt. raisin tree seed, 50-70 pts. wt. Ophiopogon root, 80-100 pts. wt. green tea, 20-40 pts. wt. medlar, 20-40 pts. wt. longan slurry, 20-40 pts. wt. ebony, 0.5-5 pts. wt. sugar or aspartame and 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. chitosan, cleaning the milk thistle and Prinsepia utilis, drying, putting into ultra-micro crusher, crushing, granulating into 50-100 meshes granularity, processing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and then introducing 0.5-1 wt.% acetone as entrainer, extracting, separating into the separation kettle, rectifying to obtain composite oil, collecting the remaining mixture, weighing Dendrobium officinale, medlar,Ophiopogon root and green tea, cleaning, drying, putting in ultra-micro crusher to crush, sieving, adding anhydrous ethanol solution to the mixture of powder for the first time, and extracting, separating the extract liquid, adding absolute ethyl alcohol solution in the mixture, extracting for the second time, combining the extracting solution of two times to obtain the first extracting solution, puting the longan slurry, ebony and sugar or aspartame into ultra-micro crusher for crushing, drying for 1-2 hours at 40-50 degrees C temperature, sieving to obtain powder with 20-50 meshes granularity, mixing uniformly to obtain the first mixture, adding 3-5 times amount of anhydrous ethanol solution into the extract, stirring uniformly, microwave extracting at 50-70 degrees C temperature for 0.2-0.5 hours, standing for 0.5-1 hours, separating the extracting solution, adding 3-5 times amount of anhydrous ethanol solution in the mixture residue, extracting for 0.2-0.5 hours, combining the extract solution of two times to obtain the second extract liquid, mixing the first extract, sieving through 200-300 meshes sieve, firstly introducing nitrogen for 20-30 minutes, decompressing and distilling, recycling ethanol, concentrating, freeze-drying to obtain mixed freeze-dry powder, adding the first mixture prepared composite oil, stirring uniformly, standing for 4-6 hours, then drying at low temperature, crushing, adding, chitosan, mixing uniformly, followed by dry granulation, and vacuum packing to obtain the beverage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Compositions: Preparing solid beverage involves weighing 100 pts. wt. milk thistle, 20 pts. wt. Prinsepia utilis, 100 pts. wt. Dendrobium officinale, 80 pts. wt. raisin tree seed, 50 pts. wt. Ophiopogon root, 80 pts. wt. green tea, 20 pts. wt. medlar, 20 pts. wt. longan slurry, 20 pts. wt. ebony, 0.5 pts. wt. sugar or aspartame and 0.1 pts. wt. chitosan. Preferred Conditions: The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is done under 20-25 Mpa pressure at 38~40 degrees C temperature with 0.45-0.5kg/hour carbon dioxide flow rate for 0.5-2 hours. The particle size of the mixture powder after sieving is 100-200 mesh. The first extraction is done by adding 5-8 times amount of anhydrous ethanol solution to the mixture powder at 20-25 degrees C temperature, allowed to stand for 10-12 hours, and the second extraction is carried out by adding 4-5 times amount of anhydrous ethanol solution to the mixture, and extracting for 1-2 hours under 30 KHz ultrasonic frequency. The microwave extraction is performed with 500~1000 W power. The vacuum distillation is processed under -100 to -85 kPa pressure at 30-35 degrees C temperature for 20-60 minutes. The freeze-drying condition pre-freezing at -20 degrees C temperature for 2 hours, and in a vacuum freeze dryer, 10-100 Pa vacuum is applied at -35 to -40 degrees C cold heading temperature for 2-12 hours. The dry granulation process is processed under 30-40 kg/cm2 hydraulic pressure, 6-8 Hz feeding pressure, 8-10 Hz granulating pressure to obtain obtained dry particles having 0.8-1.0 mm particle size and 0.5-1.0 mm length.

PN CN108251361-A
TI Preparing mesenchymal stem cells by umbilical cord            tissue block cryopreservation and resuscitation            comprises e.g. disinfecting surface of umbilical cord            tissue with disinfectant, inoculating block tissue and            adding into serum-free medium.
AU WANG J
   WANG R
   XIONG H
   QIN L
AE CHONGQING SIDEMU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201858138M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mesenchymal stem cells by umbilical                cord tissue block cryopreservation and                resuscitation comprises e.g. (i) disinfecting                surface of umbilical cord tissue with disinfectant,                cut umbilical cord, tiling, and washing umbilical                cord tissue with buffer, (ii) cutting the umbilical                cord tissue into tissue segments, (iii) peeling off                Wharton candy of tissue segment, placing Wharton                candy in clean centrifuge tube, and cutting it into                blocks by surgical shearing, (iv) adding serum-free                medium into massive tissue, adding pre-freezing                solution into serum-free medium and dispensing into                cryotube, (v) refrigerating cryotube containing                massive structure, (vi) thawing, pipette out thawed                tissue block, placing in clean centrifuge tube,                (vii) inoculating block tissue after centrifugation                into gelatin-coated petri dish, adding into                serum-free medium, and culturing in carbon dioxide                incubator, and (viii) first change of liquid after                3-5 days after culture, and changing liquid after                4-5 days.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing mesenchymal                stem cells by umbilical cord tissue block                cryopreservation and resuscitation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method protects frozen umbilical cord                tissue and is suitable for isolating and expanding                mesenchymal stem cells after resuscitation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing mesenchymal stem cells by umbilical                cord tissue block cryopreservation and                resuscitation comprises (i) disinfecting the                surface of umbilical cord tissue with disinfectant,                cut the umbilical cord, tiling, and washing the                umbilical cord tissue with buffer, (ii) cutting the                umbilical cord tissue into tissue segments, (iii)                peeling off Wharton candy of the tissue segment,                placing Wharton candy in clean centrifuge tube, and                cutting it into blocks by surgical shearing, (iv)                adding serum-free medium into the massive tissue,                where volume of serum-free medium is one-half the                volume of bulk tissue, re-suspending the massive                tissue, adding equal volume of pre-freezing                solution into the serum-free medium and dispensing                into the cryotube, (v) refrigerating cryotube                containing massive structure at 4 degrees C for 0.5                hour, refrigerating at -20 degrees C for 2 hours,                freezing at -80 degrees C for 1 day, and finally                freezing in liquid nitrogen at -196 degrees C for                use, (vi) If necessary, remove the frozen tissue                from step (v) from liquid nitrogen, thawing in                constant temperature water bath, pipette out thawed                tissue block, placing in clean centrifuge tube, and                adding it into the serum-free medium, then                centrifugating at low temperature to remove                supernatant, (vii) inoculating block tissue after                centrifugation into gelatin-coated petri dish,                adding into serum-free medium, and then culturing                in carbon dioxide incubator, and (viii) first                change of liquid after 3-5 days after the culture,                and changing liquid after 4-5 days, where cell                fusion degree reaches 70% or more, trypsin is used                for digestion and passage. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                also included for establishing database of                umbilical cord stem cells comprising freezing                passaged cells in liquid nitrogen and recording                relevant fetal information, carrying out cell                biological characteristics and multi-directional                differentiation potential identification,                performing molecular genetic diagnosis of cells,                saving all relevant data of the cells, establishing                database of umbilical cord stem cells and                associating with the cryopreserved cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The disinfectant is                  75% by volume of alcohol; and buffer is                  phosphate-buffered saline buffer containing amino                  penicillin and streptomycin sulfate. The length                  of tissue segment is 2-4 cm; and block surface                  area is 1-2 mm3. The pre-freezing solution                  contains dimethyl sulfoxide. The lipids include                  cholesterol, arachidonic acid, protocatechuic                  acid, palmitic acid, palmitoleic acid, stearic                  acid, oleic acid, linoleic acid and/or linolenic                  acid. The trace elements include magnesium,                  copper, zinc, selenium, iron, tin, germanium,                  rubidium, cobalt, and manganese. The serum-free                  medium comprises 10.3 g/l alpha -MEM, 2.3 g/l                  sodium bicarbonate, 1.8 g/l sodium carbonate, 2                  g/l sodium monohydrogen phosphate, 1.7 g/l sodium                  dihydrogen phosphate, 2-8 mM L-glutamine, 2-6 g/l                  recombinant human albumin, 10-15 mg/l recombinant                  human transferrin, 5-10 mg/l recombinant human                  insulin, 50nM beta -mercapto ethanol, 0.1-3 mg/l                  glutathione, 0.5-5 mg/l p-amino benzoic acid,                  25-40 mg/l vitamin B, 20-50 mg/l vitamin C, 10-20                  ng/ml progesterone, 5-10 mg/l putrescine, 5-10                  IU/ml heparin, 5-10 ng/ml epidermal growth                  factor, 6-10 ng/ml basic fibroblast growth                  factor, 6-10 ng/ml hepatocyte growth factor, 6-10                  ng/ml vascular endothelial growth factor, 0.1-1                  mg/l lipid, 1-8 mg/l trace elements and 6-12 mu M                  3-(1,3-bis(pyridin-4-ylamino)allyl)cyclopentanol                  (I). Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises (ix) detecting at least one of the                  following items for the umbilical cord                  mesenchymal stem cells: cell activity, cell                  contamination, genetic disease and human                  leukocyte antigen-ABC/DR matching or (x) freezing                  umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells in liquid                  nitrogen for use.

   USE - Thermal insulation material.
   ADVANTAGE - The thermal insulation material is energy-saving; and has wide material sources, simple preparation process, high strength, good heat insulation and heat preservation effect, no cracking, and long service life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Thermal insulation material comprises 6-12 pts. wt. styrene-butadiene rubber, 15-20 pts. wt. calcined gypsum, 10-13 pts. wt. sepiolite powder, 12-18 pts. wt. plant ash, 3-5 pts. wt. glass short fibers, 45-60 pts. wt. cement, 6-20 pts. wt. aqueous monomer, 3-6 pts. wt. gelatin, 1-2.4 pts. wt. foaming agent, 0.7-1.5 pts. wt. bamboo fiber, 4-8 pts. wt. floating beads, 2-4 pts. wt. water repellent, 8-15 pts. wt. shell, 1.5-2.0 pts. wt. dispersant, 0.6-1.0 pts. wt. surfactant, 1.3-2.5 pts. wt. vegetable oil, 0.6-1.4 pts. wt. UV absorber, 4-8 pts. wt. almonds, 2-6 pts. wt. 2-6 pts. wt. licorice, and 5-10 pts. wt. polyethylene. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of thermal insulation material by drying almonds; grinding to particle size of 0.2-0.8 mm; subjecting almond powder to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction at 45-58 degrees C under 25-32 MPa pressure and carbon dioxide flowrate of 20-30 l/hour; separating to obtain almond extract; crushing licorice and soaking in ether; heating to reflux for 2-3 hours; filtering to separate licorice reflux liquid and dregs; adding dregs with 3-6 times amount water; decocting at 80-86 degrees C for 2-4 times; combining decoctions and filtering to obtain licorice decoction; combining licorice reflux liquid and licorice decoction to obtain licorice extract; mixing styrene-butadiene rubber, anhydrite, sepiolite powder, short glass fiber, cement, bamboo charcoal fiber, floating beads, shell, and polyethylene; grinding to 150-180 meshes to obtain first mixture; mixing aqueous monomer, licorice extract, gelatin and vegetable oil to obtain first mixed solution; boiling first mixed solution; adding almond extract and sepiolite powder; stirring while reducing temperature at 45-53 degrees C to obtain second mixed solution; adding first mixture, ash, dispersant, and vegetable oil to second mixed solution; uniformly mixing; adding warm water at 45-55 degrees C; uniformly mixing to obtain slurry; adding foaming agent, water repellent, surfactants and UV absorber; stirring for 4-6 hours; precipitating for 12-15 hours to obtain semi-finished products; injecting semi-finished product into molding die; press-forming; drying at 40-50 degrees C; cooling; packaging; and storing to obtain finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Component: The cement is 400 Portland cement. Preferred Method: The grinding step is performed at 45-60 revolutions per minute. The press-forming step is performed at 15-22 MPa.

AE UNIV SHIHEZI (USHZ-C)
GA 201817276G
AB    NOVELTY - Saddle shape weld seam welding control system based on PLC and touch screen comprises a welding device and a control system, where the welding device is connected to the control system, where the welding device comprises a portal frame lifting mechanism, welding gun lifting mechanism, machine horizontal moving mechanism, body revolving mechanism and the welding gun clamping mechanism. The portal frame lifting deceleration motor is arranged on the deceleration motor I. The welding gun lifting mechanism is arranged up and down alternating current servo motor I.
   USE - The method is useful for carbon dioxide gas protection welding.
   ADVANTAGE - The human computer interaction interface reduces the operation difficulty of the system, improves the welding efficiency and realizes the automatic welding of the saddle-shaped welding line.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Saddle shape weld seam welding control system based on PLC and touch screen comprises a welding device and a control system, where the welding device is connected to the control system, where the welding device comprises a portal frame lifting mechanism, welding gun lifting mechanism, machine horizontal moving mechanism, body revolving mechanism and the welding gun clamping mechanism. The portal frame lifting deceleration motor is arranged on the deceleration motor I. The welding gun lifting mechanism is arranged up and down alternating current servo motor I, where the machine horizontal moving mechanism is a reducing motor II. The alternating current (AC) servo motor II is arranged on the body revolving mechanism. The welding gun clamping mechanism is located on the front welding gun. The deceleration motor I, speed deceleration motor II, AC servo motor I and AC servo motor II is connected to the control system. The welding device and the control system is connected to the cable junction, which are built in a plastic drag chain, which is convenient to protect and maintenance. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for controlling saddle shape weld seam welding control system based on PLC and touch screen, comprising (i) according to the welding type the user selects the touch screen interface, where the touch screen provides a friendly human-computer interaction Interface and then enters the corresponding welding process parameters according to the chooses type; (ii) extracting the control system extracts of the running trajectory of the welding torch pendulum in the space through algorithm analysis and calculating the respective corresponding to the space curve, where the motion pulse and speed of the axis are saved in the form of a document; (iii) transmitting of control system, which results the calculation data to the PLC before the PLC controls the motor operation, where the PLC performs the operation according to the sequence of the motion control program edited by the user when encountering, when the motor instruction is driven, the pulse and speed operands are extracted at a predetermined address for use by the program; and (iv) transmitting of the pulse signal to the servo driver to control the actuator of the output end of the servo motor using PLC to complete the movement of the saddle-shaped welding path.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the saddle shape weld seam welding control system based on PLC and touch screen
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The control system comprises a PLC controller, motor driver, servo motor driver, touch screen and an operation box, where the PLC controller is connected to the motor driver. The motor driver is connected to a reducing motor I, reducing motor II, alternating servo motor I and the alternating current servo motor II. The destination of the data transmission of the various movement parameters pre-stored in the specified storage area of PLC, then control program begins to run, then the PLC to the scanning period, when detecting the pulse output condition is satisfied, then the pulse instruction set address area obtained according to the motion parameter.
