TI Manufacturing water-decomposition material            comprises drying and filtering prepared second mixture            including first mixture (e.g. solid raw material and            solvent), anionic dispersant and organic binder to            obtain filtrate and calcining filtrate.
AU JANG Y S
   KIM U
   HYUN L J
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
GA 2022C99765
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing water-decomposition material                comprises (a) preparing first mixture comprising                solid raw material and solvent, (b) preparing                second mixture including first mixture, anionic                dispersant and organic binder, (c) drying and                filtering second mixture to obtain filtrate and (d)                calcining filtrate. The second mixture comprises                100 pts. wt. solid raw material, 1-10 pts. wt.                anionic dispersant, 1-10 pts. wt. organic binder                and 100-150 pts. wt. solvent, where organic binder                includes glycol-based binder, solid raw material                includes iron oxide-based active material and                support of a spinel structure, where support                includes magnesium aluminate (MgAl2O4) and iron                oxide-based active material has average size of                less than 17 &#956; m containing needle-shaped                ferric trioxide hydrate (Fe2O3.H2O) with a surface                area of 40-200 m2/g.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing                water-decomposition material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly,                reduces greenhouse gases and carbon dioxide,                increases purity of hydrogen, has excellent thermal                stability, durability, reactivity with water,                hydrogen production efficiency and long-term                performance and prevents performance                degradation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a method for manufacturing                water decomposition material (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
TF EXAMPLE - The second mixture (obtained by adding 2 pts.                wt. dispersant (ammonium polycarboxylic acid salt)                to the first mixture (obtained by mixing 70 wt.%                ferric trioxide and 30 wt.% spinel structure                support (magnesium aluminate) as an iron                oxide-based activator with 100 pts. wt. solid raw                material containing 100-150 pts. wt. solvent                (distilled water)) based on 100 pts. wt. solid raw                material, and stirring at 400-650                revolutions/minute to prepare a first stirred                product, adding 3 pts. wt. organic binder                (polyethylene glycol) to first stirred water and                stirring at 400-650 revolutions/minute) was dried                in an air atmosphere reflux dryer at                120-200&#176; C for 24 hours, filtered through                a sieve, and a filtrate having a size of 53-150                &#956; m was obtained. The filtrate was heated                and maintained at a temperature increase rate of                5&#176; C/minute step by step at intervals of                100&#176; C from 200&#176; C, temperature                was raised to 1000&#176; C and maintained for                12 hours and calcined to prepare a water                decomposition material.

PN CN114873640-A
TI Preparing fibrous zirconia aerogel, comprises e.g.            dissolving inorganic metal zirconium salt in absolute            ethanol, adding hydrogen peroxide solution, stirring,            adding mercaptosuccinic acid ethanol solution into            mixed solution, aging wet gel, and drying wet gel in            supercritical drying device.
AU ZHANG X
   YANG B
   OU D
   ZHI M
   HONG Z
   SHI Z
   WANG X
AE UNIV CHUZHOU (UYCH-Non-standard)
GA 2022A60155
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fibrous zirconia aerogel, comprises                (1) dissolving the inorganic metal zirconium salt                in absolute ethanol, and configuring a zirconium                salt precursor solution, where the zirconium ion                concentration is 0.1-3.0 mol/l, (2) adding the                hydrogen peroxide solution into the zirconium salt                precursor solution, and stirring to obtain a mixed                solution, (3) adding the mercaptosuccinic acid                ethanol solution into the mixed solution, stirring                rapidly, sealing and placing in a 25-70&#8451;                environment to obtain a wet gel, (4) immersing the                wet gel in an aging solution at a temperature of                25-70&#8451;, and aging for 2-7 days, where the                aging solution is anhydrous ethanol solution, and                (5) placing the wet gel into the autoclave of the                supercritical drying device, using ethanol or                carbon dioxide as the supercritical medium for                supercritical drying, releasing the gas in the                autoclave to obtain nanofibrous zirconia                aerogels.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fibrous                zirconia aerogel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enures that the aerogel has low                density, high specific surface area and high                porosity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic metal                  zirconium salt comprises zirconium(IV)                  oxychloride octahydrate, zirconium oxynitrate                  hexahydrate and/or zirconium sulfate                  heptahydrate. The mass fraction of the hydrogen                  peroxide used is 0.1-30 wt.%, and the volume                  ratio of the hydrogen peroxide to the zirconium                  salt precursor solution is 1/10-1/1.Preferred Method: In the step (5), the                  ethanol supercritical drying is performed at                  260-300&#8451; for 30-120minutes, and the                  pressure is 7-12 MPa. In the step (5), the carbon                  dioxide supercritical drying is performed at                  45-75&#8451; for 90-150 minutes, and the                  pressure is 10-15MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the                  mercaptosuccinic acid ethanol solution, the                  concentration of mercaptosuccinic acid is 0.1-10                  mol/l, and the volume ratio of the                  mercaptosuccinic acid ethanol solution to the                  zirconium salt precursor solution is                  1/30-1/2.

   TONG Y
   ZHAN Z
AE UNIV SCI & TECHNOLOGY CHINA ADVANCED TEC (UYSC-Non-standard)
GA 2022843469
AB    NOVELTY - A proton conductor solid oxide cell comprises                a positive electrode framework layer, a positive                electrode barrier layer, an electrolyte membrane                layer made of a proton conductive oxide, a negative                electrode barrier layer and a negative electrode                framework layer laminated in order from top to                bottom. The positive and negative electrode                framework layers are made of porous doped cerium                oxide. The positive and negative electrode barrier                layers are made of dense doped cerium oxide.
   USE - Proton conductor solid oxide cell used for                converting hydrogen, carbon monoxide, natural gas                and other fuel into electricity and heat, and for                storing surplus electric energy and heat                energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The cell has enhanced performance stability                due to use of oxide with higher proton conductivity                as the electrolyte membrane layer, porous doped                cerium oxide as the electrode framework layer, and                dense doped cerium oxide barrier layer between the                electrolyte membrane and the positive and negative                electrodes to avoid direct contact between the                electrolyte membrane and carbon dioxide or water                present in the porous electrode and enhance the                chemical and structural stability of the                electrolyte membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The proton conductive                  oxide comprises doped barium-cerium oxide                  (BaCeO3-delta), doped barium-zirconium oxide                  (BaZrO3-delta), barium-zirconium-cerium-yttrium                  oxide (BaZr1-x-yCexYyO3-delta,                  strontium-scandium-niobium oxide                  (Sr2Sc1+xNb1-xO6-delta) or barium-calcium-niobium                  oxide (Ba3Ca1+xNb2-xO9), where x, y and delta are                  0-1 (excluding 0 and 1). The doped cerium oxide                  comprises cerium-lanthanum oxide                  (Ce1-xLaxO2-delta), cerium-yttrium oxide                  (Ce1-xYxO2-delta), cerium-samarium oxide                  (Ce1-xSmxO2-delta), cerium-gadolinium oxide                  (Ce1-xGdxO2-delta), cerium-praseodymium oxide                  (Ce1-xPrxO2-delta), cerium-neodymium oxide                  (Ce1-xNdxO2-delta), cerium-calcium oxide                  (Ce1-xCaxO2-delta) or cerium-magnesium oxide                  (Ce1-xMgxO2-delta), where x and delta are 0-1                  (excluding 0 and 1). The positive electrode                  framework layer is loaded with a nano positive                  electrode catalyst. The negative electrode                  framework layer is loaded with a nano negative                  electrode catalyst. The nano positive electrode                  catalyst comprises oxygen ion/proton-electron                  mixed conductive oxide, or electronic conductive                  oxide, preferably lanthanum-strontium-cobalt-iron                  oxide ((La1-xSrx)(Co1-yFey)O3-delta),                  barium-strontium-cobalt-iron oxide                  ((Ba1-xSrx)(Co1-yFey)O3-delta),                  samarium-strontium-cobalt oxide                  ((Sm1-xSrx)CoO3-delta),                  lanthanum-barium-strontium-cobalt oxide                  (LaBa0.5Sr0.5Co2O5+delta),                  samarium-barium-strontium-cobalt oxide                  (SmBa0.5Sr0.5Co2O5+delta), samarium-barium-cobalt                  oxide (SmBaCo2O5+delta),                  barium-gadolinium-lanthanum-cobalt-iron oxide                  (BaGd0.8La0.2Co2O6-delta) or                  lanthanum-strontium-iron-nickel-molybdenum oxide                  (LaxSr2-xFe2-y-zNiyMozO6-delta), where x, y, z                  and delta are 0-1 (excluding 0 and 1). The nano                  negative electrode catalyst comprises metals,                  alloys, electronically conductive oxides, oxygen                  ion/proton-electronic mixed conductive oxides,                  and/or their composites, preferably nickel, iron,                  cobalt, iron-nickel (FeNi3),                  nickel-cerium-samarium oxide                  (Ni-Ce1-xSmxO2-delta), nickel-cerium-gadolinium                  oxide (NiCe1-xGdxO2-delta),                  lanthanum-strontium-chromium-iron oxide                  ((La1-xSrx)(Cr1-yFey)O3-delta),                  lanthanum-strontium-chromium-manganese oxide                  ((La1-xSrx)(Cr1-yMny)O3-delta),                  lanthanum-strontium-titanium oxide                  ((La1-xSrx)TiO3-delta) or                  lanthanum-strontium-iron-nickel-molybdenum oxide                  (La2-xSrxFe2-y-zNiyMozO6-delta), where x, y, z                  and delta are 0-1 (excluding 0 and 1).Preferred Properties: The positive and                  negative electrode framework layers have a                  porosity of 10-70% and equal thickness. The                  thicknesses difference between the positive and                  negative electrode framework layers is 0.1-2 mm.                  The positive and negative electrode barrier                  layers and the electrolyte membrane have a                  thickness of 1-30 microm. The total weight of the                  nano positive electrode catalyst is 10-50 wt.% of                  the positive electrode framework layer. The total                  weight of the nano negative electrode catalyst is                  10-50 wt.% of the negative electrode framework                  layer. The nano positive electrode catalyst and                  the nano negative electrode catalyst have a                  particle diameter of 5-500 nm.

PN CN114605651-A
TI Preparing modified polyethylene used as            thermoplastic resin, comprises e.g. dispersing            magnesium hydroxide in water, adding alcohol solution            of gamma aminopropyltriethoxysilane, stirring reaction,            separating, washing, melting, extruding, cooling,            granulating, and drying.
AU JIA J
AE NINGBO NENGZHIGUANG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOL (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202282829E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified polyethylene based on                supercritical grafting reaction comprises (1)                dispersing magnesium hydroxide in water, adding                alcohol solution of                &#947;-aminopropyltriethoxysilane, stirring                reaction, separating, washing and drying the                product to obtain amino-modified magnesium                hydroxide, (2) mixing the double-ended                hydrogen-containing silicone oil with epoxy butene,                adding catalyst, and reacting to obtain                double-ended epoxy-based silicone oil, (3) adding                amino-modified magnesium hydroxide, double-ended                epoxy silicone oil and 4-aminostyrene into solvent,                stirring uniformly, adding catalyst, and reacting                to obtain graft monomer, and (4) adding                polyethylene, grafting monomer and initiator into                the extruder, injecting supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid into the extruder, melting and                extruding, cooling, granulating, and drying to                obtain modified polyethylene.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing modified                polyethylene, which is used as a thermoplastic                resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses silicone oil and 4-amino                styrene to modify the surface of magnesium                hydroxide, connects magnesium hydroxide to the                polyethylene side chain through supercritical                grafting reaction, and effectively improves flame                retardant properties of polyethylene without                affecting the processing properties and mechanical                properties. The modified polyethylene has improved                flame retardant property.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the mass                  ratio of magnesium hydroxide and                  &#947;-aminopropyltriethoxysilane is 10-20:1.                  In the step (2), the molar ratio of active                  hydrogen and epoxybutene in the double-ended                  hydrogen-containing silicone oil is 1:1-1.2, and                  the molecular weight of the double-ended                  hydrogen-containing silicone oil is 200-400. In                  the step (2), the catalyst is chloroplatinic                  acid, where the adding amount of the catalyst is                  5-10 ppm, the reaction temperature is                  100-120&#8451;, and the reaction time is 2-4                  hours. In the step (3), the mass ratio of                  amino-modified magnesium hydroxide, double-ended                  epoxy silicone oil, and 4-aminostyrene is                  20-30:3-6:1. In the step (3), the catalyst is                  zinc chloride, where the adding amount of the                  catalyst is 1-3% of the total mass of the                  reactant, the reaction temperature is                  40-50&#8451;, and the reaction time is 6-8                  hours. In the step (4), the mass ratio of                  polyethylene, graft monomer and initiator is                  100:20~30:1-3. In the step (4), the mass of the                  supercritical carbon dioxide fluid injected is                  1-10% of the total mass of the reactants. In the                  step (4), the extruding temperature is                  180-200&#8451;.Preferred Components: In the step (4), the                  initiator is at least one dicumyl peroxide,                  azobisisobutyronitrile,                  2,5-dimethyl-2,5-di-tert-butylperoxide-n-hexane                  and dibenzoyl peroxide.

GA 2022694918
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite material comprises (i)                mixing filler powder, modifier and deionized water,                mixing, and removing supernatant to obtain glue                solution, where the mass ratio of filler powder to                deionizing water is 1:3-6, (ii) stirring and                reacting the glue solution under the supercritical                carbon dioxide atmosphere at 40-70&#8451;, and                drying, (iii) mixing the modified filler powder                with poly(butylene adipate/terephthalate),                polylactic acid and initiator, melting and mixing                the mixture material in a double-screw extruder at                150-200&#8451;, and granulating, and (iv)                drying the composite material.
   USE - The composite material is useful for                manufacturing degradable disposable lunch box, and                also useful in plastic product and paper                product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves compatibility                of composite material, uniformly dispersed filler,                improves mechanical property of the composite                material, provides degradable disposable lunch box                with improved compatibility and dispersibility of                filler by surface of the modification and chemical                reaction, and improves combination condition of the                filler powder and substrate resin interface.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                composite material for manufacturing degradable                disposable lunch box.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The filler powder is                  1-20 pts. wt., modifier is 0.1-3 pts. wt., where                  the total weight of polybutylene adipate                  terephthalate and polylactic acid is 100 pts.                  wt., and the initiator is 0.1-2 pts. wt. In the                  method, the amount of the polybutylene adipate                  terephthalate is 0-100 pts. wt., where the weight                  average molecular weight is 150000-400000, and                  polylactic acid is 0-100 pts. wt., and the weight                  average molecular weight is 100000-250000.Preferred Components: The filler powder is                  at least one of calcium carbonate powder, egg                  shell powder and shell powder, where the particle                  diameter of the filler powder is 50-500 nm. In the step (iii), the rotational                  speed of the double-screw extruder is 150-220                  revolutions/minute, and the drying temperature is                  80&#8451;.

PN CN114057566-A
TI Preparation of 2-hydroxy-6-naphthoic acid involves reacting 2-naphthol, potassium hydroxide solution and light oil into autoclave, adding diethyl ether solution with activated carbon, reacting, purifying, washing with water, and drying.
AU XU L
   YU R
   ZHANG D
AE NINGXIA QINGYAN POLYMER NEW MATERIAL CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202232082V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of 2-hydroxy-6-naphthoic acid comprises adding 2-naphthol, potassium hydroxide solution and light oil into an autoclave, setting temperature to 150-170degreesC and pressure to 400-600 kPa, stirring, reacting for 2-3 hours, decompression distilling and dehydrating at 200-400 kPa and 100-110degreesC for 1-1.5 hours, transferring to a carboxylation reactor with carbon dioxide gas, reacting at 220-240degreesC and 700-900 kPa for 1-2 hours, adding water to dissolve, adding sulfuric acid solution to acidify, washing with water, filtering, and drying; and adding to diethyl ether solution with activated carbon, stir-reacting at 90-100degreesC for 45-60 minutes, filtering, diluting the filtrate with distilled water, cooling to room temperature, filtering, washing, drying, centrifuging with distilled water and sulfuric acid solution, ultrasonically dispersing at 40-60degreesC, purifying by high-speed centrifugal dehydration in a centrifuge, washing with water, and drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing 2-hydroxy-6-naphthoic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves the purity of the 2-hydroxy-6-naphthalene-carboxylic acid material, is mild, simple, and easy to operate, and has low device requirement.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first step raw materials comprises 20-30 pts. wt. 2-naphthol, 15-20 pts. wt. potassium hydroxide solution and 50-65 pts. wt. light oil.

PN CN113620289-A
TI Preparing particle carbon material for separating propylene/propane useful in actual industrial separation of propylene and propane in fixed bed directly packed, comprises e.g. screening rice grains, performing condensation polymerization and carbonization, and naturally cooling.
AU XIA Q
   ZHANG J
   ZHOU X
   DAI Q
   LI Z
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2021D1246U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing particle carbon material for separating propylene/propane comprises (i) screening rice grains, and then immersing in an iron salt solution ultrasonically, then filtering and drying, (ii) transferring the impregnated and dried rice to an autoclave, and performing condensation polymerization and carbonization in the presence of water vapor to obtain carbon material precursor, (iii) transferring the obtained granular carbon precursor material to a tube furnace, programming the temperature in an inert atmosphere for reaching the set temperature, and carrying out deep carbonization at constant temperature, and(iv) switching the gas atmosphere of the tube furnace under carbon dioxide atmosphere, and heating to the activation temperature for activating to obtain micropores, naturally cooling to room temperature under the protection of nitrogen to obtain granular to obtain carbon material with a microporous macroporous structure.
   USE - The material is useful in actual industrial separation of propylene and propane in fixed bed directly packed.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has uniform particle size and high propane (C_3H_6/C_3H_8) adsorption selectivity, good diffusion rate, comprehensive performance of adsorption and separation can be in the forefront of the current international advanced level.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the grain size of the rice grains is 1-5 mm. In the step (i), the concentration of the iron salt is 0.03-0.4 mol/l. The ultrasonic immersion time is 5-60 minutes. The ultrasonic immersion temperature is 15-40degrees Celsius. In the step (ii), the temperature at which the polymerization reaction and carbonization are carried out in a steam atmosphere is 150-250degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 0.1-1.5 MPa. In the step (iii), the heating rate for the carbonization reaction is 2-10degrees Celsius/minute, and the carbonization temperature is 400-700degrees Celsius. In the step (iv), the activation temperature is 600-1000degrees Celsius, and the activation time is 1.2-3 hours.Preferred Components: The iron salt includes iron nitrate, iron sulfate, and/or iron chloride. In the step (iii), the inert gas atmosphere includes argon, nitrogen or a mixture of the two gases.

   (2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cleaning agent comprises 50-90 pts. wt. activated carbon, 1-10 pts. wt. copper element, 1-10 pts. wt. zinc element. The copper element exists in the form of copper oxide or copper oxide and malachite. The zinc element exists in the form of zinc oxide or zinc oxide and malachite. The purifying agent is useful in purifying gas or liquid, the gas or liquid comprises sulfur impurity, arsenic impurity, phosphorus impurity, preferably, the sulfur impurity is hydrogen sulfide, sulfur dioxide, carbonyl sulfur, preferably, the arsenic impurity is hydrogen arsenide, preferably, the phosphorus impurity is phosphine, preferably, the total content of the sulfur impurity, arsenic impurity and phosphorus impurity in the gas or liquid is 5-50000 ppm.Preferred Method: The preparation method of the coconut shell carbon comprises in the inert gas atmosphere, heating the raw material coconut shell carbon powder to 700-1000degrees Celsius, performing activation treatment for 160-300 minutes at 700-1000degrees Celsius in carbon dioxide atmosphere, and then cooling in the inert gas atmosphere, preferably, relative to 100 pts. wt. by weight of the raw material coconut shell carbon powder, the dosage of the carbon dioxide is 1-15 pts. wt. The purifying agent further comprises aluminium-containing compound, preferably aluminium oxide, 50-90 pts. wt. active carbon, 1-6 pts. wt. alumina, the purifying agent further comprises 1-5 pts. wt. binder. The contact method further comprises (1) mixing the precipitant and water to obtain solution I, (2) mixing the active carbon and solution I to obtain suspension II, (3) mixing the water soluble copper source, water-soluble zinc source, water-soluble aluminium source and water to obtain solution III; (4) mixing the precipitant and water to obtain the solution IV; the concentration of the precipitant in the solution IV is greater than the concentration of the precipitant in the solution I, (5) mixing the suspension II, solution III and solution IV, preferably, the water-soluble copper source is copper nitrate, copper sulfate, copper chloride; more preferably is copper nitrate; preferably, the water-soluble zinc source is zinc nitrate, zinc sulfate, zinc chloride, more preferably is zinc nitrate; preferably, the water-soluble aluminium source is aluminum nitrate, aluminium sulfate, aluminum chloride, more preferably is aluminum nitrate, preferably, the precipitator is water-soluble carbonate, more preferably from sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate and ammonium carbonate. The contact comprises maintaining temperature at 40-90degrees Celsius and the time is 0.5-3 hours, the baking conditions include maintaining roasting temperature at 250-500degrees Celsius preferably 350-500degrees Celsius and the roasting for 1-5 hours. The method further comprises washing the insoluble reaction mixture before roasting until the conductivity is less than or equal to 100 microS/cm, kneading and molding. The contact reaction comprises maintaining temperature at 20-100degrees Celsius, the pressure is 0.1-5 MPa, the volume space velocity of the gas to be purified is 10-10000 h1 or the mass space velocity of the to-be-purified liquid 0.1-10 h1.

Drilling holes are drilled above a                gob. PN CN113481153-A
TI Separating and culturing adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells, by soaking isolated adipose tissue in normal saline, adding digestion solution, centrifuging, adding sodium valproate and iron(II) phthalocyanine, resuspending, and adding e.g. norcantharidin and sangenone C.
AU LIU J
   LI M
AE NANTONG MEIWEIDE LIFE SCI CO LTD (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 2021B9026E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating and culturing adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells, involves obtaining isolated adipose tissue under aseptic conditions, soaking isolated adipose tissue in normal saline, cutting into pieces, adding digestion solution to the adipose tissue fragments, after the digestion is terminated, adding D-Hanks solution to dilute, centrifuging the solution to remove the supernatant, adding sodium valproate and iron(II) phthalocyanine to the sediment after centrifugation, resuspending the culture, inoculating culture into dish at 37degreesC and 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator, changing the medium after 1-2 days, adding norcantharidin, sangenone C and N-nicotinoylglycine to the medium, changing the medium with the above-mentioned components every 2-3 days, and culturing until the cells are fused after the digestion reaches 80-90% to obtain adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells for subculture process.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and culturing adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides fusion degree of the cells that reaches 80-90% within a period of time after continuous culture, and improves proliferation activity of the cells during the primary culture process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The medium is Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium (DMEM)/F12 medium. Preferred Composition: The culture medium comprises 8-13 ng/ml, preferably 10 ng/ml sodium valproate and 1-4.5 ng/ml, preferably 3 ng/ml iron(II) phthalocyanine. The culture medium comprises 1-5 mg/ml, preferably 3.5 mg/ml norcantharidin, 10-20 mug/ml, preferably 15 mug/ml sangenone C and 15-25 mug/ml, preferably 20 mug/ml N-nicotinoylglycine. The density of the cells in the medium with the addition of norcantharidin, sangenone C, and N-nicotinoylglycine is 1-4x105 pieces/ml. Preferred Method: The method involves adding adipose tissue fragments to 0.2-0.3% collagenase I for digestion treatment, where the volume of collagenase I is 3-5 times of the volume of adipose tissue, and digesting cells with 0.25% trypsin.

PN CN113461047-A
TI Method for recovering lead oxide from waste lead-acid batteries based on flash thermal decomposition, involves collecting lead oxide dust generated in vibration sieving conveying process and lead oxide powder obtained by thermal decomposification.
AU DU W
   YOU H
   DAI H
   LIU K
   CHEN X
AE TAIHE DAHUA ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZQLS-C)
GA 2021B7453X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves recycling and crushing waste lead acid storage battery, taking lead carbonate waste material and crushing until the particle size is 300-400 mesh. The recycled and crushed waste lead oxide waste material in the waste lead battery are vibrated and crushed through a vibration conveying mechanism. The lead acid waste material is cutted by an auger. The carbon dioxide gas adsorption is discharged. The lead oxide dust generated in the vibration sieving conveying process is collected by a dust collecting sedimentation recycling mechanism, and continuing to enter a Flash heat decomposition mechanism for thermal decomposing after precipitation. The lead oxide powder obtained by thermal decomposification is collected.
   USE - Method for recovering lead oxide from waste lead-acid batteries based on flash thermal decomposition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the utilization rate of the lead carbonate waste dust particles, and improves the recovery yield and purity of lead oxide. The lead oxide powder obtained by thermal decomposition is collected by the dust collecting precipitation recycling mechanism, thus improving the conveying efficiency and stability of the waste lead-acid storage battery.

PN EP3882297-A1; WO2021185710-A1
TI Process for starting up reactor for continuous            production of polyether carbonate polyol involves            mixing subset of H-functional starter substance and/or            suspension medium with specific catalyst of preset            concentration at specific condition.
AU VOR D E L
   BRAUN S
   BACHMANN R
   BECKER B
   BECKER D
   BAER F
   BACHMANN P
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 2021B0004R
AB    NOVELTY - Process for starting up the reactor for                continuous production of polyether carbonate polyol                involves adding alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide                in the presence of a double metal cyanide (DMC)                catalyst and/or a metal complex catalyst (A) based                on cobalt and/or zinc on H-functional starter                substance. The method involves mixing a subset of                H-functional starter substance and/or a suspension                medium free of H-functional groups in a reactor                with DMC catalyst and/or catalyst (A) of                concentration (s), continuously metering                H-functional starter substance, alkylene oxide and                DMC catalyst and/or catalyst (A) into reactor,                continuously discharging the mixture from reactor,                and attaining steady state. The concentration (s)                is 1.1 y or more and 10 y or less (y is catalyst                concentration based on mixture of steady state).                Alkylene oxide with a mass flow (X1) is added, and                X1 is continuously increased until mass flow X2                required for the steady state is reached. The time                to reach X2 is at least one hour.
   USE - Process for starting up reactor for continuous                production of polyether carbonate polyol.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyether carbonate polyol can be prepared                efficiently and continuously.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The concentration (s)                  is 1.5 y or more and 5 y or less, preferably 1.8                  y or more and 2.5 y or less. The mass flow rate                  (X2) is reached after two hours at the earliest                  and after six hours at the latest. Preferred                  Components: The concentration of free alkylene                  oxide in the reactor after the mass flow rate X                  is attained is 1.5-5 wt.%. The concentration of                  free alkylene oxide is 5% or less during the                  increase in the mass flow X1. Alkylene oxide is                  selected from ethylene oxide and propylene oxide.                  One or more H-functional starter substance                  containing at least 50 ppm component (K) is                  continuously metered into the reactor during the                  reaction. The component (K) is selected from at                  least one compound containing a phosphorus-oxygen                  bond, or one phosphorus compound, which can form                  one or more PO bond through reaction with                  OH-functional compounds. The H-functional starter                  substance is selected from ethylene glycol,                  propylene glycol, 1,3-propanediol,                  1,3-butanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol,                  2-methylpropane-1,3 diol, neopentyl glycol,                  1,6-hexanediol, 1,8-octanediol, diethylene                  glycol, dipropylene glycol, glycerol,                  trimethylolpropane, pentaerythritol, sorbitol,                  polyether carbonate polyol with number average                  molecular weight of 150-8000 g/mol and                  functionality of 2-3, and polyether polyol with                  molecular weight of 150-8000 g/mol and                  functionality of 2-3. The continuous process is                  restarted after standstill of 24 hours or less.                  The DMC catalyst is activated by adding a partial                  amount of alkylene oxide to the initial mixture                  optionally in presence of carbon dioxide, and                  generating temperature peak and/or pressure drop                  in the reactor due to the exothermic chemical                  reaction.

AB    NOVELTY - Waste gas recovery process, involves (a) passing pesticide production waste gas and recovering to be treated, adsorbing waste gas generated in pesticide production and processing by built-in filtration adsorption box, introducing the waste gas to waste gas collection tank using pipeline, and using inlet of upper end of waste gas collection tank in threaded connection with barometer, detecting exhaust gas collection condition using barometer, discharging exhaust gas collection tank to room temperature storage library to parallel sequence, checking, and performing to-be-processed operation, (b) performing waste gas leaching process, (c) performing waste gas separation cooling process, (d) performing exhaust gas purification recovery process, (e) performing gas storage operation for storing purified pure hydrogen gas by storage buffer tank, and reusing or transporting transport operation, and (f) storing using storage buffer tank, transporting, and avoiding collision and illumination.

   ADVANTAGE - The process relies on pressure swing adsorption method and the screening capacity of molecular sieve filler to remove carbon dioxide, water, carbon monoxide and other impurities.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves performing waste gas separation and cooling treatment process by performing waste gas separation treatment, connecting the leaching tower outlet with separator inlet, connecting separator outlet with booster buffer inlet, connecting leached waste gas to separator through pipeline, and connecting to separator, separating waste gas, and after pressurizing pressurization buffer and buffering, performing subsequent processing operations. The step (b) involves performing exhaust gas cooling treatment, connecting outlet of booster buffer to inlet of deoxygenation and dehydrocarbonization tower, connecting the outlet of deoxygenation and dehydrocarbonization tower to inlet of cooling tower, connecting the outlet of cooling tower to inlet of filter separator, deeply processing exhaust gas through various levels of equipment, cooling exhaust gas by cooling tower, deoxygenating and dehydrocarbonizing exhaust gas by deoxygenation and dehydrocarbonization tower, after series of processing treatments, performing recovery process. The step (d) involves performing waste gas purification and recovery process by performing exhaust gas purification treatment, connecting the outlet of filter separator and inlet of pressure swing adsorption purifier, purifying treated exhaust gas by pressure swing adsorption purifier, after deeply removing impurities, extracting and recycling using pipeline, performing waste gas recovery treatment, connecting the outlet of pressure swing adsorption purifier and inlet of storage buffer tank, connecting the outlet of storage buffer tank to inlet of hydrogenation tower, buffering processed gas, storing using storage buffer tank, and passing hydrogenation tower used twice. The step (b) involves when passing dehydrogenation tower gas to leaching tower, equipping leaching tower with automatic circulation device, where the automatic circulation device has circulation pump, a circulation pipe and an electromagnetic automatic control valve, one end of circulating pipe is threadedly connected to outlet of circulating pump, the other end is threaded to inlet of electromagnetic automatic control valve, the circulating pump pipeline is connected to outlet of leaching tower, and the electromagnetic automatic control valve pipeline is connected to inlet of leaching tower. In step (a), the filter adsorption box has built-in multi-layer activated carbon adsorption block. In step (d) and (a), the pressure swing adsorption purifier performs purification by pressure swing adsorption. In step (b), the leaching tower in separates the unreacted raw materials and other harmful impurities in dehydrogenation process, dissolving impurities soluble in water, and the leaching tower has a built-in water level detection switch and controls the leaching, where built-in suction pump in washing tower operates to perform automatic water replenishment, automatic circulation and automatic discharge treatment processes. The step (c) involves using separator for separating harmful impurities, and preforming deoxygenation and dehydrocarbonization process for removing oxygen and some harmful hydrocarbon impurities at 50-65 degrees C under catalyst condition. Preferred Device: In step (b), the constant pressure exhaust device has constant pressure exhaust valve, constant pressure exhaust pipe and exhaust pump, where one end of constant pressure exhaust pipe is threadedly connected to outlet of constant pressure exhaust valve, the other end is threadedly connected to inlet of exhaust pump, and the inlet of constant pressure exhaust valve is threadedly connected with inlet of leaching tower.

TI Method for operating blast furnace for producing            molten iron, involves generating regenerated methane            gas using byproduct gas discharged from blast furnace            and coke oven gas, and blowing oxygen gas and reducing            material into furnace.
AU YUYA M
   YUKI K
   TAIHEI N
   SUMITO O
   KOICHI T
   MORITA Y
   KAWASHIRI Y
   NOUCHI T
   OZAWA S
   TAKAHASHI K
   KAWASIRIYUKI
AE JFE STEEL CORP (JFES-C)
   JFE STEEL CORP (JFES-C)
GA 202179733F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for operating blast furnace involves                generating a regenerated methane gas (GG) using a                byproduct gas (AA) discharged from a blast furnace                and a coke oven gas (CC) which is a byproduct gas                discharged from a coke oven, and blowing an oxygen                gas as a blow gas (HH) and a reducing material (KK)                from a tuyere of the blast furnace into the blast                furnace, and using the regenerated methane gas as a                portion(s) of the reducing material.
   USE - Method for operating blast furnace used for                producing molten iron.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables stable and efficient                operation of blast furnace with reduced discharge                amount of carbon dioxide, content of nitrogen in                the byproduct gas discharged from blast furnace,                and usage amount of coke or powdered coal and                hydrogen for generating methane, without requiring                a pressure swing adsorption separation                apparatus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for blast                furnace ancillary facility.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view                explaining operation of blast furnace. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)
   Blast furnace gas (AA)
   Coke oven gas (CC)
   Regenerated methane gas (GG)
   Blow gas (HH)
   Oxygen (II)
   Molten iron (JJ)
   Reducing material (KK)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The output unit of                  circulating carbon atoms in the reducing material                  is 60 kg/t or more, which is the carbon                  equivalent mass of regenerated methane gas blown                  into the blast furnace as the reducing material,                  when 1 ton molten iron (JJ) is produced, and                  calculated using the relation: (output unit of                  circulating carbon atom (kg/t))=(mass of methane                  in regenerated methane gas blown into blast                  furnace as reducing material (kg))x                  (12/16)/(molten iron production amount (t)) (I).                  The output unit of the coke oven gas is 140 Nm3/t                  or less, which is the amount of coke oven gas                  used in the production of regenerated methane                  gas, when producing 1 ton molten iron. Preferred                  Composition: The concentration of oxygen (II) in                  the oxygen gas is 80 vol.% or more.

PN CN113070066-A
TI Preparing catalytic material with cobalt-copper bimetal oxide supported on foamed nickel comprises adding precipitating agent to aqueous solution containing cobalt salt and copper salt and reacting solution B with pretreated foamed nickel.
AU HE C
   YANG R
   MA M
   JIANG Z
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 202186179B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalytic material with cobalt-copper bimetal oxide supported on foamed nickel comprises adding precipitating agent to aqueous solution containing cobalt salt and copper salt to obtain solution B, reacting solution B with pretreated foamed nickel at 110-130 degrees C for 50-70 minutes, filtering, washing, drying and calcining to obtain a cobalt-copper bimetallic oxide supported on foamed nickel monolithic catalytic material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing catalytic material with cobalt-copper bimetal oxide supported on foamed nickel.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has high catalytic activity, excellent stability and universality and good carbon dioxide selectivity. The method: is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) catalytic material with cobalt-copper bimetal oxide supported on foamed nickel is prepared by the above mentioned method;
   (2) use of catalytic material with cobalt-copper bimetal oxide supported on foamed nickel in catalytic oxidation of ethyl acetate; and
   (3) use method of catalytic material with cobalt-copper bimetal oxide supported on foamed nickel comprising placing catalyst in fixed bed reactor, introducing ethyl acetate, performing reaction at 100-230 degrees C to realize the catalytic oxidation of ethyl acetate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cobalt salt is cobalt nitrate and the copper salt is copper nitrate. The molar ratio of cobalt nitrate and copper nitrate is 1:1/3-3. The mass ratio of total amount of cobalt nitrate and copper nitrate to amount of water is 20:150-170 (mmol:ml). The precipitating agent is urea. Preferred Method: The calcination is performed at 440-460 degrees C for 3-5 hours. The pretreatment of foamed nickel includes soaking the foamed nickel in 0.1 mol/l hydrochloric acid solution and ultrasonically treating for 30 minutes.

PN CN112795538-A
TI Co-culturing for activating dendritic cell (DC) in vitro by heat shock protein (HSP) glycoprotein (gp)96-polypeptide complex, includes culturing peripheral blood mononuclear cell in DC inducing medium, adding complex reagent and incubating.
AU WANG G
   FAN Z
   ZHENG Q
AE HEHONGSHANGYI CHENGDU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (HEHO-Non-standard)
GA 2021591084
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (a) preparing DC inducing culture medium, (b) culturing for 0 days, re-suspending prepared peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) using Roswell Park Memorial Institute (RPMI)1640 culture medium, spreading in a culture bottle and incubating in carbon dioxide incubator for 2 hours, (c) gently shaking the culture bottle to remove the suspended cell liquid, washing, adding the DC cell inducing culture medium into the culture bottle, continuously placing in the incubator, (d) culturing for 2 days, adding DC-III, DC-IV and DC-V to the culture bottle, continuously culturing in the incubator, (e) culturing for 3 days, collecting DC cell in a centrifugal tube, centrifuging, discarding supernatant, mixing and transferring to Eppendorf (RTM: Microcentrifuge Tubes) (EP) tube and re-suspending with DC cell culture medium, (f) adding HSP gp96-polypeptide complex reagent into the EP tube, incubating for 4 hours, and (g) washing and preserving.
   USE - The method is useful in co-culturing for activating DC cell in vitro by HSP gp96-polypeptide complex.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the DC cell in vitro activation efficiency, the ability of DC cells to uptake antigen, and efficiency of activating specific T cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The temperature of the incubator in the steps (b), (c), (d) and (f) is 37 degrees C, the carbon dioxide level is 5%. In the step (c), a pipette is used to draw RPMI1640 or phosphate buffered saline (PBS) buffer solution to gently wash the cell at the culture bottle wall while washing. In step (c), RPMI1640 culture liquid or physiological saline is used to wash for two times. In the step (e), the centrifuging process is carried out in a 600 g centrifuge for 5 minutes. In the step (g), 0.9% physiological saline is used to wash for 2 times. Preferred Components: In the step (a), the DC cell inducing culture medium comprises 45 ml DC culture medium, 5 ml DC culture medium additive, 15 mu l DC-I and 5 mu l DC-II. The culture bottle is T75 culture bottle.

PN CN112606443-A
TI Processing ultra-lightweight glass-mat-reinforced thermoplastic plate comprises adding polyethylene mixed with fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sodium sulfate and calcium carbonate, adding foamed beads into foaming agent and mixing.
AU JI H
   LI W
AE HEFEI JIEMAITE AUTOMOBILE NEW MATERIAL (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 202138361H
AB    NOVELTY - Processing ultra-lightweight glass-mat-reinforced thermoplastic (GMT) plate comprises (i) adding polyethylene mixed with fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sodium sulfate and calcium carbonate in a high-pressure reaction kettle after heating and increasing pressure, and filling with supercritical carbon dioxide for stirring and mixing, allowing to stand, releasing the pressure slowly to foam to obtain foamed beads; (2) mixing polypropylene resin, polybutadiene, azobisisobutyronitrile, cyclic anhydride type and bisphenol-A type cyanate ester, heating and increasing pressure, and stirring quickly to obtain modified polypropylene resin and performing spinneret treatment to obtain modified polypropylene fiber; (iii) opening and mixing the glass fiber and modified polypropylene fiber and preparing blended fiber mat by carding, laying, and needle punching; and (iv) adding foamed beads into the foaming agent and mixing, placing in a grinder to grind and crush to obtain mixed bead powder.
   USE - The method is useful for processing ultra-lightweight GMT plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method simplifies the processing steps, improves the heat preservation and strength of the GMT plate, reduces the density of the GMT plate, and improves the portability of the plate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (v) laying blended fiber felt flat, uniformly spraying mixed bead powder into the gaps on both sides of the blended fiber felt by an electrostatic adsorption jet machine to obtain a composite blended fiber felt; (vi) clamping composite blended fiber felt on both sides of the heating plate and heating to 80-100 degrees C, and allowing to stand for 10-15 minutes to obtain a pre-heated felt plate; (vii) heating the above-mentioned preheated felt plate to 130-170 degrees C, hot pressing using continuous roll pressing for 3-5 minutes, and placing in an oven at 160-180 degrees C to heat and baking for 4-8 minutes. In the step (i), the temperature of high-temperature and high-pressure foaming is 130-140 degrees C, the pressure is 16-18 MPa, and the rate of slow pressure relief is 0.2-0.6 Mpa/minute. In the step (ii), the modification temperature is 120-130 degrees C, the pressure is 12-16 MPa, the stirring speed is 1000-1200 rotations/minute, and the modification time is 30-40 minutes. The pressure of the rolling is 3-5 MPa. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of polyethylene, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sodium sulfate, and calcium carbonate is 20-24:1-2:1. In the step (ii), the mass ratio of polypropylene resin, polybutadiene azobisisobutyronitrile, cyclic anhydride type, and bisphenol A type cyanate ester is 30-35:2-6:1-1.8:2-2.6:1-1.2. The glass fiber and modified polypropylene fiber have a diameter of 10-30 mu m and a length of 70-80 mm. The mixing mass ratio of the glass fiber and the modified polypropylene fiber is 2-6:3-8. The mass ratio of the foamed beads and the foaming agent is 12-16:1-3. The foaming agent is ethane, propane, propylene, isobutane, butene, isobutylene, n-pentane, and isopentane and/or petroleum ether. The particle size of the foamed beads after grinding is 2-8 mu m.

PT P
PN CN112517023-A; CN112517023-B
TI Method for recovering and recycling of deactivated            non-noble metal vinyl chloride catalyst carrier,            involves performing microwave treatment on separated            activated carbon, heat-treating, grinding, loading, and            performing core treatment.
AU LI T
AE LI T (LITT-Individual)
GA 202131513F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recovering and recycling of                deactivated non-noble metal vinyl chloride catalyst                carrier involves performing impurity removal                treatment on deactivated non-noble metal vinyl                chloride catalyst and pretreating activated carbon                by performing microwave treatment on activated                carbon under nitrogen atmosphere at microwave                frequency of 22-26 MHz for 3-5 minutes,                heat-treating at 500-600 degrees C in mixed                atmosphere of nitrogen and carbon dioxide,                introducing hydrogen gas per cubic meter unit of                activated carbon at rate of 0.1 L/minute and heat                preserving for 3-5 hours, grinding, loading,                performing core treatment and formulating.
   USE - Method for recovering and recycling of                deactivated non-noble metal vinyl chloride catalyst                carrier.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively removes residual                organic matter and heavy metals in the catalyst,                and saves resources and costs consumed in the                preparation and activation of activated carbon, and                realizes sustainable resource reuse. The prepared                ozone catalyst has common oxygen demand removal                rate of 87.4% in the treatment of                high-concentration sewage with reduced dosage                amount of ozone catalyst by 23% compared with                existing catalyst, which effectively reduces the                cost of water treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves                  vacuum drying deactivated non-noble metal vinyl                  chloride catalyst, adding to airtight container,                  lowering pressure to -0.07 to 0.05 MPa, heating                  to 160 degrees C for 4 hours until water content                  of the non-precious metal activated carbon                  catalyst is less than 2% to obtain solid, adding                  the solid to 1.5-2 times the volume of treatment                  liquid, pickling at 40-45 degrees C for 20-30                  minutes under continuous stirring condition to                  ensure that the catalyst and the mixed acid                  treatment liquid are fully mixed, and filtering                  out solids, adding solid to 3-5 times the volume                  of 20% sodium hydroxide solution, soaking for                  0.5-1 hour, filtering out, washing with deionized                  water until pH of solid is 6-8, and drying at                  300-400 degrees C for 6-8 hours. The loading                  process involves adding ground 250-300 mesh                  activated carbon to 1.2-1.5 times the volume of                  the active load liquid, soaking for 12-16 hours                  under stirring condition of 20-30 rpm, separating                  activated carbon, heating to 400 degrees C at                  rate of 5-10 degrees C/minute under vacuum                  condition for 2-3 hours, naturally cooling to                  room temperature to obtain activated carbon                  loaded with active ingredient. The core treatment                  involves mixing potassium feldspar powder and                  diatomaceous earth and then mixing with                  carboxymethyl cellulose, grinding to particle                  size of 4-5 mm, calcining particles at 500-600                  degrees C for 3-4 hours to obtain core component,                  spraying mixed spray liquid on the surface of                  core component. The formulation process involves                  spraying activated carbon loaded with active                  ingredients on surface of the core component to                  thickness of 0.3-0.5 mm by electrostatic spraying                  process, placing portion of core component                  sprayed with activated carbon on surface in 30-35                  degrees C environment, leaving still for 1-2                  hours, molding remaining core component sprayed                  with activated carbon at 120-130 degrees C for                  30-50 minutes to obtain ozone catalyst,                  subjecting 77-80 mg/mL ozone catalyst to sewage                  treatment under room temperature and normal                  pressure condition with hydraulic retention time                  of 30 minutes until the common oxygen demand                  removal rate reaches 85.1-84.7% Preferred                  Component: The treatment liquid is mixture of                  10-15% nitric acid and 17-20% phosphoric acid in                  weight ratio of 1:1-2. The active load liquid                  comprises manganese nitrate, sodium cobalt                  nitrite, and copper nitrate in molar ratio of                  3:1:3, and deionized water. Preferred                  Composition: The active load liquid comprises                  20-25 %mass deionized water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The mixed spray liquid                  is mixture of polyvinyl alcohol, titanium dioxide                  particles, absolute ethanol, and deionized water.                  Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of                  potassium feldspar powder, diatomaceous earth and                  carboxymethyl cellulose is 5:10:2. The crankshaft adopts non-quenched and tempered steel for forging and machining, and is obtained by carbonitriding by maintaining the crankshaft in a mixed gas of ammonia and RX and subsequent water quenching treatment. The RX gas is 0.5% carbon dioxide, 41% hydrogen and 38.5% nitrogen.
   USE - Used as a high-strength crankshaft of engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The low carbon content in the crankshaft is easy to straighten the crankshaft blank; reduces the waste product rate of the crankshaft blank; the high manganese content improves the strength of the crankshaft material; the rare metals molybdenum and vanadium are not added, so the cost of the steel is reduced; and the bending and torsional strength of the crank shaft are greatly improved by the strengthening way of carbo-nitriding (soft nitriding) water quenching.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a preparation method of high-strength crankshaft of engine

The set of components are arranged in transport packaging that are closed on all sides. The portion of the standard electrolyte contained in the container is mixed with a portion of the acid additive contained in the one container in order to produce a strongly acidic electrolyte with a pH in the range from 1.1 to 1.5. The electrolyte space of the amperometric measuring cell an integer proportion of the strongly acidic electrolyte produced or the entire amount of the strongly acidic electrolyte produced. The chlorine dioxide sensor is calibrated within a maximum of 24 hours after the strongly acidic electrolyte has been generated and filled into the electrolyte compartment of the amperometric measuring cell. PN CN112390869-A
TI Extracting and refining high-purity kidney bean agglutinin used as raw material for food and medicine comprises e.g. introducing kidney beans into soaking and cleaning device, injecting purified water, flooding kidney beans, and processing.
AU HE S
   MA Y
   LI X
   SUN H
   LI J
   ZHANG H
AE UNIV HEFEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHE-C)
GA 202121245Y
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting and refining high-purity kidney bean agglutinin comprises (i) introducing kidney beans into soaking and cleaning device, injecting purified water at 15-35 degrees C into soaking and cleaning device, flooding kidney beans with purified water, processing for 30-45 minutes, entering water inlet into water at constant rate during cleaning process, discharging water at constant rate from water outlet, where inlet water flow rate is 100-200 times mass of kidney beans per hour, and starting ultrasonic treatment once for every 5-7 minutes during cleaning process, (ii) removing kidney beans obtained in step (i) from the surface moisture, using vacuum gauge pressure under -0.09 to -0.10 Mpa, drying at 35-40 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, introducing dried kidney beans into ball mill, and grinding under protection of nitrogen until fineness of material is less than 60 meshes to obtain kidney bean powder, and (iii) introducing into extraction kettle of carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device.
   USE - The agglutinin is used as a raw material for food and medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The agglutinin: has high purity, and excellent market prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises extracting and removing impurity, and collecting the substances left after extracting, (iv) mixing the substance with the neutral pH buffer solution according to the mass ratio of 1:8-12, stirring the mixed materials at 60-120 revolutions/minutes (rpm) for 4-6 hours, centrifuging the mixture at 3000-4000 rpm for 5-10 minutes, and collecting the kidney bean protein crude extract as supernatant, (v) adding acid solution to the crude kidney bean protein extract, continuously stirring, adjusting the pH value of the kidney bean protein crude extract to be 3.4-3.8, allowing to stand for 30-45 minutes, centrifuging the solution at 1000-2000 rpm for 5-10 minutes, and collecting the supernatant, (vi) adding 0.75-1.5 wt.% activated carbon to the supernatant obtained in the step (v), stirring for 90-180 seconds, allowing to stand for 30-45 minutes, centrifuging the static material at 1000-2000 rpm for 5-10 minutes, and collecting the supernatant, and (vii) using sephacryls-200 molecular sieve gel filtration column to analyze the supernatant obtained in the step (vi), and collecting kidney bean agglutinin as chromatographically purified materials. In the step (i), the ultrasonic frequency is 25-35 KHz, the power density is 0.25-0.50 W/cm2, and the processing each time is carried out for 1-1.5 minutes. The extracting and removing impurity are carried out by using 2 consecutive supercritical extraction operations including performing first extraction at 37 plus minus 0.3 degrees C under 25-30 Mpa for 15-30 minutes, and second extraction at 45 plus minus 0.3 degrees C under 28-33 Mpa for 15-30 minutes. Preferred Components: The neutral pH buffer solution comprises pH value 7.2 phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) buffer solution or a Tris-hydrochloric acid buffer solution. The ionic strength of the neutral pH buffer solution is 10-20 mM. The acid solution comprises citric acid, acetic acid, and/or phosphoric acid. The activated carbon comprises dry food activated carbon with a specific surface area of 10-20 m2/g. The operation of column chromatography with sephacryls-200 molecular sieve gel filtration comprises balancing the gel chromatography column by using pH value 7.2 PBS buffer solution system, sample loading of no more than 15% of the column volume, continuing to elute with pH value 7.2 PBS buffer solution at 0.8 ml/minute, collecting the first peak area sample, and freezing and drying to obtain high-purity kidney bean agglutinin.

PT P
PN CN112194872-A
TI Composite material comprises highly flame-retardant recycled polystyrene, high wear-resistant ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, graphene nanosheets, antioxidant, and compatibilizer.
AU YANG C
   DONG H
   YANG X
AE ANHUI HUANJIA TIANYI RENEWABLE RESOURCES (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202105868R
AB    NOVELTY - A composite material comprises 35-55 pts. wt. highly flame-retardant recycled polystyrene, 20-30 pts. wt. high wear-resistant ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, 3-5 pts. wt. graphene nanosheets, 1-3 pts. wt. antioxidant, and 1-3 pts. wt. compatibilizer. The composite material is prepared by: (1) adding highly flame-retardant recycle polystyrene, high wear-resistant ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, graphene nanosheets and compatibilizer to a mixer, mixing at 450-480 rpm for 30 minutes to obtain a mixture, adding the mixture to a twin-screw extruder, extruding and pelletizing at 160-170 degrees C to obtain particles; and (2) adding the particles to the extruder, adding antioxidant, extruding through die at a linear speed of 1000 mm/minute at 170-180 degrees C, cooling, and molding.
   USE - Composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material uses highly flame-retardant recycled polystyrene prepared through blending and hot pressing. When the recycled polystyrene is burned, the phosphorus and nitrogen compounds can generate carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, ammonia and other gases to prevent combustion, so that the high residual carbon content can slow down the burning rate and further improve the flame retardant performance of the recycled polystyrene.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the composite materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The antioxidant is antioxidant 1010 (pentaerythritol tetrakis(3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate)) and/or antioxidant 168 (tris(2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl) phosphite). The weight ratio of ethanolamine, polymethanol, tetrahydrofuran and dimethyl phosphite is 1:2:(2.5-3):3. The weight ratio of the product (B), triethylamine and acryloyl chloride is 2:1:(1.2-1.5).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The compatibilizer is chlorinated polypropylene and/or polypropylene grafted with carboxylic acid. Preferred Method: The preparation method of the highly flame-retardant recycled polystyrene involves: (S1) adding ethanolamine, polymethanol and tetrahydrofuran to a four-necked flask, heating in a water bath at 55-65 degrees C, stirring at a constant speed of 100-120 rpm for 4 hours, then dripping dimethyl phosphate for 15 minutes, heating at 70 degrees C, stirring at uniform speed, reacting for 8 hours, removing the solvent using a rotary evaporator to obtain a product (A), adding the product (A) to chloroform, stirring for 15 minutes, washing 3 times with 0.1 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution, and removing the chloroform by rotary evaporator to prepare a product (B); (S2) adding the product (B) to a three-necked flask containing chloroform, stirring for 10 minutes, then adding triethylamine, uniformly mixing, leaving still at -5 degrees C to -0 degrees C environment, adding acryloyl chloride for 20 minutes, stirring at 180-200 rpm, continuously reacting for 10 hours, heating at 5-10 degrees C, stirring and reacting for 10 hours, filtering, washing the filtrate 3 times with 0.1 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution and deionized water, drying, filtering, and rotary steaming to obtain a product (C); and (S3) adding the recycled polystyrene and the product (C) to an internal mixer, mixing at 150-170 degrees C for 5 minutes, transferring the mixed mixture to a vulcanizer, and hot pressing at 15 MPa and 150 degrees C for 10 minutes. The preparation method of the wear-resistant ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer involves: (S11) adding maleic anhydride, dicumyl peroxide and styrene to a reaction kettle filled with acetone, stirring at a constant speed of 120-150 rpm until completely dissolved, adding ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, heating and stirring in a water bath at 55-60 degrees C for 30 minutes, transferring the reactor to an oven at 80 degrees C, and drying until the acetone is completely volatilized to prepare a treated ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, where the weight ratio of maleic anhydride, dicumyl peroxide, styrene and ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer is 1:0.5:1:(8-10); (S12) adding magnesium nitrate and aluminum nitrate to deionized water, stirring at 100 rpm for 15 minutes, then dripping 10% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution for 5 minutes, heating in a water bath at 35-40 degrees C, continuously stirring for 15 minutes, transferring to a three-necked flask, introducing nitrogen gas, stirring at 400 rpm, heating at 70 degrees C, crystallizing for 22 hours, suction filtering, washing 3 times with 45 degrees C deionized water, and vacuum drying at 100 degrees C for 4 hours to prepared a filler, where the weight ratio of magnesium nitrate, aluminum nitrate and sodium hydroxide is 2:1:1; and (S13) adding the filler and the treated ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer in a weight ratio of (0.1-0.3):1 to the internal mixer, uniformly mixing, taking out the mixture when the current of the internal mixer reaches a steady state, then melting, plasticizing and extruding through an extruder.
DC A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.)
MC A04-C02B; A04-C04A; A04-F05; A04-F09; A04-G08; A07-A02C; A08-A06; A08-M10A; A08-R03; A11-A03; A11-B01

PN CN112078051-A; CN112078051-B
TI Environment-friendly foaming EPP production            method, involves preparing modified masterbatch by            using a double screw, injecting cooling water and            cooling airflow into reaction kettle for cooling, and            obtaining EPP foaming particles.
AU DONG X
   SHEN X
AE SUZHOU QINLIAN PLASTIC PROD CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2020C7990N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing modified                masterbatch by using a double screw. The                masterbatch is put into the reaction kettle. The                heated carbon dioxide and high-temperature steam                are introduced into the reaction kettle for heating                the masterbatch to prepare the foaming particle.                The heat preservation treatment is performed to the                reaction kettle. The cooling water and cooling                airflow are injected into the reaction kettle for                cooling. The masterbatch foaming in the reaction                kettle is dried using dryer to obtain new EPP                foaming particles.
   USE - Environment-friendly foaming Expanded                polypropylene (EPP) production process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can realize the stage heating and                stage cooling of the plastic masterbatch in the                reaction kettle. The stage heating avoids the crack                caused by the partial heating of the masterbatch,                reduces the stress in the masterbatch. The heat                carried by the heating plastic masterbatch can be                reused after discharging the airflow for the                heating plastic masterbatch. The airflow can be                used for pre-heating the airflow and liquid for                cooling the plastic masterbatch in the discharging                process, realizing the heat recycling, and reducing                the energy waste of the device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the method.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

AU MA M
   MA X

GA 2020C6556R
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a primary ionization materializing tower, a secondary hydrolysis acidification tower and an integrated circuit (IC) anaerobic tower. The one-stage ionization materializing tower can be added with ion removing agent to realize solid-liquid separation of waste water to remove or recycle fine fiber (SS) from the bottom of materializing tower. The secondary hydrolysis acidification tower firstly can make materialization of waste water. The acidification treatment, the pH value of the waste water is adjusted to 6.8-7.5 to obtain neutralization, then adding coagulant to make the colloid and fine suspension of the waste water into flocculating body, then removing the flocculating body by separating. The IC anaerobic tower add anaerobic bacteria to decompose the organic matter into small molecule. The methane bacteria is added to decompose the low molecular organic acid and lower alcohol into methane, carbon dioxide, insoluble organic matter and pigment and so on.
   USE - Fourth generation sewage anaerobic biological treatment system.
   ADVANTAGE - The operation cost of the processing technique is low. The waste water and sludge treatment process is reliable and the operation is stable.

PN CN111995103-A
TI Processing of complex cyanide-containing wastewater generated by emergent environmental incidents comprises processing segment, using sodium hydroxide solution, lime or lime milk, and adding polyferric solution, and flocculating.
AU KANG P
   LIANG X
   WANG S
   WU X
   XU S
AE COUNTRIES RING HAZARDOUS WASTE DISPOSAL (COUN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C1197N
AB    NOVELTY - Processing of complex cyanide-containing wastewater generated by emergent environmental incidents comprises (i) processing segment, (ii) adopting two-step alkaline chlorine oxidation process for second stage including using sodium hydroxide solution, lime or lime milk for first oxidation stage to control pH value at 10-11, slowly adding calcium hypochlorite oxidizer to cyanide-containing wastewater, controlling oxidation reduction potential (ORP) between 350-450 mv, carrying out reaction for 30 minutes, carrying out second step of oxidation on basis of previous step, adding calcium hypochlorite oxidizer, improving oxidation, controlling ORP between 45-650 mv, and pH value of wastewater between 8-8.5, and carrying out reaction for 30 minutes, (iii) adding 30% polyferric solution (commercially available) to waste liquid for third stage, flocculating and precipitating, and separating solid-liquid, and (iv) precipitating ferrous sulfate sodium sulfite for fourth stage.
   USE - The method is useful for processing of complex cyanide-containing wastewater generated by emergent environmental incidents.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical, environmental friendly, and safe to operate; has stable treatment effect, and low requirements on treatment facilities; removes solid impurities; completely destroys the carbon-nitrogen; and completely decomposes the cyanide in the wastewater into carbon dioxide and nitrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the segment is processed by using solid-liquid separation for first stage, and separating solid debris from liquid wastewater to make cyanide wastewater clear. In the step (iv) ferrous sulfate sodium sulfite is precipitated for fourth stage by adding the reducing agent ferrous sulfate in the waste liquid treated in the step (iii), keeping the reaction process always under the condition of pH value 10-11 for 30 minutes, adding the reducing agent sodium sulfite solution, strengthening the reducing atmosphere of the reaction, adding ferrous sulfate again, adjusting the pH value to be closing to neutral, and promoting the end point of the reaction to tend to ferrous iron blue precipitate.
