PN CN111952556-A; CN111952556-B
TI Preparing wood-derived carbon-based material            modified by Co4N nanosheet array comprises e.g.            pre-carbonizing natural wood chips, activating            pre-carbonized wood chips, carbonizing activated            material, and nitriding.
AU DU L
   ZHANG W
   LI S
   SONG H
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2020B74447
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing wood-derived carbon-based material                modified by Co4N nanosheet array comprises (i)                pre-carbonizing natural wood chips at 250-500                degrees C to obtain pre-carbonized wood chips, (ii)                activating pre-carbonized wood chips at 600-800                degrees C under carbon dioxide atmosphere for 4-8                hours to obtain activated materials, (iii)                carbonizing activated material under an inert                atmosphere at 1000-1400 degrees C for 2-6 hours,                and processing to obtain carbon-based material                derived from natural wood, (iv) using carbon-based                material as the working electrode, and aqueous                solution of cobalt salt as the electrolyte,                constant current electrodeposition, to obtain                Co(OH)2 modified natural wood-derived carbon-based                material named Co(OH)2/WCP, and (v) nitriding                Co(OH)2/WCP at 540-580 degrees C in ammonia                atmosphere.
   USE - The method is useful in preparing wood-derived                carbon-based material modified by Co4N nanosheet                array.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                wood-derived carbon-based material modified by Co4N                nano-sheet array obtained by above mentioned                method; #lithium sulfur battery anode, prepared by                the wood derived carbon-based material modified by                Co4N nano-sheet array and elemental sulfur; and                #lithium sulfur battery, comprising lithium sulfur                battery anode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (iv), the                  concentration of the transition metal ion in the                  aqueous solution of cobalt salt is 0.01-50 mol/l,                  and in the step (iv), the cobalt salt is cobalt                  nitrate, cobalt sulfate, and/or cobalt chloride.                  Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the constant                  current deposition condition: the current is                  1-100 mA cm2, and the time of electrodepositing                  is 50-150 seconds. In the step (v), the time of                  nitriding treatment is 1-6 hours, in the step                  (i), the time of pre-carbonization is 2-6 hours,                  in the step (i), the heating rate of the                  pre-carbonization is 5-10 degrees C/minute, in                  the step (ii), the heating rate of activation is                  5-10 degrees C/minute, in the step (iii), the                  heating rate of carbonization is 5-10 degrees                  C/minute, and in the step (v), the heating rate                  of nitriding is 1-10 degrees C/minute. In the                  step (iii), the subsequent treatment means that                  the surface is polished smoothly and cleaned with                  water and ethanol, and in the step (iv), the                  carbon-based material is ultrasonically treated                  in an aqueous solution of cobalt salt before the                  electrodeposition of the carbon-based material.                  The method comprises heating the wood-derived                  carbon-based material modified with the Co4N                  nanosheet array and elemental sulfur to obtain a                  lithium-sulfur battery anode. The relationship                  between the amount of elemental sulfur and the                  amount of wood-derived carbon-based material                  modified by the Co4N nanosheet array satisfies                  that the plane of the wood-derived carbon-based                  material modified by the Co4N nanosheet array is                  covered with 0.1-20 mg of elemental sulfur, and                  the temperature of the heat treatment is 150-170                  degrees C, and the time of the heat treatment is                  3-24 hours.

TI Depositing material onto surface of substrate involves e.g. providing substrate within reaction chamber, exposing surface of substrate to precursor, removing portion of adsorbed species and leaving residual species on surface.
AU UEDA S
AE ASM IP HOLDING BV (ASMI-C)
   ASM IP HOLDING BV (ASMI-C)
GA 2020B09073
AB    NOVELTY - Depositing material onto a surface of a substrate comprises: providing a substrate within a reaction chamber; exposing a surface of the substrate to a precursor, where the precursor reacts with species on the surface to form adsorbed species; removing a portion of the adsorbed species, leaving residual species on the surface; and providing a reactant to the reaction chamber, where the reactant reacts with the residual species to form the material. The sputtering comprises using an activated species formed from an inert gas, where the inert gas comprises at least one gas of the group consisting of argon, helium, neon, krypton and xenon. The reactant comprises at least one of oxygen, ozone, carbon dioxide, nitrous oxide, nitrogen, ammonia, hydrogen, methane and other hydrocarbons.
   USE - The method is useful for depositing material onto surface of substrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes a portion of the adsorbed precursor that slows the deposition rate and increases conformality of material deposited using plasma-enhanced-atomic layer deposition, preferably in high-aspect ratio features.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) depositing material onto a surface of a substrate;
   (2) a deposition apparatus configured to perform the method; and
   (3) a device structure.

AU TANG Y

AE TANG Y (TANG-Individual)
GA 202066639H
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an LNG vaporizer and an intermediate medium vaporizer. The LNG vaporizer circulates natural gas to the intermediate medium vaporizer through a circulating pump. The intermediate medium vaporizer adds the cold energy in the natural gas to an intermediate medium. The intermediate medium passes through a heat exchanger to process the natural gas into high-pressure normal temperature gas and then is sent to an intermediate medium turbine for expansion to drive the engine to generate electricity. The intermediate medium is one of water, freon, carbon dioxide, methanol or ethylene glycol.

   ADVANTAGE - The device has a simple structure and uses the characteristics of low-temperature cold energy of LNG to liquefy the working fluid, and then vaporize the working fluid, so that it can perform expansion work in the steam turbine, thus generating kinetic energy and converting it into electrical energy by a generator, optimize the use of energy, save resources, and has a strong market application prospect.

PN CN111039728-A
TI Preparing sludge-based activated carbon slow-release fertilizer involves adding biomass carburizer to one or more mixed activator solutions of potassium hydroxide, potassium carbonate, potassium chloride or phosphoric acid.
AU LUO Z
   YU L
   LI B
   KUANG P
   HONG J
AE GEZHOU DAM ZHONGGU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GEZH-Non-standard)
GA 2020346325
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sludge-based activated carbon slow-release fertilizer involves adding biomass carburizer to one or more mixed activator solutions of potassium hydroxide (KOH), potassium carbonate (K2CO3), potassium chloride (KCl) or phosphoric acid (H3PO4), and stirring uniformly and fully immersing, and getting activated carbonizer after dehydration. The activated carburizing agent is added to the original sludge, and fully stirred to make the activated carburizing agent uniformly distributed in the sludge to prepare modified sludge. The modified sludge mentioned is heat dried to a constant weight to prepare a composite precursor. The composite precursor is subjected to anaerobic heating carbonization to obtain sludge-based activated carbon coarse material under protective gas carbon dioxide (CO2) atmosphere. The sludge-based activated carbon coarse material is close to neutral, and the sludge-based activated carbon slow-release fertilizer is obtained after drying.
   USE - Method for preparing sludge-based activated carbon slow-release fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare sludge-based activated carbon slow-release fertilizer, that improves the problems of high sludge ash content, small pore volume, small specific surface, large seasonal quality fluctuation, and relatively low land use effect and the like.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The biomass recarburizer is one or more mixed powders of agricultural and forestry wastes such as straw, rice husks, peanut husks, wood chips, branches, and fallen leaves. Preferred Conditions: The concentration sum of the nutrient element of the activator solution is 1-5 mol/L, and the immersion time is not less than 3 hours. The absolute mass ratio of the activated carburizer to the original sludge is 0.3-0.5:1. The heating rate is 10-30 degrees C/minute, the carbonization temperature is 700-800 degrees C, and the carbonization time is 1-2 hours after the temperature is stable during the carbonization of sludge. The original sludge is the remaining sludge of the urban sewage treatment plant, and the water content is about 80 wt.%.

   MO Y
AE SICHUAN GOLDEN-ELEPHANT SINCERITY CHEM (SICH-Non-standard)

GA 202016289R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves purifying and decomposing a mixture of melamine tail gas and water to obtain purified melamine tail gas. The purification and decomposition are performed under the catalytic action of a catalyst. The catalyst includes a cobalt-based catalyst, an iron-based catalyst, and a titanium-based catalyst. The pressure of the melamine tail gas, after purification and decomposition is 0.2-1 Mpa. The purification and decomposition is performed in a fixed bed reactor. The melamine tail gas and water mixture is preheated before performing the heating. The temperature of the pre-heated melamine tail gas and water mixture is 150-280 degrees C.
   USE - Melamine exhaust gas purification method used in chemical industry.

PN CN110732221-A; CN110732221-B
TI Coating waste gas treatment process comprises e.g. introducing mixed waste gas in the coating chamber into the gas collection container via suction pipe, introducing organic waste gas into adsorption container, and performing desorption.
AU TANG W
   TANG Q
   GAO X
   CHEN P
   XU H
AE YANCHENG YINGKAI COATING EQUIP CO LTD (YANC-Non-standard)
GA 2020113415
AB    NOVELTY - Coating waste gas treatment process comprises (a) setting multiple air suction ducts on the top of the coating chamber, introducing the mixed waste gas in the coating chamber into the gas collection container through suction pipe, and introducing mixed waste gas into the dry defogging machine through the filtering pipe to filter to obtain the organic waste gas, (b) introducing discharged organic waste gas into the adsorption container through an adsorption fan, absorbing by activated carbon containing absorption device; (c) when the activated carbon adsorption device is in the saturated state, closing the organic gas leading-in channel and performing desorption of activated carbon containing absorption device; and (d) compressing organic waste gas desorbed from the activated carbon containing adsorption device and introducing the catalytic combustion device, and converting the water and carbon dioxide by the catalyst and discharging into the atmosphere.
   USE - The method is useful for coating waste gas treatment process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has good filtering effect; is carried out without blockage and failure; is economical; and has low energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a coating waste gas processing system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the desorption container.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The process flow of activated carbon containing adsorption device for desorption comprises (i) discharging nitrogen used for desorption from the nitrogen source and preheating, and maintaining the temperature of nitrogen at 90-250 degrees C, (ii) adjusting the gas pressure in the desorption container by controlling the introduction and export of the nitrogen from the desorption container, increasing the air pressure in the desorption container to 200-350 kPa and placing statically for 5-10 minutes, reducing the air pressure in the desorption container to 50-90 kPa, annd introducing desorbed organic waste gas in the activated carbon containing adsorption device under the air pressure environment of 50-90 kPa, and (iii) treating mixed gas containing organic waste gas and nitrogen gas for separation process after desorption, and separating organic waste gas and nitrogen. In between the steps (a) and (b), performing coat mist leveling treatment process comprising introducing organic waste gas filtered by the dry demister integrated machine into the spiral fan duct to form a spiral airflow and then introducing into the electromagnetic defogging container for complete defogging, setting mist container bottom with granular flocculant feeding device and a water mist spraying device, carrying water mist under the electromagnetic action by the granular flocculant at the bottom to the top of the electromagnetic demister container to form a misty water curtain, and passing organic waste gas through the misty water curtain in the form of a spiral airflow. The granular flocculant comprises 12-18 wt.% aluminum chloride, 5-11 wt.% polyacrylamide, 16-25 wt.% ferrous sulfate, 13-22 wt.% sodium silicate, and 30-40 wt.% magnetic powder.

PN WO2020020691-A1; CA3103062-A1; EP3827114-A1; US2021162390-A1; US11173477-B2
TI Electrocatalyst for reduction of carbon dioxide to            produce multi-carbon composite, comprises multi-metal            material comprising primary metal being copper and one            enhancer metal selected from germanium, gallium, tin,            silicon, and silver.
AU ZHONG M
   WANG C
   MIN Y
   IP A
   SARGENT E
AE UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
   TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
GA 202009586B
AB    NOVELTY - Electrocatalyst comprises a multi-metal                material comprising a primary metal being copper                and one enhancer metal selected from germanium,                gallium, tin, silicon, silver, gold, zinc and                aluminium.
   USE - Electrocatalyst used for electrocatalytic                reduction of CO2 into one multi-carbon compound and                one multi-carbon compound comprises ethylene                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a process for electrochemical production                of a multi-carbon compound from CO2, which involves                contacting CO2 gas and an electrolyte with an                electrode comprising the electrocatalyst, and                applying a voltage to provide a current density to                cause the CO2 gas contacting the electrocatalyst to                be electrochemically converted into the                multi-carbon compound, and ecovering the                multi-carbon compound;
   (2) a system for CO2 electroreduction to                produce multi-carbon hydrocarbons, which involves                electrolytic cell configured to receive a liquid                electrolyte and CO2 gas, an anode, a cathode                comprising an electrocatalyst, and voltage source                to provide a current density to cause the CO2 gas                contacting the electrocatalyst to be                electrochemically converted into the multi-carbon                hydrocarbon, which is optionally ethylene or an                alcohol; and
   (3) a method for manufacturing the                electrocatalyst, which involves vapour-phase                deposition followed by chemical etching or                ion-implanting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The electrocatalyst                  comprises a multi-metal Cu-Al material, and                  de-alloyed material, where multi- metal Cu-Al                  material comprises from 1 wt.% to 50 wt.%                  aluminum (Al) based on the total weight of the                  multi-metal Cu-Al material, preferably from 3-30                  wt.% and /or 50-99 wt.% copper (Cu) based on the                  total weight of the multi-metal Cu-Al material,                  preferably from 70-97 wt.% Cu. The multi- metal                  Cu-Al material is formed as a catalytic layer.                  The electrocatalyst comprises a gas diffusion                  membrane, and/or the catalytic layer has a                  thickness between 10 nm and 5000 nm as determined                  by cross-sectional scanning electron microscopy.                  The multi-metal Cu-Al material comprises                  Al-terminated pores or Al is ion-implanted into                  the Cu. The Al is present in the Cu as Al that is                  evaporated into the Cu and remains after                  chemically etching. The multi- metal Cu-Al                  material comprises a surface region having a                  thickness between 1 nm and 3 nm as determined by                  Auger electron spectroscopy. The surface region                  comprises an Al content of 1-50 wt.% and a Cu                  content of 50-99 wt.%. The surface region is                  nanoporous, the surface region has pores that                  have pore diameters ranging from 1-100 nanometer                  as determined by HRTEM and HAADF analysis, or                  from 5-20 nanometer. The surface region has a                  homogeneous distribution of Al and Cu, and/or the                  surface region consists of Cu and Al. The surface                  region is Cu. The electrocatalytic reduction of                  CO2 is performed in an alkaline medium comprises                  potassium hydroxide. The electrocatalytic                  reduction of CO2 is performed in a                  three-electrode flow-cell. The current density                  provided in the current collection structure is                  predetermined for selective electrochemical                  conversion of the CO2 into a target multi-carbon                  compound. The target multi-carbon compound is                  ethylene or an alcohol, and/or in that the                  electrolyte comprises an alkaline compound. The                  electrolyte comprises KOH and/or other alkaline                  solutions. A three-electrode flow-cell is                  adopted. An electrocatalyst for reduction of CO2                  to produce multi- carbon composite is                  manufactured by producing a multi-metal material                  comprising Cu and Al and subjecting the                  multi-metal material to chemical etching to                  remove a portion of the Al from a surface region                  of the metallic alloy to produce a de-alloyed                  Cu-Al material. The vapour-phase deposition                  comprises evaporating solid Al and forming a                  deposited Al layer on the Cu surface to form an                  evaporated Al-on-Cu material. The deposited Al                  layer has a thickness of ranging from 50-150                  nanometer as determined by cross-sectional                  scanning electron microscopy. The evaporation of                  the solid Al is performed at an evaporation rate                  ranging from 1-10 nanometer per second, and/or                  under a base pressure ranging from 105-108 Torr                  (133.32x 105-133.32x 108 Pascals). The chemical                  etching comprises immersing the evaporated                  Al-on-Cu material into an acidic solution to                  remove Al. The acidic solution comprises                  hydrochloric acid solution. The hydrochloric acid                  solution comprises from 1-20 wt.% hydrochloric                  acid based on the total weight of the                  hydrochloric acid solution. The Cu is deposited                  on a gas diffusion layer before producing the                  metallic alloy comprising the Cu and Al. The Cu                  is deposited by thermal evaporation onto the gas                  diffusion layer. The Cu forms a deposited Cu                  layer on the gas diffusion layer, where deposited                  Cu layer has a thickness of about 400-600                  nanometer as determined by cross-sectional                  scanning electron microscopy. A multi-metal                  material is produced by co-evaporating Al and Cu                  to form a Cu-Al alloy. The multi-metal material                  comprising Cu and Al form a layer having a                  thickness ranging from 200-1000 nanometer as                  determined by cross-sectional scanning electron                  microscopy. The chemical etching comprises                  immersing the Cu-Al alloy in an acidic solution                  to remove Al. The acidic solution comprises                  hydrochloric acid solution. An electrocatalyst                  for reduction of CO2 to produce multi- carbon                  composite is manufactured by ion-implanting Al                  into Cu.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

TI Brewing beer self-service vending machine, has vending machine main body connected with hinge top cover, lower connecting foot and beer outlet, and vending machine main body whose inner side is provided with tank cover sealing device.
AU CAI Y
AE CAI Y (CAIY-Individual)
   TONGLING TONGFENG PARK DEV CO LTD (TONG-Non-standard)
GA 202010438V
AB    NOVELTY - The machine has a vending machine main body (4) connected with a hinge top cover (5), a lower connecting foot (3) and a beer outlet (2). An inner side of the vending machine main body is provided with a tank cover sealing device, a brewing beer bucket, a wine receiving device, a linear motor assembly, an electric control telescopic wine outlet valve and a wine stacking device. The brewing beer bucket is fixed with the tank cover sealing device and the wine stacking device. The wine receiving device is connected with the linear motor assembly. The brewing beer bucket is equipped with the electric control telescopic wine outlet valve.
   USE - Brewing beer self-service vending machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The machine reduces dissolved air so as to avoid loss of carbon dioxide, thus reducing generation of foam to prevent foam overflow into tank, and hence improving sales volume of brewing beer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of a brewing beer self-service vending machine.

PT P
PN US2020028194-A1; US11139500-B2
TI Anode used in alcohol based biofuel cell, comprises electrode substrate, first region of substrate having catalyst composition that includes inorganic or metallic catalyst, and second region of substrate having enzyme composition.
AU ARUGULA M
   PINCHON E W
   SINGHAL S
AE CFD RES CORP (CFDR-Non-standard)
GA 202008815N
AB    NOVELTY - Anode comprises an electrode substrate, a first region of the substrate having a catalyst composition that includes an inorganic or metallic catalyst. The second region of the substrate having an enzyme composition. The combination of the catalyst composition and enzyme composition are converted a fuel reagent to carbon dioxide at neutral pH. The first region and second region are separate regions.
   USE - Anode used in alcohol based biofuel cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The anode is used to improves the overall performance of the biofuel cells with incorporation of hybrid nanostructures, utilizes a single enzyme system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for following:
   (1) a fuel cell, which comprises the anode, a cathode and a neutral fuel liquid in contact with the anode, and neutral fuel liquid having a neutral pH and the fuel reagent; and
   (2) a method for manufacturing method of making an anode, which involves forming an electrode substrate, contacting the electrode substrate with a hydroxide, preparing a catalyst composition, depositing the catalyst composition on a first portion of the electrode substrate, preparing an enzyme composition and depositing the enzyme composition on a second portion of the electrode substrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst of the catalyst composition includes an inorganic or metallic catalyst selected from vanadium oxide, titanium (III) chloride, Pd(OAc)2, MnO, zeolite, alumina, graphitic carbon, palladium, platinum, gold, ruthenium, rhodium, iridium, or combinations. The catalyst is in the form of a nanoparticle, nanorod, nanodot, or combination. The catalyst composition includes a conductive material mixed with the catalyst. The conductive material is selected from the group consisting of carbon black powder, carbon black, carbon powder, carbon fiber, single wall carbon nanotubes (SWCNT), multi-wall carbon nanotubes (MWCNT), double walled nanotubes, carbon nanotubes arrays, diamond-coated conductors, glassy carbon, mesoporous carbon, graphite, uncompressed graphite worms, delaminated purified flake graphite, high performance graphite, highly ordered pyrolytic graphite, pyrolytic graphite, polycrystalline graphite, and combinations. The catalyst composition includes a binder mixed with the catalyst and the conductive material. The binder polymer is selected from the group consisting of polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE), and polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF), nation, polyhexafluoropropylene, fluorinated ethylene-propylene copolymers (FEP), vinylidene fluoride-trifluorochloroethylene copolymer (PVDF-PCTFE), polyethylene, polypropylene, ethylene-propylene copolymer, or ethylene-propylene-diene (EPDM) rubbers, and combinations. The electrode substrate is selected from the group consisting of carbon felt, buckeye paper, toray paper, glassy carbon, carbon cloth, carbon paper, carbon screen printed electrodes, and combinations. The substrate has a surface and the catalyst composition is provided on a first portion of the surface and the enzyme composition is provided on a second portion of the surface. The enzyme composition includes an enzyme selected from the group consisting of oxalate decarboxylase (OxDc), oxalate oxidase (OxO), aldehyde dehydrogenase (ALDH), alcohol dehydrogenase (ADH), formate dehydrogenase, formaldehyde dehydrogenase, glucose dehydrogenase, lactic dehydrogenase and pyruvate dehydrogenase, and combinations. The catalyst composition includes magnesium oxide (MgO). The alkaline substance between the substrate and the catalyst composition. The enzyme composition includes ethylene glycol diglycidyl ether (EGDE) and/or a conductive material. The first substrate having a first catalyst composition region and a first enzyme composition region and a second substrate having a second catalyst composition region and a second enzyme composition region, where first substrate contacts the second substrate such that the first catalyst composition region contacts the second catalyst composition region and the first enzyme composition region contacts the second enzyme composition region. The neutral fuel liquid includes the fuel reagent selected from an alcohol, polyol, carbohydrate, or combination. The cathode includes a cathode enzyme. Preferred Process: The manufacturing method further involves cleaning the electrode substrate with plasma, rinsing the electrode substrate with isopropyl alcohol and water, conditioning the electrode substrate with a hydroxide, applying a load to the electrode substrate in the presence of the hydroxide, rinsing excess hydroxide from the electrode substrate, mixing the catalyst composition prior to the depositing and/or mixing the enzyme composition prior to the depositing.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PN CN110483831-A; CN110483831-B
TI Preparing metal organic framework aerogel involves taking inorganic salt of cobalt as precursor and anhydrous ethanol for preparing inorganic salt of cobalt - anhydrous ethanol solution, and adding propylene oxide to obtained solution.
AU PAN Y
   ZHI M
   HONG Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2019A03985
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a metal organic framework (MOF) aerogel involves taking inorganic salt of cobalt as a precursor and anhydrous ethanol as a solvent for preparing inorganic salt of cobalt-anhydrous ethanol solution, where concentration of cobalt salt is 0.01-1M. The propylene oxide is added to inorganic salt of cobalt-anhydrous ethanol solution, and mixed to obtain mixed solution, where concentration of propylene oxide is 0.1-10 vol.% of inorganic salt of cobalt-anhydrous ethanol solution. The propylene oxide is added to mixed solution to prepare a sol, where concentration of propylene oxide is 10-50 vol.% of mixed solution. The sol is put into a closed mold, and gel reaction is performed to obtain a precursor gel containing Co. The precursor gel is subjected to aging in methanol aging solution containing 2-methylimidazole to obtain aged gelm which is dried in supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain a MOF aerogel.
   USE - Method for preparing a metal organic framework aerogel used for various electrochemical reaction and adsorption reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a metal organic framework aerogel, which has easy availability of raw material, simple process, microporous and mesoporous grading pore structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The inorganic salt of cobalt comprises cobalt nitrate, cobalt chloride or cobalt acetate. Preferred Conditions: The gel reaction is performed in an environment of 20-80 degrees C. The aging is performed by using aging solution and precursor gel in a volume ratio of 4:1-1:1, and concentration of 2-methylimidazole in aging solution is 0.04-4M. The aging time is between 12-84 hours. The supercritical carbon dioxide drying process is performed by using carbon dioxide at a pressure of 8-20MPa and temperature of 35-60 degrees C for 20-240 minutes. The crystal structure is ZIF-67 structure, and specific surface area is between 500-1000m2/g. The microstructure of aerogel is MOF particle with a size between 10-200nm.

PN CN110452203-A; CN110452203-B
TI Preparing 1-oxo-1,3-dihydro-3-hydroxybenzofuran-5-carboxylic acid comprises dissolving 2,5-toluene dihalide in tetrahydrofuran, adding e.g. metal magnesium, acidifying, adding e.g. glacial acetic acid, extracting, washing and drying.
AU XI F
   DONG Z
   ZHAO S
   FAN J
   ZHANG J
AE YINZHOU TEACHERS COLLEGE (YINZ-Non-standard)
GA 201998013J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 1-oxo-1,3-dihydro-3-hydroxybenzofuran-5-carboxylic acid comprises (a) dissolving 2,5-toluene dihalide in tetrahydrofuran under the protection of a protective gas or a drying tube, adding metal magnesium and an initiator to the tetrahydrofuran solution and preparing a green reagent under the initiation of initiator, (b) introducing carbon dioxide into the green reagent obtained in step (a) and acidifying by adding hydrochloric acid to obtain 2-methylterephthalic acid and (3) adding 2-methyl terephthalic acid obtained in step (b) to mixed solution of glacial acetic acid, acetic anhydride and concentrated sulfuric acid, adding chromium trioxide under water bath conditions for oxidation, quenching the mixed solution, removing glacial acetic acid and acetic anhydride, extracting, washing, drying and concentrating to obtain 1-oxo-1,3-dihydro-3-hydroxybenzofuran-5-carboxylic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 1-oxo-1,3-dihydro-3-hydroxybenzofuran-5-carboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple synthesis and industrialization prospects; is cheap; utilizes easily obtained raw materials; produces product which requires only washing, extraction and filtration; convenient for large scale preparation; and improves economic benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The protective gas in step (a) is nitrogen or an inert gas. The 2,5-toluene dihalide is 2,5- toluene dichloride, 2,5- toluene bromide, and 2,5-toluene. The metal magnesium in step (a) is magnesium chip or magnesium powder and the ratio of metal magnesium to 2,5-toluene dihalide is 5-6:1. The ratio of glacial acetic acid, acetic anhydride, concentrated sulfuric acid and 2,5-toluene is 21:12.5:5:1. The ratio of chromium trioxide to the 2,5-toluene is 2-5:1. Preferred Method: The reaction in the step (a) is carried out at 50-65 degrees C for 2-8 hours. The step (c) comprises slowly adding chromium trioxide to mixed solution for oxidation at a reaction temperature of-5 degrees C for 4-24 hours and the temperature of the reaction liquid is 25 degrees C. The quenching comprises adding reactyion solution to the ice water, removing the glacial acetic acid and acetic anhydride by a method of washing organic phase with water, where the extraction solvent is ethyl acetate or dichloromethane and the washing process comprises washing with distilled water or deionized water and with saturated sodium chloride solution, the drying operation is carried out by anhydrous sodium sulfate, anhydrous magnesium sulfate or anhydrous calcium chloride.

PN CN110404534-A; WO2021000672-A1; CN110404534-B
TI High-efficiency anti-chlorine poisoning volatile            organic compound catalytic oxidation catalyst comprises            ruthenium(IV) oxide as active component and            tin-titanium oxide compound or metal oxide-tin-titanium            oxide compound as carrier.
AU GAO X
   ZHENG C
   LUO Z
   CEN K
   NI M
   YANG Y
   WU W
   SONG H
   SHI Z
   ZHOU J
   FANG M
   YU C
   WANG S
   CHENG L
   WANG Q
   ZHANG Y
   WENG W
   LI H
   ZHENG Z
   CENG K
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201994370X
AB    NOVELTY - High-efficiency anti-chlorine poisoning                volatile organic compound catalytic oxidation                catalyst comprises ruthenium(IV) oxide as active                component and tin-titanium oxide compound (I) or                metal oxide tin-titanium oxide compound (II) as                carrier.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for chlorine-containing                organic waste gas treatment in medicine, organic                synthesis, spray painting and other fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high catalytic activity for                chlorine-containing volatile organic compounds,                strong resistance to chlorine poisoning, high                product selectivity to carbon dioxide, and                excellent removal effect on ordinary volatile                organic compounds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-efficiency anti-chlorine poisoning                volatile organic compound catalytic oxidation                catalyst comprises ruthenium(IV) oxide as active                component and tin-titanium oxide compound of                formula (SnyTi1-yO2) (I) or metal                oxide-tin-titanium oxide compound of formula                (MOx-SnyTi1-yO2) (II) as carrier. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is also included for preparing the catalyst,                comprising: either (when (I) as a carrier),                preparing carrier (I) having rutile crystal form,                and then loading ruthenium(IV) oxide nanoparticles                by dip-coating method; or (when (II) as carrier),                and soaking (I) with acidic metal oxide (MOx), and                then loading ruthenium(IV) oxide nanoparticles                .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises:                  either (i) preparing mixed solution of tin source                  and titanium source, and precipitating with                  aqueous ammonia, (ii) washing the precipitate for                  several times, and drying at 110 degrees C for                  12-24 hours, (iii) calcining the precipitate at                  400-600 degrees C for 3-6 hours to obtain carrier                  (I), (iv) wet-impregnating (I) with active                  component ruthenium(IV) chloride, and spin-drying                  to obtain catalyst precursor, (v) washing for                  multiple times, and drying at 110 degrees C for                  12-24 hours, and (vi) calcining the precursor at                  400-600 degrees C for 3-6 hours to obtain                  ruthenium(IV) oxide/(I) catalyst; or (a) taking                  the carrier (I) obtained in step (iii), and                  wet-impregnating with acidic metal oxide                  precursor solution, and calcining to obtain metal                  oxide (MOx) heteropoly acid modified carrier                  (II), (ii) taking carrier (II) and repeating the                  steps (iv)-(vi) to obtain heteropoly                  acid-modified ruthenium(IV) oxide/(II) catalyst.                  Preferred Components: (II) is mixed metal oxide                  prepared by loading (I) with an acidic metal                  oxide (MOx). The acidic metal oxide is transition                  metal oxide, which is tungsten, molybdenum,                  vanadium, niobium and/or antimony oxide.                  Precursor of acidic metal oxide is nitrate,                  acetate, chloride, sulfate and/or oxalate of the                  transition metals including tungsten, molybdenum,                  vanadium, niobium and antimony. The active                  component is ruthenium(IV) oxide nanoparticles                  solution prepared by taking ruthenium trichloride                  or ruthenium nitrate as precursor and carrying                  out pre-oxidation using hydrogen peroxide. The                  titanium source is butyl titanate, tetraisopropyl                  titanate, titanium sulfate, titanium nitrate or                  titanium oxide. The tin source is tin                  tetrachloride, stannous chloride or tin oxide.                  The mass fraction of ruthenium(IV) oxide loading                  is 0.1-5%, and the mass fraction of metal oxide                  (MOx) doping is 0-20%, and doping ratio between                  tin and titanium is 1:1-9.

PN CN110408999-A
TI Production of calcium sulfate whisker using soda ash ammoniated waste liquid, involves processing steamed ammonia waste liquid, carbonizing obtained liquid, and mixing obtained calcium carbonate solution and heated saturated sodium sulfate.
AU LI K
   YE W
   LI X
   LI M
AE QINGHAI ADVENT ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201994269F
AB    NOVELTY - Production of calcium sulfate whisker using soda ash ammoniated waste liquid and gypsum produced from anhydrous thenardite, involves cooling steamed ammonia waste liquid and separating to obtain to-be-processed liquid with solid content of less than 5 g/L, filtering to-be-processed liquid and concentrating to obtain clear liquid (l1) with solid content of less than 100 mg/L, passing carbon dioxide kiln gas to liquid (l1), adjusting pH to 6-9, carbonizing liquid (l1) to obtain calcium carbonate salt solution, subjecting the salt solution to concentration treatment to obtain clear liquid (l2) with solid content of less than 100 mg/L, heating liquid (l2) to 94-100 degrees C, heating saturated solution of sodium sulfate to 80-94 degrees C and mixing with liquid (l1), maintaining reaction temperature at 100-105 degrees C for 6-8 hours and stirring rate of 150 rpm to obtain a suspension, cooling to 60-70 degrees C, filtering and washing obtained filter cake with water, and drying.
   USE - Production of calcium sulfate whisker using soda ash ammoniated waste liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of recovering and reusing steamed ammonia waste liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the steamed ammonia waste liquid and anhydrous thenardite is 1000:140. The concentration of carbon dioxide in the kiln gas is 40 vol.%. The volume ratio of carbon dioxide and ammonia waste liquid is (1.1-1.2):1. Preferred Process: The steamed ammonia waste liquid is cooled to 40 degrees C. The carbonization reaction time is 1 hour. The clear liquid (l2) is heated to 94 degrees C, and the saturated sodium sulfate solution is heated to 80 degrees C. The calcium carbonate-containing salt solution is concentrated to obtain a concentrated solution with solid content of more than 10 g/L, filtering, rinsing obtained filter cake with clean water, in the mass ratio of 1:1, and drying at 95 degrees C for 2 hours to obtain nanocalcium carbonate. The saturated sodium sulfate is prepared by dissolving anhydrous sodium sulfate with purity of 90% per 200 kg in every 410 kg of water, and filtering. The washing step is carried out by washing the filter cake with 80 degrees C-clean water, in the mass ratio of 1:1.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN US2019296165-A1
TI Manufacturing photoexcitable material for photochemical electrode by ejecting aerosol in which particles of solid solution metal nitride and zinc oxide are dispersed in gas to collide against surface of substrate or conductive layer.
AU AMADA H
   KUMASAKA F
   MANABE T
   ANAZAWA T
   IMANAKA Y
AE FUJITSU LTD (FUIT-C)
GA 201982169A
AB    NOVELTY - A photoexcitable material is manufactured by preparing aerosol in which particles of solid solution of MN, where M is gallium, aluminum, and/or indium, and zinc oxide (ZnO) are dispersed in a gas; and forming photoexcitable material layer (1) including the particles on a substrate or a conductive layer (2) by ejecting the aerosol from a nozzle onto the substrate or the conductive layer in such a manner that the particles collide against a surface of the substrate or the conductive layer.
   USE - Manufacture of photoexcitable material for photochemical electrode for use as an anode for light-dependent reactions of artificial photosynthesis in a carbon dioxide reduction apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - Simply preparing a solid solution of GaN and ZnO may result in a limited reduction in forbidden band width, providing a narrow-forbidden-band photoexcitable material that allows for efficient utilization of light energy. Heating temperature for heating the mixture has no limit, allowing effective reduction of unreacted residues and suppressing generation of impurities.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional diagram of a photochemical electrode.
   Conductive layer (1)
   Photoexcitable material layer (2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Photoexcitable material contains 30-70 mol.% ZnO and has a band gap energy of less than or equal to 2.2 eV. Preferred Processes: The method further includes annealing the photoexcitable material layer at 600-850 degrees C. Annealing comprises annealing the photoexcitable material layer in an ammonia atmosphere.

AU LI X
   XIE S
   LI M
   LI F
   LI Y
   LI Z
AE SHANXI HOUMA XINFENGKANG BLOWER CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201980496E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves moving a radial position of a hub to a central position of a wheel hub ring plate. The wheel hub ring plate and an annular plate are connected and fixed by carbon dioxide gas shielded welding mode. A curved surface of the wheel hub ring plate is changed into a plane. A bottom part of a blade and the wheel hub ring plate are tightly combined. The blade is fixed on the annular plate through a blade core and a blade nut. A blade angle of the blade core is adjusted to decrease weight of the hub.    USE - Method for reinforcing strength of an axial flow fan hub.

PN CN110218826-A; CN110218826-B
TI Blast furnace blowing of biomass hydrothermal carbon comprises carrying out hydrothermal carbonization of biomass, mixing with coal, spraying mixture into pulverized coal conveying system, and adjusting blast furnace blasting parameters.
AU WANG G
   WANG C
   ZHANG J
   NING X
   LIANG W
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 201979543U
AB    NOVELTY - Blast furnace blowing of biomass hydrothermal carbon comprises (i) carrying out hydrothermal carbonization of biomass to obtain biomass hydrothermal carbon, (ii) crushing and mixing the mixture of biomass hydrothermal carbon and coal, and uniformly mixing, (iii) spraying the mixture of crushed and mixed mixture into the pulverized coal conveying and distributing system through the blast furnace of ironworks, and uniformly spraying the coal gun into the blast furnace through the direct blowing pipe and tuyere, and (iv) correspondingly adjusting the blast furnace blasting parameters, tuyere theoretical combustion temperature and furnace belly gas volume index to ensure the normal production of iron-making blast furnace.
   USE - The method is useful for blast furnace blowing of biomass hydrothermal carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the problem of optimal utilization of biomass resources in the blast furnace blowing process, can be used for the determination of waste water, waste rubber and other municipal flammable solid waste water heat treatment preparation of hydrothermal carbon for high efficiency in blast furnace injection, realizes the blast furnace safely and efficiently performs the biomass hydrothermal carbon blowing, improves the comprehensive utilization efficiency of biomass resources, and reduces carbon dioxide emissions of iron production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mixture in step (ii) comprises 0.1-60 wt.% biomass hydrothermal carbon. Preferred Components: The biomass comprises agricultural waste, forestry waste, livestock manure, sugar making waste residue, municipal organic waste, paper organic sludge, waste containing cellulose, hemicelluloses, lignin produced in life, and other human generated in production and life. The processing device for biomass hydrothermal carbon in step (i) is high temperature hydrothermal carbonization reactor, where the hydrothermal carbonization temperature is 180-320 degrees C, working pressure is the self-generated saturated vapor pressure of the closed system, and hydrothermal carbonization time is controlled between 20-100 minutes. The biomass hydrothermal carbon comprises less than 15 wt.% base water, has a dry base grinding index (HGI) of greater than 50, dry base high calorific value (HHV) of greater than 20000 kJ/kg, and less than 0.4 wt.% dry base alkali metal content. The hot air temperature of blast furnace blasting parameter in step (iv) is 1000-1300 degrees C, hot air rich oxygen rate volume fraction is 0.1-20%, and hot air water vapor content volume fraction is 0.1-10%. The combustion rate of injected fuel in step (iv) is greater than 60% before the tuyere, theoretical combustion temperature of the tuyere is 2000-2350 degrees C, and volume of belly gas is less than 70 m/minute. Preferred Method: The step (ii) is carried out by crushing and milling the mixture of biomass hydrothermal carbon and coal by medium speed milling, ball milling and other coal mills, where the mass fraction of particles has a particle size of less than 0.074 mm in the mixture after the crushing is greater than 70%.

PN CN110124647-A; CN110124647-B
TI Non-metallic catalyst for catalyzing oxidative dehydrogenation of lower alkanes, comprises boron oxide and silicon oxide, where boron oxide is supported on silicon oxide.
AU LU A
   LI W
   LU W
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2019728290
AB    NOVELTY - A non-metallic catalyst comprises 0.1-30 %mass boron oxide and silicon oxide, where boron oxide is supported on silicon oxide.
   USE - Non-metallic catalyst for catalyzing oxidative dehydrogenation of lower alkanes (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The supported non-metal catalyst is prepared by simple process, and has highly dispersed active sites (tri-coordinated boron-oxygen species), and does not easily catalyze deep oxidation of olefins. The boron-oxygen-silicon bond formed by the calcination immobilizes the active boron species, so that the active component of the catalyst is not easily lost and good stability of the catalyst is maintained. The catalyst is rich in active tri-coordinated boron-oxygen species and its boron aerobicity of the site with high alkane conversion rate at lower temperatures (400 degrees C) and high olefin yield and low carbon dioxide generation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) Preparation of supported non-metal catalyst which involves supporting boron oxide on silica by dipping method or mechanical mixing method; and
   (2) Method for catalyzing oxidative dehydrogenation of lower alkane which involves preparing supported non-metal catalyst, carrying out catalytic dehydrogenation reaction by supported non-metal catalyst containing lower alkane and mixed gas of diluent gas andoxidant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The supported non-metal catalyst comprises 5-20 %mass, preferably 5-10 %mass boron oxide. The boron oxide is of formula: BxOy, where2/3 less than or equal to x/y less than or equal to 3, preferably BxOy is B2O3. The silica is chosen from one of amorphous silica or all-silicon molecular sieve. The amorphous silicon oxide preferably comprises monolithic silicon oxide or amorphous silica is chosen from one or more of SBA-15, SBA-16, KIT-6 and silica aerogel, preferably SBA-15 and/or silica aerogel. The all-silicon molecule is screened from one or more of all-silicon ZSM-5, MCM-41 and MCM-48, preferably all-silicon ZSM-5. The boron-containing compound is chosen from boric acid, boron oxide, triethyl borate, bisphenol diboron, triphenyl borate, type of dioxalate boric acid or bis(catechol) diborate. The oxidizing agent is chosen from one of oxygen or air, preferably oxygen. The diluent gas comprises inert gas, preferably chosen from one of nitrogen, helium or argon. The volume ratio of oxidant to diluent gas is 1:1-5:1-6, preferably 1:1-1.5: 3.5-4. The ratio of catalyst to mixed gas is 4800-115200 mL.gcat-1hour-1, preferably 28800-115200 mL.gcat-1hour-1. Preferred Process: The impregnation method further involves dissolving boron-containing compound in water or alcohol solvent to obtain boron precursor solution, impregnating silicon oxide with solution, drying and claiming to obtain supported non-metallic catalyst. The mechanical mixing involves mixing boron-containing compound with silicon oxide and roasting. The step of activating is carried out by using supported non-metal catalyst in low-carbon alkane and carrying out heat treatment in mixed gas of oxidant and diluent gas at 400-600 degrees C, preferably 500-590 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours, preferably1-3 hours. Preferred Condition: The calcinations temperature is 600-900 degrees C, preferably 600-700 degrees C, calcinations time is 1-5 hours, preferably 1-3 hours. The reaction temperature in step (2) is 300-600 degrees C, preferably 400-550 degrees C, more preferably 400 -490 degrees C
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The alcohol solvent is chosen from one of ethylene glycol, ethanol, diethyl ether or dichloromethane. The lower alkane is chosen from one of ethane, propane or butane, preferably ethane or propane.

TI Method for monitoring infrared radiation source, involves directing portion of infrared radiation emitted by infrared radiation source onto reference detector by infrared radiation-reflecting body.
AU NIEMANN T
   EGGERS T
   GOHMANN A
AE HELLA GMBH & CO KGAA (WESF-C)
GA 2019665377
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves directing portion of infrared radiation emitted by an infrared radiation source (1) onto a reference detector (2) by an infrared radiation-reflecting body (3). Infrared radiation reaching the reference detector is detected. Value of the detected infrared radiation is compared with a stored value. The infrared radiation source is temporarily heated to a temperature higher than operating temperature of the infrared radiation source in a measurement mode. Infrared radiation emitted by the infrared radiation source operated at a higher temperature is detected by the reference detector.
   USE - Method for monitoring infrared radiation source of a sensor device i.e. carbon dioxide sensor, for monitoring aging state of the infrared radiation source to generate infrared radiation to be conducted through a sample gas volume to be examined.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables decreasing radiation intensity due to aging and contamination of the infrared radiation source and monitoring the infrared radiation source in a simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for monitoring infrared radiation source.

   USE - The process is useful for producing butadiene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Microbial organisms and methods are provided for effectively producing butadiene from cheap renewable feedstocks such as molasses, sugar cane juice, and sugars derived from biomass sources, including agricultural and wood waste, as well as Cl feedstocks such as syngas and carbon dioxide.

PN CN110037224-A
TI Processing method of green tea includes washing freshly picked green tea leaves in clean water, draining water, radiation sterilizing, crushing, drying, microwave curing, quenching, checking moisture content, and placing into vacuum box.
AU TANG Y
   TANG S
AE ZHONGFANG HUAHAN TEA FARM CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 201966397J
AB    NOVELTY - Processing method of green tea comprises washing freshly picked green tea leaves in clean water and draining off water; radiation sterilizing at 60Co- gamma rays; crushing, placing in a microwave drying device, and drying at 260-300 degrees C for 5-6 minutes; microwave curing, quenching using a quenching machine, and checking moisture content; if moisture content is above 5%, repeat third step until moisture content is 1-3%; placing into a vacuum box, flushing with a mixture of argon, carbon dioxide and nitrogen for 18-22 hours, ventilating for 8-10 hours, filling with pure nitrogen and biological preservation agent, and placing in a constant temperature room at 4-6 degrees C for 15-25 days; and packaging in a nano-package bag, vacuumizing, filling with nitrogen, hot-sealing using a heat-sealing packaging machine, oxygen-proof packaging, and irradiating for 8-10 minutes.
   USE - Method for processing green tea (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can preserve the flavor, trace elements and vitamins of the green tea for a long time and improves taste of the green tea.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The biological preservation agent is Bacillus cereus.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The microwave drying step is carried out at rotating speed of 600-800 revolutions/minute and output power of 6-7 kW. The vacuumizing step is carried out at - 20 to - 30 degrees C. The packaging into nano-package bag step is carried out at - 1 to 1 degrees C.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN110029304-A
TI Atmosphere nitriding and oxidation treatment of alloy steel, comprises cleaning the workpiece of alloy steel material, drying, carrying out atmospheric nitriding treatment, and vaporizing pure water containing ammonia in nitriding furnace.
AU WANG H
AE CHANGZHOU BOLIN HEAT TREATMENT CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201965330N
AB    NOVELTY - Atmosphere nitriding and oxidation treatment of alloy steel, comprises (i) cleaning the workpiece of alloy steel material; (ii) drying the workpiece of the alloy steel material; (iii) placing the workpiece of alloy steel material into the nitriding furnace, carrying out atmospheric nitriding treatment, multi-atmosphere controlling through ammonia, nitrogen, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and carrying out nitriding treatment on the surface of the workpiece; (iv) introducing pure ammonia-containing water into the nitriding furnace, and vaporizing the pure water containing ammonia in the nitriding furnace to oxidize the surface of the workpiece; and (v) releasing the workpiece.
   USE - The method is useful for atmosphere nitriding and oxidation treatment of alloy steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The treated steel surface has high hardness and wear resistance, and has excellent corrosion resistance. The atmosphere nitriding and oxidation composite treatment process solves the environmental pollution caused by the existing nitriding and salt bath technology and hard chrome plating treatment. The process is simple, the atmosphere is nitrided first in the nitriding furnace, and then oxidized, no converter operation is required, and the efficiency is high and the quality is good. The method saves energy and avoids environmental pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) is carried out in an ultrasonic cleaner. The step (ii) incudes drying the workpiece by a drying method or a natural drying method. The atmospheric nitriding treatment temperature of the step (iii) is 520-600 degrees C. The atmosphere nitriding treatment is pulse nitriding by a digital vacuum apparatus under a pressure of 50-70 MPa. The oxidation temperature of the step (iv) is also 520-600 degrees C.

GA 201937355V
AB    NOVELTY - A laminate structure (1) comprises an alternating stack of layers of different types of polymer blends (AC) and (BD) having a sequence of -AC-(BD-AC-)n, where n is 4-36. The layer thickness of layers (AC) (3) and layers (BD) (4) is less than 3 mm. The components (A) and (B) are thermoplastic polymers. The component (B) has a functional barrier property against transmission of oxygen, nitrogen, carbon dioxide, organic vapors and moisture. The components (C) and (D) are thermoplastic elastomer each contained in amount of 3-45 wt.% in the blends. The components (B) and (D) are essentially incompatible.
   USE - Laminate structure is used for barrier packaging. Uses include but are not limited to container, bag, tray and bottle for packaging meat, fish, cheese, milk, fruit juice, medical device, enteral, topical medication, living cells, fragrance, bandage, wipes, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The laminate structure has excellent gas-barrier property, aroma-barrier property, water-vapor barrier property, and mechanical property, such as toughness, puncture resistance, impact strength, and tear initiation and propagation resistance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the laminate structure.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: At least one of a monomeric building block of component (A) is derived from a renewable source, preferably from glycerol, diol, vanillin, ferulic acid, lactic acid, levulinic acid, adipic acid, azelaic acid, succinic acid, 1,4-butanediamide, bio-based 1,4-butanediol, diacid, hydroxyacid, furan, esteramide, amide, ester, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and bio-based alkylene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The component (A) is a polyamide, nucleated polyamide or a polyamide partly based on a renewable source, a blend of a polyamide with an ethylene vinyl alcohol copolymer or a polyalkylene carbonate or a polyketone, a copolymer of an olefin with a carboxylic acid or ester and/or ionomer, a maleic anhydride-grafted polyolefin or olefin carboxylic acid or ester copolymer or ionomer, or a blend of a maleic anhydride-grafted polyolefin, olefin carboxylic acid, ester copolymer or ionomer with a non-grafted polyolefin, olefin carboxylic acid, ester copolymer or ionomer. The component (A) is preferably maleic anhydride grafted polyolefin. The component (B) is an ethylene vinyl alcohol copolymer, polyketone, polyvinyl alcohol, polyalkylene carbonate, poly(1,3-glycerol carbonate) optionally mixed with polytetramethylene succinate, or a mixture or blend of polyamide with ethylene vinyl alcohol copolymer, polyvinyl alcohol, polyketone or polyalkylene carbonate, preferably ethylene vinyl alcohol copolymer, polyketone, polyvinyl alcohol or polyalkylene carbonate. The component (C) is a styrene block copolymer of styrene with at least one of butylene, isoprene, hydrogenated butylene, hydrogenated isoprene and isobutylene. The component (D) is a styrene block copolymer of styrene with isobutylene. The components (C) and/or (D) is preferably polytrimethylene carbonate, poly(lactate/butanediol/sebacate/itaconate) with at least 40 mol% lactic acid, triblock elastomer poly(L-lactide)-b-polymyrcene-b-poly(L-lactide), polyester elastomer, e.g. poly(3-hydroxybutyrate-co-4-hydroxybutyrate or co-b-hydroxyvalerate), triblock copolymer from polyitaconate and polyitaconic amide, poly(glycerol sebacate) and/or polytetramethylene ether glycol. The component (C) is preferably a block copolymer of styrene with at least partially-hydrogenated butylene and/or isoprene or a (partly)-renewable sourced elastomer. The component (D) is preferably a styrene block copolymer of styrene with isobutylene, a mixture of styrene block copolymer of styrene with isobutylene and polyamide or polyamide from renewable sources, or any of these with bio-based multiblock elastomers based on ether or etheramide building blocks. The laminate structure further comprises functional layers in which one is a sealing layer, provided on either side of the alternating stack. The functional layers comprise two functional layers including a tie layer (L1) promoting adhesion between the alternating stack, and a functional layer (L2). The layer (L1) is at least partly derived from a polymer comprising at least one monomeric building block from a renewable source.

TI Automatic continuous gaseous tritium sampling and sample preparing device, has desorption component orderly connected with absorption component, storage component and sampling component when device body is in working state.
AU GUO G
   CHEN C
   YAO J
   YANG L
   WU L
   ZENG F
   ZHANG B
AE SUZHOU NUCLEAR POWER RES INST CO LTD (CGNP-C)
   CHINA GEN NUCLEAR POWER CORP (CGNP-C)
   CGN POWER CO LTD (CGNP-C)
   SUZHOU NUCLEAR POWER RES INST CO LTD (CGNP-C)
GA 201938472T
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an oxidation component for extracting gas and converting organic substance into water and carbon dioxide. An absorbing component absorbs water in gas absorption component. An exhaust assembly is connected with the absorption component. A desorption component desorbs adsorption water of the adsorbing component. A device body is provided with a first working state and a second working state. The oxidation component is orderly connected with the absorbing assembly and the exhaust assembly when the device body is in the first working state. The desorption component is orderly connected with the absorption component, a storage component and a sampling component when the device body is in the second working state.
   USE - Automatic continuous gaseous tritium sampling and sample preparing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes sampling process of gaseous tritium in a continuous and stable manner, places tritium sample in an ultra-low background liquid flash spectrometer, and performs program control, single-key operation and unmanned control functions in a stable and reliable manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an automatic continuous gaseous tritium sampling and sample preparing method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic circuit diagram of an automatic continuous gaseous tritium sampling and sample preparing device.

PT P
PN CN109548561-A
TI Method for cultivating pilose antler mushroom,            involves placing substrate in bottle for sterilization,            injecting velvet mushroom into bottle in sterile            environment, placing inoculated sterile bottle in dark            environment and incubating hyphae.
AU CHENG J
   QIU Y
AE KUNSHAN QINGHE EDIBLE MUSHROOMS TECHNOLO (KUNS-Non-standard)
GA 201932608J
AB    NOVELTY - A pilose antler mushroom cultivating method                involves placing the substrate in a bottle for                sterilization and injecting the velvet mushroom                into the bottle in a sterile environment at below                30 degrees C. The inoculated sterile bottle is                placed in a dark environment at 23-25 degrees C and                cultured until the hyphae are full. The hyphae is                incubated for 7-10 days, so that it reaches                physiological maturity. The bottle is turned down                into an empty basket, which is conducive to the                growth of hyphae, air humidity of 90-95% at 12-15                degrees C and moderate ventilation. The mycelium                resumes growth, the humidity drops to 80-85%, forms                a difference in humidity, the carbon dioxide                concentration is below 0.1% and cultivating the                mushroom buds. The mushroom buds are formed, the                basket is turned over, so that the bottle mouth is                facing upwards at 15-17 degrees C and the humidity                is controlled at 90-95%. The mushroom cover is                basically unfolded and the spores are harvested                when ejected.
   USE - Method for cultivating pilose antler                mushroom.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables cultivating pilose antler                mushroom with high yield, shortened production                period and reduced product pollution rate, in rapid                manner.

PN CN109529857-A
TI Core-shell catalyst preparation involves mixing            nickel source solution with surfactant naphthenic            solution, adding hydrazine hydrate with stirring            uniformly, and then adding tetraethyl orthosilicate and            ammonia water successively.
AU HAN B
   WANG F
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 201931163D
AB    NOVELTY - Core-shell catalyst preparation involves                mixing a nickel source solution with a surfactant                naphthenic solution, adding hydrazine hydrate with                stirring uniformly, and then adding tetraethyl                orthosilicate and ammonia water successively with                stirring to hydrolyze for a certain period of time.                The reaction is terminated by adding isopropanol,                where the obtained precipitate is centrifuged,                dried, and calcined to obtain a powder. The                obtained powder is dissolved in ethylene glycol,                water or ethanol, then added to aqueous solution of                cerium nitrate hexahydrate with stirring uniformly.                A certain amount of ammonia water is added at a                certain temperature for hydrolyzing for a period of                time, and then stirred for a period of time. The                formed reaction mixture is cooled at room                temperature, centrifuged to obtain a solid sample,                dried, and calcined to obtain the core-shell                catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing core-shell catalyst used                for methane carbon dioxide reforming reaction and                conversion rate of methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the core-shell catalyst                energy in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly                manner with high conversion rate of methane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic X-ray powder                diffraction spectrum of the prepared core-shell                nanosphere structure material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The nickel source is                  nickel nitrate, nickel chloride, nickel acetate,                  nickel sulfate or nickel hydroxide. The                  surfactant is polyethylene glycol hexadecyl                  ether. The nickel source solution, the                  surfactant, the hydrazine hydrate, the tetraethyl                  orthosilicate and the ammonia water are provided                  in a volume ratio of 0.5:15:0.6:2.1:1.5. The                  calcined powder is reduced by hydrogen at 750                  degrees C for 30 minutes.

AB    NOVELTY - System (100) for conversion of a biomass (8),                comprises a hydrothermal liquefaction (HTL) stage                (25) that hydrothermally liquefies the biomass in                an aqueous medium at a temperature and pressure                selected to form a conversion product comprising a                separable bio-oil and an aqueous fraction                containing residual organics in it, and a catalytic                hydrothermal gasification (CHG) stage (80)                operatively coupled to the HTL stage configured to                receive the aqueous fraction containing residual                organics from the HTL stage at a selected                temperature and pressure that forms a product gas                containing at least one medium BTU product gas                (72).

   ADVANTAGE - The system allows up to 85% of the feedstock                carbon to be recovered as fuel, with the remainder                being converted to carbon dioxide, is cost                effective, and maximizes efficiency of biomass                conversion in hydrothermal and gasification                facilities.

PN CN108850153-A
TI Silicon dioxide aerogel is used in grain storage and insect control by coating silicon dioxide aerogel on surface of grains, where silicon dioxide aerogel is used at specified amount and has specified porosity and specific surface area.
AU SUN Y
   SUN M
   HU Y
AE TIANJIN MOGEN KUNDE HIGH & NEW TECHNOLOG (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201895802B
AB    NOVELTY - Silicon dioxide aerogel is used in grain storage and insect control by coating the silicon dioxide aerogel on surface of grains, where silicon dioxide aerogel is at 0.1-3 mass% of the mass of the grain particles and has porosity of 90-98%, aperture of 20-80 nm, framework particles of 10-300 nm, specific surface area of 100-1000 m2/g and density of 3-500 kg/m3.
   USE - Silicon dioxide aerogel is used in grain storage and insect control (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Because the silicon dioxide aerogel has nanoporous structure, large specific surface area and strong surface absorbent characteristics, it can effectively absorb carbon dioxide and moisture, reducing breakage of respiration and prolonging the life of grain. The silicon dioxide aerogel can isolate the grain particles from outside air, moisture and heat, providing preservation and fresh-keeping effect, and serves as outer protective layer of the grain particles, effectively preventing direct contact of the grain pests and grain particles, achieving the purpose of pest control and simultaneously regulating temperature, humidity, gas composition, pests, and ecological environment. It is simple and safe to use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The silicon dioxide aerogel is at 0.2-2.5 mass%, preferably 0.5-2 mass% of the mass of grains. It has porosity of 92-98%, preferably 93-98%, aperture of 20-50 nm, preferably 20-40 nm, framework particles of 10-200 nm, preferably 10-100 nm, specific surface area of 100-800 m2/g, preferably 150-800 m2/g, and density of 3-300 kg/m3, preferably 5-200 kg/m3. The silicon dioxide aerogel coating thickness of the grain surface is 1-105 nm.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zinc alloy further comprises 0-3 wt.% trace element, where trace element is magnesium, calcium, barium, silicon, phosphorus, silver, copper, tin, iron and/or rare earth elements. Preferred Method: The step (i) further comprising adding trace element mixture, smelting the mixture and then allowing to stand, where smelting temperature is 420-600 degrees C. The method further comprises mechanically processing the zinc-lithium-manganese-based zinc alloy, where mechanical processing is extrusion, rolling, forging and/or rapid solidification. The homogenization method of zinc-lithium-manganese-based zinc alloy comprises temperature of treatment is 300-400 degrees C, time is 12-48 hours; extrusion temperature is 150-280 degrees C, extrusion ratio is 10-70; extrusion speed is 0.1-10 mm/second; rolling includes sequentially performing hot rolling and finish rolling, hot rolling temperature is 200-300 degrees C, finish rolling temperature is 150-250 degrees C, amount of rolling per pass is 1-20%, zinc-lithium-manganese-based zinc alloy is rolled by 1- 3 mm; forging includes insulating temperature of zinc-lithium-manganese-based zinc alloy is 150-200 degrees C, forging temperature is 200-300 degrees C, heat preservation time is 3-50 hours, rate of forging is not less than 350 mm/second; rapid solidification comprises zinc-lithium-manganese zinc alloy is made into a rapid solidification ribbon by a high vacuum quenching system under inert atmosphere, broking strip into a powder at 200-350 degrees C, and vacuum hot pressing for 1-24 hours. The method further comprises processing the zinc alloy into a capillary material.

PN KR2018058028-A; KR1865569-B1
TI Producing calcium carbonate useful as pigment in thermal pre-layer to replace plastic clay, comprises mixing additive comprising organic acid and polyacrylate with slaked lime slurry and performing carbonation reaction of lime slurry.
AU SHIN G J
   JUNG G Y
   PARK K C
   HUR H J
AE POSCO CHEM CO LTD (POSC-C)
GA 2018425174
AB    NOVELTY - Producing calcium carbonate using carbon dioxide gasification method, comprises mixing additive with slaked lime slurry and performing carbonation reaction of the slaked lime slurry. The additive comprises 0.1-0.5 wt.% organic acid and 0.1-2 wt.% polyacrylate, based on the solid content of the slaked lime. The carbonation initiation temperature is 30-70 degrees C. The carbonation reaction is carried out by reaction scheme 2, as given in of the specification.
   USE - Calcium carbonate is useful as pigment in thermal pre-layer to replace plastic clay.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the calcium carbonate that has high absorption efficiency ratio capable of replacing a calcining clay, high pore volume, low apparent specific gravity, pore volume of not less than 0.05 cm3/g, a specific surface area of not less than 30 m2/g, and an apparent ratio of not more than 0.2 kg/l, replaces the existing expensive silica and titanium dioxide, improves the printability when applied as paper pigment, improves the heat absorption, replaces the plastic clay used in the thermal layer pre-layer pigment, and is applied to additives e.g. cable, plastic and paint.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The solid content of the slaked slurry before the carbonation reaction is 7-20 wt.%. The stirring speed of the carbonation is controlled at 30-50 revolutions per minute. The method further comprises adding 1-3 wt.% of dispersant into the prepared calcium carbonate to prepare a slurry. The solid content of the highly absorbed porous calcium carbonate slurry prepared in the slurry state is limited to 45-55 wt.%. Preferred Components: The additive comprises 0.3-0.5 wt.% of the organic acid and 0.5-1.5 wt.% of the polyacrylate, based on the solid content of the slaked lime.
   EXAMPLE - Slurry of 10% slaked lime was prepared by reacting with lime and water, and then 200 mesh sieves were sieved to remove minute impurities. 60 l Slaked slurry was charged into a 100 l reactor, and the reaction initiation temperature was adjusted to 32 degrees C. As the additive, 0.5 wt.% of citric acid and 1 wt.% of sodium polyacrylate were added to the reactor. The stirring speed was adjusted to 30 revolutions/minute and then carbon dioxide was blown. The reaction was stopped when the temperature was gradually raised by the exothermic reaction, and the pH value was lowered and the pH was constant between 7-8/ After 325 mesh sieving and filtration, the slurry was dispersed at a calcium carbonate solid concentration of 45 wt.% while a dispersant was added to obtain calcium carbonate.

   MABROUK R (MABR-Individual)
