   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for reducing the amount of fuel and air used for producing hydrogen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the steam methane reformer with the catalyst capable of conducting electricity.
   Feeding line of mixture of natural gas and process hydrocarbon tail gas (1)
   Air feeding line (2)
   Steam methane reformer (A)
   Metal monolith supported catalyst (B)
   Electrical power source (C)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: Hydrogen is formed in a mixture with carbon monoxide. The electric current contacting the metal monolith supported catalyst produces heat. The heat generated by the electric current supplements the heat generated by the reaction of fuel and air.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The metal monolith supported catalyst is made of a low alloy steel. Preferred Process: The metal monolith supported catalyst is fabricated by wash coating a catalyst on a metallic support. The wash coating involves coating the metal structure support with a ceramic material-based alumina, drying the coating and impregnating the coating with a reforming material substrate which is nickel or ruthenium. The heat released by electrical resistance of the metallic structure is controlled by an amount of current passing through the metallic structure. The heat released by the electrical resistance is 10-15% of total power provided to produce hydrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fuel is selected from methane, ethane, ethylene, propane, propylene, butane, pentane and hexane. The hydrocarbons are chosen from unconverted methane and carbon monoxide, unrecovered hydrogen, and carbon dioxide.

PN DE102016223632-A1; EP3326880-A1; EP3326880-B1
TI Method for controlling hybrid drive of motor vehicle, involves determining air-fuel ratio of engine by difference between current injection quantity of engine and injection quantity, and prioritizing electric drive of hybrid drive.
AU BRUENE H
   AZAROV D
AE BAYERISCHE MOTOREN WERKE AG (BAYM-C)
   BAYERISCHE MOTOREN WERKE AG (BAYM-C)
GA 201843223Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves recognizing dynamic state of an internal combustion engine (12) or acceleration of a motor vehicle, where rinsing slope of the combustion engine is determined and/or air-fuel ratio of the engine is determined by difference between current injection quantity of the combustion engine and limiting injection quantity. An electric drive (14) of a hybrid drive (10) is prioritized when the dynamic state is recognized. Difference between current boost pressure and reference value of the boost pressure is determined. The combustion engine is connected with a control unit (16).
   USE - Method for controlling a hybrid drive of a motor vehicle (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables determining the air-fuel ratio of the engine by difference between current injection quantity of the combustion engine and limiting injection quantity so as to reduce carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide emissions of the combustion engine when combustion air ratio drops, thus improving working efficiency of the combustion engine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a hybrid drive for a motor vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a hybrid drive.
   Hybrid drive (10)
   Internal combustion engine (12)
   Electric drive (14)
   Control unit (16)
   Injection system (18)

GA 201841676M
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an intelligent irrigating system, comprising a top end of the spraying device, the shed body, a solar panel and a spray head, the roof is uniformly equipped with a solar panel, and one side of the shed is provided with a central controller. of body above said central controller is provided with an alarm indicating lamp, the output end of the water pump is connected with the spraying device through the water guide pipe, the temperature sensor, the output end of the carbon dioxide detector and the humidity sensor are input end through the lead electrically connected with the central controller. and the output end of the central controller is connected with the motor, the camera device and the alarm indication lamp input end electrically connected with the circuit board. PT P
PN US2018127346-A1; US10155712-B2
TI Production of (alpha),(beta)-unsaturated carboxylic acid e.g. acrylic acid or its salt, involves contacting e.g. transition metal-ligand complex, olefin and treated solid oxide free of organic basic moiety covalently bound with linker.
AU HLAVINKA M L
   MCDANIEL M P
   IACONO P
AE CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
   CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
GA 201835946K
AB    NOVELTY - Production of ( alpha ),( beta )-unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salt involves contacting a transition metal-ligand complex, an olefin, carbon dioxide, a diluent and a treated solid oxide comprising a transition metal-treated solid oxide and/or a transition metal-treated chemically-modified solid oxide. The treated solid oxide is free of an organic basic moiety that is covalently bound with a linking moiety. The molar yield of ( alpha ),( beta )-unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salt based on transition metal of the transition metal-ligand complex is 50% or more.
   USE - Production of ( alpha ),( beta )-unsaturated carboxylic acid e.g. acrylic acid, methacrylic acid, 2-ethylacrylic acid, cinnamic acid, sodium acrylate, magnesium acrylate and sodium methacrylate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces ( alpha ),( beta )-unsaturated carboxylic acid with high yield by the use of treated solid oxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for metallalactone elimination reaction, which involves contacting a metallalactone, the diluent and the treated solid oxide. The molar yield of ( alpha ),( beta )-unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salt based on metallalactone is 5% or more.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transition metal of the transition metal-ligand complex is a group 8-11 transition metal, and the ligand of the transition metal-ligand complex is a neutral electron donor group or Lewis base. The transition metal-treated solid oxide comprises the solid oxide (s1) chosen from alumina, titania, zirconia, magnesia, boria, calcia, zinc oxide, silica-alumina, silica-coated alumina, silica-titania, silica-zirconia, silica-magnesia, alumina-titania, alumina-zirconia, zinc-aluminate, alumina-boria, silica-boria, aluminum phosphate, aluminophosphate, aluminophosphate-silica, magnesium aluminate and/or titania-zirconia, and a transition metal or the transition metal-containing compound chosen from titanium, zirconium, hafnium, tungsten and/or zinc. The transition metal-treated chemically-modified solid oxide comprises the chemically-modified solid oxide (s2) chosen from fluorided alumina, chlorided alumina, bromided alumina, sulfated alumina, fluorided silica-alumina, chlorided silica-alumina, bromided silica-alumina, sulfated silica-alumina, fluorided silica-zirconia, chlorided silica-zirconia, bromided silica-zirconia, sulfated silica-zirconia, fluorided silica-titania, fluorided silica-coated alumina, fluorided-chlorided silica-coated alumina, sulfated silica-coated alumina and/or phosphated silica-coated alumina, and the transition metal. The electron-withdrawing anion comprises fluoride and/or sulfate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The metallalactone elimination reaction further involves contacting a solid oxide (s1) and a transition metal-containing compound and calcining to form the transition metal-treated solid oxide, contacting a solid oxide (s2) and an electron-withdrawing anion and calcining to form a chemically-modified solid oxide, and contacting the chemically-modified solid oxide with the transition metal-containing compound and calcining to form the transition metal-treated chemically-modified solid oxide, before contacting metallalactone, diluent and treated solid oxide. The metallalactone and the diluent contact a fixed bed of the treated solid oxide. Preferred Components: The metallalactone is nickelalactone. The olefin comprises ethylene. Preferred Composition: The molar yield of ( alpha ),( beta )-unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salt based on transition metal of the transition metal-ligand complex is preferably 75-10000%. The molar yield of ( alpha ),( beta )-unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salt based on metallalactone is preferably 50-10000%.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E19 (Other organic compounds general - organic compounds of unknown or indefinite structure; general mixtures of many types; organic reactions (e.g. nitration, resolution) when applied generally.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN107999030-A; CN107999030-B
TI Preparing composite oil-water separating agent comprises e.g. mixing protein powder, calcium carbonate and phosphoric acid, adding into sodium silicate in ultrasonic-assisted reaction and adding modified chitosan.
AU LIU Z
   GE Y
   CHEN Q
   MU J
   LIU J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG OCEAN (UYZO-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG OCEAN (UYZO-C)
GA 201837594G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite oil-water separating agent comprises (i) mixing protein powder, calcium carbonate and phosphoric acid having mass-to-volume ratio of 80 wt.% and adding into 2-3 wt.% sodium silicate in ultrasonic-assisted reaction to obtain mixture, where the volume ratio between sodium silicate solution and above-mentioned phosphoric acid is 2-3:1 and the mass ratio between protein powder, calcium carbonate and phosphoric acid is 0.2:1:5-7, (ii) adding 10-30 pts. wt. bamboo fiber nanoparticles and 15-23 pts. wt. modified chitosan to 50-70 pts. wt. mixture obtained in step (i), stirring and maintaining at 20-28 degrees C for 2-5 hours to obtain viscous gel, (iii) vacuum drying the viscous gel to water content of 25-35 wt.% to obtain solid powder, and then hardening in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, (iv) washing the hardened solid powder with deionized water for 2-5 times and drying in vacuum to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing composite oil-water separating agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite oil-water separating agent: has excellent stability and strength, water absorption capacity and water storage capacity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of bamboo fiber nano-particles comprises bamboo is made of normal bamboo sheet, then cooking, crushing, stewing bamboo filament, carrying out bio-enzyme degumming process made of bamboo fiber and placing bamboo fiber in carbon dioxide duration of the aging furnace, adjusting carbon dioxide flow rate to 100-150 ml/minute, heating the aging furnace at 400-500 degrees C for 3-6 hours at a rate of 5-8 degrees C/minute and then adjusting carbon dioxide flow to 300-360 ml/minute and heating the aging furnace to 800-900 degrees C at a rate of 1-2 degrees C/minute for 2-3 hours, stop carbon dioxide passing and adding deionized water vapor spray to aging furnace for 2-3 minutes and cooling to obtain carbonized bamboo fibers, and then milling to obtain bamboo fiber nano-particles having particle diameter of 10-20 nm, where the volume ratio between deionized water vapor and aging furnace is 0.6-0.7:1. The method comprises mixing nano-particle bamboo fiber and nano-silica aerogel at a mass ratio of 1:0.2-0.5, and then adjusting the pressure and temperature to obtain titanium dioxide aerogel in a supercritical state, then shaking at a speed of 100-120 revolutions/minute for 30-60 minutes and nano-silica aerogel loaded on bamboo fiber nano-particles immediately under reduced pressure. The preparation of modified chitosan comprises placing chitosan in an acetic acid/sodium acetate buffer solution with a pH of 4-5, then adding sodium fluoride solution and stirring, maintaining the temperature at 23-28 degrees C for 2-3 hours to obtain mixed system and drying mixed system in vacuum and washing with deionized water for 2-5 times to obtain modified chitosan. The step (iii) hardening process: blowing solid powder in the air passage with carbon dioxide, adjusting carbon dioxide of flux is 50-70 ml/minute and maintaining for 60-90 minutes, then increasing the flow rate of carbon dioxide to 50-70 ml/minute and maintaining for 60-90 minutes. In step (iii) and (iv), the vacuum drying temperature is 15-28 degrees C. Preferred Components: The concentration of sodium fluoride solution is 0.1-0.2 mol/l, the volume ratio between acetic acid/sodium acetate buffer solution and sodium fluoride solution is 3-7:1 and the mass ratio between sodium fluoride and chitosan is 1:6-9.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

   HU K
   LIU M

GA 202283293C
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing high-loaded nickel single atom                catalyst comprises (a) mixing and grinding                dicyandiamide, potassium chloride and sodium                chloride in a ratio of 1:(1-2):(1-2), placing in a                crucible of muffle furnace, heating to 670plus                minus20degrees Celsius for calcination under air                atmosphere, maintaining for 60 minutes, cooling,                (b) grinding the calcined solid product, adding                mixed solution of water and ethanol, washing,                drying to obtain pure carbon three nitrogen four                nano sheet, (c) adding 20plus minus2 mg carbon                three nitrogen four nano sheet into water,                dispersing, adding nickel salt below 0.001 mmol and                8plus minus1 mg carbon nanotube, stirring, mixing,                freeze-drying and (d) transferring freeze-dried                mixture to quartz boat, heating to 600plus                minus10degrees Celsius for carbonization under an                argon atmosphere, maintaining for 60 minutes and                cooling to obtain nickel single-atom                catalyst.

PN CN114524722-A
TI Method for extracting long-chain dibasic acid,            comprises e.g. converting long chain dibasic acid in            long-chain dibasic acid fermentation liquid to be            fermented into long-chain dibasic acid salt, and            filtering, crystallizing organic phase, obtaining            long-chain dibasic acid, and drying.
AU WANG P
   ZHANG L
   GAO D
   ZHU H
AE SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2022796228
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting long-chain dibasic acid,                comprises (1) converting long chain dibasic acid in                long-chain dibasic acid fermentation liquid to be                fermented into long-chain dibasic acid salt, and                filtering, (2) adding soluble long-chain dibasic                acid to the liquid organic solvent, fully stirring                and introducing carbon dioxide to obtain the                long-chain dibasic acid-rich organic phase and                long-chain dibasic acid content aqueous phase, (3)                discharging the aqueous phase, keeping the organic                phase, and (4) crystallizing the organic phase                obtained in the step (3), obtaining the long-chain                dibasic acid by solid-liquid separation, and drying                to obtain long-chain dibasic acid.
   USE - Method for extracting long-chain dibasic                acids.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes the conversion of the                long-chain dibasic acid salt to the dibasic acid                combined with the organic phase transfer process,                and the crude acid crystallization step in the                conventional method is omitted; shortens the number                of devices reduced, no traditional inorganic acid                is used, and there is no acid residue in the                wastewater, which greatly reduces the cost of                wastewater treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (2), the                  carbon dioxide introduced is carbon dioxide with                  a certain pressure, and the pressure is greater                  than or equal to 0.1 MPa, preferably 0.3-3.0 MPa.                  The organic solvent in the middle is an organic                  solvent that can dissolve long-chain dibasic                  acids and immiscible with water, hydrocarbons,                  aliphatic alcohols with a carbon number of 4 or                  ethers, esters and ketones. In the step (2), the                  amount of the organic solvent added should not be                  less than the amount of the organic solvent that                  can dissolve all the long-chain dibasic acids in                  the fermentation broth at the reaction                  temperature of the system. The reaction                  temperature of all the dibasic acid is not lower                  than the organic solvent of all the dibasic acid                  in the dissolved fermentation liquor, and not                  higher than the boiling point of the organic                  solvent. In the step (2), before introducing                  carbon dioxide to the step (3), the system should                  be reaction temperature and stirring to make the                  organic phase, the aqueous phase and the gas                  phase are fully contact, and the time is not less                  than 5. In the step (2), after adding the organic                  solvent, before introducing carbon dioxide or                  after obtaining the organic phase in step (3),                  the step of adding an adsorbent is also included.                  In the step (4) of organic phase crystallization,                  it further comprises adding water to mix with the                  organic phase, fully and statically placing the                  phase and discharging the aqueous phase. In the                  step (4), before the drying, the step of washing                  the long-chain dibasic acid crystals with water                  or the same fresh organic solvent used as the                  organic solvent.Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  long-chain dibasic acid is converted into a                  long-chain dibasic acid salt, and the long-chain                  dibasic acid salt is sodium salt, potassium salt                  and ammonium salt. In the step (2), the organic                  solvent is from n-butanol, isobutanol, n-amyl                  alcohol, isoamyl alcohol, hexanol, cyclohexanol,                  2-ethylhexanol, isooctanol, sec-octanol, toluene,                  xylene, ethyl alcohol benzene, chloroform,                  perchloroethylene, methyl isobutyl ketone, butyl                  formate, propyl acetate, isoamyl acetate,                  isobutyl acetate, isopropyl acetate, n-amyl                  acetate, ethyl acetate, butyl acetate, octyl                  acetate, isooctyl acetate, sec-octyl acetate,                  isopropyl ether, butyl ether, isobutyl ether,                  amyl ether, and/or isopentyl ether. The adsorbent                  is from activated carbon or activated clay.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The honeycomb carrier                  is a cordierite honeycomb carrier, a mullite                  honeycomb carrier, and an alumina honeycomb                  carrier, preferably a cordierite honeycomb                  carrier. AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises (i) screening out the dried black wolfberry fruit with full color, no browning on the peel, and uniform size, removing pedicel, weighing black medlar fruit, adding distilled water, soaking, placing in juicer to homogenate and filtering the homogenized homogenate; (ii) adding pectase and cellulase into the homogenate, placing in ultrasonic cleaning machine, enzymolysis, filtering, allowing to stand, cooling and keeping black medlar juice in a constant temperature water bath to inactivate the enzyme; (iii) taking dry yeast, adding sterile water, placing in electric heating constant temperature water bath, cooling to obtain the yeast liquid, adding white granulated sugar into the black medlar juice, adding yeast liquid, fermenting, removing carbon dioxide and when the alcohol concentration is not changed stop fermenting; and (iv) inoculating acetic acid bacteria to continue acetic acid fermentation, centrifuging, filtering and sterilizing to obtain the vinegar.

GA 202208717L
AB    NOVELTY - Constructing an H3K9 methylase inhibitor to inhibit the proliferation of diffuse large B lymphoma cells involves culturing lymphoma cells, the cell culture medium for culturing lymphoma cells includes RPMI-1640 medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum. After lymphoma cells grew to logarithmic growth phase, 10 mmol/L H3 K9 methylase inhibitor is added, and cultured in a 37degrees Celsius CO2 incubator. After 24 hours, lymphoma cells are replaced with cell culture medium without H3K9 methylase inhibitor, and cultured in a 37degrees Celsius carbon dioxide incubator. The lymphoma cells are injected in mice subcutaneously with H3K9 methylase inhibitor, sacrifice mice on 24th day, and remove tumor body. The mouse tumor tissue is collected, RT-qPCR detection of tumor-related genes Bcl-2, Bax and Ki-67 mRNA expression and validation of H3K9 methylase inhibitor inhibiting establishment of a diffuse large B lymphoma cell proliferation model are performed.

PN CN113476413-A; CN113476413-B
TI Preparing ganciclovir sodium freeze-dried powder for injection, involves adding activated carbon to mannitol solution, performing coarse filtration for removing carbon, adding ganciclovir sodium to purified mannitol solution, passing via sterile mixed cellulose film, and freeze-drying.
AU CAI Q
   LU Y
   LU G
AE HAINAN HAILING PHARM CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B87337
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing ganciclovir sodium freeze-dried powder for injection, involves (a) adding mannitol to water to obtain mannitol solution, (b) adding activated carbon to mannitol solution, uniformly stirring and leaving, performing coarse filtration for removing carbon, passing mannitol solution obtained after coarse filtration through 0.22 mum sterile mixed cellulose film to obtain purified mannitol solution, (c) adding ganciclovir sodium to purified mannitol solution, and passing through 0.22 mum sterile mixed cellulose film to obtain ganciclovir sodium solution, controlling the temperature of ganciclovir sodium solution to 50-60degreesC, and adjusting the pH value to 8-10 using carbon dioxide, (d) loading ganciclovir sodium solution in bottle, and performing freeze-drying process after semi-pressure plugging, and (e) after completing freeze-drying process, performing plug-in and re-pressing process to obtain ganciclovir sodium freeze-dried powder for injection.
   USE - Method for preparing ganciclovir sodium freeze-dried powder for injection.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts mannitol, which is used as protective agent of ganciclovir sodium freeze-dried powder, enables effective storage of ganciclovir sodium freeze-dried powder, and guarantees the purity of ganciclovir sodium freeze-dried powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of mannitol solution is 2-5 %mass. In step (b), the concentration of activated carbon in mannitol solution is 0.05-0.1 %mass. The weight ratio of ganciclovir sodium and mannitol solution is (0.010-0.020):1.Preferred Method: The freeze-drying process in step (d) involves pre-freezing at -45 to -35degreesC for 60 minutes, and performing primary drying and secondary drying process, where the pre-freezing is carried out at -45 to -35degreesC for 3-5 hours.Preferred Conditions: The step (b) involves uniformly stirring for 20-30 minutes, and leaving for 15-20 minutes. The primary drying process involves increasing the temperature to -5 to 5degreesC for 7-9 hours under vacuum degree of 10-15 Pa, and maintaining the temperature at -5 to 5degreesC for 5-8 hours. The secondary drying process involves increasing the temperature to 25-45degreesC under vacuum degree of 0-10 Pa for 3-4 hours, and maintaining the temperature at 25-45degreesC for 6-9 hours.

TI Preparing fermentation source calcium propionate by using ion exchange resin, comprises filtering fermentation liquid, adding calcium hydroxide to propionic acid liquid, performing solid-liquid separation, and concentrating clear liquid.
AU HUANG Y
   WU J
   QIAN W
   XU Y
AE ZHEJIANG SHENGDA BIOLOGICAL RES INST CO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021B87007
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (1) fermenting using propionic acid bacteria under indirect anaerobic condition to obtain fermentation liquid containing 15-30 g/l propionate; (2) filtering and separating fermentation liquid and collecting liquid phase; (3) pumping liquid phase into cation exchange resin pre-treatment bed by pump, and introducing air from bottom of cation exchange resin pre-treatment bed for aeration stirring for 30 minutes to discharge generated carbon dioxide; (4) flowing material liquid through pre-treated cation exchange resin bed, top washing using pure water, and collecting outflow of propionic acid liquid; (5) adding calcium hydroxide to the propionic acid liquid for calcification, adjusting pH to 6.5-7.5, and stirring for 20 minutes; (6) performing solid-liquid separation of material and liquid and collecting liquid phase to obtain calcium propionate clear liquid; and (7) concentrating calcium propionate clear liquid for 5-8 times and spray-drying material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fermentation source calcium propionate by using ion exchange resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses resin exchange to remove metal salt ions, has high efficiency, can be reused, and is industrialized production process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the propionic acid bacteria are Propionibacterium acidigenes and Propionibacterium freudenreichii.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (2), the filtration and separation method comprises ceramic membrane filtration, hollow fiber membrane filtration and plate and frame press filtration. In the step (3), the cation exchange resin pre-treatment bed is H-type cation exchange resin, the volume of the resin is 0.2-0.5 times the volume of the material liquid, and the air speed is 0.3-0.5 VVM. In the step (4), the cation exchange resin bed is H-type cation exchange resin, the volume of the resin is 0.8-1.2 times of the volume of the material liquid, the flow speed is 0.2-0.5 BV/hour resin volume, and the top washing water is 0.2-0.4 BV resin volume. In the step (5), the solid-liquid separation method is plate and frame filter pressing, tubular centrifugation, and horizontal screw centrifugation.

PN CN113230195-A
TI Preparing facial mask useful for preventing wrinkles, comprises adding resuscitated dermal cells and epidermal cells to a colloidal solution of e.g. L-glutamine and fetal bovine serum (FBS), culturing, molding gel, and adding mask liquid.
AU QIN J
   XING Z
   SHAN F
   ZHANG P
   WANG T
   LI X
   CUI C
AE PANSHENG RUIXIANG SHANDONG BIOLOGICAL (PANS-Non-standard)
GA 202194894N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing facial mask containing human dermal and epidermal cells, comprises (a) resuscitating dermal cells to survive and processing adherent culture to reach 75-80%, and collecting the cultured dermal cells, (b) resuscitating epidermal cells to survive and processing adherent culture to reach 75-80%, and collecting the cultured epidermal cells, (c) placing a beaker in an ice bath, adding minimum essential medium (MEM) (10X), L-glutamine, fetal bovine serum (FBS), Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) solution containing 10% FBS and bovine collagen, adding functional component, using sodium bicarbonate to adjust the pH of the solution to neutral, and obtaining colloidal solution, (d) adding the dermal cells and epidermal cells to the colloidal solution to obtain colloid, mixing and transferring to a mold, culturing the mold in a carbon dioxide cell incubator for 30-60 minutes, molding gel, and adding mask liquid on the surface of the colloid.
   USE - The facial mask is useful for preventing wrinkles.
   ADVANTAGE - The facial mask is rich in viable cells that continuously secrete cytokines that promote functional components to the base layer and improve skin condition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (c), the concentration of the L-glutamine is 200 mM, and the concentration of the bovine collagen is 5 mg/ml; and the MEM (10X), L-glutamine, FBS, DMEM solution containing 10% FBS, bovine collagen protein are added based on the volume ratio of 1-5:0.1-1:1-5:10-15:1-10. In the step (c), the functional component is ceramide or Centella asiatica extract, and the final concentration of the functional component in the colloidal solution is 0.4-0.5 M. In the step (d), the mask liquid contains 5 ml Hepes of 0.1 mmol/ml concentration, per 50 ml. Preferred Method: The resuscitation of dermal cells in step (a) comprises (i) taking out dermal cells from liquid nitrogen tank, thawing at 37 degrees C water bath, inoculating to centrifugal tube containing 5-15 ml DMEM culture medium of 10% FBS, centrifuging and discarding the supernatant, adding 10-15 ml DMEM culture medium, centrifuging and discarding the supernatant, adding 7-8 ml dermal stem cell culture medium, transferring to a cell culture flask in an incubator containing 5% carbon dioxide for adherent culture, (ii) culturing the adherent cells until the confluence reaches 80%, adding 3-10 ml phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) to wash, adding 1-5 ml 0.25% trypsin digestion juice, digesting at 37 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, completely separating, adding 3-10 ml DMEM culture medium containing 5% FBS to neutralize, washing the cells in the culture flask and centrifuging in centrifugal tube at 1000 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes, adding DMEM culture medium re-suspending and counting, and obtaining dermal cells. The resuscitation epidermal cells in the step (b) comprises (i) taking out the epidermal cells from liquid nitrogen tank, thawing at 37 degrees C water bath, inoculating to a centrifugal tube containing 5-15 ml DMEM culture medium containing 10% FBS, centrifuging and discarding the supernatant, adding 10-15 ml DMEM culture medium, centrifuging and discarding the supernatant, adding 7-8 ml epidermal cell culture medium, transferring to a cell culture flask in an incubator containing 3-5% carbon dioxide for adherent culture at 35-37 degrees C, (ii) culturing the adherent cells until the confluence reaches 80%, adding 3-10 ml phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) to wash, adding 1-5 ml 0.25% trypsin digestion juice, digesting at 37 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, completely separating, adding 3-10 ml DMEM culture medium containing 5% FBS to neutralize, washing the cells in the culture flask and centrifuging in centrifugal tube, adding DMEM culture medium re-suspending and counting, and obtaining epidermal cells.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. The exhaust gas exhaled by the diver enters the exhaust pipe through the breathing mask, and is transmitted to the air purification backpack through the exhaust pipe. The airbag of a thin layer of hydrophilic and water-permeable porous material separates the exhaust gas from the natural water body. The exhaust gas is fully contacted with the natural water body through the hydrophilic and water-permeable porous material thin layer, so that the carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas dissolves into the natural water body. PN CN112759960-A
TI Light-curing eye-filling ash comprises first component (comprised of epoxy resin, curing agent-A, photoinitiator, talc, and titanium dioxide) and second component (comprised of polyamine, curing agent-B, and talc).
AU HAN Y
AE HESHAN BOLI COATING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HESH-Non-standard)
GA 202154284F
AB    NOVELTY - Light-curing eye-filling ash comprises first component and second component; the first component and the second component are mixed in mass ratio of 2.5-3.5:1.
   USE - The light-curing eye-filling ash is useful for automobile repairing and scratch coating construction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The light-curing eye-filling ash: can improves flexibility and adhesion of cured paint film; has lower toxicity; is less sensitive to moisture and carbon dioxide in air; and improves water resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the light-curing eye-filling ash comprising (1) mixing epoxy resin, vinylpyrrolidone and talc, stirring uniformly, adding titanium dioxide, 2-hydroxy-2-methyl-1-phenylacetone, and diphenyl-(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phosphorus oxide, and stirring to obtain the first component, and (2) mixing the polyetheramine, vinylpyrrolidone, and talc, stirring evenly, adding diethylenetriamine and vinylcaprolactam, and stirring to obtain the second component; and
   (2) use method of light-curing eye-filling ash comprising, when using, taking out first component and second component, mixing them evenly on the scraper, applying the coating on substrate to fill the sand holes, and using the LED lamp to irradiate for 3-20 seconds to complete the curing and crosslinking.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw materials of first component comprises 30-40 wt.% epoxy resin, 10-20 wt.% curing agent-A, 2-4.5 wt.% photoinitiator, 40-50 wt.% talc, and 1-3 wt.% titanium dioxide. The second component comprises 38-45 wt.% polyamine, 10-25 wt.% curing agent-B, and 40-50 wt.% talc. The epoxy resin is at least one of epoxy resin with epoxy value of 12 or epoxy value of 20; the curing agent-A is vinylpyrrolidone; the photoinitiator is mixture of 2-hydroxy-2-methyl-1-phenylacetone and diphenyl(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phosphorus oxide. The polyamine is mixture of two kinds of polyetheramine and diethylenetriamine; the curing agent-B is mixture of vinylpyrrolidone and vinylcaprolactam. Preferred Method: In the use method of light-curing eye-filling ash, the wavelength of the LED lamp is 380-420 nm and the power is 20-60 W.

The superfine slag slurry has an average particle size of 0.5-1 mu m, and comprises greater than 20% material having a particle size of 0.7-0.9 mu m. The thixotropic agent is polyamide modified hydrogenated castor oil, and nano red brick building powder, which is a nano red brick building powder with a particle size of 100-200 nm obtained by wet grinding building powder.

   (1) method for preparing liquid-liquid phase                separation nano-droplets constructed by non-ionic                surfactants and carbon quantum dots, involves                preparing mixed solution with non-ionic surfactant                and carbon quantum dots, placing, centrifuging, and                discarding the supernatant to obtain condensed                phase viscous solid separated from liquid and                liquid, and dispersing the viscous solid of the                coacervate phase in water to obtain a nano-droplet                solution separated from liquid and liquid;                and
   (2) use of liquid-liquid phase separation                nanodroplets constructed by the nonionic surfactant                and carbon quantum dots or the nonionic surfactant                and carbon quantum dots, nano-droplets prepared by                the liquid-liquid phase separation nano-droplet                preparation method, in construction of fluorescent                tracer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The nano-droplets are                  static electricity between the surfactant and the                  carbon quantum dot fluorescent nano-droplets                  constructed by interaction forces or hydrogen                  bonds. The nonionic surfactant is palm kernel                  olein (PKOo). The carbon quantum dots are                  selenium-doped carbon quantum dots (Se-CQDs). In                  the mixed solution, the concentration of PKOO is                  30-50 mM, and the concentration of Se-CQDs is 1-5                  mg/ml. The concentration of PKOO in the mixed                  solution is 40 mM, and the concentration of                  Se-CQDs is 2 mg/ml. The nonionic surfactant is                  PKOO and carbon quantum dots are selenium-CQDs.                  In the mixed solution, the concentration of PKOO                  is 30-50 mM, and the concentration of S-CQDs is                  1-5 mg/ml. The concentration of PKOO in the mixed                  solution is 34 mM, and the concentration of                  S-CQDs is 2 mg/ml. The time of placing is 12-48                  hours, preferably 24 hours. The rotation speed of                  the centrifugation is 5000-15000, preferably                  10000 revolutions per minute (rpm). The time of                  centrifugation is 10-60 minutes, preferably 30                  minutes.

PT P
PN CN112640912-A
TI Composite active oxygen bactericide used for            inactivating chlorine-resistant bacteria in water            supply portion, comprises potassium monopersulfate            composite salt, activator, additive agent, surfactant,            complexing agent and anti-caking agent.
AU LI J
AE TAIAN HONGFU CHEM CO LTD (TAIA-Non-standard)
GA 202148599B
AB    NOVELTY - Composite active oxygen bactericide comprises                65-80 wt.% potassium monopersulfate composite salt,                5-10 wt.% activator, 5-10 wt.% additive agent, 3-5                wt.% surfactant, 6-10 wt.% complexing agent and 1-3                wt.% anti-caking agent.
   USE - The bactericide useful for inactivating                chlorine-resistant bacteria in water supply portion                and water supply pipe network.
   ADVANTAGE - The bactericide produces a variety of active                ingredients, has wide range of killing                microorganisms such as bacteria, spores, viruses                and fungi, overcomes the shortcomings of relatively                weak chlorine oxidation ability, large dosage and                by-products, and avoids the shortcomings of poor                ozone persistence, decomposes organic matter into                water, carbon dioxide and simple inorganic matter,                degrades harmful secondary residues such as                pesticide residues, chloramine residues and                chloramine carcinogens, solves the acute and                chronic poisoning caused by chemical pollution,                removes algae and decompose algae toxins that are                difficult to degrade by conventional                disinfectants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the composite active oxygen                bactericide, involving (i) drying the                above-mentioned raw materials, and (ii) weighing                and uniformly mixing the above-mentioned raw                materials, and passing through a 200 mesh                sieve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Composition: The bactericide                  comprises 72 wt.% potassium monopersulfate                  composite salt, 7 wt.% activator, 7 wt.% additive                  agent, 4 wt.% surfactant, 8 wt.% complexing agent                  and 2 wt.% anti-caking agent. Preferred                  Components: The activator comprises sodium                  chloride and/or potassium chloride. The additive                  agent is sulfamic acid. The surfactant is sodium                  dodecylbenzenesulfonate. The complexing agent                  comprises sodium gluconate and/or sodium citrate.                  The anti-caking agent is sodium silicate.                  Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the drying                  treatment is carried out at 30-90 degrees C for                  1-5 hours.
DC D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PT P
PN CN112642259-A
TI Recycling tail gas FTrPSA (Full temperature range pressure swing adsorption) from chlorine-based silicon carbide-chemical vapor deposition (SiC-CVD) epitaxial process of alkane and silane reaction comprises e.g. adsorption purifying process.
AU WANG L
   ZHONG Y
   CHEN Y
   TANG J
   CAI Y
AE ZHEJIANG TIANCAI YUNJI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202143593H
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling tail gas FTrPSA from chlorine-based silicon carbide-chemical vapor deposition epitaxial process of alkane and silane reaction, comprises: (i) utilizing raw material gas i.e. methane (CH4) or propane (C3H8) as main carbon (C) source, utilizing silane (SiH4) as silicon (Si) source, adding hydrogen chloride (HCl) for chemical vapor deposition (CVD) to prepare tail gas based on silicon carbide (SiC)-chlorine-based epitaxial growth process, which is mainly composed of hydrogen (H2), HCl, C3H8/CH4, SiH4, chlorosilane (SiHmCln), trace of carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), ethane, light hydrocarbon components above carbon two and high hydrocarbon (C2+), chloroalkane (CHmCln), water (H2O), silicon dioxide (SiO2), and Si/C fine particles under normal pressure or low pressure and room temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling tail gas FTrPSA from chlorine-based silicon carbide-chemical vapor deposition epitaxial process of alkane and silane reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes full component recycling of tail gas; and reduces tail gas discharge, and is capable of making up blank of chlorine-based SiC-CVD epitaxial process tail gas processing technology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: (ii) pre-treatment process: subjecting raw material gas into pre-processing unit composed of dust remover, particle removing filter and oil mist catcher, removing dust, particle, oil mist, partial high chlorine silane, perchloric alkane and high hydrocarbon impurity under operation condition of 0.2-0.3 MPa pressure and room temperature, and subjecting purified raw material gas into chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process; (iii) chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process: directly pressurizing purified raw material gas from pre-treatment process to cold-heat exchange at 80-200 degrees C under 0.2-1 MPa, subjecting from bottom of spraying and absorbing tower, using mixed liquid containing chlorosilane/HCl as absorbent, spraying from top of spraying and absorbing tower to perform reverse mass transfer exchange with purified raw material gas, flowing-out absorption liquid enriched with chlorosilane and HCl from bottom of chlorosilane spraying and absorbing tower, subjecting subsequent multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process, flowing-out small amount of residual particles and perchlorsilane from bottom of tower, discharging non-condensable gas-I from top of absorbing tower, compressing, condensing and separating gas and liquid, subjecting non-condensable gas-II into middle-temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration process, and returning formed liquid to subsequent multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process; (iv) middle-temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration process: subjecting non-condensable gas-II to atleast 4 adsorption tower at 60-120 degrees C under adsorption pressure of 0.5-1.5 MPa, adopting pumping vacuum for desorption, flowing-out non-adsorption phase hydrogen-rich gas from adsorption tower top of adsorption state, directly subjecting into adsorption purifying process, flowing-out adsorption phase desorption gas from tower bottom of desorption vacuum state as concentrated gas, which is mixed with non-condensable gas-I after cold-heat exchange, subjecting to compression, condensation and gas-liquid separation of chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process, and further recycling effective component; (v) adsorption purifying process: precisely filtering hydrogen-rich gas from pressure swing adsorption concentration process, subjecting to adsorption purifying process composed of two or three adsorption towers at 5-30 degrees C under operation pressure of 0.5-1.5MPa for adsorption, purifying and removing small amount of SiH4, HCl, C3H8, C2+, CO2 and chlorosilane/chloroalkane, purifying purified methane, and subjecting to pressure swing adsorption hydrogen extraction process; (vi) pressure swing adsorption hydrogen extraction process: purifying methane from adsorption purifying process, directly or pressurized to 1-3 MPa, subjecting to pressure swing adsorption hydrogen purifying process composed of at least four adsorption towers under operation pressure of 1-3 MPa for 5-40 degrees C, where at least one adsorption tower is in the adsorption step, and the other adsorption towers is in desorption regeneration step, and the formed non-adsorption phase gas is ultra-pure hydrogen with purity of greater than or equal to 99.999-99.9999% (v/v), and subjecting hydrogen purification process; where the adsorbent in pressure swing adsorption hydrogen extraction process utilizes activated alumina, silica gel, activated carbon, aluminum silicate molecular sieve and/or carbon molecular sieve; when desorbing, realizing washing, or washing and vacuumizing mode; the desorbed gas is methane-rich gas, which can be directly utilized as fuel gas to return to heat exchange of tail gas recycling system, or subjecting into low-temperature rectifying and recycling methane to obtain methane whose purity is greater than or equal to 99.99% and returning to SiC-CVD epitaxial process to be used circularly; (vii) hydrogen purification process: directly subjecting ultra-pure hydrogen from pressure swing adsorption hydrogen extraction process through intermediate product storage tank for heat exchange at 50-500 degrees C, directly subjecting through pressure reducing valve to SiC-CVD epitaxial process for hydrogen needed pressure, subjecting into hydrogen purification process coupled by metal getter, palladium film or palladium film-metal getter at 50-500 degrees C under normal pressure or SiC-CVD process using hydrogen needed pressure condition for purifying, removing trace impurities to obtain final electronic grade hydrogen product, where purity reaches national and international semiconductor industry association (SEMI) of electronic grade hydrogen product standard, hydrogen purity of greater than or equal to 7-8N grade, cooling or step-down after heat exchange, or subjecting into electronic grade hydrogen product tank for storage, or subjecting through hydrogen product buffer tank, directly returning to step of SiC-CVD epitaxial process needed hydrogen, where the operation temperature of hydrogen purification process is determined by process of metal getter or palladium film, the service life of metal getter or palladium film is at least more than 2 years without regeneration, and the yield of obtained electronic grade hydrogen product is 75-85%; (viii) multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process: mixing absorption liquid from chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process with liquid formed after its compression, condensation and gas-liquid separation, subjecting multi-stage evaporation, and subjecting to condenser directly or after being compressed to 0.6-1 MPa, obtaining gas phase crude HCl gas, obtaining HCl product gas by HCl rectification refining, or returning to SiC-CVD chlorine-based epitaxial process for recycling, or liquefying as absorbent in chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process for recycling, flowing crude chlorosilane liquid from condenser, and subjecting into chlorosilane shallow-cooling rectifying process; (ix) chlorosilane shallow-cooling rectifying process: subjecting rough chlorosilane liquid from multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process to shallow-cooling rectifying process in chlorosilane at -35 to 10 degrees C under operation pressure of 0.6-2 MPa, flowing-out non-condensable gas-III from top of rectifying tower, and subjecting to propane/silane separating process, flowing-out from bottom of rectifying tower chlorosilane liquid, or mixed with HCl liquid as absorbent to return to chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process for circulating use, or it is used as external output of chlorosilane product; (x) propane/silane separating process: mixing non-condensable gas-III from shallow-cooling rectifying process in chlorosilane with heavy component fluid at bottom of HCl rectification refining process from multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process, subjecting to rectifying tower of propane/silane separating process at -10 to 60 degrees C under operation pressure of 0.6-2 MPa, flowing-out silane-rich gas from top of rectifying tower, subjecting to silane purifying process, and subjecting propane-rich fluid flowing-out from bottom of rectifying tower to subsequent propane refining process; (xi) silane purifying process: subjecting silane-rich gas from propane/silane separating process into silane purifying process composed of two rectifying towers, where the operation temperature of rectifying tower-I is -35 to -30 degrees C under operation pressure of 2-2.5 MPa, flowing-out low-boiling point hydrogen-rich gas-IV from rectifying tower-I, mixing hydrogen-rich gas in middle-temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration process after cold-heat exchange for further recycling hydrogen, subjecting effluent from bottom of rectifying tower-I into rectifying tower-II at -37 to -35 degrees C under operation pressure of 1.6-2 MPa, flowing-out SiH4 with purity of greater than or equal to 99.99% and yield of more than 95% from top of rectifying tower-II directly or through SiH4 metal getter purifier further purified (purity greater than or equal to 99.999 %) is used as raw material gas for SiC-CVD epitaxial process, returning a part of heavy component stream flowing from bottom of rectifying tower-II to propane/silane separating process further recycling effective component, directly discharging remaining part after processing; and (xii) propane refining process: cold-heat exchanging propane-rich fluid from bottom of rectifying tower in propane/silane separating process at 20-120 degrees C directly or by vacuum extracting to 0.6-2 MPa propane rectifying tower, discharging propane product gas from top of tower with purity of greater than or equal to 99.99% and yield of greater than or equal to 95%, and discharging heavy component fluid containing C2+/CO2 flowing-out from bottom of tower after treatment or used as fuel gas. The raw material gas comprises waste gas or tail gas containing hydrogen, hydrogen chloride, silane, propane/methane, and chlorosilane as main component generated in other semiconductor manufacturing process. The chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process comprises adding secondary chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process under higher HCl and chlorosilane working condition contained in purified raw material gas, compressing, condensing and gas-liquid separating non-condensable gas-I from chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process, subjecting or pressurizing formed non-condensable gas-I to 0.2-1 MPa, cold-heat exchanging at 80-200 degrees C, subjecting from bottom of absorption tower added secondary chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process, using chlorosilane/HCl mixed liquid as absorbent, spraying from top of secondary spraying and absorbing tower and carrying out reverse transmission exchange with non-condensable gas-I, flowing from bottom of secondary chlorosilane spraying and absorbing tower and enriching absorption liquid of chlorosilane and HCl, subjecting to subsequent multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process, flowing-out non-condensable gas-II from top of absorption tower directly or through compression, condensation and gas-liquid separation, forming non-condensable gas-II, subjecting to middle-temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration process, and returning formed liquid to multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process. The desorbed gas of pressure swing adsorption hydrogen extraction process enters low-temperature rectifying tower after being condensed, and methane flows-out from bottom of rectifying tower by using liquid as product. The non-condensable gas is hydrogen-rich gas, or returned to pressure swing adsorption hydrogen extraction process after cold-heat exchange, or directly entering hydrogen purification process, and further recycling H2, where H2 product gas yield is higher than 93%. In the middle-temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration process, the non-condensable gas-II formed after compression, condensation and gas-liquid separation from chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process or the secondary chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process is not compressed, but subjected to middle-temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration process by blower pressurizing compressed manner. The process is composed of two-stage pressure swing adsorption (PSA) system. The non-condensable gas-II is pressurized to 0.2-0.3 MPa by blower and enters from bottom of first-stage PSA adsorption tower. Tthe non-adsorption phase gas flowing-out from first-stage PSA tower top is hydrogen-rich gas, and subjected to adsorption purifying process. The desorbed gas from bottom of first-stage PSA tower (reverse placing, washing or vacuumizing) is subjecting discharged air into bottom of second-stage PSA adsorption tower, flowing enriched non-adsorption phase hydrogen-rich mixed intermediate gas from second-stage PSA tower, returning to first-stage PSA feeding gas-non-condensable gas-II to further recover methane hydrogen gas. The adsorption phase gas flowing-out from bottom of second-stage PSA tower is concentrated gas containing SiH4, chlorosilane, C3H8, C2+ and H2, CH4, mixed with non-condensable gas-I to return to compression, condensation and gas-liquid separation of chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process for further recycling the effective component. The shallow-cooling rectifying process in chlorosilane is composed of rectifying tower-I and II. The crude chlorosilane liquid flowing-out from multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process subjected to shallow-cooling rectifying tower-I at -35 to -10 degrees C under operation pressure of 1-2.5 MPa. The light component of non-condensable gas-III from rectifying tower-I, mainly H2 and CH4 component; or after passing cold-heat exchanger to 5-40 degrees C, purified methane hydrogen from adsorption purifying process is mixed and enters pressure swing adsorption hydrogen extraction process for further recycling H2 and/or CH4; or after passing through the cold-heat exchanger to 60-120 degrees C and directly or vacuum extracting to 0.5-1.5 MPa, the non-condensable gas-II after compression, condensation and gas-liquid separation in the chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process, and subjected to middle-temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration process to further recycle H2 and/or CH4 to further improve H2 and/or CH4 product gas yield. The heavy component fluid flowing-out from bottom of rectifying tower-I subjected to chlorosilane shallow-cooling rectifying tower-IIat -35 to -10 degrees C under operation pressure of 1-2.5 MPa. The chlorosilane liquid flows-out from bottom of tower, or mixed with HCl liquid as absorbent returns to chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process or secondary chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process for recycling, or as chlorosilane product. The non-condensable gas-III flowing-out from rectifying tower-II enters propane/silane separating process, so that the subsequent silane purifying process is only composed of rectifying tower, and SiH4 with purity of greater than or equal to 99.99% outflow from top of tower, and there is no non-condensable gas-IV, and reduces the propane/silane separation load. The silane purifying process uses cryogenic separation i.e. low-temperature rectifying tower replaces silane purifying process composed of two towers at -180 to -110 degrees C, and SiH4 liquid with purity of more than 99.99% directly flowing-out from bottom of low-temperature rectifying tower. The non-condensable gas-IV discharged from top of rectifying tower is mixed with hydrogen-rich gas from middle-temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration process after cold-heat exchange and pressurization, and subjected to adsorption purifying process to further recycle effective component. The alkane of chlorine-based SiC-CVD epitaxial process of alkane and silane reaction is only under methane working condition. The propane/silane separating process and propane refining process is cancelled. The non-condensable gas-III from shallow-cooling rectifying process in chlorosilane directly enters silane purifying process to obtain SiH4 product gas. The alkane of chlorine-based SiC-CVD epitaxial process of alkane and silane reaction is only under propane working condition. The propane/silane separating process and silane purifying process is cancelled. The non-condensable gas-III from shallow-cooling process in chlorosilane directly enters propane refining process to obtain propane product gas. The rectifying tower-II of silane purifying process can be replaced by pressure swing adsorption tower. The fluid from bottom of rectifying tower-I in silane purifying process is cold-heat exchange to 20-40 degrees C decompress to less than 1 MPa, subjecting to operation temperature of 20-40 under operation pressure of less than 1 MPa and composed of at least two adsorption towers of pressure swing adsorption tower. The adsorption tower is filled with diatomite, silica gel, activated carbon and adsorbent molecular sieve. The adsorption tower outflow purity is greater than or equal to 99.99% silane (SiH4) product gas. The yield is greater than or equal to 90-95%. SiH4 metal getter purifier is further purified (purity of greater than or equal to 99.999%) as raw material gas for SiC-CVD epitaxial process. The adsorption tower vacuumized and desorbed from bottom of adsorption tower, and is mixed with non-condensable gas-III generated in shallow-cooling rectifying process in chlorosilane, and returned to propane/silane separating process to further recycle C3H8 and SiH4 after cold-heat exchange and pressure regulation, where the corresponding yield respectively reaches more than 95-98%. The pressure swing adsorption tower in the middle-temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration process, the pressure swing adsorption hydrogen extraction process, the adsorption purifying process and the silane purifying process under the operation condition of adsorption pressure of greater than or equal to 0.6 MPa, and the pressure change in adsorption and desorption cycle operation process. The slow control is realized through pipeline program control valve and the adjusting valve connected between each adsorption tower to prevent the system pressure change caused by the air flow scouring adsorption tower bed layer and adsorbent powder generation, so that the process system operation is stable and safe.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E11 (Containing P and/or Si.); ; J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. MC E10-H04C4; E10-H04C5; E10-J02D; E10-J02D1; E11-Q01B; E11-Q02C; E31-A02C; E31-B03D; E31-N05C; E31-P03; E31-P06B; J01-E02B; J01-E03D; J01-E03J; J01-G; L04-A01A; L04-A01B; L04-C01; L04-C01B; L04-X06; U11-C01B; U11-C01J1

PT P
PN CN112624466-A; CN214087944-U
TI Pre-treating coal chemical industry wastewater comprises introducing coal chemical wastewater into oil removal and solid removal system, removing heavy oil, light oil and oil residue, and discharging pretreated purified water.
AU LIU C
AE SHAANXI DEXINXIANG ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 202140394M
AB    NOVELTY - Pre-treating coal chemical industry wastewater comprises (i) introducing coal chemical wastewater into oil removal and solid removal system, and removing heavy oil, light oil and oil residue; (ii) connecting degreasing and solid removal wastewater to acid-saturated extraction tower, and discharging the remaining acid gas from the top of the tower; (iii) introducing pre-deoiling wastewater to the top of the deacidification tower, removing dissolved low-boiling hydrocarbons, hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide in the pre-deoiling wastewater; (iv) dissolving phenol and homologues in the deacidification and deamination wastewater, and discharging dephenolic wastewater from the bottom of the tower with a small amount of extractant; (v) connecting dephenolization wastewater containing extractant to the water tower for distillation and recovering; and (vi) discharging pretreated purified water from the bottom of the resin adsorption tower to the biochemical treatment section.
   USE - The method is useful for pre-treating coal chemical industry wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the resource treatment and comprehensive utilization of coal chemical wastewater.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for pre-treating coal chemical industry wastewater (Drawing includes non-English language text).

The alkaline solution is sodium carbonate solution and sodium hydroxide solution. The mass fraction of alkaline chemical component of alkaline solution is 10-20 %, and alkaline solution is distilled water Preferred Method: In the (i), the pasting time is controlled within 1-1.5 hours and the heating temperature is controlled within the range of 75-85 degrees C. PN JP2021042150-A
TI New ionic liquid monomer containing triazole ring containing compound used for forming polymer for forming crosslinked polymer.
AU ONO T
   WATANABE K
AE UNIV OKAYAMA (UYOK-C)
GA 202126764T
AB    NOVELTY - An ionic liquid monomer containing triazole ring containing compound (I), is new.
   USE - New ionic liquid monomer used for forming polymer for forming crosslinked polymer (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to polymeric material, composite material, solid electrolyte, carbon-dioxide absorber, heat resistant resin, and chemical resistant resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The ionic liquid monomer effectively manufactures polymer containing ion liquid portion in a principal chain.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An ionic liquid monomer containing triazole ring containing compound of formula (I), is new.
   R1,R4 = divalent hydrocarbyl optionally containing functional groups other than azido and alkyne;
   R2,R5 = monovalent hydrocarbyl optionally containing functional groups other than H or azido and alkyne;
   R3 = monovalent hydrocarbyl optionally containing functional groups other than azido and alkyne;and
   X- = anionic component.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) polymer, which comprises at least one of 3-((1H-1,2,3-triazol-4-yl)methyl)-1H-1,2,3-triazol-3-ium repeating units of formula (II) and (III), and has weight average molecular weight of 1000 or more;
   (2) manufacture of polymer, which involves polymerizing a monomer component containing the ionic liquid monomer (I); and
   (3) crosslinked polymer, which is obtained by quaternizing the triazole ring in the polymer by reacting the polymer with a hydrocarbon compound containing two or more halogen groups, and has crosslinked structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (disclosed): The ionic liquid monomer is manufactured by reacting halogenated alkyne compound of formula (IV) and azide compound of formula: N3R3 (V) in solvent containing acetonitrile or tetrahydrofuran at 10-80 degrees C and 23 degrees C to obtain a triazole ring compound of formula (VI), reacting the compound (VI) with sodium azide and agitating the halogen atom of the compound (VI) in a solvent containing N, N-dimethylformamide or dimethyl sulfoxide at 20-100 degrees C to obtain azide-containing triazole ring compound of formula (VII), reacting the compound (VII) with the halogenated alkyne compound (IV) and quaternizing the triazole ring in the compound (VII) in solvent containing acetonitrile or tetrahydrofuran at 20-50 degrees C.X2 = halo.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the crosslinked polymer, the halogen ions involved in the quaternization of the triazole ring generated from the hydrocarbon compound containing two or more halogen groups are replaced with inorganic anion components or organic anion components other than halogen ions.
   Preferred Definitions:
   X- = inorganic anion component chosen from halo, AlCl4-, Al2Cl7-, BF4-, PF6-, ClO4-, NO3-, AsF6-, SbF6-, NbF6-, TaF6- or (CN)2N- or organic anion component chosen from organic carboxy anion, organic sulfonyl anion, organic sulfonyl carbanion, organic sulfonylimide anion, organic carboxy sulfonyl carbanion, organic carboxy sulfonyl imide anion or perfluoroalkyl.
   EXAMPLE - 8.50 mmol 1-Benzyl-4-butyl-3-(hex-5-yn-1-yl)-1H-1,2,3-triazol-3-ium iodide, 9.35 mmol lithium bis (trifluoromethanesulfonyl) imide and 25 ml pure water were added in a round bottom flask. The mixture was shielded from light with aluminum foil and stirred at 40 degrees C for 24 hours under an argon atmosphere. The mixture was washed with 50 ml pure water. This washing operation was repeated 3 times. The mixture was dried under reduced pressure and viscous pale yellow (85%) 1-benzyl-4-butyl-3-(hex-5-yn-1-yl)-1H-1,2,3-triazol-3-ium bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl) imide was obtained.
DC A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.)

The dispersant is zinc stearate, calcium stearate, magnesium stearate, paraffin wax, pentaerythritol stearate, butyl stearate and fatty amide are mixed in any ratio. The plasticizer is tributyl citrate, dioctyl phthalate, butylene sebacate and tricresyl phosphate mixed in any ratio. The nucleating agent is a mixture of polyhydrazide imine compounds, potassium benzenesulfonate and zinc phenyl phosphate in any ratio. The antioxidant is amine antioxidants, phenolic antioxidants, phosphite antioxidants and thioester antioxidants mixed in any ratio.

AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202111800H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing artificial steel residue aggregate, involves (i) using a ball mill for grinding steel-making tailings to obtain uniform steel residue powder, (ii) taking 5-15 parts mass Portland cement, 10-40 parts mass admixture fine powder and 50-80 parts mass steel residue powder, and uniformly mixing to obtain aggregate raw materials, (iii) preparing admixture aqueous solution, (iv) placing the aggregate raw materials in a ball machine, and uniformly spraying the admixture aqueous solution prepared in step (iii) into balls to obtain a spherical artificial steel residue aggregate, where the water-to-cement ratio of the orthopedic raw material and the admixture aqueous solution is 0.17-0.20, (v) placing the pelletized aggregate in a closed or natural environment for pre-curing, and (vi) placing the pre-cured aggregate in a carbonization device for carbonization treatment to produce artificial steel residue aggregate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing artificial steel residue aggregate for preparing concrete (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to operate, utilizes solid waste, absorbs and solidifies carbon dioxide, avoids burning, has no low carbon and ensures environmental protection.

The water-to-cement ratio of the orthopedic raw material and the aqueous solution of the admixture in step (iv) is 0.18. Preferred Components: The admixture fine powder used in step (ii) comprises fly ash, brick fine powder and/or muck powder. The additive is an aqueous solution with a concentration of 0.02-3 wt.% prepared with EDTA, disodium edetate, sodium carbonate and/or acetic acid. Preferred Conditions: In step (v), the pre-curing is carried out for 2-14 days. In carbonization treatment in step (vi), the concentration of carbon dioxide introduced is 100 vol.%, the air pressure is maintained at 0.2 MPa, and the carbonization is carried out for 4 hours. TI Method for producing hydrocarbons from a            reservoir, involves positioning a surface mounted            combustion heater adjacent to first well, a fuel and an            oxidant is supplied to combustion heater, the            pressurized water is supplied to heat exchanger.
AU CASTROGIOVANNI A G
   VASSILELLIS G
   SCHNEIDER M J
   CAPPER L
AE WORLD ENERGY SYSTEMS INC (WORL-Non-standard)
GA 202097059A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves positioning a surface                mounted combustion heater (405) adjacent to a first                well. A fuel and an oxidant is supplied to the                combustion heater. The pressurized water is                supplied to a heat exchanger (410) within the                combustion heater. The pressurized water is heated                to form a supercritical steam. The supercritical                steam is flowed through a first conduit (435) into                the reservoir (420), thermally stimulate the                reservoir. The hydrocarbons are recovered from the                reservoir. The surplus oxygen is flowed through the                first conduit into the reservoir. The oxygen is                flowed into the first well in an annulus of an                insulated conduit extending into the                reservoir.
   USE - Method for producing hydrocarbons from a                reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides mechanisms to improve oil                recovery by the introduction of heat and carbon                dioxide, and the cost of obtaining additional water                pressure is small compared to the benefits that                accrue from the increased density since liquid                phase pumping is highly efficient.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a steam                generator system.

PN CN211601196-U
TI Carbon dioxide air source heat pump machine set, has economizer whose gas outlet is connected with outlet of air side evaporator, and heating circulation loop and defrosting circulation loop controlled by opening and closing of expansion valve.
AU XU Q
AE JIFENG ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY JIANGSU (JIFE-Non-standard)
GA 202098879S
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a CO2 air source heat pump machine set, comprising a circulating loop formed by orderly connecting a compressor, a gas cooler, an expansion valve, an economizer, a heating device and an air side evaporator; the circulating loop is filled with CO2 refrigerant; the economizer comprises a liquid outlet and a gas outlet; the liquid outlet of the economizer is connected with the inlet of the heating device; the gas outlet of the economizer is connected with the outlet of the air side evaporator and then connected with the air return end of the compressor; the circulation loop comprises a heating circulation loop, a defrosting circulation loop; The switching of the heating circulation loop and the defrosting circulation loop is controlled by the opening and closing of the expansion valve. The utility model has automatic defrosting function, fast defrosting speed, less defrosting loss, effectively realizing efficient defrosting; At the same time, the heat source in the heating device adopts the return water in the gas cooler, which effectively reduces the defrosting energy consumption.

PN CN111635978-A
TI Reducing end point carbon and oxygen product for            electric furnace, involves smelting steel by last            furnace, and adding lime and scrap steel into the            furnace to melt scrap, adding carbon powder of steel            during melting process.
AU CHEN L
   WANG J
AE PANGANG GROUP RES INST CO LTD (ANSH-C)
GA 2020895526
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing end point carbon and oxygen product                involves smelting 10t of steel by last furnace, and                adding 18-25kg/t of lime and 40-50t of scrap steel                into the furnace to melt scrap. The carbon powder                40-60kg/t of steel is added during the melting                process with 2-5kg/t steel of slagging agent. The                oxygen is blown into the furnace at the same time.                The amount of oxygen blowing is 10-30m3/t steel.                The oxygen pressure is 0.8-1.2 megapascal. The                scrap steel material is completely melted. The                2.5-3.5kg/t steel of ferrosilicon is added. The                nitrogen is added during the melting period with                flow rate of 0.06-0.25Nm3/t minute. The carbon                dioxide is used in oxidation period, flow rate is                0.20-0.45Nm3/t minute. The basicity of steel slag                is controlled at 2.8-3.2, and smelting end                temperature is controlled at 1650-1680 degrees                C.
   USE - Method for reducing end point carbon and                oxygen product for electric furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduce end point carbon                and oxygen product that has remarkable                benefit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The scrap steel                  material is scrap steel with iron content is 80%.                  The slagging agent comprises 40-45 wt.% calcium                  oxide, 30-40 wt.% iron oxide, 10-12 wt.% aluminum                  oxide, 8-10 wt.% silicon dioxide, and rest are                  unavoidable impurities. The lime is active lime                  containing 85-90 wt.% calcium oxide. The carbon                  powder is carbon powder with carbon content of                  greater than or equal to 95%. The ferrosilicon                  contains 75% silicon, 22% iron, and rest are                  unavoidable impurities. The ferrosilicon is added                  in two times, first time is 2/3, second time is                  1/3, and time interval between the two additions                  is 15-30 minutes.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PN CN111620959-A
TI Preparation of Ganoderma lucidum mycelium polysaccharide used for, e.g. medicine for treating tumor by grinding dried Ganoderma lucidum mycelium, degreasing with organic solvent, separating, drying sediment, extracting, and adding enzyme.
AU WANG Z
   XIE T
AE GUANGZHOU YOROYAL BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202091678T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of Ganoderma lucidum mycelium polysaccharide comprises grinding dried Ganoderma lucidum mycelium to 8-10 mu m powder, adding organic solvent, degreasing, centrifugally separating, collecting lower layer sediment, and drying to 50-70 degrees C; adding 10-20 times its amount deionized water, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, filtering, adding extract with composite enzyme, subjecting to thermal enzymatic hydrolysis while stirring at speed of 400-500 revolutions/minute for 3-5 hours to obtain enzymolysis solution, heating to 100-120 degrees C for 1 hour, naturally cooling to 20 degrees C, and spray-drying to obtain polysaccharide coarse powder; and adding 8-10 times its amount absolute ethanol, stirring at speed of 600-800 revolutions/minute for 1-1.5 hours, centrifugally separating, removing supernatant liquid, and spray-drying.
   USE - The method is for preparation of Ganoderma lucidum mycelium polysaccharide used for preparing healthcare product and medicine for regulating immunity, treating tumor, cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases, delaying aging, lowering blood pressure, and relieving cough and allergy (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the waste of raw materials which saves production costs, and has extraction rate of over 15.4%. The product has purity of over 76.1%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The composite enzyme comprises cellulase, keratinase and hemicellulase at mass ratio of 5-7:1-3:3-5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is mixture of propylene glycol methyl ether, acetone and dimethyl sulfoxide at mass ratio of 2-3:4-7:1-2. The volume fraction of absolute ethanol is 95%.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant; Cytostatic; Cardiovascular-Gen.; Cerebroprotective; Antitussive; Antiallergic; Hypotensive; Nootropic; Dermatological.
