PN CN111621465-A
TI Constructing liver organoid by providing e.g. single-cell suspension of primary liver cell, and single-cell suspension of mesenchymal stem cell, adding to non-tissue cultured culture plate or ultra-low adherence culture plate, and culturing.
AU ZHOU M
   OU Y
AE LIVER BIOTECHNOLOGY SHENZHEN CO LTD (LIVE-Non-standard)
GA 202087719R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for quickly constructing liver organoid involves providing single-cell suspension of primary liver cell, single-cell suspension of mesenchymal stem cell and single-cell suspension of liver non-parenchymal cell, adding to a non-tissue cultured culture plate or an ultra-low adherence culture plate, according to the ratio of viable cells of 4:0:(0.125-4) or 4:1:(0.125-4), and the cell density is 0.5x 105-2.5x 105/cm2 after mixing evenly, and placing in a 5 volume/volume% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 24 plus minus 2 hours to obtain liver organoids.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing liver organoid, which is useful in drug metabolism, drug toxicity and toxicology studies for participating in the mechanism research of liver injury and repair, and studies of inflammatory and infectious diseases of the liver, and used in the research of biological artificial liver and pathogenic mechanism (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method quickly constructs liver organoid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a liver organoid, which is constructed by the above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The primary hepatocyte single cell suspension is prepared taking the frozen primary hepatocytes, quickly thawing at 37 degrees C, adding to a 10-fold volume of resuscitation medium preheated at 37 degrees C, mixing by inversion, standing for 30-45 minutes, centrifuging and removing the supernatant, and adding the plating medium to obtain the primary hepatocyte single cell suspension. The mesenchymal stem cell single cell suspension is prepared by taking primary mesenchymal stem cells, inoculating 4500-5500 viable cells/cm2 into a culture plate treated with tissue culture, performing expansion culture in a mesenchymal stem cell expansion medium, and when the cell fusion degree is 75-85%, performing trypsin digestion to obtain a mesenchymal stem cell single cell suspension. The primary mesenchymal stem cell is any one of human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell, human bone marrow mesenchymal stem cell and human adipose mesenchymal stem cell. The mesenchymal stem cell amplification culture medium is prepared by adding 15 volume/volume% fetal bovine serum and 1 volume/volume% streptomycin to an alpha -MEM basic culture medium, and digesting by using the pancreatin within 10 generations to respectively obtain mesenchymal stem cell single cell suspensions from 1-10 generations. The single-cell suspension of liver parenchymal cells is prepared by collecting primary liver non-parenchymal cells, inoculating into the culture plate treated with tissue culture at a density of 0.5x 105/cm2, and when the cell fusion degree is 75%-85%, performing trypsin digestion to obtain a single cell suspension of liver nonparenchymal cells. The trypsin digestion is performed within 5 generations to obtain single cell suspensions of hepatic nonparenchymal cells of the first to fifth generations, respectively. Preferred Medium: The resuscitation medium is supplemented with components in minimum essential medium (MEM) medium: 1 volume/volume% internal transcribed spacer (ITS) universal culture additive, 2 mmol/l L-glutamine, 10 mu g/l epidermal growth factor, 18 mg/l hydrocortisone, 40 mu g/l dexamethasone, 1 volume/volume% penicillin, 10 volume/volume% fetal bovine serum and 2-4 m/v% bovine serum albumin. The plating medium is the William's E medium comprising 1 volume/volume% ITS universal culture additive, 2 mmol/l L-glutamine, 10 mu g/l epidermal growth factor, 18 mg/l hydrocortisone, 40 mu g/l dexamethasone, 1 volume/volume% penicillin and 5 volume/volume% fetal bovine serum. Preferred Product: The liver organoid has one or more characteristics chosen from (a) having a cell density of 500-5000 cells/organoid, (b) the organoids, which are spherical and solid having a diameter of 150-750 mu m, corresponding to the thickness of 5-30 layers of cells, (c) the cells, which are in contact with each other in three-dimensional space, and (d) showing the inherent function of liver tissue.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B01-B02; B01-C01; B02-P04; B02-S; B04-B04D2; B04-B04D4; B04-B04H; B04-F02; B04-H06A; B04-L05; B10-B02J; B11-C04G; B11-C08E1; B12-K04E3; B12-K04G2; B14-N12; D05-A02C; D05-H08B; D05-H09

PN CN111592434-A
TI Energetic substance for replacing firework gunpowder, comprises mixture of four residues comprising e.g. non-esterified bis-d-glucose residues, one hydroxy-esterified bis-glucose residue, and two hydroxy-esterified bis-glucose residues.
AU ZHOU Z
   WANG Q
AE SHENZHEN XINZHONGGONG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202086709E
AB    NOVELTY - An energetic substance comprises nitrogen, carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen. The energetic substance is a macromolecular chain composed of dehydrated d-glucose with different esterification degrees, wherein, each glucose residue is combined by the dehydration of the hydroxyl groups at positions 1,4 and there are three hydroxyl groups on each glucosyl ring in the dehydrated d-glucose macromolecular chain without esterification. Each hydroxyl group can undergo an esterification reaction with nitric acid separately. The energetic compound is composed of a mixture of four residues. The nitrogen content of the energetic compound depends on the mixing ratio of the four residues. The residues comprises non-esterified bis-d-glucose residues, one hydroxy-esterified bis-glucose residue, two hydroxy-esterified bis-glucose residues, and three hydroxy-esterified bis-glucose residues.
   USE - Energetic substance for replacing firework gunpowder.
   ADVANTAGE - The energetic substance produces decomposition products which are non-toxic and harmless gases e.g. carbon dioxide, water vapor and nitrogen, and does not produce solid pollutants. The energetic substances are smoke-free, non-toxic, have non-irritating odor and harmless to human body, and do not produce residue or inhalable particles and do not pollute the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The non-esterified bis-d-glucose residue comprises d-glucose-containing compound ((C6H10O5)2 ) of formula (I) and has nitrogen content of 0%. The one hydroxy-esterified bis-glucose residue comprises dinitrate-containing compound ((C6H9(NO2)O5)2) of formula (II) and has nitrogen content of 6.76%. The two hydroxy-esterified bis-glucose residue comprises tetranitrate-containing compound ((C6H8(NO2)2O5)2) of formula (III), and has nitrogen content of 11.1%. The three hydroxy-esterified bis-glucose residue comprises pentanitrate-containing compound ((C6H7(NO2)3O5)2) of formula (IV), and has nitrogen content of 14.14%. Preferred Property: The effective nitrogen content of the energetic substance is 10.0-14.0%.

PN CN111313041-A; CN111313041-B
TI Preparation of nickel iron hydroxide            electrocatalyst used e.g. in zinc-air battery, by            dissolving iron salt, fluorine-containing compound and            urea in water, dipping foam nickel, hydrothermally            reacting, cooling, and washing with detergent.
AU SUN C
   MENG X
   NOT P N
AE BEIJING NANOENERGY & NANOSYSTEMS INST (BJNN-C)
   BEIJING NANOENERGY & NANOSYSTEMS INST (BJNN-C)
GA 202059291X
AB    NOVELTY - A nickel iron hydroxide electrocatalyst is                prepared by dissolving iron salt,                fluorine-containing compound and urea in water,                stirring to obtain homogeneous solution, dipping                foam nickel, hydrothermally reacting, cooling, and                washing with detergent.
   USE - The method is used for preparing nickel iron                hydroxide electrocatalyst in zinc-air battery                and/or electrolytic cell and in self-energy supply                system for electrolyzing water to produce hydrogen,                electrolyzing carbon dioxide to produce carbon                monoxide and electrochemically reducing ammonia to                produce nitrogen (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has excellent catalytic activity,                hydrogen evolution catalytic activity and oxygen                evolution catalytic activity. The system realizes                effectively use of intermittent renewable                energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                self-energy supply system comprising friction                nano-generator, zinc air battery and electrolytic                cell. The friction nano-generator is used for                collecting mechanical energy and converting into                electric energy. The zinc air battery is used for                storing electric energy and supplying to                electrolytic cell. The electrolytic cell is used                for electrolyzing electrolytic solution. The                positive electrode of zinc air battery and                electrode of electrolytic cell is nickel iron                hydroxide electrocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of                  iron salt, fluorine-containing compound and urea                  in homogeneous solution is 0.01-2 mol/L, 0.05-5                  mol/L and 0.05-4 mol/L. The iron salt is                  iron(III) nitrate nonahydrate and/or ferric                  chloride hexahydrate. The fluorine-containing                  compound is ammonium fluoride and/or sodium                  fluoride. The foam nickel is sheet-shaped. The                  detergent is deionized water and/or ethanol. The                  foam nickel has thickness of 1.6 plus minus 0.6                  mm and surface density of 350 plus minus 25 g/m2.                  The volume ratio of foam nickel with homogeneous                  solution is 20-100:1, preferably 30-40:1. The                  nickel iron hydroxide has 5-20 wt.%, preferably                  10-15 wt.% iron content, 10-60 wt.%, preferably                  20-40 wt.% nickel content, 10-84.9 wt.%,                  preferably 40-60 wt.% oxygen content, and 0.1-10                  wt.%, preferably 0.5-2 wt.% fluorine content. The                  electrode of electrolytic cell is activated                  nickel iron hydroxide catalyst. Preferred Method:                  Hydrothermal reaction is done at 90-140 degrees C                  for 3-10 hours, preferably 110-130 degrees C for                  5-8 hours. The activation is oxygen evolution                  activation or hydrogen evolution activation.                  Oxygen evolution activation is done for 6-14                  hours at over-potential of 0.25-0.65 V,                  preferably 8-12 hours at 0.35-0.5 V. Hydrogen                  evolution activation is done for 6-14 hours at                  over-potential of - 0.45 to - 0.05 V, preferably                  8-12 hours at - 0.35 to - 0.15 V.

PT P
PN CN111249296-A; CN111249296-B
TI Antifungal composition comprises antifungal A drug and synergistic B drug, which has reduced minimum inhibitory concentration of drug rapamycin, improved antifungal effect of rapamycin.
AU REN B
   ZHU C
   CHENG L
   ZHOU X
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 202053760G
AB    NOVELTY - Antifungal composition comprises antifungal A drug and synergistic B drug, where the fungal inhibitory concentration coefficient FICI is (MIC of drug A in composition/MIC of drug A when used alone) + (MIC of drug B in the composition/MIC of drug B when used alone) less than or equal to 0.5.
   USE - Used as antifungal composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The antifungal composition: has reduced minimum inhibitory concentration of drug rapamycin, improved antifungal effect of rapamycin, reduced amount of rapamycin in antifungal treatment process, and reduced toxic and side effects; control fungal infection secondary to immunosuppression; expand existing antifungal drug reserve pool; provides new medication basis for patients who take immunosuppressive agents and secondary fungal infection after organ transplantation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for measuring interactive antifungal activity of antifungal composition, comprising (i) incubating Candida albicans SC5314 in RPMI 1640 medium at 37 degrees C, humidity 80%, and 5% carbon dioxide, (ii) dissolving rapamycin and ivermectin in dimethyl sulfoxide to concentration of 10 mg/ml and 20 mg/ml, and storing in refrigerator until use, (iii) determining minimum MIC concentration, and (iv) determining minimum fungal concentration MBC.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of flowchart of interactive antifungal activity determination of antifungal composition. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The antifungal A drug is rapamycin, and the synergistic B drug is ivermectin, the mass ratio of rapamycin and ivermectin is (0.0005-0.004):(0.5-64), preferably 0.0005:1 or 0.00025:1, further preferably 0.000125:1 or 0.00003125:1. The mass ratio of rapamycin and ivermectin is 0.00001562:1 or 0.00000781:1. The anti-fungal A drugs include macrolide immunosuppressants, macrolide immunosuppressant analogs, macrolide immunosuppressant derivatives, macrolide immunosuppressant prodrugs, macrolide immunosuppressant metabolites and/or macrolide immunosuppressant active salts, the synergistic drug B includes perimegalide antiparasitic drugs, macrolide antiparasitic analogs, macrolide antiparasitic drugs derivatives, macrolide antiparasitic prodrug, macrolide antiparasitic metabolite and/or macrolide antiparasitic active salt. The mass ratio of macrolide immunosuppressant and macrolide antiparasitic agent is (0.0005-0.004):(0.5-64). Preferred Method: In the step (iii), specifically includes (i) the liquid 10 mg/ml rapamycin and 20 mg/ml ivermectin solution are formulated into rapamycin and ivermectin solution, (ii) inoculate Candida albicans cells into a 96-well plate, 98 mu l per well, add 1 mu l rapamycin and ivermectin solution, the combination method of the solution is the first drug rapamycin is arranged longitudinally at a dilution concentration of 1 mu l per well in a 96-well plate from top to bottom, and the second drug ivermectin is arranged horizontally from left to right at a dilution concentration, 1 mu l per well, record the concentration of rapamycin and ivermectin in each well, set up a group with 2 mu l per well of rapamycin or ivermectin added separately and a blank control group without any drugs, and make three parallel 96-well plates, the results are averaged, (iii) test the OD value at 600 nm after incubating the 96-well plate at 37 degrees C for 16-20 hours, and (iv) MIC value is the lowest drug concentration that can inhibit fungal growth by 100%, compare OD value of each drug combination with the blank control to determine the MIC value, determine whether the anti-fungal A drug and the synergistic B drug are synergistic, additive or antagonistic by inhibiting the concentration factor FICI, FICI = (A/MIC A in the MIC drug combination alone) + (B/MIC B in the MIC drug combination alone), if the FICI value is less than or equal to 0.5, there is a synergistic effect between the anti-fungal A drug and the synergistic B drug, if the FICI value is 0.5-4.0, there is no obvious interaction between the activities of the anti-fungal A drug and the cooperative B drug, if the FICI value is not greater than 4.0, the antifungal A drug and the synergistic B drug have an antagonistic effect; in the step (vi), further includes (a) using checkerboard method to detect drug interactions and MIC, and calculate FICI, the combination method of drug solution is first drug rapamycin is arranged longitudinally in a 96-well plate at a dilution concentration from top to bottom, 1 mu l per well, and the second drug ivermectin is arranged laterally at a dilution concentration from left to right, 1 mu l per well, record the concentration of rapamycin and ivermectin in each well, set up a group with 2 mu l per well of rapamycin or ivermectin diluted separately, and a blank control group without any medicine, (b) pipette 100 mu l of bacterial solution in each well of the 96-well plate that has completed the MIC test and mix until there is no precipitation to form a uniform bacterial suspension, (c) pipette 1 mu l of the bacterial solution from each well of the 96-well plate after blowing, and transfer the seeds to the yeast extract peptone glucose agar plate without antibacterial drugs, and analyze the growth of the colony after incubating at 37 degrees C for 24 hours, (d) anti-fungal MBC value of macrolide lipid immunosuppressants is determined as the minimum drug concentration that can clearly kill fungi without fungal growth, the wells of each drug combination were compared with the blank control colony morphology to determine the MBC value.
   ACTIVITY - Antifungal.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.); C02 (Heterocyclic.); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

PN CN111054376-A; CN111054376-B
TI Catalyst for catalytic combustion of purified            terephthalic acid oxidation tail gas, comprises carrier            which is honeycomb ceramic, supporting at least one            metal composite oxide of cobalt, manganese, gallium            and/or indium.
AU JIANG J
   MIAO C
   LU Y
   SUN Q
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP SINOPEC (SNPC-C)
GA 202036698A
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises carrier which is                honeycomb ceramic, supporting at least one metal                composite oxide of cobalt, manganese, gallium                and/or indium.
   USE - Catalyst for catalytic combustion of purified                terephthalic acid oxidation tail gas                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is economical, has high activity                and excellent anti-toxicity, and can achieve                efficient catalytic combustion removal of exhaust                gas such as purified terephthalic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of catalyst, which involves                preparing cobalt metal salt, manganese metal salt,                gallium metal salt and/or indium metal salt into                aqueous solution, adding sodium carbonate, sodium                bicarbonate, sodium hydroxide, and ammoniacal                alkaline precipitation agent to obtain cobalt,                manganese, gallium and/or indium metal composite                oxide particles, uniformly dispersing particles in                aqueous phase to form coating liquid, and coating                on honeycomb ceramic; and
   (2) catalytic combustion of purified                terephthalic acid oxidation tail gas, which                involves contacting purified terephthalic acid                oxidation tail gas with catalyst, passing mixture                of nitrogen and oxygen or air at 200-450 degrees C,                and catalyzing combustion of organic substances                such as methyl acetate, xylene and dibromomethane                in purified terephthalic acid oxidation tail gas to                produce carbon dioxide, water, hydrogen bromide and                elemental bromine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  cobalt and manganese is 1:(0.1-10). The molar                  ratio of cobalt and gallium/indium is 1:(0.1-1).                  The molar ratio of cobalt and gallium is                  1:(0.1-0.5). The molar ratio of cobalt and indium                  is 1:(0.1-0.5). The amount of cobalt, manganese,                  gallium and/or indium metal composite oxide used                  for coating is 50-200 g/L, preferably 100-170                  g/L. The concentration of coating liquid is 10-36                  wt.%. Preferred Component: The cobalt salt and                  manganese salt for preparing the metal composite                  oxide particles are chosen from nitrate, acetate                  and halide. Preferred Condition: The metal                  composite oxide particles are prepared at 10-90                  degrees C. When preparing metal composite oxide                  particles, a precipitant having pH of 9-11 is                  added. Preferred Process: The method further                  involves drying the coating on honeycomb ceramic                  at 100-125 degrees C for 1-25 hours, and baking                  at 300-600 degrees C for 3-10 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves extinguishing fire by gaseous extinguishing agent with asphyxia, chemical suppression characteristics. Water mist cooling process is performed. The gaseous extinguishing agent is responded after occurrence of lithium ion battery thermal runaway. Thermal buoyancy generated by flame and concentration of smoke are reduced. The water mist is released. A determination is made to check whether mist particles reach surface of a lithium ion battery under fire absence condition. Surface temperature of the lithium ion battery is reduced for cooling the battery. The gaseous extinguishing agent is selected from one of heptafluoropropane, perfluoro hexyl ketone agent and oxygen isolating carbon dioxide fire-extinguishing agent.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficiently and quickly extinguishing fire and reducing surface temperature of the lithium ion battery for cooling the battery so as to avoid occurrence of ignition and linked thermal runaway of a battery group. TI Recycling various effective component in refinery            catalytic device dry exhaust gas by removing refinery            catalytic device dry gas impurities, cooling dry gas,            sending into methane absorption tower and sending low            temperature liquid absorbent.

GA 202030235Y
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling various effective component in                refinery catalytic device dry exhaust gas involves                removing refinery catalytic device dry gas                impurities water, carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide,                nitric oxide/nitrogen dioxide, oxygen and acid gas,                cooling dry gas to suitable inlet temperature of                methane absorption tower, sending dry gas into                methane absorption tower, sending low temperature                liquid absorbent at tower top of methane absorption                tower, absorbing methane and hydrocarbon component                in methane absorption tower, sending liquid phase                output by methane absorption tower into                demethanizer rectifying tower, separating methane                component with other hydrocarbon components,                sending liquid phase of rectifying tower output to                methane absorption tower as low-temperature liquid                absorber, cooling, sending other portion into                ethylene rectification tower, separating ethylene                and ethane, sending liquid phase into ethane                rectifying tower, and separating ethane and 3C, 4C                and its above components.
   USE - The method is used for recycling various                effective component in refinery catalytic device                dry exhaust gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high hydrogen concentration and                easy to recycle in gas output from methane                absorption tower, separates and recovers methane,                2C, olefins and alkanes, improves resource                utilization rate of catalytic dry gas, and                maximizes value of catalytic dry gas.

TI Foam used e.g. in rigid core materials for            composites contains polyamide and ionomer comprising            zinc neutralized ethylene acid copolymer that is            reaction product of monomers of ethylene,            monocarboxylic acid and unsaturated dicarboxylic            acid.
AU YAN J
   CHOU R T H
   HAUSMANN K
   WANG Z
AE PERFORMANCE MATERIALS NA INC (DUPO-C)
   YAN J (YANJ-Individual)
   CHOU R T H (CHOU-Individual)
   HAUSMANN K (HAUS-Individual)
   WANG Z (WANG-Individual)
   PERFORMANCE MATERIALS NA INC (DUPO-C)
   PERFORMANCE MATERIALS NA INC (DUPO-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 2020249735
AB    NOVELTY - Foam comprises 55-98 wt.% polyamide (PA) and                2-45 wt.% ionomer comprising a zinc neutralized                ethylene acid copolymer, where the zinc neutralized                ethylene acid copolymer is the polymerized reaction                product of ethylene monomer, monocarboxylic acid                monomer and unsaturated dicarboxylic acid monomer,                and 30-70 mole% of total acid units of the ionomer                are neutralized.
   USE - The foam is useful in light weight/temperature                resistance parts for the automotive industry,                thermal insulation materials for construction and                packaging, rigid core materials for                composites.
   ADVANTAGE - The foam: is free of polyurethane; has a                density of 0.03-0.5 g/cc, and an expansion ratio of                at least 1500%; and has increased melt                viscosity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing the foam, comprising (a) injecting                supercritical blowing agent of carbon dioxide                and/or nitrogen into a vessel to mix with a foam                precursor mixture, where the vessel is at a                temperature and pressure above the supercritical                temperature and pressure of the blowing agent, and                the foam precursor mixture comprises 55-98 wt.% PA                and 2-45 wt.% ionomer, and (b) producing the foam                by dropping the pressure below the pressure of the                supercritical blowing agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The foam comprises:                  75-97 wt.% PA; and 3-20 wt.% ionomer. The PA                  comprises: aliphatic PA; or PA6, PA11 and/or                  PA12. The zinc neutralized ethylene acid                  copolymer comprises at least 50% ethylene                  monomer, at least 5-25 wt.% monocarboxylic acid                  monomer and 2-20 wt.% unsaturated dicarboxylic                  acid monomer, based on the total wt.% of the                  monomers present in the copolymer. The                  unsaturated dicarboxylic acid monomer comprises                  maleic anhydride, maleic anhydride monomethyl                  ester, maleic anhydride monoethyl ester, maleic                  anhydride monopropyl ester, and/or maleic                  anhydride monobutyl ester. The monocarboxylic                  acid monomer comprises methacrylic acid and/or                  acrylic acid.
   EXAMPLE - Ultramid B33 L (RTM: Polyamide 6) was dry                blended at room temperature with 3% ionomer and                Irganox 1098 (RTM: Phenolic antioxidant), and then                compounded using a Brabender twin-screw extruder at                240-250 degrees C, followed by pelletizing in a                separate pelletizing unit. Independent super                floater was utilized for dry blending. The                Brabender twin-screw extruder, which had a                temperature of 240-250 degrees C, included                sequential temperature zones of 240 degrees C/245                degrees C/245 degrees C/245 degrees C/245 degrees                C/245 degrees C. Ionomer was a zinc ionomer of an                ethylene/methacrylic acid/maleic acid monoethyl                ester (at a weight ratio of 83/11/6) terpolymer                with a melt index of 1.1 as measured at 210 degrees                C, where 50 mole% of the acid moieties were                neutralized with zinc cation.

PN CN110817919-A
TI Preparing calcium oxide slag recycled calcium oxide balls by pulverizing calcium carbide slag, impregnating calcium carbide slag powder, drying, mixing and stirring acid solution with coke and calcium carbonate precipitation and calcining.
AU SU X
   ZHANG H
   LIU F
   TANG F
AE XINJIANG GUOFENG JIAHE ENVIRONMENTAL PRO (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 202018153J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing calcium oxide slag recycled calcium oxide balls, involves (a) pulverizing calcium carbide slag with a pulverizer, sieving through a 80-120 mesh sieve to obtain calcium carbide slag powder, (b) impregnating the calcium carbide slag powder with acid solution and water to remove impurities, processing by microwave reaction, filtering and separating to obtain silicon ferrosilicate impurity (A) and mixed solution (B), (c) drying the impurity (A) and grinding to obtain a ferrosilicon primary product, passing the mixed solution (B) through carbon dioxide gas to a suitable pH of the solution, and filtering to obtain the calcium carbonate precipitate (C) and the waste acid solution, and (d) mixing and stirring the acid solution with coke and calcium carbonate precipitation (C) to obtain calcium carbonate balls, and calcining the calcium carbonate balls in a high-temperature sintering furnace to obtain calcium oxide balls used in industry.
   USE - The method is used for preparing calcium oxide slag recycled calcium oxide balls.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and environmentally-friendly, utilizes calcium carbide slag, manufacture as a value-added product, and improves the economic benefits of enterprises.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid solution in step (b) is a citric acid and/or ascorbic acid and/or acetic acid solution. The acid solution in step (d) is the waste acid solution and/or other acid solutions generated in step (c). Preferred Property: In step (c), the carbon dioxide gas is passed through until the solution pH is 10-12. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the microwave reaction in step (b) is 10-50 degrees C, and the reaction time is 5-50 minutes. The temperature in the step (d) of the high-temperature sintering furnace is 1000-1400 degrees C, and the time is 0.5-4 hours.

PN CN110759956-A
TI Preparing high-purity stachyose comprises e.g.            cleaning and drying the raw materials, adding to water,            extracting the juice, adding yeast to sugar solution,            and performing aerobic fermentation, adding quicklime            heating, and filtering.
AU SONG J
   WANG D
   WAN R
   ZHANG Q
   WANG S
   HUANG X
AE ANHUI MINZHEN BIOENGINEERING CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202013765X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity stachyose comprises (1)                cleaning the raw materials, and then drying; (2)                adding the raw materials to water at 60-90 degrees                C to extract the juice to obtain a sugar solution;                (3) adding yeast to the sugar solution, adjusting                the pH of the solution to 4-6, and performing                aerobic fermentation at a temperature of 30-40                degrees C for 20-30 hours; (4) adding quicklime to                the fermentation broth until the solubility of                quicklime reaches saturation; (5) after heating to                the temperature of 50-55 degrees C, and passing                carbon dioxide to generate calcium carbonate                precipitation; (6) filtering to remove impurities;                (7) passing the filtration solution to a                nanofiltration membrane for purification to obtain                a purified solution; (8) passing the purified                solution into an ion exchange resin to obtain a                stachyose solution; and (9) concentrating and                drying the stachyose solution to obtain                stachyose.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity                stachyose.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple steps, convenient                operation, easy process control, and convenient                large-scale industrial production, shows the                experimental results that the yield of stachyose                can reach 80.39% and the purity is above                92.36%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (2), specifically                  comprises adding the raw materials to water with                  a mass of 3-5 times of the raw materials, and                  performing leaching at 60-90 degrees C for 1-2                  hours, and separating the extract from the                  residue by filtration, and collecting the                  extract, adding 2-3 times the mass of the residue                  to the residue, leaching at 60-90 degrees C for                  1-2 hours, filtering, mixing the extracts twice,                  and obtaining the sugar solution. In the step                  (8), the ion exchange resin includes an anion                  exchange resin and a cation exchange resin; and                  then passing the purification solution into the                  anion exchange resin and the cation exchange                  resin in order to obtain a stachyose solution.                  Preferred Components: The raw material is at                  least one of Stachys siebolidii, Chinese                  artichoke, Chinese artichoke, and fructus                  momordicae. The amount of yeast added is 0.3-0.5%                  of the mass of the sugar solution. The amount of                  the carbon dioxide substance is 3-5 times the                  amount of the quicklime substance. The                  nanofiltration membrane has a molecular weight                  cutoff of 1000-2000 Daltons. The anion exchange                  resin is D301 anion exchange resin and the cation                  exchange resin is D113 cation exchange                  resin.

PN CN110699142-A; CN110699142-B
TI Preparing iron sintered biomass fuel for replacing fossil fuels, involves mixing biomass powder with iron-dressing tailing slag-silica tailing slag mixed powder, pressing, and performing high-temperature carbonization treatment.
AU JI Z
   FAN X
   GAN M
   LI H
   NI J
   CHEN X
   HUANG X
   YUAN L
   HUANG B
   WU Y
   LV W
   TANG Q
   WANG G
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 202008396Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing iron sintered biomass fuel by synergistically recycling tailings slag and waste biomass involves mixing the biomass powder with the iron-dressing tailing slag-silica tailing slag mixed powder and pressing it into a biomass particle. The biomass particles are placed in a protective atmosphere, and then subjected to low-temperature pretreatment and high-temperature carbonization treatment.
   USE - Method for preparing iron sintered biomass fuel for replacing 40-70 wt.% fossil fuels for iron sintering (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare iron sintered biomass fuel of high quality that has a combustion heat value of 26-33 millijoule/kg, a volatile content of 0.5-3%, a specific surface area of 10-25 m2/g, a porosity of 15-25%, and an apparent density of 1.5-2.2 g/cm3. The biomass fuel makes full use of the inert component silica in the slag to weaken the thermochemical reaction. The comprehensive effect of iron oxides promoting the efficient cracking of volatiles can replace the use of biomass fuels with a high proportion of fossil fuels, and achieve efficient reduction of sulfur oxides (SOx), nitrogen oxides (NOx), and carbon dioxide from the source.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The biomass includes agricultural waste and forestry processing waste. Preferred Conditions: The main components in the mixed iron tailing slag-silica tailing slag mixed powder are 60-80 wt.% silica and 20-40 wt.% iron oxide. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of biomass powder and the iron powder tailings slag-silica tailings slag mixed powder is 96-99.9:0.5-4. The particle size of the biomass powder satisfies a mass percentage content of not less than 80% of 0.1-1 mm. The particle size of the mixed iron-tailing slag-silica tailings slag powder meets a mass percentage content of not less than 90% of 0-0.01 mm. The pressing process involves adopting mold-assisted molding under pressure of 300-1000 kg/cm3 at 150 degrees C for 0.5-2.5 minutes, where size of the press molding is 3 to 5 mm. The low temperature pretreatment process involves raising the temperature with rate of 15-25 degrees C/minute to 200-300 degrees C and maintaining it for 30-60 minutes. In the high-temperature carbonization treatment process, the temperature is raised to 600-800 degrees C with a heating rate of 2-8 degrees C/minute and the temperature is maintained for 15-30 minutes.

AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a fire-proof heat-insulated electric control box, its structure comprises a convex ring, a mounting plate, a fixing screw, a longitudinal rod, a door lock, a laterally positioned rod, door, a supporting bracket and a protective shell, after the convex end is fixedly connected with the protective shell. The utility model claims a fire-proof heat-insulated electric control box, the electric appliance element required for the mounting on the mounting plate, mounting rod, so that the electric control, when installing the indoor fire occurs, flame approximation control box, double-metal sheet is under the action of external temperature, starting the thermal expansion occurs, so as to generate shape change, opening the air hole so that the carbon dioxide released in the air cavity groove out, black dawn rocks around the flame is extinguished, and the inner frame and the partition frame can isolate external heat to prevent the high temperature external to the control box, effectively preventing fire, high temperature outside of the control element of the box is burning off.

PN US2019390385-A1; US10697099-B2
TI Method for manufacturing multi-use hoop framing board and accessories in hooping devices of embroidery applications, involves assembling multi-use hoop framing board for performing two hoop framing operations from printed board pieces.
AU LUCCHESE S
AE LUCCHESE S (LUCC-Individual)
   TEX INC (TEXT-Non-standard)
GA 2019A73906
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining precision cut and drilled extruded acrylic or polypropylene embroidery board pieces (110). Hardware is assembled with a multi-use hoop framing board (100). Board pieces and/or accessories are printed to provide for pre-determined alignments and utilization of the embroidery board pieces in desired operating units of two hoop framing operations. The multi-use hoop framing board is assembled for performing two hoop framing operations from the printed board pieces and the hardware. Precision cut extruded acrylic embroidery board pieces are obtained. The extruded acrylic pieces are cut by a carbon dioxide laser cutter.
   USE - Method for manufacturing a multi-use hoop framing board and accessories in hooping devices of embroidery applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving permit precision in dimensions for quick and easy transition between hooping applications. The method enables improving durability and efficiency of use for diverse purposes when a constructed board is utilized to set-up hoops to frame fabric for embroidery. The method enables adjusting sizes of clothes and an area for embroidery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a top perspective view of an acrylic blank.
   Multi-use hoop framing board (100)
   Extruded acrylic or polypropylene embroidery board pieces (110)

PN US2019390892-A1; US10941977-B2
TI Modular box assembly for shipping e.g. hot goods            at ambient temperature, has channel tab frictionally            engaged to shoulder channel to secure and suspend inner            box within box cavity between top end and bottom            end.
AU WALTERMIRE J
   OTT P
   SOLLIE G
AE PRATT RETAIL SPECIALTIES LLC (PRAT-Non-standard)
GA 2019A73832
AB    NOVELTY - The assembly (100) has a side panel extended                from a top end (102) to a bottom end (104). A                shoulder is attached to the side panel and extended                inward from the side panel into a box cavity that                contains temperature maintaining material e.g.                carbon dioxide dry ice or heat emitting material.                The shoulder is provided with a first sub-shoulder                and a second sub-shoulder to form a shoulder                channel. An inner box is placed in the box cavity                and provided with a channel tab. The channel tab is                frictionally engaged to the shoulder channel to                secure and suspend the inner box within the box                cavity between the top end and the bottom                end.
   USE - Modular box assembly for shipping hot goods,                chilled goods and frozen goods at ambient                temperature.
   ADVANTAGE - The assembly reduces manufacturing stress                around corners of an insulated box by using                insulated panels, thus preventing insulated panel                ripping or tearing phenomenon.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a                modular box assembly.
   Modular box assembly (100)
   Assembled box (101)
   Top end (102)
   Bottom end (104)
   Insulated panel (140)

PT P
PN CN209838384-U
TI Circulation-type carbon dioxide oil displacement injection device, has low pressure return branch whose end is connected with injection plunger pump and another end is connected with recycling pipe, where recycling pipe is connected with liquid storage tank.
AU ZHANG S
   TUO C
   ZHANG Y
   WANG K
   YANG T
AE SHAANXI YANCHANG PETROLEUM GROUP CO LTD (SXYP-C)
GA 202000234R
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to oil field oil displacement injection CO2 device for well field, specifically relates to a circulation type CO2 oil displacement injection device. circulation type CO2 oil displacement injection device, comprising a CO2 oil displacement injection system, the CO2 oil displacement injection system comprises a liquid storage tank and injection plunger pump via the pipeline, the pipeline after injection plunger pump is divided into 2 branches. the first branch is injection pipe; the second branch is a reflux branch, reflux branch is connected with a liquid storage tank; the return branch comprises high pressure reflux branch, a low pressure return branch and recycling pipe, wherein the high pressure return branch, one end of low pressure return branch is respectively connected with the injection plunger pump; the other end is connected with the recycling pipe, the recycling pipe is connected with the liquid storage tank. The utility model has simple structure, easy to use, at the same time recycling CO2 waste gas after being processed into liquid storage tank for secondary utilization, reducing cost, improving greatly the degree of use of the CO2.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))
MC H01-D06C; Q49-A; Q49-V35

PT P
PN EP3581683-A1
TI Electrolytic device used in production of hydrogen, comprises electrolytic cell for splitting water into product comprising hydrogen, and product comprising oxygen, and passive recombiners comprising catalyst, operated at preset temperature.
AU WOLF E
   REINER A
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 2019A5847G
AB    NOVELTY - An electrolytic device (1) comprises electrolytic cell(s) (2) for splitting water (W) into a product (PH) comprising 98 vol.% or more hydrogen, and a product (PO) comprising 98 vol.% or more oxygen, and passive recombiner(s) (10) comprising a catalyst for removing oxygen from the product (PH) and/or passive recombiner(s) (11) comprising a catalyst for removing hydrogen from the product (PO), where both can be operated at temperatures of 60 degrees C or less.
   USE - Electrolytic device is used for hydrogen electrolysis in production of hydrogen which is used in Sabatier process with carbon dioxide in production of methane for fuel cell, and as starting material in hydrogenation reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrolytic device has high efficiency, and enables efficient production of hydrogen having desired purity of 99 vol.% or more, with reduced consumption of energy for cleaning. The carrier material is porous, and has desired corrosion resistance and heat resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for operation method of the electrolytic device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the electrolytic device with passive recombiners.
   Electrolytic device (1)
   Electrolytic cell (2)
   Passive recombiners (10,11)
   Products (PH,PO)
   Water (W)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: The recombiner (10) and/or recombiner (11) can be operated at ambient temperature, preferably 35 degrees C or less, more preferably 25 degrees C or less. The electrolytic cell can be operated at a pressure of 1-7 bar.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst comprises platinum or palladium. The catalyst is applied as a layer on a carrier material comprising a ceramic. The catalyst is present as a pellet bed in the recombiners (10,11).
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC E11-N; E31-A02A; J03-B02; N07-G; X16-C17A

PN CN209800894-U
TI Environment-friendly refrigerant conveying composite air conditioner soft pipe, has reinforcing layer whose outer side is formed with high temperature resistant rubber layer, where high temperature resistant rubber layer and reinforcing layer are arranged.
AU WANG L
   TAO P
   WANG Z
   HAN Y
   YUE X
   FENG C
   SHEN W
AE NANJING ORIENTLEADER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202000649P
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a composite air conditioner soft pipe for the delivery of new-type environmental friendly refrigerant, comprising an inner layer of fluorine plastic gas barrier layer, at least one layer reinforced layer of fluorine plastic outer side of the gas barrier layer, and/or a reinforced heat resistant rubber layer outer side of the layer, or at least one layer of high temperature resistant rubber layer of fluorine plastic outer side of the gas barrier layer, a reinforced layer of the outer side of the high temperature resistant rubber layer. fluorine plastic between -50 ~ 180 material has stable property, which can avoid carbon dioxide blasting decompression caused by damage, keeping the hose having excellent performance stability. high modulus reinforced layer, can keep the hose of low expansion, stable conveying refrigerant. The hose of the utility model with a penetration resistance, high and low temperature resistance, bend resistance, low expansion, absorbent pulse better anti-vibration and excellent comprehensive performance, high anti-refrigerant permeation ability and operation effectively prevent leakage from the refrigerant pipe connected with the hose, long and stable providing reliable guarantee for the air conditioning system.

PN CN110527706-A
TI Observing dynamic changes of mouse oocytes by            dissolving low-melting agarose powder in culture            solution, placing mouse oocytes into culture liquid            drop, covering with agarose solution, cooling and            adding observation solution.
AU CHENG J
   SUN F
   FAN X
AE UNIV NANTONG (UYNT-C)
GA 2019A2962N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for observing dynamic changes of mouse                oocytes using agarose having low melting point                involves (i) placing low-melting agarose powder in                the culture solution and dissolving at 70 degrees C                to obtain low-melting agarose solution having 1                %mass, (ii) dropping culture liquid drop into Petri                dish, placing mouse oocytes into the culture liquid                drop to obtain living cell culture liquid drop,                (iii) covering living cell culture droplets with                low melting point agarose solution having mass                volume concentration of 1% to obtain low melting                point agarose solution comprising living cells with                mass volume concentration of 0.67%, (iv) cooling                low-melting agarose solution at room temperature to                obtain living cell agarose gel, and (v) adding                observation solution to Petri dish comprising live                cell agarose gel and placing the observation                solution in the observation box of the living cell                workstation for real-time observation at 37 degrees                C having 5% carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for observing dynamic                changes of mouse oocytes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses agarose which is                cost-effective and nontoxic, and has high sieving                and high transparency property. The method is                simple to perform.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (v) further                  involves changing the observation liquid using                  pipette to realize observation of mouse oocytes                  in different liquid environments. The method                  involves using 10 mu l culture liquid droplets                  and 20 mu l low melting point agarose solution.                  Preferred Component: The culture solution is                  potassium simplex optimized medium (KSOM)                  solution. The mouse oocytes is chosen from                  oocytes in the foaming stage, oocytes in the                  first meiosis stage and oocytes in the second                  meiosis stage. The oocytes in the living cell                  culture droplet are on the same horizontal                  plane.

PN CN110531413-A; CN110531413-B
TI Small fault advanced three dimensional            visualization modeling method, involves establishing            three dimensional model of fault according to parameter            information of position, trend, inclination and            nature.
AU WANG B
   SUN H
   LIU S
   ZHANG H
   LI X
   XING S
   GONG Z
   WANG Z
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2019A3839P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves arranging three-component                geophones to adjust center heights of coal roadway                to receive seismic signals. Coal seam is located on                a left side of the coal roadway to generate a                seismic source by using a carbon dioxide gun. A                three-dimensional spatial coordinate system is                established in each center height of the coal                roadway. Seismic source location and                three-component geophone location are classified                into the three-dimensional spatial coordinate                system. Seismic records of seismic sources are                analyzed. Reflected type groove wave signal is                extracted from a local disk in each seismic record.                Diffraction body wave signal is determined by using                time-frequency domain Hilbert polarization imaging                process. Drop of fault is determined by an opposite                disk according to a position of diffraction point.                A three dimensional model of the fault is                established according to parameter information of a                position, trend, inclination and nature.
   USE - Small fault advanced three dimensional                visualization modeling method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables accurately detecting                information of each element under three-dimensional                space of the fault in the roadway so as to realize                three dimensional visualization of the fault.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a small                fault advanced three dimensional visualization                modeling system.

AU LI Q
   MAO Y
   FENG Y
   SUN Y
   HUANG H
   ZHANG A
   YANG W
   QU G
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 201997868D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing a unidirectional acyclic energy substance flow model for an electric-air hybrid energy marine micro-energy system. An energy substance flow relationship is analyzed. Energy conversion coefficient of each unit is determined. Production and consumption of materials are analyzed to determine material conversion coefficients and increments. An energy-mass balance equation of the marine micro-energy system is established through an energy-material coupling matrix. A joint optimization scheduling model for an energy supply system and a production process system is established according to the energy-mass balance equation.
   USE - Marine micro-meter energy system and production technology system constraints optimization scheduling method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables fully considering energy material balance relationship during production process, realizing correlation between the energy supply system and the production process system, reducing production process system cost and carbon dioxide emission penalty cost as an optimization target, formulating an optimization scheduling strategy to optimize an overall system energy flow guidance to effectively achieve goal of complementation energy sources, and improving optimization scheduling efficiency.

PN CN110195180-A; CN110195180-B
TI High thermal conductivity die cast magnesium alloy            comprises aluminum, lanthanum, strontium, manganese,            and magnesium and other unavoidable impurities.
AU XU S
   TANG W
   ZHANG C
   ZHENG M
   XIE Y
AE CHINA BAOWU IRON & STEEL GROUP CO LTD (BAOS-C)
   BAOSHAN IRON & STEEL CO LTD (BAOS-C)
GA 2019777954
AB    NOVELTY - High thermal conductivity die cast magnesium                alloy comprises 5-7 wt.% aluminum, 5-8 wt.%                lanthanum, 0.3-1 wt.% strontium, 0.2-0.5 wt.%                manganese, and magnesium and other unavoidable                impurities (balancing amount).
   USE - Used as high thermal conductivity die cast                magnesium alloy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing the high thermal conductivity die-cast                magnesium alloy, comprising (i) preheating the                crucible in a heating furnace, and spraying the                release agent on the inner wall of the crucible,                (ii) placing the pure magnesium ingot into the                crucible and passing the mixed gas of sulfur                hexafluoride and carbon dioxide, heating up to                completely melt the pure magnesium, maintaining it                for a while, cooling to 740 plus minus 10 degrees                C, and adding pure aluminum, magnesium-lanthanum                intermediate alloy, magnesium-strontium                intermediate alloy and magnesium-manganese                intermediate alloy, (iii) after completely melting,                reducing the temperature to 720 plus minus 10                degrees C, stirring, and allowing to stand after                removing the slag, (iv) cooling the crucible and                taking out the magnesium alloy ingot, and (v)                melting the magnesium alloy ingot and insulating in                a furnace of a die casting machine, and injecting                the molten magnesium alloy into a die casting                mold.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The microstructure                  comprises a magnesium matrix and a precipitated                  second phase, and the second phase includes an                  aluminum-lanthanum phase, an aluminum-strontium                  phase, and aluminum-manganese phase. The                  aluminum-strontium phase comprises at least                  aluminum-strontium (Al4Sr). The thermal                  conductivity at room temperature is greater than                  or equal to 108 W/m-K. The yield strength at room                  temperature is greater than or equal to 190 MPa,                  tensile strength is greater than or equal to 274                  MPa, and elongation is greater than or equal to                  6%. The creep rate at 200 degrees C and 70 MPa is                  less than or equal to 50x 10-9/second. Preferred                  Composition: The content of lanthanum element is                  5-6 wt.%. The content of the strontium element is                  0.3-0.5 wt.%. Preferred Method: In the step (i),                  the crucible is preheated to 300 plus minus 10                  degrees C, and spraying the release agent on the                  inner wall of the crucible. In the step (ii),                  when the enthalpy temperature reaches 500 plus                  minus 10 degrees C, placing the pure magnesium                  ingot in the crucible and introducing the sulfur                  hexafluoride and carbon dioxide mixed gas,                  raising the temperature to 760 plus minus 10                  degrees C, after completely melting the pure                  magnesium, maintaining it for 10 plus minus 5                  minutes. In the step (iii), the mixture is                  stirred for 5-15 minutes, and after allowing to                  stand for slag, allowing to stand for 15-25                  minutes. In the step (iv), cooling the crucible                  by an annular jet cooling system, and after                  solidifying the surface of the magnesium alloy,                  completely submerging the crucible in water to                  remove the magnesium alloy ingot from the                  crucible. In the step (v), the holding                  temperature is 700 plus minus 10 degrees C, the                  molten magnesium alloy is injected into the die                  casting mold at a shot speed of 45-55 m/seconds,                  the temperature of the die-casting mold is 200                  plus minus 10 degrees C, and the casting pressure                  is 50 plus minus 5 MPa.
DC M22 (Casting; powder metallurgy - including foundry moulding, moulding machines, patterns, moulds, cores and metal casting (B22).); M26 (Non-ferrous alloys - including alloy production and composition (C22C).); P53 (Metal casting, powder metallurgy (B22).)
MC M22-G03D2; M26-A01; M26-A01A; M26-A01M; M26-A01R; M26-A01X; M26-A02; M26-A02A; M26-A02M; M26-A02R; M26-A02X; M26-B10; M26-B10A; M26-B10R; M26-B10X; P53-B01C2; P53-V03A; P53-V03B; P53-V05E; P53-V09

PN CN110051804-A
TI Gout conditioning composition comprises Ganoderma lucidum spore oil, chitooligosaccharide, semen coicis oil, rhizoma smilacis glabrae extract, Angelica extract and glycyrrhizic acid.
AU LI J
   CHUAN J
   LIU S
AE GUIZHOU LINGKANGSHI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 201967030T
AB    NOVELTY - A gout conditioning composition comprises 8-16 pts. wt. Ganoderma lucidum spore oil, 3-7 pts. wt. chitooligosaccharide, 12-20 pts. wt. semen coicis oil, 10-18 pts. wt. rhizoma smilacis glabrae extract, 2-8 pts. wt. Angelica extract and 12-22 pts. wt. glycyrrhizic acid.
   USE - Gout conditioning composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has synergistic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A gout conditioning composition comprises 8-16 pts. wt. Ganoderma lucidum spore oil, 3-7 pts. wt. chitooligosaccharide, 12-20 pts. wt. semen coicis oil, 10-18 pts. wt. rhizoma smilacis glabrae extract, 2-8 pts. wt. Angelica extract and 12-22 pts. wt. glycyrrhizic acid. The Ganoderma lucidum spore oil and semen coicis oil are prepared via supercritical carbon dioxide extraction. The chitooligosaccharide is prepared by decomposing chitosan in hydrogen peroxide solution. The tuckahoe extract is prepared by taking tuckahoe, slicing, adding 65-75 wt.% ethanol, refluxing and extraction, taking slag, adding water, soaking, refluxing and extracting, concentrating and drying, and mixing. The Angelica extract is prepared by taking Angelica, crushing, adding 94-96 wt.% ethanol solution, soaking, refluxing and extracting, taking dregs, adding 94-96 wt.%, and refluxing and extracting. The glycyrrhizic acid is prepared by taking licorice, crushing, adding 68-72 wt.% ethanol solution, soaking, and carrying out ultrasonic extraction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The gout conditioning composition comprises 10-14 pts. wt., preferably 12 pts. wt. Ganoderma lucidum spore oil, 4-6 pts. wt., preferably 5 pts. wt. chitooligosaccharide, 14-18 pts. wt., preferably 16 pts. wt. semen coicis oil, 12-16 pts. wt., preferably 14 pts. wt. rhizoma smilacis glabrae extract, 2-6 pts. wt., preferably 5 pts. wt. Angelica extract and 15-20 pts. wt., preferably 17 pts. wt. glycyrrhizic acid. Preferred Method: The Ganoderma lucidum spore oil supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is carried out under 15 MPa for 3 hours at carbon dioxide flow rate of 25 L/hour. The chitooligosaccharide is prepared by taking 8-12 wt.% chitosan, adding 8-12 wt.% hydrogen peroxide solution, stirring at 55-65 degrees C for 0.8-1.2 hours, filtering, taking dregs, adding 4-6 times of 4-6 wt.% hydrogen peroxide solution, stirring for 1-2 hours, filtering, combining filtrates, decolorizing, decompressing and concentrating, adding 2.5-3.5 times of 94-96 wt.% ethanol solution, standing at 4-6 degrees C for 10-14 hours, filtering, washing lower layer with 94-96 wt.% ethanol solution, and drying. The semen coicis powder is crushed into 45-55 mesh prior extraction. The tuckahoe extract is prepared by slicing tuckahoe, adding 7-9 times of 65-75 wt.% ethanol, soaking for 10-14 hours, refluxing and extracting for 2 times for 1.8-2.2 hours and 0.8-1.2 hours, respectively, concentrating, spray drying to obtain powder A, taking dregs, adding 7-9 times water, soaking for 10-14 hours, refluxing and extracting 3 times for 0.8-1.2 hours each time, combining water extracts, concentrating, spray drying to obtain powder B, and mixing with A. The Angelica extract is prepared by crushing Angelica, adding 5-7 times of 94-96 wt.% ethanol solution, soaking for 5-7 hours, refluxing and extracting for 1.8-2.2 hours, taking dregs, adding 5-7 times of 94-96 wt.% ethanol solution, repeating for 3 times, combining extracts, and concentrating. The glycyrrhizic acid is prepared by taking licorice, crushing and sieving into 20 mesh, adding 18-22 times of 68-72 wt.% ethanol solution, soaking for 85-95 minutes, ultrasonic processing at frequency of 18 KHz for 55-65 minutes, filtering, taking filtrate, and concentrating. Preferred Composition: The composition is in the form of soft capsule, tablet or powder.
   ACTIVITY - Antigout.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. TI Single transition metal atom catalyst comprises            single atoms of transition metal as active component            and carbon black as carrier, single atoms of transition            metal dispersed on surface, carbon black based on total            weight of transition metal.
AU ZHANG T
   YANG H
   SHANG L
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 201957351E
AB    NOVELTY - Single transition metal atom catalyst                comprises single atoms of transition metal as                active component and carbon black as carrier,                single atoms of transition metal dispersed on                surface and inside of carbon black based on total                weight of transition metal catalyst of single atom                is 100%, transition metal atoms is 0.5-6                wt.%.
   USE - Single transition metal atom catalyst for                electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction to                produce carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The single transition metal atom catalyst has                strong coordinating effect, high conductivity,                surface activity, selectivity and faraday                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing single transition metal atom                catalyst, which involves:
   (A) adding transition metal salt and organic                ligand is dissolved in organic solvent;
   (B) mixing them uniformly, obtain clear                transparent solution;
   (C) adding carbon black, uniformly mixing to                obtain reaction solution, dipping, reacting at                40-80 degrees C for 2-8 hours, drying to obtain                crude product;
   (D) dissolving crude product in argon gas                atmosphere, raising temperature to 300-580 degrees                C to 2-10 degrees C per minute heating speed,                calcining for 1-5 hours; and
   (E) cooling to room temperature to obtain                transition metal single atom catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transition metal                  atom is selected from chromium, manganese,                  cobalt, copper, zinc, niobium, molybdenum,                  ruthenium, rhodium, pladium, silver, cadmium,                  osmium, iridium or platinum. The carbon black is                  selected from Ketjenblack EC-300J carbon black or                  Vulcan XC-72 carbon black. The organic ligand is                  selected from phenanthroline, bipyridine,                  2-melamine or phenylalanine. The organic solvent                  is selected from ethanol or dimethylsulfoxide.                  The transition metal salt is selected from                  transition metal chloride, transition metal                  nitrate, transition metal acetate, transition                  metal sulfate. Preferred Conditions: The ratio of                  amount of transition metal salt and organic                  ligand in reaction liquid is 1:3-1:20. The mass                  concentration of carbon black in reaction                  solution is 10-70mg per milliliter. The drying                  temperature is 60-190 degrees C and drying time                  is 6-20 hours.

PN CN109878497-A
TI Hybrid cooperative modeling method, involves assigning high-frequency torque to driving motor, supplying electric energy from storage battery to driving motor, where power of engine is larger than required power of running vehicle.
AU XIN K
   DONG Z
   YANG C
   SHAO J
   WANG G
   XIE T
AE HANTENG AUTOMOBILE CO LTD (HTAT-C)
GA 201954982W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing an engine model. Rotating speed data of a sensor is detected. Carbon dioxide in tail gas is discharged by a gas analyzer. Detected data is transmitted to a controller through a bus. A drive motor model is established to drive rotating speed of the motor detected by the sensor. An acceleration pedal and a position of a brake pedal are detected by a position sensor. A vehicle speed and a position signal are sampled. A response time and phase response time of an engine is obtained. A high- frequency torque is assigned to a driving motor, where a power of the engine is larger than a required power of a running vehicle. Electric energy is supplied from a storage battery to the driving motor by a subsequent driving process.
   USE - Hybrid cooperative modeling method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables converting a power of the engine into electric energy by a generator and stored by the storage battery, effectively realizing mixed power automobile emission and equalization of fuel property.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of an engine model.

PT P
PN CN109824194-A
TI Treating high-salt and high-chemical oxygen demand organic wastewater, by introducing wastewater into pretreatment tank, salvaging, adding chemical oxygen demand remover, introducing into activated carbon filter tank and exporting effluent.
AU ZHOU C
   HU C
   JIA J
   WANG L
   GUO J
   SUN J
AE YIXING INT ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION ZONE TECHNOLOGY DEV CO L (YIXI-Non-standard)
GA 2019505850
AB    NOVELTY - Method for treating high-salt and high-chemical oxygen demand (COD) organic wastewater, involves (i) introducing the high-salt and high-COD organic wastewater into a pretreatment tank to filter and remove impurities, and salvaging the surface floating matter, (ii) introducing the wastewater discharged from the pretreatment tank into a reaction tank, adding COD remover, where the mass ratio of the wastewater to the COD remover is 1:0.02-1:0.08, stirring at room temperature, converting organic matter into carbon dioxide and water, converting ammonia nitrogen into nitrogen, and converting organic phosphorus into phosphate, and removing it, (iii) introducing the reaction tank discharge liquid into a activated carbon filter tank and removing the sedimented solid matter by filtration, and (iv) exporting the effluent of the activated carbon filter tank to a evaporator for flash desalination, where the condensed water after desalination reaches the discharge standard.
   USE - The method is useful for treating high-salt and high-COD organic wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures removal rate of salt and COD in wastewater up to 97%, can be processed continuously and has a large processing capacity, which is suitable for popularization and use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: In step (ii), the stirring is carried out for 1-100 hours, preferably 20-40 hours. In step (iii), the activated carbon in the activated carbon filter tank is replaced once every 3-5 days. In step (iv), the evaporator is a multi-effect or mechanical vapor recompression (MVR) evaporator. Preferred Components: The content of the inorganic salt in the high-salt and high-COD organic wastewater is 1-25 wt.%, and the content of COD is 2000-100000 mg/l. In step (i), the filter is an ultrafiltration membrane, and an external pressure type hollow fiber membrane having a pore diameter of 1-10 mu m is preferably used. Preferred Composition: The COD remover comprises 10-55 parts mass ferrate (VI), 0.1-5 parts mass magnesium chloride, 0-25 parts mass inorganic flocculant, 0-15 parts mass organic flocculant, 0.1-15 parts mass sodium sulfite, 0.1-5 parts mass sodium nitrite, 1-2.5 parts mass sodium metabisulfite, 10-55 parts mass calcium oxide and 10-25 parts mass adsorbent, where the above powder components are uniformly mixed into a mixture.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ferrate comprises sodium ferrate or potassium ferrate. The organic flocculant is poly(diallyldimethylammonium chloride). The inorganic flocculant comprises an inorganic salt having aluminum or iron as a cation and nitrate or sulfate as an anion, preferably iron (III) sulfate, polyferric sulfate, iron (III) nitrate, aluminum nitrate and/or aluminum sulfate. The adsorbent comprises activated carbon (preferred), molecular sieve or macroporous adsorption resin, where the activated carbon has an average particle size of 300-400 mesh.

PT P
PN CN109682872-A
TI Method for preparing laser induced titanium dioxide/three-dimensional porous graphene composite photoelectrode, involves dissolving titanium source into polyimide precursor solution and spin-coating viscous solution.
AU INVENTOR U
AE UNIV QINGDAO AGRIC (UNQA-C)
GA 201941028Q
AB    NOVELTY - A laser induced titanium dioxide/three-dimensional porous graphene composite photoelectrode preparing method involves dissolving a titanium source into the polyimide precursor solution to obtain viscous solution. The viscous solution is spin-coated onto the surface of the indium tin oxide conductive glass and after dehydration condensation by heating to form titanium ion doped polyimide composite film on the surface of the indium tin oxide conductive glass. The pre-designed photoelectrode pattern is engraved onto the surface of the titanium ion doped polyimide composite film by a carbon dioxide laser cutting engraving machine to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing laser induced titanium dioxide/three-dimensional porous graphene composite photoelectrode.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing laser induced titanium dioxide/three-dimensional porous graphene composite photoelectrode with better current intensity and increased phosphorus concentration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a laser-induced titanium dioxide/three-dimensional porous graphene composite photoelectrode based label-free homogeneous photoelectrochemical pesticide residue sensing strategy, comprises titanium dioxide/three-dimensional porous graphene composite photoelectric electrode and phosphate buffer solution containing thioacetylcholine, acetylcholinesterase, and different concentrations of malathion is used as a detection solution and the phosphate buffer solution containing acetylcholine hydrolyzate thiocholine is used as a signal solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The titanium source is titanium acetylacetonate or titanium dichloride. Preferred Method: The method further involves adding 1.5-2.0 g 4,4-diaminodiphenyl ether to 20-30 mL N,N-dimethylformamide solution and magnetically stirring to dissolve completely to obtain solution and adding 1.3-1.5 g pyromellitic anhydride to the solution and continue stirring to form a polyimide acid solution and adding 0.3-0.6 g titanium source to the polyimide acid solution and continue stirring to form a titanium ion-containing polyimide precursor solution, spin-coating the titanium ion-containing polyimide precursor synthesized onto the surface of the treated indium tin oxide conductive glass to form a uniform titanium ion-containing polyimide precursor film on the surface of the indium tin oxide conductive glass, vacuum-insulating the indium tin oxide conductive glass modified by the titanium ion-containing polyimide precursor at 75-100 degrees C for 1 hours and dehydrating and condensing the polyimide acid at a high temperature to form a polyimide, that is, a titanium ion doped polyimide composite film is prepared, cooling the titanium ion doped polyimide composite film at room temperature, placing in a carbon dioxide laser cutting engraving machine working platform, engraving the pre-designed photoelectrode pattern to the titanium ion doped polyimide on the surface of the composite film to obtain the finished product.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The label-free homogeneous photoelectrochemical pesticide residue sensing strategy is prepared by preparing 1.0-5.0 mM thioacetylcholine solution and 1.0-10 mU/mL acetylcholine and 0.1-0.5 M phosphate buffer solution, adding 10-30 mu L acetylcholinesterase solution, 10-30 mu L thioacetylcholine solution, 10-30 mu L different concentrations of malathion solution and 30-60 mu L phosphate buffer solution to the centrifuge tube, placing the centrifuge tube at 37 degrees C for 30-60 minutes to obtain a signal liquid, transferring the signal liquid to a titania/three-dimensional porous graphene composite photoelectrode for photoelectrochemical testing and testing the photocurrent intensity is linearly related to the concentration of malathion in the sample.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyimide precursor is a solution of polyimide acid in N,N-dimethylformamide.
