PN CN109293977-A
TI Preparing graphene oxide/polyformaldehyde porous microsphere for reinforcing master batch comprises e.g. dispersing graphene oxide in water, adding lauryl amine, heating, ultrasonically dispersing, filtering, freezing and drying.
AU CHEN Q
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM ENG SCI & TECHNIC (XKLI-C)
GA 201914676L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene oxide/polyformaldehyde porous microsphere for reinforcing master batch comprises (i) dispersing the graphene oxide in water, adding lauryl amine, heating and ultrasonically dispersing, then filtering, freezing and drying to obtain functionalized graphene oxide, (ii) adding functionalized graphene oxide/polyformaldehyde resin into the diphenyl ether, stirring to dissolve completely to obtain the polyformaldehyde solution, then ultrasonically dispersing functionalized graphene oxide for 1 hour, preparing the mixed solution, then adding into the cooling device, placing in the cold trap to quench to obtain the gel, and (iii) quickly taking out gel-containing device and filling with ethanol solution, then placing in supercritical carbon dioxide reaction kettle, reacting, quickly relieving pressure, extracting to remove the diphenyl ether in the gel, then washing, and vacuum drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing graphene oxide/polyformaldehyde porous microsphere for reinforcing master batch.
   ADVANTAGE - The microsphere has large pore size, and specific surface area, has good dispersibility, increases the contact area of graphene oxide and the reinforcement substrate, so as to improve the reinforcing effect, is prepared by simple technology, and has good popularization and large-scale application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for graphene oxide/polyformaldehyde porous microsphere for reinforcing master batch.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the weight of graphene oxide is 6-8 pts. wt., water is 90-93 pts. wt. and dodecylamine is 1-2 pts. wt.. In the step (ii), the weight of polyformaldehyde resin is 30-40 pts. wt., diphenyl ether is 52-67 pts. wt., and functionalized graphene oxide is 3-8 pts. wt.. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the heating temperature is 38-43 degrees C, ultrasonic frequency of ultrasonic dispersion is 50-80 kHz; the power is 100-150 W; the time is 1-3 hours. In the step (ii) the stirring speed is 200-250 revolutions/minute, temperature is 30 degrees C and the time is 11-13 hours. In the step (ii), the quenching temperature is 0 degrees C and the time is 1-2 hours. In the step (iii), temperature of ethanol solution is 0 degrees C, the soaking time is 25-40 minutes. In the step (iii), the reaction temperature is 40-50 degrees C, the pressure is 10-15 MPa and the time is 25-35 minutes. In the step (iii), the vacuum drying temperature is 50-60 degrees C, the vacuum degree is 0.001-0.02 Pa, and the time is 20-25 hours.

TI Making glass used in direct household e.g.            drinking glasses or indirect e.g. glass-ceramic plates            or industrial, comprises supplying mixture of water,            sand, soda and calcium oxide in glassmaking furnace,            and melting mixture by flame burner.
AU CHARLES H
   BONNINGUES J M
   DONZE S
   FAMCHON F
   IBLED X
   HERVE C
   MARIE B J
   SEBASTIEN D
   FRANCOIS F
   XAVIER I
AE ARC FRANCE (ARCF-Non-standard)
   ARC FRANCE (ARCF-Non-standard)
GA 2019153578
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing glass comprises supplying a                mixture of water, sand, soda and calcium oxide in a                glassmaking furnace, where the calcium oxide is                present at 1-20 mass% of total of the mixture, and                melting the mixture by at least one flame burner                directed to the mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing glass                (claimed) which is useful in direct household use,                e.g. drinking glasses and glazing or indirect use                e.g. glass-ceramic plates or industrial.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical; and introduces the                mixture into a glass oven by allowing a reduction                of energy necessary to produce glass and releasing                3-6% of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                industrial glassware furnace implementing the                method, comprising a molten glass tank, a                combustion heating chamber located above the tank                and delimited by piers, gables and a vault, a flue                gas vent in communication with the heating chamber,                a loop burner disposed in a direction parallel to                the flue gas discharge, and a flame burner directed                to the molten glass vessel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The oxidizer supplied to                  the burner is oxygen. The water, the sand, the                  sodium carbonate and the calcium oxide are                  present at 0-5 mass%, 40-65 mass%, 1-25 mass% and                  1-20 mass% respectively. The method comprises,                  prior to the preparation of glass precursor                  mixture for glass furnace, comprises mixing                  water, sand and sodium carbonate at 0-5 mass%,                  40-65 mass% and more than 0 mass% to at most 25                  mass% respectively, and after a delay of at least                  1 hour, adding calcium oxide at 1-20 mass%. In                  the method, cullet is added to the glass                  precursor mixture, before or after the addition                  of calcium oxide, at 5-40 mass% of the total.                  Preferred Components: The calcium oxide having a                  particle size: of 70-90 mass.% does not pass                  through the sieve by 0.1 mm, preferably 30-70                  mass% does not pass the sieve of 2 mm; or more                  than 90 mass.% does not pass the sieve by 0.1 mm                  and less than 5 mass% does not pass with a sieve                  of 4 mm, preferably more than 95 mass% does not                  pass through a sieve of 0.1 mm and less than 1                  mass% does not pass through a sieve of 4 mm. The                  calcium oxide has a mean particle size of 1-1.5                  mm. The calcium oxide is devoid of voluntary                  addition of aluminum oxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC L01 (Glass - includes chemical compositions, batch treatment, furnaces, flat glass forming, hollow-ware forming, postforming and glass/ceramics, but not lens designs, bottling, bottle-washing, closures for containers, glazing designs, glass cutting, chamfering edges, printing on glass, disposing of used glass or the production of pure sodium silicate. AE BENGBU HUIJIXING AGRIC TECHNOLOGY FARMER (BENG-Non-standard)
GA 201901173U
AB    NOVELTY - Preventing downy mildew of cucumber comprises taking cucumber seed, adding 6-8 times by weight modified nano zinc oxide, mixing, adding 30-40 times by weight magnetized water, stirring at 60-65 degrees C for 40-60 minutes, drying at 55 degrees C to obtain pre-processed cucumber seed, soaking pre-processed cucumber seed in processing liquid at weight ratio of 4-6: 1 at 35-45 degrees C for 3-5 hours, and germinating seedlings. The processing liquid is prepared by drying locust branch to water content of less than 5%, grinding into powder, sieving by 80-mesh sieve, adding 2-3 times by weight asparagus dry powder, extracting using supercritical carbon dioxide, separating to obtain solid extract and liquid extract, adding 10 times by weight purified water, stirring at 55-65 degrees C for 30-40 minutes, filtering, repeating for 2 times, combining filtrates, adding liquid extract and concentrating to 1/5 of original volume.
   USE - Method for preventing downy mildew of cucumber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can sterilize, and improve physiological activity and disease resistance ability of seed, avoids bacterial infection, and improves economic benefit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The modified nano zinc oxide is nano-zinc polyphosphate solution with concentration of 60 wt.%, prepared by reacting at 70-80 degrees C for 1-2 hours, filtering and drying to water content of less than 10%.

PN US2018344964-A1; US11219737-B2
TI Method for providing resuscitation treatment to patient in cardiac arrest, involve adjusting spartial pressure of carbon dioxide (CO2) based on two measurements of CO2 concentration to slow expiration of CO2 from patient lungs.
AU FREEMAN G A
   SILVER A E
AE ZOLL MEDICAL CORP (ZOLL-C)
   ZOLL MEDICAL CORP (ZOLL-C)
GA 201896691H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving the two measurements of the CO2 concentration in an airway of the patient. An input of whether return of spontaneous circulation (ROSC) is received in the patient to be occurred. The two measurements of the CO2 concentration are processed in the airway of the patient. A partial pressure of CO2 is adjusted based on the two measurements of the CO2 concentration to slow expiration of CO2 from the patient lungs and to maintain a low pH in the patient tissues for a period of time following ROSC. The several signals indicative of a concentration of CO2 is received in a tissue of the patient.
   USE - Method for providing resuscitation treatment to patient in cardiac arrest.
   ADVANTAGE - The reperfusion injury is reduced by maintaining a low tissue pH during the time period while increasing oxygen content relative to normal room air concentrations thus the oxygenation of the brain, heart and vital organs can be enhanced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram illustrating the system with a ventilator integrated with a mechanical chest compression device and a defibrillator.

   ZHU L
   ZHANG Y
   LONG H
AE CHONGQING YUZHUO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201894410S
AB    NOVELTY - Composite adsorbent comprises 30-35 pts. wt. coconut shell charcoal, 20-22 pts. wt. shell powder, 15-18 pts. wt. sodium bentonite, 12-15 pts. wt. fly ash, 10-12 pts. wt. modified ferroferric oxide, 12-15 pts. wt. diatomaceous earth, 6-8 pts. wt. bamboo charcoal fiber, 8-10 pts. wt. vermiculite powder, 6-8 pts. wt. expanded graphite, 5-8 pts. wt. sepiolite powder, 1-3 pts. wt. talc, 1-1.5 pts. wt. magnesium oxide and 2-4 pts. wt. aluminum oxide.
   USE - The adsorbent is useful for processing domestic waste incineration flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent removes harmful organic substances e.g. heavy metals, nitrogen oxides, sulfides, carbon dioxide and dioxins in high-temperature flue gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing composite adsorbent, comprising mixing coconut shell charcoal, shell powder, sodium bentonite, fly ash, diatomaceous earth, vermiculite powder, expanded graphite, sepiolite powder, talc powder, magnesium oxide and aluminum oxide, grinding into a fine powder, soaking the fine powder in deionized water and stirring for at least 1 hours, adding bamboo charcoal fiber and modified ferroferric oxide, stirring, mixing, subjecting to filtration treatment, collecting the solid mixture is collected, and drying the solid mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composite adsorbent comprises 32 pts. wt. coconut shell charcoal, 21 pts. wt. shell powder, 16 pts. wt. sodium bentonite, 13 pts. wt. fly ash, 11 pts. wt. modified ferroferric oxide, 14 pts. wt. diatomaceous earth, 7 pts. wt. bamboo charcoal fiber, 9 pts. wt. vermiculite powder, 7 pts. wt. expanded graphite, 6 pts. wt. sepiolite powder, 2 pts. wt. talc, 1 pts. wt. magnesium oxide and 3 pts. wt. aluminum oxide. Preferred Components: The Pseudomonas stutzeri , microzyme and Pseudomonas aeruginosa are inoculated in a medium at a ratio of 2:1:1.5 for mixed fermentation culture. The total inoculum of the Pseudomonas stutzeri , microzyme and Pseudomonas aeruginosa is 10% of the volume of the culture medium for fermentation. The modified ferroferric oxide has a particle diameter of not greater than 120 mu m. The bamboo charcoal fiber is a single filament and has a length of 1-1.2 cm. Preferred Method: The modified ferroferric oxide is prepared by mixing nanometer ferroferric oxide and lipase solution at a mass ratio of 1:5-6, placing it in a shaker at a speed of 155 rotations/minute at 25 degrees C for 4.5-5 hours, filtering and drying, where the lipase solution is secreted by mixed fermentation of Pseudomonas stutzeri , microzyme and Pseudomonas aeruginosa. The mixed fermentation culture conditions were: the culture temperature is 32-35 degrees C, the shaker rotation speed was 150 rotations/minute and the culture time is 7 days.

   ZHANG X
AE HANGZHOU TONGGETANG CHINESE HERBAL MEDIC (HANG-Non-standard)

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of bran fried rhizoma atractylodis tablet comprising taking bran fried rhizoma atractylodis extract piece, crushing, carrying out reflux extraction, filtering, concentrating, taking water hydroxysiliconite, washing with water, grinding, dispersing in ethanol solution, microwave refluxing, taking out, drying to obtain hydroxysiliconite powder, taking nanocellulose and ionic liquid to prepare nanocellulose solution, adding hydroxysiliconite powder, dispersing, stirring and heating at 60-80 degrees C for 1-2 hours, freezing at - 20 to - 16 degrees C for 2-4 days, taking out, thawing, washing to obtain wet gel, adding alcohol solvent, drying via supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, grinding, adding with bran fried rhizoma atractylodis concentrate, stirring, drying and crushing, adding polyethylene pyrrolidone and microcrystalline cellulose, mixing, crushing into 10-20 mesh, sieving and granulating, drying at 60-70 degrees C into 30-40 mesh, adding silica gel micropowder, mixing, and tableting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Methods: The bran fried rhizoma atractylodis extract piece is prepared by selecting rhizoma atractylodis, remove impurity, rinsing, softening for 6-12 hours, cutting, drying for 4-5 hours at 65-70 degrees C to water content of 11%, stir frying bran with honey according to mass ratio of 3-5:1 at 180-200 degrees C at rate of 18-22 revolutions/minute (rpm),, adding rhizoma atractylodis sheet according to mass ratio of 8-12:1, frying at 180-200 degrees C at rate of 23-27 rpm for 40-50 minutes, taking out, and cooling. The preparation of bran fried rhizoma atractylodis tablet comprising taking bran fried rhizoma atractylodis extract piece, crushing, carrying out reflux extraction at power of 600-800 W at 100-120 degrees C for 20-40 minutes, filtering, concentrating into 50-70 wt.%, taking water hydroxysiliconite, washing with water, grinding, dispersing in ethanol solution, microwave refluxing, taking out, drying to obtain hydroxysiliconite powder, taking nanocellulose and ionic liquid to prepare 6-12 wt.% nanocellulose solution, adding 4-8 wt.% hydroxysiliconite powder, dispersing, stirring and heating at 60-80 degrees C for 1-2 hours, freezing at - 20 to - 16 degrees C for 2-4 days, taking out, thawing, washing to obtain wet gel, adding alcohol solvent, drying via supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, grinding, adding with 35-45 g bran fried rhizoma atractylodis concentrate, stirring, drying and crushing, adding 6-10:13-19 mass ratio of polyethylene pyrrolidone and microcrystalline cellulose, mixing, crushing into 10-20 mesh, sieving and granulating, drying at 60-70 degrees C into 30-40 mesh, adding 0.5-1.5 g silica gel micropowder, mixing, and tableting.

PN CN108272028-A
TI Heterocyclic amine inhibitor comprises pomelo peel essential oil, naringin, 3-vinyl-1,2-dithiacyclohex-4-ene, allyl propyl disulfide and diallyl trisulfide.
AU YAN Y
   HUANG J
   YOU F
   XIE N
   ZHAG F
   YIN J
AE INST AGRO FOOD SCI & TECHNOLOGY CHINESE (CAGS-C)
GA 201856197Y
AB    NOVELTY - Heterocyclic amine inhibitor comprises 15-25 %mass pomelo peel essential oil, 10-20 %mass naringin, 7.5-12 %mass 3-vinyl-1,2-dithiacyclohex-4-ene, 5-8 %mass allyl propyl disulfide and 3-5 %mass diallyl trisulfide.
   USE - As heterocyclic amine inhibitor.
   ADVANTAGE - The heterocyclic amine inhibitor is simple to prepare, and reduces amount of heterocyclic amine in meat product after thermal processing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing heterocyclic amine inhibitor, which involves (a) uniformly mixing garlic powder and pomelo peel powder in a mass ratio of 1:5-8, superfinely crushing and sieving the crushed material using a sieve of 200 mesh size, (b) mixing the sieved material with ethanol in a mass ratio of 1:2-5, extracting at 20-30 degrees C and 25-35 kHz for 30-40 minutes and collecting the filtrate using 80-120 mu m filter paper, (c) concentrating filtrate under reduced pressure, (d) placing the concentrate in extraction vessel of supercritical extraction device and performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction, (e) placing the extract in a high-barrier packaging bag, sealing at 10-30 degrees C, using water as medium and treating at 300-500 MPa for 5-10 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (c) involves concentrating filtrate under vacuum at 80-90 KPa and 50-60 degrees C. The garlic powder is prepared by taking garlic flakes with moisture content of 60-72%, performing vacuum freeze-drying at 45-55 degrees C and 90-100 KPa for 2.5-4 hours and crushing using a sieve of 60 mesh size. In step (e), the vacuum sealing is performed at 0.09-0.1 MPa. The pomelo peel powder is prepared by taking pomelo peel with moisture content of 85-93%, vacuum freeze-drying at 65-75 degrees C and 90-100 KPa for 3-5 hours and crushing using a sieve of 60 mesh size. The supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction is performed at 45-55 degrees C and 25-30 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 150-250 ml/min for 3-4 hours.

PT P
PN WO2018121326-A1; CN108236955-A; CN108236955-B
TI Producing catalyst used for hydrogenating dimethyl            oxalate to prepare ethanol, comprises e.g. synthesizing            mixture of urea, soluble metal salt of catalytic            component and soluble salt of optional catalytic aid,            drying, and calcining.
AU CHAI J
   TSUBAKI N
   YANG G
   LI Y
   CHUN F
AE HIGHCHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HIGH-Non-standard)
   CHAI J (CHAI-Individual)
   HIGHCHEM CO LTD (HIGH-Non-standard)
GA 2018525512
AB    NOVELTY - Producing catalyst, comprises (a) providing                aqueous solution of soluble metal salt of                catalytically active component and soluble salt of                optional catalytic aid in deionized water, (b)                adding urea as precipitant into obtained aqueous                solution in weight ratio of 1:100-10:100, stirring,                and adding support to obtain mixture, (c)                hydrothermally synthesizing the mixture in                hydrothermal reaction vessel at 100-200 degrees C,                (d) filtering obtained hydrothermal product,                washing and performing carbon dioxide supercritical                drying, and (e) calcining product to obtain                catalyst.The catalyst is supported catalyst                comprising support and catalytically active                component supported on support and optional                catalytic aid. The catalyst comprises 1-50 wt.%                copper, iron, nickel, cobalt, silver and gold as                catalytic component, 0-10 wt.% third main group                element, transition element and/or lanthanide                element of periodic table of elements as catalytic                auxiliary which are different from catalytic                component, and support.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for hydrogenating                dimethyl oxalate to prepare ethanol                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) catalyst produced by the above method;                and
   (2) use of catalyst in hydrogenation of                dimethyl oxalate to ethanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises                  5-30% by weight catalytically active component,                  and 1-5% by weight catalytic aid; and 65-94% by                  weight support. The catalytically active                  component is copper, silver and iron, preferably                  copper or a combination of copper and silver. The                  catalytic aid is boron, aluminum, lanthanum,                  cerium and/or zinc. The support is carbon                  nanotubes, graphene, activated carbon, silicon                  dioxide, aluminum oxide, zirconium dioxide,                  SBA-15 (mesoporous silica), Mobil composition of                  matter no. 41 (MCM-41), MCM-48, hollow mesoporous                  silica, zinc oxide and/or zeolite socony mobil-5                  (ZSM-5), preferably carbon nanotubes, graphene,                  silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, zirconium                  dioxide and/or ZSM-5. The support is not aluminum                  oxide, when the catalytic aid is aluminum. The                  support is not zirconium dioxide when the                  catalytic aid is zirconium. The soluble metal                  salt of the catalytically active component is                  nitrate, acetate, chloride or its hydrate and/or                  a catalytic aid. The soluble salts of the agents                  are nitrates, acetates and/or chlorides or its                  hydrates. Preferred Method: The step (b) is                  carried out by adding urea as precipitant into                  the obtained aqueous solution in weight ratio of                  1:100-8:100, preferably 1:100-6:100. The step (c)                  comprises hydrothermally synthesizing the                  obtained mixture at 100-180 degrees C for 4-72                  hours, preferably 10-48 hours under at stirring                  speed of 1-10 revolutions/minute, preferably 1-5                  revolutions/minute. The step (d) comprises                  placing the washed hydrothermal synthetic solid                  in a closed autoclave, continuously feeding                  supercritical carbon dioxide at 40-60 degrees C                  for 10-48 hours under pressure of 10-20 MPa,                  preferably at 40-45 degrees C for 24-48 hours                  under pressure of 10-12 MPa. The calcination in                  step (e) is carried out at 350-550 degrees C. The                  hydrogenating reaction of dimethyl oxalate to                  ethanol is carried out using dimethyl                  oxalate/hydrogen in molar ratio of 50-300, gauge                  pressure of 1-5 MPa, reaction temperature of 150                  -350 degrees C, and liquid hourly space velocity                  of 0.1-6.4/hours, preferably dimethyl                  oxalate/hydrogen molar ratio of 100-200, gauge                  pressure of 2-4 MPa, reaction temperature of                  200-300 degrees C, and liquid hourly space                  velocity of 0.4-3.2/hour.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN107987015-A; CN107987015-B
TI New (E)-3-(2-(pyridin-4-yl)vinyl)pyridin-2-amine useful in preparing coordination polymer as a catalyst for catalytic photodegradation reaction of dye rhodamine B.
AU LI N
   LIU D
   MA W
AE UNIV HUAIBEI NORMAL (UYHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018393904
AB    NOVELTY - Asymmetric bi-pyridyl olefin compound i.e. (E)-3-(2-(pyridin-4-yl)vinyl)pyridin-2-amine (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in preparing coordination polymer as a catalyst for catalytic photodegradation reaction of dye rhodamine B (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, mild reaction conditions and high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Asymmetric bi-pyridyl olefin compound i.e. (E)-3-(2-(pyridin-4-yl)vinyl)pyridin-2-amine of formula (I) is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I);
   (2) use of (I) in preparing coordination polymer, comprising carrying out self-assembly of (I) as an organic ligand with transition metal ion to obtain final product, where the coordination polymer is (cadmium(1-(2-amino-3-pyridyl)-2-(4-pyridyl)-ethylene)(5-nitro-1,3-phthalic acid))n; and
   (3) use of a coordination polymer as catalyst in catalytic photodegradation of dye rhodamine B to make into non-polluting carbon dioxide and water, comprising taking 20 ml rhodamine B solution prepared in 100 ml beaker, adding 1 drops of hydrogen peroxide and adding coordination polymer as catalyst, irradiating solution with a 350W xenon lamp and continuously stirring, where the concentration of rhodamine B solution is 0.005-3 g/l.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) mixing 4-chloromethylpyridine hydrochloride and triphenylphosphine in an acetonitrile as a solvent and refluxing under stirring for 3 hours and filtering the mixture by suction to obtain intermediate quaternary phosphonium salt, (ii) dissolving intermediate quaternary phosphonium salt in methanol in an ice-water bath under nitrogen atmosphere and adding excessive amount of sodium methoxide in methanol with stirring, reacting for half an hour to obtain phosphorus ylide and then removing ice water bath, heating to room temperature, and adding methanol solution of 2-amino-3-pyridinecarboxaldehyde to the reaction solution under stirring, continuously stirring and reacting for 5 hours at room temperature until the Wittig reaction is complete, then separating and purifying to obtain final product. Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises carrying out separation and purification by vortexing the solvent from the reaction solution, stirring with water, extracting with dichloromethane for 2-3 times, combining the organic phases and removing the solvent by vortex to obtain yellow crude product, washing with benzene and recrystallizing the crude product from dichloromethane to obtain yellow target product. The method comprises adding 1.97 g (I), 3.08 g cadmium nitrate tetrahydrate, 80 ml deionized water and 2.11g 5-nitro-1,3-benzenedicarboxylic acid to 150 ml reactor and reacting at a constant temperature of 170 degrees C for 48 hours and then cooling to room temperature to obtain crystals of coordination polymer. The catalytic photodegradation reaction temperature is 10-50 degrees C, and the reaction time is 60-200 minutes. Preferred Components: In step (ii), the molar ratio between intermediate quaternary phosphonium salt, 2-amino-3-pyridinecarboxaldehyde and sodium methoxide is 1:1:3. The mass ratio between catalyst and rhodamine B is 1:3-5.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E23 (Heterocyclic.)

GA 201842183R
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the technology of inorganic substances and can be used in the synthesis of fluorine-containing refrigerants in the production of uranium hexafluoride. Method for extracting hydrogen fluoride from its aqueous solutions involves the reduction reaction of water in the aqueous solution at an elevated temperature to obtain carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen. Then, the resulting hydrogen fluoride and the unreacted water vapor are condensed and rectified. Mixture of hydrogen fluoride and water is reduced at a temperature of 800 K and higher at a molar ratio of carbon in the reducing agent to water of 0.5 to 4. As a reducing agent, the reducing agent of general formula CnHmOk, where k greater than or equal to 0, m is greater than 0, and n greater than 0 is used. Reducing agent is saturated, unsaturated, aromatic hydrocarbons, oxygen-containing organic compounds, their isomers, their mixtures.
   USE - Extracting fluoride from aqueous solutions.

PN KR2018038798-A
TI Method for managing car for sharing car, involves transmitting information on detected gas density to car sharing server and adding comfort point to user of vehicle based on gas density.
AU PARK J W
   CHOI J W
   PARK G J
   KANG T S
AE T MONET CO LTD (TMON-Non-standard)
GA 201833325K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting a gas density in the vehicle room by a gas sensor (50) upon receipt of a user command to end the vehicle operation. The information on the detected gas density is transmitted to a car sharing server using a vehicle terminal (40). The comfort point is added to the user of the vehicle using a car sharing server (20) based on the gas density. At least one of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and alcohol in the vehicle room is detected. The information about the gas density is outputted, when the gas density is equal to or greater than a first reference value.
   USE - Method for managing car for sharing car.
   ADVANTAGE - The improved method allows the car sharing server to continuously manage the safe driving and maintenance of a comfortable environment, so that the user uses the vehicle safely and comfortably and aging of the vehicle is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a car sharing system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the car sharing system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   User terminal (10)
   Car sharing server (20)
   Vehicle terminal (40)

PT P
PN WO2018054963-A1; LU93232-B1; EP3515590-A1
TI Use of zinc oxide support covered by cobalt nanoparticles, as catalytic system for Sabatier's reaction.
AU ROGE V
   FECHETE I
   GARIN F
   LENOBLE D
   SARR M M
   DAMIEN L
   IOANA F
   FRANCOIS G
   VINCENT R
   MOUSTAPHA S M
AE LUXEMBOURG SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST LIST (LUSC-Non-standard)
   CNRS CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV STRASBOURG (UYSB-C)
   LUXEMBOURG INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY LIST (LUSC-Non-standard)
GA 201826289A
AB    NOVELTY - Use of zinc oxide support (28) covered by cobalt nanoparticles, as a catalytic system for Sabatier's reaction.
   USE - Use of zinc oxide support e.g. zinc oxide nanorods (claimed), as catalytic system for Sabatier's reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - Zinc oxide support is efficiently used as catalytic system in Sabatier's reaction. The reaction method efficiently converts carbon dioxide into methane without forming carbon monoxide. The method does not require a reactor which is resistant to high pressure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for controlled-deposition of cobalt on zinc oxide, which involves fluidizing zinc oxide, impregnating fluidized zinc oxide by sublimated cobalt(II) acetylacetonate powder, to form the zinc oxide support covered by cobalt nanoparticles. The method is carried out in a fluidized bed reactor (100) which consists of a gas inlet (4), a gas outlet (6) located downstream from the gas inlet, a tube (2) inserted between the gas inlet and the gas outlet, and a heating component (18) connected to the tube. The tube comprises an upstream zone and a downstream zone. The upstream zone and the downstream zone are separated by a separation filter (14).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the fluidized bed reactor.
   Tube (2)
   Gas inlet (4)
   Gas outlet (6)
   Heating component (18)
   Zinc oxide support (28)
   Fluidized bed reactor (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The heating component is a heating cable, a heating jacket and/or any thermal activation source.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zinc oxide support is a macroporous, mesoporous or microporous support. The tube is made of material which is resistant to temperature of at least up to 1000 degrees C, preferably quartz. Preferred Process: The reaction involves reacting carbon dioxide gas and hydrogen gas at a pressure of 1-6 bar, preferably 6 bar, and at a temperature of 450-500 degrees C. The zinc oxide is synthesized by forming an equimolar solution of zinc acetate dihydrate and hydrazine hydrate, and then heating at 150 degrees C under a pressure of 3 bar for 30 minutes. The impregnating step is carried out with a flow of gas comprising 80 cm3/minute nitrogen gas and 20 cm3/minute hydrogen gas. Sublimated cobalt(II) acetylacetonate powder is formed by thermal decomposition of cobalt acetylacetonate powder at 600 degrees C under a vacuum of 7 mbar. Preferred Condition: The reaction is carried out for 4 hours. Preferred Properties: The tube is transparent. The surface coverage of zinc oxide nanorods by cobalt nanoparticles is 12%.

PT P
PN CN206960458-U
TI Danger source monitoring system, has shell whose lower part is provided with first telescopic rod, and carbon dioxide and nitrogen dioxide concentration sensors connected with signal collecting and processing module.
AU CAO H
   SUN J
AE JIANGSU WANGYU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201811854U
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a danger source monitoring system. the danger source monitoring system, comprising a water quality monitoring mechanism and monitoring mechanism of set gas above the water quality monitoring mechanism, water quality monitoring mechanism comprises shell body and is set in the shell of the storage battery signal collecting and processing module, a DTU network module and a control module; the lower part of the shell is provided with a first telescopic rod, said first telescopic rod lower end outside is provided with a first temperature sensor, a first camera and water quality monitoring sensor module, the gas monitoring mechanism comprises a second extension pole and a second temperature sensor end of the second telescopic rod. the pressure sensor, the CO2 concentration sensor and a NO2 concentration sensor. said danger source monitoring system, because of the water quality monitoring mechanism and gas monitoring mechanism, at the same time the water quality condition and the source of air quality monitoring in real time, and real-time transmitting the monitoring data to the control terminal does not need personnel to manual sampling and detecting on the spot and saves the labour cost.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); S02 (Engineering Instrumentation); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC J04-C01; J04-C04A; D04-A01H; S02-K01; S03-E14B; S03-E14P1
IP G01D-021/02; G01N-033/00; G01N-033/18

ER

PT P
PN EP3269361-A1
TI Dosage form used in pharmaceutical, nutraceutical, cosmetic, home and personal care product, comprises functionalized calcium carbonate-containing material dispersed in hot-melt extruded polymer resin at given weight ratio.
AU WAGNER-HATTLER L
   PUCHKOV M
   HUWYLER J
   DE MIGUEL L
   DIAZ QUIJANO C
   SCHOELKOPF J
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 201804946T
AB    NOVELTY - A dosage form comprises functionalized calcium carbonate-containing material(s) (FCC) dispersed in hot-melt extruded polymer resin(s). The weight ratio of FCC to hot-melt extruded polymer resin is 95:5 to 5:95. The FCC is a reaction product of natural ground calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide (CO2) and hydronium (H3O+) ion donor(s). The CO2 is formed in situ by the H3O+ ion donor treatment and/or is supplied from external source.
   USE - Dosage form, i.e. powder, multilayered tablet, tablet of altered geometric configuration, matrix tablet, minitablet, pellets, capsule, granules, and/or tablet-in-cup, used in pharmaceutical, nutraceutical, cosmetic, home and personal care product (claimed). No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The dosage form is lighter and less voluminous at higher drug load, compared to conventional dosage form comprising functionalized calcium carbonate-containing material; and can be prepared in highly efficient compression method without using binders and/or compacting aids.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of dosage form by mixing FCC and polymer resin, hot-melt extruding the mixture, and milling or pelletizing the hot-melt extruded product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The natural ground calcium carbonate is calcium carbonate-containing mineral including marble, chalk, dolomite, and/or limestone. The precipitated calcium carbonate is precipitated calcium carbonate having aragonitic, vateritic, and/or calcitic mineralogical crystal forms. The FCC has Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) specific surface area of 20-450 m2/g, preferably 50-140 m2/g measured using nitrogen and BET method according to ISO 9277; comprises particles having volume median grain diameter d50 of 1-50 mu m, preferably 4-12 mu m; and/or has intraparticle intruded specific pore volume of 0.15-1.35 cm3/g, preferably of 0.40-1.25 cm3/g, calculated from mercury intrusion porosimetry measurement.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The dosage form further comprises active agent(s) and/or inactive agent(s) including pharmaceutical active agent, active or inactive prodrug, nutraceutical, food additive, and/or cosmetic additive. The active agent and/or inactive agent is loaded on or mixed with FCC; is dispersed in polymer resin comprising the FCC; and/or is in the form of a core, preferably in the form of compacted tablet which is partially covered by polymer resin comprising the FCC, in the form of a layer which at least partially covers a core and is made from polymer resin comprising the FCC, or in the form of layered structure of at least two layers, in which at least one layer is made from polymer resin comprising the FCC.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer resin is polyethylene, polystyrene, PVC, polyamide 66 (nylon), polycaprolactam, acrylic polymer, acrylonitrile butadiene styrene, polybenzimidazole, polycarbonate, polyphenylene oxide/sulfide, polypopylene, Teflon (RTM: PTFE), aliphatic polyester such as polyhydroxybutyrate, poly-3-hydroxybutyrate, polyhydroxyvalerate, polyhydroxybutyrate-polyhydroxyvalerate copolymer, poly-3-hydroxybutyrate-co-3-hydroxyvalerate, polyglyconate, polydioxanone, and/or preferably polycaprolactone, polylactic acid, and/or polylactic acid-based polymer. The dosage form further comprises excipient(s) including disintegrant, lubricant, inner-phase lubricant, outer-phase lubricant, impact modifier, plasticizer, wax, stabilizer, pigment, colorant, scenting agent, taste-masking agent, flavoring agent, sweetener, mouthfeel improver, binder, diluent, film-forming agent, adhesive, buffer, adsorbent, and/or odor-masking agent. The disintegrant is modified cellulose gum, insoluble crosslinked polyvinylpyrrolidone, starch glycolate, microcrystalline cellulose, pregelatinized starch, sodium carboxymethyl starch, low-substituted hydroxypropyl cellulose, homopolymer of N-vinyl-2-pyrrolidone, alkylcellulose ester, hydroxyalkylcellulose ester, carboxyalkylcellulose ester, alginate, microcrystalline cellulose and its polymorphic forms, ion-exchange resin, gum, chitin, chitosan, clay, gellan gum, crosslinked polacrilin copolymer, agar, gelatin, dextrin, acrylic acid polymer, sodium/calcium carboxymethyl cellulose, hydroxypropyl methylcellulose phthalate, and/or shellac.
   ACTIVITY - None given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)
MC B04-C02; B04-C03; D03-H; D03-H01T2; D08-B; A03-C; A04-C02E; A04-C03; A04-E02E; A04-E08; A04-G02E3; A05-E01D; A05-E02B; A05-E06B; A05-F01E3; A05-J02; A10-E09; A11-A01; A11-C01C; A12-V01; A12-V04C

TI Apparatus for obtaining water from hydrocarbons, comprises compressor for compressing air and expansion device is provided with turbines between which a heat extraction device is arranged through which exhaust gas of combustion is feasible.
AU TREMEL A
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 2018028054
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (I) comprises a compressor (1) for compressing the air. The expansion device (5) is provided with a first turbine (5a) and a second turbine (5b) between which a heat extraction device is arranged through which the exhaust gas of the combustion is feasible. The mechanical shaft of the first turbine and second turbine drives the compressor. The heat extraction device is a heat recovery steam generator. The power generating device has a steam cycle or an organic Rankine cycle or a carbon dioxide cycle. A cooling device cools the exhaust gas of the combustion.
   USE - Apparatus for obtaining water from hydrocarbons, particularly natural gas or lean gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of a water vapor circuit or an organic Rankine circuit for the use of heat enables an efficiency to the generation of current related to the use of the natural gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process for recovering water from hydrocarbon-containing gas, particularly natural gas or lean gas.

   Second turbine (5b)

TI Decal sticker useful for attaching to e.g. various            household items for information purposes, comprises            e.g. adhesive layer adhering to fabrics and paper            materials by mixing appropriate amount of calcium with            non-toxic water-based adhesive.
AU SON D
AE SON D (SOND-Individual)
GA 2022863272
AB    NOVELTY - Decal sticker comprises an adhesive layer                adhering to various fabrics or paper materials by                mixing an appropriate amount of calcium with a                non-toxic water-based adhesive or a non-toxic                water-based adhesive to reinforce the tensile                strength and durability of the resin layer; a                conductive layer to which conductivity is added to                the adhesive layer by mixing nanosized Ketjen black                and charcoal with resin of water-based acrylic or                silicone; and an antibacterial copper layer to                which sterilizing and antibacterial power against                pneumococcal virus and Escherichia coli virus is                added by mixing nano copper or copper powder on the                conductive layer with a resin such as aqueous                acrylic or silicone.
   USE - The decal sticker is useful for attaching to                various household items i.e. fancy items,                stationery items and office supplies, automobile                items, cell phone accessories, phone hygiene                product, laptop hygiene products, computer keyboard                hygiene product, desks, dining tables, building                door glass or handle, and elevators for information                purposes.
   ADVANTAGE - The decal sticker has sterilization and                antibacterial deodorization functions due to zinc                oxide nano powder, strong oxidizing power of copper                so it is naturally oxidized in the process of                extracting copper yarn or in the grinding process,                can block pneumonia viruses i.e. Covid 19 and                various other strains of viruses by maximizing the                conductivity by mixing strong conductors i.e. black                and charcoal in predetermined ratio, provides                sterilization and antibacterial deodorization                functions (e.g. UVA and UV blocking, antifungal,                and immunity improvement, anti-fatigue, cold and                flu prevention, and antiaging improvement                functions) by attaching to fabrics or paper                materials of various materials, decomposes and                removes odors by decomposing odor factors into                carbon dioxide and water due to the excellent                photocatalytic activation of zinc oxide nano                powders, and can be used in preparation of                sterilization mask with the sterilization fabric                while reducing the use of the existing disposable                polypropylene material mask, and reused                environmentally friendly sterilization mask.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Decal sticker comprises (a) forming an                adhesive layer with enhanced tensile strength by                mixing an appropriate amount of 3-40 wt.% calcium                with non-toxic water-based adhesive or non-toxic                water-based adhesive for reinforcing the tensile                strength and durability of the resin layer, (b)                forming a conductor layer by mixing nano or powdery                Ketjen black and charcoal with 1 mu m to 40 nm and                then with a resin i.e. water-based acrylic or                silicone at weight ratio of 3-40%, (c) mixing the                nano copper or copper powder with resin i.e.                aqueous acrylic or silicone in weight ratio of                3-40% to form an antibacterial copper layer, (d)                forming zinc oxide layer by mixing zinc oxide with                antibacterial and sterilizing properties to                nano-water-based acrylic or silicone resin in                weight ratio of 3-40%, and (e) printing the steps                in a silk printing method or offset printing                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The decal sticker further                  comprises a zinc oxide layer formed by mixing                  zinc oxide with an antibacterial and sterilizing                  power to the antibacterial copper layer and                  mixing it with an aqueous acrylic or silicone                  resin.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN US2022185664-A1; CN114634159-A; EP4015076-A1; JP2022094918-A; KR2022085705-A; IN202144052806-A
TI Method of using a feedstock gas reactor comprising            a reaction chamber, involves extracting a mixed product            stream from the feedstock gas reactor, and recycling a            portion of the mixed product stream to the feedstock            gas reactor.
AU LI G
   ESQUIVEL S D
   LEBOE D A
   KRATSCHMAR K W
   SCHUBAK G E
   REID C E J
   LI G W
   EDWARD S G
   WILLIAM K K
   AARON L D
   DAWIPAY E
AE EKONA POWER INC (EKON-Non-standard)
   EKONA POWER INC (EKON-Non-standard)
   EKONA POWER INC (EKON-Non-standard)
GA 2022807838
AB    NOVELTY - A method of using a feedstock gas reactor                comprising a reaction chamber fluidly connected to                a combustion chamber via one or more fluid flow                paths, involves introducing a feedstock gas                comprising a hydrocarbon into the reaction chamber;                introducing a combustible gas into combustion                chamber; and combusting the combustible gas in the                combustion chamber to form one or more combustion                product gases and to cause at least a portion of                one or more combustion product gases to flow into                the reaction chamber via the one or more fluid flow                paths and mix with the feedstock gas, resulting in                energy transfer from a portion of combustion                product gases to the feedstock gas and chemical                reaction to decompose the feedstock gas into                reaction product gases comprising hydrogen;                extracting a mixed product stream from the                feedstock gas reactor; and recycling a portion of                the mixed product stream to the feedstock gas                reactor. The mixed product stream comprises                hydrogen, carbon, and water.
   USE - A method of using a feedstock gas reactor                comprising a reaction chamber fluidly connected to                a combustion chamber via one or more fluid flow                paths.
   ADVANTAGE - The method minimizes the carbon dioxide                emissions associated with the production of                reaction heat and accommodates for actual                feedstocks that may contain non-reactive species or                impurities. The method increases the productivity                of, and reduces the greenhouse gas emissions from,                a hydrocarbon pyrolysis reactor by recycling the                residual product gas stream (tail gas) back to the                reactor after the separation of product hydrogen,                solid carbon, and water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                comprising a feedstock gas reactor comprising a                reaction chamber and a combustion chamber fluidly                connected to the reaction chamber via one or more                fluid flow paths; and an igniter; valving for                controlling flow of gases into and out of the                feedstock gas reactor; and one or more controllers                configured to perform controlling the valving to                introduce a feedstock gas comprising a hydrocarbon                into the reaction chamber; controlling the valving                to introduce a combustible gas into the combustion                chamber; and controlling the igniter to combust the                combustible gas in the combustion chamber to form                one or more combustion product gases and to cause                one or more combustion product gases to flow into                the reaction chamber via one or more fluid flow                paths and mix with the feedstock gas; controlling                the valving to extract a mixed product stream from                the feedstock gas reactor, where the mixed product                stream comprises hydrogen, carbon, and water; and                controlling the valving to recycle at least a                portion of the mixed product stream to the                feedstock gas reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the gas                being recirculated to a pulsed methane pyrolysis                reactor.
   Pulsed methane pyrolysis reactor                (201')
   Carbon separation (205')
   Oxidant (210')
   Hydrocarbon feedstock (211')
   Pulsed flow batch reaction system                (219')
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed product                  stream consists of hydrogen, carbon, and water.                  The combustible gas comprises pure oxygen. The                  feedstock gas comprises methane or natural                  gas.Preferred Process: The method further                  comprises separating at least some of the carbon                  from the mixed product stream. Separating the at                  least some of the carbon comprises using one or                  more of a cyclone; a sintered metal filter; and a                  bag filter. The method further comprises                  separating at least some of the hydrogen from the                  mixed product stream. Separating the at least                  some of the hydrogen comprises using one or more                  of a membrane separator; a polymeric membrane                  separator; a palladium membrane separator;                  chemical adsorption; pressure swing adsorption;                  and temperature swing adsorption. Recycling the                  at least a portion of the mixed product stream to                  the feedstock gas reactor comprises separating at                  least some of the carbon and at least some of the                  hydrogen from the mixed product stream to form a                  tail gas stream; and recycling the tail gas                  stream to the feedstock gas reactor. Recycling                  the tail gas stream to the feedstock gas reactor                  comprises mixing a first fraction of the tail gas                  stream with an oxidant; and mixing a second                  fraction of the tail gas stream with a source of                  the feedstock gas. The first fraction plus the                  second fraction equals 1. Recycling the tail gas                  stream comprises splitting the tail gas stream                  into the first and second fractions, and                  splitting comprises using one or more of a flow                  splitter; one or more variable control valves; a                  variable flow diverter; one or more compressors;                  and one or more pumps. Mixing the first fraction                  of tail gas stream with the oxidant comprises                  mixing the first fraction of the tail gas stream                  with a quantity of oxidant sufficient to drive                  decomposition of the feedstock gas in the                  feedstock gas reactor. Mixing the second fraction                  of the tail gas stream with the source of the                  feedstock gas comprises mixing the second                  fraction of the tail gas stream with a quantity                  of the feedstock gas sufficient to enable the                  quantity of the feedstock gas to reach a target                  pressure and a target temperature required for                  decomposition of the quantity of the feedstock                  gas in the feedstock gas reactor. The method                  further comprises introducing the mixture of the                  oxidant and first fraction of tail gas stream                  into the combustion chamber; introducing the                  mixture of the feedstock gas and the second                  fraction of the tail gas stream into the reaction                  chamber; and combusting the mixture of the                  oxidant and the first fraction of the tail gas                  stream in the combustion chamber to form one or                  more combustion product gases and to cause the                  one or more combustion product gases to flow into                  the reaction chamber via the one or more fluid                  flow paths and mix with the mixture of the                  feedstock gas and the second fraction of the tail                  gas stream. The method further comprises                  adjusting one or more of a pressure of the                  feedstock gas at an inlet of the reaction                  chamber; a temperature of the feedstock gas at                  the inlet of the reaction chamber; a pressure of                  the combustible gas at an inlet of the combustion                  chamber; and a temperature of the combustible gas                  at the inlet of the combustion chamber; a                  stoichiometry of air in a combustible gas mixture                  comprising the combustible gas; a quantity of gas                  vented from the combustion chamber; and a degree                  of hydrogen separation of a hydrogen separator                  through which the mixed product stream is passed.                  Temperature of feedstock gas is adjusted to                  1000-1300 K. Temperature of combustible gas is                  adjusted to 300-600 K. Pressure of the                  combustible gas comprises is adjusted to 12-15                  bar. Pressure of the feedstock gas is adjusted to                  12-15 bar. Extracting the mixed product stream                  comprises extracting from the reaction chamber at                  least a portion of reaction product gases formed                  as a result of the decomposition of the feedstock                  gas. Extracting the mixed product stream                  comprises extracting at least a portion of the                  one or more combustion product gases from the                  combustion chamber. The method further comprises                  venting at least some of the extracted one or                  more combustion product gases. The method further                  comprises adjusting the venting of extracted one                  or more combustion product gases. Adjusting the                  venting comprises adjusting one or more of a                  duration of the venting of the extracted one or                  more combustion product gases; a frequency of the                  venting of the extracted one or more combustion                  product gases; a size of one or more vent                  orifices used for the venting of the extracted                  one or more combustion product gases; a number of                  vents used for the venting of the extracted one                  or more combustion product gases; and a pressure                  drop across a vent flow path used for the venting                  of the extracted one or more combustion product                  gases. Adjusting the venting comprises adjusting                  the venting based on a measured or inferred                  process parameter of the method. The measured or                  inferred process parameter comprises one or more                  of a concentration of hydrogen in the mixed                  product stream; a flow of the feedstock gas; and                  a ratio of hydrogen to nitrogen in the mixed                  product stream. Steps of introducing the                  feedstock gas, introducing the combustible gas,                  and combusting the combustible gas are repeated                  multiple times as portion of multiple                  corresponding reaction cycles, and the step of                  venting at least some of the extracted one or                  more combustion product gases is performed every                  reaction cycle or every preset number of reaction                  cycles.

TI Auxiliary smoke exhaust drainage device useful for            single-hole laparoscopic surgery in field of medical            appliance comprises outer clamping piece used for            propping against outer side of abdominal wall, and            cooperating with expanded second airbag to clamp            abdominal wall together.
AU DONG X
   GUO T
AE CHENGDU XINYUN DECODING TECHNOLOGY CO (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202271062S
AB    NOVELTY - The device comprises a needle cylinder (1), a                puncture needle (4) and an inner tube. The puncture                needle is used for puncturing the abdominal wall.                The inner tube is provided with multiple through                holes. The needle cylinder is connected with an                outer tube (3). The inner part of the needle                cylinder and inner part of the outer pipe form a                passage through which the puncture needle or inner                pipe passes. The outer wall of the outer pipe is                provided with an air inlet piece (6), an outer                clamping piece and a second air bag (9). The air                inlet piece is connected with the second air bag.                The second air bag is used for expanding when the                air inlet piece is air inlet, and is propped                against the inner side of the abdominal wall. The                outer clamping piece is used for propping against                the outer side of the abdominal wall, and                cooperating with the expanded second airbag to                clamp the abdominal wall together.
   USE - Auxiliary smoke exhaust drainage device useful                for single-hole laparoscopic surgery in the field                of medical appliance and attention field in                gynaecological field for clearly seeing tissue and                organ condition in pelvic cavity and abdominal                cavity.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: can allow the inner tube to extend                into the abdominal cavity and matched with the                single-hole laparoscope for discharging smoke; can                effectively reduce the smoke patient in the                abdominal cavity; makes the operation field clear;                reduces the harm of the smoke to the body patient                at the same time; can carry out drainage operation;                improves the utilization rate of the auxiliary                smoke drainage device; has more flexible operation;                reduces the wound caused by repeated puncture;                punctures and fixes the exhaust pipe on the                abdominal wall, so that an exhaust pipe and an air                inlet channel of a carbon dioxide of a single-hole                laparoscope keeps a far distance; increases smoke                discharge amount, thus reducing smoke amount in the                abdominal cavity and keeping an operation field                clear, and reducing harm of smoke to a body                patient; allows a bottom end of the inner pipe to                be located in an inner cavity of a patient, which                avoids an inner pipe from exhausting smoke, in a                drainage operation process moving up and down; and                improves safety of the operation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the auxiliary smoke exhaust drainage device                useful for single-hole laparoscopic surgery in                field of medical appliance.
   Needle cylinder (1)

TI Multi-cycle mode intelligent solar air purifying window, has window main body whose indoor side middle portion is provided with display screen, where indoor side lower portion of window main body is provided with return air inlet.
AU DU K
   CHEN C
   WANG Q
   EREAIHAN
   WANG X

   GU T

AE UNIV SHIHEZI (USHZ-C)
GA 202227363F
AB    NOVELTY - The window has a window main body whose outdoor side upper portion is equipped with a solar panel (14). A return air outlet (16) is formed at a lower portion of the solar panel. A fresh air inlet (12) is arranged at an outdoor side lower portion of the window main body. An indoor side upper portion of the window main body is provided with a fresh air outlet (4). An indoor side middle portion of the window main body is provided with a display screen (6). An indoor side lower portion of the window main body is provided with a return air inlet (10). The fresh air inlet, a primary filter screen (11), a heat recovery filter core (13), a high-efficiency filter screen (7), a cross-flow type silencing fan (2) and the fresh air outlet form a fresh air supply channel. The return air inlet, the heat recovery filter core and the return air outlet form a return air exhaust passage.
   USE - Multi-cycle mode intelligent solar air purifying window for being installed in a residence. Uses include but are not limited to a hotel, an office building, an apartment and a laboratory.
   ADVANTAGE - The window has compact structure, multiple circulating mode functions, certain application value, high indoor carbon dioxide concentration, better ventilation effect and better energy-recycling effect. The window realizes ventilating and air cleaning functions through an outer circulation mode when air quality is poor at outdoor air quality difference. The window realizes air filter cleaning and heating process through the internal circulation mode when the air temperature is low and effectively saves operation energy consumption. The window reduces energy loss, and can send fresh air into a room and exchange heat during ventilating process using solar energy to provide electric energy to an electric appliance so as to improve the indoor air quality. The window is installed on a window frame to effectively save precious indoor space. The window makes a sound-absorbing cotton layer located at an upper side of the cross-flow type silencing fan to absorb noise generated by the window so as to ensure practical and environment-friendly performance of the window.

PN CN113834863-A
TI Preparing high selectivity nitrogen oxide sensor based on three-dimensional composite pleated ball for detecting wearable nitrogen dioxide, involves weighing titanium aluminum carbide powder, adding concentrated hydrochloric acid.
AU LU G
   DUAN Y
   YANG T
   LIU F
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 202208422A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high selectivity nitrogen oxide sensor based on three-dimensional composite pleated ball involves weighing titanium aluminum carbide powder, adding to the mass fraction of 30-40% concentrated hydrochloric acid and lithium fluoride mixed to form the etching liquid, where the mass ratio of titanium aluminum carbide powder and lithium fluoride is 0.8-1:1, the mass volume ratio of the titanium aluminum carbide powder to the concentrated hydrochloric acid is 1g:30-50mL, stirring and reacting for 20-24 hours at 40-60degrees Celsius water bath, repeatedly washing the product after reaction by de-ionized water, centrifuging, until the supernatant pH is 6-7, then dispersing the washed product in 60-70 mL de-ionized water, ultrasonically dispersing for 30-60 minutes, centrifuging and taking the upper dispersion to obtain colloid dispersion, where the colloid dispersion concentration is 10-15mg/mL.
   USE - Method for preparing high selectivity nitrogen oxide sensor based on three-dimensional composite pleated ball used for detecting wearable nitrogen dioxide. and used for detecting carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, hydrogen chloride, hydrogen nitride, nitrogen oxides, nitrogen sulfide and nitrogen dioxide oxides.
   ADVANTAGE - The three-dimensional Ti3C2Tx/rGO composite pleated ball has higher NO2 response and lower detection lower limit than pure Ti3AlC2 powder and pure rGO powder.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high selectivity nitrogen oxide sensor based on three-dimensional composite pleated ball involves weighing titanium aluminum carbide powder, adding to the mass fraction of 30-40% concentrated hydrochloric acid and lithium fluoride mixed to form the etching liquid, where the mass ratio of titanium aluminum carbide powder and lithium fluoride is 0.8-1:1, the mass volume ratio of the titanium aluminum carbide powder to the concentrated hydrochloric acid is 1g:30-50mL, stirring and reacting for 20-24 hours at 40-60degrees Celsius water bath, repeatedly washing the product after reaction by de-ionized water, centrifuging, until the supernatant pH is 6-7, then dispersing the washed product in 60-70 mL de-ionized water, ultrasonically dispersing for 30-60 minutes, centrifuging and taking the upper dispersion to obtain colloid dispersion, where the colloid dispersion concentration is 10-15mg/mL, taking 5-10 mL colloidal dispersion, then adding the concentration of 10-15 mg/mL graphene oxide dispersion, colloidal dispersion and the mass ratio of the graphene oxide is 1.8-2.2:1, fully stirring and ultrasonic spraying, using nitrogen with flow speed of 2-6 L/minutes to bring the ultrasonic atomizing product into the tubular furnace with stable temperature at 600-800degrees Celsius. The obtained three-dimensional composite pleated ball powder is brought into the electrostatic collecting device located at the tail portion of the tubular furnace for collecting after passing through the tubular furnace, the graphene oxide is converted into reducing graphene oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The size of the polyimide substrate is (10-12) mm asterisk (10-12) mm, the electrode width is 100-120 micrometer, the electrode distance is 100-120 micrometer and the sensitive electrode thickness range is 0.1-0.4 millimeter. The method involves washing the polyimide substrate with Au interdigital electrode by de-ionized water and absolute ethyl alcohol and drying, sticking the area around the interdigital electrode by adhesive tape to mask, making the drop coating range stable in the interdigital electrode area, reducing the error between each sensor; then uniformly mixing the three-dimensional composite pleated ball powder and de-ionized water according to (3-5) mg:1 mL proportion, fully dispersing and uniformly dropping on the polyimide substrate with gold interdigital electrode, then drying for 30-40 minutes under the vacuum condition at 80-90degrees Celsius, removing the adhesive tape, to prepare the room temperature high selectivity nitrogen oxide sensor based on three-dimensional composite pleated ball.

PN CN113070322-A
TI Carrying out silicomanganese slag resource            utilization involves adding silicomanganese slag into            solution containing ammonium sulfate, and adding            sulfuric acid to obtain acid leaching solution and            silicocalcium slag.
AU ZHAO W
   KANG L
AE TIANJIN YIHAO ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202187444M
AB    NOVELTY - Carrying out silicomanganese slag resource                utilization involves adding silicomanganese slag                into a solution containing ammonium sulfate, and                adding sulfuric acid to leach to obtain an acid                leaching solution and silicocalcium slag. The                calcium silicate slag is leached by using a                calcium-poor solution containing an ultra-solvent,                added ammonia water in the reaction process to                dissolve gypsum in the solution, and then filtered                and separated to obtain a calcium-rich solution and                silicon dioxide. The ammonia and redundant water is                discharged in the solution by the calcium-rich                liquid through evaporation, concentrated the                calcium-rich liquid to convert gypsum into                precipitate again, separated to obtain a gypsum                product and a calcium-poor liquid, and performed                calcium-leaching treatment on the calcium-poor                liquid again. The silicon dioxide is acidified in                an acid washing mode, and separated to obtain                silicon dioxide.
   USE - Method for carrying out resource utilization                silicomanganese slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides resource utilization of                the silicomanganese slag in a simple,                cost-effective and eco-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carrying out silicomanganese slag resource                utilization involves adding silicomanganese slag                into a solution containing ammonium sulfate, and                adding sulfuric acid to leach to obtain an acid                leaching solution and silicocalcium slag. The                calcium silicate slag is leached by using a                calcium-poor solution containing an ultra-solvent,                added ammonia water in the reaction process to                dissolve gypsum in the solution, and then filtered                and separated to obtain a calcium-rich solution and                silicon dioxide. The ammonia and redundant water is                discharged in the solution by the calcium-rich                liquid through evaporation, concentrated the                calcium-rich liquid to convert gypsum into                precipitate again, separated to obtain a gypsum                product and a calcium-poor liquid, and performed                calcium-leaching treatment on the calcium-poor                liquid again. The silicon dioxide is acidified in                an acid washing mode, and separated to obtain                silicon dioxide. The ammonia water is slowly added                to the acid leaching solution at a high temperature                to control the pH value, so that aluminum sulfate                is converted into aluminum hydroxide precipitate,                which is filtered and separated to obtain aluminum                hydroxide and aluminum precipitation solution. The                pH value of the aluminum precipitation liquid is                continuously increased for reacting ammonium                carbonate with manganese to convert manganese into                manganese carbonate precipitate, and separated to                obtain manganese carbonate and a manganese poor                liquid. The pH value of the lean manganese solution                is continuously increased for reacting the lean                manganese solution with carbon dioxide under strong                alkalinity to obtain basic magnesium carbonate and                ammonium sulfate solution. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a device for carrying out resource                utilization silicomanganese slag, which comprises a                reaction tank, another reaction tank, a third                reaction tank, a filter pressing device, another                filter pressing device and a third filter pressing                device, where the liquid outlet of the former                reaction tank is connected with a liquid inlet of                the latter reaction tank.
