PN WO2021110870-A1
TI Producing fermentation product e.g. lactic acid, ethanol or protein antibiotic in cell culture, by determining optimized process parameters for culturing high producer cell line, and culturing high producer cell line under parameters.
AU MATTANOVICH D
   TOTARO D
   STEIGER M
   ERTL P
   ROTHBAUER M
AE ACIB GMBH (ACIB-Non-standard)
   UNIV BODENKULTUR WIEN (UYBO-Non-standard)
GA 202163109N
AB    NOVELTY - Method (M1) for producing a fermentation product in a cell culture involves (a) determining optimized process parameters for culturing a high producer cell line to produce fermentation product, where the high producer cell line is chosen from a repertoire of cell lines capable of producing fermentation product, and (b) culturing high producer cell line by an industrial scale method under the selected process parameters to produce fermentation product. The step (a) is made upon cell culture employing a multiplexed microfluidic perfusion cell culture device in perfusion mode.
   USE - The method (M1) is useful for producing fermentation product in cell culture. The fermentation product is chosen from a cell metabolite or a protein. The cell metabolite is chosen from organic acid, alcohol and amino acid, preferably lactic acid, itaconic acid, citric acid, succinic acid, glutamic acid, 3-hydroxypropionic acid, adipic acid, ethanol, propane-1,3-diol, 1-butanol, 2-butanol, butane-1,4-diol, L-glutamic acid, L-lysine, or L-threonine. The protein is chosen from heterologous protein, preferably an antigen-binding protein, therapeutic protein, an enzyme, a peptide, a protein antibiotic, a toxin fusion protein, a carbohydrate-protein conjugate, a structural protein, a regulatory protein, a vaccine antigen, a growth factor, a hormone, a cytokine, a process enzyme (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces fermentation product with increased product yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method (M2) for screening one or more process parameters for culturing a cell line and producing a fermentation product from cell line involves (a) providing a cell line capable of producing fermentation product, (b) culturing the cell line in separate microfluidic perfusion cell culture chambers under differing process parameters to produce fermentation product, where process parameters are chosen from productivity, temperature, oxygen concentration, pH value, cell culture medium composition, concentration of a cell culture medium component, flow rate, biomass concentration, metabolites concentration, and carbon dioxide concentration, (c) determining one or more process parameters optimized based on fermentation product yield, and (d) culturing the cell line in an industrial scale method, where one or more of the selected process parameters are applied;
   (2) a method (M3) for screening a production cell line and producing a fermentation product from cell line involves (a) providing a repertoire of cell lines comprising a variety of cell lines which differ in their capability of producing fermentation product, (b) culturing each of cell lines in a separate microfluidic perfusion cell culture chamber under conditions to produce fermentation product, (c) selecting a high producer cell line, (d) selecting one or more process parameters, and (e) culturing the selected cell line in an industrial scale method under conditions to produce fermentation product; and
   (3) use of process parameters or high producer cell line selected by a method employing a multiplexed microfluidic perfusion cell culture device, for producing a fermentation product in an industrial scale process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The optimized process parameters are determined by culturing high producer cell line in a multiplexed microfluidic perfusion cell culture device in perfusion mode, where optimized process parameters are selected based on amount of the produced fermentation product. The high producer cell line is selected by culturing repertoire of cell lines in a multiplexed microfluidic perfusion cell culture device, where repertoire comprises a variety of cell lines, which differ in their capability of producing fermentation product. The industrial method is a non-perfusion method, preferably a batch, fed-batch or non-perfusion continuous method. Preferred Apparatus: The multiplexed microfluidic perfusion cell culture device comprises at least (a) a cell culture chamber unit comprising at least two spatially distinct cell culture chambers, where each of cell culture chambers comprises a cell inoculation channel, a flow inlet and a flow outlet, and cell inoculation channel, flow inlet and flow outlet are separate elements, and (b) a microchannel network unit comprising a flow input channel connected to the flow inlet, and a flow output channel connected to the flow outlet, for each of cell culture chambers. The flow output channel or the flow outlet is equipped with a cell retention filter unit, where the cell retention filter unit comprises a porous filter with a pore size of less than 1 mu m. The device comprises one or more integrated sensors for assessing one or more process parameters, where each of the cell culture chambers comprises one or more integrated sensors. The distinct cell culture chamber has a volume of less than 100 mu l, preferably less than 20 mu l. Preferred Cell: The repertoire of cell lines is chosen from yeast, bacterial or mammalian cells.

PN CN112920733-A; CN215288621-U; WO2022218102-A1
TI Substrate-free anti-transfer roll or label with            RFID comprises printed layer, composite layer, metal            antenna layer, peelable thin layer, glue, and bottom            paper, where lower surface of printed layer is adhered            to upper surface of layer.
AU JIN N
   CAI Y
AE SHANGHAI ZHEYING COMML & TRADE CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI ZHEYING COMML & TRADE CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202169896C
AB    NOVELTY - Substrate-free anti-transfer roll or label                with RFID comprises printed layer, composite layer,                chip, metal antenna layer, easily peelable thin                layer, glue, and bottom paper, where the lower                surface of the printed layer is adhered to the                upper surface of the composite layer, the lower                surface of the composite layer is adhered to the                upper surface of the chip, the lower surface of the                chip is adhered to the upper surface of the metal                antenna layer. The lower surface of the metal                antenna layer is bonded to the upper surface of the                easily peelable thin layer, the lower surface of                the easily peelable thin layer is bonded to the                upper surface of the glue material, and the lower                surface of the glue material is bonded to the upper                surface of the base paper.
   USE - Substrate-free anti-transfer roll or label                with RFID.
   ADVANTAGE - The substrate-free anti-transfer roll or label                with RFID increases the speed of the bearing                substrate is peeled off and improves the                yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing a substrate-free RFID                transfer-resistant roll material or label, which                involves:
   (A) coating a layer of carbon dioxide on the                lower surface of the carrier substrate or                transparent protective film for printing colorless                isolation varnish to complete the production of the                printing layer;
   (B) coating a layer of peelable adhesive                composition on the upper surface of the supporting                substrate for the metal antenna to complete the                production of the easily peelable thin layer;
   (C) adhering the lower surface of the metal                antenna layer to the upper surface of the easily                peelable thin layer, using direct laser cutting, or                the etching antenna or the printing method of the                metal plating of the additive process, or                electroplating metal film;
   (D) compounding chip on the upper surface of                the metal antenna layer that has been bonded with                the easily peelable thin layer, and using                conductive glue to connect to generate a loop to                complete the production of the RFID dry                inlay;
   (E) tearing off the carrier substrate for the                metal antenna on the lower surface of the metal                antenna layer, and combining the lower surface of                the metal antenna layer on the lower side of the                dry inlay chip with the upper surface of the                adhesive material with bottom paper;
   (F) bonding printed layer with a carrier                substrate for printing or a transparent protective                film and the metal antenna layer with glue together                through a composite layer, where the composite                layer is made of an adhesive material, and the                adhesive material is made of hot melt adhesive,                water-based adhesive or double-sided adhesive                material; and
   (G) tearing off the carrier substrate for                printing, or keeping the peelable transparent                protective film according to customer requirements                and rolling or labeling according to customer                requirements.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The peelable                  transparent protective film is adhered to the                  upper surface of the printed layer. The printing                  layer adopts ink printing, transfer printing,                  electroplating or holographic production methods.                  The chip is placed on the upper or lower surface                  of the metal antenna layer, and the metal antenna                  layer is conductive metal materials, such as AL,                  CU or silver paste. The viscosity of the adhesive                  composition increases the speed at which the                  carrier substrate for the metal antenna is peeled                  off, and the curing method adopted is temperature                  curing or UV curing. The peelable adhesive                  composition is colorless insulate varnish or                  reduce viscosity glue. Preferred Components: The                  easily peelable thin layer is made of a peelable                  adhesive composition and the peeling adhesive                  composition is a colorless barrier varnish or a                  tack-reducing glue. The colorless isolation                  varnish is made of acrylic resin, monomer,                  photoinitiator, auxiliary or solvent composition,                  the material of the colorless barrier varnish has                  the characteristics of peeling, acid and alkali                  resistance, and chemical reagent resistance. The                  tack-reducing glue comprises reactive monomers                  such as methyl methacrylate, acrylic acid, and                  methacrylic acid, ethyl ester, methoxyethyl                  acrylate, butyl acrylate, reactive diluent, heat                  curing agent, antioxidant and solvent.

PN CN112553116-A
TI Probiotic culture medium comprises glucose, lactose, skimmed milk powder, soy peptone, betaine, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, Tween-80, deionized water and sodium hydroxide.
AU XING X
   WANG L
AE XING X (XING-Individual)
GA 202134558E
AB    NOVELTY - Probiotic culture medium comprises glucose, lactose, skimmed milk powder, soy peptone, betaine, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, Tween-80 (RTM: Polysorbate 80), deionized water and sodium hydroxide. The preparation and application method of the probiotic culture medium, involves dissolving glucose, lactose, skimmed milk powder, soy peptone, betaine and Tween-80 (RTM: Polysorbate 80) in deionized water, after the dissolution is complete, adding sodium hydroxide solution, adding probiotic culture medium to the dish, placing dish, coordinating with UV rays, performing sterilization, inoculating bifidobacteria in the sterilized dish with the streaking method, placing dish inoculated with bifidobacteria in a culture rack, adding sodium dihydrogen phosphate solution to the dish, culturing for 1-2 hours, culturing the dish in the freezer in a sealed state for freezing treatment, directly adding internal medium in a vacuum dryer and preparing bifidobacteria powder.
   USE - As probiotic culture medium.
   ADVANTAGE - The probiotic culture medium provides anaerobic environment for growth and reproduction of bifidobacteria using impact effect of the release of carbon dioxide, effectively prevents outside air, dust and impurities from entering the dish, and causes negative effects on the growth of bifidobacteria influences.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Probiotic culture medium comprises 5-8 pts. wt. glucose, 3-6 pts. wt. lactose, 8-10 pts. wt. skimmed milk powder, 1-2 pts. wt. soy peptone, 0.5-0.8 pt. wt. betaine, 0.1-0.2 pt. wt. sodium dihydrogen phosphate, 0.5-1 pt. wt. Tween-80 (RTM: Polysorbate 80), 12-14 pts. wt. deionized water and 2-3 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide. The preparation and application method of the probiotic culture medium, involves dissolving glucose, lactose, skimmed milk powder, soy peptone, betaine and Tween-80 (RTM: Polysorbate 80) in deionized water, after the dissolution is complete, adding sodium hydroxide solution to the mixture to adjust the pH to 6.5-7, obtaining probiotic culture medium, adding probiotic culture medium to the dish, placing dish in a high-temperature disinfection cabinet, controlling the temperature in the high-temperature sterilization cabinet at 115-120 degrees C, coordinating with UV rays, performing sterilization and disinfection together, inoculating bifidobacteria in the sterilized dish with the streaking method, placing dish inoculated with bifidobacteria in a culture rack for oxygen isolation and constant temperature culture, after the control time is 24-28 hours, adding sodium dihydrogen phosphate solution to the dish, culturing for 1-2 hours, culturing the dish in the freezer in a sealed state for freezing treatment, directly adding internal medium in a vacuum dryer for vacuum drying treatment and vacuum drying after completion, and preparing bifidobacteria powder. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for probiotic composition, which comprises bifidobacteria powder, starch, honey, white sugar, and enteric coating liquid, where spray probiotic powder on the surface of the core material is made of starch, honey and white sugar, and evenly coated enteric coating liquid on the outermost layer, prepared into uniform granules, and the probiotic embedded bead preparation is prepared after drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The probiotic culture medium comprises regulator. The regulator comprises sodium alginate and chitosan to coat sodium bicarbonate prepared sustained-release microcapsules. The regulator is used to neutralize the acid substances produced by the metabolism of bifidobacteria and adjust the pH value in the dish. The modifier includes corn starch and glucose. The raw materials for the preparation of the sustained-release microcapsules include sodium hydroxymethyl cellulose. The modifier is microcapsules wrapped in layers. The culture rack includes a base, a culture plate and a culture dish, where the base T design and the base is provided with a first cavity, the first cavity is filled with an aqueous solution, temperature control system is installed in the first cavity, the temperature control system is used to control the temperature of the aqueous solution, the culture plate has several design, the upper surface of the culture plate is fixedly connected with a connecting rod, the culture plate is provided with a connecting groove on the side surface close to the base, the connecting grooves are designed to extend through the culture plate to the connecting rod, the connecting grooves are all stepped designs, the connecting grooves are matched with the connecting rod, the connecting grooves are stepped A sealing ring is fixedly connected to the molded part, several culture discs are connected to each other through the connecting rod and the connecting groove, the first cavity and the connecting groove are mutually guided with upper surface of the culture plate is provided with evenly distributed first grooves, the upper surface of the culture plate is provided with a rotating groove, the culture plate is rotatably connected with a rotating plate through the rotating groove, the rotating plate and the first groove are both rotationally sealed and connected, guide block is fixedly connected to the side of the rotating plate away from the culture plate, the guide block is provided with a conduction groove, the conduction groove is designed to penetrate the guide block and the rotating plate, the guide block is rotatably connected with a sealing cover on the side away from the rotating plate, the sliding plate is elastically connected in the first groove through a spring, the rotating disk is located between the first groove gap and is provided with a filling groove, the filling groove and the connecting groove are connected to each other, the sliding plate is provided with a first sliding groove, the top block matches with the first sliding groove, the lower surface of the first sliding groove and the top block are elastically connected by a spring. In the initial state, the sliding plate is located on the top of the first groove by the spring force, and the top block upper surface is flush with the upper surface of the sliding plate. The dish includes a housing, a sliding box and a cover plate. The housing and cover plate are connected in a sealed manner. The sliding box is slidably connected to the inside of the housing. The bottom of the sliding box is elastically connected with the housing through a spring. The housing bottom is provided with a through hole. The diameter of the through hole is more than the diameter of the top block. The side wall of the sliding box is provided with a first air outlet, and the side wall of the housing is provided with a second outlet air hole. The first air outlet and the second air outlet are not connected in the initial state. The bottom of the culture plate is provided with a second cavity. The second cavity passes through a one-way conduit and a first groove second cavity extends to the side wall of the rotating disc, and the side wall of the rotating disc is provided with an air filling port. The squeezing sac is fixedly connected to the inner bottom of the first groove. The squeezing sac passes through a one-way catheter top of the second cavity is connected. The first groove is located above the sliding plate and is connected to the bottom of the second cavity through a one-way pipe. The sliding plate is fixedly connected with uniformly distributed one-way plugs.

TI High-tenacity anti-static foaming polypropylene            comprises e.g. colorless polypropylene particles,            colored polypropylene particles, carbon black,            antistatic agent, bacteriostatic agent, anti-aging            agent, lubricant and trimethyl phosphate.
AU GU P
AE JIANGSU HAOSHENG PLASTIC TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202113211J
AB    NOVELTY - High-tenacity antistatic foaming polypropylene                comprises 20-25 pts. wt. colorless polypropylene                particles, 10-15 pts. wt. colored polypropylene                particles, 2-4 pts. wt. carbon black, 5-10 pts. wt.                antistatic agent, 2-3 pts. wt. bacteriostatic                agent, 3-5 pts. wt. anti-aging agent, 2-5 pts. wt.                lubricant, 8-10 pts. wt. ring fatty acid methyl                ester (sic), 4-8 pts. wt. trimethyl phosphate and                3-5 pts. wt. sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate.
   USE - Used as High-tenacity anti-static foaming                polypropylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-tenacity antistatic foaming                polypropylene: has light weight; heat insulation;                involves screening for two times in the process of                preparation, each time screening the uniform size                of the particles, ensuring the foaming                polypropylene particle size is uniform; involves                addition of phenol with benzotrichloride and                N,N-diphenyl-p-phenylenediamine for mixing,                improving the anti-aging performance of the                product; involves addition of bromine water in the                preparation process, the combustion process will                emit inert gas, thus reduces the oxygen content,                reaching a certain flame retarding purpose, thus                improves the advantages of the flame retardant                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing high-tenacity antistatic foaming                polypropylene, comprising (i) introducing phenol,                N,N-diphenyl-p-phenylenediamine into ice water,                adding bromine water and carbon disulfide, stirring                for 1-3 hours, heating to 80-100 degrees C, adding                ethyl vanillin, vanillin and stirring to obtain                functional component, (ii) mixing the colorless                polypropylene particles and colored polypropylene                particles, heating at the temperature is 200-230                degrees C to the melting state, adding carbon                black, stirring at 500-600 revolutions/minute to                obtain mixture A, (iii) adding functional                component, ring fatty acid methyl ester (sic) and                trimethyl phosphate into mixture A, stirring,                adding benzotrichloride, sodium stearate, magnesium                stearate, stirring, introducing into double screw                extruder, heating and plasticizing by screw,                extruding into filament, introducing the obtained                filament into water at temperature of 35-40 degrees                C, cooling to obtain the polypropylene filament,                (iv) cutting the polypropylene filament, the                cutting head is provided with a blowing device,                cutting into particles to obtain polypropylene                particles, introducing the polypropylene particles                into the sieving bed, vibrating and sieving to                obtain uniform length of polypropylene particles,                (v) introducing the polypropylene particles                obtained in the step (iv) into foaming reaction                kettle, foaming, adding 1-dodecanesulfonic acid                sodium salt, octadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide,                sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, talc, kaolin,                glycerol, stirring, adding water, liquid carbon                dioxide, heating, boosting, discharging to obtain                foaming polypropylene A, (vi) sending the obtained                foaming polypropylene A into the foaming reaction                kettle, foaming for the second time, adding sodium                dodecyl sulfonate, octadecyltrimethylammonium                bromide, sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, glycerol,                stirring, adding talc, kaolin, heating, boosting to                obtain foaming polypropylene B, (vii) washing the                foaming polypropylene B by water, introducing into                dehydrating machine, dehydrating, drying by hot air                blowing by dehydrating, (viii) drying the                polypropylene particles for secondary screening,                screening the foaming polypropylene particles with                uniform length to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The antistatic agent                  is composed of 5-7 pts. wt.                  octadecyltrimethylammonium bromide and 3-7 pts.                  wt. 1-dodecanesulfonic acid sodium salt. The                  bacteriostatic agent is composed of 5-7 pts. wt.                  ethyl vanillin and 2-4 pts. wt. vanillin. The                  anti-aging agent is composed of 5-10 pts. wt.                  benzotrichloride, 2-5 pts. wt. phenol and 5-8                  pts. wt. N,N-diphenyl-p-phenylenediamine. The                  lubricant is composed of 5-7 pts. wt. glycerol,                  2-4 pts. wt. sodium stearate and 1-3 pts. wt.                  magnesium stearate. In step (v): the weight ratio                  between talc, kaolin and sodium                  dodecylbenzenesulfonate is                  0.4-0.5:0.3-0.4:0.6-0.7. Preferred Method: In                  step (v) and step (vi): the foaming reaction                  kettle is provided with a spacer; the foaming                  reaction kettle is heated by introducing steam;                  the pressure of the steam is 0.25 0.35 MPa, the                  temperature is 150-180 degrees C; the flow speed                  of the steam is 5-7 t/hour. The preparation                  method comprises after foaming for one time,                  conveying the particles by the pipeline between                  each process, the conveying wind can be used. In                  the step (v): the pressurizing way is introducing                  carbon dioxide. In step (vi): the pressurizing                  way is introducing air.

PN CN112156797-A; CN112156797-B
TI Catalyst for producing dimethyl carbonate through transesterification of cyclic carbonate and methanol, is a metal oxide and potassium fluoride catalyst supported by zinc aluminum iron oxide.
AU LI D
   GUO H
   CHEN C
   XIAO Y
   JIA L
   HOU B
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 202104099B
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst is a metal oxide (LO alpha ) and potassium fluoride (KF) catalyst supported by zinc aluminum iron oxide (ZnxAlyFezO), and mass ratio of ZnxAlyFezO to LO alpha and KF is 100:2-15:1-5, and x, y, z, and alpha are the number of atoms in the molecular formula.
   USE - Catalyst for producing dimethyl carbonate through transesterification of cyclic carbonate and methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has the advantage of easy separation of catalyst and product, and compared with other solid base catalysts, it has stronger basicity, can improve the efficiency of the transesterification reaction of cyclic carbonate and methanol, and has a higher yield of dimethyl carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing a catalyst for producing dimethyl carbonate through transesterification reaction of cyclic carbonate and methanol, whic involves dissolving soluble salts of zinc, aluminum, and iron in deionized water to prepare solution A with a total metal ion concentration of 0.50-1.50 mol/L; dissolving ammonia, ammonium carbonate ((NH4)2CO3) in deionized water to prepare solution B with an hydroxide (OH) ion concentration of 1.00-4.00 mol/L and a carbonate (CO32-) ion concentration of 0.25-1.00 mol/L; co-precipitating solution A and solution B under ultrasonic agitation at a precipitation temperature of 20-30 degrees C and pH=8.0-9.0 to obtain slurry C; sealing and aging slurry C under hydrothermal conditions of 130-180 degrees C for 6-24 hours to obtain slurry D, and filtering slurry D, then washing, and rotary evaporating and drying at 100-160 degrees C for 10-24 hours to obtain the precursor of ZnxAlyFezO; placing the precursor of ZnxAlyFezO in a vacuum device and keep the vacuum at 0.01-0.09 kilopascal; dissolving the soluble precursor salt/alkali of LO alpha and KF in deionized water to obtain solution E, the concentration of L ion in solution E is 0.20-5.00 mol/L, and the concentration of fluoride (F-) is 0.05-0.30 mol/L; keeping the vacuum degree of 0.01-0.09 kilopascal, and dropping solution E onto the precursor of ZnxAlyFezO under ultrasonic conditions; and sonicating for 30-120 minutes, and after the sonication is completed, drying the resulting product at 80-120 degrees C for 12-24 hours, and then calcining at 400-650 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain the desired catalyst; and
   (2) a method for using catalyst for the production of dimethyl carbonate by transesterification of cyclic carbonate and methanol, which involves obtaining cyclic carbonate by cycloaddition reaction of epoxide, and obtaining ethylene carbonate by cycloaddition reaction of ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide (CO2), and obtaining the propylene carbonate by cycloaddition reaction of propylene oxide and CO2, and the molar ratio of cyclic carbonate to methanol is 1:2-10.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of zinc:aluminum:iron (Zn:Al:Fe) in the zinc-aluminum-iron oxide ZnxAlyFezO is 100:20-45:1-5. Preferred Components: The metal oxide LO alpha is one of alkali metal oxides, alkaline earth metal oxides and lanthanide metal oxides, preferably sodium oxide (Na2O), potassium oxide (K2O), magnesium oxide (MgO), calcium oxide (CaO) or barium oxide (BaO). The soluble salt of zinc can be one of zinc nitrate, zinc chloride, and zinc acetate; the soluble salt of aluminum can be one of aluminum nitrate, aluminum chloride, and aluminum sulfate. The soluble salt of iron may be one of iron nitrate and iron chloride. The soluble precursor salt/alkali of LO alpha is one of sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, potassium hydroxide, potassium carbonate, magnesium nitrate, calcium nitrate, barium nitrate, strontium nitrate, lanthanum nitrate or cerium nitrate. The cyclic carbonate is one of ethylene carbonate or propylene carbonate. The resulting transesterification product is dimethyl carbonate and 1,2-propylene glycol.

PT P
PN CN112138632-A
TI Separating heavy metals from soil using water-floating charcoal, involves oxygen separating biomass particles, spreading water-floating carbon particles on soil, plowing, utilizing recovered water to float carbon, drying and refining.
AU TU Z
   YANG S
   WANG Y
   ZHENG J
   ZENG J
   ZHANG Z
   WANG N
   JIE H
AE ZHEJIANG GREEN ROCK ENVIRONMENTAL PROTEC (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021030865
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating heavy metals from soil using water-floating charcoal, involves (a) preparing water floating carbon by (i) cutting biomass materials to 1-3 cm granular biomass material as raw material, and (ii) placing selected biomass particles in mechanical kiln or soil kiln for oxygen separation, watering and cooling, and (b) separating soil heavy metal by (I) uniformly spreading water-floating carbon particles on surface of soil, and uniformly plowing, (II) maintaining soil moisture after discharging, and detecting heavy metal concentration of soil, (III) pouring water until water level is 5-20 cm above surface layer of soil, and continuously turning stirring soil, draining water to enable water floating carbon for discharging out of water along with water flow, and recycling, and (IV) utilizing recovered water to float carbon, drying in sun for serving as fuel carbon, and performing heavy metal refining on burned ash for using.
   USE - Method for separating and separating heavy metals from soil using water-floating charcoal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves the purpose of radically eradicating heavy metal pollution in soil, ensures short treatment cycle, low energy consumption, excellent effect and no harm side effects, is economical, simple and environmentally-friendly, reduces the carbon dioxide emission, and increases the farmer income.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for separating heavy metals from soil using water-floating charcoal, involves (a) preparing water floating carbon by (i) cutting biomass materials to 1-3 cm granular biomass material as raw material for preparing water floating carbon, and (ii) drying selected biomass particles until the water content is lower than 50%, placing selected biomass particles in mechanical kiln or soil kiln for oxygen separation and roasting at 450-700 degrees C, heating at from temperature to 450-700 degrees C, watering for allowing mechanical kiln or soil kiln, cooling to room temperature to obtain outer layer, which is carbonized, while the inner core is not carbonized and is lighter than water-floating biomass charcoal particles, and (b) separating soil heavy metal by (I) uniformly spreading obtained water-floating carbon particles on surface of soil, mixing obtained water-floating carbon particles to soil using plowing machine, and uniformly plowing, (II) maintaining soil moisture after discharging, and detecting heavy metal concentration of soil every 3-15 days until heavy metal concentration of soil reaches treatment target, (III) pouring water until water level is 5-20 cm above surface layer of soil, and continuously turning the stirring soil using mini-tillage mixer to enable water floating carbon in soil for floating to water surface using virtue of buoyancy of water, draining water to enable water floating carbon for discharging out of water along with water flow, and recycling water floating carbon in water separation, gauze or cloth bag, and repeating the process until the water floating carbon recovering, and (IV) utilizing recovered water to float carbon, drying in sun for serving as fuel carbon, and performing heavy metal refining on burned ash for using.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In step (a), the biomass material comprises bamboo and wood scraps, firewood, straw and/or nut shells having lighter specific gravity than water.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

PN CN112042753-A
TI Modified milk powder used for regulating blood sugar of diabetes patient, comprises whey protein powder, milk powder raw material, prebiotics, casein phosphopeptide, complex vitamin and complex mineral substance.
AU YIN L
AE GANSU HUALING CASEIN LTD (GANS-Non-standard)
   GANSU HUALING BIO-TECHNOLOGY RES CENT (GANS-Non-standard)
   GANNAN YAK MILK RES INST (GANN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C7209Y
AB    NOVELTY - Modified milk powder comprises 35-45 pts. wt. whey protein powder, 50-60 pts. wt. milk powder raw material, 10-12 pts. wt. prebiotics, 0.2-0.3 pt. wt. casein phosphopeptide, 0.15-0.25 pt. wt. complex vitamin and 1.15-1.25 pts. wt. complex mineral substance.
   USE - The modified milk powder is used for regulating blood sugar of diabetes patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The milk powder contains chromium, has high dietary fiber formulation, is rich in high quality protein and supplements nutrient substance and promotes absorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the modified milk powder, which involves (a) densely transporting the milk powder raw materials, whey protein powder, prebiotics and casein phosphopeptides into the designated metering powder bin in accordance with a predetermined ratio, then feeding into a high-speed shearing tank to dissolve complex vitamins and complex minerals in separate tanks, where the water-soluble ratio is greater than or equal to 1:20, and mixing in the high-speed shear tank in order, (b) first circulating hot water between the high-speed shearing tank and the mixing tank and then adding the raw and auxiliary materials into the high-speed shearing tank for dissolution by mixing at 40-50 degrees C for 35-60 minutes, (c) fixing the volume in the mixing tank, homogenizing at 18-22 Mpa and 55-65 degrees C and cooling to less than 15 degrees C degrees C, (d) preheating the material liquid in a preheater and flash evaporation, directly spraying the preheated material liquid with clean steam, sterilizing at 90-100 degrees C for 15 seconds, evaporating and concentrating the sterilized material liquid in a three-effect falling film evaporator, where the temperature of each effect body is observed to ensure that the discharge density is 995-1190 kg/m3, (e) fitering the thick concentrate through the double filter into high-pressure pump, then transporting to the top of the drying tower by the high-pressure pump for atomization at 130-220 bar, inlet air temperature of 100-200 degrees C and outlet air temperature of 50-90 degrees C, (f) filtering the outside air by primary, middle and high efficiency filters, then heating in a heater into the fixed fluidized bed for secondary drying of the milk powder, and controlling the fixed bed inlet temperature at 60-85 degrees C, (g) filtering and dehumidifying the outside air by the primary, middle and high efficiency filters, feeding into the vibrating fluidized bed to cool the milk powder, sieving the milk powder through the vibrating screen, then passing through the vibrating powder conveyor into temporary storage in warehouse and performing temporary storage for less than or equal to 12 hours at 20-35 degrees C and (h) using vacuum nitrogen or carbon dioxide for air-conditioned pouch packing to obtain the product and checking the removal of metal foreign bodies regularly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The milk powder raw material comprises whole yak milk powder and skimmed milk powder in a weight ratio of 1:1-1:1.5. The whey protein powder has protein content of greater than or equal to 80%. The prebiotic comprises fructooligosaccharides and inulin in a weight ratio of 1:2-1:3. The complex vitamin comprises vitamin A, vitamin D, vitamin E, vitamin C, and vitamin B6. The complex minerals comprises compounded by calcium, magnesium, iron, zinc, selenium and chromium.
   ACTIVITY - Hyperglycemic; Hypoglycemic.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PT P
PN CN111939987-A; CN111939987-B
TI Photocatalytic material for preparing synthesis gas, is mixture of cadmium selenide semiconductor photocatalyst prepared using modified water-soluble cadmium sulfide quantum dots, and water-soluble metal complex catalyst.
AU WANG H
   HU R
   XIE W
AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 2020B73073
AB    NOVELTY - A photocatalytic material is a cadmium selenide semiconductor photocatalyst modified by a sulfur-containing molecule with the ability to capture light-generated holes and a long-chain alkanoic acid containing a mercapto group as a ligand at the same time. Alternately, photocatalytic material is a mixture of a cadmium selenide semiconductor photocatalyst and a water-soluble metal complex catalyst (I). The photocatalyst is prepared by adding mercapto-containing long-chain alkanoic acid-modified water-soluble cadmium sulfide quantum dots into the ethanol aqueous solution, ultrasonically dispersing to obtain a transparent mixed solution (A), adding sulfur-containing molecules, stirring to produce a mixed solution (B), filtering, adding isopropanol, filtering, washing, and drying in vacuum.
   USE - Photocatalytic material for preparing synthesis gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst material enables economical manufacture of syngas in varying proportion with high productivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A photocatalytic material is a cadmium selenide semiconductor photocatalyst modified by a sulfur-containing molecule with the ability to capture light-generated holes and a long-chain alkanoic acid containing a mercapto group as a ligand at the same time. Alternately, photocatalytic material is a mixture of a cadmium selenide semiconductor photocatalyst and a water-soluble metal complex catalyst. The photocatalyst is prepared by adding mercapto-containing long-chain alkanoic acid-modified water-soluble cadmium sulfide quantum dots into the ethanol aqueous solution, ultrasonically dispersing to obtain a transparent mixed solution (A), adding sulfur-containing molecules, stirring to produce a mixed solution (B), filtering, adding isopropanol, filtering, washing, and drying in vacuum. The metal complex catalyst is of formula (I).
   X = Cl-, CH3COO-, NO3-, ClO4-;and
   R = 1-8C alkyl.
   (i) When n is 2, M is Ni2+, Co2+ or Fe2+; and (ii) when n is 3, M is Fe3+. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of synthesis gas by photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, which involves reacting 2,9-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline hydrate of formula (a) with selenium oxide to obtain 1,10-phenanthroline-2,9-dicarbaldehyde of formula (A), reducing obtained compound with sodium borohydride in absolute ethanol as a solvent to produce (9-(hydroxymethyl)-1,10-phenanthrolin-2-yl)methanol of formula (B), heating in a mixture of hydrogen bromide and acetic acid, performing bromination reaction to obtain 2,9-bis(bromomethyl)-1,10-phenanthroline of formula (C), performing substitution reaction of the obtained compound and amino compound of formula (D) in acetonitrile as a solvent in the presence of N,N-diisopropylethylamine to obtain prop-2-en-1-yl((9-((prop-2-en-1-ylamino)methyl)-1,10-phenanthrolin-2-yl)methyl)amine of formula (I), and coordinating the obtained compound and a metal salt in acetonitrile as a solvent. The metal salt is nickel chloride, nickel acetate, nickel perchlorate, cobalt chlorate, cobalt acetate, cobalt chlorate, iron chloride, iron acetate, iron chlorate, cobalt nitrate, nickel nitrate or iron nitrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sulfur-containing molecules with the ability to capture photo-generated holes are selected from 2-ethylthiophene, 2-vinylthiophene, 2,2'-dithiophene, 2,3-dihydrothiophene, phenothiazine, N-methylphenothiazine, 10-propionylphenothiazine, tetrahydrothiophene, 3-methoxythiophene and 2-chlorothiophene. The long-chain alkanoic acid containing mercapto group is derived from 3-mercaptopropionic acid, 6-mercaptohexanoic acid, 11-mercaptoundecanoic acid, thioglycolic acid, 3-mercaptoisobutyric acid or 4-mercaptobutyric acid. Preferred Composition: The ratio of modified water-soluble quantum dots to sulfur-containing molecules is 6 g:9-90 mmol. Preferred Property: The average particle size of the semiconductor photocatalyst is 2-7 nm. Preferred Method: The photocatalytic material is added to a transparent reactor filled with distilled water, and ultrasonically dispersed to obtain a transparent mixed solution. An electronic sacrificial agent is added to the mixed solution, ultrasonically dispersed, and acid or alkali is added to adjust the pH of the solution to 4-10. The solution is further dispersed, and carbon dioxide gas is supplied and irradiated with visible light at room temperature to prepare synthesis gas. The concentration of the semiconductor photocatalyst in the mixed solution is 0.05-0.5 mg/mL. The concentration of the water-soluble metal complex catalyst in the mixed solution is 1x 10-4 to 9x 10-4 mol/ L. The electronic sacrificial agent is triethylamine or triethanolamine. The concentration of electronic sacrificial agent in the mixed solution is 0.05-2 mol/L.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN111926386-A; CN111926386-B
TI Preparing calcium carbonate whiskers involves            dissolving silk fibroin and gum arabic in deionized            water and fully stirring to obtain crystal form control            agent for use, and taking high-purity nano-calcium            oxide and deionized water.
AU WEI M
AE GUANGXI XIAYANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUAN-Non-standard)
   DUAN CHUNXU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (DUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B54351
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium carbonate whiskers involves                dissolving silk fibroin and gum arabic in deionized                water and fully stirring to obtain a crystal form                control agent for use. The mass ratio of the silk                fibroin and gum arabic is 2:(1-4). The high-purity                nano-calcium oxide and deionized water are taken in                a mass ratio of 1:(3-5), stirred and mixed to                obtain first slurry. The first slurry is passed                through a 100-mesh sieve, it is filtered to remove                residue, and then added (13-15) times the mass of                first slurry to the slurry to dilute with deionized                water to obtain second slurry, and then added the                crystal form control agent to second slurry, heated                to 50-60 degrees C and stirred at constant                temperature for 1-2 hours to obtain third slurry.                The mass ratio of the added amount of the crystal                form control agent to first slurry is                (1-5):100.
   USE - Method for preparing calcium carbonate                whiskers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare calcium                carbonate whiskers which solves the problem of                difficult crystal form control in the production of                aragonite-type calcium carbonate whiskers, and the                problem that aragonite-type calcium carbonate is                easily converted into calcite-type calcium                carbonate, and obtains needle-shaped calcium                carbonate whiskers with high activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing calcium carbonate whiskers involves                dissolving silk fibroin and gum arabic in deionized                water and fully stir to obtain a crystal form                control agent for use. The mass ratio of the silk                fibroin and gum arabic is 2:(1-4). The high-purity                nano-calcium oxide and deionized water are taken in                a mass ratio of 1:(3-5), stirred and mixed to                obtain first slurry. The first slurry is passed                through a 100-mesh sieve, it is filtered to remove                residue, and then added (13-15) times the mass of                first slurry to the slurry to dilute with deionized                water to obtain second slurry, and then added the                crystal form control agent to second slurry, heated                to 50-60 degrees C and stirred at constant                temperature for 1-2 hours to obtain third slurry.                The mass ratio of the added amount of the crystal                form control agent to first slurry is (1-5):100.                The ammonia water is added to the third slurry to                adjust the pH to 7-8, and then put it into the                high-gravity carbonization reactor, control the                carbonization temperature to 80-90 degrees C, and                the carbon dioxide flow rate to 100-150 L/h. The                rotor speed in the high gravity carbonization                reactor is 2000-2500r/min, and the carbonization                time is 1-2 hours. The obtained slurry, after the                carbonization is centrifuged to obtain the                precipitate, and the precipitate is dried at 95-100                degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain calcium carbonate                whiskers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The stirring time is                  12-24 hours, and the stirring speed is                  100-200r/min. The purity of the high-purity                  nano-calcium oxide is above 99.5%, and the                  particle size is 10-100 nm. The stirring speed is                  300-400 r/min. The conditions for centrifugation                  are centrifugation at 3000r/min for 3-5                  minutes.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.)
MC A03-A; A03-C01; A08-M10; A08-R; A08-R09; A08-S02

PT P
PN US2020325091-A1; WO2020214574-A1; US10981850-B2
TI Cannabidiol or derivative preparation involves            providing solution comprising diene compound, allylic            alcohol or allylic ether and pyridine compound or            pharmaceutically acceptable salt or ester into            packed-bed reactor comprising solid.
AU PORCO J A
   BEELER A B
   BROWN L E
   TRILLES R V
AE UNIV BOSTON (UBOS-C)
   UNIV BOSTON (UBOS-C)
   TRILLES R V (TRIL-Individual)
GA 2020A03829
AB    NOVELTY - Cannabidiol (CBD) or derivative preparation                involves providing a solution comprising 1,3-diene                compound, an allylic alcohol or an allylic ether                and pyridine or pyridin-4(3H)-one compound (I) or                (II) or pharmaceutically acceptable salt or ester                into a packed-bed reactor (PBR) comprising a solid                or heterogeneous catalyst. The provided compounds                are circulated through the PBR to react the                compounds, where the resulting solution is                collected from the PBR comprising CBD or its                derivative.
   USE - Method for preparing cannabinoids, such as                cannabidiol (CBD) and derivatives.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the cannabidiol or                derivative in a simple, cost-effective and                eco-friendly manner with improved overall yield,                material throughput, and product purity with                minimal amounts of any toxic or difficult to remove                byproducts to meet active pharmaceutical ingredient                quality standards without necessarily requiring                additional purification processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cannabidiol (CBD) or derivative preparation                involves providing a solution comprising 1,3-diene                compound, an allylic alcohol or an allylic ether                and pyridine or pyridin-4(3H)-one compound of                formula (I) or (II) or pharmaceutically acceptable                salt or ester into a packed-bed reactor (PBR)                comprising a solid or heterogeneous catalyst. The                provided compounds are circulated through the PBR                to react the compounds, where the resulting                solution is collected from the PBR comprising CBD                or its derivative.
   Z1=CR2 or N;
   Z2=CR4 or N;
   R1, R3, and R5=independently selected from H,                OH, -CO2H, protected hydroxyl, alkyl, alkenyl,                alkynyl, acyl, aryl, heteroaryl, cycloalkyl,                heterocycle, -X2RK, or halides;
   R2 and R4=independently selected from H, OH,                -CO2H, protected hydroxyl, alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl,                acyl, aryl, heteroaryl, cycloalkyl, heterocycle,                -X2RK and not selected from halides, where the                alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl or acyl is optionally                substituted with substituents independently                selected from halogen, -OH, alkyl, O-alkyl, -NRARB,                -S-alkyl, -SO-alkyl, -SO2-alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl,                aryl, heteroaryl, cycloalkyl or heterocycle, and                the alkenyl, alkynyl, or acyl group optionally                includes -O-, -S-, -SO2-, -N(RG)- substituting                carbons in the carbon chain, where the aryl or                heteroaryl, where alone or a substituent group is                optionally substituted with substituents                independently selected from halogen, OH, alkyl,                O-alkyl, -COOH, -C(O)-1-4C alkyl, -C(O)O-1-4C                alkyl, -NRCRD, -S-alkyl, -SO-alkyl and                -SO2-alkyl;
   X1=selected from -O-, -S-, -SO2-,                -N(RE)-;
   X2=selected from -O-, -S-, -SO2-, or -N(RF)-;                and
   RA, RB, RC, RD, RE, RF, RK and                RG=independently selected from H and 1-4C                alkyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                automated, scalable, continuous flow reactor system                for preparation of CBD or derivative, which                comprises one or multiple modular flow chemistry                assembly lines comprising controlled input from                source pumps for reagents, such as diverse aromatic                compounds and oxygenated species or diene species,                a packed bed reactor, and optionally heated reactor                incorporating membrane separators for waste carbon                dioxide, an auxiliary pump, a membrane separator                for waste water, and collection tanks for the CBD                or derivative.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compounds:The compound cannabidiol                  (CBD) or derivative also has formulae                  (III)-(V)..

PT P
PN CN111037066-A
TI Welding technology for marine steel-aluminum composite material, comprises e.g. mechanically milling composite material to create a groove form, washing the area to be welded on both sides after assembling, preheating and post processing.
AU LI L
AE CSSC XIJIANG SHIPBUILDING CO LTD (CHSB-C)
GA 2020346923
AB    NOVELTY - Welding technology for marine steel-aluminum composite material, comprises e.g. (i) mechanically milling composite material to create a groove form, (ii) welding using a assembling gap control range of the joint is 5-8 mm, dislocation edge amount is not greater than 1 mm, and the assembling gap of the corner joint is not greater than 5-8 mm, (iii) washing the area to be welded on both sides within 20 mm to remove oxide film and dirt after assembling, (iv) preheating sides of the area to be welded at least 30 mm at 50-55 degrees C before welding when the ambient temperature is less than 5 degrees C or relative humidity is greater than 85%, (v) adjusting the welding parameter, sequentially welding steel and aluminum, and controlling the size of current, (vi) jointing by multilayer welding, where the thickness of each pass is not greater than 4 mm, (vii) polishing the welding after welding is finished, drilling and filling the steel-aluminum transition area with epoxy resin, and (viii) checking flushing water tightness.
   USE - The method is useful for welding technology for marine steel-aluminum composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the problem of difficult welding of butt joint of steel-aluminum composite material; ensures watertightness between the joints; reduces amount of rework and difficulty of construction; and improves efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Welding technology for marine steel-aluminum composite material, comprises (i) mechanically milling composite material to create a groove form, (ii) welding using a assembling gap control range of the joint is 5-8 mm, dislocation edge amount is not greater than 1 mm, and the assembling gap of the corner joint is not greater than 5-8 mm, (iii) washing the area to be welded on both sides within 20 mm to remove oxide film and dirt after assembling, (iv) preheating sides of the area to be welded at least 30 mm at 50-55 degrees C before welding when the ambient temperature is less than 5 degrees C or relative humidity is greater than 85%, (v) adjusting the welding parameter, sequentially welding steel and aluminum, and controlling the size of current, the welding current is 90-130 A, the welding voltage is 22-24 V when using an electric arc welding, the welding current is 160-200 A, the welding voltage is 22-24 V, the gas flow rate is 15-20 l/minute when using carbon dioxide protection welding, the welding current is 150-180 A, the welding voltage is 20.5-21.5 V, the gas flow rate is 20-25 l/minute when using melting pole argon arc welding, (vi) jointing by multilayer welding, where the thickness of each pass is not greater than 4 mm, (vii) polishing the welding after welding is finished, drilling and filling the steel-aluminum transition area with epoxy resin, and (viii) checking flushing water tightness.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (vi) comprises cleaning the layers and confirming that there are no defects before welding the next layer before the implementation of the multi-layer welding process, and staggering the welding joints of each layer from each other, where the temperature between the layers is not greater than 150 degrees C. Preferred Components: The electrode grade for electric arc welding is E4303 or E5015. The welding material brand number of carbon dioxide protection welding is ER501T-1 or ER50-6. The grade of welding material used in melting pole argon arc welding is ER5183.

PN DE102019002136-A1
TI Fertilizer obtained from natural substances by mixing e.g. animal manure, homogenizing to break lignite information, hygienizing substrate, removing ammonia nitrogen or aerating substrate, and making end products.
AU 
AE LANDSCHULZE W (LAND-Individual)
GA 202024735R
AB    NOVELTY - Fertilizer obtained from natural substances and natural raw materials and the process for producing the fertilizer are claimed. The process involves (A) measuring, pre-comminuting and mixing 30-60 pts. wt. liquid or solid fermentation residues, 20-40 pts. wt. animal excrement or animal manure, 10-40 pts. wt. raw brown coal and 10-20 pts. wt. rock powder to form a liquid substrate, (B) comminuting and homogenizing to break down lignite information from the cellulose fractions of the substrate, (C) hygienizing the substrate in a mixing container or in a hygienization container under sufficient temperature influence and sufficient residence time, (D) (i) removing the ammonia nitrogen contained in the substrate in a reactor or (ii) aerating the substrate in a MAP reactor and enriching in doses with fixed magnesium chloride (MgCl2), or (iii) bounding the substrate in a precipitation container with phosphorus salts and (E) making the intermediate and/or end products.
   USE - The fertilizer is useful as an ecological soil-forming soil conditioner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fertilizer obtained from natural substances and natural raw materials and the process for producing the fertilizer are claimed. The process involves (A) measuring, pre-comminuting and mixing 30-60 pts. wt. liquid or solid fermentation residues, 20-40 pts. wt. animal excrement or animal manure, 10-40 pts. wt. raw brown coal and 10-20 pts. wt. rock powder to form a liquid substrate, (B) comminuting and homogenizing to break down lignite information from the cellulose fractions of the substrate in a homogenizer such as a colloid mill or a dispersing machine and stored, (C) hygienizing the substrate in a mixing container by adding quicklime or calcium oxide (CaO) with the liquefaction of ammonia (NH3) for conversion to ammonium (NH4) compounds or in a hygienization container under sufficient temperature influence and sufficient residence time, where the process heat can be used, (D) (i) removing the ammonia nitrogen contained in the substrate in a reactor by the action of heat and removing negative pressure on the dispersed and hygienized substrate by 70-90% by removing the nitrogen as ammonia and using REA gypsum as the absorbent converted to ammonium sulfate (ASL) and lime (CaCO3) and withdrawing liquid fertilizer (h) or (ii) aerating the substrate in a MAP reactor and enriching in doses with fixed magnesium chloride (MgCl2), increasing the pH being to 9 by stripping off carbon dioxide (CO2), sedimenting the precipitated magnesium ammonium phosphate and packaging as a solid fertilizer and the remaining liquid substrate as a liquid fertilizer, or (iii) bounding the substrate in a precipitation container with phosphorus salts organically and minerally to calcium phosphate and to magnesium phosphate and to ammonium phosphate and (E) making the intermediate and/or end products, preferably by water separation in the separation process and/or further addition of rock powder.
TF ACTIVITY - Fertilizer. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Bioelectrochemical system for recovery of sewage nitrogen and phosphorus synchronous carbon fixation and hydrogen production has collector for displaying current of system and data collector connected to both ends of resistor using wire.
AU ZHANG Z
   WANG Z
   ZHANG J
   XIA S
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 202028484C
AB    NOVELTY - The bioelectrochemical system for recovery of sewage nitrogen and phosphorus synchronous carbon fixation and hydrogen production has bipolar membrane between anode chamber and acid generating chamber. An anion exchange membrane is installed between the acid generating chamber and desalting chamber. A cation exchange membrane is provided between the desalination chamber and cathode chamber. A gas-permeable and hydrophobic membrane is arranged between cathode chamber and absorption chamber. The biological anode chamber is filled with carbon, nitrogen and phosphorus sewage. The acid generating chamber and absorption chamber are filled with electrolyte solution. The external circuit has external power supply, a wire, a resistor and a collector. The collector is a data collector, which is used to display the current of the system. The data collector is connected to both ends of the resistor through wire.
   USE - Bioelectrochemical system for recovery of sewage nitrogen and phosphorus synchronous carbon fixation and hydrogen production.
   ADVANTAGE - The system degrades organic matter in sewage to produce electrons, hydrogen ion and carbon dioxide, neutralizes hydrogen ion, maintains neutral environment, absorbs ammonia that passes through the breathable hydrophobic membrane, purifies hydrogen, realizes simultaneous recovery of sewage nitrogen and phosphorus, fixes carbon and obtains calcium carbonate and hydrogen as by-product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The bioelectrochemical system for recovery of sewage nitrogen and phosphorus synchronous carbon fixation and hydrogen production has bipolar membrane (8) between anode chamber (1) and acid generating chamber (2). An anion exchange membrane (9) is installed between the acid generating chamber and desalting chamber (3). A cation exchange membrane (10) is provided between the desalination chamber and cathode chamber. A gas-permeable and hydrophobic membrane (11) is arranged between cathode chamber (4) and absorption chamber (5). The acid generating chamber and absorption chamber are connected through external pipeline (13) through a pump (15). The biological anode chamber is filled with carbon, nitrogen and phosphorus sewage. The acid generating chamber and absorption chamber are filled with low concentration electrolyte solution. The biological anode chamber and cathode chamber are respectively provided with an anode (6) and a cathode (7). The external circuit has external power supply (18), a wire (19), a resistor (20) and a collector (21). The external power supply is a regulated external power supply. The collector is a data collector, which is used to display the current of the system. The data collector is connected to both ends of the resistor through wire. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for processing the bioelectrochemical system for recovery of sewage nitrogen and phosphorus synchronous carbon fixation and hydrogen production.

PN CN110845821-A
TI Dust-proof electronic equipment control box useful for intelligent furniture comprises e.g. acrylonitrile butadiene styrene resin, polyethylene, dust additive, filler, coupling agent, calcium stearate, polyacrylonitrile fiber and lubricant.
AU LI M
   WANG J
   TIAN X
   LI Y
AE ANHUI SHIYOU ELECTRIC POWER FITTINGS CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020199461
AB    NOVELTY - Dust-proof electronic equipment control box comprises 80-100 pts. wt. acrylonitrile butadiene styrene resin, 30-40 pts. wt. polyethylene, 15-30 pts. wt. dust additive, 3-5 pts. wt. filler, 1-2 pts. wt. coupling agent, 2-4 pts. wt. calcium stearate, 5-8 pts. wt. polyacrylonitrile fiber, 1-2 pts. wt. nucleating agent, 0.5-1 pts. wt. antioxidant and 0.5-1 pts. wt. lubricant.
   USE - The control box is useful for intelligent furniture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The control box: has good dustproof performance; can effectively improve the cleanliness of the surface of the electronic equipment; and prolongs the service life of the electronic equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing dust-proof additive comprising (1) taking 8-12 pts. wt. tourmaline, placing in an oven at 100-120 degrees C and drying for 2-3 hours, then placing the dried tourmaline in a muffle furnace and heating to 400-500 degrees C, calcining at high temperature for 3-5 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature, passing through a 500-mesh sieve to prepare tourmaline powder, mixing 3-6 pts. wt. bamboo charcoal powder and 2-4 pts. wt. cerium oxide and placing into a ball mill for ball milling to obtain composite powder grain, (2) taking 4-7 pts. wt. chitosan, adding to the hydrochloric acid solution, stirring and dissolving to obtain chitosan solution, then adding sodium hydroxide to adjust the solution pH to 7-7.5, filtering to obtain chitosan gel, washing with distilled water until neutral and then passing carbon dioxide gas at room temperature until the solution is clear and transparent to obtain a chitosan carbonate solution, (3) adding composite powder to the chitosan carbonic acid solution, stirring at a speed of 50-80 revolutions/minute for 20-30 minutes, leaving for 5-7 hours and then filtering, placing the product in an oven at 50-60 degrees C to dry to a constant temperature to obtain composite powder treated with chitosan, (4) taking 15-20 pts. wt. oxidized carbon fiber, adding to distilled water and oscillating and dispersing under 300-500W ultrasonic for 15-30 minutes to obtain suspension of oxidized carbon fiber with a mass fraction of 3-5 wt.%, adding chitosan-treated, stirring the composite powder at a speed of 60-90 revolutions/minute for 15-25 minute, then allowing to stand for 6-10 hours and then filtering, washing the product with distilled water and drying in a 50-60 degrees C oven to a mesh sieve to obtain composite powder/carbon fiber composite and (5) taking 2-3 pts. wt. titanate and 30-40 pts. wt. toluene solution, adding composite powder/carbon fiber composite after mixing, heating to 80-90 degrees C for 4-5 hours, washing with ethanol to remove toluene, drying at 50-60 degrees C oven to prepare a dust-proof additive.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The filler is at least one of titanium dioxide, calcium carbonate and talc. The coupling agent is silane coupling agent. The nucleating agent is a polycarboxylate cementing powder nucleating agent. The antioxidant is a hindered phenolic antioxidant. The lubricant is silicone oil. Preferred Method: The step (1) comprises performing ball milling at 300-500 revolutions/minute for 5-10 hours and the ball-material ratio is 5: 1-20: 1. The step (2) comprises the degree of deacetylation of the chitosan is greater than or equal to 85%, the concentration of the hydrochloric acid is 0.5-1% and the amount of chitosan in the chitosan solution is 2-3 wt.%. The preparation method of the electronic equipment control box comprises (1) after mixing the dust-proof additives, fillers and coupling agents, stirring and mixing for 30-50 minutes at a speed of 50-80 revolutions/minute and then drying in an oven at 70-80 degrees C for 2-3 hours, heating to 100-110 degrees C, drying for 1-2 hours and then heating to 120-130 degrees C for 50-80 minutes to prepare the blend and (2) blending the blend with acrylonitrile butadiene styrene resin, polyethylene, calcium stearate, polyacrylonitrile fiber, nucleating agent, antioxidant, lubricant and adding to the mixer at 100-120 degrees C, after mixing for 5-10 minutes and passing through the first extrusion section at 170-180 degrees C, the second extrusion stage has a temperature of 190-210 degrees C and the third extrusion stage has a temperature of 220-230 degrees C.

PN CN110791527-A; CN110791527-B
TI Ultrasound-mediated intracellular cavitation of gene transfection of a gene-containing microorganism to synthesize a nanobubble complex.
AU LIU H
   WANG Y
   YAN F
AE GUANGDONG SECOND PROVINCIAL PEOPLES HOSP (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202015403G
AB    NOVELTY - Ultrasound-mediated intracellular cavitation of gene transfection of a gene-containing microorganism to synthesize a nanobubble complex, is claimed. The gene-containing microorganism-synthesized nanobubble complex includes a protein shell-coated nanobubble particle and plasmid DNA synthesized by the microorganism.
   USE - The method is useful for ultrasound-mediated intracellular cavitation of gene transfection of a gene-containing microorganism to synthesize a nanobubble complex.
   ADVANTAGE - The biological nanobubble -cell system: delivers exogenous genes carried by the nanobubbles directly into the nucleus; reduces inactivation by lysosomes, which increases gene delivery efficiency and expression.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing nanobubble complex synthesized by a gene-bearing microorganism with ultrasound-mediated cavitation in cells for gene transfection comprising (i) removing clarified solution of the lower layer of the antibacterial microorganism synthesis nanobubbles until the nanobubble concentration OD 500 = 1.0-2.0, (ii) mixing 1-10 mg/ml polyethylenimine (PEI) aqueous solution with the antibacterial microbial synthetic nano-bubbles in the volume ratio of 1:1-2:1, placing in 37 degrees C environment, incubating for 30-40 minutes, centrifuging at 150-200 g at low speed for 30-45 minutes at room temperature until the solution is completely divided into nano bubbles in which the upper layer is cationized and a clear solution containing free PEI in the lower layer, removing the lower clear solution containing free PEI, where the pH of the aqueous PEI solution is 7, and concentration is 1 mg/ml, and (iii) adding plasmid DNA to the cationized nanobubbles, mixing, where the mass ratio of the plasmid to the PEI small molecule is 1:2-3, incubating at 37 degrees C for 15-30 minutes to obtain nanobubble complex synthesized by gene-bearing microorganism, where the molecular weight of the polyethyleneimine is 25 k;
   (2) biological nanobubble -cell system for ultrasound-mediated intracellular cavitation of gene transfection comprising gene vector and/or nanobubble complexes;
   (3) preparing biological nanobubble-cell system comprising adding gene-bearing microorganism-synthesized nanovesicle complex to the cells, culturing overnight, supplementing with serum-free cell culture medium containing 1% commercial penicillin/streptomycin double antibody solution, maintaining the basic nutrition of the cells, placing in cell incubator at 37 degrees C, passing 5% carbon dioxide for 6-8 hours, washing thoroughly, replacing with ordinary complete medium to obtain biological nanobubble-cell gene transfection system mediated by ultrasound;
   (4) use of biological nanobubble -cell system in achieving efficient gene delivery in gene transfection; and
   (5) achieving efficient gene delivery comprising performing ultrasound irradiation of biological nanobubble-cell gene transfection system with plane ultrasound, stimulating gene-bearing micro-biosynthetic nanobubble complexes that are actively engulfed into the cells to occur cavitation, causing small holes in the nuclear membrane by microfluid, shock wave and jet effects, increasing the permeability of the nuclear membrane, directly improving the delivery efficiency of plasmid DNA into the nuclear, performing gene transfection of the cell system under ultrasound guidance at room temperature using 1 W/cm2 intensity of planar ultrasound irradiation, and completing transfection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The gene-containing microorganism-synthesized nanobubble complex is formed by ligating cationic small molecule polyethyleneimine-coated nanobubble particle coated with a protein shell synthesized by a negatively charged microorganism with plasmid DNA. The molecular weight of the polyethyleneimine is 25 k. The microorganism is a planktonic microorganism containing a nanobubble structure in a cell, preferably Halobacterium, Anabaena algae or Microcystis. Preferred Method: The protein shell-coated nanobubble is prepared by (1) providing frozen microbial stock solution, adding to the microbial culture medium which is sterilized at high temperature and pressure, culturing with shaking under sterile condition at 37-42 degrees C at 130-150 revolutions/minute until the culture solution turns pink, (2) pouring microorganisms cultured in step (1) into a separating funnel, allowing to stand at room temperature until ring-shaped planktonic microorganism layer is visible on the upper layer of the liquid in the bottle, separating upper dominant plankton layer and the lower culture liquid from the separating funnel, and (3) adding equal to -2 times the volume of TMC lysate to the upper dominant plankton, mixing thoroughly, centrifuging at 300 g at 4-8 degrees C for 3-4 hours, removing the lower and middle materials, adding equal volume-2 times the volume of phosphate buffered saline to the remaining upper float, centrifuging using same centrifugation conditions, repeatedly removing the lower layer solution, replenishing with phosphate buffered saline solution, gradually reducing the speed and time of centrifugation, centrifuging until the upper floating microorganisms are completely lysed into milky white GVs, and solution under the centrifuge tube is completely clear, colorless and transparent, where the TMC lysate is 10 mmol/l tris-hydrochloric acid, 2.5 mmol/l magnesium chloride and 2 mmol/l calcium chloride, and pH is 7.0-7.8, the supplemented phosphate buffered saline is changed to phosphate buffered saline containing 10-20% commercial penicillin/streptomycin double antibody before the last centrifugal extraction and purification, and sealing film is used in place of the centrifuge tube cap for sealing. The gene-containing microorganism is obtained by performing co-incubation with the cell, where the vesicle complex adheres to the cell surface, and is actively engulfed into the cell by the cell to form a biological nanovesicle-cell gene transfection system.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN110386668-A; CN210620387-U
TI Denitrification and phosphorous removing device comprises anaerobic tank, water inlet pipe, supporting plate, anaerobic tank, anoxic tank, aeration tank, connecting pipe, jar and ecological pool.
AU LIU F
   LIU D
   MEI Y
   ZHAO L
AE SHANDONG HEZHENG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019924841
AB    NOVELTY - Denitrification and phosphorous removing device comprises anaerobic tank (1) with left side fixedly connected to water inlet pipe (2). The water inlet pipe is cylindrical hollow pipe and is connected to anaerobic tank inner part. The anaerobic tank cavity are vertically and fixedly installed with supporting plate (3) and supporting plate are of four groups and is rectangular plate hollowed-out at middle position. The anaerobic tank is fixedly installed with anoxic tank (5). The anaerobic tank and anoxic tank are connected through connecting pipe. The hollow box body of anaerobic tank is rectangular. The anoxic tank is fixedly installed with aeration tank (7). The anoxic tank and aeration tank are connected by connecting pipe and aeration tank is hollow rectangular box body. The aeration tank is fixedly installed at middle of jar and fixedly mounted with ecological pool. The ecological pool and the aeration tank are connected by connecting pipe.
   USE - Used as denitrification and phosphorous removing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has double effect, ensures effluent total nitrogen is less than or equal to 15 mg/l and total phosphorous is less than or equal to 0.5 mg/l, improves removal efficiency of nitrogen and phosphorus and reduces alkalinity and carbon source consumption in system and system operating cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for double-process of denitrification and phosphorous removing, comprising (i) conveying malodorous gas generated in each treatment structure at the suction port, conveying duct and fan to spray tower for primary processing, keeping odorous gas in full contact with water and absorbing and removing malodorous particles in air by water; (ii) processing treated exhaust gas by dryer; (iii) separating moisture in exhaust gas sufficiently through dryer, processing by UV photolysis, and conveying to composite photo-catalytic metal nickel mesh unit under irradiation of C-band ultraviolet lamp, where photo-catalytic oxidation of titanium dioxide is result of interaction of active hydroxy groups with other active oxidants (dioxygen, reactive oxygen species and hydrogen peroxide), dioxygen group formed on surface of titanium dioxide is highly reactive and has synergistic effect on reaction energy of various chemical bond energies in organic matter, and addition of oxygen, reactive oxygen species and hydrogen peroxide active oxidants and quickly and efficiently decompose organic matter; (iv) allowing exhaust gas decomposed by UV photolytic to enter biological deodorizing filter to further absorb and remove organic molecules of exhaust gas and passing odor through humid, where porous and active microbial filter layer utilizes adsorption, absorption and degradation functions of odorous substances by microbial cells and decomposes odorous substances into simple inorganic substances e.g. carbon dioxide and water; and (v) discharging exhaust gas which has reached standard through chimney.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the denitrification and phosphorous removing device.
   Anaerobic tank (1)
   Water inlet pipe (2)
   Supporting plate (3)
   Anoxic tank (5)
   Aeration tank (7)
