TI Producing hydrogen and carbon products used to produce e.g. foil involves passing methane through reaction zone comprising hydrogen acceptor and molten salt/metal bed to produce gas stream comprising hydrogen and solid carbon product.
AU GEERLINGS J J
   MESTERS C M A
   SPANU L
   GEERLINGS J J C
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 2019863397
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (p1) hydrogen and carbon products comprises passing methane through a reaction zone comprising a molten salt/metal bed under reaction conditions to produce a gas stream comprising hydrogen and a solid carbon product, where the reaction zone comprises a hydrogen acceptor.
   USE - The processes are useful for producing hydrogen, carbon products (carbon nanotubes) and solid carbon (claimed), where the solid carbon product is used as a raw material to produce color pigments, fibers, foil, cables, activated carbon or tires, and to modify the mechanical, thermal and/or electric properties of materials, and the carbon nanotubes are used in nanoscale electronic devices, high strength materials, field emission devices and gas storage.
   ADVANTAGE - The processes: produce a valuable carbon product from methane without co-producing carbon dioxide and without requiring a difficult separation of hydrogen from methane; utilize the molten salt/metal bed which prevents the deposition of solid carbon on reactor walls; utilize the hydrogen acceptor which can be any metal-containing alloy or compound that has the ability to selectively accept or react with hydrogen to form a sufficiently strong hydrogen-acceptor bond and achieves the desired degree of hydrogen separation and removal; allow for overcoming the thermodynamic equilibrium limitations and for shifting the reaction equilibrium to the right, due to the binding of the hydrogen to the hydrogen acceptor and removal of the hydrogen from the molten salt/metal bed; provide hydrogen in the gas stream and any unreacted methane which will not negatively impact most downstream processes, including ammonia synthesis; and allow for easy separation of solid carbon product due to its lower density than the molten salt/metal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing (p2) hydrogen and solid carbon, comprising (a) contacting methane with a catalyst comprising iron, nickel and/or cobalt in a first reaction zone, at a temperature of 700-1200 degrees C to produce a first gas stream comprising hydrogen and unreacted methane and a first solid carbon product comprising carbon nanotubes, (b) separating at least a portion of the carbon nanotubes from the first gas stream in a gas/solid separation apparatus and (c) passing at least a portion of the unreacted methane through a second reaction zone comprising the molten salt/metal bed and a hydrogen acceptor comprising transition metals or their compounds at a temperature of 600-1000 degrees C to produce a second gas stream comprising hydrogen and unreacted methane and a second solid carbon product, where the molten salt/metal bed comprises a metal comprising iron, cobalt, nickel, tin, bismuth, indium, gallium, copper, lead or molybdenum or tungsten and/or a salt comprising lithium chloride, sodium chloride, potassium chloride, cesium chloride, magnesium chloride, calcium chloride, strontium chloride and/or barium chloride.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In process (p1): the reaction conditions comprise a temperature of 600-1000 degrees C; and the hydrogen in the gas stream is at least partially bound by the hydrogen acceptor. The process (p1) further comprises removing at least a portion of the hydrogen acceptor from the reaction zone to remove the hydrogen bound to the hydrogen acceptor. Preferred Components: The carbon product has a density lower than the molten salt/metal. The hydrogen acceptor comprises a metal or its compound that forms a hydride complex with hydrogen. The transition metal comprises titanium or zirconium.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. GA 201986182W
AB    NOVELTY - Making a foamed gypsum slurry having 15-90                vol.% gas bubbles, comprises: passing a first                slurry comprising water, 50-98 wt.% calcium sulfate                hemihydrate on a dry basis, 1-50 wt.% calcium                carbonate on a dry basis, and 0.1-10 wt.% cellulose                thickener on a dry basis via a first hose to a Wye                connector conduit (40) at Rate C; passing an alum                solution comprising an aluminum compound via a                second hose to the Wye connector conduit at Rate D;                passing the first slurry and the alum solution                through respective inlet openings of the Wye                connector conduit to combine in the Wye connector                conduit to create a combined mixed stream; mixing                the combined mixed stream in a static mixer to                activate at least a portion of the calcium                carbonate; transferring the foamed gypsum slurry                from the static mixer to a cavity between two wall                boards via a third hose; and allowing the foamed                gypsum slurry in the cavity to expand, harden and                dry.
   USE - The method is useful for making a foamed                gypsum slurry having 15-90 vol.% gas bubbles and                preparing gypsum product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides filler material with low                density and improved insulation properties (e.g.                thermal and sound) by using foam structured                material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Making a foamed gypsum slurry having 15-90                vol.% gas bubbles, comprises: passing a first                slurry comprising water, 50-98 wt.% calcium sulfate                hemihydrate on a dry basis, 1-50 wt.% calcium                carbonate on a dry basis, and 0.1-10 wt.% cellulose                thickener on a dry basis via a first hose to a Wye                connector conduit at Rate C, where the first slurry                has a residence time in the first hose of Time 2;                passing an alum solution comprising an aluminum                compound via a second hose to the Wye connector                conduit at Rate D; passing the first slurry and the                alum solution through respective inlet openings of                the Wye connector conduit to combine in the Wye                connector conduit to create a combined mixed stream                that discharges from the Wye connector conduit                through a discharge opening; mixing the combined                mixed stream in a static mixer for Time 3 to                activate at least a portion of the calcium                carbonate by reacting the portion of the calcium                carbonate with the aluminum compound to generate                carbon dioxide and create the foamed gypsum slurry;                transferring the foamed gypsum slurry from the                static mixer to a cavity between two wall boards                via a third hose, where the residence time in the                third hose is Time 4; and allowing the foamed                gypsum slurry in the cavity to expand, harden and                dry. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing gypsum product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the Wye                connector.
   Wye connector conduit (40)
   First side inlet pipe (42)
   Second side inlet pipe (44)
   Second inlet opening (45)
   Discharge pipe (46)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises: adding a mixture comprising the                  calcium sulfate hemihydrate, the calcium                  carbonate, and the cellulose thickener to a                  mixing chamber at Rate A; adding water to the                  mixing chamber at Rate B; and mixing the mixture                  with water in the mixing chamber for Time 1 to                  form the first slurry. Preferred Components: The                  mixture further comprises: an accelerant, a                  retarder, and/or a chelating agent; at least one                  of 0.1-1 wt.% chelating agent, and 0.05-1 wt.%                  biocide; at least one additive comprising 0.1-10                  wt.% acrylate thickener comprising at least one                  member of sodium polyacrylates and water-soluble                  copolymers based on acrylic and (meth)acrylic                  acid, 0.1-10 wt.% casein, gum arabic, guar gum,                  tragacanth gum, starch and sodium alginate,                  0.02-1 wt.% citric acid, tartaric acid, malic                  acid, acetic acid and boric acid, 0.02-2 wt.%                  alkali metal salt of citric acid, 0.02-2 wt.%                  accelerator comprising potassium sulfate, an                  organic phosphonic compound, a                  phosphate-containing compound or a calcium                  sulfate dihydrate and sugar-containing                  composition, 0.1-5 wt.% foaming agent, 1-20 wt.%                  latex polymer, 0.01-1 wt.%                  2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, 0.05-2 wt.%                  polycarboxylate ether dispersant, 0.05-2 wt.%                  polyphosphate, 0.01-2 wt.% naphthalene dispersant                  or lignosulfonate dispersant, 0.05-1 wt.%                  biocide, 0.01-0.5 wt.% silicon based defoamer,                  1-5 wt.% inorganic particles comprising clay                  and/or pigment particles, and 0.05-1%                  polyethylene oxide; and lightweight aggregate                  comprising at least one of perlite and                  polystyrene. The ingredients comprise: 50-98 wt.%                  calcium sulfate hemihydrate; 1.5-50 wt.% calcium                  carbonate; 1.5-30 wt.% aluminum sulfate; 0-2 wt.%                  sodium citrate; 0-2 wt.% the accelerator                  comprising calcium sulfate dihydrate and sugar;                  0.2-3 wt.%, cellulose thickener comprising                  hydroxymethyl propyl cellulose; 0-3 wt.% foaming                  agent comprising alkyl benzene sulfonate, fatty                  acid salts, sodium lauryl sulfate, alkyl sulfate                  salts, sodium lauryl ether sulfate, sodium alkyl                  ether sulfate, sodium 14-16C olefin sulfonate,                  alpha -olefin sulfonates, phosphate esters,                  sulfosuccinates, alkyl phenol ether sulfates, or                  isethionates; 0-20 wt.% latex comprising                  surfactant and latex polymer dispersed as solids                  in aqueous medium, where the latex polymer is at                  least one member of acrylic polymer and styrene                  butadiene polymer; 0-1 wt.%                  2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol; 0-1 wt.% modifier                  comprising calcium hydroxide; 0.1-2 wt.%                  dispersant comprising at least one member of                  polycarboxylate dispersant, polyphosphate                  dispersant, and naphthalene dispersant, where the                  polycarboxylate dispersant comprises a                  polycarboxylic ether dispersant, the naphthalene                  dispersant is at least one of beta -naphthalene                  sulfonate, naphthalene sulfonate formaldehyde                  condensate and sodium naphthalene sulfate                  formaldehyde condensate, the polyphosphate                  dispersant is at least one of sodium                  trimetaphosphate, sodium tripolyphosphate,                  potassium tripolyphosphate, tetrasodium                  pyrophosphate, tetrapotassium pyrophosphate and                  tetrapotassium pyrophosphate; 0-2 wt.% acrylate                  thickener comprising at least one member of                  sodium polyacrylate, acrylic acid/acrylamide and                  (meth)acrylic acid/acrylic ester copolymers; 0-1                  wt.% chelating agent comprising                  diethylenetriaminepentaacetic acid (DTPA), EDTA,                  sodium polyacrylate and polyphosphate, where if                  the polyphosphate is also present as a dispersant                  the polyphosphate present as a chelating agent is                  in addition to the polyphosphate present as a                  dispersant; 0-0.5 wt.% silicon based defoamer;                  0-5 wt.% inorganic particles comprising clay                  and/or pigment particles; and 0-10% wt.% of                  lightweight aggregate. The aluminum compound is                  aluminum sulfate or potassium aluminum sulfate.                  The aluminum compound comprises particles of                  powder encapsulated in a shell for a controlled                  release of the powder when adequate shear force                  is applied to the particles of powder                  encapsulated particles in the shell in the slurry                  during the mixing process. The aluminum compound                  comprises particles coated with a water soluble                  coating.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The cellulose                  thickener is at least one member of hydroxypropyl                  methylcellulose, hydroxyethyl methylcellulose,                  hydroxyethyl cellulose, methyl cellulose, methyl                  ethyl cellulose, ethyl cellulose and                  carboxymethyl cellulose.

PT P
PN JP6590359-B1; WO2020008621-A1; JP2019513470-X; CN112368236-A
TI Hydrogen production method using biomass as raw material, involves partially oxidizing first pyrolysis gas in outer cylinder, controlling temperature in outer cylinder, and decomposing tar in first pyrolysis gas.
AU NAITO S
   WATARU S
   KAZUYUKI H
   KENICHI G
   BAI S
   HARADA K
AE TSUBASA ENG SERVICE KK (TSUB-Non-standard)
   NAITO S (NAIT-Individual)
   TSUBASA ENG SERVICE CO LTD (TSUB-Non-standard)
   TSUBASA ENG SERVICE CO LTD (TSUB-Non-standard)
   NAITO S (NAIT-Individual)
GA 201986400E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pyrolyzing the raw material supplied to an inner cylinder (2) by heat of an outer cylinder (3) to generate a first pyrolysis gas. The first pyrolysis gas is introduced into the outer cylinder. The second pyrolysis gas is extracted from the outer cylinder and introduced into a reforming furnace (7). The crude reformed gas having a hydrogen content increased by raising the temperature of the introduced second pyrolysis gas in the reforming furnace is obtained. The hydrogen is recovered from the crude reformed gas in the reforming step. The outer cylinder is combined with at least one of oxygen or air and water vapor, in the step of obtaining the second pyrolysis gas. The first pyrolysis gas is partially oxidized in the outer cylinder. The temperature in the outer cylinder is controlled to 640-740 degrees C. The tar in the first pyrolysis gas is decomposed.
   USE - Hydrogen production method using biomass as raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The raw material having high water content is efficiently dried. The hydrogen is stably recovered in the reforming furnace. The pyrolysis gas without tar is obtained stably and efficiently over a long period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The raw material is dried by a raw material dryer (32). The raw material that has undergone the raw material drying step is supplied to the inner tube of an external combustion rotary kiln (1) having an inner tube and an outer tube. The second pyrolysis gas is removed from the outer cylinder and introduced into the reforming furnace and a combustion furnace (8). The gas temperature of the reforming furnace is increased. The crude reformed gas having a high hydrogen content ratio is obtained from the second pyrolysis gas. The hydrogen is removed from the crude reformed gas in the reforming step. The second pyrolysis gas combining the outer cylinder with at least one of oxygen or air and water vapor is obtained. The oxygen gas or at least one of air and water vapor are supplied to the chamber so that the ratio of the number of moles of water vapor to the number of moles of oxygen gas is 0.4-4. The temperature in the chamber is controlled within a range of 300 to less than 640 degrees C. The biomass is introduced into the outer cylinder or the second outer cylinder. The remaining residue carbide is recovered from a lower collection portion of the chamber. The carbon dioxide gas prior to the hydrogen recovery step is removed when the sulfur content of the raw material is 0.2% by mass (dry base) or less. The electricity is generated by using carbon monoxide gas contained in the gas that has undergone the hydrogen recovery step as a heat source for gas engine power generation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the hydrogen production process.
   External combustion rotary kiln (1)
   Inner cylinder (2)
   Outer cylinder (3)
   Reforming furnace (7)
   Raw material dryer (32)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
MC E31-A02C; H09-D; H09-F03

PT P
PN CN110122170-A
TI Culturing toadstool in basket, by preparing            culture medium, adding quicklime for sterilization            treatment, crushing, spreading substrate in basket,            maintaining hyphal culture period, and harvesting            toadstool fruit body after maturity.
AU TANG L
   CHEN H
   GUO Q
   ZHA L
   BAO D
   HUANG J
   ZHANG J
   YANG R
   SHANG J
   MAO W
AE SHANGHAI AGRIC SCI ACAD (SHNY-C)
GA 2019728835
AB    NOVELTY - Method for culturing toadstool in basket,                involves selecting morel toadstool strain,                preparing culture medium in the nutrition bag                comprising wheat grains and wood chips, selecting                the soil as deep-cultivated soil or humus soil of                the rice field, exposing the sun, adding the                quicklime for sterilization treatment, controlling                the pH of the soil, performing air-drying                treatment, crushing the mixture, mixing rice straw,                the wheat straw and cotton husk after the                fermentation pretreatment, selecting cultivation                basket, adding the cultivation substrate into                cultivation basket, filling the substrate in                basket, uniformly spreading the cultivating                substrate in culture basket, covering with soil,                covering the soil with black mulch on the                cultivation basket after sowing, maintaining hyphal                culture period, reducing the humidity, maintaining                the soil moisture, increasing the ventilation and                ventilation times of the culture room, and                harvesting toadstool fruit body after                maturity.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing toadstool                in basket (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces planting growth chamber to                80-90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for culturing toadstool in basket,                involves selecting morel toadstool strain having                strong adaptability, stable traits, stable yield,                short cycle, and easy to be sturdy, preparing                culture medium in the nutrition bag comprising                wheat grains and wood chips, where the mass ratio                of the wheat grains and wood chips is 50-80:20-50,                selecting the soil as deep-cultivated soil or humus                soil of the rice field, exposing the sun, adding                the quicklime for sterilization treatment,                controlling the pH of the soil to 6.5-7.5,                performing air-drying treatment, crushing the                mixture, mixing rice straw, the wheat straw and                cotton husk after the fermentation pretreatment,                where the mass ratio of the soil to the rice straw,                the wheat straw or the cotton husk after the                fermentation pretreatment is 60-90:10-40, selecting                a cultivation basket having volume of 10-60 l,                adding the cultivation substrate into AU LEI X

   ZHANG Q
AE UNIV JIANGSU NORMAL (UJSN-C)
GA 201969212U
AB    NOVELTY - Filter separator comprises filter device (1) and a gas delivery device (2), the filter device being in communication with a gas delivery device. The filter device includes a first housing (3), and two ends of the first housing extend outwardly with a boss (4), and the middle portions of the two protrusions respectively respectively define an air inlet tube (5) and an air outlet tube (6), a first filter (7) and a second filter (8) are mounted in the first housing. The first filter includes an outer frame (9), and the outer frame is equidistantly disposed with an inner frame, and a nylon filter bag (11) is fixed on a side of the inner frame near the air outlet pipe. The surface of the nylon filter bag has a triangular convex structure. The second filter comprises a box body (12) , the box body is arranged in a rectangular parallele piped structure, an air inlet is arranged on a lower left side of the box body, and an air outlet is arranged on a right side of the box body.
   USE - Filter separator.
   ADVANTAGE - The filter separator has filtering and transportation function, has removes various forms of impurities, has low maintenance, good fuel and chemical raw material, and has low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing lithium hydroxide filler layer, which involves:
   (A) placing 100-110 parts of spodumene ore in a rotary kiln at 1100-1150 degrees C, and roasting for 2-3 hours, after natural cooling, grinding is carried out using a mineral grinder, and the rotation speed is maintained at 750-880 r/min to obtain a powder having a particle diameter of 0.01 mm;
   (B) mixing the powder with 20-52 pts. wt. sodium carbonate in a blender;
   (C) adjusting the stirring speed to 1200-1300r/min, and mixing uniformly;
   (D) placing the mixed raw materials into a reaction vessel;
   (E) heating to 235 degrees C, and carbon dioxide is continuously introduced for 20-30 minutes;
   (F) leaching using water to obtain a leachate, and filtering the residue;
   (G) adding 20-30 parts of lime milk to the leachate;
   (H) putting it into the reaction kettle together, adjust the rotation speed of the reaction kettle to 3500-4000r/min, react for 5-8 minutes, heat to 100 degrees C;
   (I) concentrating and crystallize the filler can be obtained;
   (J) preparing the filler into a pellet having a diameter of 5 mm using a granulator; and
   (K) laiding the pellets in a filler web.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of filter separator.

Preferred Composition: The lithium hydroxide filler layer includes 100-110 pts. wt. xenophosite, 20-52 pts. wt. sodium carbonate, and 20-30 pts. wt. lime milk. The lithium oxide in the spodumene ore has a mass content of 4.5-5.5%.

TI Anti-aging catalyst used to purify volatile            organic compound comprises coating oxidizing catalyst            on the surface of cordierite honeycomb ceramic carrier,            where oxidizing catalyst coating layer comprises e.g.            aluminum oxide and platinum.
AU RAO C

   ZHANG H
   LV Y
   PAN C
   CAI X
   HU Y
   YUE J
   JIA L
AE WUXI WEIFU ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION CATA (WXWF-C)
GA 2019134433
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-aging catalyst comprises coating the                surface of the cordierite honeycomb ceramic carrier                with an oxidizing catalyst coating, where the                oxidizing catalyst coating layer comprises aluminum                oxide, cerium dioxide, manganese dioxide, noble                metal platinum and palladium, corresponding to each                cordierite honeycomb ceramic carrier using 80-200 g                aluminum oxide 80 to 200 g, 5-30 g cerium dioxide                and 10-30 g manganese dioxide, and for each cubic                foot cordierite honeycomb ceramic carrier using                6-50 g platinum and 10-20 g palladium.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for purifying volatile                organic compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst not only has good stability but                also can catalyze the oxidation, emits                non-polluting gases e.g. carbon dioxide and water                to meet emission regulations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the anti-aging catalyst comprising                firstly measuring the volume of cordierite                honeycomb ceramic carrier, then preparing                platinum(II) nitrate and palladium(II) nitrate                according to each cubic foot of cordierite                honeycomb ceramic carrier corresponding to platinum                and palladium, mixing the platinum(II) nitrate and                palladium(II) nitrate to obtain a noble metal mixed                oxide, then adding polyethylene glycol-200 solution                (5-30 times) the sum of the weights of platinum(II)                nitrate and palladium(II) nitrate, stirring and                ,mixing uniformly to obtain noble metal mixed oxide                solution, then preparing aluminum oxide powder per                liter of cordierite honeycomb ceramic carrier, and                uniformly impregnating the noble metal mixed oxide                solution onto the aluminum oxide powder by the                incipient wetness impregnation method to obtain an                aluminum oxide slurry loaded with a noble metal                platinum and palladium, (b) drying the aluminum                oxide slurry loaded with noble metal platinum and                palladium, controlling the drying temperature at                100-120 degrees C, then calcining, and controlling                the baking temperature at 500-600 degrees C for 1-3                hours to obtain aluminum oxide powder loaded with                noble metal platinum and palladium, (c) crushing                the aluminum oxide powder loaded with noble metal                platinum and palladium to D90-40 mu m, then setting                as a slurry, then grinding the slurry in a range of                D90 to 10-30 mu m, and controlling the solid                content to 35-40%, (d) preparing a precursor of                cerium dioxide and manganese dioxide according to                each liter of cordierite honeycomb ceramic carrier                corresponding to cerium dioxide and manganese                dioxide, and then mixing the precursor of cerium                dioxide and manganese dioxide in an aqueous                solution to obtain an aqueous solution of the                precursor, (e) adding the aqueous solution of the                precursor in a drop wise manner to the slurry                obtained in the step (c), stirring and mixing                uniformly to obtain a mixed slurry, (f) coating the                mixed slurry on the surface of the cordierite                honeycomb ceramic carrier, and then drying at                145-155 degrees C after coating, and (g) calcining                the dried cordierite honeycomb ceramic carrier at                500-600 degrees C for 2-4 hours, and then cooling                to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The precursor of                  cerium dioxide is cerium(III) nitrate hexahydrate                  or cerium(II) acetate. The precursor of manganese                  dioxide is manganese(III) nitrate or                  manganese(II) acetate.

PT P
PN CN109174094-A
TI Preparing rutile phase solid solution used to prepare ruthenium/tin titanium oxide catalyst, by performing hydrothermal reaction on mixed aqueous solution of titanium- and tin-source, followed by centrifuging, washing, drying and grinding.
AU SUN Q
   FAN L
   TIAN M
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201909497D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a rutile phase solid solution involves: (1) thoroughly mixing a titanium source aqueous solution and a tin source aqueous solution to obtain a reaction liquid, and controlling the total concentration of the titanium source and the tin source in the reaction liquid at 0.1-0.6 mol/l; (2) heating the reaction liquid to carry out hydrothermal reaction at 120-220 degrees C for 6-96 hours to obtain a suspension after the reaction is completed; and (3) centrifuging and dehydrating the suspension, taking the suspension, followed by washing and drying to obtain suspended particles and grinding the suspended particles into powder to obtain a rutile phase with an adjustable tin/titanium ratio. The rutile phase comprises tin titanium oxide compound (I).
   USE - The one-step hydrothermal method is useful for preparing rutile phase solid solution, which is useful for preparing a ruthenium/tin titanium oxide catalyst in the field of catalytic oxidation of carbon monoxide, nitrogen oxide and hydrocarbons (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The one-step hydrothermal method enables simple preparation of the rutile phase solid solution with uniform nanoparticle size, good crystallinity and high catalytic conversion rate of 40% and oxidation efficiency of 90% or more and without requiring subsequent heat treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a rutile phase solid solution involves: (1) thoroughly mixing a titanium source aqueous solution and a tin source aqueous solution to obtain a reaction liquid and controlling the total concentration of the titanium source and the tin source in the reaction liquid at 0.1-0.6 mol/l; (2) heating the reaction liquid to carry out hydrothermal reaction at 120-220 degrees C for 6-96 hours to obtain a suspension after the reaction is completed; and (3) centrifuging and dehydrating the suspension, taking the suspension, followed by washing and drying to obtain suspended particles and grinding the suspended particles into powder to obtain a rutile phase with an adjustable tin/titanium ratio. The rutile phase comprises tin titanium oxide compound of formula: SnxTi1-xO2 (I).
   x = 0-1.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing a ruthenium/tin titanium oxide catalyst using the rutile phase solid solution, which involves immersing the above-prepared rutile phase solid solution in 1-10 %mass ruthenium trichloride solution for 1-3 hours, drying the solid solution loaded with ruthenium trichloride after the impregnation is completed and then calcining at a heating rate of 3-10 degrees C/minute to 200-600 degrees C in an air atmosphere for 3 hours; and
   (2) a method of using the catalyst prepared by the above method, which involves introducing the catalyst into a quartz tube of fixed bed reactor and then catalytically oxidizing carbon monoxide, nitrogen oxide and hydrocarbons to form carbon dioxide, nitrogen dioxide and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The titanium source is butyl titanate, titanium isopropoxide, titanium oxysulfate or titanium tetrachloride. The tin source is tin tetrachloride, stannous chloride or stannous sulfate.

PN CN109097325-A
TI Preparing fat stem cell solution useful for maintaining cosmetic comprises e.g. washing adipose with D-Hanks buffer, removing residual blood and tissue fragments and cutting adipose tissue into small fat masses and adding digestive juice.
AU DING B
AE DING B (DING-Individual)
GA 201902431P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fat stem cell solution comprises e.g. taking adipose tissue, washing with D-Hanks buffer, removing residual blood and tissue fragments, cutting adipose tissue into small fat masses and adding adipose tissue to the digestive juice, digesting cells in incubator, allowing to stand for stratification, aspirating upper fat cell solution and transferring to DMEM containing fetal bovine serum, centrifuging mixture, removing supernatant, adding required amount of DMEM medium containing fetal bovine serum again and blowing cells to prepare suspension of adipose stem cells, inoculating into culture flask, incubating and periodically changing medium until fat stem cells spread over the bottom of the culture flask, taking adipose stem cells from the culture medium, adding with D-Hanks buffer solution, adding to enzyme solution for digestion and subculturing after adipose stem cells are confluent, washing fat stem cells taken out with D-Hanks buffer and adding with enzyme solution.
   USE - The fat stem cell solution is useful for maintaining cosmetic (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy, is suitable for large-scale production of cell liquid products, economical, and product has good efficacy, storage time of fat stem cell liquid can be prolonged by low-temperature freezing and frozen state can be released when needed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing fat stem cell solution comprises (i) taking adipose tissue, washing with D-Hanks buffer, removing residual blood and tissue fragments, cutting adipose tissue into small fat masses and adding adipose tissue to the digestive juice, digesting cells in 37 degrees C incubator for 20-60 minutes, allowing to stand for stratification, aspirating upper fat cell solution and transferring to Dulbecco's modification of Eagle medium (DMEM) containing fetal bovine serum, centrifuging mixture, removing supernatant, adding required amount of DMEM medium containing fetal bovine serum again and blowing cells to prepare suspension of adipose stem cells, inoculating into culture flask, incubating at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide (CO2) constant temperature and periodically changing medium until fat stem cells spread over the bottom of the culture flask; (ii) taking adipose stem cells obtained in step (i) from the culture medium, adding with D-Hanks buffer solution, adding to enzyme solution for digestion and subculturing after adipose stem cells are 80-90% confluent; (iii) washing fat stem cells taken out in step (ii) with D-Hanks buffer, adding with enzyme solution for 1-3 minutes, according to adipose stem cells, DMEM culture solution, mixing three low temperature protection agents from 2-4:2-4:1, slowly lowering to 0 degrees C and placing in cryostat to be frozen and storing to prepare frozen stem cell solution; and (iv) taking mixture of step (iii) from the cryogenic freezing device, thawing in constant temperature water bath at 37 degrees C and adding cell growth factor and D-Hanks buffer slowly during thawing process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: In the step (i) digestive juice is a mixture of trypsin and type I collagenase in the ratio of 1:1, where pancreatin is 0.25% and type I collagenase is 0.1%. In the step (ii) enzyme solution is 0.25% trypsin. In the step (ii) algebra of adipose stem cell growth is four or six generations. In the step (iv) cell growth factors includes epidermal growth factor, fibroblast growth factor, keratinocyte growth factor, nerve growth factor, hepatocyte growth factor, transforming growth factor- alpha , and/or vascular endothelial growth factor. In the step (iv) cell growth factor is first mixed with D-Hanks buffer. In the step (iv) amount of cell growth factor and D-Hanks buffer is 5-20 mg cytokine and 500-1000 ml D-Hanks buffer per 500 ml frozen stem cell solution. Preferred Method: In the step (i) maintaining centrifugal separation speed at 800-1800 rotations/minute and centrifuging for 5-15 minutes. In the step (iii) freezing temperature of cryogenic refrigeration device is -40 to -80 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In the step (iii) cryoprotectant is glycerol.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. PN CN108970419-A
TI Method for preparing metal organic framework material/alginate fiber cloth composite film, involves placing calcium-alginate fiber cloth in beaker, and adding ethanol, followed by subjecting zeolitic imidazolate framework to separation test.
AU YANG D
   ZHAO X
   LV C
   ZOU Y
AE UNIV QINGDAO (UNQD-C)
GA 2018A1757V
AB    NOVELTY - A metal organic framework material/alginate fiber cloth composite film preparing method involves placing a calcium-alginate fiber cloth in a 100 ml beaker with tweezers, pouring the beaker into hydrochloric acid, adding a ethanol to the beaker to wash the fiber cloth, pouring out the ethanol, drying, configuring and pouring the configured cobaltous cation solution into a beaker containing fiber cloth, pouring out the supernatant, and placing the fiber cloth in a watch glass containing filter paper to be dried, dissolving a 2-methylimidazole in a mixed solution of 20 ml of methanol and 20 ml of ethanol, transferring a seaweed fiber cloth to the mixed solution of the cobalt nitrate, slowly pouring the 2-methylimidazole solution into the solution, and placing the metal organic framework-containing fiber cloth on a surface dish containing filter paper, drying, and subjecting the zeolitic imidazolate framework-67-cobalt/alginate fiber cloth to gas separation test.
   USE - Method for preparing metal organic framework material/alginate fiber cloth composite film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing metal organic framework material/alginate fiber cloth composite film with excellent hydrogen/carbon dioxide separation ratio.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A metal organic framework material/alginate fiber cloth composite film preparing method involves cutting a calcium-alginate fiber cloth into a size of 3 x 3 cm, placing the calcium-alginate fiber cloth in a 100 ml beaker with tweezers, pouring the beaker into 50 ml of hydrochloric acid, adding a small amount of ethanol to the beaker to wash the fiber cloth, pouring out the ethanol, drying the fiber cloth with the beaker, and configuring the cobaltous cation solution, pouring the configured cobaltous cation solution into a beaker containing fiber cloth, standing for 8 hours, pouring out the supernatant, and placing the fiber cloth in a watch glass containing filter paper to be dried, dissolving 3.49g cobalt nitrate hexahydrate in a mixed solution of 20 ml of methanol and 20 ml of ethanol, dissolving 3.94g 2-methylimidazole in a mixed solution of 20 ml of methanol and 20 ml of ethanol, carefully transferring a seaweed fiber cloth to the mixed solution of the cobalt nitrate, slowly pouring the 2-methylimidazole solution into the solution, standing for 20 hours, pouring the liquid, carefully removing a metal organic framework-containing fiber cloth with a pair of tweezers and placing the metal organic framework-containing fiber cloth on a surface dish containing filter paper, and drying in an oven at 60 degrees C, subjecting the zeolitic imidazolate framework-67-cobalt/alginate fiber cloth to gas separation performance test, and preparing a zeolitic imidazolate framework-8-zinc/alginate fiber cloth, metal complex-66-zirconium/alginate fiber cloth (UiO-66-Zr/AFC), metal organic framework-74-nickel-alginate fiber cloth by the method.

PN CN108634096-A
TI Microorganism compound agent used e.g. for enhancing aquatic animal disease resistance ability, is prepared by mixing photosynthetic bacteria, Bacillus, Lactobacillus, microzyme, actinomycete and sea buckthorn freeze-dried powder.
AU ZHANG K
   ZHANG T
   TANG X
   ZHANG L
AE PIZHOU GAXING MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY SERVICE (PIZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018812247
AB    NOVELTY - A microorganism compound agent is prepared by uniformly mixing photosynthetic bacteria, nitrobacteria, nitrosification Bacillus, Bacillus, Lactobacillus, microzyme, actinomycete, sea buckthorn freeze-dried powder, roxburgh rose freeze-dried powder, winter cherry freeze-dried powder, blackcurrant freeze-dried powder, cactus freeze-dried powder and carbonized egg shell powder. The freeze-dried powder is prepared by removing fresh sea buckthorn, roxburgh rose, winter cherry, blackcurrant and cactus stem and impurity, rinsing, freezing, subliming and drying, crushing, and removing seeds. Rinsing is done by adding water with citric acid, rinsing, freezing, and cooling. The carbonized egg shell powder is prepared by soaking egg shell, drying, crushing, spraying common salt (sodium chloride) solution, mixing, uniformly mixing, rotary carbonizing, activating, cooling, rinsing, soaking in hydrochloric acid, washing, filtering, drying, and sieving and crushing.
   USE - Microorganism compound agent used for adjusting aquatic animals inner and outer environment and enhancing disease resistance ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A microorganism compound agent is prepared by uniformly mixing 6000-50000 billion photosynthetic bacteria, 5000-10000 billion nitrobacteria, 5000-10000 billion nitrosification Bacillus, 5000-10000 billion Bacillus, 5000-10000 billion Lactobacillus, 5000-10000 billion microzyme, 5000-10000 billion actinomycete, 5-50 g sea buckthorn freeze-dried powder, 5-50 g roxburgh rose freeze-dried powder, 3-30 g winter cherry freeze-dried powder, 3-30 g blackcurrant freeze-dried powder, 50-100 g cactus freeze-dried powder and remaining carbonized egg shell powder. The freeze-dried powder is prepared by removing fresh sea buckthorn, roxburgh rose, winter cherry, blackcurrant and cactus stem and impurity, rinsing, freezing, subliming and drying at - 22-32 degrees C at vacuum degree of 120-150 Pa and water content of less than 6% (weight/weight, w/w), crushing at low temperature, and removing seeds. Rinsing is done by adding 40 degrees C water with 0.1% citric acid, rinsing for 15 minutes, continuously stirring, taking into purified water to rinse 3 times, freezing, and cooling to less than - 45 degrees C. The carbonized egg shell powder is prepared by soaking egg shell, drying moisture for 1-2 hours, crushing into 20-30-mesh coarse powder, spraying common salt (sodium chloride) solution, mixing, placing for 6-7 hours, uniformly mixing, placing for 6-7 hours, adding into rotary carbonization pot, rotary carbonizing at 420-450 degrees C for 70-80 minutes, observing complete black raw material, quickly moving in activating furnace, closing furnace, infusing carbon dioxide gas, activating at 720-800 degrees C for 55-85 minutes, cooling to room temperature, rinsing with clean water, soaking in 12% hydrochloric acid for 2.5-3 hours, washing using 95 degrees C purified water, rinsing using room temperature purified water to neutral, filtering, drying at 115-120 degrees C to water content of less than 6% w/w, and sieving and crushing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The agent is prepared by uniformly mixing 12000-50000 billion photosynthetic bacteria, 2000-10000 billion nitrobacteria, 8000-10000 billion nitrosification Bacillus, 8000-10000 billion Bacillus, 8000-10000 billion Lactobacillus, 8000-10000 billion microzyme, 8000-10000 billion actinomycete, 10-50 g sea buckthorn freeze-dried powder, 8-50 g roxburgh rose freeze-dried powder, 5-30 g winter cherry freeze-dried powder, 5-30 g blackcurrant freeze-dried powder, 80-100 g cactus freeze-dried powder and remaining carbonized egg shell powder. The Bacillus is Bacillus licheniformis, Bacillus lentus, Bacillus cereus, Bacillus subtilis, Bacillus coagulans, Bacillus orientalis, Bacillus laterosporus, Bacillus pumilus and/or Bacillus natto. The Lactobacillus is Lactobacillus lactis, Lactobacillus acidophilus, Lactobacillus plantarum, Lactobacillus fermentum, Propionibacterium freudenreichii, Lactobacillus lactis, Propionibacterium faecalis, Lactobacillus plantarum, Lactobacillus brevis, Lactobacillus cellulans, Lactobacillus bulgaricus and/or Bifidobacterium. The microzyme is Candida, brewing yeast, beer yeast, red yeast and/or fodder yeast. Preferred Method: Drying is hot air drying or far infrared drying at 180-300 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The egg shell is chicken egg shell, duck egg shell, goose egg shell and/or quail egg shell.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN108467522-A; CN108467522-B
TI Hose inner layer rubber compound contains nitrile rubber, hydrogenated nitrile rubber, carbon black, zinc oxide, stearic acid, and low molecular weight polypropylene carbonate (PPC) premix prepared using e.g. PPC and maleic anhydride.
AU ZHAO G
   WANG Z
   XU Y
   LI P
   LI J
   YU S
   LIU H
   WEI H
AE XUZHOU COLLEGE IND TECHNOLOGY (XUZU-C)
GA 201870067J
AB    NOVELTY - A hose inner layer rubber compound comprises 80 parts mass nitrile rubber, 20 parts mass hydrogenated nitrile rubber, 50-63 parts mass carbon black, 0-10 parts mass alkaline white carbon, 2.5-3.5 parts mass vulcanizing agent, 1.8-2.2 parts mass vulcanization aid, 3-5 parts mass zinc oxide, 1-2 parts mass stearic acid, 2-3 parts mass antioxidant system, 4.4-22 parts mass low molecular weight PPC premix, and 0-8 parts mass plasticizer. The low molecular weight PPC premix is prepared by heating the low molecular weight PPC to 60-75 degrees C, adding maleic anhydride to the PPC, in a mass ratio of 2:10, adding the modified PPC to the operation modifier carbon black N550 (soft particle surface carbon black), in a mass ratio of 1:1, mixing and stirring using kneader, at a stirring speed of 40 rpm, increasing the temperature slowly while stirring until the temperature reaches 55 degrees C, and stirring the mixture at a constant temperature for 1 hour.
   USE - Hose inner layer rubber compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The hose inner layer rubber compound uses low molecular weight PPC which alleviates environmental pollution, reduces greenhouse effect and enhances overall utilization of carbon dioxide resources, and uses the basic white carbon black to replace the existing reinforcing agent carbon black, and improves the mechanical properties, aging resistance and oil resistance of the inner rubber of the hose, and provides economical hose with prolonged service life, and at reduced energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the hose inner layer rubber compound, which involves adjusting roll distance of two-roll mill to 0.1 mm, laminating nitrile rubber and hydrogenated nitrile rubber separately for 3-4 times, adjusting the roll distance to 0.5 mm, adding nitrile rubber and hydrogenated nitrile rubber, and kneading into mixture, adjusting the roll distance to 2.2-2.5 mm, and adding stearic acid, zinc oxide, antioxidant system to the mixture when the rubber compound roll has a small amount of accumulated glue, adding alkaline white carbon black, carbon black, plasticizer and low molecular weight PPC premix, adjusting the roll distance to 0.5 mm when the feeding is complete, and thinning 4-5 times, adjusting the roll distance to 2.5-2.7 mm, adding the vulcanizing agent and vulcanization aid, adjusting the roll distance to 0.3 mm when the feed is complete, and thinning for 4-6 times, controlling the temperature to 50-60 degrees C, adjusting the roll distance to 3.5 mm to reduce the thickness of the sheet to obtain a rubber compound, placing the obtained rubber compound on a flat surface at 15-35 degrees C for 12 hours, and carrying out vulcanization on the rubber vulcanizer according to the measured vulcanization time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon black is N550 (soft particle surface carbon black). Preferred Property: The alkaline white carbon black has a pH of 10.5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The vulcanizing agent is dicumyl peroxide. The vulcanization aid is triallyl isocyanurate. The plasticizer is dioctyl succinate. Preferred Composition: The antioxidant system comprises antioxidant 4010NA (N-isopropyl-N'-phenyl-P-phenylenediamine), and antioxidant BLE (9,9-dimethylacridine), in a mass ratio of 0.8-1.2:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The nitrile rubber is NBR-41. The hydrogenated nitrile rubber is HNBR-4307 (rubber with 43 wt.% acrylonitrile and 0.12% residual double bond content). Preferred Property: The PPC has a number average molecular weight of 2000-4500.

PN CN108392497-A
TI Lipopolysaccharide D-galactosamine-induced liver            injury protection animal model, inhibiting oxidative            stress, apoptosis and inflammatory response, obtained            by taking mice, feeding with sterile water and            establishing acute liver injury model.
AU PAN C
   CAI Z
   CHEN W
   XIANG L
   JIN L
   LI J
   ZHUGE L
   ZHOU G
   FANG P
   LIN X
AE UNIV WENZHOU MEDICAL SECOND AFFILIATED (WEZN-C)
GA 2018653413
AB    NOVELTY - Lipopolysaccharide D-galactosamine-induced                liver injury protection animal model is 20-25 g                male BALB-c mice, obtained by (i) placing mice in a                room controlled by temperature and humidity,                feeding with sterile water and food; (ii)                establishing acute liver injury model by 50 mu g/kg                intraperitoneal injection of LPS with 800 mg/kg                D-GalN; (iii) dividing mice randomly into 6 groups                comprising control group, mangiferin (20 mg/kg)                group, LPS-GalN group and mangiferin (5, 10 and 20                mg/kg) LPS-GalN group; and (iv) dividing control                group of the mice given the same amount of                physiological saline comprising 50 mu g/kg LPS-GalN                group mice and 800 mg/kg D- GalN, Mangiferin (5, 10                and 20 mg-kg) mice in the LPS-GalN group in                mangiferin (5, 10 and 20 mg-kg) 1 hour after                LPS-GalN challenge and collecting LPS-GalN                challenge, blood and liver tissues after 8 hours                for subsequent analysis.
   USE - The model is useful for inhibiting oxidative                stress, apoptosis and inflammatory response,                activating Nrf2-HO-1 signaling pathway and                inhibiting NLRP3 inflammasome activation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                constructing lipopolysaccharide                D-galactosamine-induced liver injury protection                animal model, comprising (a) dividing mice randomly                into 6 groups comprising control group, (20 mg/kg)                mangiferin group, (5, 10 and 20 mg-kg) LPS-GalN                group and mangiferin LPS-GalN group; saline                LPS-GalN group mice (50 mu g/kg) LPS and 800 mg/kg                D-GalN, mangiferin (5, 10 and 20 mg-kg) LPS-GalN                mice, mangiferin (5, 10 and 20 mg-kg) 1 hour after                LPS-GalN challenge and collecting LPS-GalN                challenge, blood was collected and liver tissue                after 8 hours for subsequent analysis; (b)                isolating primary hepatocytes from male C57BL-6                mice, suspending cells in Dulbecco's Modified                Eagle's medium (DMEM) containing 10% fetal bovine                serum, 100 units/ml penicillin and 100 mg/ml                streptomycin, culturing at 37 degrees C with 5%                carbon dioxide, treating cells treated with                different concentrations of mangiferin for 1 hour                and stimulating with lipoploysaccharide for 24                hours; (c) fixing liver tissue with 4%                paraformaldehyde, embedding in paraffin, cutting                tissue cut into a thickness of 5 mu m each, and                staining sections with hematoxylin-eosin to                evaluate pathological changes of liver tissue; (d)                collecting serum alanine aminotransferase (ALT) and                aspartate aminotransferase (AST), blood samples,                storing at 4 degrees C for 2 hours, centrifuging                samples at 3000 revolutions/minute for 10 minutes                at 4 degrees C, collecting serum and detecting                serum ALT and AST using a test kit according to the                manufacturer's instructions level; and (e)                determining malondialdehyde (MDA) and reactive                oxygen species (ROS) content and detecting relative                liver ROS levels and MDA content in liver by                thiobarbituric acid reaction substance method using                MDA test kit according to the manufacturer's                instructions.
TF ACTIVITY - Antiapoptotic; Antiinflammatory.

PT P
PN CN108384676-A
TI Sugar modulation method of nutritious waxberry wine comprises juicing the waxberry and jujube, mixing, adding pectinase, fermenting, adding gelatin, allowing it to stand, filtering and bottling.
AU LU T
   WANG J
AE SUZHOU SUDONGTING BIO-TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201864242G
AB    NOVELTY - Sugar modulation method of nutritious waxberry wine comprises: (i) sorting and washing the waxberries, removing core and pressing into waxberry juice, removing the core of jujube, drying at 60 degrees C, removing the mildew and metamorphose fruit, crushing via pulverizer and extracting the juice at 100 degrees C; (ii) mixing waxberry juice and jujube juice, adjusting its pH to 4 using citric acid, adjusting the sugar content according to alcohol content in the physical and chemical indicators of the final composite wine, stirring to dissolve and to obtain juice; (iii) adding sodium metabisulfite into raw juice; (iv) adding 0.03-0.05 g/l pectinase into the juice, impregnating for 48-72 hours; (v) adding 0.1 g/l dry yeast into the juice and activating at 30 degrees C for 30 minutes; (vi) pouring the activated yeast liquid into fermenter containing the raw juice, stirring, fermenting; and (vii) adding 0.6 g/l gelatin into the wine, allowing it stand, filtering via ultra-filtration membrane, and bottling.
   USE - The method is useful for sugar modulation of nutritious waxberry wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The wine is rich in nutrition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Sugar modulation method of nutritious waxberry wine comprises: (i) sorting and washing the waxberries, removing core and pressing into waxberry juice, removing the core of jujube, drying at 60 degrees C, removing the mildew and metamorphose fruit, crushing via pulverizer and extracting the juice at 100 degrees C; (ii) mixing bayberry juice and jujube juice in a weight ratio of 2:1, adjusting its pH to 4 using citric acid, adjusting the sugar content according to alcohol content in the physical and chemical indicators of the final composite wine, stirring to dissolve and to obtain juice; (iii) adding 140-180 mg/l sodium metabisulfite into raw juice and adjusting sulfur dioxide content in the juice to 70-90 mg/l; (iv) adding 0.03-0.05 g/l pectinase into the juice, impregnating for 48-72 hours; (v) adding 0.1 g/l dry yeast into the juice and activating at 30 degrees C for 30 minutes; (vi) pouring the activated yeast liquid into fermenter containing the raw juice, stirring, fermenting at 24-28 degrees C for 8-12 hours, where the amount of juice is 3/4th of the fermenter, when the alcohol is close to the highest, the temperature gradually approaches room temperature, the carbon dioxide bubbles decrease, and the liquid juice begins to clear, after the main fermentation is finished, pouring into can to separate the precipitate and carry out post-fermentation, immediately transferring the upper layer of the fermented liquor into another clean fermenter to reduce the contact of the fermented liquor with air, closing and fermenting at 15-18 degrees C for 25-30 days to prevent the wine from being oxidized; and (vii) adding 0.6 g/l gelatin into the wine, allowing it stand for 24 hours, filtering via ultra-filtration membrane, and bottling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The preparation of jujube juice comprises adding 5 times amount of water into jujube and juicing for 4 hours, then adding 3 times amount of water into jujube residue and juicing for 2 hours, filtering the twice extracted jujube juice and mixing. The acid and sugar adjustment process includes adding 1 g white granulated sugar into 1 l raw juice according to standard and stirring to dissolve.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN108096216-A; CN108096216-B
TI Preparation of Ganoderma lucidum immunity-improving complete active ingredient combination microcapsule by taking Dendrobium huoshanense ultra-micropowder, protein powder, Ganoderma lucidum spore oil and Ganoderma lucidum extract.
AU DENG H
   CHEN N
   CHEN C
   HAN B
   SONG X
   ZHU F
   ZANG Y
   JIANG X
AE UNIV ANHUI WEST (UYWX-C)
   UNIV ANHUI WEST (UYWX-C)
GA 2018444582
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of Ganoderma lucidum immunity-improving complete active ingredient combination microcapsule comprises preparation of G. lucidum extract from mature G. lucidum, preparation of G. lucidum spore oil from mature G. lucidum spore powder, preparing Dendrobium huoshanense ultra-micropowder from fresh D. huoshanense, taking D. huoshanense ultra-micropowder, edible gum and protein powder as wall materials and G. lucidum spore oil as core material, adding water and G. lucidum extract, performing high-speed homogenizing emulsification to form oil-in-water microcapsule emulsion, and spray drying to prepare into microcapsule particles.
   USE - Preparation of G. lucidum immunity-improving complete active ingredient combination microcapsule used for improving human immunity and reducing blood sugar.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the prior art problem of not fully utilizing the active ingredients of G. lucidum, complicated extraction, and product, which is easily oxidized and not stored.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of G. lucidum immunity-improving complete active ingredient combination microcapsule comprises preparation of G. lucidum extract by harvesting mature G. lucidum, washing with flowing water, breaking into small blocks, passivation enzyme treating at 105-115 degrees C for 4-6 minutes, drying at 65-75 degrees C to constant weight, respectively reflux extracting with 2-4 times of 12% citric acid+90-95% ethanol solution, 1-2% citric acid+70-75% ethanol solution, 1-2% citric acid+45-50% ethanol solution, 1-2% citric acid+20-25% ethanol solution and 1-2% citric acid+deionized water solution at 65-75 degrees C for 2-4 hours, filtering, collecting the filtrate and combining, evaporating and concentrating into paste at negative pressure and 65-75 degrees C, and obtaining G. lucidum extract, preparation of G. lucidum spore oil by collecting mature G. lucidum spore powder, sieving with 180-220-mesh screen to remove impurities, adding 3-5% acetic acid solution, stirring for 6-12 hours at room temperature, washing with water, filtering, adding 3-5% sodium hydroxide solution, stirring for 6-12 hours at room temperature, washing with water, filtering, drying at 55-65 degrees C under negative pressure, obtaining wall spore powder, putting in less than or equal to 10 degrees C environment to break the wall, collecting broken spore powder, supercritical carbon dioxide extracting and obtaining G. lucidum spore oil, preparing D. huoshanense ultra-micropowder by picking fresh D. huoshanense, removing the leaves, cleaning, drying, 105-115 degrees C passivation enzyme treating for 4-6 minutes, drying to constant weight at 65-75 degrees C, crushing, sieving with 95-105-mesh sieve, ultra-microcrushing, sieving with 195-205-mesh sieve and obtaining D. huoshanense ultra-micropowder, taking D. huoshanense ultra-micropowder, edible gum and protein powder as wall materials and G. lucidum spore oil as core material, adding water and G. lucidum extract, performing high-speed homogenizing emulsification to form oil-in-water microcapsule emulsion, and spray drying to prepare into microcapsule particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises after washing with flowing water, breaking G. lucidum into small blocks having particle size of less than or equal to 1 mm. It comprises filtering the different G. lucidum extract liquids with Buchner funnel. The water content of G. lucidum extract paste is 20-30%. The acetic acid and sodium hydroxide are food-grade. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is conducted at 14-15 MPa and 34-36 degrees C for 3.0-4.0 hours; and the separation is conducted at 4.0-5.0 MPa and 30-36 degrees C, where carbon dioxide (CO2) flow rate is 24-26 L/hour. The extraction rate of G. lucidum spore oil is 30-34%. The triterpene content in G. lucidum spore oil is 18.0-18.5%. The method comprises selecting fresh D. huoshanense, removing the leaves, cleaning, drying, passivation treating, drying, crushing using blade type crusher, sieving, and superfine crushing using pulse vibration mill. The method comprises adding D. huoshanense superfine powder, G. lucidum extract, protein powder and water into a container at room temperature, uniformly stirring, simultaneously, adding edible gum and water into another container at room temperature, stirring and dissolving, mixing the two mixtures by stirring uniformly, cooling the mixed solution to 8-12 degrees C, adding prestored G. lucidum spore oil, performing high-speed homogenizing emulsification in high-speed homogenizing device for more than 20 minutes at 10000-12000 revolutions/minute, and spray drying by high temperature and high pressure spray drying device having air inlet temperature of 100-120 degrees C and air outlet temperature of 60-70 degrees C to prepare microcapsule particles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The G. lucidum immunity-improving complete active ingredient combination microcapsule comprises 1-4 mass% edible gum, 4-8 mass% protein powder, 5-6 mass% D. huoshanense ultra-micropowder, 5-8 mass% G. lucidum spore oil, 5-8 mass% G. lucidum extract, and balance water.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant; Hypoglycemic.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. TI Preparation of pressure-resistant aerated brick, involves reacting fluorite slag powder, cement, gypsum, modified mixed powder, pretreated vitrified beads, mixed nano-powder, aluminum powder, sepiolite, graphite oxide and calcium stearate.
AU JIANG W
   XU M
   ZHANG M
AE JIANG W (JIAN-Individual)
   SHENGZHOU PINGLONG NEW WALL BUILDING MATER (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201844339C
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of pressure-resistant aerated brick, involves (1) preparing modified mixed powder, (2) preparing modified vitrified beads, (3) preparing pretreated modified vitrified beads, (4) mixing fluorite slag powder, cement, gypsum, modified mixed powder, pretreated vitrified beads, mixed nano-powder, aluminum powder, sepiolite, graphite oxide, talc powder, calcium stearate, sodium stearate, dispersant and water, calcining fluorite slag powder, pretreated fluorite slag powder, mixing pretreated fluorite slag powder gypsum, adding water and mixing to obtain pretreated mixed slurry, mixing pretreated mixed slurry with cement, adding modified mixed powder, pretreated vitrified beads, mixing nano-powder, aluminum powder, sepiolite, oxidized graphite, talc powder, calcium stearate, sodium stearate and dispersant and mixing to obtain slurry, casting mixed slurry, pretreating blank, demolding blank and heating.
   USE - Preparation of pressure-resistant aerated brick.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of pressure-resistant aerated brick with excellent compressive property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The step (1) involves preparing modified mixed powder by crushing and sieving zeolite to obtain zeolite powder, calcining zeolite powder at high temperature to obtain pretreated zeolite powder, crushing porous ceramic and sifting to obtain porous ceramic powder, mixing pretreated zeolite powder and porous ceramic powder at mass ratio of 3:1-4:1 and stirring to obtain mixed powder, (A) mixing mixed powder with saturated sodium carbonate solution at mass ratio of 1:5-1:10, cooling, filtering and drying to obtain pretreated mixed powder, mixing pretreated mixed powder and calcium hydroxide solution at mass ratio of 1:3-1:5, purging with carbon dioxide gas, filtering and freeze-drying. The step (2) involves preparing modified vitrified beads by mixing vitrified beads with sodium hydroxide solution at mass ratio of 1:3-1:5, soaking at room temperature and filtering to obtain pretreated vitrified beads, mixing pretreated vitrified microbeads and phenolic resin at mass ratio of 1:4-1:6, adding silane coupling agent 0.2-0.3 times the mass of pretreated vitrified beads, mixing and filtering. The step (3) involves preparing pretreated modified vitrified beads by mixing modified vitrified microbeads with paraffin at mass ratio of 1:6-1:8, adding modified paraffin 0.10-0.12 times the mass of curing agent and modified paraffin, mixing, freezing and crushing. The step (4) involves using mixing 20-25 pts. wt. fluorite slag powder, 15-18 pts. wt. cement, 8-10 pts. wt. gypsum, 5-10 pts. wt. modified mixed powder, 8-12 pts. wt. pretreated vitrified beads, 4-6 pts. wt. mixed nano-powder, 4-6 pts. wt. aluminum powder, 3-5 pts. wt. sepiolite, 2-3 pts. wt. graphite oxide, 2-4 pts. wt. talc powder, 1-2 pts. wt. calcium stearate, 1-2 pts. wt. sodium stearate, 0.1-0.2 pt. wt. dispersant and 5-12 pts. wt. water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The step (1) involves using porous ceramic such as ceramic corundum material, aluminosilicate porous ceramic or cordierite porous ceramic. Preferred Process: The step (4) involves using mixed nano-powder prepared by crushing waste concrete to 80-160 mesh to obtain concrete powder, mixing nano-titanium dioxide and nano-silicon dioxide at mass ratio of 1:2-1:3, adding 5-6 times the mass of nano-titanium dioxide concrete powder and mixing.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (A), involves preparing saturated sodium carbonate solution by adding water to beaker, heating at 60 degrees C, adding sodium carbonate, crystallizing precipitate and filtering.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The step (2) involves using silane coupling agent such as KH-550 (RTM: gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane), KH-560 (RTM: gamma -glycidoxypropyltrimethoxysilane) or silane coupling agent KH-570 (RTM: gamma -methacryloyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane). The step (3) involves using curing agent such as m-phenylenediamine, vinyl triamine or ethylenediamine. The step (4) involves using dispersant such as naphthalenesulfonic acid, sodium methylene dinaphthalenesulfonate or sodium polyacid salt type dispersant.

TI System of deoxidation of primary nitrogen gas using common energy gas as nitrogen generator machine comprises e.g. energy-saving heat exchanger provided by nitrogen input pipeline, heater, catalyst bed, cooler, and nitrogen dryer.
AU PANG J
   YANG C
   BU X
AE DALIAN HISCIEN ENG CO LTD (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 201843436G
AB    NOVELTY - System of deoxidation of primary nitrogen gas using common energy gas as nitrogen generator machine comprises an energy-saving heat exchanger, a heater, a catalyst bed, a cooler and a nitrogen dryer. The upstream of the energy-saving heat exchanger provided with a primary nitrogen input pipeline; and the downstream through the pipeline orderly connected with the heater, the catalytic bed, energy-saving heat exchanger, cooler and nitrogen dryer, and the output port of the nitrogen output high-purity nitrogen. The heater and the catalyst bed is also set on the pipeline between the natural gas input channels; and for common energy resource fuel gas input in the primary nitrogen containing oxygen equivalent of the same equivalent proportion.
   USE - Used as system of deoxidation of primary nitrogen gas using common energy gas as nitrogen generator machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The system does not introduce other dangerous gas including hydrogen and ammonia, and has high safety; is environmentally friendly, has low operating costs, and is easy to use; and has a good prospect of industrial use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for deoxidation of primary nitrogen gas using common energy gas as nitrogen generator machine using the device comprising introducing elementary nitrogen at 40 degrees C from the molecular sieve pressure swing adsorption nitrogen generator has an oxygen content of 500-10000 partspermillion (ppm), part of the nitrogen energy-saving heat exchanger to 250-350 degrees C, the other part nitrogen mixed gas with the upper portion from temperature control bypass line bypassing the energy-saving heat exchanger, by controlling the gas flow controller is set on the temperature control bypass pipeline; flow control of the partial nitrogen such that the temperature of the mixing is not greater than 310 degrees C, and then gas entering to the heater to 310-350 degrees C; common energy resource fuel gas injection as the equivalent ratio of primary oxygen containing equivalent nitrogen in the nitrogen heater after heating, then mixed gas enters the fixed catalytic bed to deoxidize reaction, and emitting a large amount of heating to 350-400 degrees C gas after reaction enter the energy-saving heat exchanger cooling to below 180 degrees C after recycling most of the heat, part of the heat gas further using waste heat as regeneration heat source bypass passage loop of nitrogen drier being regenerated after entering into the cooler, other part of high purity nitrogen gas directly into the cooler by the energy-saving heat exchanger with cooling water, cooling to /~ 40 degrees C, and then enter into nitrogen removal reaction to generate water and carbon dioxide to obtain the oxygen not greater than 5 ppm.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic representation of system of deoxidation of primary nitrogen gas using common energy gas as nitrogen generator machine (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system respectively set on the energy-saving heat exchanger upstream and downstream of the pipeline, and through temperature control bypass pipeline. The temperature control bypass pipeline is further provided with a gas flow controller. The pipeline between the energy-saving heat exchanger and cooler, and is further provided with a regeneration passage bypass loop of nitrogen flowing through the dryer. The regenerated bypass channel circuit comprises: output by the energy-saving heat exchanger of heat source gas passage, and flowing nitrogen after reducing the temperature of gas recovery channel. The catalyst bed is a fixed catalyst bed, which is made of active ingredient is a particulate catalyst containing metal platinum or mixtures of platinum, palladium. The catalytic bed is a double-sealing head cylinder structure; the gas outtake; middle of the cylinder supporting sieve, supporting sieve is the catalyst particulate is tiled, and the paving thickness of the catalyst is 150-300 mm. The nitrogen gas dryer is two upper and lower end opening of the hollow cylindrical dryer is provided with a molecular sieve particle, the working mode of the two dryers is one absorption dehumidifying, another desorption and regeneration periodically switched. The nitrogen filled in the dryer molecular sieve dehydrating and removing carbon dioxide is a function of the 5 A-type molecular sieve or 13X molecular sieve.

PN KR1855120-B1
TI Cement concrete composition useful for repairing concrete pavement, and as maintenance material for concrete pavement and concrete structure, comprises cement-based binder, coarse aggregate, fine aggregate, water and mixed latex modifier.
AU CHUL K J
   NAM H K
   HYOUNG J C
AE ROAD KOREA CO LTD (ROAD-Non-standard)
GA 2018381358
AB    NOVELTY - Cement concrete composition comprises 12-22 wt.% cement-based binder, 37-40 wt.% coarse aggregate, 33-38 wt.% fine aggregate, 4.5-5.5 wt.% water and 3.5-4.5 wt.% mixed latex modifier. The mixed latex modifier comprises polymer latex formed by mixing natural rubber latex with synthetic rubber latex in weight ratio of 0.1-4%. The natural rubber latex is prepared by mixing 3-8 wt.% nonionic surfactant and 0.2-0.6 wt.% antifoaming agent.
   USE - The cement concrete composition is useful for repairing concrete pavement (claimed), and as maintenance material for concrete pavement and concrete structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The cement concrete composition has improved durability, prevents sudden phenomenon of natural rubber latex, reduces amount of carbon dioxide generated in production of petrochemical products, and improves engineering characteristics and durability of cement concrete and cement mortar.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cement concrete composition comprises 12-22 wt.% cement-based binder, 37-40 wt.% coarse aggregate, 33-38 wt.% fine aggregate, 4.5-5.5 wt.% water and 3.5-4.5 wt.% mixed latex modifier. The mixed latex modifier comprises polymer latex formed by mixing natural rubber latex with synthetic rubber latex in weight ratio of 0.1-4%. The natural rubber latex is prepared by mixing 3-8 wt.% nonionic surfactant and 0.2-0.6 wt.% antifoaming agent. The nonionic surfactant is nonylphenol or tetrakis(dimethylamino)ethylene. The antifoaming agent is alcohol-based antifoaming agent or silicone-based antifoaming agent. The cement concrete composition is modified with latex modifier. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) cement mortar composition comprising 13-24 wt.% cement-based binder, 66-75 wt.% fine aggregate, 5.5-6.5 wt.% water and 4.5-5.5 wt.% mixed latex modifier;
   (2) repairing concrete pavement, comprising (a) removing bottom plate laitance, foreign matter and deteriorated portion of the surface layer of the concrete pavement, (b) suctioning the removed laitance and foreign matter using a high-pressure vacuum suction device equipped with a suction hose, (c) applying a primer to the surface layer of the concrete pavement using natural rubber latex and cleaning the foreign matter, (d) placing the cement concrete composition modified with natural rubber latex on the surface layer of the concrete pavement, (e) curing the laid cement concrete composition, and (f) spraying the coating curing agent; and
   (3) repairing concrete structure using cement mortar composition modified with latex modifier, comprising (i) removing the deterioration portion of the concrete of the concrete structure, (ii) applying a primer with natural rubber latex on the removed concrete deterioration portion and cleaning the foreign matter, (iii) restoring cross section of the concrete structure by laying the cement mortar composition modified with natural rubber latex, and (iv) curing the laid cement mortar composition to complete the repair of the concrete structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the bottom plate latent layer is removed by using a high-pressure short blast, a high-capacity ultra-precision water jet or a grinding machine to remove foreign matter. In the step (e), the surface treatment is performed by high-performance diamond grinding for ensuring flatness to have international flatness index value of greater than or equal to 1.6 m/km when unevenness of greater than or equal to 5 mm. In step (i), the concrete deteriorated portion is removed by using a high-pressure short blast, a high-capacity ultra-precision water jet or a grinding machine.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
