TI Separating gallic acid in purified common seep            weed herb involves selecting common seep weed herb with            proper size, pre-treating common seep weed herb,            washing processing, putting common seep weed herb in            clean water, washing, and drying.
AU LIU H
   LI H
   ZHANG H
   ZHU D
   LI J
   YANG L
   HAO C
   ZHAO H
   LI X
AE UNIV BOGHAI (UYBO-C)
   PANJIN TAICHENG INNOVATION & TECHNOLOGY (PANJ-Non-standard)
GA 201833401E
AB    NOVELTY - Separating gallic acid in purified common seep                weed herb involves selecting common seep weed herb                with proper size, pre-treating common seep weed                herb, washing processing, putting common seep weed                herb in clean water, washing, drying, paving common                seep weed herb and drying under environment of good                ventilation at 50-60 degrees C for 2-4 hours,                removing water, spreading common seep weed herb                with thickness of 2-5 cm, treating under 2 kw                microwave for 10-15 minutes, crushing, common seep                weed herb, processing in crusher at room                temperature, sieving with 60-100 mesh.
   USE - Method for separating gallic acid in purified                common seep weed herb.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to separate gallic acid in                purified common seep weed herb in cost-effective                and eco-friendly manner which enhances continuity,                and has high purity short production period.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating gallic acid in purified common seep                weed herb involves selecting common seep weed herb                with proper size, pre-treating common seep weed                herb, washing processing, putting common seep weed                herb in clean water, washing, drying, paving common                seep weed herb and drying under environment of good                ventilation at 50-60 degrees C for 2-4 hours,                removing water, spreading common seep weed herb                with thickness of 2-5 cm, treating under 2 kw                microwave for 10-15 minutes, crushing, common seep                weed herb, processing in crusher at room                temperature, sieving with 60-100 mesh, extracting                gallic acid by ultrasonic wave by using complex                enzyme such as cellulase and pectinase with mass                ratio of 1:1 followed by enzymolysis at 40-60                degrees C for 30-60 minutes to adjust pH to                4.0-6.0, adding 1000-5000U/g enzyme with ratio of                material of 1:10-1:30 under ultrasonic power with                200-600 watt, purifying pre-centrifuging extract                for 3-10 minutes under 3000-5000                revolutions/minute, taking supernatant to 0.45 mu m                filter, filtering supernatant liquid by rotary                evaporation and drying, dissolving 50% ethanol and                purifying gallic acid by using supercritical                fluid-simulated moving bed (SF-SMB) chromatography                system. The 4-mer 3-zone SMB is used to separate                gallic acid extracts and reversed phase octadecyl                silane bonding silica gel is prepared whose                specification is 4.6 mm/asterisk150 mm, 5 mu m as                separating filler, 50% ethanol aqueous solution as                flowing phase to adjust pH to 2.5 where sampling                flow rate is 0.1-2.0 mL/minute, eluting flow rate                is 1.0-5.0 mL/minute, washing flow rate is 1.0-5.0                mL/minute, eluting flow rate is 1.0-5.0 mL/minute                for 20-60 minutes and supercritical carbon dioxide                pressure is 10-30 megaPascal and kettle temperature                is 30-60 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The purifying rate is                  90-99%.

PN CN107840355-A
TI Preparation of electrode-level lithium carbonate involves crushing massive lithium salt ore in a ball mill, mixing with water, stirring to dissolve, centrifuging, and separating to obtain crude lithium salt mine.
AU LI L
   DENG Z
   ZHANG X
   LIAO C
   XU X
   FU X
   JIANG Y
   LIU M
AE JIANGXI GANFENG LITHIUM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018252601
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of electrode-level lithium carbonate involves crushing massive lithium salt ore in a ball mill into 0.02-0.08 mm particle with lithium ion content of 6-13%, sodium ion content of 4-7%, potassium ion content of 1.5-5%, boron ion content of 0.1-0.3%, calcium ion content of 0.2-0.6%, magnesium ion content of 0.2-1%, and other cation of 0.01-0.3%; and mixing the particles with water according to liquid-solid ratio of 1-3, stirring for 30-60 minutes to dissolve, centrifuging, and separating to obtain crude lithium salt mine.
   USE - Method for preparing electrode-level lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple with low cost, high yield, high production efficiency and reduced energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of electrode-level lithium carbonate comprises crushing massive lithium salt ore in a ball mill into 0.02-0.08 mm particle with lithium ion content of 6-13%, sodium ion content of 4-7%, potassium ion content of 1.5-5%, boron ion content of 0.1-0.3%, calcium ion content of 0.2-0.6%, magnesium ion content of 0.2-1%, and other cation of 0.01-0.3%, where the cation comprises iron, aluminum, copper, zinc, manganese, strontium, nickel, lead and/or silicon; mixing the particles with water according to liquid-solid ratio of 1-3, stirring for 30-60 minutes to dissolve, centrifuging, and separating to obtain crude lithium salt mine with sodium content of 0.1-2%, potassium content of 0.05-0.2%, and boron content of 0.005-0.02%; adding 15-31% hydrochloric acid, adjusting pH to 1-3, dissolving, regulating pH to 9-13 using 15-32% liquid alkali to remove the magnesium, copper and nickel impurities, and filtering to obtain primary refined lithium chloride solution with lithium content of 30-45 g/L, calcium content of 0.2-0.7 g/L, and aluminum, magnesium, copper, manganese, zinc, nickel and lead impurity of less than 2 mg/L; treating the solution with carbon dioxide until pH value is 7 for roughly removing the calcium, filtering to obtain secondary refined lithium chloride solution with calcium content of less than 0.08 g/L; adding liquid alkali to adjust the pH to 9-12, then adding 0.5-1 time calcium removing agent, precipitating using 200-230 g/L sodium carbonate solution, centrifuging, and separating to obtain crude lithium carbonate; and washing, and drying to obtain the electrode-level lithium carbonate with sodium content of less than 0.025%, potassium-boron content of less than 0.003%, calcium content of less than 0.005%, and magnesium, iron, aluminum, copper, zinc, manganese, strontium, nickel and lead content of less than 0.001%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Second step stirring is done at 80-100 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The calcium removing agent is EDTA or EDTA-disodium.

PN CN107603846-A
TI Preparing vinegar used for reducing blood pressure, by mixing black rice powder, wood ear fungus powder, sugar and pearl plum hydrolyzate, fermenting mixture, adding e.g. tuckahoe, hawthorn, black beans and Ginkgo, and aging product.
AU FANG X
   HE X
   LI C
   LI J
   LI L
   LI Z
   LIN B
   LING D
   LIU G
   SHENG J
   SUN J
   TANG Y
   XIN M
   ZHENG F
AE CHINESE ACAD TROPICAL AGRIC SCI AGRO-PRO (CTAG-C)
   TIANE GANDONG XIANGTAI AGRIC TECHNOLOGY SERVICE PROFE (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201809653B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing vinegar involves (a) ripening pearl plums, removing seeds, extracting juice, adding pectinase, and hydrolyzing juice, (b) mixing black rice powder, wood ear fungus powder, sugar and pearl plum hydrolyzate, sterilizing and fermenting mixture, and (c) inoculating acetic acid bacteria and fermenting product obtained in step (a), adding auxiliary materials, where the auxiliary materials contain tuckahoe, corn stigma, hawthorn, black beans, Ginkgo and Eucommia bark, performing acetic acid fermentation, and aging product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing vinegar, which is used for reducing blood pressure (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases selenium and non-volatile acid content, palmitic acid content to 17.66% and anthocyanin content to 497 mg/100 ml.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing vinegar involves (a) storing 8 pearl plums in storeroom at 25-30 degrees C, maintaining relative humidity of 90-95%, ethylene concentration at 20-40 parts per million (ppm) and carbon dioxide concentration at 0.8-1% for 20-24 hours, and ripening pearl plums, removing seeds, extracting juice, adding 1-2 %mass pectinase, and hydrolyzing juice at 30-45 degrees C for 1.5-3 hours, (b) mixing black rice powder, wood ear fungus powder, sugar and pearl plum hydrolyzate in a ratio of 3-5:2-5:1-2:88-94, adjusting soluble solid content to 20-30% using white sugar, sterilizing mixture at 80-100 degrees C for 10-30 minutes using water bath, cooling mixture to 30-45 degrees C, adding 0.1-0.5% mixed fermentation strain, fermenting mixture until alcohol content of fermentation broth reaches 4-6 degrees , centrifuging and filtering fermentation broth, where the mixed fermentation strain comprises Lalvin EC1118 (RTM: Dry active yeast strain of Saccharomyces bayanus), selenium-enriched yeast and Lactobacillus plantarum strain ST-III in a ratio of 1-2:3-5:1-2, and (c) inoculating 5-10% LB type acetic acid bacteria and fermenting product obtained in step (a) at 30-40 degrees C for 24-48 hours until alcohol content reaches 0 and total acid content reaches 4-5 g/100 ml, centrifuging fermentation broth, and performing ultra-high temperature sterilization, adding 10-20 times water and extracting auxiliary materials for 30-45 minutes, filtering and adding 2-5 vol.% extract to sterilized fermentation broth, where the auxiliary materials comprise 5-8 pts. wt. Moringa oleifera seeds, 4-6 pts. wt. Cyclocarya paliurus, 3-5 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 1-3 pts. wt. rhizoma polygonati, 4-6 pts. wt. Paraphlomis javanica, 4-6 pts. wt. corn stigma, 1-3 pts. wt. hawthorn, 8-10 pts. wt. black beans, 4-6 pts. wt. Ginkgo, 4-6 pts. wt. Eurycoma longifolia and 1-3 pts. wt. Eucommia bark, performing acetic acid fermentation, cooling fermented product to 5-10 degrees C, aging fermented product for 7-10 days, and aseptically filling the product into a glass bottle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method involves (a) storing 8 pearl plums in storeroom at 25-30 degrees C, maintaining relative humidity of 90-93%, ethylene concentration at 20-30 ppm and carbon dioxide concentration at 0.8-1% for 20-24 hours, and ripening pearl plums, removing seeds, extracting juice, adding 1-2 %mass pectinase, and hydrolyzing juice at 30-45 degrees C for 1.5-3 hours, (b)mixing black rice powder, wood ear fungus powder, sugar and pearl plum hydrolyzate in a ratio of 3-4:2-3:1-2:91-94, adjusting soluble solid content to 20-30% using white sugar, sterilizing mixture at 85-100 degrees C for 10-30 minutes using water bath, cooling mixture to 30-38 degrees C, adding 0.3-0.5% mixed fermentation strain, fermenting mixture until alcohol content of fermentation broth reaches 4.5-5.5 degrees , centrifuging and filtering fermentation broth, where the mixed fermentation strain comprises Lalvin EC1118 (RTM: Dry active yeast strain of Saccharomyces bayanus), selenium-enriched yeast and Lactobacillus plantarum strain ST-III in a ratio of 1-2:3-5:1-2, and (c) inoculating 6-9% LB type acetic acid bacteria and fermenting product obtained in step (a) at 35-40 degrees C for 38-44 hours until alcohol content reaches 0 and total acid content reaches 4-5 g/100 ml, centrifuging fermentation broth, and performing ultra-high temperature sterilization, adding 12-15 times water and extracting auxiliary materials for 35-40 minutes, filtering and adding 3-5 vol.% extract to sterilized fermentation broth, performing acetic acid fermentation, cooling fermented product to 6-9 degrees C, aging fermented product for 7 days, and aseptically filling the product into a glass bottle.
   ACTIVITY - Hypotensive.

PN US2022227643-A1
TI Treatment method for waters containing suspended            organic solids, negative colloids, heavy metals,            phosphates, nitrates, carbonates, silicates, chlorides            and sodium ions, involves exposing filtrate to            ultraviolet light for pathogen disinfection, exciting            sulfites to reduce nitrates to nitrogen gas.
AU THEODORE M G
AE THEODORE M G (THEO-Individual)
   EARTH RENAISSANCE TECHNOLOGIES LLC (EART-Non-standard)
GA 202293511C
AB    NOVELTY - Wastewater treatment method involves adding                sulfur dioxide (SO2) or sulfurous acid with free                SO2, sulfites and bisulfites to the wastewater at a                pH and dwell time to acid leach heavy metals                contained in and on the suspended organic solids                into the SO2 or sulfurous acid solution for                subsequent removal and separation, and precipitate                the suspended organic solids and sorbed                pharmaceuticals and personal care products (PPCPs),                perfluoroalkyl and polyfluorolkyl substance (PFAS),                and negative colloids, cobalt sulfite, nickel                sulfite, and lead sulfite. The suspended organic                solids and sorbed PPCPs, PFAS, negative colloids,                and metal precipitates are separated from the                filtrate solutions of SO2 or sulfurous acid and                allow them to dry to create metal                precipitates.
   USE - Treatment method for waters containing                suspended organic solids, PPCPs, PFAS, negative                colloids, heavy metals, phosphates, nitrates,                carbonates, silicates, chlorides and sodium                ions.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves wastewater water quality,                increases net energy production, and reduces                greenhouse gas emissions without employing                bioreactor processing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Wastewater treatment method involves adding                sulfur dioxide (SO2) or sulfurous acid with free                SO2, sulfites and bisulfites to the wastewater at a                pH and dwell time to acid leach heavy metals                contained in and on the suspended organic solids                into the SO2 or sulfurous acid solution for                subsequent removal and separation, and precipitate                the suspended organic solids and sorbed                pharmaceuticals and personal care products (PPCPs),                perfluoroalkyl and polyfluorolkyl substance (PFAS),                and negative colloids, cobalt sulfite, nickel                sulfite, and lead sulfite. The suspended organic                solids and sorbed PPCPs, PFAS, negative colloids,                and metal precipitates are separated from the                filtrate solutions of SO2 or sulfurous acid and                allow them to dry to create metal precipitates, and                a biofuel with less than 10% by weight water and a                BTU content between 6,000 and 9,000 BTU/lb for                gasifying or combustion to produce power or energy                with greenhouse gas emissions less than emitted by                landfilling and/or anaerobic digestion. The lime is                added to raise the pH above 9 of the SO2 or                sulfurous acid filtrate solution to precipitate                calcium phosphate, calcium sulfate, calcium                carbonate, calcium silicate and metal hydroxides                for filtration removal leaving a second filtrate                with PFAS, PPCPs, pathogens, nitrates, sulfites,                chlorides, and sodium and monovalent ions. The                second filtrate is exposed to ultraviolet light at                the wave length and dwell time for pathogen                disinfection, and the sulfites are excited to                reduce nitrates to nitrogen gas and degrade PFAS,                and PPCPs, a disinfected metal free salt balanced                reclaimed wastewater is formed with degraded PPCPs,                PFAS and reduced nitrogen, phosphorous. The                gasification produced biochar and is land applied                to adsorb atmospheric carbon dioxide.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                treatment apparatus for waters containing total                suspended organic solids, PPCPs, PFAS, negative                colloids, heavy metals, phosphates, nitrates,                carbonates, silicates, chlorides and/or sodium                ions.

AE ZHANG Y (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 202275728Y
AB    NOVELTY - Safe and efficient high-energy expanding                agent, comprises 5-15 wt.% metal or non-metal                powder, 10-20 wt.% organic powder, 10-25 wt.%                high-energy nitrogen-containing material, 40-75                wt.% oxidizing agent, 0.5-2.5 wt.% speed control                agent, 1.5-3 wt.% fixing adhesive, and 1.0-2.0 wt.%                plasticizer. The metal powder is aluminum powder,                titanium powder and aluminum magnesium alloy                powder, and the non-metal powder is silicon powder.                The organic powder is guanidine nitrate. The high                nitrogen energetic material is LLM-105.
   USE - Safe and efficient high-energy expanding agent                used in coal and rock mining, natural gas mining,                oil mining or mine roadway excavation, subway                construction and tunnel construction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The high-energy expanding agent does not                explode in an open space, and only in a closed                space, so that it is converted from combustion to                detonation due to the generation of a large amount                of high-temperature and high-pressure gas, and has                the dual effects of pre-splitting and detonation,                and the influence of its fissures and pore network                in range of 10-15 times that of the influence range                of cracks and pores, which is suitable for coal                mining, natural gas mining, oil mining or mine                roadway excavation, subway construction and tunnel                construction, etc.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) A method for preparing the safe and                high-efficiency high-energy expanding agent, which                involves: weighing and grinding the materials                according to the components of the formula, then                putting metal powder or nonmetal powder, organic                powder, high-nitrogen energetic material, oxidant,                speed control agent, adhesive and plasticizer into                a mixer for mixing, and discharging after mixing                uniformly to obtain the high-energy expanding                agent, or firstly putting metal powder or nonmetal                powder, organic powder, high-nitrogen energetic                material, speed control agent, adhesive and                plasticizer into a mixer for uniform mixing, then                adding oxidant for mixing, discharging after                uniform mixing, and obtaining the high-energy                expanding agent.
   (2) Use of a safe and efficient high energy                expanding agent in coal and rock mining, natural                gas mining, oil mining or mine roadway excavation,                subway construction and tunnel construction, where                high-energy expansion agent is placed in a closed                space during the application process, and ignited                by a special igniter to generate a large amount of                high-temperature and high-pressure gas during the                combustion process, which drives the expansion of                the surrounding initial cracks. When a certain                pressure is reached, the high-energy expansion                agent is rapidly changed from combustion to                detonation, The high-temperature and high-pressure                gas generated during the detonation breaks the rock                and further expands the fracture. The main                components of the high-temperature and                high-pressure gas generated during the combustion                and detonation process are 40-55 wt.% water vapor,                30-35 wt.% nitrogen, and 15-20 wt.% carbon                dioxide.

TI Preparing mechanical nano sealing glue comprises            e.g. uniformly mixing alpha, omega-dihydroxy            polydimethylsiloxane, modified silica composite            thorn-shaped polydopamine-calcium carbonate composite            hollow sphere and reinforcing agent, dehydrating,            cooling, and stirring under low pressure.
AU WEN J
AE HUNAN JINJIAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2022714475
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mechanical nano sealing glue                comprises (S1) adding pore-forming agent to calcium                hydroxide saturated solution to obtain aqueous                phase, (S2) adding the aqueous phase obtained in                step (S1) to an organic solvent containing                surfactant, (S3) dissolving the cobalt salt in                tris-hydrochloride buffer to obtain a catalyst                solution, (S4) dissolving dopamine hydrochloride in                water to form a uniform solution, (S5) dispersing                the thorn-shaped polydopamine-calcium carbonate                composite hollow spheres prepared in step (S4) into                the silica sol, (S6) adding silica composite                thorn-shaped polydopamine-calcium carbonate                composite hollow spheres prepared in step (S5) to                the ethanol solution, and (S7) uniformly mixing                alpha, omega-dihydroxy polydimethylsiloxane,                modified silica composite thorn-shaped                polydopamine-calcium carbonate composite hollow                sphere obtained in step (S6), and reinforcing                agent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing mechanical                nano sealing glue, which is used in machine,                building and automobile, and electronic                appliance.
   ADVANTAGE - The mechanical nano sealing glue has improved                viscosity and flame retardance, and enhances the                mechanical property of the sealing glue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing mechanical nano sealing glue                comprises (S1) adding pore-forming agent to calcium                hydroxide saturated solution to obtain aqueous                phase, (S2) adding the aqueous phase obtained in                step (S1) to an organic solvent containing                surfactant, emulsifying, introducing carbon dioxide                gas into the obtained emulsion, repeating the                emulsification and ventilation steps, centrifuging,                and drying to obtain porous nano-calcium carbonate                hollow microspheres, (S3) dissolving the cobalt                salt in tris-hydrochloride buffer to obtain a                catalyst solution, (S4) dissolving dopamine                hydrochloride in water to form a uniform solution,                adding the porous nano-calcium carbonate hollow                microspheres prepared in step (S2), stirring at                room temperature, centrifuging, washing, dispersing                into water, dropwise adding the catalyst solution                prepared in step (S3), heating and reacting,                centrifuging the product, washing, and drying to                obtain thorn-shaped polydopamine-calcium carbonate                composite hollow spheres, (S5) dispersing the                thorn-shaped polydopamine-calcium carbonate                composite hollow spheres prepared in step (S4) into                the silica sol, heating and reacting, centrifuging,                and washing to obtain silica composite thorn-shaped                polydopamine-calcium carbonate composite hollow                spheres, (S6) adding silica composite thorn-shaped                polydopamine-calcium carbonate composite hollow                spheres prepared in step (S5) to the ethanol                solution, adding coupling agent, heating and                reacting, centrifuging, washing and drying to                obtain modified silica composite thorn-shaped                polydopamine-calcium carbonate composite hollow                spheres, and (S7) uniformly mixing alpha,                omega-dihydroxy polydimethylsiloxane, modified                silica composite thorn-shaped polydopamine-calcium                carbonate composite hollow sphere obtained in step                (S6), and reinforcing agent, dehydrating, cooling                to room temperature, adding plasticizer,                crosslinking agent, catalyst, and coupling agent,                and stirring under low pressure to obtain                mechanical nano sealing glue.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                mechanical nano sealing glue prepared by the above                preparation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The pore-forming agent                  is polyethylene glycol octyl phenyl ether, and/or                  polyoxyethylene sorbitan fatty acid ester. The                  content of the pore-forming agent in the aqueous                  phase is 1-3 wt.%. In the step (2), the                  surfactant is Span-80, glycerol monostearate,                  monoalkyl ether phosphate, cetyl hexadecate,                  and/or octenyl succinate starch. The content of                  surfactant in the organic solvent is 3-6 wt.%.                  The organic solvent is toluene, xylene,                  cyclohexane, n-hexane, cyclopentane, petroleum                  ether, methyl formate, ethyl acetate, methyl                  acetate, dichloromethane, trichloromethane,                  and/or chloroform. The rotating speed in the                  emulsification is 11000-13000 revolutions/minute                  for 5-10 minutes. The time for introducing carbon                  dioxide is 10-15 minutes, the ventilation volume                  is 5-10 l/minute, and repeating the                  emulsification and aeration steps for 3-7 times.                  In the step (3), the cobalt salt is cobalt                  chloride, cobalt sulfate, and/or cobalt bromide.                  The pH of the Tris-hydrochloride buffer is 8-8.5,                  and the concentration of cobalt ions in the                  catalyst solution is 1-2 wt.%. In the step (4),                  the mass ratio of dopamine hydrochloride and                  porous nano-calcium carbonate hollow microspheres                  is (2-3):10. The room temperature stirring time                  is 20-25 hours. The heating temperature is                  40-50&#176;C, and the reaction time is 10-15                  hours. In the step (S6), the coupling agent is                  composite coupling agent, including silane                  coupling agent and titanate coupling agent in a                  mass ratio of (3-7):2. The silane coupling agent                  is KH550, KH560, KH570, KH580, KH590, KH6902,                  and/or KH792. The titanate coupling agent is                  KR-138S, KR-38S, KR-238S, TMC-201, TMC-102,                  TMC-101, TMC-311w, TMC-105, TMC311, TMC-3, TMC-2,                  TMC-27, and/or TMC-4, preferably compound mixture                  of KH560 and KR-138S in a mass ratio of 5:2, the                  heating reaction temperature is 60-80&#176;C                  for 3-5 hours, the mass ratio of the silica                  composite thorn-shaped polydopamine-calcium                  carbonate composite hollow sphere and the                  coupling agent is 100:(3-7). In the step (S7),                  the mass ratio of alpha, omega-dihydroxy                  polydimethylsiloxane, modified silica composite                  thorn-shaped polydopamine-calcium carbonate                  composite hollow sphere, reinforcing agent,                  plasticizer, crosslinking agent, catalyst and                  coupling agent is                  100:(3-7):(2-5):(3-6):(1-3):(0.5-1.5):(0.1-1).                  The dehydration temperature is 90-120&#176;C,                  and the vacuum degree is 0.07-0.09 MPa. The low                  pressure is 0.07-0.09 MPa, the stirring time is                  1-2 hours. The reinforcing agent is carbon                  nanofibers, carbon nanotubes, and/or nanoglass                  fibers. The plasticizer is dimethyl silicone oil,                  MDT silicone oil, and/or monohydroxy silicone                  oil. The crosslinking agent is                  methyltriacetoxysilane, propyltriacetoxysilane,                  and/or vinyltriacetoxysilane. The catalyst is                  dibutyltin dilaurate, dibutyltin diacetate,                  dioctyltin dilaurate, and/or stannous octoate.                  The coupling agent is methyl tributyl ketoxime                  silane, vinyl tributyl ketoxime silane,                  tetrabutyl ketoxime silane, and/or phenyl                  tributyl ketoxime silane.Preferred Method: In the step (S5), the                  preparation method of the silica sol comprises                  uniformly mixing 3-5 pts. wt. water, 3-7 pts. wt.                  ethanol, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. 1-3 mol/l hydrochloric                  acid, and 1-2 pts. wt. ethyl orthosilicate to                  obtain the silica sol, the hydrochloric acid                  concentration is (1-3) mol/l, the mass ratio of                  the water, ethanol, hydrochloric acid and ethyl                  orthosilicate is (3-5):(3-7):(0.1-0.5):(1-2), and                  the heating reaction temperature is                  50-70&#176;C for 20-30 hours. The preparation                  method of the mechanical nano sealing glue                  comprises (S1) adding pore-forming agent to                  calcium hydroxide saturated solution to obtain                  aqueous phase, where the content of the                  pore-forming agent is 1-3 wt.%, (S2) adding the                  aqueous phase obtained in step (S1) to an organic                  solvent containing 3-6 wt.% surfactant,                  emulsifying at emulsification speed of                  11000-13000 revolutions/minute for 5-10 minutes,                  introducing carbon dioxide gas into the obtained                  emulsion, where the time of feeding carbon                  dioxide is 10-15 minutes and the ventilation rate                  is 5-10 l/minute, repeating the emulsification                  and ventilation steps for 3-7 times,                  centrifuging, and drying to obtain porous                  nano-calcium carbonate hollow microspheres, (S3)                  dissolving the cobalt salt in tris-hydrochloride                  buffer with pH value of 8-8.5 to obtain a                  catalyst solution, where the cobalt ion                  concentration is 1-2 wt.%, (S4) dissolving 2-3                  pts. wt. dopamine hydrochloride in water to form                  a uniform solution, adding 10 pts. wt. porous                  nano-calcium carbonate hollow microspheres                  prepared in step (S2), stirring at room                  temperature for 20-25 hours, centrifuging,                  washing, dispersing into water, dropwise adding                  1-3 pts. wt. catalyst solution prepared in step                  (S3), heating and reacting at 40-50&#8451;                  for 10-15 hours, centrifuging the product,                  washing, and drying to obtain thorn-shaped                  polydopamine-calcium carbonate composite hollow                  spheres, (S5) dispersing the thorn-shaped                  polydopamine-calcium carbonate composite hollow                  spheres prepared in step (S4) into the silica                  sol, heating and reacting at 50-70&#8451; for                  20-30 hours, centrifuging, and washing to obtain                  silica composite thorn-shaped                  polydopamine-calcium carbonate composite hollow                  spheres, (S6) adding 100 pts. wt. silica                  composite thorn-shaped polydopamine-calcium                  carbonate composite hollow spheres prepared in                  step (S5) to the ethanol solution, adding 3-7                  pts. wt. coupling agent, heating and reacting at                  60-80&#8451; for 3-5 hours, centrifuging,                  washing and drying to obtain modified silica                  composite thorn-shaped polydopamine-calcium                  carbonate composite hollow spheres, and (S7)                  uniformly mixing 100 pts. wt. alpha,                  omega-dihydroxy polydimethylsiloxane, 3-7 pts.                  wt. modified silica composite thorn-shaped                  polydopamine-calcium carbonate composite hollow                  sphere obtained in step (S6), and 2-5 pts. wt.                  reinforcing agent, dehydrating, where the                  dehydration temperature is 90-120&#8451; and                  the vacuum degree is 0.07-0.09 MPa, cooling to                  room temperature, adding 3-6 pts. wt.                  plasticizer, 1-3 pts. wt. crosslinking agent,                  0.5-1.5 pts. wt. catalyst, and 0.1-1 pts. wt.                  coupling agent, and stirring for 1-2 hours under                  the pressure of 0.07-0.09 MPa to obtain                  mechanical nano sealing glue.

TI Preparing pasta by germinating grain, drying germinated grains, grinding sprouted grains, mixing sprouted ground grains with whole meal wheat flour, homogenizing, pressing dough, cutting product, drying, cooling pasta, and packaging product.
AU BOBRYK-MAMCZARZ A
   STAROPIETKA E
   GLOWACKI P
   COMBRZYNSKI M
AE POLSKIE ZAKLADY ZBOZOWE LUBELLA GMW (POLS-Non-standard)
GA 202283756N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing pasta with reduced glycemic index, and pasta being prepared by the method, are disclosed. The pasta is prepared by (i) germinating grain, (ii) drying the germinated grains, (iii) grinding the sprouted raw material, (iv) sieving the ground material, (v) depositing the screened material in a mill, (vi) mixing sprouted ground raw material with whole meal wheat flour, (vii) mixing dough in full vacuum mixer, (viii) pressing the dough through a die, (ix) cutting the product, (x) pre-drying at not greater than 90 degrees C for 1-50 minutes, (xi) proper-drying at a temperature of not greater than 95 degrees C to a humidity of not greater than 12.5% for 150-600 minutes, (xii) cooling the product to a temperature of not greater than 28 degrees C, and (xiii) packaging the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing pasta.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares pasta with reduced glycemic index.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing pasta with reduced glycemic index, and pasta being prepared by the method, are disclosed. The pasta is prepared by (i) germinating grain by soaking grain for 24-36 hours, performing aeration for 140-240 minutes, performing carbon dioxide (CO2) suction for 6-12 hours, and performing grain germination at 10-20 degrees C for 48-52 hours, (ii) drying the germinated grains by pre-drying at 55-70 degrees C for 10-16 hours, proper drying at 80-90 degrees C for 6-12 hours, and cooling to 26 degrees C for 16-24 hours, (iii) grinding the sprouted raw material, (iv) sieving the ground material, (v) depositing the screened material in a mill, (vi) mixing sprouted ground raw material with whole meal wheat flour in a ratio of 1:99% to 50:50%, transporting the raw material to a production line, mixing and homogenizing raw material in the flow, and initially mixing the flour mixture with water, (vii) mixing dough in full vacuum mixer for 10-16 minutes, (viii) pressing the dough through a die at an extrusion temperature of 30-50 degrees C, where die has cylinder with temperature of 30-46 degrees C and head with temperature of 35-48 degrees C, and temperature of the water to the dough is 32-50 degrees C, (ix) cutting the product, (x) pre-drying at not greater than 90 degrees C for 1-50 minutes, (xi) proper-drying at a temperature of not greater than 95 degrees C to a humidity of not greater than 12.5% for 150-600 minutes, (xii) cooling the product to a temperature of not greater than 28 degrees C, and (xiii) packaging the product. The pasta comprises sprouted wheat grain flour and whole wheat flour in a ratio of 1:90% to 50:50%.

   ADVANTAGE - The composite film: has excellent physical performance; enhance the shelf life of fruit and vegetable; can control the transmission coefficient of oxygen, carbon dioxide and water vapor; and solve the problem of white pollution caused by traditional non-degradable fruit and vegetable fresh-keeping bag.

oil and placing into a parallel double-screw extruder, setting the temperature of the double-screw extruder to be 185degrees Celsius and carrying out melt blending and air cooling granulation to extruder to be 135degrees Celsius and carrying out melt blending and air cooling granulation to prepare a bonding layer modified material, (iii) adding PBAT, nano barium sulfate and an aluminum titanium composite coupling agent into a high-speed mixer, setting the rotating speed of the high-speed mixer to be 500rpm, uniformly mixing materials in the high-speed mixer with PPC, an antioxidant 168, erucamide and white oil when the temperature of the high-speed mixer is raised to 130degrees Celsius, adding the materials into a parallel double-screw extruder together, setting the temperature PN IN202141051936-A; IN397935-B
TI Preparing Rutin crosslinked clay-agar based nanocomposite film use for packaging film, packaging container, involves adding agar nano solution in conical flask and heating solution by slowly increasing temperature under continuous stirring.
AU SAHITYARANI M
AE SAHITYARANI M (SAHI-Individual)
GA 2021F0596S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a Rutin crosslinked clay-agar based nanocomposite film involves adding 100 ml, 4% agar nano solution in a conical flask and heating the solution to 100 degrees C by slowly increasing the temperature under continuous stirring to provide a solution one, adding 100 ml, 3% clay nano solution at 100 degrees C to solution two to provide solution two, where solution two is heated for 5 minutes and cooling down the solution two gradually to 50?OC adding 100 ml, 0.5 wt.% rutin solution to the cooled solution three to provide solution three, where the solution three is allowed to cross link at 50?Oc for 30 minutes under continuous stirring to provide solution four adding glycerol 3% the solution four post 10 minutes of the addition of rutin to allow the polymerization reaction to provide solution five or nanocomposite film forming solution, subsequently after 30 minutes of cross linking a clay-agar based nanocomposite film from the solution five is allowed to cool.
   USE - Preparing a Rutin crosslinked clay-agar based nanocomposite film use for packaging film, packaging container and its combinations (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The film has a good mechanical strength with tensile strength and barrier properties that reduces permeation oxygen, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, moisture, and aroma molecules, and are able to maintain freshness of foodstuff for upto twenty five days, where the foodstuff is fresh, frozen, very chilled or thawed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a Rutin crosslinked clay-agar based nanocomposite film involves adding 100 ml, 4% agar nano solution in a conical flask and heating the solution to 100 degrees C by slowly increasing the temperature under continuous stirring to provide a solution one, adding 100 ml, 3% clay nano solution at 100 degrees C to solution two to provide solution two, where solution two is heated for 5 minutes and cooling down the solution two gradually to 50?OC adding 100 ml, 0.5 wt.% rutin solution to the cooled solution three to provide solution three, where the solution three is allowed to cross link at 50?Oc for 30 minutes under continuous stirring to provide solution four adding glycerol 3% the solution four post 10 minutes of the addition of rutin to allow the polymerization reaction to provide solution five or nanocomposite film forming solution, subsequently after 30 minutes of cross linking a clay-agar based nanocomposite film from the solution five is allowed to cool to 20 degrees C for proper gelation, finally 40 ml nanocomposite film forming solution or solution five is casted on petriplates and dried at 55 degrees C for 24 hours, where the rutin crosslinked clay-agar based nanocomposite films are peeled off and stored in dessicator at 20 degrees C for the further study, and control films is developed without the addition of Rutin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The Rutin crosslinked clay-agar based nanocomposite has a good mechanical strength with tensile strength ranging from 32-38 Mpa, % elongation at break 9.42-10.56%, thickness 0.211-0.220mm and water vapor permeability ranging from 12-15x 10-11gm-1s-1pa-1 Preferred Components: The Rutin crosslinked clay-agar based nanocomposite film exhibited good antioxidant activity in terms of sustained release of 2,2-diphenyl-1-picrylhydrazyl radical scavenging assay for 12 hours in distilled water. The 3% clay nanosolution is one or more selected from montmorillonite, bentonite, kaolinite, mica, hectorite, fluorohectorite, saponite, beidellite, nontronite, stevensite, vermiculite, hallosite, volkonskoite, suconite, magadite, and kenyalite.

PN CN113579244-A
TI Gold-silver-platinum ternary alloy nanoparticles obtained by e.g. reducing ternary alloy nanoparticles to gold nanoparticles, silver nanoparticles and platinum nanoparticles, dispersing in isopropanol solution, adding silicon source, calcining, and removing silicon dioxide shell by alkaline etching.
AU WANG Y
   WANG J
   LIU Y
   ZHANG C
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 2021C6231L
AB    NOVELTY - Gold-silver-platinum (AuAgPt) ternary alloy nanoparticles obtained by reducing the ternary alloy nanoparticles to gold nanoparticles, silver nanoparticles, and platinum nanoparticles by reducing the gold-containing acid, the platinum-containing acid salt, and the silver-containing acid salt, respectively, dispersing the three kinds of nanoparticles in an isopropanol solution, adding a silicon source to prepare a nano core-shell material, where the outer layer of which is silica, dispersing the inner core gold nanoparticles, silver nanoparticles, and platinum nanoparticles, calcining the core-shell nano material at a high temperature to obtain the AuAgPt ternary alloy nano-core-shell material, and removing the silicon dioxide shell by alkaline etching.
   USE - Used as gold-silver-platinum ternary alloy nanoparticles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high thermal stability and catalytic activity, and good application prospect in the catalytic field of carbon monoxide low temperature oxidation, carbon dioxide hydrogenation to prepare methanol, photolysis water hydrogen and water gas shift.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the nanoparticles, comprising (i) heating 0.01-0.3 wt.% gold-containing acid aqueous solution to boiling at room temperature, adding 5-15 ml a reducing agent aqueous solution with a concentration of 1-5 mol/l, reacting for 2-5 hours, and cooling to obtain a gold sol; (ii) heating an aqueous solution containing 0.01-0.3 wt.% platinum salt to boiling at room temperature, adding 5-15 ml reducing agent aqueous solution with concentration of 1-5 mol/l, reacting for 2-5 hours, and cooling to obtain platinum sol; (iii) heating the silver-containing acid aqueous solution with a mass percentage of 0.01-0.3 wt.% to boiling at room temperature, adding 5-15 ml reducing agent aqueous solution with a concentration of 1-5 mol/l, reacting for 2-5 hours, and cooling to obtain silver sol; (iv) adding 0-10 ml gold sol, 0-10 ml platinum sol, and 0-10 ml silver sol into 50-100 ml isopropanol solution, and ultrasonically dispersing for 0.5-2 hours to obtain a metal sol isopropanol mixture, adding silicon source at room temperature according to the mass ratio of silicon source to metal sol 1:20-1:100, stirring at room temperature for 12-24 hours, centrifuging, and vacuum drying at 60-100degrees Celsius for 610 hours to obtain AuAgPt trimetallic nano-core-shell material; (v) calcining the above-mentioned nano-core-shell material at 500-1000degrees Celsius for 2-10 hours under a specific atmosphere to prepare the AuAgPt ternary alloy nano-core-shell material; and (vi) dispersing the AuAgPt ternary alloy nano core-shell material in an inorganic alkali solution with a pH of 8-11, stirring for 12-24 hours at 30-50degrees Celsius, centrifuging, and vacuum drying at 60-100degrees Celsius for 6-10 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the gold-containing acid is tetrachloroauric acid and/or gold acetate, and the reducing agent is trisodium citrate. In the step (ii), the platinum-containing salt is potassium chloroplatinate and/or potassium chloroplatinate, and the reducing agent is trisodium citrate. In the step (iii), the silver-containing salt is silver nitrate and/or silver acetate, and the reducing agent is trisodium citrate. In the step (iv), the silicon source is tetraethyl orthosilicate and/or hexamethyldisiloxane. In the step (v), the atmosphere is nitrogen, air, and/or argon. In the step (vi), the inorganic base is sodium hydroxide, ammonia water and/or potassium hydroxide.

AB    NOVELTY - Preheat-free high-efficiency welding of                wear-resistant steel plates involves preparing weld                steel plate, where the yield strength of the base                material is 1050 megaPascal, the tensile strength                is 1350 megaPascal, the elongation is 9%, and the                impact energy at -20 degrees C is KV2 os 24J and                the chemical composition comprises 0.25 wt.%                carbon, 0.60 wt.% silicon, 1.50 wt.% manganese,                0.50 wt.% nickel, 0.50 wt.% molybdenum, 0.06 wt.%                aluminum, 0.012 wt.% phosphors, 0.002 wt.% sulfur                and balance is iron and unavoidable impurities,                using welding wire with low tensile strength. The                gas-electric vertical welding is adopted, and the                welding parameters includes adopting symmetrical                X-shaped groove, the front groove surface is 16-18                millimeter, the back groove surface is 16-18                millimeter, the welding groove blunt edge size is                1-2 millimeter, and the groove assembly leaves a                4-6 millimeter gap, interlayer temperature is 150                degrees C.
   USE - Preheat-free high-efficiency welding of                wear-resistant steel plates.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can avoid the welding joint                generating cold crack, ensures the welding joint                -20 degrees C low temperature impact power of not                less than 24J comprehensive mechanical property,                20-32mm thick HBW450 hardness level of                wear-resistant steel plate welding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preheat-free high-efficiency welding of                wear-resistant steel plates involves preparing weld                steel plate, where the yield strength of the base                material is 1050 megaPascal, the tensile strength                is 1350 megaPascal, the elongation is 9%, and the                impact energy at -20 degrees C is KV2 os 24J and                the chemical composition comprises 0.25 wt.%                carbon, 0.60 wt.% silicon, 1.50 wt.% manganese,                0.50 wt.% nickel, 0.50 wt.% molybdenum, 0.06 wt.%                aluminum, 0.012 wt.% phosphors, 0.002 wt.% sulfur                and balance is iron and unavoidable impurities,                using welding wire with low tensile strength. The                gas-electric vertical welding is adopted, and the                welding parameters includes adopting symmetrical                X-shaped groove, the front groove surface is 16-18                millimeter, the back groove surface is 16-18                millimeter, the welding groove blunt edge size is                1-2 millimeter, and the groove assembly leaves a                4-6 millimeter gap, interlayer temperature is 150                degrees C, performed front-side gas-electric                vertical welding to form a front-side welding seam,                and installed a triangular water-cooled copper                gasket on the back of the welding test board for                forced cooling, performed back gas-electric                vertical welding to form a back welding seam, and                installed a water-cooled copper gasket with notches                on the completed front welding seam for forced                cooling, gas electric vertical welding welding                current of 360-380A, arc voltage 36-45V, welding                speed 11-15cm/minutes, shielding gas carbon                dioxide, gas flow rate of 24-28 L/minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The diameter of the                  welding wire is F1.6 millimeter. The welding test                  plate combination is 20mm+20mm, and the test                  plate size is 500mm (length) x 200mm (width) x                  20mm (thickness). The front bevel surface of the                  symmetric X-shaped groove is 17mm, the back bevel                  surface is 17mm, the blunt side of the welding                  groove is 2mm, and the groove assembly leaves a                  6mm gap, gas-electric vertical welding welding                  current 360A, arc voltage 38V, welding speed                  15cm/minutes, shielding gas carbon dioxide, gas                  flow 26L/minutes. PN CN113058988-A; CN113058988-B
TI Embedded acid soil repairing device has            pre-embedded repairing pipe having upper end fixedly            connected with connecting ring, where inner portion of            pre-embedded repairing pipe is provided with            degradation repairing device.
AU XU L
AE NINGBO YINZHOU HUAZHI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202178497G
AB    NOVELTY - An embedded acid soil repairing device                includes a pre-embedded repairing pipe (1) having                upper end fixedly connected with a connecting ring                (2). An outer end of the connecting ring is fixedly                connected with multiple uniformly distributed                supporting rods (3). The supporting rods are                fixedly connected with a heat-resistant layer. An                inner side of the connecting ring is provided with                a fixing ring. An inner wall of a connecting pipe                is formed with a sliding groove that is slidingly                connected with a sliding block. An inner portion of                the pre-embedded repairing pipe is provided with                multiple degradation repairing devices.
   USE - The device is used in a soil repairing                field.
   ADVANTAGE - The temperature-controlled expansion ring                quickly expands when the temperature is too high.                The carbon dioxide gas is punctured by the thimble.                The soil protective liquid flows out and covers the                soil surface. The soil is humidified while reducing                the soil temperature. The guide fiber is inserted                in the depth of the soil according to the acidity                degree of the soil. The latex ball is broken by                acid soil, and enters the soil through the through                hole to reach the effect of neutralizing acid soil.                The repairing ball of the upper side in the                pre-embedded repairing pipe in turn downwards, to                continuously neutralize and repair the acid                soil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An embedded acid soil repairing device                comprises a pre-embedded repairing pipe (1) having                upper end fixedly connected with connecting ring.                The outer end of the connecting ring (2) is fixedly                connected with uniformly distributed supporting rod                (3). Supporting rods are fixedly connected with the                heat-resistant layer. The inner side of the                connecting ring is provided with a fixing ring. The                fixing ring and the connecting ring are fixedly                connected with uniformly distributed connecting                pipes. The inner wall of the connecting pipe is                provided with two sliding grooves. The sliding                groove is slidingly connected with a sliding block.                Two sliding blocks are provided between with                adjusting sliding plate. The supporting rod is                fixedly connected with the fixing rope. One end of                the fixing rope is fixedly connected with the                adjusting sliding plate. One end of the adjusting                sliding plate far away from the fixing rope is                fixedly connected with shape memory spring. The                shape memory spring is located in the inside of the                connecting pipe. The inner wall of the fixing ring                is fixedly connected with optical heating plate.                The outer end of the optical heating plate is                contacted with the shape memory spring. The upper                end layer is provided with limiting holes. The                limiting hole is provided with a soil protection                device. The inner portion of the pre-embedded                repairing pipe is provided with degradation                repairing devices.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic diagram of the                embedded acid soil repairing device.
   Pre-embedded repairing pipe (1)
   Connecting ring (2)
   Supporting rods (3)
   Guide fiber (4)
   Repairing ball (5)

PT P
PN KR2240695-B1
TI Producing alumina clinker and potassium sulfate used for e.g., cement composition by mixing oyster shell pulverized product and alum stone pulverized product to obtain a mixture, pelletizing into briquettes and heat-treating the mixture.
AU KIM H S
   AHN J
   PARK J G
   MOSCOSO P F G
AE KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
GA 202140174P
AB    NOVELTY - Alumina clinker and potassium sulfate are produced by (a) washing an oyster shell with water to remove chlorine components and organic matter contained in the oyster shell; (b) drying step of removing moisture from washed oyster shells; (c) grinding the dried oyster shell to obtain a pulverized oyster shell; (d) after the step (c), drying the separated and sorted alum stone concentrate and pulverizing to obtain a pulverized alum stone; (e) mixing the oyster shell pulverized product and alum stone pulverized product to obtain a mixture of oyster shell pulverized product and alum stone pulverized product; (f) pelletizing step of preparing a mixture of pulverized oyster shell and pulverized alum stone into briquettes; (g) first firing step of heat-treating a mixture of pelletized oyster shell pulverized product and alum stone pulverized product to synthesize a first alumina clinker; and (h) filtration step of washing the calcined first alumina clinker with water, and filtering.
   USE - Production of alumina clinker and potassium sulfate used for a powder, cement composition and potash fertilizer composition (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method recycles oyster shells, solving environmental problems and civil complaints caused by odors caused by long-term stacking of oyster shells and saves foreign currency due to the alternative use of all imported raw materials for calyx fertilizer and special alumina cement, and has a lower firing temperature than normal Portland cement. The method reduces generation amount of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Alumina clinker and potassium sulfate are produced by (a) washing an oyster shell with water to remove chlorine components and organic matter contained in the oyster shell; (b) drying step of removing moisture from washed oyster shells; (c) grinding the dried oyster shell to obtain a pulverized oyster shell; (d) after the step (c), drying the separated and sorted alum stone concentrate and pulverizing to obtain a pulverized alum stone; (e) mixing the oyster shell pulverized product and alum stone pulverized product to obtain a mixture of oyster shell pulverized product and alum stone pulverized product; (f) pelletizing step of preparing a mixture of pulverized oyster shell and pulverized alum stone into briquettes; (g) first firing step of heat-treating a mixture of pelletized oyster shell pulverized product and alum stone pulverized product to synthesize a first alumina clinker; (h) filtration step of washing the calcined first alumina clinker with water, filtering, and separating the filtrate into a potassium sulfate filtrate and a filtrate; (i) evaporating and concentrating the potassium sulfate filtrate to obtain a potassium sulfate concentrate; (j) calcination step of evaporating moisture from potassium sulfate concentrate to prepare a potassium sulfate powder; (k) second firing step of drying and roasting the filtrate to synthesize a second fast-hardening alumina clinker; and (l) pulverizing the fired secondary alumina clinker.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The component contains 50-56 wt.% calcium oxide (CaO), 0.05-5 wt.% silicon oxide (SiO2), 0.1-2 wt.% chlorine (Cl), and 41-47 wt.% ignition loss based on washed oyster shell. The separated and sorted alum stone concentrate is 7-11.4 wt.% potassium oxide (K2O) and sodiu oxide (Na2O), 30-38 wt.% alumina (Al2O3), 31-39 wt.% sulfur trioxide (SO3), 1-13 wt.% SiO2 and 9-14 wt.% loss on ignition based on total weight of the separated and sorted alum stone concentrate. Molar ratio between the minerals of the pulverized oyster shell and the pulverized alum is greater than or equal to 3. Molar ratio of minerals with respect to pulverized alum stone is greater than or equal to 6, and molar ratio of calcium carbonate and silicon oxide is greater than or equal to 2. The first alumina clinker is calcium lanbainite (2CaSO4.K2SO4). The second alumina clinker is calcium sulfoaluminate and anhydrite (II-CaSO4). Preferred Process: Pulverization in step (c) of shell or step (d) of alum stone is performed so that the residue having a particle size of 150 mu m is less than or equal to 15%. Sintering in step (g) is heat-treated at 700-1350 degrees C for 10 minutes-5 hours. In first firing step, chlorine components volatilized by heat treatment are collected to total amount of less than or equal to 0.3 kg/m3. After step (c) or step (d), the pulverized oyster shell is subjected to heat treatment and volatilization using the waste heat of the first firing step. The calcination step of removing the component is further included, and by collecting the chlorine component volatilized by the heat treatment, chloride has total amount of 0.3 kg/m3. The water washing in step (h) comprises calcium langbainite, which is a soluble substance in the first alumina clinker, with syngenite (K2SO4.CaSO4.H2O). Evaporation in calcination step is performed by heating the potassium sulfate concentrate to greater than or equal to 100 degrees C. Roasting in the second firing step is heat-treating at 400-1250 degrees C. Powdering after the second firing step is performed by finely powdering second alumina to have a specific surface area of greater than or equal to 2500 cm2/g.

PN CN112568438-A; WO2022121739-A1; CN112568438-B
TI Sweet liquid used for food for preventing and            relieving intolerance of intestinal sugar alcohol and            reducing diarrhea comprises low-esterification pectin,            kappa-type carrageenan, locust bean gum, xylitol,            maltitol, sorbitol and water.
AU ZUO Q
   HAN F
   ZHU X
   CHENG X
   LI M
   SHI L
   ZHANG W
   WANG Y
AE ZHEJIANG HUAKANG PHARM CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   ZHEJIANG HUAKANG PHARM CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202137106N
AB    NOVELTY - A sweet liquid comprises 0.01-0.04 g                low-esterification pectin, 0.01-0.1 g kappa-type                carrageenan, 0.01-0.3 g locust bean gum, 30-60 g                xylitol, 5-15 g maltitol, 1-10 g sorbitol and                balance water for each 100 mL sweet liquid. The                low-esterification pectin is pectin concentrated                solution comprising 15 pts. wt. calcium citrate, 60                pts. wt. low molecular weight pectin, 85 pts. wt.                ethylene glycol, 5 pts. wt. sodium carboxymethyl                cellulose and 30 pts. wt. sodium citrate. The low                molecular weight pectin comprises low-sugar fruit                juice and lemon juice. The sweet liquid has pH                value of 5-6.5 and viscosity of 0.1-25                mPa-second.
   USE - The sweet liquid is useful for food for                preventing and relieving intestinal sugar alcohol                intolerance (claimed) and reducing diarrhea caused                by excessive edible sugar alcohol.
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid reduces the diarrhea caused by                excessive edible sugar alcohol, and improves the                safety and practicability of the sugar alcohol in                the product application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the sweet liquid comprising                dissolving low-esterification pectin, kappa-type                carrageenan, locust bean gum, xylitol, maltitol and                sorbitol in water to obtain sweet liquid. The                low-esterification pectin is prepared by putting                cleaned low-sugar fruit in sealed dryer,                introducing carbon dioxide with volume fraction of                75%, processing for 12-18 hours, pulping to obtain                grapefruit fruit pulp, adding lemon juice with pH                of 1.5-2.0, and continuously hydrolyzing and                extracting fruit pulp at 85 degrees C for 65-80                minutes to obtain pectin extract solution; mixing                activated carbon with particle size of 180 mesh and                water at mass ratio of 1: 5 to obtain activated                carbon mixed solution, adding nitric acid solution                with mass fraction of 80%, heating at 100-110                degrees C for 3-3.5 hours, stopping heating,                naturally cooling to 65 degrees C, stopping                stirring and continuously cooling to room                temperature to obtain acid boiling activated carbon                liquid; coarse filtering pectin extract by 160-mesh                gauze, centrifuging filtrate in high-speed                centrifuge at rotating speed of 4500-5000                revolutions/minute (rpm), collecting lower layer                gel, adding acid boiling activated carbon liquid                into lower layer gel, stirring and decoloring at 45                degrees C for 35-60 minutes to obtain decoloring                pectin filtrate; filtering decoloring pectin                filtrate by diatomite, putting in dialysis bag,                putting in pulsating pressure chamber filled with                deionized water and pulsating pressure processing                at 4 degrees C for 10 hours at pulse period of 200                minutes to obtain low molecular weight pectin; and                adding low molecular weight pectin, calcium                citrate, ethylene glycol, sodium carboxymethyl                cellulose and sodium citrate in sealed stirring                kettle, stirring at rotating speed of 550-600 rpm,                heating to 55-65 degrees C, introducing ammonia                into stirring kettle, putting into vacuum                evaporator with vacuum degree of - 0.1 kPa and                processing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The low-sugar fruit                  comprises grapefruit, cherry and/or guava.
   ACTIVITY - Antidiarrheic.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN CN112481202-A
TI Isolating and cultivating primary umbilical cord            mesenchymal stem cells involves processing umbilical            cord samples, obtaining Wharton's jelly tissue,            culturing, washing cells, digesting, centrifuging and            resuspending cell pellet.
AU XIAO H
AE SHENZHEN BOYA PERCEPTION PHARM CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021327667
AB    NOVELTY - Method for isolating and cultivating primary                umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells involves (a)                transporting umbilical cord samples to laboratory                through 2-8 degrees C cold chain in a biological                safety cabinet, (b) using D-Hanks thoroughly to                clean and remove blood stains on the surface,                cutting the umbilical cord into small sections with                surgical scissors, placing in a Petri dish,                repeatedly squeezing with surgical forceps to                remove residual blood clots in the tissue and                cleaning with D-Hanks, (c) cutting the tissue,                peeling off the epidermis, removing arteries and                veins to obtain Wharton's jelly tissue and cutting,                (d) inoculating tissue, then adding original                complete medium, mixing, making the tissue block                evenly spread at the bottom of the bottle and                culturing, and (e) cultivating, washing cells,                digesting, diluting, centrifuging and resuspending                cell pellet in primary complete medium.
   USE - The method or medium is useful for isolating                and cultivating primary umbilical cord mesenchymal                stem cells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method cultures primary umbilical cord                mesenchymal stem cells positive for CD73, CD90, and                CD105 (all greater than 98%) and negative for CD19,                CD11b, CD31, CD45, HLADR and CD34 (all less than                2%).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for isolating and cultivating primary                umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells involves (a)                transporting umbilical cord samples to laboratory                through 2-8 degrees C cold chain in a biological                safety cabinet, (b) using D-Hanks thoroughly to                clean and remove blood stains on the surface,                cutting the umbilical cord into small sections with                surgical scissors, placing in a Petri dish,                repeatedly squeezing with surgical forceps to                remove residual blood clots in the tissue and                cleaning with D-Hanks, (c) cutting the tissue,                peeling off the epidermis, removing arteries and                veins to obtain Wharton's jelly tissue, cutting the                Wharton's jelly into small portions and weighing                portions, (d) inoculating culture bottle with                respect to the prescribed amount of tissue, then                adding original complete medium, mixing thoroughly,                making the tissue block evenly spread at the bottom                of the bottle and placing in a carbon dioxide                incubator for culture, and (e) cultivating to                supplement complete medium on the 3rd day,                continuously cultivating, supplementing the                original complete medium on the 5th day, changing                the medium on the 7th day, when the cell confluence                is above 80%, removing old medium, washing the                cells with D-hanks solution, adding recombinant                trypsin solution to digest the cells, making the                cells fall off, adding D-hanks solution to dilute,                centrifuging and resuspending cell pellet in                primary complete medium to obtain primary umbilical                cord mesenchymal stem cells. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM                is included for medium i.e. primary complete                medium, which is prepared with Dulbecco's modified                Eagle medium/nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM-F12)                medium as the matrix and contains 0.8% platelet                lysate, 1% human albumin, 2 mu g/ml recombinant                insulin, 15 ng/ml epidermal growth factor (EGF) and                25 ng/ml basic fibroblast growth factor (bFGF), and                optionally 0.1% thioglycerol and 1% fructose.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The D-Hanks solution                  comprises 8 g sodium chloride, 0.4 g potassium                  chloride, 0.06 g potassium dihydrogen phosphate,                  0.08 g disodium phosphate dodecahydrate, 0.35 g                  sodium bicarbonate and water (to make up to 1000                  ml). Preferred Medium: The DMEM-F12 medium                  comprises 116.6 mg anhydrous calcium chloride,                  59.05 mg L-leucine, 0.042 mg linoleic acid,                  0.0013 mg copper sulfate pentahydrate, 91.25 mg                  L-lysine hydrochloride, 0.105 mg lipoic acid,                  0.05 mg ferric nitrate nonahydrate, 17.24 mg                  L-methionine, 8.1 mg phenol red, 0.417 mg ferrous                  sulfate heptahydrate, 35.48 mg L-phenylalanine,                  0.081 mg1,4-butanediamine dihydrochloride, 311.8                  mg potassium chloride, 26.25 mg L-serine, 55 mg                  sodium pyruvate, 28.64 mg magnesium chloride,                  53.45 mg L-threonine, 0.0035 mg vitamin H, 48.84                  mg anhydrous magnesium sulfate, 4.45 mg                  L-alanine, 2.24 mg D-calcium pantothenate, 7000                  mg sodium chloride, 7.5 mg L-asparagine, 8.98 mg                  choline chloride, 54.35 mg anhydrous sodium                  dihydrogen phosphate, 6.65 mg L-aspartic acid,                  2.65 mg folic acid, 71.02 mg disodium hydrogen                  phosphate, 17.56 mg L-cysteine hydrochloride,                  12.6 mg i-inositol, 0.432 mg zinc sulfate                  heptahydrate, 7.35 mg L-glutamic acid, 2.02 mg                  nicotinamide, 147.5 mg L-arginine hydrochloride,                  17.25 mg L-proline, 2 mg pyridoxal hydrochloride,                  31.29 mg L-cystine hydrochloride, 9.02 mg                  L-tryptophan, 0.031 mg pyridoxine hydrochloride,                  365 mg L-glutamine, 38.4 mg L-tyrosine, 0.219 mg                  riboflavin, 18.75 mg glycine, 52.85 mg L-valine,                  2.17 mg thiamine hydrochloride, 31.48 mg                  L-histidine hydrochloride, 3151 mg D-glucose,                  0.365 mg thymidine, 54.47 mg L-isoleucine, 2 mg                  hypoxanthine, 0.68 mg vitamin B12 and water (to                  make up to 1000 ml). The DMEM-F12 medium is                  prepared by dissolving the raw materials in 1000                  ml water, filtering and sterilizing with 0.22 mu                  m microporous membrane to obtain product.                  Preferred Method: In step (c), the Wharton's                  jelly is cut into small portion with a size of                  0.25 cm2. The step (d) involves inoculating into                  the culture flask with respect to the prescribed                  amount of tissue refers to weighing 1.5 g                  Wharton's jelly tissue block and inoculating into                  T225 culture flask, or adding 15 ml primary                  complete medium to each bottle, mixing uniformly,                  making the tissue block evenly spread at the                  bottom of the bottle and placing in a carbon                  dioxide incubator for culture. In step (d), the                  culturing step is performed at 37 degrees C in a                  carbon dioxide incubator in presence of 5% carbon                  dioxide and saturated humidity. The step (e)                  involves culturing to the 3rd day supplemented                  with 10 ml primary complete medium to continue                  culturing, continuously cultivating until the 5th                  day by supplementing with 10 ml primary complete                  medium, adding 5 ml recombinant trypsin solution                  to each bottle to digest the cells for 2 minutes,                  adding 25 ml D-hanks solution to each bottle to                  dilute, and centrifuging at 100 g for 10 minutes.                  The D-Hanks solution is prepared by dissolving                  each material in 1000 ml water, filtering and                  sterilizing with a 0.22 mu m microporous                  membrane. The method further involves detecting                  the primary umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells                  obtained by isolation and culture, or detecting                  cell morphology and/or immunophenotype                  identification. The immunophenotypic                  identification refers to the detection of cluster                  of differentiation (CD)73, CD90, CD105, CD19,                  CD11b, CD31, CD45, human leukocyte antigen-DR                  isotype (HLADR) and CD34.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN112355479-A; CN112355479-B
TI Composite welding method capable of matching and uniform hardness of welded joints of ultra-fast cooling and high-strength axle housing steel by selecting steel plate, double-sided welding with welding wire, and filling welding.
AU HUANG Z
   PENG T
   HE J
   WANG H
   CUI L
   CHEN F
AE WUHAN STEEL CO LTD (WSGC-C)
GA 202118261X
AB    NOVELTY - A composite welding method capable of matching and uniform hardness of welded joints of ultra-fast cooling and high-strength axle housing steel includes selecting steel plate; selecting welding method comprising combined laser welding and gas shielded welding; setting welding process conditions; determining composition of welding wire comprising carbon, manganese, silicon, nickel, copper, titanium, chromium and molybdenum, sulfur, and phosphorus; double-sided welding using laser beam in front and gas shielded arc at the back, and filling welding until welding seam meets the requirements. The steel plate is carbon-silicon-manganese series steel and is niobium-titanium microalloyed.
   USE - The method is for composite welding capable of matching and uniform hardness of welded joints of ultra-fast cooling and high-strength axle housing steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of making the hardness of ultra-fast cooling high-strength bridge shell steel welding joint match and uniform.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A composite welding method capable of matching and uniform hardness of welded joints of ultra-fast cooling and high-strength axle housing steel comprises selecting steel plate having thickness of 10-20 mm, tensile strength of 650-700 MPa, HV10 hardness of 205-230, carbon equivalent (CE) of 0.29-0.33 and sensitive crack coefficient (Pcm) of 0.13-0.15; selecting weld bevel form that is double-sided U-shaped and has bottom arc radius (R) of 1.5-2.5 mm, blunt edge of 6-7.5 mm and bevel angle of 10-15 degrees ; selecting welding method comprising combined laser welding and gas shielded welding; setting welding process conditions comprising shielding gas which is mixture of 78-82 wt.% argon and 18-22 wt.% carbon dioxide, welding current at 210-280 A, welding voltage at 17-28 V, welding speed at 0.4-0.8 meters/minute, gas shielded welding energy (Eg) at 2.6-10 kW, laser welding power (E1) at 1.5-4.5 kW, heat input of composite welding at 6-12 kJ/cm and cooling rate at 220-500 K/hour; determining composition of 0.8-1 mm diameter welding wire comprising less than or equal to 0.09 wt.% carbon, 1.20-15. wt.% manganese, 0.40-0.7 wt.% silicon, 0.6-1 wt.% nickel, 0.10-0.25 wt.% copper, 0.01-0.10 wt.% titanium, less than or equal to 0.15 wt.% chromium and molybdenum, less than or equal to 0.015 wt.% sulfur and less than or equal to 0.020 wt.% phosphorus; double-sided welding using laser beam in front and gas shielded arc at the back at laser beam and welding wire distance of 1.8-2.2 mm, and filling welding until welding seam meets the requirements. The steel plate is carbon-silicon-manganese series steel and is niobium-titanium microalloyed.
