PT P
PN EP3730474-A1
TI Production of methanol involves purifying methanol in crude methanol product by removing light and heavy ends, using net heat input provided by non-carbon based energy source, renewable energy source, and/or electricity.
AU WARD A M
   STEVENSON S
   OPRINS A J M
   ZHAO Z
   ABBOTT T
   LAWSON K F
   SCHROER J W
   HUCKMAN M E
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 2020A61766
AB    NOVELTY - Production of methanol involves heating compressed, purified reformer product by a net heat input (Q5) to provide a methanol synthesis feed, producing a product comprising methanol from the methanol synthesis feed, cooling the product comprising methanol by a net heat removal (Q6) to remove a crude methanol stream and provide a recycle gas stream comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen, compressing the recycle gas stream through a recycle compressor, (k) purging through a purge gas system, by using a net heat input or removal (Q7), purifying the methanol in the crude methanol product by removing light ends, heavy byproducts, water, and/or crude methanol stream, using a net heat input or removal (Q8). The net energy input or removal is provided by a non-carbon based energy source, a renewable energy source, and/or electricity.
   USE - Production of methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical manufacture of methanol using the renewable energy source such as wind, solar, geothermal, hydroelectric, nuclear, tide, and wave sources, preheating the feed using the heat obtained from the cooling of the reformer product, maintains reforming temperature which is effected without combusting a fuel, a fossil fuel, and/or a carbon-based fuel, externally to the feed, and reduces the environmental emission e.g. carbon dioxide emission, by utilizing the renewable energy, and reducing the combustion of hydrocarbons such as natural gas and fossil fuel for fuel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of methanol involves (a) reforming a feed comprising methane, ethane, propane, butane, LPG, naphtha, and/or biogas, to produce a reformer product comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen, at a reforming temperature is maintained by a net heat input (Q1), (b) cooling the reformer product to produce a cooled reformer product by effecting a net heat removal (Q2), (c) shifting the reformer product to produce a shifted reformer product, (d) cooling the shifted product by effecting a net heat removal (Q3), (e) cooling and condensing water from the reformer product by effecting a net heat removal (Q4) to provide a purified reformer product, (f) compressing the purified reformer product, (g) heating the compressed, purified reformer product by a net heat input (Q5) to provide a methanol synthesis feed, (h) producing a product comprising methanol from the methanol synthesis feed, (i) cooling the product comprising methanol by a net heat removal (Q6) to remove a crude methanol stream and provide a recycle gas stream comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen, (j) compressing the recycle gas stream through a recycle compressor, (k) purging through a purge gas system, by using a net heat input or removal (Q7), (l) purifying the methanol in the crude methanol product by removing light ends, heavy byproducts, water, and/or crude methanol stream, using at a net heat input or removal (Q8). The net energy input or removal is provided by a non-carbon based energy source, a renewable energy source, and/or electricity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The reforming is performed in reformer(s) which are maintained at the reforming temperature through heating from electricity or renewable electricity and including associated convective, radiant or other heat transfer units. The steam is not utilized as a primary energy transfer medium. The heat removal is performed without producing steam for use in a steam turbine. The compression of the dry syngas or the purified reformer product involves compressing the recycle gas stream, compressing the feed stream for introduction into reformers, and/or compressing with a compressor driven by an electric motor, an electrically-driven turbine, or a turbine driven by steam produced electrically.

PN CN111798430-A; CN111798430-B
TI Centrifugal pump axial thrombus identification platform includes gas exchanger, real-time imager, centrifugal pump shaft, curve processing equipment, smoothing processing device, band-stop filter device, and thrombus identification device.
AU ZHANG X
   CHEN W
   LIN Y
   CHANG P
AE ZHANG X (ZHAN-Individual)
   UNIV NO 2 AFFILIATED HOSPITAL NO 3 MILIT (UYTW-Non-standard)
GA 2020A4731A
AB    NOVELTY - A centrifugal pump axial thrombus identification platform includes gas exchanger set in the operating room, real-time imager packaged on inner wall of the centrifugal pump shaft of gas exchanger, curve processing equipment connected to real-time imager, smoothing processing device used to perform non-scaling transformation blur processing on the received radian adjustment image and to obtain and output the corresponding smoothed image, band-stop filter device used to perform band-stop filtering on the received smoothed image to obtain and output the corresponding band-stop filtered image, and thrombus identification device used to identify each thrombus pixel point from the band-stop filtered image based on thrombus color imaging characteristics and issues a thrombus formation instruction when the percentage of the number of thrombus pixels in the band-stop filtered image occupying the total number of pixels in the band-stop filtered image exceeds a limit.
   USE - Centrifugal pump axial thrombus identification platform.
   ADVANTAGE - The thrombus identification platform is convenient to promptly remind medical staff to implement corresponding emergency measures after thrombus identification is successful, and can perform real-time thrombus identification operation on the centrifugal pump axis of the gas exchanger that is most prone to thrombosis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A centrifugal pump axial thrombus identification platform comprises gas exchanger set in the operating room and is used to replace the human lungs to perform the corresponding carbon dioxide to oxygen gas exchange work, real-time imager packaged on inner wall of the centrifugal pump shaft of gas exchanger and is used to perform real-time imaging actions on the scene where the centrifugal pump axis is located to obtain work scene images, curve processing equipment connected to real-time imager and is used to adjust the maximum arc of each curve in the received work scene image to be lower than or equal to the preset maximum arc threshold of the curve to obtain and output the corresponding radian adjustment image, smoothing processing device used to perform non-scaling transformation blur processing on the received radian adjustment image and to obtain and output the corresponding smoothed image, band-stop filter device used to perform band-stop filtering on the received smoothed image to obtain and output the corresponding band-stop filtered image, and thrombus identification device used to identify each thrombus pixel point from the band-stop filtered image based on thrombus color imaging characteristics and issues a thrombus formation instruction when the percentage of the number of thrombus pixels in the band-stop filtered image occupying the total number of pixels in the band-stop filtered image exceeds a limit. The thrombus identification device and thrombus color imaging features are different from blood color imaging features. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an identification method using the thrombus identification platform.

PN WO2020141285-A1; FR3092576-A1; CA3125574-A1; BR112021012918-A2; CN113454043-A; EP3906220-A1; IN202117034381-A; ZA202105390-A; US2022119313-A1; JP2022519011-W; EP3906220-B1; EP3906220-B8
TI Construction binder used e.g. in construction            material e.g. mortar, plaster, plaster, insulator,            light concrete and precast element comprises raw clay            matrix, deflocculation agent and activating            composition.
AU NEUVILLE M
AE MATERRUP (MATE-Non-standard)
   NEUVILLE M (NEUV-Individual)
   MATERRUP (MATE-Non-standard)
   MATERRUP (MATE-Non-standard)
   MATERRUP (MATE-Non-standard)
GA 202062169G
AB    NOVELTY - Construction binder comprises at least 30 wt.%                a raw clay matrix, a deflocculation agent and an                activating composition.
   USE - The construction binder is useful: in a                construction material e.g. a mortar, a plaster, a                plaster, an insulator, a light concrete and a                precast element; for the production of covering                elements, preferably floor coverings, e.g. tiles,                slabs, pavers or borders, floor coverings, walls,                e.g. interior or exterior facade elements, cladding                panels, cladding elements, or tile-type roof                coverings, for the realization of extruded or                molded building modules, e.g. bricks, or for the                production of various extruded shapes; for the                production of composite materials, e.g.                construction panels of the prefabricated panel                type, of prefabricated blocks e.g. door or window                lintels, prefabricated wall elements, or any other                prefabricated construction element; for the                production of insulation modules, e.g. partition                panels, or light insulating construction modules                (of density less than 1.5 kg/l, preferably less                than 0.7 kg/l); for the production by additive                manufacturing, e.g. by means of a three-dimensional                printer, of construction elements, buildings or                houses, or decorative items; and for the production                of mastic, glue or sealing mortar (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The construction binder: allows the                manufacture of construction materials having                mechanical properties at least equivalent to                concretes formulated with Portland cement or low                carbon materials; is able to comprise a high                quantity of raw clay matrix without affecting the                mechanical properties of the construction materials                making it possible to produce construction                materials with performance adapted mechanics;                exhibits improved stability; and has a lower                clinker rate than equivalent products and makes it                possible, with equivalent mechanical properties, to                reduce carbon dioxide emissions and the production                costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (1)                preparing the construction binder, comprising                preparing a clay suspension comprising at least one                raw clay matrix, deflocculating agent and water,                adding the activating composition to the clay                suspension, in which the activating composition is                an alkaline activating composition and/or comprises                metal oxides, and mixing to obtain the binder                comprising at least 30 wt.% green clay matrix; (2)                construction material e.g. a mortar, a plaster, a                plaster, an insulator, a light concrete, a precast                element, comprising the construction binder; (3)                use of the binder, in the form of a two-component                system with either on the one hand the constituents                under solid form, and on the other hand the                constituents in liquid form, or the constituents in                the form of two pastes, for the production of                mastic, glue or sealing mortar; and (4) use, for                the preparation of a building binder, of a                deflocculating agent in combination with at least                30 wt.% green clay matrix, and an activating                composition allowing to obtain a concrete having a                minimum compressive strength on cylinders at 28                days as measured by standard NF EN 206-1 of greater                than or equal to 20 MPa, preferably greater than or                equal to 40 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The construction                  binder comprises: less than 30 wt.% metakaolin;                  at least 40 wt.% raw clay matrix; at least 0.25                  wt.% deflocculation agent; at least 10 wt.%                  activating composition; at least 2 wt.% metal                  oxides; at least 5 wt.% a composition of blast                  furnace slag; and excavated earth comprising the                  raw clay matrix. The construction binder                  preferably comprises 45-70 wt.% raw clay matrix,                  0.5-5 wt.% deflocculating agent, 10-25 wt.%                  activating composition, 5-20 wt.% blast furnace                  slag, and 15-30 wt.% metakaolin. The construction                  material further comprises: at least one filler                  comprising mineral fillers or vegetable fillers;                  and an expanding or foaming agent, e.g. aluminum                  powder. Preferred Components: The deflocculation                  agent is: a nonionic surfactant e.g. a                  polyoxyethylene ether; an anionic agent e.g. an                  anionic agent comprising alkylaryl sulfonates,                  aminoalcohol, carbonates, silicates, fatty acids,                  humates (e.g. sodium humates), carboxylic acids,                  lignosulfonates (e.g. lignosulfonates of sodium),                  polyacrylates, phosphates or polyphosphates e.g.                  sodium hexametaphosphate, sodium                  tripolyphosphate, sodium orthophosphate and/or                  carboxymethyl celluloses; a polyacrylate e.g. a                  polyacrylate comprising sodium polyacrylate or                  ammonium polyacrylate; an amine e.g. an amine                  comprising 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol; mono-,                  di- or triethanolamine; and/or isopropanolamines                  (1-amino-2-propanol, diisopropanolamine and                  triisopropanolamine) and N-alkylated                  ethanolamines. The raw clay matrix, the                  deflocculating agent and the activating                  composition form a two-component or                  multi-component system. The activating                  composition is an alkaline activating                  composition. The activating composition comprises                  at least 2 wt.% an organophosphorus compound e.g.                  sodium tripolyphosphate. The metal oxides                  comprise e.g. iron oxides e.g. ferrous oxide,                  alumina, manganese(II) oxide and/or titanium(IV)                  oxide. The activating composition comprises:                  Portland cement; and metakaolin.

PT P
PN CN111172109-A
TI Culturing immune cell by isolating mononuclear cells in umbilical cord blood, inoculating into antibody coated culture bottle, adding medium, PBS9 (-) and ErbB2 stock solution, culturing, adding calf serum and harvesting cells.
AU LIANG Y
   ZHANG S
   XIE H
AE GUANGZHOU HAIRUN KANGHUA BIOTECHNOLOGY (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202047723Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for culturing immune cell involves using hemostatic forceps to clamp and cut the umbilical cord of newborn, cleaning the umbilical cord with sterile gauze, then sterilizing with iodophor, collecting umbilical cord blood, isolating mononuclear cells in umbilical cord blood by dextran diatrizoate density gradient centrifugation, inoculating the isolated mononuclear cells into the antibody coated culture bottle, adding phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) and receptor tyrosine-protein kinase ErbB2 antibody into the culture bottle, incubating, then gently washing along the side wall of the culture bottle using PBS, adding medium into the culture bottle coated with ErbB2 antibody, adding PBS9 (-) and ErbB2 stock solution, evenly stirring the cell suspension, adding cell suspension into culture flasks, culturing in an incubator, adding calf serum, ALys505NK-AC medium, interleukin-5 (IL-5) and fresh plasma, continuing culturing and harvesting the cells.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing immune cell which is useful for preparing immune cell products (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and conducive to a large number of clinical application of immune cells or clinical use of immune cells, has high culture efficiency and high added value, improves growth rate of immune cells and forms larger cell colonies, hence improves the cultivation efficiency of immune cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for culturing immune cell involves (a) using hemostatic forceps to clamp and cut the umbilical cord at 10-15 cm end of the newborn, cleaning the umbilical cord with sterile gauze and then sterilizing with iodophor, (b) passing a needle through the umbilical vein, collecting the umbilical cord blood in the blood bag containing the anticoagulant and when the umbilical vein gradually turns white, it indicates the umbilical blood collection is completed, (c) isolating mononuclear cells in umbilical cord blood by dextran diatrizoate density gradient centrifugation, (d) inoculating the isolated mononuclear cells into the antibody coated culture bottle, adding 10 ml phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) and 1 ml 2.4 mg/ml receptor tyrosine-protein kinase ErbB2 antibody into the culture bottle, incubating for 1 hour, then gently washing twice along the side wall of the culture bottle using 10 ml PBS, adding medium into the culture bottle coated with ErbB2 antibody, adding 4 ml PBS9 (-) and 1 ml ErbB2 stock solution into 75 cm2 culture bottle, evenly stirring the cell suspension, adding 1x 107 cell/ml cell suspension into culture flasks separately, observing under an inverted microscope, culturing at 37 degrees C and humidity of 50%-70% under 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator and (e) continuing culturing for 36 hours, adding 20 ng/ml calf serum into the flask, continuing culturing for 48 hours, then adding 500 ml ALys505NK-AC medium, 1000 u/ml interleukin-5 (IL-5) and 5 ml fresh plasma, continuing culturing for 36 hours, adding 300 ml ALys505NK-AC medium and 1000 u/ml IL-5, continuing culturing for 24 hours and harvesting the cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves rapidly cleaning the umbilical cord with sterile gauze in the direction of the placenta and then sterilizing twice with iodophor. The step (b) involves when the umbilical cord blood flows into the blood bag, gradually the blood bag to fully mix the cord blood and the anticoagulant. The step (c) involves (i) adding 340 g/l diatrizoate meglumine to Ficoll (RTM: hydrophilic polysaccharide) solution to prepare a layered liquid with proper density, (ii) adding the layering liquid on the bottom layer of the test tube during separation, then diluting the heparinized whole blood with Hanks solution or PBS solution, and gently superimposing on the layering liquid to form a clear interface between the two and (iii) performing horizontal centrifugation, so that several different levels of liquid and cell bands will appear in the centrifuge tube, where the density of red blood cells and granulocytes is greater than that of the layered liquid, the red blood cells meet Ficoll (RTM: hydrophilic polysaccharide) and condense into a string of money and are deposited at the bottom of the tube, the platelets are suspended in the plasma due to the low density, the mononuclear cells equivalent to the density of the stratified fluid are dense in the interface between the plasma layer and the stratified fluid and is in the form of a white membrane, and absorb the layer of cells and washing and resuspending the cells.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-F04B; B04-G03; B11-A04A; C04-F04B; C04-G03; C11-A04A; D05-H08B

PT P
PN CN110951455-A; CN110951455-B
TI Preparing light-weight heat insulation wave            absorbing material based on graphene involves            performing pretreatment of graphene oxide and            carbonylated nano-iron particles respectively,            performing heat drying treatment followed by            acetone.
AU JIA K
   LIU W
   ZHAO W
   WANG D
   MA C
   WANG P
   WANG Q
AE CETC NO 33 INST (CETC-C)
GA 202028249V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing light-weight heat insulation wave                absorbing material based on graphene involves                performing pretreatment of graphene oxide and                carbonylated nano-iron particles respectively,                performing heat drying treatment for 1-2 hours                followed by acetone, ethanol, drying after                cleaning. The graphene oxide powder after                pretreatment is dispersed in N-methylpyrrolidone to                obtain graphene oxide solution. The graphene oxide                solution is added to the N-methylpyrrolidone                solution dissolving iron ferrite under continuous                nitrogen atmosphere stirring at 80 degrees C. The                obtained reaction mixture is transferred to                hydrothermal kettle and reacted in drying oven at                80 degrees C for 16-32 hours, suction filtration of                the mixed liquid, repeated washing with ethanol                during suction filtration process, dried to obtain                modified graphene oxide powder.
   USE - Method for preparing light-weight heat                insulation wave absorbing material based on                graphene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare light-weight                heat insulation wave absorbing material based on                graphene that has simple powder preparation                process, low production cost and excellent                electromagnetic wave absorption performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing light-weight heat insulation wave                absorbing material based on graphene involves                performing pretreatment of graphene oxide and                carbonylated nano-iron particles respectively,                performing heat drying treatment for 1-2 hours                followed by acetone, ethanol, drying after                cleaning. The graphene oxide powder after                pretreatment is dispersed in N-methylpyrrolidone to                obtain graphene oxide solution. The graphene oxide                solution is added to the N-methylpyrrolidone                solution dissolving iron ferrite under continuous                nitrogen atmosphere stirring at 80 degrees C. The                obtained reaction mixture is transferred to                hydrothermal kettle and reacted in drying oven at                80 degrees C for 16-32 hours, suction filtration of                the mixed liquid, repeated washing with ethanol                during suction filtration process, dried to obtain                modified graphene oxide powder. The aqueous                graphene oxide solution is prepared by dispersing                modified graphene oxide powder obtained with                distilled water, diluted to concentration of                0.01-0.05g/mL, centrifuged to remove unstripped                graphite and other impurities, and passed machine                for 1-2 hours, mixed, and finally forms the                required modified graphene oxide aqueous solution.                The aqueous solution of the modified graphene oxide                obtained is sonicated at room temperature for 24                hours to divide the pre-treated carbonylated                nano-iron particles, add to the modified graphene                oxide aqueous solution every two hours. The nafion                is added at the last two hours to form a viscous                suspension. The suspension is injected into liquid                nitrogen and freezed for 15-20 minutes to finalize                shape to obtain gel. The gel is placed in carbon                dioxide supercritical drying kettle for drying to                obtain graphene oxide/ carbonylated nano-iron                composite aerogel. The temperature and vacuum                degree are (-)85 degrees C and 0.06 megapascal                respectively. The graphene oxide/carbonylated                nano-iron composite aerogel is placed in tube                furnace and heat treated at 600-700 degrees C for                2-4 hours under the protection of argon atmosphere                to obtain light weight based on graphene insulation                absorbing material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The concentration of                  graphene oxide is less than or equal to 0.01g/mL                  and concentration of iron amide is less than or                  equal to 0.01g/ mL. The total mass ratio of                  carbonylated nano-iron particles to the mass of                  modified graphene oxide is 5-10:1. The mass ratio                  of nafion to modified graphene oxide is                  1:5-10.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); G04 (Miscellaneous compositions - including luminescent and tenebrescent materials, de-icing/de-misting compositions, mastics, heat transfer compositions and aerosol-can filling mixtures (C09H).)
MC A04-A; A04-E09; A04-E10C; A10-E05B; A12-R06; A12-W14; G04-B

PT P
PN CN110699040-A
TI Preparing complex microwave absorption material using coal gangue comprises e.g. ball milling gangue, sieving, drying the gangue powder, preparing a salt solution, and spray drying the slurry through a spray dryer.
AU LI G
   CHANG X
   WU Y
   QIN M
   ZHANG K
   TIAN Y
   CHAI Y
   LI Z
AE UNIV TAIYUAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNTK-C)
GA 202008399E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing complex microwave absorption material using coal gangue comprises e.g. (i) ball milling gangue, sieving through a 100-300 mesh standard sieve, drying the gangue powder screened out at 80-100 degrees C for 3-4 hours to obtain the gangue powder and storing, (ii) preparing a salt solution with a concentration of 0.5-2 mol/l for subsequent use, where the salt solution is a salt solution containing ferric ions, cobalt ion, and nickel ion, and/or a salt solution containing ferric ions and manganese ion, (iii) adding the coal gangue powder and sodium hexametaphosphate dried into distilled water, stirring for 1-2 hours, then adding the salt solution to the mixture, continue to stirring for 2-3 hours at room temperature to obtain a slurry, where the mass ratio of coal gangue powder, sodium hexametaphosphate, distilled water, and salt solution is 2: 0.15: 3: 4-6, and (iv) spray drying the slurry through a spray dryer to obtain a gangue-based complex powder.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing complex microwave absorption material using coal gangue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts solid waste coal gangue as a raw material; realizes recycling of coal gangue; saves resources and costs; ensures in-situ spray-drying concrete loading process; and is simple and easy to implement and suitable for large-scale industrial production applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing complex microwave absorption material using coal gangue comprises (i) ball milling gangue, sieving through a 100-300 mesh standard sieve, drying the gangue powder screened out at 80-100 degrees C for 3-4 hours to obtain the gangue powder and storing, (ii) preparing a salt solution with a concentration of 0.5-2 mol/l for subsequent use, where the salt solution is a salt solution containing ferric ions, carbon dioxide, and nickel ion, and/or a salt solution containing ferric ions and manganese ion, (iii) adding the coal gangue powder and sodium hexametaphosphate dried into distilled water, stirring for 1-2 hours, then adding the salt solution to the mixture, continue to stirring for 2-3 hours at room temperature to obtain a slurry, where the mass ratio of coal gangue powder, sodium hexametaphosphate, distilled water, and salt solution is 2: 0.15: 3: 4-6, (iv) spray drying the slurry through a spray dryer to obtain a gangue-based complex powder, where the working parameters of spray dryer are: inlet temperature is 200 degrees C, outlet air temperature is 100 degrees C, feed speed is 60 ml/hour, and atomizer pressure is 10 MPa, (v) placing the gangue-based complex powder in a tube furnace, sintering at 400-900 degrees C in an inert gas atmosphere for 2-3 hours and then cooling to room temperature with the furnace to obtain complex microwave absorption material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The prepared complex microwave absorption material has magnetic properties, and the magnetic components are elemental iron, elemental cobalt, elemental nickle, iron(II, III) oxide, cobalt ferrite, nickel ferrite, nickel cobaltite, manganese iron oxide , iron-cobalt alloy, iron-nickel alloy, cobalt-nickel alloy, and/or ferro manganese alloy.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); G04 (Miscellaneous compositions - including luminescent and tenebrescent materials, de-icing/de-misting compositions, mastics, heat transfer compositions and aerosol-can filling mixtures (C09H).)
MC E31-K06; E31-P02D; E35-S; E35-U; E35-V; E35-W; J04-X; G04-B

PN IN201831026406-A
TI Chemical looping combustion-based steam generation system for producing e.g., power, has solid fuel reactor, syngas reactor, metal oxide regeneration reactor, cyclone separators, heat supply and recovery systems, and flue gas recycle system.
AU SEEPANA S
   RAJAVEL M P
   CHAKRABORTY A
   GURUCHANDRAN P S
AE BHARAT HEAVY ELECTRICALS LTD (BHAR-C)
GA 202014352V
AB    NOVELTY - A chemical looping combustion (CLC)-based steam generation system comprises solid fuel reactor (1) (SFR) for solid fuel (7) and metal oxide (18) reaction; syngas reactor (3) (GR) for reacting syngas (25) and metal oxide (43); metal oxide regeneration reactor (2) (MRR) for reaction of oxygen depleted metal oxide (19) with air (35); cyclone separators (11, 14, 27, 39, 45) to separate solid and gaseous phases; heat supply system to SFR and GR from MRR using dedicated compressible inert fluid (53, 99); heat recovery system in SFR and GR using steam (72, 75); flue gas recycle system for loop seals (30, 41, 47, 54) and SFR; syngas heating system (24) and air heating system (34) before sending to GR and MRR, respectively; carbonaceous solid fuel pulverizer (6) and pulverized fuel transportation system to SFR turbine-generator system for power production; and gas cleaning system flue gas desulfurization (FGD) (94) and cooling system (97) for carbon dioxide capture.
   USE - A chemical looping combustion (CLC)-based steam generation system for carbonaceous solid fuel to produce steam, power, and syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The advantage of CLC process is that the elimination of energy intensive air separation unit (ASU) to generate oxygen from air, which results in higher energy efficiency than with oxy-fuel combustion during CO2 capture and sequestration. Since the total amount of energy released per kilogram of fuel is the same, the heat integration makes CLC process more viable and efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for CLC-based steam generation scheme for gaseous fuels to produce steam and/or power, comprising gaseous fuel reactor for gaseous fuel and metal oxide reaction; metal oxide regeneration reactor for reaction of oxygen depleted metal oxide with air; cyclone separators to separate solid and gaseous phases; heat supply system from MRR to GR using a dedicated compressible inert fluid; heat recovery system in GR using steam; flue gas recycle system for loop seals and GR; gaseous fuel heating system and air heating system before sending to GR and MRR, respectively; turbine-generator system for power production; and CO2 capture system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the chemical looping combustion process for solid fuels for generation of steam based power when SFR and GR are endothermic in nature.
   Solid fuel reactor (1)
   Metal oxide regeneration reactor (2)
   Syngas reactor (3)
   Carbonaceous solid fuel pulverizer (6)
   Solid fuel (7)
   Cyclone separators (11, 14, 27, 39, 45)
   Metal oxide for reaction with solid fuel (18)
   Oxygen depleted metal oxide (19)
   Syngas heating system (24)
   Syngas (25)
   Loop seals (30, 41, 47, 54)
   Air heating system (34)
   Air (35)
   Metal oxide for reaction with syngas (43)
   Compressible inert fluid (53, 99)
   Steam (72, 75)
   Gas cleaning system flue gas desulfurization (94)
   Cooling system (97)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Processes: Syngas and fresh air is heated with flue gas and oxygen depleted air, respectively, before admitting into GR and MRR. Energy required for endothermic reaction between solid fuel and metal oxides in SFR and syngas and metal oxides in GR is supplied using dedicated compressible inert fluid from MRR. To regenerate metal oxides, a single MRR is used for reaction between air and oxygen depleted metal oxides. A portion of syngas generated from SFR can be utilized for liquid fuel manufacturing. Flue gas is used for loop seals, carbon stripper, and to fluidize pulverized solid fuel and metal oxides in GR. Superheated steam is used in SFR to enhance carbon conversion. CO2-rich flue gas can also be used for fluidization of solid fuel in SFR. Cooled CO2-rich flue gas can be sent to CO2 capture or utilization. Preferred Components: Metallic oxygen carriers used for solid fuel and syngas reaction can be nickel (Ni), iron (Fe), cobalt (Co), copper (Cu), calcium (Ca), manganese-based metal oxides, natural ores, or combination of these metals with support materials, such as alumina (Al2O3), magnesium oxide (MgO), magnesium aluminate (MgAl2O4), nickel aluminate (NiAl2O4), zirconia (ZrO2), ceria (CeO2), and yttria-stabilized zirconia (YSZ). Preferred Conditions: Operating temperature range for MRR is 750-1100 degrees C, and operating temperature range for SFR and GR is 700-1050 degrees C. Operating pressure of SFR, MRR, and GR can be 1-30 bar. In the case of exothermic nature of fuel reaction in GR and SFR, superheated steam is used to recover energy. If SFR is exothermic and GR is endothermic or vice-versa, steam and inert compressible fluid are used for recovery and supply energy or vice-versa, respectively. The scheme can be used from wide thermal output of 1-500 MWe.

PN CN109499249-A
TI New boiler flue gas treating method comprises e.g. passing static dust of boiler flue gas into electrostatic precipitator, removing boiler smoke dust and heavy metal using double-electrode generating electrostatic field to first channel.
AU WU C
   ZHANG E
AE JIANGSU ENLAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201928754H
AB    NOVELTY - New boiler flue gas treating method comprises e.g. (i) passing static dust of the boiler flue gas into electrostatic precipitator, removing the boiler smoke dust and heavy metal using double-electrode generating electrostatic field to first channel, (ii) passing the boiler flue gas into the washing tower, producing OH-rich high-alkali water through electrolyte electrolysis method, introducing the high alkaline water into the washing tower, the tower adopts continuous water film design, and uses high alkali water to increase the adsorption force, carry out the second dust and heavy metal removal, and removing the first NOx and sulfur dioxide, (iii) introducing the boiler flue gas into super-cavitation device, generating micro-nano bubble using micro-nano bubble generating device, performing second NOx and sulfur dioxide removal on the boiler flue gas using shearing and energy micro-nano bubble generating device, and removing the organic pollutant in the boiler flue gas.
   USE - Used as new boiler flue gas treating method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method purifies the boiler flue gas in industrial production, has no harmful byproduct and secondary pollution, and the product algae and potassium also can produce economic benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - New boiler flue gas treating method comprises (i) passing static dust of the boiler flue gas into electrostatic precipitator, removing the boiler smoke dust and heavy metal using double-electrode generating electrostatic field to first channel, (ii) passing the boiler flue gas obtained in the step (i) into the washing tower, producing OH-rich high-alkali water through electrolyte electrolysis method, introducing the high alkaline water into the washing tower, the tower adopts continuous water film design, and uses high alkali water to increase the adsorption force, carry out the second dust and heavy metal removal, and removing the first NOx and sulfur dioxide, (iii) introducing the boiler flue gas obtained in the step (ii) into super-cavitation device, generating micro-nano bubble using micro-nano bubble generating device, performing second NOx and sulfur dioxide removal on the boiler flue gas using shearing and energy micro-nano bubble generating device, and removing the organic pollutant in the boiler flue gas, (iv) pressurizing the boiler smoke processed by step (iii) into the algae barrel cultured by the microalgae, the microalgae cooperates with the light source in the algae barrel for photosynthesis to remove carbon dioxide from the boiler flue gas, (v) separating the mature microalgae from water using algae-water separator, and storing the microalgae in the cold state, and (vi) evaporating and desalting the waste water obtained in the step (v) using mechanical vapor recompressors (MVR) evaporator to remove potassium salt, where the potassium salt is used for producing fertilizer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the boiler flue gas treating method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the water used is high-alkali water. Preferred Method: In the method, the high alkali water is produced by an electrolyte electrolysis method and is mainly produced by an electrochemical reaction.

TI Preparing chimeric antigen receptor T cells            targeting CD19 useful for preparing anti-B cell            malignant medicine, comprises adding a restriction site            to both ends of the chimeric antigen receptor gene            sequence targeting CD19.
AU RU K
   CHEN S
   SONG G
   LIN Y
   WEI W
AE QINGDAO SINO-US DIAGNOSTICS MEDICAL DIAG (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201926513F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing chimeric antigen receptor T cells                targeting CD19 comprises adding a restriction site                to both ends of the chimeric antigen receptor gene                sequence targeting CD19, double digestion with                lentiviral expression vector Plv-EF1a, recovering                DNA fragments, ligating, transformation, picking                monoclonals, extracting plasmids, enzymatic                digestion and sequencing to obtain CD19-CAR                lentiviral vector Plv-EF1a-CD19ScFv; where the                chimeric antigen receptor gene targeting CD19 is a                single chain antibody CD19ScFv of CD19, a hinge                region and a transmembrane region of CD8,                intracellular signal domain CD28, intracellular                signal domain 4-1BB and intracellular signal                domains of CD3, constructed in series, extracting                the lentiviral expression plasmid                Plv-EF1a-CD19ScFv.
   USE - The chimeric antigen receptor T cells                targeting CD19 is useful for preparing anti-B cell                malignant medicine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The chimeric antigen receptor T cells                targeting CD19 improves the killing ability of CD19                positive cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing chimeric antigen receptor T cells                targeting CD19 comprises: (i) adding a restriction                site to both ends of the chimeric antigen receptor                gene sequence targeting CD19, double digestion with                lentiviral expression vector Plv-EF1a, recovering                DNA fragments, ligating, transformation, picking                monoclonals, extracting plasmids, enzymatic                digestion and sequencing to obtain CD19-CAR                lentiviral vector Plv-EF1a-CD19ScFv; where the                chimeric antigen receptor gene targeting CD19 is a                single chain antibody CD19ScFv of CD19, a hinge                region and a transmembrane region of CD8,                intracellular signal domain CD28, intracellular                signal domain 4-1BB and intracellular signal                domains of CD3, constructed in series; (ii)                extracting the lentiviral expression plasmid                Plv-EF1a-CD19ScFv or the lentiviral backbone                plasmid Plv-EF1a, where the helper plasmids psPAX2                and pMD2.G are mixed in a mass ratio of 6-8:5:3,                determining the concentration of the plasmid,                transfection system is prepared with LipoFilter                transfection reagent and serum-free medium,                transfection of HEK293T/17 cells, harvesting the                culture supernatant and centrifuging to obtain a                lentivirus concentrate, the serum-free medium                includes serum-free Dulbecco's modified eagle                medium, dextran sulfate, sodium pyruvate and                4-hydroxyethylpiperazineethanesulfonic acid; (iii)                isolation and culture of T cells, taking healthy                peripheral blood and separate lymphocytes,                resuspending with culture medium 1, inoculation in                CD3 and CD28 antibody-coated culture plates to                stimulate culture for 24-48 hours, where the                culture solution 1 is added with                polyhydroxyalkanoate and autologous plasma in                X-VIVO15 medium; and (iv) the stimulated cultured T                cells are inoculated into the RetroNectin coated                plate i.e. 2x 106 cells per well, adding the                lentiviral concentrate and polybrene, infection for                8-12 hours in carbon dioxide incubator at 37                degrees C and saturated humidity of 5%, replacing                fresh culture solution 2, cultivating for 7-10 days                to obtain chimeric antigen receptor T cells                targeting CD19, where the culture solution 2 is                prepared by adding autologous plasma to X-VIVO15                medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The chimeric antigen                  receptor gene targeting CD19 is ligated with                  BamH1 and Sal1 at both ends, and the lentiviral                  expression vector Plv-EF1a is digested with                  restriction enzymes BamH1 and Sal1. The                  lentiviral expression plasmid Plv-EF1a-CD19ScFv                  or the lentiviral backbone plasmid Plv-EF1a,                  where the mass ratio of helper plasmids psPAX2                  and pMD2.G is 8:5:3. Preferred Components: The                  serum-free medium includes serum-free Dulbecco's                  modified eagle medium, 15 mg/l dextran sulfate, 1                  mM sodium pyruvate and 15 mM                  4-hydroxyethylpiperazineethanesulfonic acid. The                  culture solution 1 comprises 1 ng/ml                  polyhydroxyalkanoate and 2 vol.% autologous                  plasma in X-VIVO15 medium. The culture solution 2                  comprises 2 vol.% autologous plasma in X-VIVO15                  medium. The culture plate is supplemented with                  500 U/ml recombinant human interleukin 2, 30                  ng/ml recombinant human interleukin 7 and 15-30                  ng/ml recombinant human interleukin.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.

PN CN109182766-A
TI Treating metallurgical waste tailing slag involves preparing electrolyte by adding dilute hydrochloric acid with water, stirring it to prepare hydrochloric acid solution, and mixing sodium chloride and hypochlorite to acid solution.
AU ZHANG S
   ZHANG R
AE ZHANG R (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2019092834
AB    NOVELTY - Treating metallurgical waste tailing slag involves preparing electrolyte by adding a certain amount of dilute hydrochloric acid with water, stirring it to prepare a hydrochloric acid solution with pH of 2-4, and then adding and mixing certain amount of sodium chloride and hypochlorite uniformly to hydrochloric acid solution to obtain electrolyte. The portion of electrolyte is mixed with metallurgical waste tailing slag in a solid liquid ratio of 1.5-2:1 to form a slurry. The slurry and electrolyte are conveyed to anode groove and cathode groove of electrolytic cell to keep liquid level of anode and cathode to the surface. The anode electrolytic cell having electrolyte is stirred at a certain speed, power supply is turned on, compressed air and wet resin are introduced into anode electrolytic cell, where current magnitude and agitation speed are controlled strictly to prevent formation of chlorine gas.
   USE - Method for treating metallurgical waste tailing slag used in electrolysis process and electrolysis equipment technology field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat metallurgical waste tailing slag, which reduces processing cost, and realizes continuous production of electrochlorination during extraction of gold material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The sodium chloride in slurry is electrolyzed in an acidic liquid to generate chlorine gas and sodium ions, generated chlorine gas chlorinates and precipitates gold in slurry under oxidation, and then adsorbed by a wet resin in slurry. The original sodium cyanide in chlorine gas and slurry reacts to form carbon dioxide and ammonia, and hydrochloric acid in slurry and alkali in original tailings are neutralized to form salt and water, where addition of hypochlorite increases potential of solution and facilitates electrochlorination of gold to obtain electrochlorinated mixture. The electrochlorinated mixture is sent to a filter for washing and pressure filtration to obtain filter cake having neutral pH value and is fed into a tailings pond or mixed with clay. The brick and filtered liquid is returned to electrolyte preparation box, and is added with certain amount of hydrochloric acid, hypochlorite and sodium chloride, and then resend into the electrolytic cell for electrolysis reaction. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for treating metallurgical waste tailing slag, which comprises an electrolytic cell tank, where electrolytic cell tank is arranged in step-shape manner, and interior of each of electrolytic cell tank is separated by two clapboard arranged at a distance, where clapboard is provided with a through hole, and through hole is covered with a filter scree, where two of anode plates are located between two clapboards and form an anode groove near the inside of the clapboard, and two cathode plates are located at outer side of clapboard and is adjacent to the clapboard to form two cathode grooves, where anode plate is provided with an electric stirrer, and a conveying tube group for transporting the electrolytic mixture is fixed between each of the two electrolytic cell tank.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The amount of sodium chloride added is 30-50Kg/T, and amount of hypochlorite added is 10Kg/T. The stirring speed is 3-4 revolutions per second, electrochlorination voltage is 5V, and current density of anode electrode plate is 750A/m2 and not more than 850A/m2. The filter cake contains 28-30% water, so that 30% of water is added for washing at time of pressure filtration.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PN CN108947705-A
TI Preparing coated sustained-release bio-efficient organic fertilizer comprises mixing e.g. rotten livestock manure, mushroom residue, cassava residue, and nitrogen fertilizer, adding de-ionized water, pelleting, freezing and drying.
AU WANG T
   HE Y
   ZHAO L
AE WANG T (WANG-Individual)
GA 2018A05339
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing coated sustained-release bio-efficient organic fertilizer comprises mixing rotten livestock manure, mushroom residue, cassava residue, oyster shell powder, plant ash, humic acid, bamboo charcoal powder, phosphate fertilizer, potassium fertilizer and nitrogen fertilizer, adding proper de-ionized water, stirring and pelleting, (2) adding polyvinyl alcohol into de-ionized water, heating, stirring, cooling, (3) mixing bark, thunder god vine, fructus quisqualis, chinese prickly ash, fructus cnidii selinum, radix stemonae stemona and fructus carpesii, crushing, freezing and drying, (4) adding the organic fertilizer granule in the rotating disk, firstly spraying the inner envelope paste on the organic fertilizer particle surface, hot air blow-drying, spraying the outer coating slurry on the inner envelope layer surface, hot air drying, forming outer coating layer to obtain the crude product, adding the crude product into drying box, drying at 50-55 degrees C for 18-25 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing coated sustained-release bio-efficient organic fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The fertilizer has excellent insecticidal and antibacterial, sustained-release property and provides rich nutritional substances for planting agricultural crops.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing coated sustained-release bio-efficient organic fertilizer comprises (1) mixing the rotten livestock manure, mushroom residue, cassava residue, oyster shell powder, plant ash, humic acid, bamboo charcoal powder, phosphate fertilizer, potassium fertilizer and nitrogen fertilizer, adding proper de-ionized water, stirring at 600-800 revolutions/minutes for 10-30 minutes, adding the mixed material into the double screw extruder, extruding the twin-screw and pelleting the material to obtain organic fertilizer particle, (2) adding polyvinyl alcohol into de-ionized water, heating the temperature to 80-90 degrees C, stirring at 600-800 revolutions/minutes for 1-1.5 hours , cooling down to 40-55 degrees C, stirring at the speed of 1200-1400 revolutions/minutes, adding excipient, finishing adding, continuing to stir for 1-3 minutes to obtain outer coating slurry, (3) mixing the bark, thunder god vine, fructus quisqualis, chinese prickly ash, fructus cnidii selinum, radix stemonae stemona and fructus carpesii, crushing by 80-mesh sieve, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, vacuum drying and concentrating the extracting liquid, freezing and drying at -50-55 degrees C at low temperature, grinding into powder, adding to ethanol aqueous solution, adding sodium carboxymethyl starch, mixing and stirring at 600-800 revolutions/minutes for 30-50 minutes to obtain the coating slurry and (4) adding the organic fertilizer granule in the rotating disk, firstly spraying the inner envelope paste on the organic fertilizer particle surface, hot air blow-drying to obtain coating layer, spraying the outer coating slurry on the inner envelope layer surface, hot air drying, forming outer coating layer to obtain the crude product, adding the crude product into drying box, drying at 50-55 degrees C for 18-25 hours to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the preparation method of rotten livestock manure is as follows: adding chicken manure, pig manure and cow dung according to mass ratio of 1-5:1-5:1-5 into proper water, stirring uniformly, composting, stack height 1.5-2 m, covering plastic film, composting for 10-15 days, during lifting the plastic film heat, maintaining an internal temperature less than 35 degrees C. In the step (1), the double extruding section extruding of screw bulking machine is 55-60 kg/m3 and temperature is 120-140 degrees C. In the step (2), the volume ratio of the polyvinyl alcohol and deionized water is 1:10-20. In the step (2), the excipient is methyl cellulose, carboxymethyl cellulose and hydroxypropyl methyl cellulose. In the step (3), the mass ratio of bark, thunder god vine, fructus quisqualis, chinese prickly ash, fructus cnidii selinum, radix stemonae stemona and fructus carpesii is 2-5:5-10:1-3:10-12:10-15:1-5:1-5. In the step (3), the parameters for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is as follows: the extracting pressure is 8-12MPa, the extracting temperature is 45-50 degrees C and the time is 1.5-2 hours. In the step (4), the inner envelope layer thickness is 0.5-1.5 mu m and the thickness of outer coating layer is 0.1-0.5 mu m.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PT P
PN CN108878931-A
TI High temperature polymer electrolyte membrane fuel            cell comprises front end plate, bipolar plate, membrane            electrode and rear end plate, where membrane electrode            are stacked as repeating unit and rear end plates are            constitute stack.
AU ZHANG W
   SU H
   XU Q
   MA Q
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2018958688
AB    NOVELTY - High temperature polymer electrolyte membrane                fuel cell comprises front end plate, the bipolar                plate, the membrane electrode and the rear end                plate. The bipolar plate and the membrane electrode                are stacked as a repeating unit and the front end                plate and the rear end plate are constitute a stack                of a high temperature polymer electrolyte membrane                fuel cell. The anode is used hydrogen as a fuel and                the cathode is used carbon dioxide as a fuel. The                membrane electrode includes a high temperature                polymer electrolyte membrane and a gas diffusion                electrode on both sides of the high temperature                polymer electrolyte membrane.
   USE - High temperature polymer electrolyte membrane                fuel cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The high temperature polymer electrolyte                membrane fuel cell is cost-effective, consumes less                energy and recycles low energy consumption, and has                economic and social significance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing                high temperature polymer electrolyte membrane fuel                cell, which involves:
   (A) preparing membrane electrode by using gas                diffusion back layer treatment and macroporous                carbon paper, and using cloth or carbon felt as a                gas diffusion back layer;
   (B) subjecting back layer to hydrophobic                treatment, and directly immersing gas diffusion                back layer in the hydrophobic polymer dispersion,                after a certain period of time, taking out, drying                and sintering at a high temperature to form a                network of hydrophobic polymer to obtain a gas                diffusion back layer;
   (C) preparing microporous layer by mixing the                carbon material powder and performing dispersion of                the microporous layer polymer binder together with                the relevant solvent to obtain a microporous layer                slurry;
   (D) preparing the microporous layer involves                depositing pore layer slurry onto the gas diffusion                back layer by spraying, knife coating and after                drying to form gas diffusion back layer with a                microporous layer;
   (E) preparing catalytic layer by using high                catalyst powder and catalytic layer and dispersion                of the molecular binder which is mixed with the                relevant solvent to obtain a catalyst slurry;
   (F) depositing prepared catalyst slurry by                spraying, scraping or other methods onto the                microporous layer of the gas diffusion back layer,                after drying, a gas diffusion back layer having a                microporous layer and a catalytic layer to obtain                gas diffusion electrode;
   (G) performing post treatment of the gas                diffusion electrode by preparing gas diffusion                electrode and protecting by an inert gas in a                muffle furnace of 340-430 degrees C for 20-60                minutes to obtain the final gas diffusion                electrode;
   (H) performing assembly of the membrane                electrode by using catalysis on two gas diffusion                electrodes by post-treatment, where layers are                closely adhered to the two faces of the acid doped                high temperature resistant film respectively;                and
   (I) mechanically assembling gas diffusion                electrode and the acid doped high temperature                resistant film to obtain a membrane electrode,                where one of the two gas diffusion electrodes is an                anode gas diffusion electrode, and other is a                cathode gas diffusion electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The high temperature                  polymer electrolyte membrane is an acid doped                  high temperature resistant membrane, and the acid                  is selected from the group consisting of                  phosphoric acid, sulfuric acid, methanesulfonic                  acid polyvinylphosphoric acid,                  trifluoroalkylsulfonic acid, or a mixture. The                  high temperature resistant film is                  polybenzimidazole and its derivative,                  polybenzimidazole-polyetheretherketone composite                  film, sulfonated polyetheretherketone, polyether                  ether, polyimide, a polyimide-polybenzimidazole,                  and a sulfonated polyetherether                  ketone-polybenzimidazole composite film.                  Preferred Compositions: The acid doping amount is                  300-450 wt.%. The hydrophobic polymer has a mass                  percentage of 10-50 wt.%. The carbon material                  powder is one of XC-72, KB-300, KB-600, and                  BP2000 activated carbon. The microporous layer                  polymer binder is polytetrafluoroethylene, iron                  phosphide or polyvinylidene fluoride. The                  microporous layer polymer binder has a mass                  percentage of 10-40 wt.% in the microporous                  layer. The mass percentage of the polymer binder                  of the catalytic layer in the catalytic layer is                  10-50 wt.%. The catalyst is a platinum-based                  catalyst or a transition metal-based catalyst or                  a non-metal catalyst having a mass percentage of                  platinum of 10-60 wt.%. Preferred Conditions: The                  high temperature polymer electrolyte membrane                  fuel cell operating temperature is 120-200                  degrees C.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); ; X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PT P
PN CN108530705-A
TI Preparing nitrile butadiene rubber by adding e.g. nitrile rubber and butyl rubber to furnace, then sequentially adding sodium stearate and paraffin, stirring, pressurizing, and calcining, and then mixing with modified treatment solution.
AU ZHANG X
AE SUZHOU NICETY PLASTIC CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201874829M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrile butadiene rubber involves: (1) mixing 2,2-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)butane, sodium chloride and triethyl acetyl citrate, fully stirring, putting into a conical flask, placing the conical flask in a drying reflux stirring reaction device for reaction, carrying out an argon protection reaction to obtain a modified treatment solution; (2) adding nitrile rubber, butyl rubber, polylactic acid fiber, linear phenolic resin and manganese dioxide to a high-temperature calcining furnace, then sequentially adding sodium stearate and paraffin, fully stirring, pressurizing, filling argon, and calcining; and (3) mixing the product obtained in step (2) and the modified treatment solution obtained in step (1), then adding triphenylmethylsulfenamide, glyceryl tristearate and potassium sorbate, and carrying out ultrasonic treatment to obtain a raw material suspension liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nitrile butadiene rubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the nitrile butadiene rubber with soft texture, high flexibility and excellent aging resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nitrile butadiene rubber involves: (1) mixing 2,2-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)butane, sodium chloride and triethyl acetyl citrate in a mass ratio of 1:3:10, fully stirring, putting into a conical flask, placing the conical flask in a drying reflux stirring reaction device for reaction, carrying out an argon protection reaction for 60 minutes to obtain a modified treatment solution, where anhydrous calcium chloride and concentrated sulfuric acid are selected as drying agent; (2) adding 10-20 pts. wt. nitrile rubber, 10-16 pts. wt. butyl rubber, 5-10 pts. wt. polylactic acid fiber, 4-10 pts. wt. linear phenolic resin and 2-3 pts. wt. manganese dioxide to a high-temperature calcining furnace, then sequentially adding 2-6 pts. wt. sodium stearate and 1-2 pts. wt. paraffin, fully stirring, pressurizing, filling argon, and calcining at a high-temperature of 620-625 degrees C for 150 minutes; (3) mixing the product obtained in step (2) and the modified treatment solution obtained in step (1), then adding 1-3 pts. wt. triphenylmethylsulfenamide, 1-4 pts. wt. glyceryl tristearate and 1-3 pts. wt. potassium sorbate, and carrying out ultrasonic treatment for 2-4 hours to obtain a raw material suspension liquid; (4) adding a mixture of an antifoaming agent and a stabilizer, 2-4 pts. wt. sulfur powder and 1-2 pts. wt. vulcanizing agent to the suspension liquid obtained in step (3), uniformly stirring, and leaving still for 10-18 hours; (5) injecting the mixed solution obtained in step (4) into a twin-screw extruder, extruding, pressing and shaping; (6) putting the pressure-molded portion obtained in step (5) into a flat vulcanizing machine for vulcanization to obtain a vulcanized composite material; and (7) putting the vulcanized composite material obtained in step (6) into a high-temperature water vapor for shaping and curing, then filling with a recycled carbon dioxide gas, reacting for 30-60 minutes, taking out, naturally cooling and airing and packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixture of antifoaming agent and stabilizer in step (4) is chosen from emulsified silicone oil, polydimethylsiloxane, high alcohol fatty acid ester, (2-bromoethyl)trimethylsilane and glycerol in a molar mass ratio of 2:3:1:2:5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Condition: The reflux temperature in step (1) is 98-102 degrees C. In step (2), the pressure in the furnace is 4-8 MPa. Preferred Components: The vulcanizing agent in step (4) is chosen from p-tert-butyl phenolic resin, p-tert-octyl phenolic resin, hexamethylenediamine carbamate and/or ethylenediamine formate.
DC A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.); A12 (Polymers of di-and higher olefins; acetylenics; nitroso compounds.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A28 (Condensation polymers in general.)

PT P
PN US2018220283-A1; US10966070-B2
TI Method for managing communication of data packets            by node of network of moving things using heterogeneous            communication links, involves transmitting next packet            for transmission by node to next node in path towards            destination node.
AU CONDEIXA T S
   LOPES D M A
   MAGALHAES D M R J
AE VENIAM INC (VENI-Non-standard)
GA 2018602503
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving (1002) a packet                of a flow of packets from a source node to a                destination node, from multiple communication                interfaces of the node. The received packet is                classified (1004) with regard to multiple packet                characteristics and with regard to requirements                defined for the flow of packets. The received                packet is stored in a first buffer or in a second                buffer according to the order of the received                packet in the flow of packets. A next packet is                scheduled for transmission by the node towards the                destination node from the second buffer based upon                the classifying. The communication interfaces are                selected from the communication interfaces                according to respective context information for                communication links formed using the communication                interfaces. The next packet is transmitted for                transmission by the node to a next node in a path                towards the destination node. The selected                communication interfaces are used.
   USE - Method for managing communication of data                packets by node of network of moving things using                heterogeneous communication links and for                supporting network of mobile nodes.
   ADVANTAGE - The platform is flexibly optimized for                different purposes such as to reduce the latency,                increase throughput, reduce power consumption, load                balance, increase reliability, make more robust                with regard to failures or other disturbances. The                communication network allows a port operator to                improve the coordination of the ship loading                processes and increase the throughput of the harbor                by gathering real-time information on the position,                speed, fuel consumption and carbon-dioxide                emissions of the vehicles. The redundant elements                and/or functionality are provided with                load-balancing of control and/or data communication                functionality, as well as to decrease failure                probability. The network customizes multi-path                selection to fit traffic requirements into                characteristics and conditions of the links and                nodes/devices of the network, which allows the                network to increase the overall quality of                experience (QoE) for all traffic flows since they                are treated according to their needs. The network                provides increased resource optimization, provides                fallback mechanisms, and to increase QoE of the                end-user/device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a non-transitory computer-readable medium                storing program for managing communication of data                packets by node of network of moving things using                heterogeneous communication links; and
   (2) a system for managing communication of                data packets by node of network of moving things                using heterogeneous communication links.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the method for managing communication of data                packets by node of network of moving things using                heterogeneous communication links.
   Step for receiving packet of flow of packets                from source node to destination node (1002)
   Step for classifying received packet with                regard to multiple packet characteristics and with                regard to requirements (1004)
   Step for discarding received packets                (1008)
   Step for storing received packet in buffer of                received packets to be transmitted (1012)
   Step for storing received packet in buffer of                received packets to be reordered (1014)
DC T01 (Digital Computers); W01 (Telephone and Data Transmission Systems)

PN CN107841417-A
TI Preparing blueberry fruity wine involves washing            blueberry fruit, crushing blueberry fruit and mixing            with sulfur dioxide to obtain blueberry fruit liquid,            and adding gelatin to remove astringent, and adding            citric acid to protect color.
AU CHEN C
   WANG J
   YANG C
   DONG S
   JI P
   XU H
   LI M
   TONG W
   ZENG X
   MA S
   WANG Y
   YU Q
   LIU J
   XIAO Q
   YAO Y
   MA H
   HE H
   LIANG Q
   LIAO T
   DONG X
   CHEN D
   ZHANG W
   LI H
   LI Y
   CHEN Z
   WEN Z
   REN X
   CHEN K
   ZHANG J
   HWANG S
   LIU Q
   QU J
   GENGADENGZHEN` C
AE SICHUAN ACAD AGRIC SCI IND CORP RES INST (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201827217J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing blueberry fruity wine involves                washing the blueberry fruit, crushing the blueberry                fruit and mixing with sulfur dioxide to obtain                blueberry fruit liquid, and adding gelatin to                remove the astringent. The citric acid is added to                blueberry fruit liquid and protect the color, and                then mixed with pectinase and chitosan, stirred                uniformly, standing and then filtering to obtain                blueberry juice. The blueberry juice is chilled at                0-5 degrees C and blueberry fruit having 8.5-9.5g/L                acidity content and 6.5-8 wt.% sugar and sulfur                dioxide is added, crushed, and mixed with                pectinase.
   USE - Method for preparing blueberry fruity                wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare blueberry fruity                wine, which has sour and better taste, anthocyanin                content more than 200mg/L, which greatly retains                nutrient content, total acid content less than                6.8g/L and shelf life of 6 months.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing blueberry fruity wine involves                washing the blueberry fruit, crushing the blueberry                fruit and mixing with sulfur dioxide to obtain                blueberry fruit liquid, and adding gelatin to                remove the astringent. The citric acid is added to                blueberry fruit liquid and protect the color, and                then mixed with pectinase and chitosan, stirred                uniformly, standing and then filtering to obtain                blueberry juice. The blueberry juice is chilled at                0-5 degrees C and blueberry fruit having 8.5-9.5g/L                acidity content and 6.5-8 wt.% sugar and sulfur                dioxide is added, crushed, and mixed with                pectinase, and then subjected to cold immersion                treatment to obtain blueberry turbid juice. The                clarifier in the blueberry turbid juice for                clarification and separating the blueberry juice                mixed with sucrose to the blueberry clear juice,                the initial fermentation sugar content is 18degrees                Bx, and calcium carbonate is added to adjust the pH                to 3.5. The non Saccharomyces cerevisiae is                inoculate to produced blueberry juice and subjected                into aroma fermentation by addition of alcohol                fermentation, and monitoring the proportion of                blueberry meal during alcoholic fermentation,                separating the feet when the specific gravity drops                to 0.993. The inoculation of blueberry wine at the                foot of the wine is inoculated with alcoholic                coccus and lactic acid bacteria for malic                acid-lactic acid fermentation. The malic                acid-lactic acid fermented blueberry wine is sealed                at 10-15 degrees C for 3-6 months, and the free                sulfur dioxide concentration is controlled to 20-30                mg/L. The clarifying agent is added to the                blueberry wine sealed to obtain a blueberry base                wine, and then blending the blueberry juice to                achieve an alcohol level 4-6 vol.% fruity blueberry                wine. The blueberry wine is cold-stabilized and                filtered and is filtered and sterilized by use of                0.45-0.20 mu m membranes in sequence. The blueberry                dew, and food-grade carbon dioxide is charged to a                pressure of 0.3-0.5 MPa and filled to obtain the                desired product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The control conditions                  of the astringent treatment is carried out at                  contains amount of gelatin added is 1.1-1.3 g/L                  blueberry fruit liquid, the astringent                  temperature is 13-17 degrees C, and the                  oscillating for 15-25 minutes. The amount of                  citric acid added is 0.6 wt.% blueberry fruit                  juice. The amount of pectinase and chitosan is                  20-50 mg/L blueberry fruit liquid and 0.09 g/L                  blueberry fruit liquid, and the mixing at 5-8                  degrees C. The control condition of the cold                  immersion treatment is carried out by adding                  amount of 28-32 mg/L pectinase into blueberry                  fruit liquid at 5-8 degrees C for 12-96 hours.                  The conditions of aroma fermentation are the                  quantity of non Saccharomyces cerevisiae produced                  is not less than 106 colony-forming units                  (CFU)/mL blueberry juice, and fermentation at                  10-12 degrees C for 24-72 hours. The control                  conditions for alcohol fermentation is carried                  out by number of Saccharomyces cerevisiae is not                  less than 106 colony-forming units (CFU)/mL of                  blueberry juice produced by the aroma                  fermentation. The fermentation is carried out at                  20-26 degrees C for 10-13 days, and separated                  wine is controlled at 5-10 degrees C. The malic                  acid-lactic acid fermentation is controlled under                  by adding a amount of alcoholic yeast and lactic                  acid bacteria are 20-40 mg/L blueberry wine, the                  fermentation is carried out at 0-25 degrees C for                  12-15 days. The cold immersion treatment is                  carried out at -5 degrees C to -4 degrees C for                  5-15 days.
