PN EP3272743-A1; WO2018015093-A1; CA3028514-A1; AU2017299043-A1; IN201947006241-A; CN109415328-A; US2019152928-A1; BR112019000856-A2; EP3487841-A1; EP3487841-B1; RU2019104114-A; IN361439-B; AU2017299043-B2; US11059793-B2; RU2754221-C2; US2021332018-A1; US2021332017-A1; CN109415328-B; MY185631-A
TI Purification of melamine melt involves quenching            melamine melt, decomposing, stripping, crystallizing,            separating from mother liquor, treating mother            liquor(s) to obtain aqueous solution, decomposing            solution, and recycling.
AU GAMBA S
   SCOTTO A
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE CO LTD (CASA-Non-standard)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 2018064159
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of melamine melt (5) involves                quenching melamine melt, decomposing a                by-product(s) with alkali to obtain an alkaline                aqueous solution (26), stripping the solution to                obtain stripped melamine solution (28),                crystallizing with a alkali-containing aqueous                solution (6b) and separating melamine (7) from a                mother liquor (8), treating the liquor(s) to obtain                an aqueous solution (11), decomposing the solution                to obtain a alkali-containing aqueous solution (30)                and a water stream (13), and recycling the                alkali-containing solution(s)(30) to at least one                process.
   USE - Purification of melamine melt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently purifies melamine salt                and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purification of melamine melt involves                quenching melamine melt, decomposing at least a                portion of a by-product with alkali to obtain an                alkaline aqueous solution of melamine, stripping                the alkaline aqueous solution to obtain stripped                melamine solution, crystallizing melamine from the                stripped melamine solution with a alkali-containing                aqueous solution and separating melamine from a                mother liquor, treating at least a portion of the                mother liquor to obtain an aqueous solution                comprising carbonates, decomposing the aqueous                solution into carbon dioxide and alkali to obtain                an alkali-containing aqueous solution and a water                stream, and recycling at least a portion of the                alkali-containing aqueous solution to at least one                process of quenching, decomposing and                crystallizing. The melamine salt comprises melamine                and by-products obtained from a high pressure                non-catalytic synthesis. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are                included for the following:
   (1) melamine purification section (1) of a                high pressure melamine plant, which consists of a                quencher (21) for receiving the melamine melt, a                decomposer (22) in which at least a portion of the                by-products are decomposed by the alkali, a                stripper (23) fed with the alkaline aqueous                solution (6a) of melamine and with the stripping                medium (29) provides the stripped melamine                solution, a crystallizer (24) fed with the stripped                melamine solution and the alkali-containing aqueous                solution (6b), a treatment unit (3) for at least a                portion of the mother liquid provides the aqueous                solution, a decomposition unit (4) of at least a                portion of the carbonates provides the                alkali-containing aqueous solution (30) and the                water stream, at least one line for recycling at                least a portion of the alkali-containing aqueous                solution (30) provided by the decomposition unit to                at least one of the quencher, the decomposer and                the crystallizer; and
   (2) method of revamping, which involves using                the melamine purification section.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The decomposition process                  is performed by an electrolysis process of the                  aqueous solution. The aqueous solution is                  subjected to an ultrafiltration process before                  the electrolysis process. The alkali-containing                  aqueous solution is at least partially subjected                  to a concentration process before recycling. The                  alkali-containing aqueous solution (6b) is                  entirely formed by at least a portion of the                  alkali-containing aqueous solution (30). The                  quenching process is carried out in the presence                  of alkali and a alkali-containing aqueous                  solution (6a) is fed as a quenching medium. The                  portion of the mother liquor and at least a                  portion of the alkali-containing aqueous solution                  (30) are mixed to form the alkali-containing                  aqueous solution (6a) or their portion. The water                  stream is at least partially recycled back to a                  melamine plant. The plant comprises the quencher                  of the melamine melt operating with water or with                  the aqueous solution of ammonia. Preferred                  Components: The alkali-containing aqueous                  solution comprises the portion of the mother                  liquor provided from the crystallization process.                  The alkali-containing solutions are sodium or                  potassium hydroxide solutions. The decomposition                  unit is an electrolytic cell. The purification                  section comprises one or more lines for recycling                  at least a portion of the water stream to the                  melamine plant.

PN CN210584225-U; CN111632456-A
TI bunker coal plant resource-cleaning discharge system, has ammonia synthesis module connected with air separation module and dehydrogenation module, and flue gas washing module connected with gas recycling module and water treatment module.
AU DI J
   ZANG Y
   GUO X
   SONG Y
AE BSS BEIJING ENERGY SAVING & ENVIRONMENTA (BSSB-Non-standard)
GA 202047321T
AB    (CN210584225-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a bunker coal plant recycling cleaning discharge system, using air separation process to obtain the O2, N2 and Ar, using O2 as the oxidant to coal gasification producing H2, the synthesis of NH3 H2 and N2. SO2 and CO2 for purifying smoke by flue gas treatment process, and recycling carbon dioxide in smoke, obtained NH3, SO2, and CO2 can be further converted into fertilizer product, using steam boiler to generate steam and generate electricity, using water processing process solves the recycling of water. the waste residue sludge generated by smoke, water treatment and other procedures to generate the waste catalyst containing harmful substance in the slag processing module processing to manufacture the vitreous advanced environment-friendly building material, and recycling heavy metal, all the raw materials in the utility model only coal and air, almost no waste discharge, make the treatment effect of energy-saving and environment-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A resource cleaning and discharge system comprises air separation module to filter dust and impurities in the air and separate oxygen, nitrogen and argon, gasification module connected to air separation module, dehydrogenation module connected to gasification module, steam power generation module connected to air separation module and gasification module, ammonia synthesis module connected to air separation module and dehydrogenation module and flue gas recycling module for preliminary treatment of the flue gas after the fuel of the steam power generation module is burned, removing dust and heavy metals in the flue gas, and recycling the water in accordance with discharge standard. The dehydrogenation module is used to separate and purify syngas to obtain hydrogen. The steam power generation module is used to add coal slag, coal, syngas and separated hydrogen gas to oxygen gas separated by the air separation module or add refluxed flue gas to oxygen to produce ultra-high critical or high, medium and low pressure steam which drives a steam generator to generate electricity. The ammonia synthesis module is used to generate ammonia from the purified hydrogen and separated nitrogen under high temperature and pressure and action of catalyst. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for resource cleaning and discharging method of coal-fired power plants using the system, which involves separating air through an air separation module to obtain oxygen, nitrogen and argon gas, using separated oxygen, coal and water to undergo gasification reaction under high temperature conditions of gasification module to obtain synthesis gas and coal slag, separating and purifying synthesis gas by dehydrogenation module to obtain pure hydrogen and other gases, passing other gases to steam boiler for combustion, synthesizing ammonia from nitrogen by separating purified hydrogen from air in ammonia synthesis module, heating coal, coal slag and syngas in steam boiler, heating the water to generate steam to drive a steam turbine to generate electricity, using flue gas recycling module to scrub the flue gas discharged from the steam boiler to remove dust and recover the water and heat in the flue gas, recovering sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide in flue gas after cooling through the gas recovery module, processing sewage generated by flue gas washing, steam boiler drainage and other process wastewater into system circulating water or recycled water that meets the discharge standard through water treatment module and using solid slag processing module for processing coal ash, waste slag and sludge and recovered heavy metals into glass which can be used as building material.
EA    NOVELTY - A resource cleaning and discharge system comprises air separation module to filter dust and impurities in the air and separate oxygen, nitrogen and argon, gasification module connected to air separation module, dehydrogenation module connected to gasification module, steam power generation module connected to air separation module and gasification module, ammonia synthesis module connected to air separation module and dehydrogenation module and flue gas recycling module for preliminary treatment of flue gas after the fuel of the steam power generation module is burned, removing dust and heavy metals in the flue gas, and recycling water in accordance with discharge standard. The steam power generation module is used to add coal slag, coal, syngas and separated hydrogen gas to oxygen gas separated by the air separation module or add refluxed flue gas to oxygen to produce ultra-high critical or high, medium and low pressure steam which drives a steam generator to generate electricity.
   USE - Resource cleaning and discharge system for treating coal-fired power plants (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system can effectively separate and discharge all raw materials in coal and air in form of nitrogen, argon, hydrogen, ammonia, sulfur dioxide, carbon dioxide, ammonium bicarbonate, ammonium sulfate, heavy metals and building material with no discharge waste, thereby achieving energy-saving and environmentally-friendly treatment effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The sewage processing process is carried out by cutting or crushing the sludge, coal ash and sewage into particles of 1-5 mm and subjecting to plasma furnace. Preferred Process: The ammonia synthesis involves by feeding nitrogen and hydrogen gas in ratio of 1:2.8-2.9 into ammonia reaction tower, reacting at 450-550 degrees C under pressure of 30-35 MPa to obtain ammonia, cooling ammonia to 20-35 degrees C by refrigerator, discharging from outlet of the reaction tower and storing under pressure condition of 1.6-1.8 MPa.

   INVISTA TEXTILES UK LTD (INVT-C)
GA 201983271R
AB    NOVELTY - Method (M1) for recycling an off-gas stream in a gas fermentation biosynthetic system, involves providing a culture of a chemoautotrophic organism capable of synthesizing one or more biosynthetic products to a gas fermentation reactor system, introducing a supply gas to the culture, where the supply gas comprises a recycle gas, producing an off-gas with the culture, forming the recycle gas from at least a portion of the off-gas, measuring the concentrations of each of the one or more target components in the recycle gas, calculating an amount of each of the one or more target components to be combined with the recycle gas to form the supply gas having concentrations of the one or more target components within the target concentration ranges, and introducing the recycle gas and the calculated amounts of each of the one or more target components to the culture.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling an off-gas stream in a gas fermentation biosynthetic system, or preparing one or more biosynthetic products. The reactor system is useful for performing aerobic gas fermentation process. The biosynthetic products are extracellular products chosen from organic acids, alcohols, olefins, fatty acids, amino acids, alkanes, and amines. The biosynthetic products are poly(hydroxyalkanoate), poly(3-hydroxybutyrate) or single-cell protein (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the gas utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method (M1) for recycling an off-gas stream in a gas fermentation biosynthetic system, involves providing a culture of a chemoautotrophic organism capable of synthesizing one or more biosynthetic products to a gas fermentation reactor system, introducing a supply gas to the culture, where the supply gas comprises a recycle gas, where the concentration of nitrogen in the supply gas is less than 5% (volume/volume), where the concentrations of one or more target components in the supply gas are each within a target concentration range, and the target components are chosen from hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and oxygen, producing an off-gas with the culture, forming the recycle gas from at least a portion of the off-gas, measuring the concentrations of each of the one or more target components in the recycle gas, calculating an amount of each of the one or more target components to be combined with the recycle gas to form the supply gas having concentrations of the one or more target components within the target concentration ranges, and introducing the recycle gas and the calculated amounts of each of the one or more target components to the culture. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a reactor system comprising a gas fermentation reactor comprising at least two oxygen addition points, a culture of a chemoautotrophic organism capable of synthesis of one or more biosynthetic products, a supply gas comprising a recycle gas, where the concentration of nitrogen in the supply gas is less than 5% (volume/volume), where the concentrations of one or more target components in the supply gas are each within a target concentration range, and the one or more target components are chosen from hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and oxygen, a first control loop configured to measure and control dissolved oxygen content in a fermentation liquid in the gas fermentation reactor, a second control loop configured to measure and control gaseous oxygen concentration in a headspace of the gas fermentation reactor, a recycle loop configured to recycle at least a portion of an off-gas stream to the culture, analytical equipment to measure the concentrations of each of the one or more target components in the recycle gas, and a third control loop configured to calculate an amount of each of the one or more target components to be combined with the recycle gas to form the supply gas having concentrations of the one or more target components within the target concentration ranges, where the third control loop is further configured to introduce the recycle gas and the calculated amounts of each of the one or more target components to the culture; and
   (2) a method (M2) for preparing one or more biosynthetic products, involves providing a culture of a chemoautotrophic organism capable of synthesizing one or more biosynthetic products to a gas fermentation reactor system, introducing a supply gas to the culture, where the supply gas comprises a recycle gas, where the concentration of nitrogen in the supply gas is less than 5% (volume/volume), where the concentrations of one or more target components in the supply gas are each within a target concentration range, and the one or more target components are chosen from hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and oxygen, producing an off-gas and the one or more biosynthetic products with the culture, forming the recycle gas from at least a portion of the off-gas, measuring the concentrations of each of the one or more target components in the recycle gas, calculating an amount of each of the one or more target components to be combined with the recycle gas to form the supply gas having concentrations of the one or more target components within the target concentration ranges, and introducing the recycle gas and the calculated amounts of each of the one or more target components to the culture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The flow rates of the one or more target components in the supply gas are each within a target flow rate range, and the method (M1) further involves measuring the flow rates of each of the one or more target components in the recycle gas, and the step of calculating is performed by calculating an amount of each of the one or more target components to be combined with the recycle gas to form the supply gas having concentrations of the one or more target components within the target concentration ranges and having flow rates of the one or more target components within the target flow rate ranges, where the one or more target components comprise hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and oxygen. The step of forming recycle gas does not involve separating the one or more target components from the off-gas, where the one or more target components comprise oxygen, and the target concentration of oxygen is 2-20% (volume/volume), the one or more target components comprise hydrogen, and the target concentration of hydrogen is 10-95% (volume/volume), and the one or more target components comprise carbon dioxide, and the target concentration of carbon dioxide is 1-50% (volume/volume). The method (M1) further involves maintaining the gaseous oxygen concentration in a headspace of the gas fermentation reactor below the LOC by controlling the flow rate and composition of the supply gas, where the gaseous oxygen concentration in the headspace is maintained below 75% of the LOC. The method (M1) further involves collecting at least a portion of the one or more biosynthetic products. Preferred Components: The concentration of nitrogen in the supply gas is less than 1% (volume/volume), where the concentration of nitrogen in the supply gas is less than 0.1% (volume/volume). The supply gas further comprises one or more supplemental gas streams, where the supplemental gas streams comprise the calculated amounts of the one or more target components, and the supply gas has a flow rate of 0.5-5 standard liter/minute/(liter of culture). The supplemental gas streams comprise oxygen, where the one or more supplemental gas streams comprise two or more supplemental gas streams, where the concentrations and flow rates of the one or more target components in at least one of the two or more supplemental gas streams is different from the concentrations and flow rates of the one or more target components in each other of the two or more supplemental gas streams. The supplemental gas streams are combined with the recycle gas before introduction to the culture. The flow rate and composition of the recycle gas are each different from the flow rate and composition of each of the one or more supplemental gas streams. The flow rate and composition of the recycle gas are each within 10% flow rate and composition of at least one of the one or more supplemental gas streams. The recycle gas and each of the one or more supplemental gas streams is non-flammable before introduction to the culture. The oxygen concentration in the recycle gas and the one or more supplemental gas streams is less than the limiting oxygen concentration (LOC), or the hydrogen concentration in the recycle gas and the one or more supplemental gas streams is below the lower flammability limit (LFL). The gaseous oxygen concentration in the supply gas is higher than the LOC. The supply gas is introduced into the culture as at least two supply gas streams, where at least one of the supply gas streams comprises gaseous oxygen and either no hydrogen or hydrogen at a concentration below the LFL, and at least one other of the supply gas streams comprises hydrogen and either no oxygen or oxygen at a concentration below the LOC. The concentration of the culture is 5-100 g/l. Preferred System: The gas fermentation reactor system is chosen from a single fermenter, multiple fermenters in series, a membrane fermenter, a fixed-bed fermenter, a fluidized-bed fermenter, a single autoclave, multiple autoclaves in series, a plug flow fermenter, a pneumatically agitated fermenter, a gas-lift fermenter with an external loop having forced-circulation, a bubble-column fermenter, a fixed (packed) bed column fermenter, a horizontal single fermenter with multiple compartments, and multistage column fermenters. The gas fermentation reactor system is a chemostat. The gas fermentation reactor system is not actively stirred. The gas fermentation reactor system has a gauge pressure of 1-10 bar. The recycle gas is formed from more than 50% (volume/volume) of the off-gas. The portion of the off-gas not used to form the recycle gas is purged from the gas fermentation reactor, where more than 50% or 90% hydrogen in the supply gas is converted by the culture. Preferred Microorganisms: The chemoautotrophic organism is chosen from non-pathogenic members of the genera Ralstonia, Wausteria, Cupriavidus, Alcaligenes, Burkholderia or Pandoraea. The chemoautotrophic organism is C.necator or C.metallidurans. The chemoautotrophic organism is a genetically modified organism.

PN CN110226451-A
TI Method for preparing edible mushroom factory            formula, involves mixing raw materials into culture            material, culture material is subjecting to feeding,            dry mixing and wet mixing to prepare medium.
AU YU Y
   FENG Z
   LI H
   HUANG L
   LIU Y
AE JIANGSU CHINA GREEN BIOTECH CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019805877
AB    NOVELTY - An edible mushroom factory formula preparing                method involves mixing the raw materials into a                culture material, the culture material is                subjecting to feeding, dry mixing and wet mixing to                prepare the medium. The prepared medium is filled                into the cultivation bottle and the hole is punched                in the middle and the cover is tightly covered. The                cultivation basket containing the cultivation                bottle is placed on the turnover car and pushed                into a vacuum steam sterilizer for sterilization.                The culture bottle is pushed into the cooling                chamber and the inoculum is inoculated, the liquid                mushroom of the Flammulina velutipes is inserted                into the cultivation bottle. The culture bottle is                transferred to the culture chamber for mycelial                culture and protected from light and better air                circulation that the hyphae are overgrown with the                culture bottle. The cultivation bottle is                transferred to the breeding room and the bud                treatment is carried out and harvested.
   USE - Method for preparing edible mushroom factory                formula.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables solving the problem that                the price of raw materials for supplying nitrogen                sources is rising and the supply shortage is                beginning to appear in the market with the rapid                development of the edible fungus factory by adding                bean straw as a part of raw materials and improving                the overall cost of industrialized cultivation of                edible fungi.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An edible mushroom factory formula preparing                method involves mixing 4-6 pts. wt. bean straw,                32-36 pts. wt. maize cob, 25-30 pts. wt. rice bran,                10-14 pts. wt. bran, 4-6 pts. wt. beer grains, 5-8                pts. wt. soybean hulls, 1-3 pts. wt. shell powder,                1-3 pts. wt. light calcium carbonate, 6-8 pts. wt.                cottonseed hulls and 5-7 pts. wt. beet pulp into a                culture material according to a prescribed ratio,                the culture material is subjecting to feeding, dry                mixing and wet mixing, and controlling the moisture                content of the test culture material at 67-69% and                the pH is 6.2-6.8 to prepare the medium. The                prepared medium is filled into the cultivation                bottle by a fully automatic bottling machine that                the cultivation bottle capacity is 1200ml and the                loading amount of each bottle is 960-1050g of one                basket per 16 cultivation bottles. The hole is                punched in the middle and the periphery of the                material through the automatic punching machine to                form a total of 5 holes and the hole extended to                the bottom of the bottle that the material surface                is required to be flat, the cover is tightly                covered by the automatic cover machine after the                hole is punched. The cultivation basket containing                the cultivation bottle is placed on the turnover                car and pushed into a vacuum steam sterilizer for                sterilization, where the sterilization temperature                is 121 degrees C and the sterilization time is 90                minutes. The culture bottle is pushed into the                cooling chamber for strong refrigeration after the                sterilization is finished and the inoculum is                inoculated when the temperature of the material is                lowered to 15-23 degrees C, the liquid mushroom of                the Flammulina velutipes is inserted into the                cultivation bottle under the laminar flow cover by                the automatic inoculation machine with the inoculum                size of the bottle is 25-35ml. The culture bottle                is transferred to the culture chamber for mycelial                culture at 13-15 degrees C with an air humidity of                75-85% and protected from light and better air                circulation that the hyphae is overgrown with the                culture bottle after 22-24 days of culture. The                cultivation bottle is filled with mycelium is                transferred into the sputum fungus workshop and the                sputum sterilization treatment is carried out by an                automatic sterilizing machine to remove the aged                hyphae, which formed mechanical stimulation to                promote the formation and differentiation of the                primordium of the velutipes fruit body. 10-15ml                water is sprayed on the surface to keep the surface                moist after the bacteria are finished. The                cultivation bottle is transferred to the breeding                room and the bud treatment is carried out under the                condition of room temperature at 15-17 degrees C,                air humidity of 85-95%, and carbon dioxide                concentration of 3000-4000 ppm. The surface of the                material is covered with mushroom buds after 4-6                days and the mushroom buds are photo-suppressed and                wind-suppressed, and the carbon dioxide                concentration is controlled at 3000-8000ppm to make                the mushroom buds grow neatly that the final                temperature is maintained at 4-6 degrees C for                10-12 days, where the small mushroom buds are grown                to the cultivation bottle mouth. The plastic bag                mushroom is wrapped around the bottle mouth to                increase the carbon dioxide concentration in the                film tube to 10000ppm or more when the mushroom bud                grows out of the culture bottle mouth 1-2cm, which                promotes the elongation of the mushroom mushroom                stipe and appropriately illuminates according to                the uniformity of the mushroom and harvested when                the Flammulina velutipes grows to 16-17cm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The culture bottle is                  a polypropylene plastic culture bottle.

TI Detecting effect of heat-not-burn tobacco product total particulate matter on superoxide dismutase activity in vitro by pre-treating sample, lysing nasal epithelial cell, adding sample and nitroblue tetrazolium and measuring absorbance.
AU GUAN Y
   LI X
   TANG J
   ZHU D
   ZHANG X
   HAN J
   HE D
   ZHANG W
   GAO Q
   XU Y
AE CHINA TOBACCO YUNNAN IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 201924745Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for detecting effect of heat-not-burn tobacco product total particulate matter on superoxide dismutase activity in vitro involves (a) pre-treating the sample using a linear smoking machine, (b) preparing single cell suspension of human nasal epithelial cells using Roswell Park Memorial Institute (RPMI) 1640 medium, (c) calculating the cell concentration, (d) inoculating cell suspension into a cell culture plate, (e) diluting the pretreated sample with phosphate buffered saline (PBS), adding test solution to the cell culture plate and incubating, (f) adding trypsin solution, suspending the cells in each well with RPMI1640 cell culture medium and centrifuging, (g) adding cell lysate, centrifuging the mixture and collecting the supernatant, (h) adding supernatant to the test sample, adding nitroblue tetrazolium, incubating and measuring the absorbance and calculating the superoxide dismutase enzyme activity.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting effect of heat-not-burn tobacco product total particulate matter on micronucleus in vitro.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for detecting effect of heat-not-burn tobacco product total particulate matter on micronucleus in vitro involves (a) pretreating sample by continuously performing suction of 10 ports using a linear smoking machine with a suction capacity of 55.0 ml for 3 seconds and a suction frequency of 30 seconds and weighing flue gas trap before and after pumping with a Cambridge filter with a diameter of 44 mm, calculating the mass mTPM of the total particulate matter of the non-burning cigarette in accordance with formula (I): mTPM=m1-m0, where m0 is the mass of the flue gas trap before pumping, in mg and m1 is the mass of the flue gas trap after pumping in mg, placing the captured Cambridge filter in a 50 ml flask, adding dimethyl sulfoxide to make the concentration of the total non-combusted cigarette phase in dimethyl sulfoxide 40 mg/ml, placing the flask on the ultrasound system for 30 minutes, filtering through a sterile filter paper to obtain dimethyl sulfoxide extract, filling into a 1 ml cryotube and storing at -80 degrees C, (b) resuscitating and inoculating the human nasal epithelial cell single cell suspension into a 25 cm2 cell culture flask containing 10 ml Roswell Park Memorial Institute (RPMI) 1640 cell culture medium, incubating in a incubator at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide, observing the confluence and morphology of the cultured cells under an inverted microscope, when the cells were grown to a confluence of 70-90%, removing the RPMI1640 cell culture medium in the culture flask, washing he cells twice with phosphate buffer, discarding the washing solution, adding 1 ml 0.25 weight/volume% trypsin solution, incubating for 1-2 minutes and suspending in RPMI 1640 cell culture medium to form a single cell suspension, (c) calculating the cell concentration of the human nasal epithelial cell single cell suspension obtained in the step (b) by a hemocytometer counting method to obtain number of living cells per ml in a single cell suspension, (d) diluting the single cell suspension of human nasal epithelial cells to 2x 105 cells/ml with RPMI1640 cell culture medium, inoculating 6 mL/well cell suspension into a 100 mm cell culture plate and incubating in a incubator at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours, (e) diluting the pretreated sample with phosphate buffered saline (PBS) into a working solution of 400 mu g/ml, discarding the cell culture medium, adding different doses of the test solution to the cell culture plate and incubating in a incubator at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours, (f) discarding the cell culture medium, washing the cells once with PBS, adding 0.25 wt.% trypsin solution, suspending the cells in each well with RPMI1640 cell culture medium, collecting suspension in a 15 ml centrifuge tube separately, centrifuging at 1000 rpm and 4 degrees C for 10 minutes and discarding the supernatant, (g) adding 100 mu l cell lysate to each centrifuge tube, centrifuging the mixture at 0 degrees C for 1 minutes, again centrifuging at 1600 rpm and 4 degrees C for 10 minutes and collecting the supernatant, (h) adding 20 mu l supernatant to the test sample group in different centrifuge tubes, adding 20 mu l detection buffer to blank control 1, adding 40 mu l detection buffer to blank control 2, adding 180 mu l nitroblue tetrazolium to the test sample group and the blank control group, incubating the color developing working solution at 37 degrees C for 30 minutes, and measuring the absorbance at 560 nm and (i) (i) calculating the percentage of inhibition in accordance with the absorbance value obtained in the step (h), where the percentage of inhibition = (A blank control 1-A sample)/(A blank control 1-A blank control 2)x 100% and (ii) calculating superoxide dismutase enzyme activity, where SOD enzyme activity unit in the sample to be tested=percent inhibition/(1-percent inhibition) units.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN108728333-A
TI Cell culture incubator comprises e.g. a box body,            fixed through the cylinder body with a supporting            plate, connected with a rotating disc and movably            connected with the top of the rotary plate.
AU MAO H
AE GUANGZHOU BINXIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018901187
AB    NOVELTY - Cell culture incubator comprises e.g. a box                body (1). The internal bottom end of the box body                is fixed through the cylinder body (2) with a                supporting plate (3). The top middle part of the                supporting plate by a rolling bearing rotationally                is connected with a rotating disc (4) and movably                connected with the top of the rotary plate, and                placed with a culture vessel. The bottom of the                supporting plate is fixed with the output shaft and                the rotary disk is fixed connection of stepping                motor (5). The top of the supporting plate on the                rotating disc of outside is embedded with electric                heating pipe. The interior of the box body one side                is provided with an end opposite to the video                microscope main body of the culture vessel. The one                side of the box body is provided with an observing                eyepiece and matched with the video microscope main                body. The inner side of the box body is provided                with a sensor component. The top end of the box                body is hinged with a box cover.
   USE - The incubator is useful for biotechnology                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The incubator: reduces bacterial contamination                of cells, is simple and reasonable; has easy to                operate, and less difficult to cultivate; improves                the survival rate of cell culture; is safe, stable                and easy to observe cell culture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cell culture incubator comprises a box body                (1). The internal bottom end of the box body is                fixed through the cylinder body (2) with a                supporting plate (3). The top middle part of the                supporting plate by a rolling bearing rotationally                is connected with a rotating disc (4) and movably                connected with the top of the rotary plate, and                placed with a culture vessel. The bottom of the                supporting plate is fixed with the output shaft and                the rotary disk is fixed connection of stepping                motor (5). The top of the supporting plate on the                rotating disc of outside is embedded with electric                heating pipe. The bottom of the box cover is                located at the outside of the lamp and embedded                with an ultraviolet lamp. The middle of the top                part of the box cover is fixed with a servo motor.                The output shaft of the servo motor is fixedly                connected through a support rod with a mounting                frame. The box cover at one side                of the top side is fixed with injection pipe is                plugged with a rubber plug. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                also included for method of culturing cells for                biotechnology, comprising placing the thawed cell                solution into a centrifuge tube, inserting the tube                into the tube placement hole at the top of the                mounting frame, controlling the servo motor by the                FPGA controller to drive the mounting frame and                rotating, centrifuging the cell fluid in the                centrifuge tube for 3-5 minutes and the speed of                the servo motor is 2000-4000 revolutions/minute                (rpm), (ii) removing the supernatant from the                centrifuged cell solution and pouring into the                culture vessel, placing the culture vessel on the                rotating plate at the top of the pallet and fixing,                closing the end cap, using the FPGA controller,                controlling the heating tube, heating the tank and                maintaining the temperature inside the box at 25-45                degrees C, (iii) where the sensor assembly senses                the temperature, carbon dioxide concentration and                oxygen concentration in the tank in real time,                passing the sensed numerical control to the FPGA                controller for processing and analysis, and                displaying on the LED display on the surface,                injecting oxygen and carbon dioxide gas into the                tank from the gas injection pipe, maintaining the                concentration of carbon dioxide in the tank at                20-30%, and maintaining the oxygen concentration at                40-50%, (iv) culturing the cell, controlling the                controller by the FPGA controller to lift the tray,                and shaking the pallet, controlling the stepping                motor to drive the rotating disc and rotating,                shaking the cell liquid in the culture vessel,                uniformly distribute the culture solution and the                cells, heating and receiving external factors                uniformly, and(v) controlling the operation of the                illuminator to simulate sunlight can achieve the                photosynthesis of plant cells by promoting                irradiation of plant cells, controlling the                operation of the UV lamp and sterilizing the inside                of the box.

PN CN107828854-A
TI Detecting effect of electronic smoke aerosol water extract on cell catalase activity involves using linear smoking machine, aspirating pieces of electronic cigarette continuously under certain condition, and collecting e-cigarette smoke.
AU GUAN Y
   YAO J
   LI X
   JIANG W
   GAO Q
   ZHU Z
   XU Y
   LU S
   MI Q
AE CHINA TOBACCO YUNNAN IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 201824319S
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting the effect of an electronic smoke aerosol water extract on cell catalase activity involves using a linear smoking machine, aspirating 100 pieces of electronic cigarette continuously under the conditions of a suction capacity of 55.0 ml for 3 seconds, and a suction frequency of 30 seconds. The e-cigarette smoke is collected in a capture bottle equipped with an fibroblast medium (FM) cell culture medium, and the trap concentration is 20 cells/mL.
   USE - Method for detecting the effect of an electronic smoke aerosol water extract on cell catalase activity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to detect the effect of an electronic smoke aerosol water extract on cell catalase activity, which effectively evaluates electronic smoke oxidative damage to the cell.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting the effect of an electronic smoke aerosol water extract on cell catalase activity involves using a linear smoking machine, aspirating 100 pieces of electronic cigarette continuously under the conditions of a suction capacity of 55.0 ml for 3 seconds, and a suction frequency of 30 seconds. The e-cigarette smoke is collected in a capture bottle equipped with an fibroblast medium (FM) cell culture medium, and the trap concentration is 20 cells/mL. The capture solution is filtered and sterilized using a 0.22 mu m sterile filter to obtain an aerosol water extract of electronic cigarette smoke as a test solution, and store at -80 degrees C. The Human pulmonary fibroblast (HPF) is resuscitated, inoculated into a 25 square centimeter cell culture flask, which is added with 10mL FM cell culture medium, and cultured at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator. The inverted microscopes are used to observe the confluence and morphology of the cultured cells when the cells grew to a confluence of 70-90%, the FM cell culture medium in the culture flask is removed, washed twice with phosphate buffer, the wash solution is discarded, added with 1mL of 0.25% trypsin solution to monolayers, incubated for 1-2 minutes, and suspended with FM cell culture medium to form single cell suspension. The cell concentration of the single cell suspension is calculated by hemocytometer counting method to obtain the number of viable cells per ml of single cell suspension. The counted single cell suspension is diluted with an FM cell culture medium to 2.0x 105 cells per mL, inoculated into a 100 mm cell culture dish, in an amount of 6 ml per dish, and the cell culture dish is placed at 37 degrees C for 24 hours in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator. The test substance is exposed to the cell culture dish, the cell culture solution is removed, different doses of the test solution is added to the cell culture dish, set 3 replicates per dose, and placed at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours. The culture medium is removed from the cell culture dish, the cells in the petri dish are washed with PBS phosphate buffer and added with 0.25% trypsin solution. The cells in each well are suspended with HPF cell culture medium, collected into 15 ml centrifuge tubes, centrifuged at 1000 rpm and 4 degrees C for 10 minutes, and the supernatant is removed. The cell lysate is added with 100 mu L cell lysis solution in each centrifuge tube, incubated at 0 degrees C for 1 minute in an ice bath, centrifuged at 1600 rpm and 4/oC for 10 minutes, and the supernatant is sampled and tested. The 0, 12.5, 25, 50, and 75 mu L of 5 mM hydrogen peroxide standard solution is taken, placed in different 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes, and added with the catalase detection buffer to constant volume of 100 mu L and mixed. The sample test solution is prepared, quickly mixed and reacted at 25 degrees C for 5 minutes, added with 450 mu L catalase to stop the solution and mixed by shaking. A new centrifuge tube is taken and added with 40 mu L catalase assay buffer, added with 10 mu L reaction system that is terminated and mix. The 4 mu L standard solution is added to 96-well plate, the blank control group and the sample test group are respectively taken 10 mu L of the test liquid and added to a 96-well plate. In the standard curve group, blank control group, and sample test group, 200 mu L of chromogenic working solution is added, incubated at 25 degrees C for 15 minutes, and the absorbance at 520 nm is measured with a microplate reader. The protein concentration of the serum is measured. The catalase enzyme activity is calculated according to the standard solution absorbance value to draw a standard curve, where A520=k (hydrogen peroxide micromolar) + b, the value of k and b are calculated from the standard curve. The residual micromolar hydrogen peroxide in the sample is calculated by residual hydrogen peroxide micromolar=(A520-b) divide by k. The catalase activity is calculated by (Sample catalase activity) = ((minus residual hydrogen peroxide in blank control) - (micromoles residual hydrogen peroxide in sample))x (dilution multiple)/(5 minx 40 mu lx (protein concentration)).

PN WO2021183390-A1; CN115244039-A
TI Ethylene oxide (EO) production process by            introducing reactant mixture to reactor to produce            effluent stream, where reactant mixture comprises            ethane and oxygen and effluent stream comprises            ethylene, ethane, and oxygen, introducing another            reactant mixture to reactor, separating and            recycling.
AU BROEKHUIS R R
   GAUTAM P S
   GU T
   SARSANI V S R
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   BROEKHUIS R R (BROE-Individual)
   GAUTAM P S (GAUT-Individual)
   GU T (GUTT-Individual)
   SARSANI V S R (SARS-Individual)
GA 2021A66180
AB    NOVELTY - Ethylene oxide (EO) production process                involves (i) (a) introducing a first reactant                mixture to a first reactor to produce a first                effluent stream, where the first reactant mixture                comprises ethane and oxygen and the first effluent                stream comprises ethylene, ethane, and oxygen, (b)                introducing a second reactant mixture to a second                reactor to produce a second effluent stream, (c)                separating at least a portion of the second                effluent stream into an EO product stream and a                recycle stream, and (d) recycling a first portion                of the recycle stream to the first reactor in step                (a), and a second portion of the recycle stream to                the second reactor in step (b), where ethylene is                not separated from the recycle stream, and the                recycle split ratio is defined as the ratio of the                volumetric flowrate of the first portion of the                recycle stream divided by the sum of the volumetric                flowrate of the first portion of the recycle stream                and the volumetric flowrate of the second portion                of the recycle stream.
   USE - As ethylene oxide production process.
   ADVANTAGE - The process produces EO that integrates                oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane with ethylene                epoxidation, combines the ODH and EO technologies,                while providing for an increased chemical                efficiency and/or lower capital cost, can employ                ODH catalysts, EO catalysts and moderators and                provides a relatively high ethylene oxide yield,                while balancing the competing objective of low                equipment cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ethylene oxide (EO) production process                involves (i) (a) introducing a first reactant                mixture to a first reactor to produce a first                effluent stream, where the first reactant mixture                comprises ethane and oxygen and the first effluent                stream comprises ethylene, ethane, and oxygen, (b)                introducing a second reactant mixture to a second                reactor to produce a second effluent stream, where                the second reactant mixture comprises at least a                portion of the first effluent stream; ethylene is                not separated from the first effluent stream and                the second effluent stream comprises EO, ethane,                ethylene, and oxygen, (c) separating at least a                portion of the second effluent stream into an EO                product stream and a recycle stream, where the EO                product stream comprises at least a portion of the                EO in the second effluent stream and the recycle                stream comprises ethane, ethylene, and oxygen and                (d) recycling a first portion of the recycle stream                to the first reactor in step (a), and a second                portion of the recycle stream to the second reactor                in step (b), where ethylene is not separated from                the recycle stream, a recycle split ratio is                0.2-0.6 and the recycle split ratio is defined as                the ratio of the volumetric flowrate of the first                portion of the recycle stream divided by the sum of                the volumetric flowrate of the first portion of the                recycle stream and the volumetric flowrate of the                second portion of the recycle stream or (ii) (a1)                introducing a first reactant mixture to an                oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) reactor to produce                a first effluent stream, where the first reactant                mixture comprises ethane, and oxygen; and the first                effluent stream comprises ethylene, ethane, and                oxygen; (b1) introducing a second reactant mixture                to an EO reactor to produce a second effluent                stream; where the second reactant mixture comprises                at least a portion of the first effluent stream;                ethylene is not separated from the first effluent                stream; and the second effluent stream comprises                EO, ethane, ethylene, oxygen, and carbon dioxide;                (c1) separating at least a portion of the second                effluent stream into an EO product stream and a                recycle stream; where the EO product stream                comprises at least a portion of the EO in the                second effluent stream; the recycle stream                comprises ethane, ethylene, oxygen, and carbon                dioxide; and at least a portion of the carbon                dioxide is optionally removed from the recycle                stream; (d1) recycling a first portion of the                recycle stream to the ODH reactor in step (a1), and                a second portion of the recycle stream to the EO                reactor in step (b1), where ethylene is not                separated from the recycle stream and a recycle                split ratio is defined as the ratio of the                volumetric flowrate of the first portion of the                recycle stream divided by the sum of the volumetric                flowrate of the first portion of the recycle stream                and the volumetric flowrate of the second portion                of the recycle stream; (e1) determining a molar                concentration of ethylene in the recycle stream;                (f1) comparing the molar concentration of ethylene                in the recycle stream with a target molar                concentration of ethylene in the recycle stream;                (g1) responsive to step (f1), when the molar                concentration of ethylene in the recycle stream is                less than the target molar concentration of                ethylene in the recycle stream, increasing the                recycle split ratio; and (h1) responsive to step                (f1), when the molar concentration of ethylene in                the recycle stream is greater than the target molar                concentration of ethylene in the recycle stream,                decreasing the recycle split ratio.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further                  involves withdrawing a third portion of the                  recycle stream as a purge stream, where the purge                  stream is characterized by a purge ratio of                  0.0001-0.005 and the purge ratio is defined as                  the ratio of the volumetric flowrate of the purge                  stream divided by the volumetric flowrate of the                  recycle stream. The process further involves                  introducing methane to the first reactor and/or                  the second reactor, where the recycle stream is                  characterized by a methane concentration of 20-80                  mol%. The process introducing supplemental oxygen                  to the first reaction mixture, in addition to the                  oxygen recycled to the first reactor via the                  first portion of the recycle stream, where the                  supplemental oxygen is introduced to the second                  reaction mixture, in addition to the oxygen                  introduced to the second reactor via the second                  portion of the recycle stream and/or via the                  first effluent stream; and the first effluent                  stream, the second effluent stream, and the                  recycle stream comprise carbon dioxide; and (i)                  at least a portion of the carbon dioxide is                  removed from the first effluent stream prior to                  feeding the first effluent stream to the second                  reactor in step (b); and/or (ii) at least a                  portion of the carbon dioxide is removed from the                  recycle stream prior to the step (d) of recycling                  a first portion of the recycle stream to the                  first reactor in step (a), and a second portion                  of the recycle stream to the second reactor in                  step (b). Preferred Composition: The first                  reactor is characterized by an oxygen conversion                  of 30-100%. The first effluent stream is                  characterized by an ethane concentration of 0.1-5                  mol%. The recycle stream is characterized by an                  ethane concentration of 0.1-5 mol%. The                  concentration of ethane in the second reactant                  mixture and/or the recycle stream is 0.1-3 mol%.                  The recycle stream is characterized by a combined                  concentration of ethane and ethylene of 15-50                  mol%. In the process, the recycle split ratio is                  less than 0.45, preferably less than 0.3. The                  first reactor is characterized by an ethane                  conversion of 60-100%. The first reactant mixture                  is characterized by a molar ratio of ethylene to                  ethane of 1.3-3. The target molar concentration                  of ethylene in the recycle stream is 25-40 mol%.                  The concentration of ethane in the second                  reactant mixture and/or the recycle stream is                  0.1-3 mol%. The ODH reactor is characterized by                  an ethane conversion of 70-100%, where the                  recycle split ratio is modified to be in a range                  of 0.2-0.6.
   EXAMPLE - The examples do not provide sufficient details                of an ethylene oxide production process, so they do                not support claims.

TI Lotion comprises lobular essential oil, edum palustre stem leaf extract, peppermint essential oil, jasmine essential oil, Aloe extract, sorbitan laurate, glycerol polyoxyethylene ether, cyclodextrin, glycerin, and hyaluronic acid.
AU XIE Y
   LI P
   ZHANG X
   SUI X
   YANG F
   FU D
   HUANG B
AE HEILONGJIANG ACAD SCI ADVANCED TECHNOLOG (HEIL-Non-standard)
   HEILONGJIANG ACAD SCI ADVANCED TECHNOLOG (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 2019919910
AB    NOVELTY - Lotion comprises 1-8 wt.% lobular essential oil, 2-10 wt.% edum palustre stem leaf extract, 0.2-0.6 wt.% peppermint essential oil, 1-3 wt.% jasmine essential oil, 0.5-4 wt.% Aloe extract, 1-4 wt.% sorbitan laurate, 1-4 wt.% glycerol polyoxyethylene ether,0.2-2 wt.% gamma -cyclodextrin, 2-10 wt.% glycerin, 0.3-1 wt.% hyaluronic acid, 0.2-0.6 wt.% methylparaben, 0.5-2 wt.% water-soluble vitamin E, 10-20 wt.% ethanol and deionized water (remining amount).
   USE - Used as lotion containing fine lobular essential oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the lotion comprising collecting fresh flowers and young leaves of edum palustre from May to July, washing, drying in cool ventilated place, drying eucalyptus and young leaves, placing in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction unit, under the conditions of extraction temperature at 40-50 degrees C, an extraction pressure of 15-30 MPa, and flow rate of 10-20 l/h, and extracting for 90-150 minutes to obtain crude oil of fine edum palustre leave essential oil, placing essential crude oil into the wiped film molecular distillation equipment with the distillation pressure of 0.1-20 Pa, and distillation temperature is 60-200 degrees c, carrying out distillation under the conditions of feed amount of 20-80 ml/h and wiper speed of 150-450 rotations/minute (rpm), (ii) collecting the stems and leaves of fresh ledum palustre from August to October, washing and drying in cool ventilated place, drying the Eucalyptus urophylla stalks and leaves are crushing and passing through 60 meshes sieve, collecting the powder passing through 60 meshes sieve to obtain stalk and lobular leave powder, immersing the stalk and leave powder in ethanol solution having 45-85 wt.%, soaking for 24-72 hours at a temperature of 80-105 degrees C, extracting the extract by soxhlet extraction tank for 60-150 minutes, filtering the extract, concentrating the filtrate to 1/4-1/8 of original volume, and placing the concentrate in lyophilizer, carrying out freeze drying to suspend in water, extracting with petroleum ether and ethyl acetate, concentrating the ethyl acetate phase and lyophilizing to powder to obtain ledum palustre crude extract, dissolving the crude extract in an ethanol solution having 50-90 wt.%, adding activated carbon at 40-80 degrees C, decolorizing and stirring for 20-60 minutes, and finally centrifuging, concentrating the supernatant to 1/4-1/8 of the original volume, and placing the concentrate in lyophilizer and lyophilizing into powder to obtain ledum palustre stem leaf extract, where the mass ratio of the fine leave stalk and leave powder to the ethanol solution having mass percentage of 45-85% is 1: (10-25), the mass ratio of the crude extract of Eucalyptus urophylla and ethanol solution having mass percentage of 50-90% is 1: (5-20), and the mass ratio of the crude extract of lobular fine leaves and activated carbon is 1: (0.02-0.1), (iii) collecting the fresh mint leaves and stems, washing and drying in cool ventilated place, drying mint leaves and stems, crushing and passing through 60 meshes sieve, collecting the powder through 60 meshes sieve, placing mint leaves and stem powder into the extraction kettle of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction unit, extraction temperature of 35-50 degrees C, extraction pressure of 20-30 MPa, under the condition of carbon dioxide flow rate of 8-20 l/hours, extracting from 90-120 minutes to obtain peppermint essential oil, (iv) collecting fresh jasmine, washing and drying in cool ventilated place, extracting by steam distillation to obtain jasmine essential oil, (v) collecting fresh Aloe vera, washing, chopping, and soaking in deionized water for 18-48 hours, centrifuging, taking out supernatant for concentrating and lyophilized to obtain Aloe extract, where the volume ratio of chopped aloe and deionized water is 1:(10-25), (vi) taking 1-8 wt.% lobular essential oil, 2-10 wt.% ledum palustre stem leaf extract, 0.2-0.6 wt.% peppermint essential oil, 1-3 wt.% jasmine essential oil, 0.5-4 wt.% Aloe extract, 1-4 wt.% sorbitan laurate, 1-4 wt.% glycerol polyoxyethylene ether,0.2-2% gamma -cyclodextrin, 2-10 wt.% glycerin, 0.3-1 wt.% hyaluronic acid, 0.2-0.6 wt.% methylparaben, 0.5-2 wt.% water-soluble vitamin E, 10-20 wt.% ethanol and deionized water (remining amount), (vii) taking sorbitan laurate, glycerol ethoxylate, gamma -cyclodextrin, glycerin, hyaluronic acid, methylparaben, water-soluble vitamin E, mixing ethanol and deionized water at ultrasonic frequency of 25-50 kHz with rotational speed of 4000-5000 rpm, carrying out ultrasonic dispersion for 20-45 minutes to obtain the mixture, adding with fine leave essential oil, leave extract, peppermint essential oil, Jasmine essential oil and Aloe extract, under the condition of ultrasonic frequency of 25-40KHz with rotational speed of 4000-5000 rpm, carrying out ultrasonic dispersion for 20-45 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The lotion comprises 6 wt.% lobular essential oil, 4 wt.% edum palustre stem leaf extract, 0.2 wt.% peppermint essential oil, 3 wt.% jasmine essential oil, 2.5 wt.% Aloe extract, 1 wt.% sorbitan laurate, 2 wt.% glycerol polyoxyethylene ether, 1 gamma -cyclodextrin, 6 wt.% glycerin, 0.8 wt.% hyaluronic acid, 0.6 wt.% methylparaben, 1 wt.% water-soluble vitamin E, 18 wt.% ethanol and deionized water (remining amount). Preferred Method: In the step (i), the dried flowers and young leaves are placed in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction unit at 42-50 degrees C under 18-30 MPa pressure, and with carbon dioxide flow rate is 15-20 L/hours for 120-150 minutes to obtain fine leaves essential oil. In the step (ii), the stalks and leaf powders of the leaves are immersed in an ethanol solution having a mass percentage of 70-85%, soaked for 36-72 hours, and then extracted by reflux in a Soxhlet extraction tank for 120-150 minutes. In the step (ii), the crude extract of Eucalyptus urophylla is dissolved in an ethanol solution having a mass percentage of 70-90%, and then activated carbon is added, and the mixture is decolorized by stirring at a temperature of 50-80 degrees C for 40-60 minutes. In the step (iii), the mint leaves and stem powder are placed in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction apparatus extraction tank at an extraction temperature of 45-50 degrees C for 90-120 minutes under 28-30 MPa with carbon dioxide flow rate of 12-20 L/hours to obtain mint essential oil. In the step (vii), sorbitan monolaurate ester, glycerin polyoxyethylene ether, gamma -cyclodextrin, glycerol, hyaluronic acid, methylparaben, water-soluble vitamin E, ethanol and de-ionized water are taken, mixture is ultrasonically dispersed for 30-45 minutes under the conditions of an ultrasonic frequency of 25-40 kHz and a rotational speed of 4000-5000 revolutions/minute (rpm) to obtain mixture. In the step (vii), the mixture is added to the mixture, and the extract of the leaves, the extract of the leaves, the essential oil of peppermint, the essential oil of jasmine and the aloe extract are obtained at an ultrasonic frequency of 25-40 kHz and a rotational speed of 4000-5000 rotations/minute (rpm) Under the conditions, the ultrasound is dispersed for 25-45 minutes.

PN CN110217922-A
TI Desulfurization wastewater resource utilization process system comprises e.g. waste water tanks, wastewater cyclone, clarifier, ceramic membrane, sodium hydroxide dosing device, related cleaning devices and instrument control.
AU CHEN F
   ZHANG B
   LU R
   LI H
   MA Q
   LI Y
   XIAN K
AE BEIJING LUCENCY ENVIRO TECH CO LTD (CGUO-C)
GA 2019795637
AB    NOVELTY - Desulfurization wastewater resource utilization process system comprises a waste water tanks, a wastewater cyclone, a high efficiency clarifier, a clarifier, a preconcentration device, a ceramic membrane, a HIRO, an electrolytic device, a clean water tank, a vacuum belt conveyor, a coagulant dosing device, a sodium hydroxide dosing device, a composite flocculant dosing device, a carbon dioxide dosing device, a soda medicinal device to ensure long-term stable operation of this process system, a related cleaning devices, a dosing device, an electrical system and a instrument control.
   USE - Used as desulfurization wastewater resource utilization process system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: ensures complete utilization of desulfurization wastewater resources; produces sodium hypochlorite products; solves high energy consumption for evaporation and crystallization; shortens the processes time; recycles the each by-product; is economical; and solves the encountered in the current zero-discharge of high-salt wastewater.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Desulfurization wastewater resource utilization process system comprises a waste water tanks, a wastewater cyclone, a high efficiency clarifier, a clarifier, a preconcentration device, a ceramic membrane, a HIRO, an electrolytic device, a clean water tank, a vacuum belt conveyor, a coagulant dosing device, a sodium hydroxide dosing device, a composite flocculant dosing device, a carbon dioxide dosing device, a soda medicinal device to ensure long-term stable operation of this process system, a related cleaning devices, a dosing device, an electrical system and a instrument control.The desulfurization wastewater resource utilization process comprises connecting the coagulant dosing device to the waste water tank through a pipeline, providing the waste water tank with a stirrer, adding 0.5-1 ppm medicine, stirring, flocculating the wastewater in the waste water tank to form larger particles, performing precipitate separation in the wastewater cyclone by swirling gravity, and ensuring the effluent solid content less than 1%, (ii) providing two quick mixing boxes in front of the high-efficiency clarifier, stirring at a rate of 200 revolutions/minute (rpm), arranging a baffle and a guide tube in the mixing box, connecting the sodium hydroxide dosing device to a first quick mixing tank, adding sodium hydroxide, allowing to stand for 30 minutes, maintaining the pH of the water at 11-12, connecting the composite flocculant dosing device to the second quick mixing tank, adding composite flocculant, allowing to stand for 10 minutes, passing waste water to the first and the second quick mixing box, then passing to the high efficiency clarifier with a guide tube, rotating the baffle, highly centrifugally rotating the wastewater in the high-efficiency clarifier, increase the flocculating body grain size and density to achieve speed separation, passing clean water to the next stage clarifier through the pipeline, and the main reaction formula (I) of Mg2+2OH gives Mg(OH)2 (magnesium ion reacts with hydroxy ions to produce magnesium hydroxide), (HCO3)+2OH gives (CO3)2-+H2O (bicarbonate reacts with water to produce carbonate ion and water), (iii) connecting the carbon dioxide dosing device to a perforated pipe at the bottom of the clarifier through a pipe, having the opening diameter of 5 mm, residence time in the reaction cell of 30 minutes, loading surface of 1-3 m/hour, water depth of 5-6 m, maintaining the pH of the water above 9.5, and having main reaction formula of (II) Ca2+CO32- gives CaCO3 (calcium ion reacts with carbonate ion to produce calcium carbonate), connecting the clarifier to the preconcentration device, ceramic membrane, and sodium hydroxide dosing device through pipes, pumps and valves, hardening for 2 times, maintaining the hardness below 1 mol/l, and maintaining the sludge concentration of the preconcentration unit within 4% with a concentration meter, returning to the high efficiency clarifier when it reaches to 4%, sintering the ceramic membrane with ceramic, influent pH of 1-14, the influent turbidity of 5000, and the influent temperature of 10-80 degrees C, water production SDI of less than 1, turbidity of 0.2 NUT and having the main reaction formula (III) of Ca2+CO32- gives CaCO3, (iv) taking the HIRO by a rolling membrane having a flow passage of 0.8-4 mm using a vertical packaging, according to the water quality adopting vertical series of not greater than 7, adjusting the pH of the water to 6.5-7.5, providing with an electronic scale, an ultraviolet sterilizer, an electronic scale forming electron beam, forming a bond to hardness ions to reduces the fouling tendency, (v) passing the HIRO produced water into the clear water tank, passing concentrated water to the electrolysis unit through the pipeline, where the electrolysis is sodium hypochlorite (NaClo), and having the concentrated water sodium chloride (NaCl) content is 10000-30000 mg/l, and (vi) passing the sludge in the high-efficiency clarifier to the front end of the vacuum belt conveyor, separating from the liquid in the gypsum cyclone due to the small diameter of the molecules in the sludge, which is easy to foul the vacuum belt conveyor, and placing behind the underflow of the gypsum cyclone to prevent fouling.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the desulfurization wastewater resource utilization process system.

PN CN107828850-A
TI Detecting micronucleus in vitro against aerosol aqueous extract of electronic cigarette smoke by preparing aerosol aqueous extract, producing single cell suspension, adding sample, staining and observing micronucleus.
AU YAO J
   GUAN Y
   LI X
   KONG W
   XU Y
   LU S
   MI Q
   GAO Q
   ZHU Z
AE CHINA TOBACCO YUNNAN IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 201824319W
AB    NOVELTY - Method of detecting micronucleus in vitro against aerosol aqueous extract of the electronic cigarette smoke involves preparing aerosol aqueous extract of the electronic cigarette smoke, producing single cell suspension of human pulmonary fibroblasts (HPF), calculating cell concentration, diluting and inoculating the single cell suspension into a 12-well cell culture plate, incubating, adding the sample, grouping the test substance, incubating with test liquid, collecting the cells, dripping on a glass slide, staining the cells and observing the micronucleus.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting micronucleus in vitro against aerosol aqueous extract of the electronic cigarette smoke and determining the genetic toxicity of the electronic cigarette.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is accurate and comprehensively inspects the use characteristics and the action modes of the electronic cigarette products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of detecting micronucleus in vitro against aerosol aqueous extract of the electronic cigarette smoke in vitro involves (a) continuously pumping 100 electronic cigarettes using a linear smoking machine with a suction capacity of 55.0 ml, duration of 3 seconds and suction frequency of 30 seconds, capturing electronic cigarette smoke with a capturing flask containing fibroblast medium (FM) cell culture medium, where the capture concentration is 20 suctions/ml, filtering and sterilizing the capture liquid using a 0.22 mu m sterile filter to obtain aerosol aqueous extract of the electronic cigarette smoke as the test liquid and storing at -80 degrees C, (b) reviving and inoculating the human pulmonary fibroblasts (HPF) into 25 cm2 cell culture flasks, adding 10 ml FM cell culture medium into the flask, incubating at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator, observing the confluence and morphology of cultured cells under an inverted microscope, removing the FM cell culture medium in the flask when the cells grow to 70-90% confluency, washing the cells twice with phosphate buffer, discarding the buffer, digesting the monolayer using 1 ml 0.25 weight/volume% trypsin solution for 1-2 minutes and resuspending the cells in FM cell culture medium to form a single cell suspension, (c) counting and calculating the cell concentration of the single cell suspension by using hemocytometer to obtain the number of viable cells per ml of single cell suspension, (d) diluting the single cell suspension to 2x 105 cells/ml using FM cell culture medium, inoculating 1 ml/well suspension into a 12-well cell culture plate and incubating the 12-well cell culture plate at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator, (e) removing the cell culture medium, dividing the test substances into three groups: a cell control group, positive control group and test sample group, culturing the cells, adding FM cell culture medium to the control group, adding 0.5 mu l 1 mg/ml cyclophosphamide solution to the positive control group, then adding test liquid obtained in step (a) to test sample group, controlling final concentration of 4 groups as 4 non-zero doses, which are: 12.5% test liquid, 25% test liquid, 50% test liquid and 100% test liquid, adding 6 mu l 1 mg/ml cytochalasin B solution to the cell control group, positive control group and test sample group and making up the cell volume of each well in the cell culture plate up to 2 ml/well with FM cell culture medium, (f) adding the sample and incubating the 6-well cell culture plate in an incubator at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours, (g) adding 0.25 weight/volume% trypsin solution to collect the cells from the wells of the 6-well cell culture plate, (h) adding the cells into a centrifuge tube, centrifuging for 5 minutes at 1000 revolutions per minute (rpm), discarding the supernatant, adding 3 ml potassium chloride solution, gently mixing the cells by using pipette, immediately adding 2 ml methanol-glacial acetic acid solution and centrifuging for 5 minutes at 1000 rpm, (i) discarding the supernatant, adding 300 mu l methanol-glacial acetic acid solution, gently mixing the cells by using pipette to form a cell suspension, dripping the cell suspension on a glass slide, naturally drying and dripping 3 slides per test dose, (j) placing the air-dried slides in Giemsa's staining solution, staining for 15 minutes, rinsing the slides with water, naturally drying and storing the slides, (j) selecting micronuclear slides and selecting excellent visual field with a suitable cell density to observe the count of micronuclei in 1000 binucleated cells, maintaining the selected binucleated cells in the cytoplasm, where the boundaries of the cell membrane are clear, separating the nucleus from each other such that the micronucleus is easy to distinguish and (k) confirming as a positive result when there is a significant increase in the micronucleus rate and a dose-response relationship between the test sample group and the cell control group, if the difference is statistically significant, but there is no dose-response relationship, again performing the tests and determining as positive.
