TI Calcining method for powdered lime production comprises e.g. making limestone mine slag into powder, measuring powder, conveying to suspending pre-heating device, heating, introducing decomposition device to decompose and decarburizing.
AU SUN W
   LI H
   DAN Y
   LI G
   LIU X
   CHEN J
   LI F
   XIN S
AE TAIAN ZHONGYI HEAVY IND EQUIP CO LTD (TAIA-Non-standard)
GA 201819988C
AB    NOVELTY - Calcining method for powdered lime production comprises e.g. making the limestone mine slag into powder, measuring the powder, conveying to a suspending pre-heating device, heating to the critical decomposition temperature, introducing the decomposition device to decompose, carrying out complete decarburization, generating high temperature powdered lime, unloading lime high temperature powder into cooling device to obtain the powdered active limes. The suspending pre-heating device comprises multiple heat exchange units, each stage of the heat exchange unit comprises heat exchange channel and cyclone separator.
   USE - The method is useful for producing powdered lime.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes limestone quarry as raw material to achieve the resource utilization of the stone slag discharge, and uses various industrial wastes as raw materials; and yields highly active powdered lime e.g. sugar mud; produces calcium carbide slag by production of acetylene gas from paper white mud and calcium carbide, phosphogypsum made of ammonium sulfate fertilizer calcium carbonate slag and other calcium residue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Calcining method for powdered lime production comprises making the limestone mine slag into powder, measuring the powder, conveying to a suspending pre-heating device, heating to the critical decomposition temperature, introducing the decomposition device to decompose, carrying out complete decarburization, generating high temperature powdered lime, unloading lime high temperature powder into cooling device to obtain the powdered active limes. The suspending pre-heating device comprises multiple heat exchange units, each stage of the heat exchange unit comprises heat exchange channel and cyclone separator. The multistage heat exchange unit is arranged in series, the uppermost heat exchange unit as the first stage, sequentially downward as the second stage, then to the final stage. The number of stages of the heat exchange unit is determined as appropriate according to the control requirements for the temperature of the exhaust gas at the outlet of the first stage heat exchange unit, usually consists of three to four levels. The heat exchange unit according to the first-stage heat exchange unit outlet to control discretion of exhaust gas temperature determination, normal condition is composed of three to four-stage. The decomposing device comprises decomposing unit and cyclone separator. The deep decarburization device consists of a deep decarburization kiln, kiln bonnet and kiln bonnet cover. The cooling device comprises a fluidized suspension cooler, cooling fan, cyclone, induced fan and other components. Making the limestone mine slag into powder, delivering and storing the powder in the powder warehouse, measuring by measuring equipment, transporting to the feed inlet of the heat exchange channel of the first heat exchange unit of the suspension preheating device, together with the air flow from the air outlet of the cyclone of the second heat exchange unit, the heat exchange channel of the first stage heat exchange unit enters the cyclone separator of the first stage heat exchange unit, passing the materials of the first stage heat exchange unit cyclone separator and the outlet air stream from the third stage heat exchange unit cyclone separator are through the second stage heat exchange unit heat exchange channel to enter the second stage heat exchange unit cyclone separator, allowing the high temperature material of the cyclone separator of the final heat exchange unit to enter the decomposition unit, where in the decomposition unit most of calcium carbonate is decomposed into calcium oxide and carbon dioxide, and the carbon dioxide in the core calcium carbonate of rare stone slag particles does not completely escape, allowing the decomposition unit material to enter the cyclone separator of the decomposition device along with the high-temperature flue gas, separatin by a cyclone separator, allowing the air flow to enter the heat exchange channel of the final heat exchange unit of the suspension preheating device, allowing the materials enter the deep decarburization kiln through the kiln bonnet, continuing to decarbonize completely the incomplete decomposition pf calcium carbonate, where the decarburization refers to a process in which carbon dioxide completely escapes in the decomposition reaction of calcium carbonate, discharging the highly decarbonized high temperature lime from the kiln bonnet into a fluidized suspension cooler, cooling lime with cooling air, heating cooling air while cooling the lime, cyclone dust collector processing, introducing the induced draft fan into the kiln burner, decomposing the burner as a combustion air, allowing the cooled active lime enter into the cyclone with the cooling air, transporting the separated active lime by conveying equipment to the finished product warehouse for storage, producing high-temperature flame by combustion, allowing the kiln burner to enter the deep decarbonization kiln to form a lime decarburization temperature field, providing heat for deep decarburization, producing high-temperature flame by decomposition, burner burns into the decomposition unit to form a limestone decomposition temperature field, providing heat for the decomposition of calcium carbonate, allowing the high-temperature air flow out of the deep decarburization kiln first to enter the decomposition unit under the action of the system fan, carrying out conjunction with the high-temperature flue gas in the decomposition unit, passing through the cyclone of the decomposition device, suspending preheating unit at all levels of heat exchange units, and discharges the system by the first heat exchange unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcining device comprises orderly connected milling device, powder storehouse, measuring device, suspending pre-heating device, decomposing device, deep decarburization device, and cooling device, the powder spraying device is used for discharging the stone into powder. The powder storehouse is for storing the powder material. The measuring device is for metering into the lime burning system material. TI Power system for supplying off-grid power to            remote hydrocarbon production or processing facility,            comprises photovoltaic (PV) assembly that has multiple            PV cells configured to produce direct current (DC)            electrical power from solar energy.
AU TURAL T
   LEE K
   KATIKANENI S P
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202237099L
AB    NOVELTY - Power system (100) comprises a photovoltaic                (PV) assembly that comprises PV cells (104)                configured to produce direct current (DC)                electrical power (132) from solar energy; a                hydrogen-based polymer exchange membrane (PEM) fuel                cell assembly (106) configured to produce DC                electrical power from a hydrogen fuel source (108),                the hydrogen fuel source comprising one or more                portable hydrogen fuel storage tanks (110); a power                electronics (114) assembly comprising at least one                of: at least one DC/DC converter (117, 119)                configured to convert the produced DC electrical                power from one or both of the PV assembly or the                hydrogen-based PEM fuel assembly to a DC electrical                power supply, or at least one DC/AC inverter                configured to convert the produced DC electrical                power from one or both of the PV assembly or the                hydrogen-based PEM fuel assembly to an alternating                current (AC) electrical power supply; and an                off-grid hydrocarbon production or processing                facility.
   USE - The power system is useful for producing                electrical power; and for supplying off-grid power                to a remote hydrocarbon production or processing                facility i.e. oil and facilities at remote                locations, by a power transmission and distribution                grid.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides a higher efficiency and                energy density for a remote, off-grid facility as                compared to conventional power systems, such as                solar only systems or solar with diesel generator                secondary power systems. The system can provide a                greater overall efficiency, and reduce battery                capacity and system cost, and provide significant                improvement with respect to carbon dioxide,                nitrogen oxide and particulate matter (PM)                emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for producing                electrical power which involves producing DC                electrical power from solar energy with PV cells of                PV assembly; producing DC electrical power from a                hydrogen fuel source with a hydrogen-based PEM fuel                cell assembly, the hydrogen fuel source comprising                one or more portable hydrogen fuel storage tanks; PN CN113755264-A
TI Brewing beer using chamomile by preparing malt, performing saccharification, boiling wort, preparing chamomile flavoring agent, adding flavoring agent, activating yeast, performing residue removal, adding dry hops and bottling beer.
AU LI M
   LI H
   FANG H
   ZHANG X
AE ZHANG X (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2022036557
AB    NOVELTY - Method for brewing beer using chamomile involves (a) preparing malt, (b) performing step-by-step saccharification, (c) boiling the wort, (d) preparing chamomile flavoring agent, (e) adding flavoring agent, (f) activating yeast, (g) performing residue removal and adding dry hops and (i) bottling the beer.
   USE - The method is useful for brewing beer using chamomile.
   ADVANTAGE - The method changes original flavor of the beer and prepares beer, which has a floral flavor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for brewing beer using chamomile with initial wort concentration of 1.052 in 20 l capacity involves (a) (i) preparing 6 kg malt, including 4 kg basic ale malt and 2 kg light-colored wheat malt, (ii) adjusting the upper and lower grinding knives of the malt grinder to 0.9 and adding the basic ale malt into grinder for grinding, (iii) sharpening the knives of the malt grinder up and down to modulate 1.3, and pouring the light-colored wheat malt into the grinder for grinding and (iv) again adding the ground wheat malt into the malt grinder to grind, (b) (i) adding the wheat malt into a standard 85 l American three-barrel saccharification pot, (ii) adding 70degreesC tap water with a pH value of 6.5-7.2 to soak the wheat until it is wet, (iii) continuing adding ground ale malt into same mashing pot, (iv) adding 70degreesC tap water with a pH value of 6.5-7.2 until the water level just below the malt, (v) stirring the malt clockwise until there is no agglomerated malt in the mashing pot, (vi) maintaining the temperature of the wort in the mashing pot at 65degreesC for 30 minutes, (vii) heating the wort in the mashing pot at 72degreesC, (viii) stirring the malt in the mashing pot to make the temperature uniform, (ix) incubating the wort at 72degreesC for 30 minutes and (x) heating the wort at 78degreesC for 5 minutes, (c) (i) opening the connection valve between the mashing pot and the boiling pot, and pouring all the wort into the boiling pot, (ii) closing the valves of the saccharification pot and the boiling pot, and pouring 15 l 80degreesC hot water into the saccharification pot, (iii) stirring the wort in the mashing pot in clockwise direction to make the temperature uniform, (iv) opening the valve between the mashing pot and the boiling pot again, and pumping all the wort into the boiling pot to obtain 25 l wort, (v) adjusting the boiler thermostat to 110degrees Celsius and (vi) boiling the wort for 1 hour and performing volatilization to obtain 20 l wort, (d) (i) heating the Belgian candi sugar at 80degreesC, (ii) adding 20 g chamomile into Belgian candi sugar at 80degreesC, (iii) heating at 100degrees Celsius and stirring to heat evenly and (iv) boiling the Belgian candi sugar for 10 minutes, so that the chamomile aroma molecules are fully integrated with the Belgian candi sugar, (e)(i) adding the chamomile flavoring agent into the wort in the last 20 minutes of boiling the wort, (ii) stirring the wort evenly, (iii) performing gyratory precipitation, and adding 20 g chamomile, (iv) performing gyratory precipitation for 20 minutes and using a cooling coil to rapidly cool the wort to 18degreesC and (v) filtering the cooled wort into the sterilized fermentation tank and (vi) adding 20 g chamomile in a hop bag and adding into the fermentation tank, (f) (i) preparing 100 ml sterile water at 20degreesC, (ii) adding 5 g WB06 yeast and 5 g M36 yeast into sterile water, (iii) fully dissolving the yeast in sterile water by shaking and (vi) incubating the sterile water containing mixed yeast at 20degreesC for 20 minutes to activate the yeast, (vii) adding the activated yeast into the fermentation tank, (ii) opening the exhaust valve of the fermentation tank and injecting oxygen to enhance the yeast activity and (iii) maintaining temperature of the fermentation tank at 18degreesC, (h) (i) opening the residue valve of the fermentation tank for the first residue removal after 72 hours of fermentation, (ii) adding 2 g/l Xichu hops from the feeding port of the fermentation tank, (iii) performing second residue removal 72 hours after the hops are added and cooling to less than 5degrees Celsius and (iv) cooling for 72 days and performing third residue removal and (i) (i) adding 5 g/l white granulated sugar syrup, (ii) injecting carbon dioxide from the sampling valve of the fermentation tank for 5-10 minutes and closing the exhaust valve, (iii) continuing injecting carbon dioxide to make the pressure in the barrel reach 1 Pa, (iv) bottling from the connection pipeline of the sampling valve and (v) allowing to stand at room temperature for 96 hours, transferring to the refrigerator and storing in the refrigerator for 72 hours before drinking.

PN CN113247861-A; CN216972005-U
TI Hydrogen recovery system includes compression cooling and gas-liquid separating device, membrane separation device, pressure swing adsorption device, and temperature swing adsorption device for absorbing component with high boiling point.
AU TANG Q
   SHU C
   TIAN X
   NI W
AE GUANGDONG SAIRUI NEW ENERGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202198838R
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogen recovery system using gas as raw material gas, includes compression cooling and gas-liquid separating device, membrane separation device (7) and pressure swing adsorption device (11). The compression cooling and gas-liquid separation device is used for preparing high-pressure gas phase. The membrane separation device is used for obtaining hydrogen-containing permeation and gas-rich hydrogen at low pressure side. The system further includes temperature swing adsorption device (4) in middle of compression cooling and gas-liquid separating device and membrane separation device. The temperature swing adsorption device is used for absorbing component with high boiling point, and using lower energy consumption to reduce dew point temperature of raw material gas. The variable temperature adsorption device has two adsorption towers designed by redundancy, so as to reduce adverse effect of components with higher boiling point in gas of complex variable components to filter membrane.
   USE - Hydrogen recovery system.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogen recovery system is capable of realizing unification of hydrogen separation efficiency and safety, and the service life of separation membrane of the membrane separation device is prolonged.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydrogen recovery system using gas as raw material gas, includes a compression cooling and gas-liquid separating device, a membrane separation device and a pressure swing adsorption device. The compression cooling and gas-liquid separation device is used for preparing high-pressure gas phase. The membrane separation device is used for obtaining high hydrogen-containing permeation and gas-rich hydrogen at low pressure side and obtaining low-hydrogen-containing leakage gas at high-pressure side. The pressure swing adsorption device is used for carrying out pressure swing adsorption to the hydrogen-rich hydrogen to prepare finished hydrogen. The system further includes a temperature swing adsorption device set in the middle of the compression cooling and gas-liquid separating device and the membrane separation device. The temperature swing adsorption device is used for selectively absorbing and removing component with high boiling point and portion of water from high-pressure gas phase, and effectively using lower energy consumption to reduce dew point temperature of raw material gas. The variable temperature adsorption device has at least two adsorption towers designed by redundancy, so as to reduce adverse effect of the components with higher boiling point in the gas of complex variable components to filter membrane. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) hydrogen recovery method using hydrogen recovery system, which involves performing compression-cooling and gas-liquid separation to prepare high-pressure gas phase, performing membrane separation to prepare hydrogen-rich gas, performing pressure-swing adsorption to prepare finished hydrogen, removing component with higher boiling point and portion of water, effectively using lower energy consumption to reduce dew point temperature, component with larger molecular weight, introducing high-pressure gas phase after temperature change adsorption treatment into film separating front heater, heating and fine filtering the precise filter, so that the dew point temperature of the gas before being sent into the film separating device is far lower than the working temperature of the film separating device, discarding heavy hydrocarbon component and portion of water absorbed by temperature change through regeneration process of temperature swing adsorption treatment, directly entering fuel gas pipe network or liquefied gas absorption stable system, using variable temperature adsorption of at least two adsorption towers designed by redundancy, so as to reduce theadverse effect of excessive high boiling point components in the gas of the complex variable components to the filter membrane, where one adsorption tower is adsorbed and other adsorption towers are regenerated, when switching, the large adsorption residual capacity is reserved, so as to deal with complex gas component and the content of each component is relatively large, so that the system has time adjusting operation, and protecting subsequent film separating device, heating and precise filtering, heating high-pressure gas phase at 50-90 degrees C and precisely filtering by precise filter, filtering the particle impurity in gas, and entering film separating device; and
   (2) application of the hydrogen recovery method in recovering hydrogen gas with complex variable component gas as raw material gas, which involves using gas of complex variable component as gas of hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and high-boiling point hydrocarbon, and its component comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrocarbon with high boiling point, gas, such as oxygen and nitrogen, and water, alcohol and aldehyde oxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the hydrogen recovery system.
   Gas-liquid separating tank (3)
   Temperature swing adsorption device (4)
   Precise filter (6)
   Membrane separation device (7)
   Pressure swing adsorption device (11)

   USE - Manufacture of urea in urea plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of urea in urea                plant including equipment made of ferritic steel                alloys having excellent corrosion resistance in                high pressure autoclave with ammonium carbamate,                thereby simulating process conditions without added                passivation air. The component is provided directly                without need for further machining or                drilling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of urea in urea plant involves                reacting ammonia feed and carbon dioxide feed under                urea formation conditions in a reactor to form a                urea synthesis solution comprising urea, water,                carbamate and ammonia, and contacting a                carbamate-containing liquid stream with equipment                component of the high pressure synthesis section                comprising ferritic steel alloy comprising 0.005                wt.% or less carbon, 0.1-0.4 wt.% silicon, 0.4 wt.%                or less manganese, 0.020 wt.% or less phosphorus,                0.020 wt.% or less sulfur, 0.25 wt.% or less                copper, 0.50 wt.% nickel, 20-35 wt.% chromium,                0.75-1.50 wt.% molybdenum, 0.0050-0.0125 wt.%                nitrogen, 0.060-0.375 wt.% niobium and remainder of                iron and 0.50 wt.% or less total added                processability elements and impurities. The content                of niobium and nitrogen satisfy the relation:                12(wt.% N) not less than Nb not less than 30x (wt.%                N). The urea plant comprises high pressure                synthesis section comprising a reactor. INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) high pressure stripper, which is a                shell-and-tube heat exchanger comprising tubes, a                shell, and an upper tube sheet and a lower tube                sheet. The stripper is falling-film type vertical                shell-and-tube heat exchanger, comprising an inlet                for receiving urea solution and further comprises                carbamate in the tubes at an upper portion of the                tubes, an inlet for receiving steam in the shell                space between the shell and the tubes and the two                tube sheets. The tube comprises at least a portion                comprising ferritic steel and is in operation in                contact with the urea solution comprising                carbamate;
   (2) use of ferritic stainless steel in                ammonium carbamate environment, which is exposed to                the fluid comprising ammonium carbamate;
   (3) manufacture of component, which involves                providing a mold defining the shape of an article                to be manufactured, filling a portion (a) of the                mold corresponding to component (C1) with stainless                steel alloy powder (P1) which is a ferritic                stainless steel alloy powder, filling a portion (b)                of the mold corresponding to the component (C2)                with a stainless steel alloy powder (P2) having a                different elemental composition than stainless                steel alloy powder (P1), subjecting the mold, as                filled with stainless steel alloy powder (P1) and                (P2) to hot isostatic pressing (HIP);
   (4) heat exchanger tube, which is a bimetallic                tube comprising inner tube layer and outer tube                layer. The inner tube layer is made of a ferritic                steel alloy and the outer tube layer is made of a                material chosen from duplex stainless steel,                titanium, titanium alloys, zirconium, zirconium                alloys and austenite stainless steel. The component                comprises components (C1) and (C2) having                metallurgical bond to each other. The portion (a)                is made of ferritic stainless steel. The portion                (b) is made of steel than the portion (a);                and
   (5) apparatus, which comprises at least one                steel tube, at least one holder element comprising                steel having a duplex austenite-ferrite micro                structure or purely austenitic micro structure. The                steel tube penetrates the holder element and is                attached to the holder element by weld joint                provided on an outer surface of the tube and on the                holder element. The steel tube is a compound tube                comprising an inner tube portion having purely                ferritic micro structure, and an outer tube portion                having duplex austenite-ferrite microstructure or                purely austenitic micro structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The high pressure                  synthesis section comprises a stripper. The tubes                  are bimetallic tubes comprises an inner tube                  layer and outer tube layer. The stripper                  comprises a weld between the outer tube to an                  upper side of the upper tube sheet. The upper                  tube sheet comprises a pressure-bearing inner                  components comprising carbon steel and an overlay                  of corrosion resistant steel. The weld is between                  the outer tube and the overlay. The apparatus                  comprises unit for introduction of corrosive                  media into the tube and unit for heating the tube                  from outside. The corrosive media is media for                  which the ferritic steel has higher corrosion                  resistance than duplex austenite-ferrite micro                  structure or purely austenitic micro structure at                  the elevated temperatures caused by the means for                  heating the tube from outside.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The ferritic steel                  alloy preferably comprises 0.0030 wt.% or less                  carbon, 0.1-0.3 wt.% silicon, 0.2 wt.% or less                  manganese, 0.25 wt.% or less nickel, 25-27.5 wt.%                  chromium, 0.75-1.50 wt.% nitrogen, 0.060-0.375                  wt.% niobium and remainder of iron. The content                  of niobium and nitrogen preferably satisfy the                  relation: 15( wt.% N) not less than Nb not less                  than 25x (wt.% N). The portion (a) has a purely                  ferritic micro structure. The portion (b) is made                  of austenitic or duplex stainless steel. The                  outer tube layer is made of a duplex stainless                  steel alloy comprising 25% or more chromium, 4-9%                  nickel and 1-5% molybdenum. The inner tube layer                  has a purely ferritic micro structure. The inner                  tube layer and the outer tube layer have a                  metallurgical bond to each other.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The manufacture of urea                  involves subjecting the urea synthesis solution                  to a stripping step in the stripper, and further                  involves obtaining carbon dioxide feed by                  separating carbon dioxide from gas stream (S1)                  comprising carbon dioxide and oxygen, in which                  amount of oxygen present in the high pressure                  synthesis section is at least 90 mol% derived                  from gas stream (S1). Preferred Conditions: The                  temperature in at least portion of the stripper                  tubes is 200 degrees C or more.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); M27 (Ferrous alloys - including alloy production and composition (C22C).)

PN AU2019201185-A1
TI Producing ready to eat table grape product, by destemming grape berries of grape bunches, washing in wash solution, de-watering washed destemmed grape berries, weighing and filling punnets with berries, and enclosing berries in punnets.
AU SADAN G
   HEEPS J
AE FRUIT MASTER AUSTRALIA PTY LTD (FRUI-Non-standard)
GA 201981527P
AB    NOVELTY - Method (M1) of producing a ready to eat table grape product, involves receiving pre-chilled grape bunches from storage into one or more automated conveying production lines that are in one of a serial or parallel configuration, destemming the grape berries of the grape bunches by inducing rolling contact with a continuous web surface of a conveyor to gently remove the stems from the grape berries such that the grape berries remain substantially intact, washing the destemmed grape berries in a sterilizing wash solution, de-watering the washed destemmed grape berries to induce draining and evaporation of the residual wash solution adhering to the grape berries, weighing and filling punnets with grape berries, and enclosing the grape berries in the punnets with a resealable film comprising micro apertures adapted for the respiration of grapes to extend shelf life of the ready to eat grape berries.
   USE - The method is useful for (a) producing a ready to eat table grape product, (b) processing picked grapes into a ready to eat table grape product, (c) controlling and/or monitoring wash solution, (d) dewatering grape berries, or (e) filling punnets with grape berries (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces ready to eat table grape product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method (M1) of producing a ready to eat table grape product, involves receiving pre-chilled grape bunches from storage into one or more automated conveying production lines that are in one of a serial or parallel configuration, destemming the grape berries of the grape bunches by inducing rolling contact with a continuous web surface of a conveyor to gently remove the stems from the grape berries such that the grape berries remain substantially intact, washing the destemmed grape berries in a sterilizing wash solution, de-watering the washed destemmed grape berries by conveying the grape berries along an extended conveyor path with perforations or apertures to induce draining and evaporation of the residual wash solution adhering to the grape berries, weighing and filling punnets with grape berries, and enclosing the grape berries in the punnets with a resealable film comprising micro apertures adapted for the respiration of grapes to extend shelf life of the ready to eat grape berries by providing one or a combination of a carbon dioxide rich environment or oxygen poor environment in the punnet. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a system;
   (2) a method (M2) of controlling and/or monitoring wash solution, involves monitoring the concentration of antibacterial agent in the wash solution, and continuously maintaining a predetermined concentration of antibacterial agent in the wash solution;
   (3) a method (M3) of dewatering grape berries, involves conveying the grape berries along an extended conveyor path with perforations or apertures to induce draining and evaporation of the residual wash solution adhering to the grape berries;
   (4) a method (M4) of filling punnets with grape berries, involves conveying grape berries on a weight sensing path with an upward incline for controlled movement, and dropping grapes berries from the weight sensing conveyor path with an upward incline into punnets positioned under the edges of the weight sensing path, where the punnets are positioned with an indexed motion;
   (5) a film closure of a fruit punnet comprising a resealable laminated film for closure of the fruit punnet, at least one micro-perforation adapted for the respiration of grapes to extend shelf life of the ready to eat grape berries by providing one or a combination of a carbon dioxide rich environment or oxygen poor environment in the punnet;
   (6) a fruit punnet adapted for re-sealable enclosure of ready to eat grape berries in the form of a bowl having an open upper end for access to the punnet contents, where the punnet comprises at least one substantially planar side wall portion and an adjacent substantially straight edge of the lip of the open upper end which allows for the punnet to stand on end for display purposes;
   (7) an apparatus, comprising processor unit adapted to operate in accordance with a predetermined instruction set, where apparatus, in conjunction with the instruction set, being adapted to perform the above-mentioned methods; and
   (8) a computer program product comprising a computer usable medium having computer readable program code and computer readable system code embodied on the medium for processing picked grapes into a ready to eat table grape product within a data processing system, where the computer program product comprises computer readable code within the computer usable medium for performing the method steps.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The automated conveying production lines that are in one of a serial or parallel configuration allows the processing of one or a combination of more than one grape variety into separate punnets, and mixing of more than one grape variety into each punnet. The step of washing further involves re-channeling the wash solution for reuse, and auto-dosing the re-channeled wash solution with sterilizing agent, where the used wash solution is probed to measure concentration of sterilizing agent and additional sterilizing agent is added to the re-channeled wash solution in correspondence with the measured concentration. The method (M1) further involves drying the destemmed and washed grape berries. The step of weighing and filling punnets, involves conveying grape berries on a weight sensing path with an upward incline for controlled movement, and dropping grapes berries from the weight sensing conveyor path with an upward incline into punnets positioned under the edges of the weight sensing path in an indexed motion. The step of dropping grape berries is performed with a resolution down to one grape berry at a time. The method (M1) further involves drying the grape berries after the step of dewatering. The method (M1) further involves labeling sealed punnets, detecting metal in sealed and labelled punnets and rejecting metal contaminated punnets with a controlled and timed air push substantially perpendicular to the main production flow, checking weight of sealed and labelled punnets, and packing sealed and labelled punnets into multiple decks for transport and retail display. The steps of destemming, washing, de-watering, weighing and filling punnets and enclosing are performed in a high care environment. Preferred System: The system comprising (1) at least one input chute adapted for the initial input of fruit product to the production system for receiving pre-chilled grape bunches from storage into one or more automated conveying production lines that are in one of a serial or parallel configuration, a destemmer in each production line for destemming the grape berries of the grape bunches by inducing rolling contact with a continuous web surface of a conveyor to gently remove the stems from the grape berries such that the grape berries remain substantially intact, a washing station in each production line for washing the destemmed grape berries in a sterilizing wash solution, a de-watering station in each production line for de-watering the washed destemmed grape berries by conveying the grape berries along an extended conveyor path with perforations or apertures to induce draining and evaporation of the residual wash solution adhering to the grape berries, a weigh and fill station in each production line for weighing and filling punnets with grape berries, a sealing station in each production line for enclosing the grape berries in the punnets with a resealable film comprising micro apertures adapted for the respiration of grapes to extend shelf life of the ready to eat grape berries by providing one or a combination of a carbon dioxide rich environment or oxygen poor environment in the punnet, (2) an input chute adapted for the initial input of fruit product to the production system having a hopper that conforms to the size and shape of a fruit container for one or a combination of releasing the contents of the open fruit container into the receiving chute, and holding the open fruit container in an up-ended position, or (3) destemming station for separating stems from grape berries, a moving continuous web which in combination with a rolling motion of conveyed grape bunches provides a gentle twisting motion that removes the stems from the grape berries, and a level sensor proximate the moving continuous web for sensing an overflow of fruit in the destemming station.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); T01 (Digital Computers)

PT P
PN CN108546683-A; CN108546683-B
TI Producing buffalo transgenic embryos by performing buffalo oocytes culture in vitro, performing in vitro fertilization, infecting buffalo granular cells using prepared adenovirus, and infecting embryo to-be-infected cultured.
AU LU X
   LIANG X
   PANG C
   DENG T
   DUAN A
   MA X
   LIANG S
AE GUANGXI ZHUANG AUTONOMOUS REGION INST (GUAG-C)
   GUANGXI ZHUANG AUTONOMOUS REGION INST (GUAG-C)
GA 201875849K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing buffalo transgenic embryos involves (a) performing buffalo oocytes culture in vitro by collecting buffalo ovaries from slaughterhouse, storing the ovaries, removing excess tissues of the ovarian mesangial and fallopian tubes, washing the ovaries using physiological saline containing double antibodies, extracting the oocytes in the follicles with a diameter of 2-6 mm, collecting buffalo oocytes with uniform cytoplasm and extracellular granule cells of 3 or more layers, washing and placing the oocytes in mature medium at 39 degrees C, (b) performing in vitro fertilization, (c) preparing single-layer co-culture system, (d) performing embryo culture in vitro, (e) performing adenoviral vector packing and amplification, (f) performing adenovirus titer dilution, (g) infecting the buffalo granular cells using prepared adenovirus, and (h) infecting embryo to-be-infected cultured in the microtiter plate monolayer co-culture system.
   USE - The method is useful for producing buffalo transgenic embryos.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves cleavage rate, and transfection blastocyst formation rate in buffalo embryo.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing buffalo transgenic embryos involves (a) performing buffalo oocytes culture in vitro by collecting buffalo ovaries from slaughterhouse, storing the ovaries using physiological saline solution at 39 degrees C for 1-2 hours, removing excess tissues of the ovarian mesangial and fallopian tubes, washing the ovaries using physiological saline containing double antibodies, extracting the oocytes in the follicles with a diameter of 2-6 mm, collecting buffalo oocytes with uniform cytoplasm and extracellular granule cells of 3 or more layers, washing and placing the oocytes in mature medium at 39 degrees C using 5% carbon dioxide incubator with saturated humidity, (b) performing in vitro fertilization by thawing buffalo frozen semen for 30 minutes, blowing off 22-24 hours cultured mature buffalo oocytes into microtiter of the fertilization plate, adding 10-15 eggs, incubating for 30 minutes, washing the sperm, adding 1x 106-1.5x 6 cells/ml sperm dropwise to fertilization plate, and incubating the plate at 39 degrees C, (c) preparing single-layer co-culture system by uniformly blowing 30 mu l cumulus granule cells on microtiter plate to obtain single-layer co-culture system, covering the system with paraffin oil, and culturing at 39 degrees C using 5% carbon dioxide incubator with saturated humidity, (d) performing embryo culture in vitro by blowing off the sperm attached to the surface of the fertilized egg after fertilization for 18 hours, washing the fertilized egg using embryo culture medium for 2-3 times, placing the fertilized eggs in a single-layer co-culture system, cultured at 39 degrees C, recording development of each stage of the embryo, partially digesting zona pellucida of the embryo to-be-infected after development, and placing in a microplate single-layer co-culture system to continue cultivation, (e) performing adenoviral vector packing and amplification by (i) collecting adenovirus supernatant by inoculating 293 cells into a 60 mm culture dish at a cell density of 70%, transfecting the cells using Lipofectamine (RTM: Not defined) 3000 reagent, adding pBHGloxdelE13cre and pDC316-eGFP plasmid in a ratio of 2:1, adding culture medium, observing the cells after transfection on the 10th day, where the 90% cells became round and 60% cells are detached from the bottom of the culture dish, collecting the cells using cell scraper, repeatedly freezing the cells, thawing using liquid nitrogen at 37 degrees C for 4-5 times, then centrifuging and collecting adenovirus supernatant, and (ii) performing adenovirus amplification by preparing 293 cells culture, adding adenovirus supernatant, amplifying the adenovirus, processing to obtain adenovirus supernatant and freezing the supernatant using refrigerator at -80 degrees C, (f) performing adenovirus titer dilution by obtaining 1 green fluorescent units (GFU)/ml adenovirus supernatant, serially diluting embryo culture medium (TCM199 and 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS)) 10 times to obtain 1x 10-1-1x 10-5 GFU/ml viral solution, respectively, and refrigerating at -80 degrees C, (g) infecting the buffalo granular cells using prepared adenovirus by (i) infecting the buffalo granular cells using 1x 10-1-1x 10-5 GFU/ml viral solution, respectively, observing the results using inverted fluorescence microscope after 24 hours, 48 hours and 72 hours respectively, and selecting optimal concentration of buffalo granulosa cells, and (ii) again infecting the selected concentration of buffalo granulosa cells using buffalo granule cell monolayer respectively, observing the results using inverted fluorescence microscope after 24 hours, 48 hours, 72 hours and 96 hours, respectively, and selecting optimal concentration of buffalo granulosa cells, obtaining buffalo granular monolayer cell, and culturing the cells, and (h) infecting embryo to-be-infected cultured in the microtiter plate monolayer co-culture system using optimal concentration of adenovirus solution to obtain adenovirus-mediated buffalo transgenic embryo.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Virus: The adenovirus is a recombinant adenovirus particle containing an enhanced green fluorescent protein (EGFP) gene. Preferred Components: The embryo culture solution is TCM199 and 10% FBS, 0.1-0.5% Grifola frondosa polysaccharide, and 0.01-0.05% Spirulina polysaccharide. Preferred Method: The culture solution in the step (a) is Dulbecco's modification of Eagle medium (DMEM) and 10% FBS.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN WO2018148581-A1; CA3052969-A1; IN201917035344-A; EP3579919-A1; US2020043592-A1; EP3579919-A4
TI Treating e.g. kidney failure in patient, by            obtaining several measurements, determining organ            demand and capacity scores, calculating demand and            capacity ratio, diagnosing patient as having organ            failure and administering organ support.
AU MEHTA R
   MEHTA R L
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
GA 201863737Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method (M1) for treating a patient, involves                (a) obtaining several measurements, determining an                organ demand score and an organ capacity score from                several measurements, calculating a demand and                capacity ratio by a clinical points ratio                calculation, a logistic ratio calculation, a                logistic additive ratio calculation, clinical index                product ratio calculation, a logistic product ratio                calculation, or their combinations, diagnosing the                patient as having organ failure and needing                intervention when the demand and capacity ratio is                in a target range, and administering organ support                to the diagnosed patient.
   USE - The method is useful for treating kidney                failure, liver failure, heart failure and lung                failure in a patient (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method (M1) for treating a patient, involves                (a) obtaining several measurements, determining an                organ demand score and an organ capacity score from                several measurements, calculating a demand and                capacity ratio by a clinical points ratio                calculation, a logistic ratio calculation, a                logistic additive ratio calculation, clinical index                product ratio calculation, a logistic product ratio                calculation, or their combinations, diagnosing the                patient as having organ failure and needing                intervention when the demand and capacity ratio is                in a target range, and administering organ support                to the diagnosed patient, or (b) obtaining several                measurements, determining an organ demand score and                an organ capacity score from several measurements,                dividing the organ demand score by the organ                capacity score to provide a demand and capacity                ratio, diagnosing the patient as needing                intervention based on the demand and capacity                ratio, and administering organ support to the                diagnosed patient when the demand and capacity                ratio is in a target range or terminating organ                support to the diagnosed patient when the demand                and capacity ratio is above or below the target                range. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) method (M2) for treating kidney failure in                a patient, involves obtaining at least a urine                sample, a blood sample, a liquid input value, and a                sequential organ failure assessment score for the                patient, determining a kidney demand score and a                kidney capacity score from several measurements,                determining a kidney capacity score from several                measurements, dividing the kidney demand score by                the kidney capacity score to provide a demand and                capacity ratio, diagnosing the patient as having                kidney failure and needing intervention when the                demand and capacity ratio is in a target range, and                administering dialysis to the diagnosed                patient;
   (2) a method (M3) for treating liver failure                in a patient, involves obtaining at least a blood                sample, an international normalized ratio (INR)                value, and a sequential organ failure assessment                score, determining a liver demand score from the                blood sample and the sequential organ failure                assessment score and a liver capacity score from                the blood sample and an INR value, dividing the                liver demand score by the liver capacity score to                provide a demand and capacity ratio, diagnosing the                patient as having liver failure and needing                intervention when the demand and capacity ratio is                in a target range, and administering molecular                adsorbents recirculating system (MARS), cell based                liver support, or their combination to the                diagnosed patient;
   (3) a method (M4) for treating heart failure                in a patient, involves obtaining at least a heart                rate, a blood pressure, a cardiac output, and a                sequential organ failure assessment score,                determining a heart demand score from the blood                sample and the sequential organ failure assessment                score and a heart capacity score from the heart                rate, the blood pressure, and the cardiac output,                dividing the heart demand score by the heart                capacity score to provide a demand and capacity                ratio, diagnosing the patient as having heart                failure and needing intervention when the demand                and capacity ratio is in a target range, and                administering a balloon pump, a right ventricular                assist devices (VAD), a heartmate, or their                combination to the diagnosed patient; and
   (4) a method (M5) for treating lung failure in                a patient, involves obtaining at least a blood                sample, partial pressure of oxygen (pO2), carbon                dioxide (CO2) removal value, a forced expiratory                volume in one second (FeV1) test, a pH value, and a                sequential organ failure assessment score,                determining a lung demand score from the blood                sample and the sequential organ failure assessment                score and a lung capacity score from the pO2, CO2                removal value, the FeV1 test, and the pH value,                dividing the lung demand score by the lung capacity                score to provide a demand and capacity ratio,                diagnosing the patient as having lung failure and                needing intervention when the demand and capacity                ratio is in a target range, and administering a                ventilator, extracorporeal carbon dioxide removal                (ECCOR), extracorporeal membrane oxygenation                (ECMO), or their combination to the diagnosed                patient.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In method (M2),                  calculating is performed by dividing the organ                  demand score by the organ capacity score to                  provide the demand and capacity ratio. At least                  obtaining, determining, and calculating steps can                  be repeated to provide a dynamic demand and                  capacity ratio. The steps are repeated at                  intervals of time. The dynamic demand and                  capacity ratio can be used to determine a change                  in therapy needed. At least one heart output                  measurement comprises heart rate, blood pressure                  or cardiac output. At least one blood sample is                  used to determine blood pH, bilirubin levels,                  albumin levels, or their combination. Each of at                  least one sequential organ failure assessment                  score, at least one urine sample, at least one                  blood sample, at least one urine sample, at least                  one blood sample, at least one liquid input                  value, at least one heart output measurement and                  at least one sequential organ failure assessment                  score has a result that is given a score. The                  organ support is molecular adsorbents                  recirculating system (MARS), cell based liver                  support, a balloon pump, a ventricular assist                  device (VAD), a heartmate, a ventilator, an ECMO,                  an ECCOR, dialysis, or their combination. The                  several measurements comprise at least one urine                  sample, at least one blood sample, at least one                  liquid input value, at least one heart output                  measurement, at least one sequential organ                  failure assessment score for the patient, or                  their combination. The method further involves                  diagnosing the patient as not needing                  intervention when the demand and capacity ratio                  is in a low range. The method further involves                  diagnosing the patient as having organ failure                  and intervention being futile when the demand and                  capacity ratio is in a high range. The score is                  0-5. The method further adjusting organ support                  to the diagnosed patient when the demand and                  capacity ratio deviates from an initial value.                  Preferred Organ: The organ failure is heart                  failure, lung failure, kidney failure, liver                  failure, brain failure, or their                  combination.
   ACTIVITY - Nephrotropic; Hepatotropic; Respiratory-Gen.;                Cardiant. Test details are described but no results                given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PT P
PN CN108102461-A
TI Ink useful for calligraphy, comprises e.g. pigment paste, bisphenol A, glycerin, anhydrous sodium sulfate, triethanolamine, triethylamine, acetone, bisthiocarbamate, active mineral powder, surfactant composition and deionized water.
AU 
AE TIANJIN JUNRU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201844990Q
AB    NOVELTY - Ink comprises e.g. pigment paste, bisphenol A, glycerin, anhydrous sodium sulfate, triethanolamine, triethylamine, acetone, bisthiocarbamate, active mineral powder, surfactant composition, release retarder, essential oil of Chinese medicine, and deionized water, where the pigment of the paste is high-pigment carbon black, and the active mineral powder is vermiculite, fluorspar, tourmaline, and zinc spinel, where the tourmaline is magnesium tourmaline and lithium tourmaline, where the essential oil of the traditional Chinese medicine is prepared by washing bitter fleas, naturally drying, and placing in a frying pan, removing bad odor, spraying deionized water, placing in a constant temperature oven and dry, washing lavender, soaking in brine, and steaming in the pot, removing and drying at concentration of salt water, crushing bowl flowers, musk, and phellodendron, sweet marjoram, removing impurities and pulverizing.
   USE - The ink is useful for calligraphy (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ink releases air anions for a long time, purifies air, and is beneficial to human health, has inhibition and killing rate against Staphylococcus, Escherichia coli, gram-positive bacteria and germs and fungi, soothes stress, leaves a lasting aroma and refreshing, and is suitable for people of all ages.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ink comprises 23 pts. wt. pigment paste, 19 pts. wt.bisphenol A, 4.5 pts. wt. glycerin, 2.3 pts. wt. anhydrous sodium sulfate, 2.7 pts. wt. triethanolamine, 3.3 pts. wt. triethylamine, 3.6 pts. wt. acetone, 0.25 pts. wt. bisthiocarbamate, 1.7 pts. wt. active mineral powder, 1.2 pts. wt. surfactant composition, 0.55 pts. wt. release retarder, 3.85 pts. wt. essential oil of Chinese medicine, and 62 pts. wt. deionized water, where the pigment of the paste is high-pigment carbon black, the average particle size of the pigment paste is 75 nm, and the active mineral powder is vermiculite, fluorspar, tourmaline, and zinc spinel in a in a weight ratio of 2.5:3:3.7:2, where the tourmaline is magnesium tourmaline and lithium tourmaline in a weight ratio of 5:3, where the particle size of active mineral powder is greater than or equal to 13 mu m, where the essential oil of the traditional Chinese medicine is prepared by (i) washing 35 pts. wt. bitter fleas, naturally drying, and placing in a frying pan at 90 degrees C, removing bad odor, spraying 17 pts. wt. deionized water, placing in a constant temperature oven and dry to below 60 degrees C and to a moisture content of greater than or equal to 6.5%; (ii) washing 17.5 pts. wt. lavender, soaking in brine for 3.5 hours, where the added amount of salt water is 3.7 times the mass of soaked material, and steaming in the pot, removing and drying at concentration of salt water in 27%; (iii) crushing 4.5 pts. wt. bowl flowers, 4.5 pts. wt. musk, and 1 pts. wt. phellodendron, 0.75 pts. wt. sweet marjoram, removing impurities, and together placing with the raw materials prepared in steps (i) and (ii) into an ultrafine pulverizer and pulverizing to 60 meshes to prepare raw material powders; (iv) placing the obtained raw material powder into the extraction kettle of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, and adjusting the extraction pressure to 27.65 MPa at 45-10 degrees C, where the carbon dioxide flow rate is 0.6 rri3/hour, and the dynamic extraction is 2.5 hours to obtain a directly separation kettle outlet with a triangular flask and collecting volatile oil A; (v) drying 13 pts. wt. jujube kernels, 15.5 pts. wt. wolfberry fruit, 8.5 pts. wt. elm bark, 2.5 pts. wt. chinese Torreya, 15.5 pts. wt. Eucalyptus, 4.5 pts. wt. calamus, placing into the ultrafine crusher to 70 mesh together, adding deionized water and soaking for 6 hours, adding water to 6.5 times the mass of the soaked raw material, dispersing and gradually mixing to obtain the mixture of traditional Chinese medicine; (vi) decocting the obtained traditional Chinese medicine mixture at 80 degrees C for 2 hours, collecting the distillate and adding diethyl ether, where the volume ratio of distillate to diethyl ether is 2:1, taking out the upper oil phase, and adding anhydrous sodium sulfate and drying to obtain the volatile oil B; and (vii) mixing the volatile oil A and the volatile oil B prepared in steps (iv) and (vi) to obtain the mixed oil, and adding the lemon essential oil, cypress essential oil, geranium essential oil, wheat sprout oil and mint oil at a mass ratio of 1.3:0.4: 0.75:0.6: 0.5:0.3, stirring for 35 minutes and allowing to stand for 30 minutes, uniformly stirring, filtering and removing impurities to obtain the traditional Chinese medicine essential oil.
DC G02 (Inks, paints, polishes â€“ polymer-based paints and inks are also classified in Section A (C09D, F, G).)

PT P
PN WO2018071372-A1; CA3046630-A1; EP3522733-A1
TI Extracting and isolating essential oil from biomass by combining and milling biomass with cover, dissolving essential oil in cover, forming mixture containing solution of essential oil in solvent and separating solution.
AU CARBERRY J
AE SUSTAINABLE AQUATICS INC (SUST-Non-standard)
   SUSTAINABLE AQUATICS INC (SUST-Non-standard)
GA 201830844H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting and isolating an essential oil from a biomass, involves combining and milling biomass with cover, reducing particulate size of the biomass, releasing the essential oil from the biomass to the cover, dissolving the essential oil in the cover, forming a mixture comprising solution of essential oil in solvent, and a milled byproduct biomass, and separating solution from the milled byproduct biomass.
   USE - The methods are useful for extracting and isolating essential oil from a biomass, producing solution of astaxanthin from H.pluvialis algae biomass, and preparing bioavailable essential oil enriched food additive or feed.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high yield with improved purity, and precludes or reduces thermochemical stress, oxidation, and contamination of the essential oils.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting and isolating an essential oil from a biomass, involves (a) combining a biomass comprising an essential oil with a cover within an attrition mill, where the essential oil is soluble in the cover, and the attrition mill comprises milling media, (b) milling the biomass and the cover in the attrition mill for a duration, (c) reducing the particulate size of the biomass during the milling by repeatedly contacting the biomass with the milling media, (d) releasing the essential oil from the biomass to the cover during the milling, (e) dissolving at least a portion of the essential oil released from the biomass in the cover during the milling, where the cover reduces the oxidation of the released essential oil in relation to the milling without the cover, (f) forming a mixture during the milling comprising a solution of the essential oil in a solvent, where the essential oil is a solute and the cover comprises the solvent, and a milled byproduct biomass, and (g) separating the solution from the milled byproduct biomass, where the solution comprises the essential oil. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: (1) method for producing a solution of astaxanthin from a Haematococcus pluvialis algae biomass, involves (a) combining an initial feedstock of healthy algae and nutrients in water, (b) amplifying the algae concentration in the water during a growth phase comprising supplying light from a light source and carbon dioxide to the initial feedstock, and supplying the nutrients in the water, (c) removing at least a portion of the nutrients from the water after the growth phase, (d) stressing the amplified algae by supplying additional light and carbon dioxide to the amplified algae to promote cyst formation by the amplified algae, (e) combining the amplified algae with ethanol in an attrition mill including milling media, (f) milling the amplified algae in the ethanol in the attrition mill to release astaxanthin to the ethanol, (g) reducing oxidation of the released astaxanthin with the ethanol, (h) dissolving at least a portion of the astaxanthin in the ethanol to form a mixture comprising a solution of astaxanthin in the ethanol, where the astaxanthin is a solute and the ethanol is a solvent, and a milled byproduct of the amplified and stressed algae, and (i) separating the solution from the milled byproduct of the amplified algae; and (2) method for preparing bioavailable essential oil enriched food additive or feed involves (a) (i) combining a biomass comprising an essential oil with a first cover within a mill, where the essential oil is soluble in the first cover, (ii) milling the biomass and the first cover in the mill to release the essential oil to the first cover, where the first cover reduces oxidation of the essential oil, and at least a portion of the essential oil dissolves in the first cover to produce a mixture comprising a solution of the essential oil in the first cover, where the essential oil is a solute and the first cover comprises a solvent, and a milled byproduct biomass, (iii) separating the solution from the milled byproduct biomass, (iv) blending the solution with an edible material, (v) milling the solution and the edible material to transfer at least a portion of the essential oil from the solvent to the edible material, and (vi) removing the solvent from the edible material to provide the bioavailable essential oil enriched feed or food additive as the edible material comprising transferred essential oil, or (b) (i) combining a biomass including an essential oil with a cover within a mill, (ii) milling the biomass and the cover in the mill to release the essential oil to the cover, (iii) washing the milled byproduct biomass with additional solvent for the essential oil to provide additional solution including the essential oil, and (iv) separating the solution and the additional solution from the milled byproduct biomass to provide the bioavailable essential oil enriched food additive or feed as the milled byproduct biomass.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further involves drying and shredding the biomass before combining the biomass with the cover. The method further involves mixing the essential oil with a liquefied gelling agent, forming the essential oil and the liquefied gelling agent mixture into one or more desired portions and shapes, and allowing the gelling agent to set. The method further involves blending the solution with an edible material, milling the solution and the edible material to transfer at least a portion of the essential oil from the solvent to the edible material, and removing the solvent from the edible material to provide a bioavailable essential oil enriched feed or food additive as the edible material comprising transferred essential oil. The method involves separating the solution from the milled byproduct biomass comprising settling the milled byproduct biomass from the solution, optionally by centrifugation. The method further involves washing the milled byproduct biomass with additional solvent for the essential oil to provide additional solution including the essential oil, and separating the solution and the additional solution from the milled byproduct biomass to provide a bioavailable essential oil enriched food additive or feed as the milled byproduct biomass. The method further involves reducing the particulate size of the essential oil by repeatedly contacting the released essential oil with the milling media. The milling duration continues until the average particulate diameter of the essential oil is from 100 nm-100 microns, preferably 100 nm-30 microns. The method further involves maintaining the temperature within the mill at 60 degrees C during a majority of the milling duration. The milling continues until aggregated particulates of the essential oil reach an average diameter of less than 3 microns. The method further involves maintaining the temperature within the mill below room temperature or more than 60 degrees C during a majority of the milling duration. The method further involves removing the mixture from the mill after the milling and before the separating. The method involves removing the mixture from the mill comprises rinsing the interior surfaces of the mill with additional cover. The milling continues until aggregated particulates of the essential oil reach an average diameter of less than 3 microns. The method involves separating the solution from the milled byproduct biomass by settling the milled byproduct biomass from the solution. The method involves performing settling process by subjecting the mixture to centrifugal force. The method further involves at least partially evaporating the solvent from the solution. The method further involves dosing the essential oil into carrier oil. The method further involves forming the essential oil into a powder. The method further involves drying and shredding the biomass before combining the biomass with the cover. The method further involves mixing at least one additional nutrient with the liquefied gelling agent, where the at least one additional nutrient comprises at least one of eicosapentaenoic acid and docosahexaenoic acid. The method further involves performing mixing process by mixing at least one additional ingredient with the liquefied gelling agent, where the additional ingredient is chosen from flavoring ingredient, coloring ingredient, preservative, and their combinations. The portion of the nutrients removed during the removing is at least 50% by weight in relation to the average nutrient concentration in the water during the growth phase. The method further involves adding salt to the water during at least one of the nutrient removal, stressing, or both nutrient removal and stressing process. The method further involves drying at least a portion of the water from the amplified algae before the combining the amplified algae with the ethanol in the mill. The method further involves harvesting the mixture from the mill after the dissolving, but before the separating, where the mill including interior surfaces. The method further involves harvesting the mixture from the mill after the dissolving, but before the separating by rinsing interior surfaces and the milling media of the mill with additional ethanol. The method involves performing separating process by settling solids of the milled byproduct from the solution by subjecting the mixture to centrifugal force. The method further involves at least partially evaporating the ethanol from the solution to form a carotenoid concentrate comprising the astaxanthin. The method involves supplying light from the light source to the initial feedstock during the growth phase conforms to a flashing pattern, where the light having a wavelength of 700-800 nm. The method further involves combining an additional ingredient in the mill before the milling, where the additional ingredient comprises eicosapentaenoic acid, docosahexaenoic acid, and their combinations. The method involves performing milling by controlling the temperature of the amplified algae within the attrition mill. The method further involves adding a second cover during the blending, where the second cover is different than the first cover. The method involves removing the solvent from the edible material by removing the second cover, settling the edible material, or evaporating the solvent, where the edible material is spirulina algae. The milled byproduct biomass is washed from 1 to 10 times with the additional solvent. The milled byproduct biomass is washed from 4-6 times with the additional solvent. The method involves washing by agitating the milled byproduct biomass with the additional solvent, where the additional solvent comprises a different solvent than the cover. The method involves performing separation by filtration, or forced settling. The method further involves blending the solution with an edible material. Preferred Components: The essential oil is chosen from astaxanthin, sea food extract, collagen extract, docosahexaenoic acid, eicosapentaenoic acid, capsaicin, dihydrocapsaicin, cannabinoids, lycopene, hop concentrate, germ oil, ginsenoside, oil of grape seed, lecithin, and pigment, preferably cannabinoids. The cover is chosen from olive oil, sunflower oil, fish oil, vegetable oil, ethanol, an oxidatively inert gas under the milling conditions, and their combinations. The cover comprises ethanol. The mill comprises interior surfaces, where the interior surfaces and the milling media non-reactive to the essential oil and the cover. The biomass comprises a material chosen from H.pluvialis algae, peppers, cannabis, garlic, tomatoes, hops, wheat, ginseng, and grapes. The biomass comprises a material chosen from green shell mussels, shark cartilage, shell fish, fish, collagen, and egg yolk. The biomass comprises H.pluvialis algae and the essential oil comprises astaxanthin. The cover comprises ethanol. The biomass comprises pepper and the essential oil comprises capsaicin compounds. The biomass comprises cannabis plant structures and the essential oil comprises cannabinoids. The carotenoid concentrate comprises 60-98 wt.% astaxanthin and canthaxanthin. The flashing pattern comprises comprising from 3 to 5 flashes per second. The edible material is a previously available feed or food additive lacking the essential oil.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. MC B04-A08G2; B04-A10G; B04-B01B; B04-B01C1; B04-N02; B05-B01P; B10-C04E2; B10-D03; B11-B03A; B15-A00; B15-A00A; D03-G01; D10-A05

   LI D
   GOU Y
   LI X

AE SHANGHAI SERUM BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022A3516A
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-tetanus monoclonal antibody composition                comprises 4 strains of monoclonal antibodies i.e.                S3A4, 9-7A4, 9-8C7, S1D2. Each cloned antibody is                composed of light chain and heavy chain                respectively.
   USE - The antibody composition is useful in                preparing medicines for preventing/treating tetanus                and producing tetanus toxoid antigen, as diagnostic                reagent in detecting product quality                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: uses 4 strains of monoclonal                antibodies with synergistic protective effect,                which is further used for Binding fragment                positioning and reaches the titer of its                synergistic neutralization and protection effect to                70 IU/mg after humanization and recombinant                expression.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing anti-tetanus monoclonal antibody                composition comprising (a) first immunization:                uniformly mixing 20 ug antigen with 200 &#956;l                complete Freund adjuvant, preparing into 400                &#956;l solution, injecting subcutaneous                multi-point mouse in mice, (b) performing 2nd to                5th immunization: uniformly mixing 20 ug antigen                with 200 &#956;l incomplete Freund adjuvant                after two weeks of last immunization interval,                configuring into 400 &#956;l solution,                injecting to Ba1b/c mouse subcutaneous multi-point,                collecting blood from the tail vein of the mouse,                after the fourth immunization and the serum titer                is more than 105, (c) strengthening immunity:                injecting 20 ug antigen into the tail vein of mice,                (d) taking immune mice, bleeding, neck quenching,                soaking for 3-4 minutes in 75% alcohol, taking out                the mouse spleen under sterile condition, placing                into 15 ml centrifugal tube, adding little                serum-free RPM 1640 culture solution, lightly                beating by using a pipetting, crushing, until there                is no tissue agglomeration and the cells are                uniform, (e) using serum free RPM 1640 culture                solution to wash the mouse spleen cell three times,                (f) taking the mouse myeloma cell of Sp2/0-Ag14 in                logarithmic phase, washing for three times in the                serum-free RPM 1640 culture solution, (g) mixing                the mouse spleen cell and Sp2/0 -Ag14 mouse myeloma                cell at a ratio of 10 1, centrifuging at 1500                revolutions/minute for 7 minutes, washing the                supernatant, (h) slowly adding 1 ml polyethylene                glycol in 1 minutes, lightly shaking for 90                seconds, adding 5 ml serum-free RPM1640 culture                solution in the 2.5 minutes, adding 5 ml serum-free                culture solution to stop the reaction, allowing to                stand for 5 minutes, centrifuging at 1280                revolutions/minute for 8 minutes, discarding,                adding conventional RPM 1640 culture solution                (containing 10% fetal calf serum), preparing into                cell suspension, (i) seeding the above cell                suspension into a 96-well plate at a density of                2x104 hole per well, each hole 200 ul, placing in                37&#8451;, 5% carbon dioxide cell culture box                for incubation, culturing for 24 hours, replacing                the conventional RPM 1640 culture liquid containing                HAT (25X), placing at 37&#8451;, 5% carbon                dioxide cell culture box for continuous incubation,                culturing for 14 days, screening, when the dilution                factor is less than 100 times and the OD450 is                greater than 1, (j) obtaining strain monoclonal                antibody S3A4, 9-7A4 9-8C7 and S1D2, and their                neutralizing titers are 52 IU/ml, 53 IU/ml, 58                IU/ml and 60 IU/ml, respectively, using ELISA                superposition to determine 4 monoclonal antibody of                the synergistic, (k) determining the synergy of the                four monoclonal antibodies by ELISA stacking                method, (l) extracting total RNA from four                monoclonal antibodies, obtaining variable region                coding sequence of 4 monoclonal antibody, (m)                randomly extracting 2 monoclonal antibody in the 4                monoclonal antibody, performing humanization                operations on their light chain nucleotide                sequences and heavy chain nucleotide sequences                respectively to obtain light chain V region protein                sequence and heavy chain V region protein sequence,                (n) ligating the front leader peptide sequence of                amino acid sequence kappa chain, human kappa chain                constant region and light chain V region protein                sequence in series, optimizing, obtaining the                nucleotides of the zumb light chain, (o) ligating                the nucleotide of the light chain, the front leader                peptide sequence of mouse kappa amino acid                sequence, human immunoglobulin G1 (IgG1) constant                region protein sequence and heavy chain V region                protein sequence in series, optimizing, obtaining                the nucleotides of the zumb light chain, (p)                performing gene synthesis on the nucleotides of the                zumb light chain and the zumb heavy chain,                subcloning them into the vector pCDNA3.1(+) to                obtain a monoclonal antibody composition, (q)                carrying out the establishment of a protein                expression stable strain with the monoclonal                antibody composition and (r) purifying the                monoclonal antibody composition after the protein                expression stable strain is established and                obtaining a safe and effective amount of                anti-tetanus monoclonal antibody composition.
