PT P
PN DE102020128968-A1
TI Controlling purity of drinking water during water treatment, useful in drinking water supply device with reverse osmosis unit used in e.g. households, comprises e.g. passing water through first line section entering from input connection.
AU PETERSEN J
   GLAUBITZ R
   NAGEL E
AE INVI WATER GMBH (INVI-Non-standard)
GA 202258889M
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling purity of drinking water during water treatment, comprises e.g. (1) flowing through a first line section with input stream of water entering from an input connection (30); (2) pre-filtering water downstream into first water purification unit for mechanical separation of solid particles dispersed in input stream of water to form pre-filtered stream of water; (3) separating pre-filtered stream of water downstream in a second water purification unit using reverse osmosis unit, into a partial stream of permeate stream of water further purified from dissolved solids and a retentate water associated with other partial flow; (4) discharging retentate stream of water downstream via a third line section for discharging through an outlet connection (40); (5) flowing the permeate stream of water through a second line section having a first determination unit arranged for determining a first aqueous concentration of dissolved solids.
   USE - The method is useful in a continuously and/or semi-continuously operable drinking water supply device (10) with a reverse osmosis unit, for use in households, gastronomy or the like, and in a drinking water purity control device. A mineral preparation useful as a prefabricated portion, as a cartridge in the drinking water purity control device and the drinking water supply device with reverse osmosis unit for purifying drinking water (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: effectively eliminates any contamination e.g. drug residues, microplastics, viruses, metal ions and/or nitrates from drinking water, preferably from tap water in a reliable manner or in a transparent manner; can be carried out at any time; assures wholesomeness and purity of portable water; is sustainable and achieves climate protection goals, preferably carbon dioxide transport balance; and completely and permanently replaces need to purchase bottled mineral water from retailers. The device: is reliable, fully automatic and convenient to use; enables cleaning of storage and/or maintenance in a simple manner; enables improved cooling efficiency, has smaller design, and improved carbonation; avoids formation of a microbiological film, thus prevents microbiological fouling on inner wall of storage tank; avoids overfilling of storage tank by carbon dioxide using emergency float switch; and enables cooling of device parts and checking of water that is fed in, automatically emptied and replaced or exchanged if necessary thus avoiding contamination of water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Controlling purity of drinking water during water treatment, comprises: (1) flowing through a first line section with an input stream of water entering from an input connection (30); (2) pre-filtering the input stream of water downstream into a first water purification unit for mechanical separation of solid particles dispersed in the input stream of water to form a pre-filtered stream of water; (3) separating the pre-filtered stream of water downstream in a second water purification unit using the reverse osmosis unit, which is activated by pressurization, into a partial stream of a permeate stream of water further purified from dissolved solids and a retentate water associated with other partial flow; (4) discharging the retentate stream of water downstream via a third line section for discharging through an outlet connection (40); (5) flowing the permeate stream of water through a second line section having a first determination unit arranged for determining a first aqueous concentration of dissolved solids; (6) determining the first aqueous concentration of dissolved solids in the permeate stream of water using the first determining unit; (7) checking, preferably automated checking by a control unit, to attain a predetermined constant, first target value for the first aqueous concentration of dissolved solids in the permeate stream water, preferably to achieve lower value; and (8) discarding the permeate flow of water using the control unit automated discarding, with respect to the negative case of the above step, which is based on an exceeding of the first target value by the first aqueous concentration of dissolved solids, by exiting via a fourth line section to the outlet connection. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a drinking water purity control device (1) comprising a control unit;
   (2) computer program loaded into a digital memory of a computer, preferably mobile, operator interface for a user, for carrying out the above method;
   (3) a drinking water supply device comprising a reverse osmosis unit;
   (4) a mineral preparation prepared as a prefabricated portion and as a cartridge designed for the drinking water supply device; and
   (5) a product set with at least one consumable and/or spare part, for controlling the drinking water purity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the system of the drinking water supply device with the drinking water purity control device for controlling the drinking water purity during drinking water treatment.
   Drinking water purity control device (1)
   Drinking water supply device (10)
   Input connection (30)
   Outlet connection (40)

PN CN113830740-A
TI Preparation of acid-base based on bipolar membrane of electrodialysis technology, by injecting high concentrated sodium chloride concentrated water into high concentration sodium chloride desalination liquid tank through security filter.
AU YANG P
   SHI X
   WANG D
AE ZHEJIANG ZHICHENG ENVIRONMENTAL RESOURCES TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202210302V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing acid-base based on bipolar membrane of electrodialysis technology, involves (a) injecting the high-concentration sodium chloride desalination liquid tank into the high-concentration sodium chloride desalination liquid tank through the lift pump, through the security filter, adopting a five-channel module by the bipolar membrane electrodialysis, assembling a bivalent selective anion membrane and a standard cationic membrane, (b) decreasing the salt content of the high-concentration sodium chloride concentrated water in channel C, (c) decreasing the concentration of 5% sodium bicarbonate solution in channel D, (d) forming three concentrates in channels A, B, and E, and (e) concentrating, overflowing it from the bicarbonate concentrated liquid tank, decomposing sodium bicarbonate, calcium bicarbonate and magnesium bicarbonate into sodium carbonate, precipitating calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate, and introducing carbon dioxide into sodium bicarbonate.
   USE - The method is used for preparing acid-base based on bipolar membrane of electrodialysis technology, where the alkali is useful in chemical production, chemical industry and metallurgy industrial waste water treatment, biochemical, organic electrochemical, industrial waste gas and other applications to bring revolutionary change.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses high sodium chloride containing concentrated water and sodium bicarbonate as raw material, realizes the resource utilization of the salt, at the same time, avoids the bipolar membrane alkali when divalent cation enters the alkali chamber to form fouling, and uses a divalent selective cathode film, improves the purity of hydrochloric acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing acid-base based on bipolar membrane of electrodialysis technology, involves (a) injecting pure water into the hydrochloric acid concentrated solution tank and the sodium hydroxide concentrated solution tank respectively, configuring an initial 5% concentration of sodium bicarbonate solution respectively in the sodium bicarbonate desalination tank and the bicarbonate concentrate tank, injecting the high-concentration sodium chloride desalination liquid tank into the high-concentration sodium chloride desalination liquid tank through the lift pump, through the security filter, adopting a five-channel module with a special structure by the bipolar membrane electrodialysis, assembling a bivalent selective anion membrane and a standard cationic membrane, when the concentrated water containing high sodium chloride enters the channel C of the bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit, the 5% concentration sodium bicarbonate solution enters from the channels D and E of the bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit respectively, after turning on the power, using the five channels of the bipolar membrane electrodialysis to form two desalination liquid channels C and D respectively, and three concentrated liquid channels A, B and E, and circulating in the electrodialysis unit through the circulating pump, (b) continuously decreasing the salt content of the high-concentration sodium chloride concentrated water in channel C, and after reaching the set target value, using the water tank to overflow to the next treatment unit for processing, (c) continuously decreasing the concentration of 5% sodium bicarbonate solution in channel D, after reaching the set target value, using the water tank that overflows and returns to the sodium bicarbonate tank, using the sodium bicarbonate tank to supplying with high concentration sodium bicarbonate solution to the sodium bicarbonate desalination liquid tank through the lifting pump and the security filter, (d) forming three concentrates in channels A, B, and E, respectively, where the channel A is a hydrochloric acid chamber formed by the combination of chloride ion in the channel C and the hydrogen ion dissociated from the channel A, which is concentrated to 8% concentration and overflows from the hydrochloric acid concentrated liquid tank to the next level treatment, where the channel B is a sodium hydroxide chamber formed by the combination of sodium ion in the channel D and the hydroxide ion dissociated from the channel B, after being concentrated to 8% concentration, it overflows from the sodium hydroxide concentrated liquid tank to the next level treatment, and where the channel E is a bicarbonate chamber of sodium bicarbonate, calcium bicarbonate and magnesium bicarbonate formed by the combination of sodium ion, calcium ion, magnesium ion in the channel C and bicarbonate ion in the channel D, and
   (e) concentrating to 10% concentration, overflowing it from the bicarbonate concentrated liquid tank, adjusting the pH to 9 with sodium hydroxide, decomposing by heating, decomposing sodium bicarbonate, calcium bicarbonate and magnesium bicarbonate into sodium carbonate, flowing calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate into the sedimentation tank, simultaneously precipitating calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate in the precipitation tank, where the supernatant is sodium carbonate solution, and introducing carbon dioxide into sodium bicarbonate and overflowing to the sodium bicarbonate tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flow chart of the preparation of acid and alkali based on the bipolar membrane of electrodialysis technology. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: In the five-channel module of the bipolar membrane electrodialysis, the five strands of solutions are separated by ion exchange membranes, and are not in contact with each other, and the five-channel electrodialysis can produce high-purity hydrochloric acid and sodium hydroxide solutions under the action of an external direct current (DC) electric field. A bivalent selective anion membrane is used to intercept sulfate radicals in concentrated water containing high sodium chloride to improve the purity of hydrochloric acid. For the treatment of concentrated water containing high sodium sulfate, the monovalent selective anion membrane is replaced with a common anion membrane, and sulfuric acid and sodium hydroxide solutions can be obtained.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC D04-A01; E31-F03; E33-A01; E33-D; E34-B02; E31-B01; E11-N

PT P
PN CN111960826-A; CN111960826-B
TI Preparing ceramic cup comprises e.g. using silicon carbide, silicon nitride, cubic boron nitride, zirconium oxide as raw materials for ceramic firing, filtering, taking the filter residue, drying and sintering at constant temperature.
AU HOU Q
   ZHANG K
AE JINGDEZHEN QINGRONG HEXIN CERAMICS CO (JING-Non-standard)
GA 2020B9280V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ceramic cup comprises e.g. using silicon carbide, silicon nitride, cubic boron nitride, zirconium oxide as raw materials for ceramic firing, and using ultra-fine composite powder and pore formers as auxiliary materials for firing, placing the ceramic firing raw materials, ultra-fine composite powder, and pore-forming agent into a ball mill in a mass ratio of 95-100:3-7:2-5, adding mixed solution of ethanol and acetone of 0.5 times the total mass of the above raw and auxiliary materials, where the mass ratio of ethanol and acetone is 1:0.5-2, ball milling the mixture in the above ball milling tank at rate of 200-500 rpm for 22-35 minutes, filtering, taking the filter residue, repeating the above ball milling process, repeating for 3-5 times, filtering and drying, using 400 meshes screen and sieving the powder, adding the above mentioned powder to a ceramic cupping pot and compacting and sintering at constant temperature for 12-15 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ceramic cup.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids the pain caused by the negative pressure in the traditional cupping pot; can make the health care people happy and accepted by the wide and large health care.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ceramic cup comprises using silicon carbide, silicon nitride, cubic boron nitride, zirconium oxide as raw materials for ceramic firing, and using ultra-fine composite powder and pore formers as auxiliary materials for firing, placing the ceramic firing raw materials, ultra-fine composite powder, and pore-forming agent into a ball mill in a mass ratio of 95-100:3-7:2-5, adding mixed solution of ethanol and acetone of 0.5 times the total mass of the above raw and auxiliary materials, where the mass ratio of ethanol and acetone is 1:0.5-2, ball milling the mixture in the above ball milling tank at rate of 200-500 rpm for 22-35 minutes, filtering, taking the filter residue, repeating the above ball milling process, repeating for 3-5 times, filtering and drying, using 400 meshes screen and sieving the powder, adding the above mentioned powder to a ceramic cupping pot and compacting at pressure of 25 MPa, increasing heating rate to 1600 plus minus 5 degrees C at 8-10 degrees c/min and sintering at constant temperature for 12-15 hours, then passing in filtered compressed air as cooling medium for quenching treatment, releasing the pressure, vacuumizing to -0.1 Mpa, adding 95-97 wt.% oxygen and carbon dioxide gas mixture makes the pressure in the reaction chamber 1.5-2.5 Mpa, and controlling the heating rate to 4-6 degrees C/minutes, raising slowly and uniformly from room temperature to sintering temperature 1600 plus minus 5 degrees C and insulating at constant for 4 hours, injecting liquid carbon dioxide to drop to room temperature at constant rate of 2-4 degrees C/minutes to obtain semi-finished ceramic cupping pot. The ceramic cupping pot body is prepared by placing semi-finished ceramic cup obtained in the step (i) in an evaporation machine at speed of 1-3 revolutions/minutes (rpm) and evaporating metallic silver for 30-60 minutes, making the outer surface and inner surface respectively vapor-deposit uniform and dense vapor-deposition layer, adding carbon powder and aluminum oxide powder with mass ratio of 1:1-3 into deionized water, stirring, mixing and ball milling for 6-10 hours to obtain suspension with close particle size and uniform dispersion, slowly immersing the semi-finished ceramic cupping pot and evaporating into the suspension, ensuring that the inner surface and outer surface are completely immersing in the suspension, and maintaining the immersion time for 10-15 seconds, taking out, drying and sintering at 1600 plus minus 5 degrees C to obtain body of the ceramic cup. The ceramic cup comprises ceramic cupping pot body and ignition seat. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for method of using the ceramic cup, comprising placing the ignition seat at Shenque, Tanzhong, Mingmen, Shendai and jade pillow points respectively, taking the cotton ball and immersing in the ignition material, immersing for 3-5 seconds, taking out and placing on five ignition seats and igniting, taking five ceramic cup bodies and placing them on the ignition seat accurately and gently, adsorbing to the skin immediately, taking the cotton ball and soaking in the ignition material for 6-8 seconds, taking out and placing five ceramic cupping pots on the outside of the bottom of the body, and igniting, when the cotton ball on the outside of the bottom of the ceramic cup body burns, removing the ceramic cup body and carrying out detoxification treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diameter of the ceramic cup body is 6-20 cm, and the height is 10-15 cm. The ignition seat is a tripod structure. The raw material for ceramic firing is a mixture of silicon carbide, silicon nitride, cubic boron nitride, and zirconium oxide with a mass ratio of 8-10:4-7:5-8:3-5. The pore-forming agent is mixture of silicon carbonate, ammonium bicarbonate, and ammonium chloride with a mass ratio of 1:3-5:2-4. Preferred Method: The ultrafine composite powder is prepared by selecting mixture of polynitrosilane and polyborosilazane organic polymer with a volume ratio of 1:1-2 as the organic precursor, and dissolving ethanol and toluene solution in a mass ratio of 1:1, adding divinylbenzene, fully stirring and mixing for 25-35 minutes, mixing organic precursor, ethanol and toluene mixed solution and divinylbenzene, and transferring to the high-temperature reactor in a volume ratio of 8:1-3:2-3, heating at 220 plus minus 2 degrees C at rate of 2-5 degrees C/minutes, maintaining the reaction at constant temperature for 15-20 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature, centrifuging at speed of 3000-3500 rpm, taking the centrifugal sediment, filtering, drying and superfinely pulverizing to within 25 mu m, pyrolyzing under vacuum at 900-1300 degrees C for 1-3h to obtain ultrafine composite powder. The ignition material is prepared by fennel, radix scutellariae, sandalwood, graphite powder, and calcium chloride powder with mass ratio of 1-3:2-3:1-3:2-4:2-5:2-4, uniformly mixing, ultra-micro crushing to 20-50 mu m, adding 6-10 times of absolute ethyl alcohol, fully stirring and dissolving, where the dissolving liquid is ignition material, and packaging.

PT P
PN CN111512806-A; CN111512806-B
TI Growing seedlings of rose plant factory involves dividing production area into seedling insertion area, rooting area, buffer zone, seedling area and finished product area, using automatic seedling cutting machine to trim rose branches.
AU ZHOU L
   CAI Y
   ZHANG Y
   YANG L
   ZHAO B
   HUANG S
AE SHANGHAI AGRIC SCI ACAD (SHNY-C)
GA 202079651C
AB    NOVELTY - Growing seedlings of rose plant factory involves dividing the production area into seedling insertion area, rooting area, buffer zone, seedling area and finished product area, using automatic seedling cutting machine to trim rose branches to obtain semi-lignified branches, cutting trimmed semi-lignified branches into cuttings of 2-3 cm each. The only one bud point is left on the cuttings. The upper cut is 0.2-0.5 cm from the bud, and the lower cut is 1.5-2.5 cm from the bud. The upper and lower cuts are both flat, and kept the whole leaf on the bud point, the leaves should be complete, stretched and moist. The water-retaining substrate is choose, water the night before the cutting, and water again before the cutting to achieve the maximum water content of the substrate. The cuttings are dipped in the solution for preventing and controlling diseases and insect pests, and then the rooting agent is dipped in the cutting depth.
   USE - Method for growing seedlings of rose plant factory.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to grow seedlings of rose plant factory that has low labor cost, fast rooting efficiency, short breeding period, high seedling uniformity, low disease incidence, large unit area seedling amount, and keeps the excellent property of the parent complete, improves the enterprise production profit, and meets the supply requirement of the market and market demand of the quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Growing seedlings of rose plant factory involve dividing the production area into seedling insertion area, rooting area, buffer zone, seedling area and finished product area, using automatic seedling cutting machine to trim rose branches to obtain semi-lignified branches, cutting trimmed semi-lignified branches into cuttings of 2-3 cm each. The only one bud point is left on the cuttings. The upper cut is 0.2-0.5 cm from the bud, and the lower cut is 1.5-2.5 cm from the bud. The upper and lower cuts are both flat, and kept the whole leaf on the bud point, the leaves should be complete, stretched and moist. The water-retaining substrate is choose, water the night before the cutting, and water again before the cutting to achieve the maximum water content of the substrate. The cuttings are dipped in the solution for preventing and controlling diseases and insect pests, and then the rooting agent is dipped in the cutting depth. The cutting depth is based on the bud point just touching the pot soil. The cutting process is carried out in the shaded seedling area. The cuttings are inserted into the potting soil vertically, and covered the cuttings with a layer of non-woven fabric, sprayed a certain amount of water on the non-woven fabric, and then covered with a layer of film. The film has a number of air-permeable small holes in the wrong position. The inserted cuttings are moved to the rooting area, and taken root for 6-9 days without watering during rooting. The humidity is between 70-80%, the light intensity is between 2500-3000lx, and the light is filled in time if it is below 1000lx, and the shading net is opened in time for shade if it is higher than 3000lx. The seedlings are tempered in the rooting area, the film is opened, the seedlings are exercised by air, cooling and water control measures, and then covered again, and the rooting of the cuttings is always observed. The film is used, pushed the seedbed to the buffer zone, removed the non-woven fabric the next night, and kept the seedlings with sufficient moisture before removing the non-woven fabric. The temperature of the buffer zone is controlled at around 25 degrees C. The light intensity is controlled between 8000-10000lx and not more than 15000lx, and the humidity is controlled at 75-90%. The water-soluble fertilizer is added every morning during the buffer period. The seedling bed is put in the seedling area and water it twice a day. The light intensity is about 18000-20000lx and carbon dioxide gas is added every morning. The each batch of seedlings in the small seedling area is sprayed with anti-disease and insect pests. The first seedling trimming is carried out after 30 days of growth in the small seedling area, and the pruned branches are used for subsequent cuttings. The finished product area is watered twice a day with fertilizer and water, and carbon dioxide gas is appropriately added to promote plant growth, and observed the growth of the plants every day. If the plants are diseased, and removed in time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The water-retaining substrate is peat, the pH value is between 5.1-5.6, and the EC value is lower than 3.5 mS/cm. The solution for preventing and controlling diseases and insect pests comprises 2000 times liquid of Ekamite and 1000 times liquid of bacteriostat, and the rooting agent is A mixture of NAA and IBA.
DC P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PT P
PN US2020032155-A1; CA3012804-A1; US10883060-B2
TI Processing petroleum cokes comprises providing solid green petroleum coke, solubilizing a soluble portion of the solid green petroleum coke in a basic subcritical aqueous solubilization liquid and precipitating recovered solids fraction.
AU NASSAR N N
   MANASRAH A D
   VITALE G
AE UTI LP (UTIU-C)
   UTI LP (UTIU-C)
GA 202009501Y
AB    NOVELTY - Processing (M1) petroleum cokes, comprises: providing a solid green petroleum coke comprising a carbonaceous component and a transition metal component; solubilizing a soluble portion of the solid green petroleum coke in a basic subcritical aqueous solubilization liquid under solubilization conditions that comprise a solubilization pressure of at least 500 psi , a solubilization temperature of 220-240 degrees C, and an added solubilization base concentration effective to maintain a solubilization pH of greater than or equal to 8, for an effective solubilization time of not greater than 2 hours, in the presence of an oxidizing atmosphere comprising more than 21% oxygen, with agitation to generate a stirred reaction region in the solubilization liquid having a Reynolds number of greater than or equal to 10000; and precipitating a recovered solids fraction from the pregnant solubilization solution by lowering the pH to less than or equal to 6.
   USE - The method is useful for processing petroleum cokes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing (M1) petroleum cokes, comprises: providing a solid green petroleum coke comprising a carbonaceous component and a transition metal component, where the carbonaceous component comprises polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons, and the solid green petroleum coke has an elemental composition including 80-98 wt.% carbon, 1-8 wt.% hydrogen, 2.5-10 wt.% oxygen, 0.5-1 wt.% nitrogen, 1-10 wt.% sulfur, 0.001-0.8 wt.% vanadium, 0.001-0.5 wt.% iron, 0.001-0.5 wt.% nickel, 0.001-0.1 wt.% molybdenum and 0.01-1 wt.% cobalt; solubilizing a soluble portion of the solid green petroleum coke in a basic subcritical aqueous solubilization liquid under solubilization conditions that comprise a solubilization pressure of at least 500 psi , a solubilization temperature of 220-240 degrees C, and an added solubilization base concentration effective to maintain a solubilization pH of greater than or equal to 8, for an effective solubilization time of not greater than 2 hours, in the presence of an oxidizing atmosphere comprising more than 21% oxygen, with agitation to generate a stirred reaction region in the solubilization liquid having a Reynolds number of greater than or equal to 10000, so as to solubilize at least 75% of the solid green petroleum coke leaving a leached solid residue and producing a pregnant solubilization solution comprising a solubilized organics fraction, where the solubilization conditions and effective solubilization time are selected so that the solubilization reaction has a selectivity for production of the solubilized organics fraction over a carbon dioxide gas fraction of at least 80%, and at least 25% of the sulfur in the solid green petroleum coke reports to the leached solid residue, and not greater than 60% of the transition metal component of the solid green petroleum coke reports to the pregnant solubilization solution as a dissolved transition metallic component; and precipitating a recovered solids fraction from the pregnant solubilization solution by lowering the pH to less than or equal to 6, where the recovered solids fraction comprises greater than or equal to 80% of total organic carbon (TOC) present in the pregnant solubilization solution as the solubilized organics fraction, and the recovered solids fraction comprises an acid precipitated transition metal component, where the precipitated transition metal component makes up a smaller weight percent fraction of the recovered solids fraction than the weight percent fraction of the transition metal component in the solid green petroleum coke. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for catalytically processing (M2) petroleum cokes, comprising providing the solid green petroleum coke comprising the carbonaceous component and the transition metal component, where the carbonaceous component comprises polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons; solubilizing a soluble portion of the solid green petroleum coke in a basic subcritical aqueous solubilization liquid under solubilization conditions that comprise a solubilization pressure of at least 500 psi , a solubilization temperature of 150-230 degrees C, and an added solubilization base concentration effective to maintain a solubilization pH of less than or equal to 8, for less than 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6 hours, in the presence of an oxidizing atmosphere comprising more than 21% oxygen, and in the presence of a copper tetrasilicate catalyst, with agitation to generate a stirred reaction region in the solubilization liquid, so as to solubilize at least 90% of the solid green petroleum coke leaving a leached solid residue and producing a pregnant solubilization solution comprising a solubilized organics fraction, where the solubilization conditions and effective solubilization time are selected so that the solubilization reaction has a selectivity for production of the solubilized organics fraction over a carbon dioxide gas fraction of at least 85%, and precipitating a recovered solids fraction from the pregnant solubilization solution by lowering the pH to less than or equal to 6, where the recovered solids fraction comprises less than or equal to 80% of TOC present in the pregnant solubilization solution as the solubilized organics fraction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The methods (M1) and (M2) further comprise blending the recovered solids fraction with a virgin petcoke. Preferred Components: In the methods (M1) and (M2): the solubilization pressure is less than or equal to 1000 psi ; the added solubilization base is a hydroxide including potassium hydroxide or sodium hydroxide; the solubilization pH is less than or equal to 13; and the recovered solids fraction has an ignition temperature of not greater than 450 degrees C, volatile material content of more than 15 wt.%, a volatile material to fixed carbon ratio of at least 0.2 and a heating value of at least 25 MJ/kg. In the method (M1): the effective solubilization time is not greater than 1 hour; the oxidizing atmosphere comprises at least 50% oxygen and at least 90% oxygen; not greater than 50% of the transition metal component of the solid green petroleum coke reports to the pregnant solubilization solution as the dissolved transition metal component; the precipitated transition metal component comprises less than or equal to 1% of the recovered solids fraction; the added solubilization base is potassium hydroxide, and the solubilization conditions comprise a mass ratio of solid green petroleum coke to potassium hydroxide is 0.5:1-5:1, preferably 1:1; and the agitation comprises stirring at less than or equal to 500 rpm. In the method (M2): the recovered solids fraction comprises humic acid analog compounds; the copper tetrasilicate catalyst comprises a Gillespite group-type solid catalyst; the solid catalyst is characterized by nanocrystalline domain sizes; the copper tetrasilicate catalyst is at least one of cuprorivaite, wesselsite and/or effenbergerite; the copper tetrasilicate catalyst is prepared by co-precipitation-thermal treatment or hydrothermal treatment; the effective solubilization time is not greater than 2 hours; the oxidizing atmosphere comprises at least 50% oxygen; not greater than 50% of the transition metal component of the solid green petroleum coke reports to the pregnant solubilization solution as a dissolved transition metal component; and the recovered solids fraction comprises an acid precipitated transition metal component that comprises less than or equal to 1% of the recovered solids fraction.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H08 (Petroleum products, other than fuels and lubricants - this includes hydraulic fluids and electrical oils even when of non-petroleum origin (C10M).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Zero-emission treatment method of copper smelting            sulfuric acid waste water comprises e.g. calcium            removal stage by adding the filtrate obtained after            lime reaction into the middle section of sulfuric acid            wastewater treatment process.
AU DU Y
   DU D
   DONG J
   CHEN S
   XIE Y
AE HUBEI JINRUNDE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (HUBE-Non-standard)
   UNIV SOUTH CENT NATIONALITIES (USOC-C)
GA 201939144R
AB    NOVELTY - Zero-emission treatment method of copper                smelting sulfuric acid waste water comprises (1)                calcium removal stage by adding the filtrate                obtained after lime reaction into the middle                section of the sulfuric acid wastewater treatment                process, and mixing the carbon dioxide gas with the                filtrate through a jet device, carrying out                reaction, measuring the pH value to 9-9.5, and                carrying out precipitation and sand filtration, so                as to obtain a calcium carbonate thick slurry and a                filtrate a, where the concentration of calcium ions                in the filtrate an is 1-5 mg/l, (2) removing                arsenic and fluorine, namely adding an aluminum                sulfate solution or an aluminum chloride solution                into the filtrate an obtained in the step, carrying                out arsenic removal and fluorine reaction, and then                carrying out two-stage acid adding to adjust the pH                value, the pH value of the two-stage regulation is                7-8 and 6-7 respectively, reacting to be stable,                precipitating.
   USE - The method is useful for zero-emission                treatment method of copper smelting sulfuric acid                waste water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical; and avoids the                additional crystallization of concentrated                water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Zero-emission treatment method of copper                smelting sulfuric acid waste water comprises (1)                calcium removal stage by adding the filtrate                obtained after lime reaction into the middle                section of the sulfuric acid waste acid wastewater                treatment process, and mixing the carbon dioxide                gas with the filtrate through a jet device,                carrying out reaction, measuring the pH value to                9-9.5, and carrying out precipitation and sand                filtration, so as        used for removing arsenic, and the sodium sulfate                concentrated water generated in the step and the                step is respectively concentrated, sodium chloride                concentrated water, and carrying out intermittent                sequence type arsenic removal by adopting ferric                salt, after arsenic removal, the arsenic removal                agent enters the reuse points for reuse                respectively, removing arsenic from sodium sulfate                concentrated water is ferric sulfate and sodium                hydroxide, where the reaction pH is 8-9, and the                molar ratio of iron and arsenic is 10-13, the                intermittent sequence type arsenic removal reaction                comprises feeding water and measuring arsenic,                adding an iron salt medicament, adding liquid                alkali to adjust the pH value, precipitating and                filtering, aluminum salt arsenic slag and iron salt                arsenic slag generated by the water and removing                arsenic and subjecting to solidification treatment                and safe landfill.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pH value of the                  filtrate after the lime reaction is added is                  larger than 12, the content of calcium ions is                  600-800 mg/l, and the content of arsenic is 1-20                  mg/l, the content of the fluorine element is                  10-80 mg/l, and the TDS is 10-20 g/l. PT P
PN CN114381427-A
TI Performing in vitro expansion of natural killer cells for in vitro ADCC experiment, involves transferring the human interleukin 15 plasmid combined with human membrane and the human 4-1BBL plasmid into K562 cell in electrotransfection mode.
AU GU J
   NIE S
   WAN Y
   LIN Y
   LI C
AE SHANGHAI YAOMING BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI WUXI BIOPHARM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022625980
AB    NOVELTY - Performing in vitro expansion of natural killer (NK) cells involves transferring the human interleukin 15 (IL15) plasmid combined with human membrane and the human 4-1BBL plasmid into human immortalized myelogenous leukemia line (K562) cell in electrotransfection mode, adding antibiotics to maintain stable overexpression of target gene, obtaining K562 cell pool K562-mbIL15-4-1BBL of human mbIL15 and human 4-1BBL overexpression, and detecting the overexpression condition of the target gene.
   USE - Method for performing in vitro expansion of NK cells is used in an in vitro ADCC experiment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to find the NK cell in-vitro amplification method which is low in cost, simple and convenient to operate, high-efficient and stable, provides a substantial data basis for the efficacy evaluation of the amplified NK cells in ADCC, and relieve the limitation of research and development of NK-related drugs due to the shortage of NK cell sources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing in vitro expansion of natural killer (NK) cells involves transferring the human interleukin 15 (IL15) plasmid combined with human membrane and the human 4-1BBL plasmid into human immortalized myelogenous leukemia line (K562) cell in electrotransfection mode, adding antibiotics to maintain stable overexpression of target gene, obtaining K562 cell pool K562-mbIL15-4-1BBL of human mbIL15 and human 4-1BBL overexpression, and detecting the overexpression condition of the target gene. The K562-mbIL15-4-1BBL cells in logarithmic growth phase is taken, cell density is adjusted after centrifugation, mitomycin C is added, and placed in a 37degrees Celsius, 5% carbon dioxide (CO2) incubator for 1 hour, and cells are washed with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) to remove residual mitomycin C, and density of trophoblast cells and density of fresh peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) is adjusted. The 1 mL of K562-mbIL15-4-1BBL trophoblast cells and PBMC are mixed and cultured in one well of a six-well plate after treatment, named NK-K562-IL15-4-1BBL, recombinant human interleukin-2 (rhIL-2) is added, and placed in a 37degrees Celsius, and 5% CO2 incubator for normal culture. The survival rate and number of cells are counted by cell counter from the next day, and state of cells and color of culture medium are observed at the same time. The cells are centrifuged and passaged at a cell density of 2x106 cells/mL when the cell density reached 1x107 cells/mL. The medium is RPMI-1640 complete medium and comprises 200 international unit/milliliter (IU/mL) of rhIL-2. The cells are centrifuged and replaced with fresh medium, and placed in a 37degrees Celsius, 5% CO2 incubator for normal culture when the color of the medium turns yellow. The half of the NK cells are taken on the seventh day of expansion to continue to expand and culture, and named as NK-K562-IL15-4-1BBL-1. The other half of NK cells and mitomycin C-treated trophoblast cells are mixed and cultured at a ratio of 1:1, and named as NK-K5 62-IL15-4-1B BL-2. The cell state and color of the medium is observed every day, and cell counter is used to detect the cell viability and number. The cell state, viability and number reach the best when the NK cells expand to about the fifteenth day.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an application of the method for in vitro expansion of NK cells, which is used in an in vitro antibody-mediated isothermal amplification (ADCC) experiment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The overexpression of target gene is detected by flow cytometry, where Fc blocking agent is added into K562-mbIL15-4-1BBL cell suspension, reaction is performed at room temperature, anti-IL-15 antibody and the anti-4-1 BBL antibody are added, incubation is performed at 4degrees Celsius, the corresponding fluorescent secondary antibodies are added after the cells are washed, and binding of anti-IL-15 antibody and anti-4-1 BBL antibody to the K562-mbIL15-4-1BBL cells is detected. The mean fluorescence intensity of cells is measured by flow cytometry and analyzed by FlowJo software. The Fc blocking agent is added in amount of 5 microliter (microL), reaction is performed at room temperature for 10 minutes, and 1 microg/mL anti- interleukin-15 (anti-IL-15) antibody and 1 microg/mL anti-4-1 BBL antibody are added and incubated at 4degrees Celsius for 1 hour. The cell density is adjusted to 2x106 cells/mL after centrifugation, 50 microg/mL mitomycin C is added, cells are incubated in a 37degrees Celsius, 5% CO2 incubator for 1 hour, cells are washed with PBS to remove residues of mitomycin C, resuspend in RPMI-1640 complete medium, density of trophoblast cells is adjusted to 5x106 cells/mL, and density of fresh PBMCs is adjusted to 5x106 cells/mL with RPMI-1640 complete medium, where addition of described rhIL-2 is 200 IU/mL. The proportion of NK cells in the expanded NK cells are detected by flow cytometry. The cell suspension of the second week of expansion is taken, Fc blocking agent are added, reacted at room temperature, and fluorescein isothiocyanate (FITC) labeled anti-human CD3 direct-labeled antibody and phycoerythrin (PE) labeled anti-human CD56 direct-labeled antibody are added and incubated at 4degrees Celsius for 1 hour. The cells are detected by flow cytometry after washing the cells, and flow cytometry is used to analyze the proliferation of NK cells after expansion using FlowJo software.Preferred Composition: The antibiotic is 0.5 mg/mL hygromycin B and 10 microgram/milliliter (microg/mL) blasticidin. The addition amount of the Fc blocking agent is 5 microL, the addition amount of the FTIC-labeled anti-human CD3 direct-labeled antibody is 2 microL, and amount of PE labeled anti-human CD56 direct-labeled antibody is 2 microL.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN113956012-A; CN113956012-B
TI Preparation method of full solid waste steel pipe concrete containing waste incineration fly ash, involves mixing composite gel material, fine aggregate, coarse aggregate, bean curd waste water, and water reducing agent.
AU SHI L
   DONG L
   QU Y
   ZHANG H
   PING H
   JING J
   MA J
   CHEN J
   LI X
   TIAN X
   ZHANG K
   WANG F
   WANG C
   DI Y
AE TIANJIN TIANXING FUDA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV HEBEI ENG (UYEG-C)
   CHINA RAILWAY CONSTR GROUP CO LTD (CREN-C)
GA 202219335B
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises (a) crushing steel slag to 5-20 mm, carbonizing, drying, crushing, and sieving 5-10 mm steel slag particles as coarse aggregate and sieving less than 5 mm steel slag particles, (b) sieving molybdenum tailings, drying, taking dried molybdenum tailings particles larger than 0.074mm as fine aggregate, and putting dried 0.038-0.074mm molybdenum tailings particles for future use, (c) drying waste incineration fly ash, then putting into ball mill together with the less than 5mm steel slag particles and 0.038-0.074 mm molybdenum tailing particles, and grinding, (d) drying the refining slag and vanadium titanium slag, and grinding, (e) drying phosphogypsum and grinding, and (f) mixing the powder material obtained from steps (c), (d) and (e), obtaining composite gel material, (g) uniformly mixing the composite gel material, fine aggregate and coarse aggregate, then adding bean curd wastewater and water reducing agent.
   USE - Preparation method of full solid waste steel pipe concrete containing waste incineration fly ash used in incineration power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of solving the rubbish incineration fly ash and large industrial solid waste are harmless, reduction, resource difficulty, so as to change waste into valuable, making the solid waste generate higher economic value, realizing solid waste green sustainable development, energy-saving and environment-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method comprises (a) crushing steel slag to 5-20 mm by jaw crusher, putting into a carbonizing tank for carbonizing for 60-72 hours, where carbonizing condition comprises carbon dioxide concentration of 15%, temperature of 20plus minus1 degrees Celsius, and humidity of 85plus minus1 %, drying steel slag after carbonization for 12 hours at 105 degrees Celsius, then putting into hollow ball mill for crushing and shaping, crushing for 30-60 minutes at 48 revolutions/minute rotating speed, sieving 5-10 mm steel slag particles as coarse aggregate for further use, sieving less than 5 mm steel slag particles for future use, (b) sieving molybdenum tailings to obtain particles more than 0.074mm and 0.038-0.074mm particles, respectively drying for 12 hours at 105 degrees Celsius, taking the dried molybdenum tailings particles larger than 0.074mm as fine aggregate, putting the dried 0.038-0.074mm molybdenum tailings particles for future use, (c) drying the waste incineration fly ash at 105 degrees Celsius for 12 hours, then putting into the ball mill together with the less than 5mm steel slag particles and 0.038-0.074 mm molybdenum tailing particles after drying, grinding to 350-500 m2/kg, (d) respectively drying the refining slag, and vanadium titanium slag at 105 degrees Celsiusfor 12 hours, and grinding to 500-650m2/kg, (e) drying the phosphogypsum at 105 degrees Celsiusfor 12 hours, grinding the dried material powder to 400-500 m2/kg, (f) mixing the powder material obtained from steps (c), (d) and (e), obtaining composite gel material, (g) uniformly mixing the composite gel material, fine aggregate and coarse aggregate to obtain mixed material, then adding the bean curd wastewater and water reducing agent after refrigerating and filtering at 4-6 degrees Celsius, using stirrer to uniformly stir to obtain mixed slurry, pouring the mixed slurry, demolding, maintaining, obtaining the concrete product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the preparation method of full-solid waste steel pipe concrete containing waste incineration fly ash. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The molybdenum tailings comprises 35-70 mass% silicon oxide, 5-20 mass% aluminum oxide, 1-7 mass% magnesium oxide, 1-8 mass% calcium oxide, 1-12 mass% iron oxide, 0.01-3 mass% sodium oxide, 0.01-3 mass% potassium oxide, and 0.01-3 mass% phosphorus oxide. The waste comprises 25-45 mass% calcium oxide, 1-10 mass% silicon oxide, 1-5 mass aluminum oxide, 0.1-5 mass% iron oxide, 1-8 mass% magnesium oxide, 0.01-2 mass% iron (II) oxide, 1-7 mass% sodium oxide, 1-7 mass% potassium oxide, and 10-30 mass% chlorine.The water reducing agent is polycarboxylic acid series, naphthalene series, ammonia benzene sulfonic acid series, and/or high efficiency water reducing agent of melamine.

PT P
PN BR102020010065-A2
TI Evaluating natural extract in thermo-oxidative            stability of biodiesel, by preparing extract from            Esenbeckia grandiflora leaf using liquid solvent by            ultrasound-assisted solvent extraction technique or            supercritical extraction technique, and performing            evaporation.
AU SOUSA D M D
   SILVA E A D
   VIANA A P B
   RODRIGUES M L F
AE UNIV ESTADUAL DO OESTE DO PARANA (UYES-Non-standard)
GA 2022495268
AB    NOVELTY - Method for evaluating natural extract in                thermo-oxidative stability of biodiesel involves                preparing extract from Esenbeckia grandiflora leaf                using liquid solvent by ultrasound-assisted solvent                extraction technique or supercritical extraction                technique, performing evaporation until the                residual mass is constant, verifying and performing                yield calculation.
   USE - The method is useful for evaluating natural                extract in thermo-oxidative stability of biodiesel.                The natural extract is useful as additive to                biodiesel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases stability of biodiesel.                The natural extract is safe, less polluting and                cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for evaluating natural extract in                thermo-oxidative stability of biodiesel involves                (a) using Esenbeckia grandiflora leaf as raw                material, (b) preparing extract from raw material                by existing extraction technique using liquid                solvent in a continuous reflux system, preferably                Golfish equipment, and using ultrasound-assisted                solvent extraction technique, (c) contacting                solvent with the sample for 3 hours, and performing                existing extraction, (d) using 10 g dry sample with                respect to 100 ml solvent (mass/volume ratio of                1:10), (e) counting beginning of extraction from                beginning of boiling of the solvent, after                continuous heating at 100 degrees C for 1 hour 30                minutes, (f) re-boiling suspension and dripping                solvent by heating to 130 degrees C for 1 hour, (g)                recovering solvent for 30 minutes with closed                valve, (h) drying extracts in an oven at 60 degrees                C until total evaporation of the remaining solvent,                storing in amber bottles and keeping under                refrigeration, (i) weighing 10 g sample extract                obtained through supercritical extraction                technique, and introducing into extractor of 2.52                cm internal diameter and 16 cm height, with a                capacity of 79.80 ml, (j) cooling solvent to 10                degrees C before entering pump, pumping solvent,                introducing into extractor, and initially                pressurizing the system until reaching the design                pressure with the closed expansion valve, (k)                pressurizing the system upon reaching the desired                temperature to increase solubility of solid matter                in supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) for 3 hours,                and performing extraction by activating the syringe                pump and opening the expansion valves, (l)                controlling CO2 flow by adjusting micrometric valve                at 2 ml/minute, maintaining extractor outlet                temperature at 120 degrees C by a thermo-regulator,                performing depressurization, and performing                separation between the extract and the solvent, (m)                storing extracts in amber glass flasks, weighing                every 10 minutes for 3 hours, obtaining extracts,                and performing extraction at central point (50                degrees C and 200 bar) in triplicates, (n)                preparing extract through ultrasonic extraction,                and performing test with liquid solvents with                different polarities, (o) placing dried leaf in                contact with 50 ml volumes of solvent, (p)                immersing probe-type ultrasound device 10 mm in the                solution, (q) exposing mixture (sample and solvent)                to high power sound waves emitted by ultrasound for                15 minutes, (r) performing evaporation until                residual mass is constant, verifying and performing                yield calculation, (s) preparing extract through                technique of ultrasound extraction, and performing                tests with liquid solvents with different                polarities, (t) placing dried leaf in contact with                50 ml volumes of solvent (water, ethanol, ethyl                acetate and hexane), (u) immersing probe-type                ultrasound device 10 mm in the solution, (v)                exposing mixture (sample and solvent) to high power                sound waves emitted by ultrasound for 15 minutes,                and (w) performing evaporation until the residual                mass is constant, verifying and performing yield                calculation. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                use of extract as additive to biodiesel, where 1 mg                solvent-free purified extract is added with 1 g                biodiesel or diesel/biodiesel mixture.
TF EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN112515162-A
TI Selenium-enriched additive composition for betel nut useful for preventing novel Coronavirus pneumonia, comprises Chinese herbal mixture and selenium-enriched sauce containing selenium-enriched solid powder and sweet potato turbid liquid.
AU SONG J
AE SONG J (SONG-Individual)
GA 202131642W
AB    NOVELTY - Selenium-enriched additive composition for betel nut comprises selenium-enriched sauce and Chinese herbal mixture. The selenium-enriched sauce comprises selenium-enriched solid powder, cinnamon oil and selenium-enriched sweet potato turbid liquid. The Chinese herbal mixture comprises raw Ephedra, raw gypsum, almonds, Notopterygium root, semen lepidii, Cyrtomium fortunei, earthworm, Cynanchum paniculatum, Ageratum, Eupatorium, rhizoma atractylodis, Poria, rhizoma atractylodis macrocephalae, semen zanthoxyli bungeani, Magnolia officinalis, Amomum tsaoko fruit and ginger.
   USE - The selenium-enriched additive composition for betel nut is useful for preventing novel Coronavirus pneumonia.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Selenium-enriched additive composition for betel nut comprises 12-27 pts. wt. selenium-enriched sauce and 3-16 pts. wt. Chinese herbal mixture. The selenium-enriched sauce comprises selenium-enriched solid powder, 0.6-0.9 %mass cinnamon oil and 12-23 %mass selenium-enriched sweet potato turbid liquid. The Chinese herbal mixture comprises (a) 3-9 pts. wt. raw Ephedra, 10-20 pts. wt. raw gypsum, 6-12 pts. wt. almonds, 10-20 pts. wt. Notopterygium root, 10-20 pts. wt. semen lepidii, 6-12 pts. wt. Cyrtomium fortunei, 10-20 pts. wt. earthworm, 10-20 pts. wt. Cynanchum paniculatum, 10-20 pts. wt. Ageratum, 6-12 pts. wt. Eupatorium, 10-20 pts. wt. rhizoma atractylodis, 35-55 pts. wt. Poria, 20-40 pts. wt. rhizoma atractylodis macrocephalae, 3-12 pts. wt. semen zanthoxyli bungeani, 10-20 pts. wt. Magnolia officinalis, 3-12 pts. wt. Amomum tsaoko fruit and 10-20 pts. wt. ginger, (b) 10-20 pts. wt. rhizoma atractylodis, 5-15 pts. wt. dried orange peel, 5-15 pts. wt. M.officinalis, 5-15 pts. wt. Ageratum, 3-9 pts. wt. Notopterygium root, 3-9 pts. wt. raw Ephedra, 5-15 pts. wt. Notopterygium root and 5-15 pts. wt. ginger or (c) 20-26 pts. wt. Uncaria, 20-28 pts. wt. Prunella vulgaris, 15-21 pts. wt. peach kernels, 15-19 pts. wt. radix salviae miltiorrhizae, 15-21 pts. wt. radix clematidis, 10-16 pts. wt. rhubarb, 30-42 pts. wt. radix astragali, 20-26 pts. wt. radix codonopsitis, 15-19 pts. wt. baked licorice, 15-21 pts. wt. ginger, 30-38 pts. wt. fried Coix seeds, 20-26 pts. wt. dendrobe, 20-27 pts. wt. radix cyathulae, 20-26 pts. wt. medlar, 20-28 pts. wt. herba epimedii, 15-21 pts. wt. spina date seeds, 20-27 pts. wt. radix paeoniae alba, 15-19 pts. wt. radix bupleuri, 15-19 pts. wt. plantain seeds, 20-26 pts. wt. cassia twig, 20-25 pts. wt. sticktight, 15-23 pts. wt. licorice, 15-19 pts. wt. radix scutellariae and 10-19 pts. wt. dried orange peel. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the selenium-enriched additive composition for betel nut, which involves (a) drying the Chinese herbal mixture and grinding the Chinese herbal mixture into Chinese herbal powder, (b) adding ethanol to the Chinese herbal powder in a solid-to-liquid ratio 1:10-1:30 (g/ml) for ultrasonic treatment, (c) drying the ethanol dispersion obtained in step (b) and performing low-temperature supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain extract and (d) uniformly mixing extract with Chinese herbal powder and selenium-enriched solid powder, adding cinnamon oil and selenium-enriched sweet potato turbid liquid to the mixture, and optionally adding salt and/or calcium hydroxide solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: In Chinese herbal mixture (a), the raw Ephedra, raw gypsum, almonds, Notopterygium root, semen lepidii, C.fortunei, earthworm, C.paniculatum, Ageratum and Eupatorium are ground into powder and the rhizoma atractylodis, Poria, rhizoma atractylodis macrocephalae, semen zanthoxyli bungeani, M.officinalis, A.tsaoko fruit and ginger are in the form of aqueous formulation. In Chinese herbal mixture (c), the Uncaria, P.vulgaris, peach kernels, radix salviae miltiorrhizae, radix clematidis, rhubarb, radix astragali, radix codonopsitis and baked licorice are ground into powder and the ginger, fried Coix seeds, dendrobe, radix cyathulae, medlar, herba epimedii, spina date seeds, radix paeoniae alba, radix bupleuri, plantain seeds, cassia twig, sticktight, licorice, radix scutellariae and dried orange peel are in the form of aqueous formulation. The Chinese herbal mixture (b) is in powder form. The selenium-enriched solid powder comprises (a) first aromatic selenium-enriched flavor material prepared by crushing and mixing selenium-enriched sweet potato starch, selenium-enriched coffee powder, selenium-enriched tea, selenium-enriched soybeans and selenium-enriched balsam pear seeds in a mass ratio of (4.2-5.6):(0.8-1.1):(0.5-1.1):(2.1-3.2):(0.8-1.6), (b) second aromatic selenium-enriched flavor material prepared by crushing and mixing selenium-enriched sweet potato starch, selenium-enriched rice, selenium-enriched Sorghum and selenium-enriched mung bean flour in a mass ratio (3.1-3.8):(2.6-3.5):(1.6-2.4):(0.8-1.5) and (c) third aromatic selenium-enriched flavor material prepared by crushing and mixing selenium-enriched sweet potato starch, selenium-enriched fruits, selenium-enriched sesame seeds, selenium-enriched pumpkin seeds and selenium-enriched sunflower seeds in a mass ratio of (4.6-5.5):(2.7-3.3):(0.6-1.1):(0.3-0.8):(0.3-0.8), where the selenium-enriched fruit comprises one or more combinations of selenium-enriched apple, selenium-enriched orange, selenium-enriched pear, selenium-enriched watermelon, selenium-enriched red jujube and selenium-enriched Citrus. The selenium-enriched sweet potato starch is a starch processed using selenium-enriched sweet potatoes, and selenium content of more than 10 g/kg. The selenium-enriched coffee powder is obtained by crushing selenium-enriched coffee and has selenium content of more than 0.35 mg/kg. The selenium-enriched mung bean powder is obtained by crushing selenium-enriched mung bean and has selenium content of more than 0.35 mg/kg. The selenium-enriched fruit comprises 15-26 %mass selenium-enriched apple, 18-30 %mass selenium-enriched orange, 18-30 %mass selenium-enriched pears, 10-22 %mass selenium-enriched red jujube, 18-31 %mass selenium-enriched watermelon and 8-18 %mass selenium-enriched Citrus. Preferred Composition: The additive composition further comprises 0.8-2 pts. wt. table salt and/or 3-10 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide solution. Preferred Method: The selenium-enriched sweet potato turbid liquid is prepared by (a) crushing and separating the washed selenium-enriched sweet potato to obtain the upper suspension, (b) precipitating the upper suspension, and performing separation to obtain supernatant and (c) heating the supernatant liquid in a 65-90 degrees C water bath, exporting the steam of the supernatant liquid to obtain the selenium-rich liquid, precipitating and filtering to obtain the selenium-rich sweet potato turbid liquid.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Respiratory-Gen.; Virucide.

PN CN112430096-A
TI High temperature heating of conductive bamboo charcoal comprises crushing bamboo, mixing, stirring, conveying bamboo plate, drying, pre-baking two surfaces of bamboo plate, adding material, and loading material vehicle.
AU XU J
AE DEHUA SANFU CERAMIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DEHU-Non-standard)
GA 202124126N
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (a) cutting the bamboo, crushing into powder, (b) bamboo powder, lignin, GaO powder, mixing and stirring the zeolite powder, (c) conveying the bamboo plate by the conveying device to the pressurizing mold seat of the pressurizing device, pressing the pressurizing disc downwards, drying, (d) respectively pre-baking the two surfaces of the bamboo plate, (e) placing the bamboo plate on the material frame, adding the material in the material vehicle by the loading and unloading device, (f) loading the material vehicle with bamboo board enters the lifting channel, (g) loading the material vehicle with bamboo plate into the drying chamber, drying, (h) loading the material vehicle with bamboo plate into the pre-carbonizing chamber, (i) loading the material vehicle with bamboo plate into the carbonizing chamber, (j) loading the material vehicle with bamboo plate into the calcining chamber, (k) loading the material vehicle with bamboo plate into the cooling channel for cooling.
   USE - The method is useful for high temperature heating of conductive bamboo charcoal.
   ADVANTAGE - The charcoal has excellent antibacterial effect, and crack resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High temperature heating of conductive bamboo charcoal comprises (a) cutting the bamboo, crushing into powder, (b) bamboo powder, lignin, GaO powder, mixing and stirring the zeolite powder, passing the mixed material to a bamboo sheet forming device for extrusion forming, forming an annular extruding pipe by annular discharging port, cutting the top of the annular extruding pipe along the axial direction under the cutting of the longitudinal cutting line, then transversely cutting the annular extruding pipe under transverse cutting line, supporting the extrusion pipe in the process of forming and cutting by the left arc plate and the right arc plate, flatly spreading the extrusion pipe to the conveyor under the action of the gravity and the sub- spreading machine after the cutting is finished, (c) conveying the bamboo plate by the conveying device to the pressurizing mold seat of the pressurizing device, pressing the pressurizing disc downwards, increasing the bamboo plate density, then naturally drying for 2-3 hours, (d) respectively pre-baking the two surfaces of the bamboo plate, (e) placing the bamboo plate on the material frame, adding the material in the material vehicle by the loading and unloading device, (f) loading the material vehicle with bamboo board enters the lifting channel to pre-heat under the driving of the circulating conveying line, (g) loading the material vehicle with bamboo plate into the drying chamber, drying the bamboo plate at 150 degrees C, (h) loading the material vehicle with bamboo plate into the pre-carbonizing chamber, pre-carbonizing the bamboo plate under the 150-300 degrees C, decomposing hemicellulose in the bamboo to generate carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and a little acetic acid, (i) loading the material vehicle with bamboo plate into the carbonizing chamber, carbonizing bamboo plate under 300-480 degrees C, thermally decomposing the bamboo into acetic acid, methanol, methane and ethane, (j) loading the material vehicle with bamboo plate into the calcining chamber, calcining the bamboo plate under 700-800 degrees C, generating the decomposed product by the pyrolysis of the carbonizing chamber and the carbonizing chamber enters into the residual gas recycling furnace for gas-liquid separation, entering combustible gas to the calcining chamber for re-burning, (k) loading the material vehicle with bamboo plate into the cooling channel for cooling, and then taking out the material frame to obtain the bamboo carbon plate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The step (b) uses raw materials comprises 100-120 pts. wt. bamboo powder, 5-8 pts. wt. lignin, 6-8 pts. wt. GaO powder, and 4-5 pts. wt. zeolite powder.

PN CN111979839-A
TI Medical paper towel comprises fiber wood pulp,            natural fragrance, wet-strength agent, antibacterial            essential oil, defoamer, dispersant and moisturizing            factor.
AU ZHAO J
   ZHANG Y
   ZHENG Y
AE ANHUI RUILAIER INSTR CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020C19465
AB    NOVELTY - A medical paper towel comprises 90-95 pts. wt.                fiber wood pulp, 1-5 pts. wt. natural fragrance,                1-3 pts. wt. wet-strength agent, 2-6 pts. wt.                antibacterial essential oil, 0.1-0.6 pt. wt.                defoamer, 0.1-0.6 pts. wt. dispersant and 2-4 pts.                wt. moisturizing factor.
   USE - Medical paper towel.
   ADVANTAGE - The medical paper towel has excellent                antibacterial effect due to integration of                antibacterial essential oil in paper and no                wrinkles like the soaked liquid medical paper which                eliminates need for smoothing steps in preparing                medical paper.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                production of the medical paper towel, which                involves washing wild Chrysanthemum, honeysuckle                and rosemary with water, drying at 28-37 degrees C                in drying room for 24-36 hours, curing at 160-180                degrees C using steam curing machine for 40-60                seconds, using jet dryer to drying to moisture                content of 10-15%, crushing to 10-15 mesh to obtain                crushed material, adding solution of carboxymethyl                cellulose, sodium bicarbonate, sodium chloride,                ethanol and water in ratio of                1:1.5-1.7:1.8-2.0:8.5-10.5:32-36 to the crushed                materials, heating at rate of 5 degrees C/minute to                50-60 degrees C, stop heating, stirring and                evaporating for 20-40 minutes, adding 4.5-5.5 %mass                coconut oil to form mixture, placing mixture in 0-4                degrees C environment for 3-5 hours, heating to                28-40 degrees C, adding to distillation equipment,                vacuum distilling at low temperature, performing                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 40-50                degrees C under extraction pressure of 15-17 MPa                for 71-91 minutes to obtain natural fragrance,                washing Rhodiola rosea, Forsythia suspensa, Aloe                and Artemisia leaf with sterile water, drying until                the surface moisture evaporates, spreading them in                drying room, drying at 40-45 degrees C under                heating rate of 2 degrees C/minute for 12-20 hours,                using air dryer to dry to moisture content of                60-80% to obtain dried R. rosea, F. suspensa, Aloe                and Artemisia, adding dried material to the steam                generator, adding menthol and distilled water,                heating to boiling, stop heating when essential oil                in the steam generator does not increase,                extracting oil layer with ethyl acetate, rotary                evaporating extract to obtain antibacterial                essential oil where ratio of R. rosea, F. suspensa,                Aloe, Artemisia and menthol to distilled water is                5:6-11, adding long-fiber wood pulp in fiber wood                pulp into the pulper, adding sterile water to                adjust the wood pulp concentration to 7-10 wt.% and                pulping for 15-20 minutes, passing pulped material                to fiber decomposing machine to decomposes the                fibers, passing to beating machine with beating                degree of 20-40 degrees SR, passing through                pressure screen to obtain long fiber pulp with                length of 1.5-2.0 mm, adding short fiber wood pulp                in fiber wood pulp into the pulper, adding sterile                water to adjust the wood pulp concentration to 7-10                wt.%, pulping for 10-15 minutes, passing pulped                material to fiber decomposing machine to decomposes                the fibers, beating in beating machine with beating                degree of 20-40 degrees SR, passing through                pressure screen to obtain short fiber pulp with the                length of 0.3-0.5 mm, pumping long fiber good pulp                and short fiber good pulp into the mixing tank,                stirring, sequentially adding we-strength agent,                defoamer, dispersant and moisturizing factor under                stirring condition, stirring for 10-20 minutes,                adding natural fragrance, stirring for 10-20                minutes, adding antibacterial essential oil,                stirring for 5-10 minutes to obtain pulp paste,                leaving still for 30 minutes, feeding pulp paste                into paper machine, papermaking and                rewinding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The natural fragrance                  comprises 70-80 pts. wt. wild Chrysanthemum,                  20-30 pts. wt. honeysuckle and 30-40 pts. wt.                  rosemary. The antibacterial essential oil                  comprises 4-5 pts. wt. R. rosea, 16-24 pts. wt.                  F. suspensa, 10-20 pts. wt. menthol, 8-12 pts.                  wt. Aloe and 8-12 pts. wt. Artemisia leaf.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The wet-strength agent                  comprises dialdehyde starch and chitosan in ratio                  of 1:2-2.5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Component: The fiber wood pulp                  comprises long fiber wood pulp and short fiber                  wood pulp in mass ratio of 2-4:1. The long-fiber                  wood pulp is prepared from softwood fiber and                  bamboo wood fiber in weight ratio of 2-2.5:1. The                  short-fiber wood pulp is prepared from hardwood                  fibers and straw fibers in weight ratio of 5:2-3.                  Preferred Condition: The airflow drying speed of                  dryer is 4-5 m3/hour. The low-temperature                  distillation is carried out at distillation                  temperature of 30-40 degrees C under vacuum                  degree of 0.2-0.4 Pa and condensation temperature                  of 1-2 degrees C.

PN CN111793506-A; CN111793506-B
TI Energy-saving and emission-reducing coal tar extraction machine with cleaning function comprises shell, dry distillation chamber set in shell, carbonization cavity fixed with combustion machine, and temperature adjusting turntable.
AU CHENG J
AE CHENG J (CHEN-Individual)
GA 2020A42592
AB    NOVELTY - Energy-saving and emission-reducing coal tar extraction machine with cleaning function comprises shell (1) and dry distillation chamber (8) set in shell. The bottom wall of carbonization cavity is fixed with combustion machine (14). The left side of combustion machine is provided with temperature adjusting turntable (11). The fixing plate is provided with reaction tube. The right side surface of reaction tube is provided with reaction chamber (12). The reaction chamber is placed with reactant. The left side of processor is provided with air return pipe connected with reaction cavity. The top surface of processor is provided with exhaust pipe passing through top surface of shell. The reflux pipe is provided with ball valve. The first rotating cavity is provided with control device for controlling reversing valve and ball valve to rotate. The first rotating shaft is fixedly provided with fan blade extending intoexhaust pipe.
   USE - Energy-saving and emission-reducing coal tar extraction machine with cleaning function.
   ADVANTAGE - The machine is simple in structure, and convenient to use. The produced combustible gas generated by coal tar is converted into carbon dioxide and water. The heat generated by reaction is transferred to reaction tube for heating reactant to reduce heat output of combustion engine, reducing energy consumption. The machine can control combustion engine to change heat output according to temperature change in reaction tube through adjusting device, which solves problem that output temperature of combustion engine is not matched with temperature in reaction tube.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Energy-saving and emission-reducing coal tar extraction machine with cleaning function comprises shell (1) and dry distillation chamber (8) set in shell. The bottom wall of carbonization cavity is fixed with combustion machine (14). The left side of combustion machine is provided with temperature adjusting turntable (11). The left side of combustion machine is provided with adjusting device capable of controlling rotation of temperature adjusting turntable to adjust temperature of combustion machine. The left and right walls of dry distillation cavity above combustion machine are symmetrically fixed with fixing plate. The fixing plate is provided with reaction tube. The right side surface of reaction tube is provided with reaction chamber (12). The reaction chamber is placed with reactant. The right side of reaction tube is plugged with bottle plug fixedly provided with discharging pipe. The right side of dry distillation chamber is provided with first rotating cavity. The front wall of first rotating cavity is fixed with separating pipe through first connecting rod. The separating pipe is provided with reversing valve. The bottom surface of separating pipe is connected with liquid outlet pipe extending out of dry distillation machine. The upper section of first rotating cavity is provided with processing cavity fixedly provided with processor. The processor and separating pipe are connected through air outlet pipe. The left side of processor is provided with air return pipe connected with reaction cavity. The top surface of processor is provided with exhaust pipe passing through top surface of shell. The reflux pipe is provided with ball valve. The first rotating cavity is provided with control device for controlling reversing valve and ball valve to rotate. The first rotating shaft is fixedly provided with fan blade extending intoexhaust pipe.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of energy-saving and emission-reducing coal tar extraction machine.
   Shell (1)
   Dry distillation chamber (8)
   Temperature adjusting turntable (11)
   Reaction chamber (12)
   Combustion machine (14)
