PN CN111485638-A
TI House building material insulation board comprises board body comprising insulation layer and a waterproof layer, where insulation layer is located on waterproof layer.
AU YUAN Z
AE YUAN Z (YUAN-Individual)
GA 2020759419
AB    NOVELTY - House building material insulation board comprises board body (1) comprising an insulation layer and a waterproof layer. The insulation layer is located on the waterproof layer, inside of the thermal insulation layer is fixedly connected with a thermally conductive sheet and the front of the thermally conductive sheet is fixedly connected with a thermally conductive rod. The one end of the heat conducting rod extends to the outside of the board body is fixedly connected with a heat absorption plate (4), and the heat preservation layer is provided with a cavity (5) inside, and a sealing plug (6) is movably connected to the side of the cavity close to the outside of the plate body. The sliding rail (7) is fixedly connected, and the other side and the top of the plate body are fixedly connected with a sliding plate (8).
   USE - House building material insulation board.
   ADVANTAGE - The house building material insulation board reduces overall weight, enhances effect of heat conduction and stability of the heat absorption plate, and has protective effect and function of preventing heat loss.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing thermal insulation board for house building materials, which involves:
   (A) selecting straws, soaking in disinfectant water for 3-5 days, taking out and drying for 30-50 minutes at 60-90 degrees C to make the water content 1-2%, putting straw into the crusher for crushing for 10-20 minutes to 200-300 meshes, putting appropriate amount of waste paper into crushing crushing in the machine, and taking out straw residue and waste paper powder;
   (B) selecting appropriate amount of polystyrene resin, polypropylene fiber and nylon, and puting in a pulverizer for pulverization for 20-30 minutes, taking out powder and pouring into the mixer for stirring, and adding appropriate amount bentonite and silica sol at 50-60 degrees C, and the stirring rate is controlled at 300-500 revolutions per minute, adding appropriate amount of modified additives, waterproofing agent and defoamer, continue stirring, increase the temperature of the mixer to 80-90 degrees C, and controlling the stirring rate at 350-550 revolution/minutes for 20-30 minutes, adding the straw residue and waste paper powder, stirring at 350-550 revolution/minute for 20-30 minutes to obtain the raw material of the insulation layer; and
   (C) choosing clean sheet metal mold, pouring a layer of insulation layer raw materials, air drying for a certain period of time, maintaining a certain degree of humidity, placing the thermal conductive sheet fixed with the thermal conductive rod, pouring the materials of the insulation layer, using four tubes to be placed in the insulation layer to make hollow, and using another mold to make the other side of the insulation layer, using four tubes to be placed in the thermal insulation layer, reserving through hole suitable for the heat conduction rod, and using the thermal insulation raw materials of layer, air drying, demoulding, using glue to adhere the surface of the insulation layer to a waterproof layer and sliding plate on the surface of the board using the mounting parts, fixing the heat transfer rod on the heat absorption board, filling cavity with carbon dioxide gas, and plugging cavity with a sealing plug to obtain a new type of house building material insulation board.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows perspective view of house building material insulation board.
   Board body (1)
   Heat absorption plate (4)
   Cavity (5)
   Sealing plug (6)
   Sliding rail (7)
   Sliding plate (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The waterproofing agent is a melamine waterproofing agent, and the defoaming agent is a co-cluster of silicone ether. Preferred Composition: The house building material insulation board comprises 30-60 pts. wt. polystyrene resin, 30-40 pts. wt. straw, and 20-pts. wt. bentonite. 30 parts, 25-45 pts. wt. polypropylene fiber, 15-30 pts. wt. waste paper, 30-40 pts. wt. silica sol, 15-35 pts. wt. modified additives, 20-40 pts. wt. water repellent, 20-40 pts. wt. defoamer and 25-45 pts. wt. nylon.

PN CN111470789-A
TI Improving utilization efficiency of gypsum in sulfoaluminate cement involves pretreating gypsum to improve activation performance, and using for preparation of sulphoaluminate cement clinker.
AU GUO P
AE FEIXI CHUANGXI BUILDING MATERIALS TECHNO (FEIX-Non-standard)
GA 2020745329
AB    NOVELTY - Improving utilization efficiency of gypsum in sulfoaluminate cement involves pretreating gypsum to improve activation performance, and using for the preparation of sulphoaluminate cement clinker. The gypsum pretreatment is performed by mixing gypsum, zinc-containing polymetallic nano-powder additives, and light calcium carbonate in mass ratio of 180-190:0.45-0.47:3.3-3.8 by a mixer for 2-3 hours to obtain a uniformly mixed inorganic powder, and calcinated at 300-320 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and rapidly cooled to 30-35 degrees C at a rate of 18-20 degrees C/minute. The raw materials of zinc ore are washed and dried, ground and crushed to pass a 20-30 mesh sieve, 7-9 times the volume of sodium hydroxide solution is added with a concentration of 2.0-2.4 mol/L, and heated to 68-70 degrees C under stirring for 30-34 minutes, and filtered. The resulting filtrate is allowed to stand and age for 12-14 hours at 50-55 degrees C, and 0.36-0.40 times the volume of 16-20% acetic acid solution is added.
   USE - Method for improving utilization efficiency of gypsum in sulfoaluminate cement.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve utilization efficiency of gypsum in sulfoaluminate cement, which improves the strength of the gel and reduces the water demand, promotes firing of sulphoaluminate cement clinker, reduces the combustion temperature and carbon dioxide output, and uses zinc-containing polymetallic nano-powder to improve the hydration activity of calcium sulphoaluminate minerals without the need for a large amount of gypsum to be added, has good cumulative effect, and the resistance, durability, and effectively suppresses the late compression strength shrinkage defect of sulfoaluminate cement.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving utilization efficiency of gypsum in sulfoaluminate cement involves pretreating gypsum to improve activation performance, and using for the preparation of sulphoaluminate cement clinker and the clinker. The gypsum pretreatment is performed by mixing gypsum, zinc-containing polymetallic nano-powder additives, and light calcium carbonate in mass ratio of 180-190:0.45-0.47:3.3-3.8 by a mixer for 2-3 hours to obtain a uniformly mixed inorganic powder, and calcinated at 300-320 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and rapidly cooled to 30-35 degrees C at a rate of 18-20 degrees C/minute. The raw materials of zinc ore are washed and dried, ground and crushed to pass a 20-30 mesh sieve, 7-9 times the volume of sodium hydroxide solution is added with a concentration of 2.0-2.4 mol/L, and heated to 68-70 degrees C under stirring for 30-34 minutes, and filtered. The resulting filtrate is allowed to stand and age for 12-14 hours at 50-55 degrees C, and 0.36-0.40 times the volume of 16-20% acetic acid solution is added. The tetrabutyl titanate is added with a mass of 22-26% of the acetic acid solution under stirring, stirred at 22-25 degrees C, and 0.60-0.65 times the molar concentration of the volume to the mixture is added under stirring, 1.1-1.2 mol/L hydrochloric acid solution is added, heated to 45-55 degrees C for 2-3 hours, placed in an oven at 68-70 degrees C for 20-24 hours to obtain a wet gel, calcined at at 450-500 degrees C for 1.5-2.0 hours and cooled to normal temperature to obtain zinc-containing polymetallic nano powder additive.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The gypsum is dihydrate gypsum or natural anhydrite. The light calcium carbonate has a fineness of 800 mesh and a purity of 98%. The main components of the sulphoaluminate cement raw meal are calculated by mass percentage: limestone accounts for 48-54%, bauxite accounts for 27-33%, pretreatment gypsum accounts for 4.5-5.5%, and the remainder is aluminum ash and a small amount of impurities. The blending amount of pretreatment gypsum in the sulphoaluminate cement raw meal accounts for 4.8-5.3%, and the amount of pretreatment gypsum mixed with the prepared cement clinker is 0.8-1.0%. The particle size of the zinc-containing polymetallic nano-powder additive is between 30-45 nanometers, The zinc ore used in the preparation is willemite. Preferred Process: The sulphoaluminate cement clinker is prepared by grinding raw material with a good ratio, pulverizing to pass a 70-80 micron sieve, and performing heating and calcination at 1090-1100 degrees C, heat preservation and firing for 20-25 minutes, cooling to obtain sulphoaluminate cement clinker.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PN CN110892987-A
TI Preparing fructus lycii syrup by pre-treating fructus lycii raw materials, adding to extractant, extracting, recovering, collecting extract, filtering, adding to ethanol, separating clear solution, purifying, and concentrating clear liquid.
AU ZHOU X
AE ZHOU X (ZHOU-Individual)
GA 202024860C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fructus lycii syrup involves (a) pre-treating fructus lycii raw materials, (b) adding fructus lycii pulp to extraction system, adding extractant, extracting, recovering extractant, decompressing, recovering, collecting zeaxanthin dipalmitate extract, discharging pulp, and recovering extractant, (c) vacuum-dissolving and collecting zeaxanthin dipalmitate extract, (d) making zeaxanthin dipalmitate extract into zeaxanthin dipalmitate microcapsule powder, (e) extracting pulp, and collecting pulp, (f) filtering collected pulp to obtain pulp residue and clear liquid, and concentrating clear liquid to obtain a concentrated liquid, (g) adding ethanol to concentrated liquid, and separating precipitate and clear solution, (h) separating flavonoid and remaining residual liquid, and purifying, (i) subjecting liquid to ion exchange treatment to obtain permeate and eluent, desalting eluate, concentrating, crystallizing, and drying, and (j) concentrating clear liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fructus lycii syrup.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the raw material utilization rate of fructus lycii, and increases the added value of raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing fructus lycii syrup involves (a) pre-treating fructus lycii raw materials by (i) rehydrating dry fructus lycii, rehydrating fructus lycii residue, pulping, and removing the seeds to obtain fructus lycii pulp, (ii) cleaning fresh fructus lycii, pulping, and removing the seeds to obtain fructus lycii pulp, (iii) adding fresh fructus lycii, rehydrated fructus lycii and rehydrated fructus lycii residue, pulping, removing the seeds to obtain fructus lycii pulp, adding 0-100 wt.% fructus lycii pulp with raw material, and evenly stirring mixture to obtain fructus lycii pulp, adding fresh fructus lycii and a certain proportion of dried fructus lycii to obtain fructus lycii pulp, and dissolving fructus lycii whole powder to obtain fructus lycii pulp, and (iv) adding pectinase and cellulase to the obtained fructus lycii pulp for performing enzymatic hydrolysis, (b) adding enzymolyzed fructus lycii pulp to an extraction system, adding an extractant, performing closed extraction with extraction pressure of 0.1-10 MPa, recovering extractant into analysis tank when the equilibrium is reached, decompressing to extract, recovering extractant, collecting zeaxanthin dipalmitate extract, discharging extracted pulp from the bottom of the extraction tank after several levels of extraction, and recovering extractant through a compressor, where extractant is chosen from propane, butane, isobutane (R600a), 1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane (R134a), dimethyl ether (DME), liquefied petroleum gas (LPG), carbon dioxide and sulfur fluoride, (c) vacuum-dissolving collected zeaxanthin dipalmitate extract until the residual amount of extractant is less than 8 ppm, and collecting zeaxanthin dipalmitate extract, (d) making obtained zeaxanthin dipalmitate extract into zeaxanthin dipalmitate microcapsule powder with different content, (e) extracting pulp released from the bottom of the final extraction tank until the residual amount of extractant is less than 8 ppm, and collecting pulp, (f) filtering collected pulp to obtain pulp residue and clear liquid, and concentrating clear liquid to obtain a concentrated liquid, (g) adding ethanol to the concentrated liquid, adjusting the ethanol content to more than 80%, maintaining, and separating the precipitate and clear solution, (h) separating flavonoid and the remaining residual liquid after recovering the ethanol from the clear liquid, and purifying, (i) subjecting residual liquid to ion exchange or macroporous resin treatment to obtain permeate and eluent, desalting eluate, concentrating, crystallizing, and drying to obtain fructus lycii alkaloids, and (j) obtaining fructus lycii syrup by (i) concentrating the clear liquid, after recovering ethanol, (ii) concentrating the residual liquid and (iii) concentrating the permeate.

TI Calculation process of compression factor of natural gas flow computer, and is useful for indicating deviation in volume after compressing actual gas and ideal gas at same pressure and for judging a parameter error of flow computer.
AU WANG Y
   WANG J
   XU M
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CO LTD GAS BRANCH (SNPC-C)
   SHANGHAI INST PROCESS AUTOMATION INSTR (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020112045
AB    NOVELTY - Calculation process of compression factor of natural gas flow computer, is claimed. The definition of compression factor is Z=( rho V/nTR)=( rho Vm(real)/RT), where R is constant; the size of Z reflects the deviation of the real gas from the ideal gas, where R is a constant; the size of Z reflects the deviation of the real gas from the ideal gas, scope of application includes absolute pressure (p = 0-12 Mpa) thermodynamic temperature (T = 263-338K) high air volume (Hs = 30-45 MJ.m-3) relative density (d = 0.55-0.8); The formula is as specified in equation, where rho m and rho r satisfy the equations rho r = K3 rho mK and is the solution formula of the mixture volume parameter rho m:pm = p/(ZRT). where P-absolute pressure; R-molar gas constant; T-thermodynamic temperature, entering T, p, xi, calculating the state equation coefficients B and Cnasterisk (n = 13-58), solving the density, and checking whether |p (calculation)-p (input)| not less than 10-6 holds.
   USE - The method is useful for indicating the deviation in volume after compressing actual gas and ideal gas at same pressure, and for judging a parameter error of a flow computer (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can accurately judges the accuracy of flow computer compression factor, judges and corrects the error of flow calculation, and reduces the trade error of natural gas flow.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Calculation process of compression factor of natural gas flow computer, is claimed. The definition of compression factor is Z=( rho V/nTR)=( rho Vm(real)/RT), where R is constant; the size of Z reflects the deviation of the real gas from the ideal gas, where R is a constant; the size of Z reflects the deviation of the real gas from the ideal gas, scope of application includes absolute pressure (p = 0-12 Mpa) thermodynamic temperature (T = 263-338K) high air volume (Hs = 30-45 MJ.m-3) relative density (d = 0.55-0.8); The formula is as specified in equation, where rho m and rho r satisfy the equations rho r = K3 rho mK and is the solution formula of the mixture volume parameter rho m:pm = p/(ZRT), where P-absolute pressure, R-molar gas constant and T-thermodynamic temperature, entering T, p, xi, calculating the state equation coefficients B and Cnasterisk (n = 13-58), solving the density, and checking whether |p (calculation)-p (input)| not less than 10-6 holds, if not, go back to the cyclic solution density, otherwise, if it is satisfied, then output Z = less than or equal to r / ( rho mRT), where T and P unit is K and MPa, and the sum of the molar fractions Xi of the 24 components in MPa is 1; the order of the 24 components is methane, nitrogen (N2), carbon dioxide, ethane, propane, water, hydrogen sulfide, hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide, oxygen (O2), IC4H10, NC4H10, IC5H12, NC5H12, IC6H14, heptane (C7H16), octane (C8H18), nonane, decane, HE, and AR. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for calculating the compression factor of natural gas flow computer comprising (1) calculation equation of two-dimensional profit coefficient B is as specified, (2) calculating Cnasterisk by using equation is as specified, (3) calculating K; and (4) calculating the real value P.

PN CN110583690-A
TI Indoor air purification gel useful as disinfectant in home and office, comprises high-molecular hydrophilic resin beads, chlorite powder, activator, water-absorbing resin powder, and reactor.
AU ZHANG Q
   WANG Y
   ZHANG L
AE GUANGZHOU BAIFENBAI ENVIRONMENTAL MANAGE (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A77533
AB    NOVELTY - Indoor air purification gel comprises high-molecular hydrophilic resin beads, chlorite powder, activator, water-absorbing resin powder, and a reactor.
   USE - The indoor air purification gel is useful as disinfectant in home and office.
   ADVANTAGE - The air purification gel: is environmentally friendly; can change osmotic pressure and diffusion rate of substances in the environment; quickly oxidize formaldehyde to carbon dioxide and water by reaction, and convert benzene, TVOC, and ammonia toxic substances to other non-toxic substances; can inhibit or kill Staphylococcus aureus, Escherichia coli and influenza viruses; contains non-toxic and harmless component, thus does not generate harmful gases to the human body when used, which can ensure its safety during storage and use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use method of indoor air purification gel, comprising (1) wearing masks and gloves in advance, and reading the product instructions carefully, (2) opening outer packaging of the product in a cool, ventilated and dry environment, opening the lid of the tank, and removing the package from the tank for later use, (3) opening golden material bag (material bag 1), placing the chlorite powder in the bag uniformly on the bottom of the tank, (4) opening the silver material bag (material bag 2), placing translucent granular activator in the bag uniformly on the chlorite powder, (5) opening the transparent material bag (material bag 3), and placing the gel beads in the bag uniformly on the activator, (6) opening the frosted material bag (material bag 4), spreading it uniformly on the hydrophilic resin beads, then quickly filling the tank with water to 85% (position of the tick mark) and install the tank cover, (7) the material in the tank reacts gently, the liquid is brown or golden yellow and is gradually absorbed by the gel beads, and the chlorine dioxide gas is slowly released from the pores on the top of the tank, (8) moving the tank into the uninhabited room to be treated quickly and completely, avoiding high temperature and direct sunlight, closing the doors and windows and let them stand for 24-72 hours, if the fan is turned on to circulate indoor air, the effect is better, (9) closing the tank, opening the window for ventilation, and enter the room to check the treatment effect after the odor is dissipated, if it is not ideal, can extend the treatment time appropriately, (10) the first 6 hours of the product configuration is the onset period, at which time the release efficiency of chlorine dioxide gradually increases to the maximum, the stable period is 6-72 hours, at which time the release efficiency of chlorine dioxide is maintained at a high level, the maintenance period is 72 hours later, at this time, water should be replenished at any time according to the amount of water in the tank to maintain the release efficiency of the new chlorine dioxide, over time, the release of chlorine dioxide will gradually decrease until it completely fails, the yellow-green color in the jar disappeared, the action time depends on the specific situation, usually about 20 days, (11) this product can continuously release chlorine dioxide, and the designed action time is more than 20 days, the scope of action ranges from 15-20 m2 or standard civil residences, improper use or storage of this product may lead to premature failure, and (12) residues in the tank should be properly treated after use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chlorite powder is composed of sodium chlorite, potassium chlorite and/or magnesium chlorite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reactor consists of a tank (I), a lid (II) and a material bag (III).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The activator is citric acid, sodium sulfate, sodium hydrogen sulfate and malic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The indoor air purification gel comprises 6-15 pts. wt. high-molecular hydrophilic resin beads, 5-13 pts. wt. chlorite powder, 5-11 pts. wt. activator, and 3-9 pts. wt. water-absorbing resin powder. Preferred Components: The high-molecular hydrophilic resin beads are sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, chitosan, hydroxypropyl cellulose and/or polyacrylamide. The hydrophilic resin powder is phenolic rubber, and/or melamine-urea-formaldehyde glue gum. The specification of the purified magic beads is 680 ml/can, and the generated chlorine dioxide is not less than 3 g/can.

PN KR2014755-B1
TI Cement concrete composition used for roadside tree planting protection block, comprises binder for improving performance made of Portland cement, blast furnace slag, whiskers and anhydrous gypsum, fine aggregate, coarse aggregate and water.
AU LEE G H
AE HANCON CO LTD (HANC-Non-standard)
GA 2019754890
AB    NOVELTY - Cement concrete composition for roadside tree planting protection block, comprises 10-45 wt.% binder for improving performance, 10-60 wt.% fine aggregate, 15-75 wt.% coarse aggregate and 5-35 wt.% water. The binder for improving performance comprises 10-60 wt.% Portland cement, 10-45 wt.% blast furnace slag, 5-20 wt.% whiskers, 5-15 wt.% magnesium oxychloride, 5-10 wt.% wood ash, 5-10 wt.% anhydrous gypsum, 5-10 wt.% mica, 1-10 wt.% sulfur powder, 1-10 wt.% sepiolite, 1-10 wt.% styrene-methylacrylate-butylacrylate copolymer, 1-10 wt.% an ethylene-methyl methacrylate-vinyl acetate copolymer and 1-10 wt.% itaconic acid.
   USE - The composition is useful for roadside tree planting protection block (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: saves landscape beauty of sidewalks or roads; prevents safety accidents e.g. pedestrians tripping over fixing means and bracing means; is buried underground to secure a space for root growth; has excellent carbon dioxide reduction, strength, workability and durability, preferably water resistance, chemical resistance, abrasion resistance and corrosion resistance; and provides tree-plant protection block that has excellent workability and does not interfere with traffic by laying fixing means and support means underground.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cement concrete composition for roadside tree planting protection block, comprises 10-45 wt.% binder for improving performance, 10-60 wt.% fine aggregate, 15-75 wt.% coarse aggregate and 5-35 wt.% water. The binder for improving performance comprises 10-60 wt.% Portland cement, 10-45 wt.% blast furnace slag, 5-20 wt.% whiskers, 5-15 wt.% magnesium oxychloride, 5-10 wt.% wood ash, 5-10 wt.% anhydrous gypsum, 5-10 wt.% mica, 1-10 wt.% sulfur powder, 1-10 wt.% sepiolite, 1-10 wt.% styrene-methylacrylate-butylacrylate copolymer, 1-10 wt.% an ethylene-methyl methacrylate-vinyl acetate copolymer and 1-10 wt.% itaconic acid. The binder further comprises methyl polyacrylate, polyisobutyl methacrylate, an antifoaming agent and a reducing agent in a ratio of 1:1-10. The block comprises ladder type root stem receiving units (13), upper incision unit, a lower protrusion unit (12) and an upper protrusion unit (14) and a lower incision unit. The upper unit is straight and the lower unit has two ladder root stem receiving units. The upper incision unit in which a portion of one end in the longitudinal direction is cut from the top to the half in the width direction. The lower protrusion unit is formed below the upper incision unit from the width half to the bottom. The upper protrusion unit having a portion of the other end portion is formed in the longitudinal direction from the top to the half in the width direction, and part of the other end portion in the longitudinal direction is made of four block fragments (10) having a lower incision unit which is cut from half to the bottom in the width direction. The protrusion unit of one block fragment is engaged at the side of the upper incision unit of another block fragment. The remaining two block fragments are also configured to be engaged in the same manner. The blocks can be assembled in the field and can be constructed according to the shape of the roots of the trees, and four blocks in one formwork to form a space for root growth.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the roadside tree plant protection block.
   Block fragment (10)
   Lower protrusion unit (12)
   Ladder type root stem receiving unit (13)
   Upper protrusion unit (14)
TF EXAMPLE - 18 kg Binder for improving performance (prepared by mixing 50 kg Portland cement, 17 kg Blast furnace slag, 5 kg whiskers, 5 kg magnesium oxychloride, 5 kg wood ash, 5 kg gypsum, 5 kg mica, 1 kg sulfur powder, 1 kg sepiolite, 1 kg styrene-methylacrylate-butylacrylate copolymer, 1 kg ethylene-methylmethacrylate-vinyl acetate copolymer, 1 kg itaconic acid, 1 kg methyl polyacrylate, 1 kg polyisobutyl methacrylate, 0.5 kg silicone-based antifoaming agent and 0.5 kg polycarboxylic acid-based water reducing agent), 34 kg fine aggregate and 40 kg coarse aggregate were stirred in a forced mixer for 2 minutes, then 8 kg water was added and stirred for 3 minutes in a forced mixer to obtain 100 kg cement concrete composition.

PN CN110015822-A
TI Waste percolate capacitive deionizing desalting purification treatment comprises passing the waste waste percolate concentrated water into adjustment tank, flocculation, pumping aerobic treated waste percolate, and discharging.
AU ZHAO J
   GUO Y
   WANG G
   LIU G
   XIE W
AE SHENZHEN PANGU ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2019637745
AB    NOVELTY - Waste percolate capacitive deionizing desalting purification treatment comprises passing the waste waste percolate reverse osmosis concentrated water into the adjustment tank, performing flocculation and precipitation of the waste percolate for removing the ammonia nitrogen, adding flocculant to the chemical coagulation reaction in the coagulation precipitation reaction tank, electrolyzing the waste percolate after flocculation and precipitation into a three-dimensional electrochemically coupled three-dimensional electro-biological unit, passing the anaerobic treatment of the waste percolate to the hydrolysis acidification tank and the anoxic tank in turn, pumping the aerobic treated waste percolate, opening the macromolecular organic matter, crushing, introducing the effluent after secondary electrolysis of capacitor, and discharging.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing waste percolate capacitive deionizing desalting purification treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high treatment efficiency, small floor space and has low energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Waste percolate capacitive deionizing desalting purification treatment comprises (a) passing the waste waste percolate reverse osmosis concentrated water into the adjustment tank, then balancing by adding water, then adding pH adjuster through the dosing device in the adjustment tank, adjusting the pH value, flowing into the ammonia nitrogen stripping device or the reaction tank, then blowing out by converting the high concentration ammonia nitrogen in the waste percolate into free ammonia by steam or air blowing in the ammonia nitrogen stripping device, (b) performing flocculation and precipitation of the waste percolate for removing the ammonia nitrogen, then flowing into the coagulation precipitation reaction tank, adding flocculant to the chemical coagulation reaction in the coagulation precipitation reaction tank, and precipitating by dosing device for removing suspended solids, colloidal impurities, calcium and magnesium ions in the wastewater, (c) electrolyzing the waste percolate after flocculation and precipitation into a three-dimensional electrochemically coupled three-dimensional electro-biological unit, then subjecting the wastewater to three-dimensional electrochemical degradation and three-dimensional electrobiodegradation in a three-dimensional electrochemically coupled three-dimensional electro-biounit, then passing to the intermediate tank after electrolysis, then adding reducing agent for removing residual radicals generated by electrolysis, where the voltage between adjacent electrodes of the electrolysis machine is 2-12 V, and the current density is 10-320 mA/cm2, (d) passing the anaerobic treatment of the waste percolate to the hydrolysis acidification tank and the anoxic tank in turn, then hydrolyzing the macromolecular organic matter in the waste percolate in the hydrolysis acidification tank, and acidifying into small molecular organic substances under the action of the acid-producing bacteria, then decomposing into methane and carbon dioxide by anaerobic bacteria, anaerobic bacteria adsorption, fermentation, and methanogenesis in an anoxic tank, (e) passing the anaerobically treated waste percolate to an aerobic tank containing microorganisms e.g. aerobic bacteria, nitrifying bacteria and nitrosated bacteria, then further oxidizing and decomposing the organic matter in the waste percolate by aerobic microorganisms for deeply removing the waste seepage, simultaneously converting the chemical oxygen demand and biological oxygen demand in the filtrate to nitrate nitrogen or nitrite nitrogen by nitrification of nitrifying bacteria and nitrosation of nitrosating bacteria, (f) pumping the aerobic treated waste percolate into three-dimensional electrochemically coupled three-dimensional electrobiological unit for performing secondary electrolysis, opening the macromolecular organic matter, then crushing for improving the biodegradability, where during the electrolysis, the voltage between the adjacent electrodes of the electrolysis machine is 3-18 V, and the current density is 20-320 mA/cm2, and (g) introducing the effluent after the secondary electrolysis of the capacitor desalting into the bipolar membrane electrodialysis reactor for removing the salt, and then introducing the effluent after desalination treatment into the discharge tank for discharging. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for wastewater disposal system, is claimed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the wastewater disposal system (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The ammonia nitrogen stripping device is stripping tower, packed tower or gravity machine. Preferred Method: The blown off ammonia gas is absorbed by hydrochloric acid to obtain ammonium chloride which can be used for the production of soda ash as a mother liquor, aqueous ammonia by water absorption, or ammonium sulfate by-products by absorption with sulfuric acid.

PN CN109793787-A
TI Seed fat-soluble part of Hevea brasiliensis prepared by drying and shelling fresh Hevea brasiliensis seeds, heating, static hydraulic extrusion, pressing, and extruding, used e.g. to prepare medicine for treating gestational hyperglycemia.
AU XU S
AE XU S (XUSS-Individual)
GA 201949099C
AB    NOVELTY - Seed fat-soluble part of Hevea brasiliensis prepared by (a) mechanical extrusion method: using fresh Hevea brasiliensis seeds as raw materials, then drying and shelling to obtain seed kernels, heating the seed material to 80-100 degrees C before extrusion, subjecting to static hydraulic extrusion or dynamic screw extrusion, performing pressing and extruding liquid substance into fat-soluble portion of the seed of Hevea brasiliensis, or (b) solvent leaching method: using fresh Hevea brasiliensis seeds as raw materials, drying and shelling to obtain seed kernels, expanding the seed kernels, granulating or crushing and processing, then placing in sealed leaching container, adding solvent, stirring and mixing the material and solvent, performing ultrasonic treatment for sufficiently contacting the material with solvent, leaching solvent, volatilizing solvent, and the remaining liquid substance is a seed fat-soluble part of Hevea brasiliensis.
   USE - The seed fat soluble part which is sole active ingredient is useful in preparing medicine, healthcare food, and food product for patient having hyperglycemia during pregnancy, and treating gestational hyperglycemia (claimed), and lowering blood sugar.
   ADVANTAGE - The seed fat soluble part: has good hypoglycemic effect by taking or eating medicines, health foods, and food product prepared by soluble part every day, and has no side effects; and is very safe for both mother and infant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Seed fat-soluble part of Hevea brasiliensis prepared by (a) mechanical extrusion method: using fresh Hevea brasiliensis seeds as raw materials, then drying and shelling to obtain seed kernels, heating the seed material to 80-100 degrees C before extrusion, then subjecting to static hydraulic extrusion or dynamic screw extrusion, performing pressing and extruding the liquid substance into a fat-soluble portion of the seed of Hevea brasiliensis, (b) solvent leaching method: using fresh Hevea brasiliensis seeds as raw materials, then drying and shelling to obtain seed kernels, expanding the seed kernels, granulating or crushing and processing, then placing in a sealed leaching container, then adding solvent, where the solvent is No. 6 extraction solvent oil, petroleum ether having a boiling point range of 30-90 degrees C, n-hexane, diethyl ether, ethyl acetate, and/or n-butanol under the temperature lower than the boiling point of the solvent condition and normal pressure, then stirring and mixing the material and solvent, performing ultrasonic treatment for sufficiently contacting the material with solvent, allowing to stand the precipitate, leaching the solvent, volatilizing the solvent under the temperature higher than the boiling point of the solvent and vacuum conditions, and the remaining liquid substance is a seed fat-soluble part of Hevea brasiliensis, or (c) supercritical extraction: using fresh Hevea brasiliensis seeds as raw materials, drying and shelling to obtain seed kernels, expanding the seed kernels, granulating or crushing, placing them in a sealed pressure-resistant supercritical extraction vessel, adding supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent under the condition that the critical temperature and critical pressure, stirring and mixing the material and solvent, performing ultrasonic treatment for sufficiently contacting the material with solvent, allowing to stand the precipitate, draining the solvent at room temperature, then reducing the pressure, volatilizing the solvent, and the remaining liquid material is the seed fat-soluble part of Hevea brasiliensis. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (1) medicinal composition comprising seed fat-soluble part of Hevea brasiliensis and medically acceptable vector; (2) healthcare food comprising seed fat-soluble part of Hevea brasiliensis and vector; and (3) food product comprising seed fat-soluble part of Hevea brasiliensis and normal food vector.

PT P
PN CN109706334-A; CN109706334-B
TI Preparation of endogenous aluminum dicalcium/magnesium dicalcium-reinforced magnesium composite material involves adding magnesium-calcium composite particles to semi-solid magnesium-aluminum alloy melt, mechanical-agitating, and extruding.
AU NIE K
   LI Y
   DENG K
   YANG A
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201942656A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of endogenous aluminum dicalcium/magnesium dicalcium-reinforced magnesium matrix composite material involves heating magnesium block at 750-780 degrees C to a molten magnesium block under protective atmosphere, adding a calcium block, obtaining a molten metal, mechanical-stirring the molten metal at 750-780 degrees C for 2-3 minutes, heat-preserving at 750-780 degrees C for 5-6 minutes, casting the metal liquid in a cast iron mold at 200-220 degrees C, cooling to room temperature naturally, removing from the cast iron mold, obtaining cast metal, cutting the cast metal into magnesium-calcium composite particles having a particle diameter of 10 mm or less by mechanical processing, heating, adding to a semi-solid magnesium-aluminum alloy melt, performing constant-temperature bidirectional helical mechanical-agitation, heating, heat-preserving, obtaining a liquid magnesium-based composite material, performing constant-temperature rapid pressure forming, homogenizing and heat-treating, and extruding.
   USE - Preparation of endogenous aluminum dicalcium/magnesium dicalcium-reinforced magnesium matrix composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides matrix composite material with improved mechanical properties due to the presence of endogenous aluminum dicalcium/magnesium dicalcium phase as composite particle, adopts bidirectional helical mechanical agitation to facilitate diffusion of aluminum and calcium elements and to provide matrix composite with improved strength and toughness, improves the uniformity matrix composite microstructure, promotes uniform precipitation of endogenous aluminum dicalcium/magnesium dicalcium phase, and adopts constant-temperature and rapid pressure forming to refine grains and improve distribution of second phase while reducing casting defects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of endogenous aluminum dicalcium/magnesium dicalcium-reinforced magnesium matrix composite material involves heating magnesium block at 750-780 degrees C to a molten magnesium block under protective atmosphere, adding a calcium block, obtaining a molten metal, mechanical-stirring the molten metal at 750-780 degrees C for 2-3 minutes, heat-preserving at 750-780 degrees C for 5-6 minutes, casting the metal liquid in a cast iron mold at 200-220 degrees C, cooling to room temperature naturally, removing from the cast iron mold, obtaining cast metal, cutting the cast metal into magnesium-calcium composite particles having a particle diameter of 10 mm or less by mechanical processing, heating the composite particles to 300-400 degrees C, adding to a semi-solid magnesium-aluminum alloy melt, performing constant-temperature bidirectional helical mechanical-agitation, heating to 750-760 degrees C, heat-preserving at 750-760 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, obtaining a liquid magnesium-based composite material, performing constant-temperature rapid pressure forming at 300-460 degrees C and 300-500 MPa for 3-10 minutes, homogenizing and heat-treating at 400-420 degrees C for 24-25 hours, and extruding at 350-450 degrees C at 1 mm/second with an extrusion ratio of 16:1. The semi-solid magnesium-aluminum alloy melt comprises 20-40 %mass, preferably 30 %mass aluminum. The usage amount of magnesium-calcium composite particles is 60-100 %mass, preferably 60 %mass based on the amount of semi-solid alloy melt. The constant-temperature double helical mechanical-stirring is performed by mechanically stirring in a clockwise direction at 550-600 degrees C and 450-1600 rpm for 10-30 minutes. The mass ratio of magnesium block and calcium block is 1:0.4-0.5, preferably 1:0.4. The protective atmosphere is a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and 5-6 vol.% sulfur hexafluoride.

PN CN109621924-A; CN109621924-B
TI Preparing greigite-magnetic nanomaterial comprises dissolving ferric chloride hexahydrate and thiourea in ethylene glycol, magnetic stirring, transferring the mixed solution to polytetrafluoroethylene tank and heating.
AU LI Y
   WANG X
   LIU W
   DONG G
   WANG H
   ZHOU P
   GAO M
AE UNIV WENZHOU MEDICAL (WEZN-C)
GA 2019394851
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing greigite-magnetic nanomaterial comprises (i) dissolving a certain amount of 1-5 mmol ferric chloride hexahydrate and 2-10 mmol thiourea in 40-80 ml ethylene glycol, 20-40 minutes magnetic stirring to make it dissolve completely and mixing uniformly, transferring the mixed solution to 100 ml polytetrafluoroethylene tank, then placing liner in the stainless steel high temperature high pressure reaction kettle and placing in the constant temperature oven heating, and setting the temperature at 180 degrees C, and reacting for 12 hours, (ii) naturally cooling to room temperature, then collecting the solid product after the reaction, and washing with deionized water, carbon disulfide and ethanol three times, and then performing magnetic separation, and drying obtained solid in a vacuum oven at 60 degrees C for 12 hours, and cooling the sample to room temperature in a vacuum oven, collecting, grinding and weighing final sample i.e. final product.
   USE - The greigite-magnetic nanomaterial is useful in preparing magnetic effervescent tablet for detecting polybrominated diphenyl ethers in river water and tap water, whole fat and skim milk, and maternal serum and normal female serum by magnetic effervescent tablet-assisted ionic liquid-based dispersion liquid-liquid extraction method (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The greigite-magnetic nanomaterial is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing magnetic effervescent tablet comprising (i) storing sodium carbonate and sodium dihydrogen phosphate as an effervescent precursor in an oven at 90 degrees C for 3 hours and storing in a desiccator for use. mixing a whole portion of 0.452 g of effervescent precursor, 10 mg greigite-magnetic nanomaterial and 10 mu l (CnMIM)(PF 6) (n=4-8) and grinding, where the effervescent precursor comprises 0.212 g sodium carbonate and 0.24 g sodium dihydrogen phosphate, and (ii) compressing uniform powder consisting of the above three parts into a magnetic effervescent tablet by a 0.4T tablet press in 1 minute, placing obtained tablets in a sealed bag and placing in a desiccator for use; and
   (2) detecting polybrominated diphenyl ethers using magnetic effervescent tablet-assisted ionic liquid-based dispersion liquid-liquid micro-extraction technique, comprising (i) placing 7 ml sample in a water bath at 30 degrees C, (ii) placing magnetic effervescent tablet into the test tube, and a large amount of carbon dioxide bubbles are formed from the bottom up due to the effervescence reaction, and disintegrates the effervescent tablet collapses completely within 1 minute, and uniformly dispersing (CnMIM)(PF6) and greigite-magnetic nanomaterial in the water sample, and at the same time, and are simultaneously extracting analytes into (C n MIM)(PF 6 ) and greigite-magnetic nanomaterial, (iii) placing neodymium magnet at the bottom end of the test tube can be coated with (CnMIM) (PF6) without centrifuging and analyte can be adsorbed to the bottom of the test tube, (iv) discarding the supernatant, and precipitating magnetic nanoparticles at the bottom can be eluted with 500 mu l, 300 mu l, and 200 mu l of acetonitrile, and separating the magnetic nanoparticles and the acetonitrile eluate containing the test substance each time using neodymium magnet, combining the three acetonitrile eluates, and filtering with 0.22- mu m filter, and (v) drying the filtered acetonitrile eluent, adding 60 mu l acetonitrile to re-dissolve and transfering to an auto-injection vial containing an end cannula, and carrying out measurement by placing 20 mu l each time in an high-performance liquid chromatography with diode array detector system.
   p
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The analytical conditions for the high-performance liquid chromatography with diode array detector system for detecting polybrominated diphenyl ethers are: Japan Hitachi CM5000 high performance liquid chromatograph-5430 diode array detector, LaChrom C18 column, 150x 4.6 mm, 5 mu m; mobile phase and ratio, acetonitrile and water in a ratio of 85:15; column temperature, 25 degrees C; flow rate of 1 mlmin-1; injection volume, 20 mu l; detection wavelength, gamma is 226 nm.

TI Traditional Chinese medicine composition useful for e.g. promoting hair blacking, comprises e.g. rhizoma polygonati, medlar, lindera root leaf, Eucommia staminate flower, mulberry, yam, gordon euryale seed, tuckahoe and black bean.
AU ZHOU S
AE ANHUI LVZHIRUN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019206268
AB    NOVELTY - Traditional Chinese medicine composition comprises 7-16 pts. wt. rhizoma polygonati, 2-9 pts. wt. medlar, 11-19 pts. wt. lindera root leaf, 12-30 pts. wt. Eucommia staminate flower, 16-18 pts. wt. mulberry, 10-14 pts. wt. yam, 7-10 pts. wt. gordon euryale seed, 6-14 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 12-18 pts. wt. black bean, 16-30 pts. wt. black sesame, 5-10 pts. wt. lotus seed, 10-15 pts. wt. raspberry, 5-10 pts. wt. silkworm, 2-5 pts. wt. colla corii asini, 5-15 pts. wt. polygonum multiflorum, 5-10 pts. wt. cistanche, 44-60 pts. wt. maca and 15-35 pts. wt. honey.
   USE - The composition is useful for promoting hair blacking (claimed), tonifying the kidney, nourishing qi, promoting blood circulation and nourishing human body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for preparing the composition, comprising (i) taking the raw material, cutting the rhizoma polygonati, medlar, tuckahoe, eucommia staminate flower, mulberry, yam, gordon euryale seed, polygonum multiflorum, maca and cistanche into sections and drying, drying the silkworm, black sesame and lotus seed, grinding into powder and sieving the obtained powder, (ii) mixing the medlar, lindera root leaf, raspberry, gordon euryale seed and yam, boiling, filtering the mixed components and retaining the liquid components, recording the obtained liquid components as the liquid a, boiling the black beans, the black beans are decoctioned, filtered, drying the obtained solid components, grinding together with mulberry, sieving, blending the obtained powder with black sesame powder to obtain a powder a, filtering the black beans decoction, steaming the liquid component obtained into the mashed Polygonum multiflorum, drying and crushing the obtained components, (iii) adding the Eucommia flower, medlar, cistanche and huangjing into the pot, stir frying, adding the lotus root powder, honey and the lotus seed powder treated in step (i) treated in step (ii), continuing to medium heat, stir frying until the honey is completely absorbed by the medicinal material, after the end of the stir fry, air-drying the components and recording the obtained components as component b, (iv) washing and cutting the maca for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, where the extraction parameters are pressure 35-45MPa, the temperature is 55-65 degrees C and the time is 1.5-3 hours with ethanol as cosolvent, where the ethanol amount is 25-30% of the volume of the extraction kettle, the volume of maca is 5-15% of the volume of the extraction kettle, and collecting the material after the extraction, (v) melting the gelatin in a frying pan, blending the powder a and the component b into a frying pan, adding water to make the liquid component have a mass of 10-15 times that of the solid component, boiling for 1-3 hours, waiting until the liquid is reduced to 30-50% of the original liquid, adding water to the original water level again, cooking for 0.5-1.5 hours, mixing the liquid component obtained after boiling with the extract obtained in the step (iv), concentrating the mixed component into an extract and drying the extract to obtain an extract powder; and #the use of the traditional Chinese medicine compositions in a tablet, a granule, a capsule, and a supplement, obtained by adding an auxiliary material which is acceptable in the medicinal field to the active ingredient of the traditional Chinese medicine composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The traditional Chinese medicine composition comprises 14 pts. wt. rhizoma polygonati, 4 pts. wt. medlar, 16 pts. wt. lindera root leaf, 25 pts. wt. eucommia staminate flower, 16 pts. wt. mulberry, 13 pts. wt. yam, 9 pts. wt. gordon euryale seed, 12 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 16 pts. wt. black bean, 20 pts. wt. black sesame, 8 pts. wt. lotus seed, 13 pts. wt. raspberry, 9 pts. wt. silkworm, 4 pts. wt. colla corii asini, 8 pts. wt. polygonum multiflorum, 6 pts. wt. cistanche, 55 pts. wt. maca and 20 pts. wt. honey. Preferred Components: The maca is black maca. The honey is royal jelly. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the Polygonum multiflorum is wrapped in a gauze for sputum and the Polygonum multiflorum is dried before the sputum.

PN CN109339550-A
TI Manufacturing extra-high voltage direct-current transmission tower comprises processing iron tower components according to the design requirements.
AU QI S
   ZHOU X
   LI J
AE JIANGSU QITIAN IRON TOWER MFG CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201918507A
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing extra-high voltage direct-current transmission tower comprises processing iron tower components according to the design requirements, where the raw materials and fasteners for the angle steel and steel plates used to manufacture the transmission line tower are prepared; the sum of the inner and outer surface protrusions, the surface of the pockmarks and the pits of the angle steel is not greater than the inner and outer areas of the angle steel 8%, the depth of the pit not greater than 0.6 mm, the height of the protrusion is not greater than 1 mm; the maximum thickness of the angle steel and steel plate allows shearing of 10-20 mm; the ambient temperature of the angle steel and steel plate during cold working is not less than -20 degrees C, the ambient temperature is not less than -16 degrees C during cold correction, and the ambient temperature is not less than -6 degrees C during cold bending; when making curved plates, the intersecting distance of bending line is formed before bending the sides of the bending line are polished within 40 mm.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing extra-high voltage direct-current transmission tower.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures safe operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing extra-high voltage direct-current transmission tower comprises: (i) processing iron tower components according to the design requirements, where the raw materials and fasteners for the angle steel and steel plates used to manufacture the transmission line tower are prepared; the sum of the inner and outer surface protrusions, the surface of the pockmarks and the pits of the angle steel is not greater than the inner and outer areas of the angle steel 8%, the depth of the pit not greater than 0.6 mm, the height of the protrusion is not greater than 1 mm; the maximum thickness of the angle steel and steel plate allows shearing of 10-20 mm; the ambient temperature of the angle steel and steel plate during cold working is not less than -20 degrees C, the ambient temperature is not less than -16 degrees C during cold correction, and the ambient temperature is not less than -6 degrees C during cold bending; when making curved plates, the intersecting distance of bending line is formed before bending the sides of the bending line are polished within 40 mm, and the cut burrs or cuts left by the flame cutting are removed, and the edges are chamfered by 3 mm; the angles of the large-size corners are just opened and bent, the processing method adopts a hot working method, and the hot bending temperature is 900-950 degrees C; the iron tower assembly is processed according to the method; and (ii) processing welding component by welding the welding parts with the main angle steel which are positioned by the positioning of the tire mold, the positioning pin of each contact surface is not less than 2, the contact surface is 3-4 holes with 1 set of locking bolts, and then each 200-250 mm increase in 1 set of locking bolts, the positioning pin adopts diagonal positioning, the locking bolts are locked by the upper and lower spacing; the welding is carried out using alkaline low-hydrogen welding rods, before welding, the welding rods are dried and treated at 350-450 degrees C for 2-3 hours, drying, stored in an oven at 100-150 degrees C, the base metal to be welded at 100-200 degrees C, preheating for 10-20 minutes, welding with wind speed of less than 1 m/second, where the welding gas includes 80-85% argon and 15-20% carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Method: The manufacturing of extra-high voltage direct-current transmission tower comprises: (i) processing iron tower components according to the design requirements, where the raw materials and fasteners for the angle steel and steel plates used to manufacture the transmission line tower are prepared; the sum of the inner and outer surface protrusions, the surface of the pockmarks and the pits of the angle steel is not greater than the inner and outer areas of the angle steel 8%, the depth of the pit not greater than 0.6 mm, the height of the protrusion is not greater than 1 mm; the maximum thickness of the angle steel and steel plate allows shearing of 10-20 mm; the ambient temperature of the angle steel and steel plate during cold working is not less than -20 degrees C, the ambient temperature is not less than -16 degrees C during cold correction, and the ambient temperature is not less than -6 degrees C during cold bending; when making curved plates, the intersecting distance of bending line is formed before bending the sides of the bending line are polished within 40 mm, and the cut burrs or cuts left by the flame cutting are removed, and the edges are chamfered by 3 mm; the angles of the large-size corners are just opened and bent, the processing method adopts a hot working method, and the hot bending temperature is 900-950 degrees C; the iron tower assembly is processed according to the method; (ii) processing welding component by welding the welding parts with the main angle steel which are positioned by the positioning of the tire mold, the positioning pin of each contact surface is not less than 2, the contact surface is 3-4 holes with 1 set of locking bolts, and then each 200-250 mm increase in 1 set of locking bolts, the positioning pin adopts diagonal positioning, the locking bolts are locked by the upper and lower spacing; the welding is carried out using alkaline low-hydrogen welding rods, before welding, the welding rods are dried and treated at 350-450 degrees C for 2-3 hours, drying, stored in an oven at 100-150 degrees C, the base metal to be welded at 100-200 degrees C, preheating for 10-20 minutes, welding with wind speed of less than 1 m/second, where the welding gas includes 80-85% argon and 15-20% carbon dioxide; (iii) post-weld stress relief treatment of welded parts; except for the bottoming and capping layers without hammering elimination treatment, the other parts of each layer shall be hammered to eliminate stress method, the metal in the weld and the near seam area is extended and deformed to compensate or offset the compression plastic deformation generated during welding for lowering the welding residual stress; (iv) the head of the tower is assembled vertically, and all the bolts are tightened and installed to check the pre-arching value of the cross-hook line; the tower body is assembled with 4 faces, all components are assembled according to the construction requirements, the test assembly site is flat, and the whole tower is based on the connection between the height of the base of the tower at the lowest height and the elevation of the tower head by 200 mm, the height of the main control support points is determined; the direction of penetration of the bolts is the same as the direction of installation at the construction site, the horizontal direction is from the inside to the outside, the vertical direction is from the bottom to the top, and the oblique direction should be obliquely inclined downwards; the test assembly starts from the lower position of the tower head and is assembled in the order of the tower joint and the leg in the order of the joint, and is connected to the lowest call height of the tower type; assembling, but each leg section must be assembled and connected with the tower body section simultaneously; all the connected sections must be tested and assembled, and the part number is tested by the test assembly connection; the head of the tower is assembled vertically, the vertical assembly part is assembled on the flat ground and then mounted by the machine, the bolts are pre-tightened, all assembled vertically, and the dimensions are adjusted, one unified tightening and reinforcement; the main material of the prosthetic leg should be welded firmly and the ribs should be reinforced, after adjusting the center, the ottoman at both ends of the cross arm should be withdrawn to ensure the required pre-arching; the tower body is assembled horizontally, the materials used for the four faces are all assembled; assembly of the leg pts., each leg tower of the height is assembled with two legs close to the ground, the hanging point uses a pin that is 0.5 m smaller than the hanging hole to check the coaxiality of the hanging hole; the assembly sequence in each section is firstly complete a bottom surface, all components are installed and connected, then using the locomotive to hoist the upper main material of the upper side, connecting the oblique material on the side, then an upper main material on the other side is attached, and the oblique material on the side is coupled, the 45 degrees support rod member supporting the side is correspondingly coupled in time, top slanting material, the two sides further assembled on the ground and assembled as a whole; (v) demolition and assembly of the iron tower is carried out by assembling the assembled tower by sectional disassembly; where the disassembly sequence in each section is first removing the oblique material on the top surface, removing the two side parts separately, when removing one side, then removing the connecting bolts on the diagonal material on the side and the main material on the bottom surface, then disassemble the entire side as a whole, lifting the entire side and slowly laying it flat to the ground, removing the other side in the same way, finally disassembling the joint oblique material on the bottom surface; (vi) galvanizing the detached iron tower assembly, subjecting to rust removal and hot dip galvanizing treatment, forming iron-zinc alloy layer and pure zinc layer on the surface of the component from inside to outside; and (vii) packing the angle steel with steel strips, and packing the iron plates by basic frame bolt connection or bolt insertion connection. The galvanizing process comprises: hanging the workpiece to be galvanized, pickling and rust removal, rinsing, supporting, galvanizing, cooling, passivating and rinsing. The method comprises during the pickling and rust removal process, using greater than or equal to 28% industrial hydrochloric acid, adding water into 40-200 g/l pickling aqueous solution, during pickling and rust removal, the concentration of the pickling aqueous solution drops to less than 40 g/l and the ferrous chloride content rises more than 200 g/l, treating with spent acid; when the content of ferrous chloride in the pickling aqueous solution is less than 200 g/l, when the concentration of hydrochloric acid is less than 40 g/l, proper amount of industrial hydrochloric acid are added into the pickling aqueous solution. The pickling and rust removal is carried out for 20-25 minutes using 180-200 g/l hydrochloric acid aqueous solution. The pickling and rust removal is carried out for 25-30 minutes using 150-180 g/l hydrochloric acid aqueous solution. The pickling and rust removal is carried out for 30-40 minutes using 100-150 g/l hydrochloric acid aqueous solution. The pickling and rust removal is carried out for 40-45 minutes using 40-100 g/l hydrochloric acid aqueous solution. The workpiece is pickled up and down for 2-3 times, and proposed liquid level inspection pickling and derusting quality; the surface of the partially under-washed and rust-removed surface is removed by a grinding wheel, a wire brush or other tools, followed by pickling and rust removal. The plating process is carried out at 435-445 degrees C for 1-2 minutes, during galvanizing, the surface of the workpiece is immersed in zinc liquid to a depth of 100-400 mm, where the content of zinc chloride or ammonium chloride is 80-150 g/l. The pickling and rusting process comprises degreasing the workpiece, degreasing the paint, minerals, synthetic resin, tar or other attachments attached to the surface during the manufacturing process including sanding or heat roasting; when galvanizing works continuously, according to the actual situation, the slag is taken, reacting the zinc solution at 450-460 degrees C before the slag is removed, after the zinc slag sinks, then removing the zinc slag.
DC M13 (Coating material with metals, diffusion processes, enamelling and vitreous coatings - including coating from liquid metal or solution, spraying, cementation, cathodic sputtering, enamelling and oilfree lubricant coatings, but not coatings for the production of semiconductors (C23C, D).); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); Q46 (Building aids, special structures, ladders (E04G,H, E06C))
MC M13-A; D09-C; Q46-B10
IP C23C-002/06; E04H-012/00; E04H-012/08

PT P
PN CN109321526-A
TI Culturing rat liver macrophages involves dissolving collagenase in Kupffer cell-specific basal medium to prepare digestive juice, and preheating in water bath, and weight sodium heparin is dissolving in Kupffer cell-specific medium.
AU KE Y
   BAO T
   WANG Y
   LI Y
   WANG L
AE UNIV KUNMING MEDICAL SECOND AFFILIATED (UKMM-C)
GA 2019172580
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing rat liver macrophages involves dissolving 100 mg collagenase in 100 ml Kupffer cell-specific basal medium to prepare 0.1% digestive juice, and preheating in water bath at 40 degrees C for 10 minutes. The weight 300 mg sodium heparin is dissolved in 100 ml Kupffer cell-specific medium to prepare a 0.3% washing solution, and preheated in water bath at 40 degrees C for 10 minutes. Take 1 healthy female Sprague-Dawley (SD) rat, weigh about 200g, anesthetize the rats with isoflurane anesthesia machine, and wipe the whole body skin of rats with iodophor disinfectant. The rat is fixed in the supine position, the cross-shaped incision opens the abdominal cavity, and the stomach and intestine are pushed to the left side of the rat to expose the liver.
   USE - Method for culturing rat liver macrophages.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to culture rat liver macrophages, which has established hepatic macrophage function, which plays an important role in the study of hepatic macrophages in fibrosis, hepatitis, hepatic obesity and liver transplantation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The indwelling needle is insert in the hepatic portal vein and ligature the infusion needle with the surgical suture, quickly cut the inferior vena cava from below, and inject the 80ml washing solution from the indwelling needle immediately after cutting the inferior vena cava with perfusion flow rate is 12ml/minute. The inferior vena cava incision is blocked with a sterile cotton ball for sufficient perfusion, during the perfusion period, and the cotton ball is replaced once during the infusion period. The weight 50 ml digestive juice is poured, after the cleaning is completed, the flow rate is 10 ml/minute, and the cotton ball is replaced every 30 seconds during the perfusion. The liver is cut into a Petri dish, and the liver is cut to a size of 1.5 mm3 by an ophthalmic scissors, transferred into a 30 ml digestive juice, and digested at 37 degrees C at 190 rmp/ml for 18 minutes. The digested cell suspension is filter with 70 mu m cell sieve filter and centrifuge at 100 g/minute for 3 minutes to get supernatant. The supernatant is taken in another 50 centrifuge tube, centrifuge at 400g/minute for 8 minutes, discard the supernatant, and resuspend the cells with 10ml of washing solution. The resuspend the cells are poured into 50 ml centrifuge tube, then add 10 ml of 65% percoll separation solution, add 10 ml of 35% percoll separation solution to the upper layer, and finally add 10 ml cell suspension to the uppermost layer, then centrifuge at 950g/ml for 20 minutes. The cell suspension is carefully absorb with the white membrane layer at the junction of 35% and 65% percoll separation solution with a Pasteur pipette, resuspend the cells with 10ml washing solution, centrifuge the cells at 400g/minute for 8 minutes and discard the supernatant. Resuspend the cells in 2 ml Kupffer dedicated complete medium, count, adjust the concentration to 105 cells/ml, inoculate 5 ml of cell suspension to 100 mu g/ml polylysine (15-30 million units) overnight and cultured in T25 bottle at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide incubator and inoculated for 2.5 hours. The medium is discarded, and the adherent cells are gently washed three times with a dedicated medium, and further culture is continued after adding 5 ml of complete medium to obtain the desired process.

PN CN109322215-A
TI Preparing pressure-resistant and flame-retardant paper box, comprises e.g. soaking corrugated paper in warm water, soaking, adding sodium hydroxide solution, beating, centrifuging, kneading, extruding, pulverizing and hot pressing.
AU LI C
AE MAANSHAN KANGHUI CARTON BOX & PAPER CO (MAAN-Non-standard)
GA 201917242M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pressure-resistant and flame-retardant paper box, comprises (i) soaking corrugated paper in warm water, soaking the corrugated paper into the stirrer, immersing, adding sodium hydroxide solution, and beating, (ii) allowing to stand the obtained pulp slurry, removing the suspended impurities, centrifuging, and washing the obtained pulp with distilled water until neutral and filtering, (iii) mixing the modified polyethylene terephthalate and polyethylene, placing in a kneader, heating, kneading, pouring into a twin-screw extruder through a twin screw granulated extruder is used to prepare modified polyethylene terephthalate-polyethylene blend pellets, and (iv) pulverizing the modified polyethylene terephthalate-polyethylene composite by a mill and mixed with pulp, pouring into a paper machine, injecting into the paper machine through a metering pump and the injection pressure is 15-20 MPa and hot pressing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing pressure-resistant and flame-retardant paper box.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple. The paper box has good flame retarding and compressive effect, and has a good prospect of popularization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing pressure-resistant and flame-retardant paper box, comprises (i) soaking corrugated paper in warm water, soaking the corrugated paper into the stirrer, immersing for 3-5 hours, adding the sodium hydroxide solution with the concentration of 1-2%, and beating at the speed of 500-800 revolutions/minutes for 2-3 hours, (ii) allowing to stand yhe obtained pulp slurry for 5-8 hours, removing the suspended impurities, centrifuging at 4000-6000 revolutions/minute for 20-30 minutes, and washing the obtained pulp with distilled water until neutral and filtering, (iii) drying polyethylene terephthalate and polyethylene in an oven at 100-120 degrees C and 70-80 degrees C for 10-15 hours, respectively, and adding polyethylene terephthalate and 2-3 wt.% aluminum hydroxide, 1-2 wt.% cross-linking agent and 0.5-1 wt.% stabilizer, mixing, adding into a twin-screw extruder, which is modified by twin-screw melting and extrusion granulation, where the temperature of each section of the extruder is controlled at 250 degrees C, 280 degrees C, 320 degrees C, 270 degrees C and the rotation speed is 200 revolutions/minute, (iv) mixing the modified polyethylene terephthalate and polyethylene, placing in a kneader, heating to 230-260 degrees C, kneading for 10-15 minutes, pouring into a twin-screw extruder through a twin screw granulated extruder is used to prepare modified polyethylene terephthalate-polyethylene blend pellets, where the temperature of each section of the extruder is controlled at 200 degrees C, 240 degrees C, 250 degrees C, 230 degrees C and speed is 180 revolutions/minute, (v) drying the modified polyethylene terephthalate-polyethylene blend pellets in a vacuum oven at 70-80 degrees C for 5-8 hours, pouring into a twin-screw extruder, and adding into the equivalent modified polyethylene terephthalate-polyethylene blend pellets 3-5 wt.% nano-silica, melt blending in a twin-screw extruder to produce modified polyethylene terephthalate a diol ester-polyethylene composite, where the temperature of each section of the extruder is controlled at 230 degrees C, 260 degrees C, 275 degrees C, 245 degrees C and the rotation speed is 230 revolutions/minutes, and (vi) pulverizing the modified polyethylene terephthalate-polyethylene composite by a mill and mixed with pulp, pouring into a paper machine, and injecting the supercritical carbon dioxide into the paper machine through a metering pump, the injection amount is 8-12 wt.% modified polyethylene terephthalate-polyethylene composite, and the injection pressure is 15-20 MPa, which is copied into a paper sheet by a paper machine, drying and baking, drying at 50-60 degrees C for 3-4 hours, and subjecting the paper sheet into a hot pressing process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the temperature of warm water is 50-80 degrees C. In the step (iii), the particle diameter of aluminum hydroxide is 10-30 nm. In the step (vi), the hot pressing temperature in the hot pressing process is 80-85 degrees C, and the hot pressing time is 2-3 minutes. Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the polyethylene is a low density polyethylene, and the mass ratio of the polyethylene terephthalate to the polyethylene is 15-20:80-90. In the step (iii), the crosslinking agent comprises dibenzoyl peroxide, dicumyl peroxide and/or 2,5-dimethyl-2,5 di-tert-butylperoxyhexane. The stabilizer comprises phenyl o-hydroxybenzoate, resorcinol monobenzoate and/or hexamethylphosphoric triamide. In the step (vi), the particle diameter of modified polyethylene terephthalate-polyethylene composite is 10-30 nm after pulverization.
