PN WO2019020419-A1; EP3471971-A1; TW201910355-A; EP3471971-B1; CN110536804-A; US2020071505-A1; SG11201909798-A1; KR2020036806-A; JP2020528465-W; RU2019134190-A
TI Preparing synthetic rubber blend having diene polymer derived from butadiene monomers, comprises anionically polymerizing butadiene monomers and optionally monomer of e.g. aromatic vinyl monomers and further conjugated diene monomers.
AU EBERT H
   HEIDENREICH D
   THIELE S K H
   TILESBEN-KH
AE TRINSEO EURO GMBH (STRY-C)
   TRINSEO EURO GMBH (STRY-C)
   TRINSEO EURO GMBH (STRY-C)
GA 2019122856
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of a synthetic rubber blend comprising high molecular weight diene polymer (A) derived from butadiene monomers, comprises anionically polymerizing butadiene monomers and optionally at least one monomer of aromatic vinyl monomers, alpha -olefins and further conjugated diene monomers in presence of polymerization initiator in organic solvent, where the step of polymerizing butadiene monomers and optionally aromatic vinyl monomers, alpha -olefins and further conjugated diene monomers comprises (i) first stage of providing butadiene monomers and optionally aromatic vinyl monomers, alpha -olefins and further conjugated diene monomers and first portion of polymerization initiator having organolithium compound, group IIa metal salt, and organoaluminum compound, and polymerizing butadiene monomer to give (A), and (ii) subsequent second stage of adding second portion of polymerization initiator having organolithium compound and polymerizing to obtain blend of (A) and (B1).
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of the synthetic rubber blend which is useful in rubber composition for preparing crosslinked rubber composition, where the crosslinked rubber composition is useful in a cured rubber composition and an article comprising a tire, a tire tread, a tire side wall, a conveyer belt, a seal or a hose (all claimed), preferably tire for trucks.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides the synthetic rubber blend that exhibits good mechanical properties and improved tire performance, reduction of fuel consumption and carbon dioxide emission and safety requirements (associated with grip performance and abrasion resistance); and provides the crosslinked polymer formulation that exhibits reduced DIN abrasion, maintains tensile strength and elongation at break, and low tan delta at 60 degrees C values.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of a synthetic rubber blend comprising a high molecular weight diene polymer (A) derived from butadiene monomers and, optionally, alpha olefins and further conjugated diene monomers, polymer (A) having 55-100 wt.% of units derived from butadiene monomers and 0-45 wt.% of units derived from aromatic vinyl monomers, alpha -olefins and further conjugated diene monomers, polymer (A) further having a number average molecular weight (Mn) of 50000-1000000 g/mol, where 65 wt.% or more of the butadiene monomers are incorporated into the polymer chains in the form of the trans isomer and a low molecular weight diene polymer (B1) having a number average molecular weight (Mn) of 250-10000 g/mol, comprises anionically polymerizing butadiene monomers and optionally at least one monomer of aromatic vinyl monomers, alpha -olefins and further conjugated diene monomers in the presence of at least one polymerization initiator in an organic solvent, where the step of polymerizing the butadiene monomers and optionally the aromatic vinyl monomers, alpha -olefins and further conjugated diene monomers comprises (i) a first stage of providing butadiene monomers and optionally, aromatic vinyl monomers, alpha -olefins and further conjugated diene monomers, and a first portion of a polymerization initiator comprising an organolithium compound, a group IIa metal salt, and an organoaluminum compound, and polymerizing the butadiene monomer up to a conversion rate of 80%, preferably 90%, to give (A), where 65 wt.% or more of the butadiene monomers are incorporated into the polymer chains in the form of the trans isomer, and (ii) a subsequent second stage of adding a second portion of a polymerization initiator comprising an organolithium compound and polymerizing to obtain blend of (A) and (B1). INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) synthetic rubber blend obtainable according to the method;
   (2) a rubber composition comprising the blend; and
   (3) preparation of a crosslinked rubber composition, comprising adding at least one vulcanizing agent to the synthetic rubber blend or to the rubber composition, and crosslinking the composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polymerization initiator used in stage (i) comprises n-butyllithium as the organolithium compound, the barium salt of di(ethyleneglycol)ethyl ether as the group lla metal salt and tri-n-octylaluminum as the organoaluminum compound.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The butadiene monomers and/or the optional further conjugated diene monomer comprises 1,3-butadiene, 2-alkyl-1,3-butadiene, 2-methyl-1,3-butadiene, 2,3-dimethyl-1,3-butadiene, 1,3-pentadiene, 2,4-hexadiene, 1,3-hexadiene, 1,3-heptadiene, 1,3-octadiene, 2-methyl-2,4-pentadiene, cyclopentadiene, 2,4-hexadiene and/or 1,3-cyclooctadiene, preferably 1,3-butadiene and/or 2-methyl-1,3-butadiene and/or the vinyl aromatic monomer comprises styrene, 2-methylstyrene, 3-methylstyrene, 4-methylstyrene, 2,4-dimethylstyrene, 2,4,6-trimethylstyrene, alpha -methylstyrene, stilbene, 2,4-diisopropylstyrene, 4-tert-butylstyrene, vinyl benzyl dimethylamine, (4-vinylbenzyl)dimethyl aminoethyl ether, N,N-dimethylaminoethyl styrene, N,N-bis-(trialkylsilyl)aminostyrene, tert-butoxystyrene, vinylpyridine and/or divinylbenzene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: (A) and/or (B1) are modified by way of addition of and reaction with at least one functionalizing component. The first and/or the second stage of polymerizing the butadiene monomers and optionally the aromatic vinyl monomers, alpha olefins and further conjugated diene monomers is carried out at 60-150 degrees C. Preferred Components: The synthetic rubber blend comprises (c) 100 pts.wt. of (A) having units derived from butadiene monomers and optionally aromatic vinyl monomers, alpha olefins and further conjugated diene monomers, further having a number average molecular weight of 50000-1000000 g/mol, where 65 wt.% or more of the diene monomers are incorporated into the polymer chains in the form of the trans isomer, and (d) 0.01-20 pts.wt. of (B1) having units derived from butadiene monomers and optionally aromatic vinyl monomers, alpha olefins and further conjugated diene monomers, and further having a number average molecular weight of 250-10000 g/mol. (A) has a number average molecular weight (Mn) of 100000-180000 g/mol and/or a weight average molecular weight (Mw) of 280000-500000 g/mol and/or a polydispersity Mw/Mn of 2.8-3.8. Preferred Composition: The composition further comprises: at least one additional rubber of styrene butadiene rubber, butadiene rubber, synthetic isoprene rubber or natural rubber; and filler, preferably silica.
   EXAMPLE - 4264 g Cyclohexane, 490 g of butadiene (15%) and 148 g of styrene were charged to an air free 10 l reactor and the stirred mixture was heated up to 80 degrees C. n-Butyl lithium was charged dropwise to react the impurities until the color of the reaction mixture changed to yellowish (titration). Afterwards the recipe quantity of initiator corresponding to the target molecular weight of the polymer was charged immediately to start the polymerization. The start time of the charge of the initiator was used as the start time of the polymerization. Parallel with the charge of the initiator the temperature was increased by heating the wall of the reactor to the final polymerization temperature of 110 degrees C in 30 minutes and incremental butadiene (42.5%) was charged over 30 minutes. After charging was completed the mixture was stirred at 110 degrees C for further 30 minutes before the residual butadiene (42.5%) was charged over 30 minutes. After stirring for further 30 minutes at 110 degrees C an additional amount of n-butyl lithium was dosed. The mixture was stirred at 110 degrees C for further 20 minutes before termination of the polymerization with charge of methanol. The polymer solution was stabilized with Irganox 1520D (RTM: Multifunctional phenolic antioxidant) by steam stripping and dried to obtain synthetic rubber blend.

TI Preparing artificial blood stem cells using artificial blood progenitor cells, by obtaining cord blood hematopoietic progenitor cells from human umbilical cord blood, inoculating into special transformation medium and culturing cells.
AU LIU D
   CHEN L
   YANG H
   GUO X
   QI H
   WANG X

GA 2019095241
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing artificial blood stem cells using artificial blood progenitor cells involves (a) obtaining cluster of differentiation 34 (CD34)+CD90- and CD34+CD45RA+ cord blood hematopoietic progenitor cells from human umbilical cord blood, (b) inoculating the CD34+CD90- and CD34+CD45RA+ cord blood hematopoietic progenitor cells into a special transformation medium for cord blood hematopoietic stem cells and culturing the cells, where the umbilical cord blood hematopoietic stem cell special transformation medium adopts StemSpan (RTM: SFEMII serum-free medium) and 100 ng/ml SCF, 100 ng/ml FLT3 and 50 ng/ml TPO and the cell seeding density in the 24-well plate is 0.55x 104/well for CD34+CD90-cells and 0.13x 104/well for CD34+CD45RA+ cells, adding 1 mu M sodium butyrate and culturing at 37 degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing artificial blood stem cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preferred Method: The method further involves adding 500 mu l umbilical cord blood stem cell-specific transformation medium for every 2 days and obtaining a large number of cells in 7-10 days and amplification factor for 4- 20 times. The step (a) involves (i) collecting 80-120 ml cord blood with a disposable blood bag containing anticoagulant such as heparin sodium, transferring the cord blood from the blood bag to a 500 ml culture flask, diluting with physiological saline for 2-3 times, mixing and adding 0.4 times lymphocyte separation solution, (ii) CD34+ cord blood hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells from the above PBMC by using magnetic-activated cell sorting (MACS) by resuspending each umbilical cord blood peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) in a mixture of 50 ul human CD34+ magnetic beads, 50 ul FcR blocker and 150 ul BSA (0.5%) and incubating at 4 degrees C for 30 minutes, irradiating the magnet and the magnetic frame to the ultra-clean stage for ultraviolet light for 30 minutes, adding 10 ml sterile phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), mixing the material, performing centrifugation at 1500-2000 revolution per minute for 5-10 minutes and discarding the supernatant, adding the MACS special adsorption column into the magnet, adding 500 ul BSA (0.5%) and discarding the liquid by 15 ml tube, resuspending the PBMC agglomerate with 500 ul BSA (0.5%), mixing and transferring to the special adsorption column of MACS until the liquid completely flows out and washing 3 times with 500 ul BSA (0.5%), taking the adsorption, placing in 15 ml tube, adding 0.5% BSA, pushing the liquid into 15 ml tube with piston and diluting the resulting liquid contained CD34+ cord blood hematopoietic stem and (iii) staining the CD34+ cells with flow cytometry CD34, CD90, CD45RA for 0.5 hour with S13 and sorting CD34+CD90- and CD34+CD45RA+ hematopoietic progenitor cells by flow sorter, placing the solution in a centrifuge tube, performing centrifugation at 1500-2000 revolutions per minute for 20 minutes, dividing the centrifuge tube into 4 layers from top to bottom due to different densities, where the first layer is plasma layer, the second layer is ring-shaped milky white mononuclear cell layer PBMC, the third layer is a transparent separation liquid layer and the fourth layer is an red blood cell layer, pipetting the second layer of ring-shaped milky white mononuclear cell layer PBMC into another 50 ml centrifuge tube, adding physiological salt, centrifuging at 1500-2000 revolutions per minute for 5-10 minutes, discarding the supernatant, resuspending with normal saline, centrifuging at 1500-2000 revolutions per minute for 5-10 minutes and discarding the supernatant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The obtained liquid is frozen in liquid nitrogen with a cryoprotectant dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO). Preferred Method: The method further involves adding 500 mu l umbilical cord blood stem cell-specific transformation medium for every 2 days and obtaining a large number of cells in 7-10 days and amplification factor for 4- 20 times. The step (a) involves (i) collecting 80-120 ml cord blood with a disposable blood bag containing anticoagulant such as heparin sodium, transferring the cord blood from the blood bag to a 500 ml tube with piston and diluting the resulting liquid contained CD34+ cord blood hematopoietic stem and (iii) staining the CD34+ cells with flow cytometry CD34, CD90, CD45RA for 0.5 hour with S13 and sorting CD34+CD90- and CD34+CD45RA+ hematopoietic progenitor cells by flow sorter, placing the solution in a centrifuge tube, performing centrifugation at 1500-2000 revolutions per minute for 20 minutes, dividing the centrifuge tube into 4 layers from top to bottom due to different densities, where the first layer is plasma layer, the second layer is ring-shaped milky white mononuclear cell layer PBMC, the third layer is a transparent separation liquid layer and the fourth layer is an red blood cell layer, pipetting the second layer of ring-shaped milky white mononuclear cell layer PBMC into another 50 ml centrifuge tube, adding physiological salt, centrifuging at 1500-2000 revolutions per minute for 5-10 minutes, discarding the supernatant, resuspending with normal saline, centrifuging at 1500-2000 revolutions per minute for 5-10 minutes and discarding the supernatant.

PN CN108912871-A; CN108912871-B
TI Preparing aqueous transparent heat insulating glass coating comprises e.g. adding tetrabutyl titanate and anhydrous ethanol to reaction vessel, stirring to obtain titanium alkoxide solution, and mixing titanium alkoxide solution in water.
AU HU C
   MEI C
   WANG S
AE FOSHAN GAOMING ZHUAHE NEW MATERIAL TECHN (FOSH-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI JINTAIZI ARCHITECTURAL DECORATION (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018983940
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing an aqueous transparent heat insulating glass coating comprises e.g. adding tetrabutyl titanate and anhydrous ethanol to the reaction vessel, stirring to obtain a titanium alkoxide solution, and then mixing titanium alkoxide solution in water, adjusting the pH of solution with hydrochloric acid, and reacting to obtain a titanium oxide sol, mixing the above titanium dioxide sol with phenol to obtain a mixture, and then mixing the mixture and n-heptane in an equal mass ratio to obtain a pre-reaction liquid, stirring, and continuously adding formaldehyde solution of the pre-reaction liquid to the reaction vessel, allowing to stand, to obtain the pre-product, and placing the pre-product in a muffle furnace and calcining to obtain a porous titanium nanocrystal of titanium dioxide, adding deionized water, stearic acid amide, polyethylene glycol to the high-speed dispersing machine, and mixing uniformly and then adding prepared titanium dioxide nanocrystals.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing aqueous transparent heat insulating glass coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces glass coating has an ultra-high adhesion, has high performance of the aqueous-based transparent heat-insulating glass coating, decomposes the organic pollutants adsorbed on the surface of the nano-titanium dioxide into carbon dioxide and oxygen, and maintains the original brightness of the glass, which can achieve a good insulation effect which does not affect the transparency of glass, has strong adhesion, long service life, good water resistance, strong anti-adhesive and anti-staining properties, strong UV resistance, high transparency, good thermal insulation effect; and decomposes indoor harmful organic compounds, namely formaldehyde.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing an aqueous transparent heat insulating glass coating comprises (i) adding tetrabutyl titanate and anhydrous ethanol to the reaction vessel at a rotation speed of 200-300 revolutions/minute (rpm), stirring to obtain a titanium alkoxide solution, and then mixing titanium alkoxide solution in water, adjusting the pH of solution with hydrochloric acid, and reacting to obtain a titanium oxide sol; (ii) mixing the above titanium dioxide sol with phenol to obtain a mixture, and then mixing the mixture and n-heptane in an equal mass ratio to obtain a pre-reaction liquid, stirring, and continuously adding 10% formaldehyde solution of the pre-reaction liquid to the reaction vessel, allowing to stand, to obtain the pre-product, and placing the pre-product in a muffle furnace and calcining to obtain a porous titanium nanocrystal of titanium dioxide; (iii) adding 80-100 pts. wt. deionized water, 8-10 pts. wt. stearic acid amide, 8-10 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol to the high-speed dispersing machine, and mixing uniformly, then adding prepared 30-40 pts. wt. titanium dioxide nanocrystals and adjusting a pH with sodium hydrogencarbonate, and dispersing and sanding at a rotation speed of 3000-4000 rpm to obtain a titanium dioxide nano slurry and reserving; (iv) adding ethylene glycol methacrylate phosphate and polytetrafluoroethylene resin to an ultrasonic oscillator, ultrasonically mixing to prepare a premix, and adding the premix to a reflux condenser and a stirrer in the reaction vessel, heating the reactor, stirring at a number of revolutions of 600-800 rpm, and reacting to discharge to obtain a modified polytetrafluoroethylene resin; and (v) adding 10-12 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol, 8-10 pts. wt. polydimethylsiloxane, 9-13 pts. wt. propylene glycol butyl ether, and 10-15 pts. wt. sodium carboxymethylcellulose and 400-500 pts. wt. polytetrafluoroethylene resin to the high-speed disperser, dispersing at a high speed of 1000-1500 rpm, then reducing the rotation speed to 400-500 rpm, and adding 20-30 pts. wt. spare titanium dioxide nano-paste, and continuously stirring.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the molar ratio of tetrabutyl titanate to absolute ethanol is 9:2. The molar ratio of titanium alkoxide and water is 25:3, the concentration of hydrochloric acid is 0.5 mol/l, the pH is adjusted to 3-5, and the reaction time is 30-60 minutes. In the step (ii), the molar ratio of titanium dioxide sol to the phenol is 4:1, and the concentration of formaldehyde solution is 1 mol/l, the temperature is 80-90 degrees C, the time is 1.5-2 hours the calcination time is 2-3 hours, and the calcination temperature is 500-600 degrees C. In the step (i), the mass fraction of sodium hydrogencarbonate is 30%, and the pH is adjusted to 7.5-8 and the time for dispersion sanding is 6-8 hours. In the step (iv), the mass ratio of diol methacrylate phosphate to polytetrafluoroethylene resin is 1:100, the mixing time is 1-1.5 hours, the temperature is raised to 80-90 degrees C, and the reaction time is 20-24 hours. In the step (v), the dispersion time is 30-40 minutes, and the stirring time is 30-40 minutes.

TI Traditional Chinese medicinal granule useful for treating vertigo, prepared by using e.g. rhizoma gastrodiae and hawthorn, extracting materials, concentrating extract, granulating concentrate, drying granules and packaging final product.
AU LI M
   DAI X
   LI K
AE LI M (LIMM-Individual)
   SHANDONG XINGU HEALTH IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018944260
AB    NOVELTY - Traditional Chinese medicinal granule is prepared by using rhizoma gastrodiae, Uncaria, concha haliotidis, Gardenia, dried bark of Phellodendron chinense, Achyranthes, Eucommia bark, mistletoe, Gentiana scabra, Salvia miltiorrhiza, tuckahoe, prepared rhizome of Rehmannia, hawthorn, peony bark, yam, medlar, Alisma orientale, Astragalus root and turtle as raw materials, extracting the raw materials, purifying the extract, concentrating the purified material, granulating the concentrate, drying the granules and packaging the dried material, where the filler, flavoring agent, adhesive and lubricant are added during the granulation process.
   USE - The traditional Chinese medicinal granule is useful for treating vertigo.
   ADVANTAGE - The traditional Chinese medicinal granule is convenient to carry and non-toxic, hence does not cause side effects, exhibits long-lasting effect and accurate dosage and has high active ingredient transfer rate to 92.6% and 87.3%, rapid absorbance, good taste and good solubility. The method is simple to perform.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing a traditional Chinese medicinal granule, which involves (a) crushing rhizome gastrodiae, Uncaria, concha haliotidis, Gardenia, dried bark of P.chinense, Achyranthes, mistletoe, G.scabra, tuckahoe, prepared rhizome of Rehmannia, hawthorn, yam, medlar, A.orientale and turtle using a crusher, sieving the crushed material using a sieve of 20-30 mesh into coarse powder extracting 2 times using the multi-purpose extraction tank, adding 10-12 times the above-mentioned medicines, adding 50-70% ethanol solution, adding 8-10 times above-mentioned medicinal materials to 50-70% ethanol solution, extracting the materials at 60-70 degrees C for 3 hours in first time and 2 hours in second time to obtain extract (A), placing the remaining material in the carbon dioxide supercritical extractor and extracting the materials at 25-35 MPa and 50-75 degrees C, preferably 70-75 degrees C for 2-3 hours, where the separation column pressure is 22-25 MPa and temperature is 30-50 degrees C and the separation tank pressure is 6-10 MPa and 30-40 degrees C and the extract separation time is 2-3 hours, obtaining extract (B), (b) subjecting the extract (A) to the ultrafiltration treatment, where the pore diameter of the ultrafiltration membrane is 0.22-0.45 mu m and molecular weight of 18000-24000, performing ultrafiltration at 0.8-1 MPa and 30-35 degrees C and collecting the filtrate, (c) combining the filtrate with extract (B), concentrating the mixture under reduced pressure at -0.05 to -0.08 MPa and 50-70 degrees C and concentrating the thick paste to the relative density of 1.25-1.28 and 60 degrees C, (d) adding thick paste to mixed powder of dextrin, sodium carboxymethylcellulose in a ratio of 1:2-5, adding thick paste to sucralose in a mass ratio of 1:0.02-0.05 and adding thick paste to 30-50 vol.% ethanol in a mass ratio of 1:0.2-0.5, placing in a wet granulator and granulating the material using a sieve of 18 mesh size, (e) placing the granules in a vacuum drying oven at -0.08-0.1 MPa and 50-55 degrees C for 24-36 hours, cooling the dried material at room temperature, (f) granulating the dried materials using 18 mesh sieve, (g) mixing the granules, placing the mixed granules in a three-dimensional motion mixer and adding 0.2-0.5 %mass magnesium stearate to the mixer and (h) packaging the mixed material, qualifying by performing inspection and storing the final product in a warehouse.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The filler is one or more of starch, dextrin, lactose, sucrose, microcrystalline cellulose, glucose and sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, preferably mixed powder of dextrin and sodium carboxymethylcellulose in a mass ratio of 1:1-3. The flavoring agent is one or more of maltose, ethyl maltol, sucralose, stevioside, mannitol, and aspartame, preferably sucralose. The adhesive is ethanol, starch slurry and polyvinylpyrrolidone, preferably 30-50% ethanol. The lubricant is talc and magnesium stearate, preferably magnesium stearate. Preferred Composition: The traditional Chinese medicinal granule is prepared by using 18-22 pts. wt., preferably 19-21 pts. wt. rhizoma gastrodiae, 18-22 pts. wt., preferably 18-20 pts. wt. Uncaria, 6-9 pts. wt., preferably 7-9 pts. wt. concha haliotidis, 10-13 pts. wt., preferably 11-13 pts. wt. Gardenia, 6-9 pts. wt., preferably 7-9 pts. wt. dried bark of P.chinense, 18-22 pts. wt., preferably 19-21 pts. wt. Achyranthes, 6-9 pts. wt., preferably 7-9 pts. wt. Eucommia bark, 6-9 pts. wt., preferably 6-8 pts. wt. mistletoe, 6-9 pts. wt., preferably 6-8 pts. wt. G.scabra, 18-22 pts. wt., preferably 20-22 pts. wt. S.miltiorrhiza, 21-25 pts. wt., preferably 23-25 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 18-22 pts. wt., preferably 20-22 pts. wt. prepared rhizome of Rehmannia, 18-22 pts. wt., preferably 20-22 pts. wt. hawthorn, 18-22 pts. wt., preferably 19-21 pts. wt. peony bark, 18-22 pts. wt., preferably 19-21 pts. wt. yam, 18-22 pts., preferably 19-21 pts. wt. medlar, 18-22 pts. wt., preferably 19-21 pts. wt. A.orientale, 18-22 pts. wt., preferably 20-22 pts. wt. Astragalus root and 17-21 pts. wt., preferably 19-21 pts. wt. turtle.
   ACTIVITY - Auditory.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B02 (Fused ring heterocyclics.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. PN CN108719584-A
TI Preparing forage complex microbial additive by mixing e.g. Bacillus, Saccharomycetes, Hippophae rhamnoides, Rosa roxburghii, Physalis alkekengi, and cactus freeze-dried powder, and carbonized eggshell powder, and drying and crushing product.
AU ZHANG K
   ZHANG T
   TANG X
   ZHANG L
AE PIZHOU GAXING MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY SERVICE (PIZH-Non-standard)
GA 201887397Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing forage complex microbial additive, involves (a) mixing Bacillus, Saccharomycetes, Hippophae rhamnoides freeze-dried powder, Rosa roxburghii, Physalis alkekengi freeze-dried powder, Ribes nigrum freeze-dried powder and cactus powder and remaining carbonized eggshell powder, uniformly mixing, preparing H.rhamnoides freeze-dried powder, R.roxbunghii, P.alkekengi freeze-dried powder, R.nigrum freeze-dried powder and cactus powder by taking fresh H.rhamnoides, R.roxbunghii, P.alkekengi and R.nigrum, (c) carbonizing soaked egg shell powder, (d) rapidly transferring the carbonized raw material to activation furnace, (e) washing and cooling, soaking activated material in hydrochloric acid, washing the activated material, and (f) drying activated material, and crushing product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing forage complex microbial additive for improving disease resistance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares product with improved stability, nutritional value, which promotes growth and metabolism of animal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing forage complex microbial additive, involves (a) mixing 10000-30000 million Bacillus, 10000-60000 million Saccharomycetes, 5-50 g Hippophae rhamnoides freeze-dried powder, 5-50 g Rosa roxburghii, 3-30 g Physalis alkekengi freeze-dried powder, 3-30 g Ribes nigrum freeze-dried powder and 5-150 g cactus powder and remaining carbonized eggshell powder, uniformly mixing, preparing H.rhamnoides freeze-dried powder, R.roxbunghii, P.alkekengi freeze-dried powder, R.nigrum freeze-dried powder and cactus powder by taking fresh H.rhamnoides, R.roxbunghii, P.alkekengi and R.nigrum by removing stems, rot fruits and impurities of above-mentioned raw materials, rinsing, freezing, drying, crushing at low temperature, removing peel and seeds, rinsing using 0.1 wt.% citric acid with 40 degrees C water for 15 minutes, constantly stirring, rinsing in purified water for 3 times, cooling to -45 degrees C or less, performing sublimation drying at -22 degrees C to -32 degrees C, and vacuum degree of 120-150 Pa, until the moisture content reaches less than 6 weight/weight%, where the carbonized eggshell powder is prepared by (b) washing the egg shell, drying for 1-2 hours, crushing and sieving using a sieve of 20-30 mesh sieve to obtain coarse powder, spraying 25 wt.% sodium chloride solution on eggshell coarse powder in a weight ratio of 1:0.5, mixing, leaving the material to stand for 6-7 hours, uniformly mixing, leaving for 6-7 hours, allowing salt to penetrate into inner space of eggshell tissue, (c) carbonizing the soaked egg shell powder using rotary carbonization pot, performing rotary carbonization at 420-450 degrees C for 70-80 minutes to observe the raw material is completely blackened, (d) rapidly transferring the carbonized raw material to activation furnace, closing the furnace, inducing carbon dioxide gas, exhausting air and activating at 720-800 degrees C for 55-85 minutes, (e) removing the activated material, washing and cooling to room temperature, washing in washing tank using water, until there is no sodium chloride, soaking the activated material in 12 %mass hydrochloric acid for 2.5-3 hours, washing the activated material using 95 degrees C purified water, rinsing using normal temperature purified water to neutral, filtering and rinsing, and (f) drying activated material at 115-120 degrees C, until the moisture content reaches less than 6 weight/weight%, sieving using a sieve of 60 mesh size, and crushing the sieved coarse particles and sieving using a sieve of 60 mesh size.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The eggshell comprises duck eggshell, goose eggshell and quail eggshell. The complex package comprises inner and outer bag complex package, where the inner bag is made of polypropylene plastic bag, outer package is made of aluminum foil bag, and the inner and outer bags are filled with silica gel desiccant sachet. The forage complex microbe additive is prepared by (a) mixing 14000-30000 million, 18000-30000 million, or 22000-30000 million Bacillus, 20000-60000 million, 30000-60000 million, or 40000-60000 million Saccharomycetes, 10-50 g, 15-50 g or 20-50 g H.rhamnoides freeze-dried powder, 8-50 g, 12-50 g or 15-50 g R.roxbunghii, 8-30 g, 8-30 g or 10-30 g P.alkekengi freeze-dried powder, 5-30 g, 8-30 g or 10-30 g R.nigrum freeze-dried powder and 7-150 g, 85-150 g or 100-150 g cactus powder and remainder of carbonized eggshell powder, where the amount of above mentioned component is for every 1000g of additive, uniformly mixing, preparing H.rhamnoides freeze-dried powder, R.roxbunghii, P.alkekengi freeze-dried powder, R.nigrum freeze-dried powder and cactus powder by taking fresh H.rhamnoides, R.roxbunghii, P.alkekengi and R.nigrum, removing stems, rot fruits and impurities, rinsing the above mentioned raw material using 0.1 wt.% citric acid with 40 degrees C water, rinsing for 15 minutes, stirring constantly, removing the material and rinsing in pure water for 3 times, cooling the material at -45 degrees C or less, performing sublimation drying at -22 degrees C to -32 degrees C, performing vacuum degree at 120-150 Pa, drying until the moisture is less than 6 weight/weight%, where the carbonized eggshell powder is obtained by (b) cleaning the egg shell, drying for 1-2 hours, crushing through 20-30 mesh sieve to obtain coarse powder, spraying the prepared 25 wt.% salt (sodium chloride) solution on the eggshell coarse powder at a weight ratio of 1:0.5, mixing while spraying, leaving the material to stand for 6-7 hours, mixing uniformly, leaving for 6-7 hours, where the purpose is to allow the salt to effectively penetrate into inner space of the eggshell tissue, (c) carbonizing the soaked egg shell powder raw material into rotary carbonization pot, performing rotary carbonization at 420-450 degrees C for 70-80 minutes to observe the raw material is completely blackened, (d) rapidly transferring the carbonized raw material to activation furnace, closing the furnace, inducing carbon dioxide gas, exhausting air and activating at 720-800 degrees C for 55-85 minutes, (e) removing the activated material, washing and cooling to room temperature, washing the material in washing tank using water until substantially no sodium chloride is detected, soaking the activated material in 12 %mass hydrochloric acid for 2.5-3 hours after washing the sodium chloride, washing the activated material using 95 degrees C pure water until the material particles has no white, rinsing using normal temperature pure water to neutral, filtering and rinsing the active material, and (f) drying the rinsed activated material at 115-120 degrees C until the water content is less than 6 weight/weight%, sieving through 60 mesh sieve, and crushing the sieved coarse particles through 60 mesh sieve. In step (a), the Bacillus and Saccharomycetes dried powder product contains thalli having a viable count of not less than 200 million and crushing at low temperature of 0 degrees C, performing drying using hot air drying or far infrared drying at 180-300 degrees C. Preferred Organism: The Bacillus comprises B.licheniformis, B.lentus, B.cereus, B.subtilis, B.coagulans, B.toyonensis, B.laterosporus, B.pumilus and B. subtilis natto. The yeast is chosen from Candida, brewing yeast, beer yeast, red yeast or fodder yeast.
   ACTIVITY - Metabolic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. TI Calcium oxide calcination kiln includes kiln body            provided with kiln door and kiln cavity, kiln car            provided with mounting column set with supporting plate            and blowing blades, and electric heating wire.
AU XU Y
AE JIANDE TAIHE NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018648063
AB    NOVELTY - Calcium oxide calcination kiln comprises kiln                body (1) provided with kiln door (4), and kiln                cavity (7) formed between inner wall of kiln door                and inner wall of kiln; and kiln car arranged at                lower end of kiln cavity and has upper surface                provided with mounting column (8) having upper end                provided with supporting plate (9) and under which                blowing blades (10) are arranged. The supporting                plate is provided with vent holes and calcite                forming block. The kiln also includes electric                heating wire arranged on inner wall of kiln body                and on inner surface of kiln door. The kiln body is                provided with air inlet hole; and temperature                detector and pressure detector installed in kiln                cavity. The air inlet mechanism includes gas                storage tank, solenoid valve, flow detector,                controller, gas storage tank and inlet hole.
   USE - Calcium oxide calcination kiln.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcination kiln produces high purity and                high activity calcium oxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Calcium oxide calcination kiln comprises kiln                body (1) and kiln car (2). The kiln body is                provided with kiln door (4), and kiln cavity (7)                formed between inner wall of kiln door and inner                wall of kiln. The kiln car is arranged at lower end                of kiln cavity. It has upper surface provided with                mounting column (8) having upper end provided with                supporting plate (9) and under which blowing blades                (10) are arranged. The supporting plate is provided                with vent holes and stacked with calcite forming                block formed by crushing calcite compression                molding. The kiln also includes electric heating                wire arranged on inner wall of kiln body and on                inner surface of kiln door. The kiln body is                provided with air inlet hole with one end connected                to lower end of kiln cavity to be close to upper                surface of kiln car, and has other end connected                with air inlet mechanism; and temperature detector                and pressure detector installed in kiln cavity. The                air inlet mechanism includes gas storage tank,                solenoid valve, flow detector, controller, gas                storage tank and inlet hole. The solenoid valve and                flow detector are installed on intake pipe. The                electric heating wire, temperature detector,                pressure detector, solenoid valve, and flow                detector are electrically connected to controller.                The upper end of kiln body is equipped with outlet.                An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for calcium oxide                calcination using calcination kiln by selecting                calcite raw material; washing and draining;                pulverizing calcite; selecting calcite particles                with close particle sizes; press-fitting calcite                particles into square shaped calcite forming block;                dehydrating and drying; stacking dehydrated and                dried calcite forming block on support plate above                kiln car; pushing kiln car into kiln body to cover                kiln door; calcining calcite forming bloc and                subjecting to medium temperature heating and                endothermic stage at 500-850 degrees C for 20-30                hours, so that calcite forming block reaches 850                degrees C from outside to inside; further calcining                at 850-1150 degrees C for 10-15 hours at 15-20                degrees C/ hour; maintaining at 1150 degrees C for                25-35 hours, in which oxygen is introduced into                kiln during high-temperature calcination to remove                carbon dioxide while kiln car is rotated and                blowing blade rotates to blow air upward;                discharging calcium oxide from kiln; reducing kiln                temperature at 580 degrees C; discharging calcium                oxide from kiln and immediately milling in fully                enclosed workshop; and immediately packaging                calcium oxide from kiln for 1 hour.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of calcium                oxide calcination kiln.

   Blowing blades (10)

PN CN108272736-A
TI Highly active lotion for e.g. providing whitening effect, is prepared by dispersing e.g. hydroxyethyl urea, deer collagen and glycolic acid, and mixing with e.g. rose extract, chamomile extract and jasmine extract, and antibacterial agent.
AU WANG F
AE WANG F (WANG-Individual)
GA 201856210B
AB    NOVELTY - A highly active lotion is prepared by ultrasonically dispersing 30-50 wt.% collagen deer skin, 8-12 wt.% propylparaben, 12-20 wt.% glycolic acid, 5-10 wt.% hydroxyethyl urea, 10-25 wt.% linden flower extract, 6-10 wt.% ylang-ylang oil, 10-15 wt.% white nut extract and 20-22 wt.% water at high speed, adding 50% ionized water of the resultant concentrated extract, stirring uniformly in a high-speed stirrer to obtain a diluted extract, leaving still for 2-4 hours, adding mother liquor comprising 10-15 wt.% propolis extract, 5-10 wt.% chamomile extract, 12-20 wt.% black berry extract, 5-10 wt.% rose extract, 5-10 wt.% jasmine extract and ionized water, stored in a sealed sterilization container for 24 hours, mixing at 60-80 degrees C, cooling the resultant mixed solution to 40-50 degrees C, adding an antibacterial agent and mixing uniformly.
   USE - Highly active lotion is used for providing skin whitening effect.
   ADVANTAGE - The highly active lotion is mild and non-irritating, and has excellent therapeutic effect on human hemorrhoids and swollen poisons, especially sores, and can maintain the activity of the skin, and relieve swelling and pain. The collagen deer skin can reduce swelling and detoxification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The white nut extract and linden flower extract are prepared by pulverizing white nut and linden flower, soaking in water, ultrasonically extracting for 10-15 minutes, filtering, centrifuging, discarding the precipitate, and concentrating the supernatant to 15 g/mL. The collagen deerskin is prepared by cleaning, dicing, and chopping the deer skin, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to remove fat, then placing in a high-voltage pulse device, adding 1-5% sodium chloride solution, adjusting the pH value with an organic acid solution such as citric acid, tartaric acid or malic acid, adding protease, extracting, centrifuging and extracting concentrated solution. The propolis extract is prepared by freezing propolis at less than -5 degrees C for 24-48 hours after removing the impurities, rapidly crushing frozen propolis, sieving, adding ethanol solution, immersing at 22-26 degrees C for 4-5 hours, leaving still for 1-2 hours, removing the supernatant liquid, adding ethanol solution to the remaining material to continue impregnation, leaving still, repeating the above process at least three times, combining supernatant liquid each time, cooling to less than 5 degrees C, filtering, heating the filtrate, evaporating at 60 degrees C or less and concentrating to 10 g/mL. The black berry extract is prepared by adding dried powder of black berry to 50 mL deionized distilled water, extracting twice in 50 degrees C-water bath for 15 minutes each time, filtering, adding 100 mL water to the residue, extracting 2 times at 75 degrees C for 15 minutes, combining the extracts, filtering and concentrating to 10 g/mL. The chamomile extract is prepared by crushing chamomile, soaking in water for 1-3 hours, adding 10-20 times of water to chamomile, ultrasonically extracting for 10-15 minutes, filtering, centrifuging, discarding the precipitating, and concentrating the supernatant to 10 g/mL. The rose extract and jasmine extract are prepared by adding nine mature rose and jasmine flowers into a stainless steel distillation equipment, adding water, such that the ratio of water to flowers is 2:1, and maintaining temperature in the distillation equipment to above 100 degrees C and pressure at above 1 atmosphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The antibacterial agent is chosen from Kathon CG (RTM: methylchloroisothiazolinone), Germall II (RTM: diazolidinyl urea), Germall Plus (RTM: diazolidinyl urea), methylparaben, and propylparaben.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. AB    NOVELTY - Aniline waste water processing device comprises aniline extracting device (1), aniline stripping device (2), the biodegradable device (3), the aniline recovery device (4) and the extraction agent cabin (5). The aniline extraction device is provided with aniline-containing extraction agent outlet and the extraction agent inlet. The extracting agent cabin is connected with outlet of the stripping device.    ADVANTAGE - The device has simple and reasonable technique, no secondary pollution and good processing effect; and realizes repeated using of extracting agent and stripping and reduces energy consumption; and is economical.

PN CN108017237-A; CN108017237-B
TI Processing dairy product wastewater comprises discharging waste water in to isoelectric precipitation pool, adding acidic solution, adjusting the pH value, filtering to remove suspended substance and precipitating.
AU CHEN Z
   CHEN F
   XU R
AE HUIZHOU HEXIN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 201839294C
AB    NOVELTY - Processing dairy product wastewater comprises discharging waste water in the isoelectric precipitation pool, adding acidic solution, adjusting the pH value, adding casein micelles, generating the casein precipitate, discharging casein precipitate, filtering to remove suspended substance and precipitating, discharging filtered waste water into the two-stage anaerobic fermentation reaction tank, decomposing the waste water after flowing into the methane anaerobic fermenting reaction tank, passing the microorganism producing methane anaerobic into fermenting reaction tank, passing waste water after conversion through the denitrification filter pool, nitrite nitrogen nitrosation and filter tank with waste water and reflowing to the denitrification filter pool, the denitrifying bacteria and the nitrite nitrogen and ammonia nitrogen in the waste water into nitrogen, circularly discharging waste water in the denitrification filter pool with the nitroso-filtering tank.
   USE - The method is useful in processing dairy product wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The waste water has chemical oxygen demand of not greater than 100 mg/l and total nitrogen content of not greater than 30 mg/l. The method has low energy consumption and low generation of the residual sludge; and reduces the secondary pollution; and is economical..
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing dairy product wastewater comprises discharging waste water in the isoelectric precipitation pool, adding acidic solution, adjusting the pH value of the waste water to 4.7-4.9, adding casein micelles in the waste water in the coagulation process of absorption of fat and whey protein, generating the casein precipitate, discharging casein precipitate containing waste water into the filtering pool, filtering to remove suspended substance and precipitating, discharging filtered waste water into the two-stage anaerobic fermentation reaction tank, where the two-stage anaerobic fermentation reaction tank which is connected with hydrogen-producing anaerobic fermentation reaction tank and methane anaerobic fermenting reaction tank, discharging filtered waste water into the hydrogen-producing anaerobic fermentation reaction tank, where the hydrogen producing anaerobic fermentation reaction tank of the first microorganism the fat, protein and lactose is decomposed into organic acids, alcohols, hydrogen or amino, and generating hydrogen, where the pH value of hydrogen producing anaerobic fermentation reaction tank is 67, maintaining the temperature to 30-37 degrees C, Where the waste water retention time is 6-24 hours, decomposing the waste water after flowing into the methane anaerobic fermenting reaction tank, passing the microorganism producing methane anaerobic, organic acid, carbon dioxide and hydrogen into fermenting reaction tank, where the pH value of the methane-producing anaerobic fermentation reaction tank is 78, maintaining the temperature at 20-37 degrees C, where the waste water retention time is 6-24 hours, passing waste water after converting to the biological filter tank, where the biological filter pool comprises orderly and bidirectional communication of the denitrification filter tank and nitrification filtering pond, the dissolved oxygen of the denitrification filter pool is 0.1% of 0.2 mg/l, the pH value is 7-7.5, the temperature is 30 degrees C and containing denitrification bacteria in the denitrification filter pool, where the oxygen content in nitrification filter pool is 0.8-1 mg/l and the pH value is 7-7.5, the temperature is 30?0C, and the nitrification filter contained in the nitrosobacteria, passing waste water after conversion through the denitrification filter pool enters nitrosation filter, nitrite nitrogen nitrosation and filter tank with waste water and reflowing to the denitrification filter pool, the denitrifying bacteria and the nitrite nitrogen and ammonia nitrogen in the waste water into nitrogen, circularly discharging waste water in the denitrification filter pool with the nitroso-filtering tank untill COD of waste water is not greater than 100 mg/l and the total nitrogen content is not greater than 30 mg/l.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first microorganism comprises hydrolysis acidification bacteria, hydrogen-producing acetogenic bacteria and homotype for producing culture acetic acid bacteria. The second microbe comprises homotype for producing culture acetic acid bacteria and methanogen. the filtering pool is the sand filter tank. The padding in the biological filter tank is ceramic, active carbon or natural zeolite. The acidic solution is sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, phosphoric acid, acetic acid or propionic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components:The denitrification filter pool is provided with an air pump. the nitrification filter tank is provided with an air pump.

PN CN107951904-A; CN107951904-B
TI Use of adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cell drug containing adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells, follicle stimulating hormone-secreting cells, glutathione, calcium heparin, and stabilizer in treatment of premature ovarian failure.
AU SONG Y
   HU S
   ZHANG Z
AE SHENZHEN LAILISAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201834222T
AB    NOVELTY - Use of adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cell drug, is claimed in treatment of premature ovarian failure. The adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cell drug comprises 8-12 wt.% adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells, 6-8 wt.% follicle stimulating hormone-secreting cells, 20-22 wt.% nanosustained release magnetic microspheres, 4-6 wt.% human serum albumin stock, 5-7 wt.% Coix seed oil, 1-1.5 wt.% flavonol glycosides, 1-2 wt.% vitamin E, 0.5-1 wt.% paclitaxel, 0.4-0.8 wt.% glutathione, 0.5-1.5 wt.% sodium hyaluronate, 0.3-1.2 wt.% cyanophycin, 0.4-1.8 wt.% zinc gluconate, 0.5-0.8 wt.% calcium heparin, 0.5-1 wt.% emulsifier, 0.5-1 wt.% stabilizer, 0.3-0.5 wt.% pH regulator and physiological saline (remainder).
   USE - The adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cell drug is useful in treatment of premature ovarian failure, treating ovarian endometriosis, and promoting ovulation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing mesenchymal stem cell drug, which involves (a) extracting 5-10 ml autologous adipose tissue from patient, washing blood in the adipose tissue using a washing solution, cutting the adipose tissue into pieces with a diameter of 3-5 mm using scissors, adding mixture of 0.25% trypsin and 0.1% collagenase in a volume ratio of 1:1 to the fat particles, digesting at 37 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, shaking at 1500-2000 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 5-10 minutes using an oscillator, standing for 10-20 minutes and aspirating the lower layer liquid into a culture flask containing the cell-induction medium, (b) adding the flask into a carbon dioxide incubator, incubating at a constant temperature of 37 degrees C for 3-10 days, inducing the adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells under induction of the cell-inducing culture fluid and generating follicle stimulating hormone-secreting cells, (c) using a human follicle stimulating hormone (FSH) enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) kit to identify the presence of follicle stimulating hormone in culture flasks, determining whether adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells induce follicle-stimulating hormone-secreting cells and then using flow cytometry to detect the quantity and quality of follicle stimulating hormone secreting cells, (d) moving the liquid in the culture flask to the centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 1500 rpm for 8-10 minutes, discarding the supernatant and extracting the underlying cells to obtain a mixture of follicle stimulating hormone-secreting cells and adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells, (e) diluting the mixed cells using human serum albumin stock solution, adding the diluted mixed cell solution to the nano-sustained release magnetic microspheres at 37 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, until the microspheres are completely immersed, assisting the drug loading by ultrasonic dispersion and obtained follicle stimulating hormone sustained release monomer, (f) dissolving Coix seed oil, zinc gluconate, heparin calcium and sodium hyaluronate in one-fourth of the saline solution, separately obtaining Coix seed oil solution, zinc gluconate solution, heparin calcium solution and sodium hyaluronate solution, mixing the follicle stimulating hormone sustained-release monomer with the Coix seed oil solution and uniformly stirring to obtain follicle stimulating hormone sustained-release monomer-Coix seed oil solution, adding zinc gluconate solution, heparin calcium solution and sodium hyaluronate solution and stirring to obtain a mixed solution A, (g) adding the flavonol glycosides, vitamin E, paclitaxel, glutathione and cyanophycin to the mixed solution A in order and stirring to obtain mixed solution B and (h) adding the emulsifier and stabilizer to the mixed solution B, stirring, adjusting the pH of the mixed solution B to 7.2-7.5 using a pH adjuster.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Medium: The cell induction medium comprises 4-6 wt.% transforming growth factor, 4-6 wt.% epidermal growth factor, 10-12 wt.% human hemoglobin, 3-9 wt.% all-trans retinoic acid, 5-9 wt.% follicle stimulating hormone and growth factor, 2-4 wt.% pyridoxine, 1-3 wt.% neuropeptides, 2-4 wt. diethylstilbestrol, 0.5-1.5 wt.% progesterone, 1-2 wt.% antioxidant and Dulbecco's modified eagle's medium (DMEM)-high glucose medium (remainder). The antioxidant is one or combination of vitamin E, L-ascorbic acid, alpha -lipoic acid and reduced glutathione. The method involves replacing traditional Chinese medicinal ingredients chosen from licorice root, musk, Salvia miltiorrhiza, resina draconis and sargentgloryvine stem in a mass ratio of 3:1:1:1:2. The drug is in form of patch which is applied to the patient's navel. The patch is also provided with an electrode paste and an electromagnetic pulse generator.
   ACTIVITY - Gynecological.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

TI Hydrophobic melamine foam resin liquid comprises melamine, formaldehyde, modified monomers and/or functional compounds, catalyst and water, and is obtained by polymerization of mentioned raw materials.
AU LIU S
   WANG G
   LI Z
AE CHENGDU ORGANIC CHEM CO LTD CHINESE ACAD (CAYJ-C)
GA 2018319259
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrophobic melamine foam resin liquid comprises 100 pts. wt. melamine, 40-100 pts. wt. formaldehyde, 0-10 pts. wt. modified monomers and/or functional compounds, 1-3 pts. wt. catalyst and 50-80 pts. wt. water, and is obtained by polymerization of mentioned raw materials, where the modified monomer is polyethylene glycol monomethyl ether, p-carboxyphenoxy cyclotriphosphazene, glycine methyl ester or chloropolyphosphazene, the functional compound is at least one functional compounds A and functional compounds B.
   USE - Used as hydrophobic melamine foam resin liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrophobic melamine foam resin liquid: can prepare the soft controllable adjustment with the density of 6-100 kg/m3, semi-rigid, hard hydrophobic melamine foam; the water absorption of soft melamine foams with density less than 20 kg/m3 is less than 10%; semi-rigid and rigid melamine foams with contact angles greater than 120 degrees and densities greater than 25 kg/m3 have water absorption of less than 5%; has greater than 130 degrees contact angle and greater than 120 kPa tensile strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Hydrophobic melamine foam resin liquid comprises 100 pts. wt. melamine, 40-100 pts. wt. formaldehyde, 0-10 pts. wt. modified monomers and/or functional compounds, 1-3 pts. wt. catalyst and 50-80 pts. wt. water, and is obtained by polymerization of mentioned raw materials, where the modified monomer is polyethylene glycol monomethyl ether, p-carboxyphenoxy cyclotriphosphazene, glycine methyl ester or chloropolyphosphazene, the functional compound is at least one functional compounds A and functional compounds B, the functional compound A is phosphazene compound, ammonium polyphosphate compound, organosilicon polymer or fluororesin, the functional compound B is compound containing aminal, hemi-acetal, amide, amine, hydroxy, carbonyl, carboxy, or epoxy groups, the catalyst is diethanolamine, triethanolamine, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium borate, potassium borate, sodium metabisulfite, and sodium hydrogensulfite. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing hydrophobic melamine foam resin liquid, comprising (a) reacting modified monomer at 60-70 degrees C reaction temperature for 30-50 minutes, adding other raw materials, reacting at 70-100 degrees C for 50-80 minutes to obtain resin liquid, (b) adding other materials except functional compound, reacting at 70-100 degrees C for 30-40 minutes, adding functional compound, and continuing reaction for 30-50 minutes to obtain resin solution, and (c) reacting the modified monomer at reaction temperature of 60-70 degrees C for 30-50 minutes, adding other raw materials except the functional compound, reacting at 70-100 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, adding the functional compound, continuing reacting at 70-100 degrees C for 30-50 minutes to obtain special resin solution; and
   (2) melamine foaming fluid, comprising 100 pts. wt. special resin solution, 0-10 pts. wt. functional compound A, 1-10 pts. wt, preferably 2-6 pts. wt. surfactant, 1-15 pts. wt, preferably 3-10 pts. wt. foaming agent and 0.5-15 pts. wt, preferably 2-10 pts. wt. cross-linking agent, where the surfactant is anionic surfactant, cationic surfactant, nonionic surfactant, and/or reactive surfactant, the foaming agent is water, pentane, hexane, alkoxy fluoroalkane, petroleum ether, oxalate and/or carbon dioxide, the cross-linking agent is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid, metaphosphoric acid, pyrophosphoric acid, boric acid, citric acid, formic acid, acetic acid, oxalic acid, acrylic acid, p-toluenesulfonic acid, oleic acid, stearic acid, palmitic acid, lactic acid, sulfosalicylic acid, naphthalene disulfonic acid, maleic acid, and/or polyhydroxy acids and corresponding anhydrides, the functional compound A is phosphazene compound, ammonium polyphosphate compound, silicone polymer and/or fluororesin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The phosphazene compound is ammonium polyhydroxyphenylphosphate, polyalkoxyphosphazene, polyaryloxyphosphazene, polyfluoroalkoxyphosphazene or polybromoalkoxyphosphazene, the ammonium polyphosphate compound is ammonium polyhydroxyphenylphosphate, ammonium polyphosphate or surface modified ammonium polyphosphate, the silicone polymers is hydroxy-containing, amino-containing or double-bond containing polyorganosilanes or siloxanes. Preferred Method: In the step (a), adding other raw materials, reacting at 85-100 degrees C for 50-80 minutes obtain viscosity special resin liquid, in the step (b), the reaction temperature is 85-100 degrees C, in the step (c), adding functional compound, continuing reaction at 85-100 degrees C for 30-50 minute to obtain special resin liquid.

PN WO2022176712-A1; JP2022125565-A
TI Recovery of lithium from battery slag, involves            adding acid to battery slag containing lithium to form            leachate, adding additive containing calcium and/or            sodium carbonate, neutralizing leachate to form            processed product and filtering to form processed            filtrate.
AU KENTA K
   ATSUSHI M
   HIROKI M
   KURAMOCHI K
   MIYAZAKI A
   MURAOKA H
AE MITSUBISHI MATERIALS CORP (MITV-C)
   MITSUBISHI MATERIALS CORP (MITV-C)
GA 2022A8565B
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of lithium from battery slag involves                performing an acid leaching process (S4) by adding                acid to battery slag containing lithium to form                leachate, adding (S5) an additive (a1) containing                calcium to the leachate, neutralizing the leachate                to produce a processed product (p1), filtering (S6)                the product (p1) to separate it to a processed                filtrate (f1) and a processed residue (r1), adding                (S9) an additive (a2) containing sodium carbonate                and/or sodium carbonate hydrate to the processed                filtrate (f1) to form a processed product (p2),                filtering (S10) the processed product (p2) to                separate it into a processed filtrate (f2)                containing lithium and sodium components and a                processed residue (r2) containing calcium, heating                (S11) the processed filtrate (f2), blowing (S12)                carbon dioxide gas into the heated processed                filtrate (f2), and filtering (S13) the processed                product (p3) to separate into a processed filtrate                (f3) containing sodium and processed residue (r3)                containing lithium.
   USE - Recovery of lithium from battery slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently recovers lithium in the                battery slag. The separation load between lithium                and sodium can be suppressed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovery of lithium from battery slag involves                performing an acid leaching process by adding acid                to battery slag containing lithium to generate a                leachate, adding an additive (a1) containing                calcium to the leachate, neutralizing the leachate                to produce a processed product (p1), filtering the                processed product (p1) to separate it to a                processed filtrate (f1) and a processed residue                (r1), adding an additive (a2) containing sodium                carbonate and/or sodium carbonate hydrate to the                processed filtrate (f1) to produce a processed                product (p2), filtering the processed product (p2)                to separate it into a processed filtrate (f2)                containing lithium and sodium components and a                processed residue (r2) containing calcium, heating                the processed filtrate (f2), blowing carbon dioxide                gas into the heated processed filtrate (f2) or                adding a carbonate to produce a processed product                (p3), and filtering the processed product (p3) to                separate into a processed filtrate (f3) containing                sodium and processed residue (r3) containing                lithium. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                device for recovering lithium.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the recovery                of lithium. (Drawing includes non-English language                text)
   S4Acid leaching process
   S5,S9Addition process
   S6,S10,S13Filtration process
   S11Heating process
   S12Blowing process

AU SWINAREW A
   OKLA H
   GABOR J
   PRZYBYLA M
   TREJNOWSKA E
   SKOCZYNSKI S

   MIZIA STEC K

AE UNIV SLASKI (UYSL-Non-standard)
   UNIV SLASKI MEDYCZNY (UYSL-Non-standard)
GA 202274713D
AB    NOVELTY - The device has gas exchange chamber (1) of                longitudinal shape in a form of a straight cylinder                or a elliptic cylinder. An inner part of the                chamber is provided with the membrane in the form                of capillary bundle (8) made of a semi-permeable                material that is permeable to gas mixture particles                and non-permeable to blood particles. The                capillaries are tensed with a tension force of a                value from 1 to 100 N, preferably 10 N, and are                parallel to the longitudinal axis of the chamber                and to each other or are arranged spirally and is                twisted along the longitudinal axis of the chamber                by the same angle falling within the range from 15                to 720 degrees, preferably by the angle from 90 to                360 degrees. The side wall (9) of the chamber near                each chamber base (2) is provided with an                inlet/outlet opening (10'') of blood stream                provided with the capillaries forming the membrane                have the form of tubes of external diameter from 30                to 600pm, preferably 100pm.
   USE - Device e.g. blood oxygenators for blood                oxygenation with a membrane made of an organic                material of blowing properties used in blood                oxygenators, implantable biomedical devices, blood                sampling or analysis devices or purification                devices, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The extracorporeal blood oxygen saturation                device eliminates the carbon dioxide from blood,                preferably with organic membrane of blowing                properties. The device is applied in the conditions                of potentially reversible deep lung function                disorders to the extent preventing the effective                gas exchange using the mechanical ventilation. The                oxygenator functions as lungs and forms the                integral component of cardiopulmonary bypass. The                gas exchange process takes place, in which oxygen                supplied to the oxygenator chamber replaces blood                carbon dioxide. This demonstrates that structural                optimization in order to improve treatment safety                is necessary. The circulatory system receives                thousands of gaseous microemboli (GME) during the                Extra Corporeal Membrane Oxygenation (ECMO) therapy                despite the use of membrane oxygenator and arterial                filtration. The system will eliminate the GME                production.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an axonometric view of the                device for blood oxygenation.
   Gas exchange chamber (1)
   Chamber base (2)
   Capillary bundle (8)
   Side wall (9)
   Inlet/outlet opening (10'')

PN CN114405048-A; CN216877921-U; CN114405048-B
TI Sublimation crystallization method for industrial            continuous production, involves scraping crystal of            heat exchange surface and re-generating crystal nucleus            layer on heat exchange surface, and repeating            nucleation stage and nucleus growth stage.
AU CHEN X
   CHEN P
   ZHANG L
   HU J
   FU W
   WEI J
   FU T
   PENG G
AE GUANGDONG GUANGDA NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202264530F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing crystal                nucleus forming stage, gas raw material into the                material of cavity crystallizing device, heat                exchange surface and medium containing the cavity                medium heat exchange and sublimation                crystallization to form a crystal core layer and                attached to the heat exchange surface. An                interlayer is provided between the medium cavity                and the material cavity. The inner wall of the                interlayer forms the heat exchange surface. A                crystal nucleus growth stage, the gas raw material                through the crystal nucleus layer and the heat                exchange surface continue to exchange heat with the                condensing medium. The crystal nucleus layer                surface is attached with newly generated crystal                nucleus and aggregated to form crystal. The scraper                on the stirrer in the material cavity is provided.                The crystal of the heat exchange surface is scraped                and the crystal nucleus layer is re-generated on                the heat exchange surface, and the nucleation stage                and nucleus growth stage are repeated.
   USE - Sublimation crystallization method used for                industrial continuous production of solid crystal                used in chemical production field. Can also be used                in industrial production, high pressure, liquid                carbon dioxide through porous plug or throttle                valve to lower pressure direction.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas raw material and the condensing medium                are respectively introduced into the material                containing material cavity the crystallizing device                and the medium cavity after the heat exchange of                the gas raw materials and the condensed medium is                finished, so that the solid crystal with high                purity is obtained, and the impurity is not mixed                in the heat exchanging condensing media. The method                can be applied to industrially produce a large                amount of solid crystal. Because of the rotation of                the scraper, the carbon dioxide gas in the material                cavity is prevented from turbulent state of                irregular movement into regular state of movement,                so as to prevent crystal aggregation to generate                frost layer compact period.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                sublimation crystallization system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure schematic                diagram of the condensation crystal system.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

PN CN113997464-A; CN113997464-B
TI Method of utilizing waste ethylene vinyl acetate (EVA) base material to prepare composite regenerated foam material, involves controlling temperature of grinding surface of grinding disc by feeding constant temperature circulating liquid medium with temperature of certain degree.
AU GUO W
   LIU Z
   LI L
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2022260038
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining the waste ethylene vinyl acetate (EVA)-based ultrafine flake powder is collected and obtained, after the grinding is completed. The process parameters of the disc-shaped solid-phase mechano-chemical reactor are the grinding pressure is 0.2-1.5MPa. The temperature of the grinding surface of the grinding disc is controlled by feeding a constant temperature circulating liquid medium with a temperature of -5 to -20 degreesC. The circular grinding is performed for 10 to 12 times, and the speed of the grinding disc is 40 to 60 rpm. The thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) or waste EVA-based composite material is prepared by molding process including 18-30 portions of waste EVA-based ultra-fine flake powder and 70-82 portions of TPU. The TPU or waste EVA-based composite material prepared is prepared into a TPU or waste EVA-based composite foamed material through a supercritical carbon dioxide autoclave foaming process.
   USE - Method of utilizing waste ethylene vinyl acetate (EVA) base material to prepare composite regenerated foam material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the solid phase shearing milling technology to effectively reduce the particle size of waste EVA base material powder, improves surface activity, and is blended with TPU. The TPU composite foam is prepared by supercritical carbon dioxide foaming technology, which greatly improved the cell density of TPU foam. The material cost is greatly reduced, and the high-value recycling of waste EVA-based recycled products is realized. The method finds that the waste EVA-based ultra-fine flake powder is obtained by solid phase shear milling technology, which appears between granular and strip powder, and the formation conditions are relatively harsh, and microscopic morphology is different from that of strip powder. The performance of the obtained recycled products is better, which provides guidance for further improving the recycling of waste EVA-based materials in the future. The method simple production process, easy operation, batch production and continuous production, and is suitable for most waste EVA-based materials, especially waste EVA-based foam materials, and has obvious commercial promotion advantages.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of electron microscope photographic of method of utilizing waste EVA base material to prepare composite regenerated foam material.

PN CN113319478-A
TI Welding method of driving axle of forklift truck            comprises heating driving axle shaft head, inserting            driving axle shaft head at end of driving axle housing,            and welding joint of drive axle shaft head and drive            axle housing.
AU LI F
   JIANG W
   CUI M
   YANG Y
AE HEFEI CASTING FACTORY ANHUI HELI CO LTD (AHCC-C)
GA 2021A4196L
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises heating driving axle                shaft head to 250-350 degrees C, inserting driving                axle shaft head at end of driving axle housing, and                welding joint of drive axle shaft head and drive                axle housing by welding machine. The welding method                is argon gas and carbon dioxide mixed gas                protection welding, in which ratio of argon gas and                carbon dioxide is 80:20. The welding machine uses                KR-500 type gas protection welding machine. The                type of welding wire is H08Mn2SiA. The diameter is                1.6 mm. When welding, the voltage is 28-32 V and                the current is 200-240 A.
   USE - The method is useful for welding driving axle                of forklift.
   ADVANTAGE - The welding system does not need special                person to drive the bridge shaft head thermal                sleeve work, the worker labor intensity is small,                it avoids the traditional vertical thermal sleeve                method lifting the driving axle shaft head when                there is the safety trouble; through the structure                design of the supporting trolley, ensuring the                coaxiality shell and the shaft head, and the                operation is simple, further reducing the labor                intensity; the welding device does not need                operating personnel hand welding gun the forklift                drive axle rotates while welding; the welding seam                is uniform, the welding efficiency is high, and the                welding quality is ensured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                safety-efficiency welding system of the driving                axle of the forklift truck comprising shaft head                hot sleeve device and welding device. The shaft                head heat sleeve device comprises working table,                heating welding gun, cylinder and supporting                trolley. The working table is provided with rotary                table provided with connecting part. The welding                device comprises a locating clamping device (21)                and a welding device (22). The locating clamping                device comprises first propping device (211) and                second propping device (212) arranged oppositely. A                motor (2111) drives the first propping device to                axially rotate along driving axle housing. A hand                wheel device (2121) drives the second propping                device close to or far away from the driving axle                shaft head.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic view illustrating                the welding system.
   Locating clamping device (21)
   Welding device (22)
   First propping device (211)
   Second propping device (212)
   Motor (2111)
   Hand wheel device (2121)
