PN WO2021152114-A1; CN115003412-A; KR2022130799-A; IN202227044083-A
TI Preparing molding comprising mixed oxide of            oxygen, magnesium, and nickel for use as e.g.,            catalyst, by mixing water, magnesium source, nickel            source, and acid, shaping the mixture, and calcining            molding comprising the mixed oxide.
AU KAUFMAN RECHULSKI M D
   KURETSCHKA C
   MAGNUS S
   BORCHERS S
   AMANN J
   KAUFMAN R M D
   DANIEL K L M
   CHRISTIAN K
   SANDRA M
   SABINE B
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 202190315P
AB    NOVELTY - A molding comprising mixed oxide of oxygen                (O), magnesium (Mg), and nickel (Ni) is prepared by                (i) mixing water, Mg source, Ni source, and an                acid, to obtain a mixture; (ii) subjecting the                mixture obtained from step (i) to a shaping process                to obtain a molding comprising the mixed oxide; and                (iii) calcining the molding obtained from step (ii)                in a gas atmosphere at 700-1400 degrees C, where                molar ratio of the acid used in step (i) to Ni,                calculated as elemental Ni, of Ni source used in                step (i), acid:Ni, is greater than or equal to                0.001:1.
   USE - Preparation of molding comprising mixed oxide                of oxygen, magnesium, and nickel used for obtaining                re-shaped molding for use as catalytically active                material, as a catalyst component, or as a catalyst                for reforming hydrocarbons to a synthesis gas                comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The molding shows good longevity and improved                catalytic performance, in particular with regard to                the conversion of methane and carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) molding comprising a mixed oxide of O, Mg,                and Ni;
   (2) preparation of re-shaped molding,                preferably for preparing a re-shaped molding of the                molding comprising the mixed oxide, which involves                (a) calcining the molding in a gas atmosphere at                350-550 degrees C, preferably 425-475 degrees C,                (b) crushing the molding to particles having an                average particle size of 0.1-0.9 mm, preferably                0.2-0.8 mm, preparing a mixture comprising greater                than or equal to 1 binders and the molding, (d)                subjecting the molding to a re-shaping process, and                (e) calcining the molding in a gas atmosphere at                800-1300 degrees C, obtaining a re-shaped molding;                and
   (3) reforming greater than or equal to 1                hydrocarbons to a synthesis gas comprising hydrogen                and carbon monoxide, which involves providing a                reactor comprising a reaction zone which comprises                the molding or the re-shaped molding, and passing a                reactant gas stream into the reaction zone, where                reactant gas stream passed into the reaction zone                comprises hydrocarbons, carbon dioxide, and water,                subjecting reactant gas stream to reforming                conditions in the reaction zone, and removing a                product stream from the reaction zone, where                product stream comprises hydrogen and carbon                monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Weight ratio of Ni,                  calculated as elemental Ni, of the Ni source used                  in step (i), relative to Mg, calculated as                  elemental Mg, of the Mg source used in step (i),                  Ni:Mg, is 0.1:1-5:1, preferably 0.2:1-0.75:1. Mg                  source comprises magnesium carbonate, magnesium                  chloride, magnesium citrate, magnesium hydroxide,                  magnesium oxide, hydrotalcite, and/or aluminum                  magnesium hydroxy carbonate. Ni source comprises                  one or more of elemental nickel, nickel                  carbonate, nickel nitrate, nickel formate, nickel                  acetate, nickel chloride, nickel hydroxide,                  nickel nitrite, and nickel oxide. Weight ratio of                  total weight of Mg source used in step (i) and                  weight of Ni source used in step (i) to total                  weight of acid used in step (i) and weight of                  water used in step (i), is 0.1:1-1:0.1. In step                  (i), source of metal M is further admixed, where                  M is selected from aluminum, gallium, indium,                  silicon, germanium, tin, titanium, and zirconium.                  Acid used in step (i) comprises organic acid                  and/or inorganic acid. Mixed oxide comprising O,                  Mg, and Ni, is a crystalline phase NixMgyO. Mixed                  oxide further comprises a crystalline phase                  NiaMgbO.x+y=1;y= greater than or equal to 0.52;a+b=1; anda= greater than or equal to 0.7, provided                  that x is not equal to a.
   EXAMPLE - 200 g Aluminum magnesium hydroxyl carbonate                and 52.7 g nickel(II)carbonate were mixed for in a                kneader, then 200 ml aqueous solution of formic                acid in deionized water and 50 ml deionized water                were added within 10 minutes. Under further mixing                during 10 minutes, a dough-like homogeneous mass                was formed, and was then extruded to strands with                3.5 mm in diameter. Extrudates were dried at 120                degrees C for 16 hours, and were calcined in an                annealing furnace under air at 950 degrees C for 4                hours. Nickel content of calcined moldings was 15.3                wt.%, magnesium content was 13.1 wt.%, and aluminum                content was 29.1 wt.%, calculated as the elements,                respectively.

TI Preparing composite material of polyethyleneimine and metal-organic framework-74 useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. dissolving zinc nitrate hexahydrate and 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid in N,N-dimethylformamide solution.
AU GAI F
   DUAN Q
   YIN L
   WANG Y
   ZHAO Z
   AO Y
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN TECHNOLOGY (UYGL-C)
GA 202181822K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite material of polyethyleneimine and metal-organic framework-74 (MOF-74), comprises e.g. (I) dissolving 2.329 g zinc nitrate hexahydrate and 0.513 g 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid in 50 ml N,N-dimethylformamide solution, sonicating for 30 minutes, adding 2.5 ml deionized water and uniformly mixing to obtain a precursor mixed solution, (II) transferring the mixed solution to a hydrothermal reactor, conducting a hydrothermal reaction at 120 degrees C for 24 hours, after the reaction is completed, naturally cooling to room temperature, subjecting the obtained precipitate to solvent exchange and centrifugal cleaning to remove excess ligand molecules, drying the reaction precipitate after solvent exchange and centrifugation at 100 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain a dry powder sample, and (III) dissolving the required amount (5-40 wt.%) of polyethyleneimine in 40 ml anhydrous methanol for 5 minutes, and dropping the methanol solution of polyethyleneimine into 2 g of zinc-MOF-74.
   USE - The composite material is useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, economical, and easy to realize the industrialized production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing composite material of polyethyleneimine and metal-organic framework-74 (MOF-74), comprises (I) dissolving 2.329 g zinc nitrate hexahydrate and 0.513 g 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid in 50 ml N,N-dimethylformamide solution, sonicating for 30 minutes, adding 2.5 ml deionized water and uniformly mixing to obtain a precursor mixed solution, (II) transferring the mixed solution to a hydrothermal reactor, conducting a hydrothermal reaction at 120 degrees C for 24 hours, after the reaction is completed, naturally cooling to room temperature, subjecting the obtained precipitate to solvent exchange and centrifugal cleaning to remove excess ligand molecules, drying the reaction precipitate after solvent exchange and centrifugation at 100 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain a dry powder sample, and (III) under ultrasound, dissolving the required amount (5-40 wt.%) of polyethyleneimine in 40 ml anhydrous methanol for 5 minutes, dropping the methanol solution of polyethyleneimine into 2 g of zinc-MOF-74 under ultrasound, in the powder, then at a constant temperature of 40 degrees C for 8 hours under the protection of nitrogen, and vacuum-drying at 100 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the concentration of the polyethyleneimine methanol solution is 5-40 wt.%. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the method of assembling Zn-MOF-74 and polyethyleneimine is the dipping method. In the preferred step (iii), the reaction condition of the impregnation method is a constant temperature of 40 degrees C for 8 hours under the protection of nitrogen.

PN KR2021072961-A
TI Substrate cleaning system used for glass substrate used in mobile device, has transfer unit provided for transferring first tray, on which substrate is mounted, from tray receiving unit through cleaning unit and back to tray receiving unit.
AU LEE J S
   CHOI W C
   PARK J
   YOON C N
AE KCTECH CO LTD (KCTE-C)
GA 202171270S
AB    NOVELTY - The substrate cleaning system (100) includes a tray receiving unit (110) provided for accommodating a tray. The tray includes a first tray on which a substrate is mounted. A cleaning unit (130,150) is provided for cleaning the substrate on the first tray. A transfer unit is provided for transferring the first tray from the tray receiving unit through the cleaning unit back to the tray receiving unit.
   USE - Substrate cleaning system used for a glass substrate used in a mobile device. Can be used for cleaning glass substrates using carbon dioxide particles.
   ADVANTAGE - Provides a substrate cleaning system that performs dry cleaning on a substrate by spraying compressed air and carbon dioxide particles together on the substrate, thus increasing process efficiency. Performs the inspection process, cleaning process, and re-inspection process of the substrate using one equipment, thus obtaining a compact arrangement structure that minimizes the equipment space of the substrate cleaning system. Uses an inverter to turn the substrate at 180 degrees to reliably prevent errors due to foreign matter when cleaning both surfaces of the substrate. Prevents re-contamination as the surface of the already cleaned substrate comes into contact with the inverter or the transfer robot by holding only the edge of the substrate by the inverter and the transfer robot. Shortens the process time as the substrate is cleaned while being transported. Performs pre-inspection of the foreign substances remaining on the substrate and cleans only the area where the foreign substances remaining above the reference value are located, thus reducing the time of cleaning the entire substrate. Transports the substrate while being seated in the first tray provided with the porous holder, and maintains a flat state during the cleaning process even if the thickness of the substrate becomes thin as the cleaning process is performed to prevent cleaning deviation due to bending effect. Re-inspects the cleaned substrate and immediately goes through the cleaning process again in the circulatory path when the cleaning condition is poor, so that the effect of implementing complete cleaning in which the input substrate meets the standard value.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a layout view illustrating the configuration of the substrate cleaning system.
   Substrate cleaning system (100)
   Tray receiving unit (110)
   Inspection unit (120)
   Cleaning unit (130,150)
   Re-inspection unit (160)

MC P43-B99; U11-C06A1B

TI Absorbing selectively ammonia in melamine exhaust            gas involves absorbing melamine exhaust gas by            absorbing liquid in super-gravity rotating packed bed,            and discharging melamine exhaust gas from liquid            outlet.
AU ZHANG Q
   LIU Y
   XUE X
   GUO Q
   YUAN Z
   QI G
   ZHANG C
AE UNIV NORTH CHINA (UYZB-C)
GA 202170895C
AB    NOVELTY - Absorbing selectively ammonia in melamine                exhaust gas involves absorbing melamine exhaust gas                by absorbing liquid in super-gravity rotating                packed bed (1), enriching a large amount of ammonia                in the absorbing liquid, discharging melamine                exhaust gas from the liquid outlet (1.4) after                absorbing, and discharging exhaust gas containing                portion of ammonia and carbon dioxide from the gas                outlet (1.2), where the molar ratio of ammonia gas                and carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas is not more                than 2:1.
   USE - Method for absorbing selectively ammonia in                melamine exhaust gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has super-gravity to perform                non-equilibrium selective absorption, breaks                through the fussy process route, low product                utilization rate, realize the efficient and fast                liquid, and provide fast absorption                environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for selectively absorbing ammonia in melamine                exhaust gas by super-gravity non-equilibrium, which                comprises rotating packed bed is provided with gas                inlet and outlet and liquid inlet and outlet, the                gas inlet of the super-gravity rotating packed bed                is connected with the melamine exhaust gas source,                the gas outlet of the super-gravity rotating packed                bed is connected with the urea production line or                ammonium bicarbonate production line, the liquid                inlet of the super-gravity rotating packed bed is                connected with the absorbing liquid source by the                liquid pump, the liquid outlet of the super-gravity                rotating packed bed is connected with the ammonia                enrichment device by the cooler the gas inlet and                the gas outlet of the super-gravity rotating packed                bed are further provided with a sampling analysis                system, the inner portion of the super-gravity                rotating packed bed is provided with a filling                layer, the outer edge of each packing ring of the                inner rotor of the super-gravity rotating packed                bed is constructed with multiple body resistance                pieces, the body resistance piece will                synchronously rotate with the packing ring,                increasing the shear stress and deformation rate of                the gas, and realizing the turbulent motion of the                gas until the turbulent motion.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of device                for selectively absorbing ammonia in melamine                exhaust gas.
   Super-gravity rotating packed bed (1)
   Gas outlet (1.2)
   Liquid outlet (1.4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The absorbing ammonia in                  melamine exhaust gas by super-gravity                  non-equilibrium involves conveying the melamine                  exhaust gas and the absorbing liquid to the                  super-gravity rotating packed bed, cutting the                  melamine exhaust gas and the absorbing liquid at                  high speed by high speed rotating filler layer                  and fully contact, finishing the selective                  absorption of ammonia in the residence time of                  the millisecond level, discharging the absorbed                  gas from the gas outlet into the down stream                  process, satisfying the mol ratio of ammonia and                  carbon dioxide is 2:1 or 1:1 into ammonium                  bicarbonate production process after absorbing,                  discharging the diluted ammonia aqueous solution                  from the liquid outlet, cooling by cooler,                  entering the solution in ammonia enrichment                  device, and increasing the concentration to 20 %                  for the sale of the commodity. Preferred                  Conditions: The melamine exhaust gas amount is                  200-800 cubic meters per hour, The molar ratio of                  ammonia and carbon dioxide is (2.2-2.4):1. The                  gas temperature is 100-150 degrees C. The gas                  pressure of the super-gravity rotating packed bed                  is 0.2-0.4 megapascal. The absorption process                  residence time is 8-12 meter second. The                  concentration of dilute ammonia aqueous solution                  is 7-10 wt.%. The absorption liquid comprises                  7-10% ammonia and less than 3% carbon dioxide.                  The corresponding ammonia selectivity range is                  86-92%. The corresponding absorption rate is                  35-45%. The rotating speed of the airflow                  opposite to the shearing rotary packed is                  500-1200 revolutions per minutes. The device                  operation stable time is 5-10 minutes. Preferred                  Components: The absorption liquid is normal                  temperature soft water. The filler in inner                  portion is metal mesh filler.

PN CN112744792-A; CN112744792-B
TI Preparation of metal oxide powder and nitric acid            involves pyrolyzing molten nitrate, passing gas through            dust collection device to obtain metal oxide powder,            passing mixed gas to absorption device, cooling,            condensing and concentrating.
AU ZHAO L
   WANG C
   DAN Y
   MA B
   JIN C
   CHEN Y
   ZHAO P
   GAO B
   ZHAO D
AE SICHUAN SHUNYING POWER BATTERY MATERIALS (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202150852T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of metal oxide powder and nitric                acid by decomposing nitrate involves adding molten                nitrate to a pyrolysis furnace, pyrolyzing to                generate high-temperature dust gas, passing the gas                through the high-temperature dust collection device                to obtain metal oxide powder, passing mixed gas                after high-temperature dust collection through a                hot air blower, treating one portion in nitric acid                absorption device to prepare nitric acid, and using                the other portion as circulating gas, mixing one                portion of circulating gas with oxygen-enriched air                in a mixing chamber to form an oxygen-enriched                combustion-supporting gas, combusting resulting gas                with a combustion gas in combustion chamber to                release high-temperature fuel gas, mixing remaining                portion with high temperature gas, circulating to                the pyrolysis furnace to pyrolyze the nitrate melt,                cooling gas entering nitric acid absorption device,                condensing, cooling, pressurizing, concentrating,                and absorbing to produce finished nitric                acid.
   USE - Preparation of metal oxide powder and nitric                acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal oxide powder and nitric acid can be                prepared efficiently by simple method without                affecting the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of metal oxide powder and nitric                acid by decomposing nitrate involves adding molten                nitrate to a pyrolysis furnace, pyrolyzing to                generate high-temperature dust gas, passing the gas                through the high-temperature dust collection device                to obtain metal oxide powder after gas-solid                separation, passing mixed gas after                high-temperature dust collection through a hot air                blower, treating one portion in nitric acid                absorption device to prepare nitric acid, and using                the other portion as circulating gas, mixing one                portion of circulating gas with oxygen-enriched air                in a mixing chamber to form an oxygen-enriched                combustion-supporting gas, combusting resulting gas                with a combustion gas in the combustion chamber to                release high-temperature fuel gas, mixing remaining                portion with high temperature gas at a                predetermined temperature in the mixing chamber,                circulating into the pyrolysis furnace to pyrolyze                the nitrate melt, cooling gas entering the nitric                acid absorption device by a waste heat boiler to                produce steam, performing deep condensation to                remove water, performing multi-stage cooling to a                predetermined temperature, pressurizing and                concentrating cooled nitrogen oxide gas by a                compressor, absorbing in absorption tower,                producing finished nitric acid at the bottom of the                absorption tower, and supplying absorbed tail gas                into carbon dioxide recovery system to produce                carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrate is calcium                  nitrate, aluminum nitrate, magnesium nitrate,                  iron nitrate, scandium nitrate, nickel nitrate,                  cobalt nitrate, manganese nitrate, chromium                  nitrate and/or zinc nitrate. The high-temperature                  dust gas is a mixed gas containing metal oxide                  powder, nitrogen oxide gas, water vapor, and                  oxygen. The oxygen content of the oxygen-enriched                  air is 30-100%. The oxygen content of the                  oxygen-enriched combustion-supporting gas is                  15-30%. The combustion gas is natural gas,                  coal-based gas and/or coke oven gas. The mixing                  ratio of the oxygen-enriched                  combustion-supporting gas and the combustion gas                  is 2-20:1. Preferred Method: Pyrolysis is carried                  out at 300-1500 degrees C. The temperature of the                  mixed gas after high-temperature dust collection                  is 300-700 degrees C. 10-70% Mixed gas after                  high-temperature dust collection is supplied to                  nitric acid absorption device to prepare nitric                  acid. The temperature of the combustion chamber                  is controlled to 900-1500 degrees C. The                  temperature of the high-temperature gas formed by                  mixing the high-temperature fuel gas and the                  circulating gas in the mixing chamber is 500-1300                  degrees C. The gas is cooled to 120-200 degrees C                  by the waste heat boiler, and the temperature                  after multi-stage cooling is 35-50 degrees C. The                  pressure of the compressor is 0.3-1.2 MPa. The                  concentration of the finished nitric acid is                  45-65%. Preferred Apparatus: The pyrolysis                  furnace includes a rotary kiln decomposition,                  cyclone dynamic calcination decomposition, and                  boiling furnace type thermal decomposition type                  furnace. The high-temperature dust collection                  device includes high-temperature metal film dust                  collection device, high-temperature metal wire                  dust collection device, and high-temperature                  porous ceramic dust collection device.

PN MY184501-A
TI Co-current system for separating e.g. carbon            dioxide from gas stream by using static or centrifugal            type separator in thermal power plant, has solid            collection tank receiving solidified material and            solvent tank to receive separated solvent.
AU SHARIFF A B M
   KEONG L K
   MELLON N B
   SEE T L
AE INST TECHNOLOGY PETRONAS SDN BHD (TECH-Non-standard)
GA 2022A07531
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a contacting unit for                contacting a gas stream (1) with solvent stream (3)                to allow absorption of acid gas into the solvent                stream. A recovering unit recovers the used solvent                discharged from the contacting unit. The contacting                unit comprises an in-line gas-liquid contactor (2),                where co-current flow is created for interaction                between the gas stream and the solvent stream to                form a contacting stream (4). The recovering unit                comprises an in-line gas-liquid separator (5) e.g.                static or centrifugal type separator, in connection                with the gasliquid contactor to separate the                discharged contacting stream to form a first stream                as a treated gas stream (6) and a stream as used                solvent (7) with captured acid gas. An air blown or                heat exchange typed cooler (8) is connected with                the gas-liquid separator to reduce temperature of                the used solvent. A solid collection tank (15)                receives solidified material (13) and a solvent                tank (18) to receive separated solvent (11).
   USE - Co-current system for separating acid gas e.g.                carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfhide, sulfur dioxide,                carbon disulfide, carbonyl sulfhide and mercaptan,                from a gas stream by using an in-line gas-liquid                contactor-separator i.e. static or centrifugal type                separator, in a thermal power plant for high                pressure onshore gas processing operation and                offshore gas processing operation.
   ADVANTAGE - The used solvent can be recovered by cooling                and separating the solidified material from the                used solvent, so that the recovered solvent can be                circulated for subsequent usage in the acid gas                separation process. The system can be carried out                at variable pressure from atmospheric pressure                onward and temperature of industrial interest. The                system is suitable for separation of acid gas                content from gas stream with concentration of up to                50%.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                co-current system for acid gas separation from gas                stream for onshore gas processing operation.
   1Gas stream
   2In-line gas-liquid contactor
   3Solvent stream
   4Contacting stream
   5In-line gas-liquid separator
   6Treated gas stream
   7Used solvent
   8Air blown or heat exchange typed                cooler
   11Separated solvent
   13Solidified material
   15Solid collection tank
   18Solvent tank

PN CN112441951-A
TI Synthesizing ether bond-containing diisocyanate by salt-forming phosgenation method, comprises e.g. mixing ether bond-containing diamine with inert solvent, adding para-amino hydrochloride or amino carbonate solution, and heating.
AU TENG Z
   LIU Y
   LI X
   MA R
   TIAN Z
   TAO C
   LIU J
   LIU S
   ZHANG F
   XU H
AE GANSU YINGUANG JUYIN CHEM CO LTD (GANS-Non-standard)
GA 202124391P
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing ether bond-containing diisocyanate by salt-forming phosgenation method, comprises reacting ether bond-containing diamine as raw material to obtain ether bond-containing diisocyanate, preferably (a) mixing ether bond-containing diamine with inert solvent, and introducing hydrogen chloride or carbon dioxide gas at a certain flow rate under the condition of -10 to 40 degrees C, and generating amino hydrochloride or amino carbonate, (b) heating para-amino hydrochloride or amino carbonate solution at 70-100 degrees C, introducing phosgene at a certain flow rate, and generating carbamoyl chloride by the reaction, (c) heating at 100-200 degrees C for thermo-photochemical reaction to remove hydrogen chloride from carbamoyl chloride to produce diisocyanate photochemical liquid containing ether bonds, and (iv) de-phosgenizing, desolvating, and refining the photochemical solution of diisocyanate containing ether linkage to obtain qualified diisocyanate containing ether linkage.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing ether bond-containing diisocyanate by salt-forming phosgenation method.
   ADVANTAGE - The ether bond-containing diisocyanate: has yellowing resistance and high temperature resistance; and can meet the special requirements of downstream product performance. The method is simple process, easy to control, and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing ether bond-containing diisocyanate by salt-forming phosgenation method, comprises reacting ether bond-containing diamine of formula (H2N(CH2) nO(CH2)2O(CH2) nNH2) as raw material to obtain ether bond-containing diisocyanate of formula (OCN(CH2)nO(CH2)2O(CH2)nNCO), preferably (a) mixing ether bond-containing diamine with inert solvent, and introducing hydrogen chloride or carbon dioxide gas at a certain flow rate under the condition of -10 to 40 degrees C, and generating amino hydrochloride or amino carbonate, where the reaction time is 1-5 hours, (b) heating para-amino hydrochloride or amino carbonate solution at 70-100 degrees C, introducing phosgene at a certain flow rate, and generating carbamoyl chloride by the reaction, where the reaction time is 1-5 hours, (c) heating at 100-200 degrees C for thermo-photochemical reaction to remove hydrogen chloride from carbamoyl chloride to produce diisocyanate photochemical liquid containing ether bonds, and (iv) de-phosgenizing, desolvating, and refining the photochemical solution of diisocyanate containing ether linkage to obtain qualified diisocyanate containing ether linkage.
   n = less than or equal to 15.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (a), the mass ratio of inert liquid to ether bond-containing diamine is 20:1-3:1. The molar ratio of hydrogen chloride or carbon dioxide gas and ether bond-containing diamine is 10:1-2:1. The phosgene used in the reaction can be a liquid or gas phase feed, and the mass ratio of total amount of phosgene used in the reaction to the ether bond-containing diamine is 15:1-4:1. The ether bond-containing diamine is preferably H2NCH2O(CH2) 2OCH2NH2(EDR-120), H2N(CH2)2O(CH2)2O(CH2)2NH2(EDR-148), H2N(CH2)3O(CH2)2O(CH2)3NH2(EDR-176), H2N(CH2)4O(CH2)2O(CH2)4NH2(EDR-204); and the ether bond-containing diisocyanate is preferably OCNCH2O(CH2)2OCH2NCO (EDI-172), OCN(CH2)2O(CH2)2O(CH2)2NCO(EDI-200), OCN(CH2)3O(CH2)2O(CH2)3NCO (EDI-228), OCN(CH2)4O(CH2)2O(CH2)4NCO(EDI-256). The inert liquid is toluene, chlorobenzene or dichlorobenzene, diethyl isophthalate, amyl acetate, and methyl salicylate.

PT P
PN CN112320967-A
TI Utilization of sunlight heat energy involves using plastic greenhouse as a good tool for collecting sunlight heat as long as there is sunlight, collecting solar energy resources all year round, and using the cheap energy.
AU YANG H
AE YANG H (YANG-Individual)
GA 202117209K
AB    NOVELTY - Utilization of sunlight heat energy involves using plastic greenhouse as a good tool for collecting sunlight heat as long as there is sunlight, it can collect solar energy resources all year round. The cheap energy is used to increase the temperature of the domestic sewage in the shed to achieve the best conditions for bacterial reproduction, so that various bacterial organisms in the sewage can multiply. The proteins, fats, biological particles and other organisms in the sewage have qualitative changes after a certain period of decomposition and treatment. The turbid sewage becomes clear, the particles settle, and it accumulates into sludge, which is organic fertilizer and humus. The sunlight heat energy is used, the drained sludge can also be dried, and facilitated the collection and transportation of sludge. The plastic greenhouse collects the heat of the sun and replaces fossil energy. The carbon dioxide emissions is reduced, reduced the global greenhouse effect, and reduced smog.
   USE - Method for utilization of sunlight heat energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is contributes to our clean mountains and green waters, ensures the development of the country's strategic plan for applying more farm manure to farmland, and ensures my country's food security.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Utilization of sunlight heat energy involves using plastic greenhouse as a good tool for collecting sunlight heat as long as there is sunlight, it can collect solar energy resources all year round. The cheap energy is used to increase the temperature of the domestic sewage in the shed to achieve the best conditions for bacterial reproduction, so that various bacterial organisms in the sewage can multiply. The proteins, fats, biological particles and other organisms in the sewage have qualitative changes after a certain period of decomposition and treatment. The turbid sewage becomes clear, the particles settle, and it accumulates into sludge, which is organic fertilizer and humus. The sunlight heat energy is used, the drained sludge can also be dried, and facilitated the collection and transportation of sludge. The plastic greenhouse collects the heat of the sun and replaces fossil energy. The carbon dioxide emissions is reduced, reduced the global greenhouse effect, and reduced smog. The plastic greenhouses and sunlight is used to generate heat can promote the sustainable development of the sunshine water sewage treatment plant. The rural villages and towns are popularized, and the whole people act together to treat sewage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The solar water heater chip group are on both sides outside the processing shed. The solar water heaters are installed on the upper parts of the aisle, and enter the processing shed through the pipeline. The control gate is open after the domestic sewage is placed into the treatment tank. The heat generated by the solar heat passed through the heat pipe, coupled with the heat collected in the greenhouse. The double temperature increase can accelerate the increase of the sewage temperature in the pond, make the sewage in the pond reach the optimal temperature for the reproduction of biological bacteria as soon as possible. The decomposition processing time is shorten and improved the utilization rate of the equipment. The number of solar water heaters are increased or decreased appropriately according to the local climate of the treatment site. The heat preservation measures are taken in winter in the northern area, and the number of solar water heaters is increased, which can decompose and treat domestic sewage throughout the year. The collection of sludge is the collection of organic fertilizer and humus, these things are substances that take tens of thousands of years to form, the dead animals and plants in the sewage, protein, fat. The inorganic matter precipitated after the organic matter is decomposed by biological bacteria. The growth of all kinds of plants is inseparable from them, the land does not have it. The taste of the produced agricultural products is not good, the grain is not fragrant, the dishes are tasteless, and there is little arable land in our country. The arable land is used for plundered production, and more fertilizers are used to receive agricultural products. The farmland soil has no humus soil, and no soil aggregate structure. The land is slowly deserting, which endangers the national food security so we have to act immediately and collect all kinds of humus for farmland, and there is a lot of humus in domestic sewage, which is produced every day. The sunshine water wastewater treatment plant is used to collect. The little by little are collected, gather little and make more. The land on which it depends for survival slowly repair, ensures the development of the country's strategic plan for applying more farm manure to farmland, and ensures my country's food security.

PN PL430243-A1
TI Preparing dried fruit cubes in chocolate by analyzing and flattening fruits, cutting it, drying it, spraying it with chocolate, cooling it and performing conditioning process for it in modified atmosphere.
AU OGRYZEK S
   KOCHMANSKI K
AE ROS-SWEET SP ZOO (ROSS-Non-standard)
GA 2021131172
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing dried fruit cubes in chocolate involves analyzing seedless fruits for damage and differences in color, flattening fruits with a favorable water content of 21-25% into fruit pulp, cutting the obtained flakes of fruit pulp into cubic fruit cubes, drying the prepared cube in a fluidized bed under nitrogen atmosphere until it reaches a favorable humidity of 18-20%, placing the dried fruit cube in a dragee drum, spraying chocolate on it at 45-50 degrees C, spraying it with compressed air from a bottle under pressure to 200 bar, cooling the interior drum to a favorable temperature of 20-25 degrees C, and conditioning the fruit cubes with the chocolate applied to the conditioning process in open crates at 15-20 degrees C for 24 hours in a modified atmosphere filled with a mixture of nitrogen and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing dried fruit cubes in chocolate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing dried fruit cubes in chocolate involves analyzing seedless fruits for damage and differences in color, flattening fruits with a favorable water content of 21-25% into fruit pulp with a preferred thickness of 6-12 mm, cutting the obtained flakes of fruit pulp into cubic fruit cubes with a preferred length of 6-12 mm, under aseptic conditions with the use of UV lamps with a bandwidth of 100-280 nm and a power of 100-110 W/m2, drying the prepared cube in a fluidized bed under nitrogen atmosphere with a purity of at least 99.95%, in a gas stream with a flow of 10 l/minute, until it reaches a favorable humidity of 18-20%, placing the dried fruit cube in a dragee drum, spraying chocolate on it at 45-50 degrees C, spraying it with compressed air with a purity of at least 99.99% from a bottle under pressure to 200 bar, cooling the interior drum to a favorable temperature of 20-25 degrees C, with an air stream with a flow of 4-6 m3/minute and a relative humidity of 40-50%, and conditioning the fruit cubes with the chocolate in open crates at 15-20 degrees C for 24 hours in a modified atmosphere filled with a mixture of nitrogen and carbon dioxide in the ratio of 80:20.

PN CN112103591-A; CN112103591-B
TI Harmless recycling of waste lithium battery electrolyte includes soaking spent lithium batteries in sodium carbonate aqueous solution, separating steel, plastic, diaphragm and copper, discharging gas into clean water, filtering and drying.
AU ZHANG Y
AE GUANGZHOU HAOYUE NEW ENERGY EQUIP CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   ANHUI LIYUAN NEW ENERGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020C9165F
AB    NOVELTY - Harmless recycling of waste lithium battery electrolyte includes soaking spent lithium batteries in sodium carbonate aqueous solution, washing spent lithium batteries with water, drying, storing washed water, filling mixture of argon and carbon dioxide to crush waste lithium battery to obtain crushed material, discharging gas into clean water, placing crushed materials in N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP) solvent for ultrasonic treatment, drying, heating, separating steel, plastic, diaphragm and copper, cleaning steel, plastic, diaphragm and copper, transferring remaining waste battery material to closed container, adding clean water, mixing for 10-30 minutes, discharging gas into clean water, filtering, using sodium hydroxide solution to treat remaining solid materials, mixing for 10-30 minutes, filtering, using citric acid and hydrogen peroxide mixed solution to treat remaining solid materials, filtering, drying graphite particles and recovering clear liquid.
   USE - The method is for harmless recycling of waste lithium battery electrolyte.
   ADVANTAGE - The method recovers electrolyte components and separates cathode materials, anode materials and other components to achieve safety and reduce environmental pollution in recycling process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Harmless recycling of waste lithium battery electrolyte comprises soaking spent lithium batteries in 5-10 wt.% sodium carbonate aqueous solution for 8-12 hours, treating by discharge operation, washing spent lithium batteries with water, drying at 40-50 degrees C, storing washed water, filling mixture of argon and carbon dioxide to mechanically crush waste lithium battery in closed environment to obtain crushed material, discharging gas into clean water, placing crushed materials in N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP) solvent at 60-70 degrees C for ultrasonic treatment for 0.5-1 hour, centrifuging, drying for 1-2 hours, heating solvent at 150-200 degrees C, using wind separator, magnetic separator and gravity separator to separate crushed materials, separating steel, plastic, diaphragm and copper, cleaning steel, plastic, diaphragm and copper by ultrasonic in clean water to obtain clean and electrolyte free steel, plastic, diaphragm and copper materials, transferring remaining waste battery material to closed container, adding clean water, mixing for 10-30 minutes, discharging gas into clean water, filtering, using 20-30 g/L sodium hydroxide solution to treat remaining solid materials, mixing for 10-30 minutes, filtering, using citric acid and hydrogen peroxide mixed solution to treat remaining solid materials, filtering, drying graphite particles and recovering clear liquid.

AE LIU X (LIUX-Individual)
GA 2020B6386T
AB    NOVELTY - The greenhouse has a liquid distribution pipe (6) which is connected to the lower side wall of a support crossbar (3). The liquid outlet pipe (5) is arranged corresponding to the water inlet of the liquid distribution pipe. A liquid discharge control mechanism (11) is connected between the liquid outlet pipe and the liquid dispensing pipe. The connecting branch pipes (7) are connected to the liquid distribution pipe. The connecting branch pipe is provided with a one-way valve inside. A spherical balloon (8) is connected to the bottom end of connecting branch pipe, and is filled with ammonium bicarbonate (9). The lower half of spherical balloon is provided with evenly distributed expansion liquid outlet slits (10). The liquid outlet slit is in a tightly closed state, when the spherical balloon is inflated in the initial state. The expansion liquid outlet slit forms a vent hole of 0.5 to 1mm, when the spherical balloon is expanded to the maximum volume.
   USE - Self-adjusting yield increasing greenhouse shed.

The entire process can be self-regulated. The entire device has a simple structure, low cost, and is suitable for a large range use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The greenhouse has a support crossbar (3) which is fixed at the connection between a shed case (1) and a shed roof (2). A dilute hydrochloric acid storage barrel (4) is fixed on the inner top of the roof. The bottom end of dilute hydrochloric acid storage barrel is connected with a liquid outlet pipe (5).

The meshed conduit comprises porous openings sufficient for gas to pass through, but for the CO2 snow block to remain within the snow chamber. A CO2 snow charger (4) is operably or integrally connected to or penetrated through the meshed conduit. The CO2 snow charger is configured to selectively direct CO2 fluid into the snow chamber. The CO2 snow charger has one or more openings in fluid communication with the snow chamber.
   USE - Carbon dioxide (CO2) snow-making kit for charging CO2 and generating CO2 snow block within container used for preservation and transport of various items. TI Improving low-temperature impact toughness of welded joints of X80 pipeline steel pipes by selecting low-temperature pipeline steel, milling, pre-welding, fine internal welding and external welding.
AU LIU B
   WEI F
   ZHAO H
   XI M
   ZHAO Y
   TIAN L
   NIU A
   HUANG X
   ZHANG C
   ZHU S
   WANG J
AE CHINA NAT PETROLEUM CORP (CNPC-C)
   BAOJI PETROLEUM STEEL PIPE CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2020819497
AB    NOVELTY - Improving low-temperature impact toughness of welded joints of X80 pipeline steel pipes comprises selecting X80 low-temperature pipeline steel, rough milling and fine milling, and processing into X-shaped groove; groove processing, pre-bending JCOE molding, and performing pre-welding treatment adopting mixed gas shielded welding with carbon dioxide and argon; performing fine internal welding adopting double-wire four-layer multi-channel submerged arc automatic welding, determining specific number of welding passes according to wall thickness of pipeline steel where number of internal welding passes is 8-9, and controlling welding heat input of each pass, welding current, welding voltage, wire spacing, dry elongation and welding speed; and performing the external welding adopting double wire four-layer multi-channel submerged arc automatic welding method.
   USE - The method is used for improving low-temperature impact toughness of welded joints of X80 pipeline steel pipes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving low-temperature impact toughness of welded joints of X80 pipeline steel pipes comprises selecting X80 low-temperature pipeline steel with wall thickness of 30-40 mm, rough milling and fine milling, and processing into X-shaped groove where inner and outer groove angles are both 60 degrees , outer groove depth is 11-14 mm, and blunt edge is 8-12 mm; groove processing, pre-bending JCOE molding, and performing pre-welding treatment adopting mixed gas shielded welding with 55-65 wt.% carbon dioxide and 35-45 wt.% argon; performing fine internal welding adopting double-wire four-layer multi-channel submerged arc automatic welding where front wire uses direct current (DC) reverse connection, back wire uses alternative current (AC), and interlayer temperature is controlled below 150 degrees C, determining specific number of welding passes according to wall thickness of pipeline steel where number of internal welding passes is 8-9, and controlling welding heat input of each pass at 10-25 KJ/cm, internal welding current at 400-700 A, internal welding voltage at 32-34 V, wire spacing at 19 mm, dry elongation of front wire and back wire at 26 and 26mm respectively and welding speed at 1.0-1.3 m/minute; and performing the external welding adopting double wire four-layer multi-channel submerged arc automatic welding method.

PN CN111392758-A
TI Preparing nano/micro rare earth material involves dissolving rare earth salt in deionized water to form rare earth salt solution, and adding powdered calcium carbonate to rare earth salt solution, and dispersing to obtain suspension.
AU GAO Y
   YAN X
AE ZIBO GUANHAI IND & TRADE CO LTD (ZIBO-Non-standard)
GA 202067612F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano/micro rare earth material involves dissolving rare earth salt in deionized water to form a rare earth salt solution, where the concentration of the rare earth salt solution is 0.005-0.5mol/l. The powdered calcium carbonate is added to the rare earth salt solution, and ultrasonically dispersed to obtain a calcium carbonate and rare earth salt suspension. The calcium carbonate and rare earth salt suspension is transferred to a reactor, where the air is replaced in the reactor with carbon dioxide gas, and then the hydrothermal reaction is performed under certain conditions. The rare earth carbonate is separated and dried after the completion of the hydrothermal reaction to obtain slurry. The slurry is immediately filtered while hot, and the filtered precipitate is washed and dried to obtain rare earth carbonate. The obtained rare earth carbonate is calcined to obtain rare earth oxide nano material.
   USE - Clean preparation method for preparing nano/micro rare earth material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare nano/micro rare earth material with high versatility and controllable morphology and particle size in a cost-effective, simple and eco-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano/micro rare earth material involves dissolving rare earth salt in deionized water to form a rare earth salt solution, where the concentration of the rare earth salt solution is 0.005-0.5mol/l. The powdered calcium carbonate is added to the rare earth salt solution, and ultrasonically dispersed to obtain a calcium carbonate and rare earth salt suspension. The calcium carbonate and rare earth salt suspension is transferred to a reactor, where the air is replaced in the reactor with carbon dioxide gas, and then the hydrothermal reaction is performed under certain conditions. The rare earth carbonate is separated and dried after the completion of the hydrothermal reaction to obtain slurry. The slurry is immediately filtered while hot, and the filtered precipitate is washed and dried to obtain rare earth carbonate. The obtained rare earth carbonate is calcined to obtain rare earth oxide nano material. The filtrate is heated and boiled, then a certain concentration of carbonate is added, the reaction is performed for a certain period of time, cooled to room temperature naturally, and filtered to obtain calcium carbonate, which is washed and dried to obtain calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The rare earth metal in the rare earth salt solution is rare earth elements, preferably rare earth chloride or rare earth nitrate. The carbonate is sodium carbonate or ammonium carbonate.

TI Preparing enriched oxygen comprises filtering raw material air, sending the filtered gas to raw material booster removing e.g. water, separating gas and raw material gas, compressing, expanding and obtaining liquid air.

   WANG J
   FANG L
AE ZHEJIANG ZHIHAI CHEM EQUIP ENG CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing enriched oxygen comprises (i) filtering raw material air using self-cleaning air filter, sending filtered gas to raw material booster, using pre-cooling system and molecular sieve to remove water, carbon dioxide and macromolecular impurities and (ii) separating gas and raw material gas into two streams, compressing one stream by pressurizing end of expander and entering expander after heat exchange by the heat exchanger, expanding to pressure of mixing tower, sending to mixing tower from bottom and allowing another stream of air directly into heat exchanger, passing through separation tank heat exchanger and sending separated liquid into high-pressure rectification tower, dividing the separated gas into two streams, sending one directly into high-pressure rectification tower and using other heat source of rectification tower in liquid oxygen tower evaporator to evaporate liquid oxygen and obtaining liquid air and sending into high-pressure rectification tower.

TI Capture device for capturing radioactive inert gas, has upper plate that is arranged at upper end of collection column and lower plate that is arranged on air outlet of inlet pipe and provided with flow path through which air passes.

   KIM H C
   LEE W
   CHOI S D
   LIM J M
   CHOI K S
AE KOREA ATOMIC ENERGY RES INST (KAER-C)
GA 202053103K
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a hollow fiber membrane module for removing impurities from a target gas. A multi-valve unit for controlling communication to paths with respect to the target gas passing through the hollow fiber membrane module. A collection unit is provided with two collection columns in which an adsorbent adsorbing a radioactive inert gas in a target gas passing through the multi-valve unit is accommodated. An outlet is arranged for discharging air from the collection column. An inlet pipe is arranged to allow air introduced through the inlet to be discharged to a lower end of the collection column. An upper plate is arranged at the upper end of the collection column and provided with holes for discharging air passing through the adsorbent. A lower plate is arranged on the air outlet of the inlet pipe and provided with a flow path through which air passes. The collecting column is configured to maintain a temperature of 0 degrees C or higher.
   USE - Capture device for capturing radioactive inert gas such as krypton, xenon, radon, and argon for monitoring nuclear activity (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The capturing device of the radioactive inert gas is featured with higher krypton adsorption efficiency than the conventional device, so that the capture cycle is shortened, and dilution of the radioactive elements is minimized and precise nuclear activity monitoring is achieved since samples are stored in different capture columns for each capture cycle. The drying gas prevents phenomenon that water or carbon dioxide discharged from the hollow fiber membrane prevents water and carbon dioxide from flowing out of the hollow fiber membrane wall by generating water droplets on the surface of the hollow fiber membrane. The collector replacement cycle is increased, and the problem of occurrence of a safety accident according to a manual process is solved. The adsorbent filling area is increased when the stirring element is provided in the collection column so that the stirring member installation area overlaps the adsorbent filling area partially without the need for the adsorbent filling area and the stirring element installation area to be separated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for capturing radioactive inert gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device for capturing radioactive inert gas. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

TI Producing edible spice seasoning (essential) oil comprises performing quantitative analysis, extracting flavored sesame oil resin, extracting spices, performing Maillard thermal reaction, aging and flavoring.

GA 202052689S

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing edible spice seasoning (essential) oil comprises (a) performing quantitative analysis of the effective characteristic components of various spices, (b) crushing various natural edible spices, extracting the flavored sesame oil resin or essential oil in the spices by traditional solvent method, supercritical carbon dioxide method and subcritical method to obtain product A, extracting spices one by one, compounding into compound spices on the basis of quantitative analysis, extracting at once, (c) subjecting the single spice oleoresin (or compound spice oleoresin) to Maillard thermal reaction at 80-120 degrees C on the basis of quantitative analysis, aging to obtain product B, where the three major elements of Maillard reaction are sugar (monosaccharide or disaccharide, polysaccharide), amino acids (including meat digests), fats and oils (including vegetable fats and animal fats) and (d) diluting product B with edible oil to obtain various concentrations of cooked spice seasoning oil, using the cooked spice seasoning (essential) oil with other edible flavors and fragrances for flavoring (essential) oils of various flavors to obtain product C.

PN KR2055621-B1
TI Fine dust notification system for use by e.g. infants in e.g. day care center, has ventilation alarm unit that outputs ventilation recommendation message to user regardless of fine dust concentration based on carbon dioxide estimation value.
AU AN Y J
   LEE H
AE AN Y J (ANYJ-Individual)
GA 2019A5931Q
AB    NOVELTY - The fine dust notification system has a first receiver (25) for receiving a first measured value from a second receiver (26). The second receiver receives a second measured value from an external standby information server (40). A data estimator (27) estimates an alert value to be provided to the user by applying the comparison result between the first measured value and the second measured value to a predetermined rule. A display unit (23) visually displays the estimated alert value. When the estimated alert value is lower than the predetermined reference value, a ventilation alarm unit (28) outputs a ventilation recommendation message to the user. The ventilation alarm unit outputs a ventilation recommendation message to the user regardless of the fine dust concentration when the carbon dioxide estimation value is higher than a predetermined reference value.
   USE - Fine dust notification system for use by infants and children in day care center, kindergarten, elementary school and home.
   ADVANTAGE - The fine dust and ultra-fine dust concentration are provided visually to children. The measurement value of the neighborhood where the user resides is provided, without relying only on the atmospheric information provided in a regional unit. The atmospheric information such as the actual fine dust concentration is provided according to the characteristics around the residence. Since the display unit is displayed in a different color step by step, even in the case of a user such as infants and elderly person difficult to identify the characters easily, the information such as dust and ultrafine dust is confirmed, and the necessary measures such as wearing a mask when going out is taken. The overall structure of the notification system is configured as small and simple, and the manufacturing cost is maintained at a low cost. Since a fine dust distribution map is generated and provided, the fine dust concentration of the area to be moved or required is checked easily by the user.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the fine dust notification system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Display unit (23)
   Receiver (25,26)
   Data estimator (27)
   Ventilation alarm unit (28)
   External standby information server (40)

PN CN110551277-A
TI Modified-polyethylene terephthalate used for            preparing polyethylene terephthalate foaming material,            is obtained by esterifying terephthalic acid and            ethylene glycol, and adding stabilizer, branching            agent, nucleating agent, and catalyst.
AU LI N
   FAN Y
   PAN X
   PANG D
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC YIZHENG CHEM FIBER CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 2019A5792A
AB    NOVELTY - A modified-polyethylene terephthalate is                obtained by performing direct esterification of                terephthalic acid and ethylene glycol as main raw                material, and adding a stabilizer, a branching                agent, a nucleating agent, and a catalyst.
   USE - Modified-polyethylene terephthalate in the                form chip used for preparing polyethylene                terephthalate foaming material (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The modified-polyethylene terephthalate has                excellent viscosity, melt strength, and                crystallinity which is required for foaming, and                provides polyethylene terephthalate foaming                material having excellent foaming magnification,                cell size distribution, and mechanical                property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of modified-polyethylene                terephthalate, which involves adding terephthalic                acid, ethylene glycol, the stabilizer, the                branching agent, the nucleating agent, and the                catalyst into the reaction kettle, performing                esterification reaction at 220-265 degrees C, after                the water outlet amount reaches theoretical water                yield of more than 95%, carrying out                polycondensation under vacuum at 265-275 degrees C                and 250-600 Pa for a hour, gradually adjusting the                pressure to 10-60 Pa and temperature to 275-285                degrees C, when melt viscosity of 0.65-0.75 dl/g,                discharging, cooling, cutting, and drying to obtain                modified-polyethylene terephthalate-base, slicing                the base by putting the base into a tumbling                device, performing solid-phase polycondensation                reaction by heating and crystallizing at 10-40 Pa                and 215-230 degrees C, cooling, after reaction                step;
   (2) polyethylene terephthalate foaming                material, which is obtained by continuously                extruding the modified-polyethylene terephthalate                as a raw material and supercritical carbon dioxide                as a foaming agent; and
   (3) preparation of polyethylene terephthalate                foaming material, which involves forming a foaming                raw material by drying the modified-polyethylene                terephthalate slice at 100-160 degrees C for 4-8                hours, performing continuous extrusion foaming by                putting the foaming raw material and carbon dioxide                into an extruder, mixing the raw materials at an                injection pressure at 4-20 MPa, in an static mixer,                and extrusion foaming which is carried out by                four-stage heating, at stage (A) temperature of                240-280 degrees C, stage (B) temperature of 250-290                degrees C, stage (C) temperature of 220-280 degrees                C, stage (D) temperature of 220-280 degrees C, and                in which the temperature in the static mixer and                the machine head temperature is 220-280 degrees                C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The direct esterification                  method includes esterification reaction, melt                  polycondensation reaction, and solid-phase                  polycondensation reaction. Preferred Composition:                  The modified-polyethylene terephthalate comprises                  25-40 wt.% glycol. The method is performed using                  55-70 wt.% terephthalic acid, 0.015-0.06 wt.%                  catalyst, and 0.035-5 wt.% nucleating agent, and                  0.01-0.8 mol% branching agent based on molar                  amount of terephthalic acid. Preferred                  Components: The catalyst is antimony trioxide,                  antimony acetate, ethylene glycol antimony,                  germanium dioxide, tetra-n-butyl titanate,                  titanium potassium oxalate, manganese acetate,                  and/or zinc acetate, preferably antimony                  trioxide, antimony acetate, and/or ethylene                  glycol antimony. The stabilizer is chosen from                  phosphoric acid, polyphosphoric acid, trimethyl                  phosphate, triphenyl phosphate, and triphenyl                  phosphite. The nucleating agent is chosen from                  talc powder, mica powder, barium sulfate, calcium                  carbonate, silicon dioxide nanoparticles, boron                  nitride, and titanium dioxide. The branching                  agent is chosen from glycerol, pentaerythritol,                  trimellitic anhydride, pyromellitic anhydride,                  glycerol carbonate,                  5-methyl-5-hydroxymethylene-trimethylene                  carbonate, and                  5-ethyl-5-hydroxymethylene-trimethylene                  carbonate. Preferred Property: The                  modified-polyethylene terephthalate has intrinsic                  viscosity of 0.8-1.4 dL/g. Preferred Conditions:                  The extrusion foaming is carried out by                  four-stage heating including stage (A)                  temperature of 240-260 degrees C, stage (B)                  temperature of 260-280 degrees C, stage (C)                  temperature of 250-270 degrees C, and stage (D)                  temperature of 230-260 degrees C.

PN CN110403843-A
TI Preparing skin care products with cannabidiol comprises e.g. adding tetrahydrocannabinol from e.g. Cannabis leaves or industrial cannabidiol to feed and feeding poultry to produce poultry eggs to obtain eggs rich in cannabidiol nutrients.
AU LIU Z
   LEI G
   WANG C
   JIAO J
AE ZHONGMING BIOTECHNOLOGY SHENZHEN CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 201994385K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing skin care products with cannabidiol comprises e.g. adding THC obtained from industrial Cannabis leaves, stems, flowers, root or other crushed material or industrial Cannabis extract CBD to feed and feeding poultry to produce poultry eggs to obtains eggs enriched with CBD nutrients in egg yolk, breaking egg, separating egg white and egg yolk by egg white separation device and obtaining egg yolk liquid rich in CBD nutrients, subjecting egg yolk liquid to microwave drying method or spray drying method or freeze drying method to obtain nutrient-rich egg yolk powder, obtaining egg butter of the CBD nutrient rich by subcritical or carbon dioxide supercritical method using egg yolk powder, pasteurizing egg butter rich in CBD nutrient, sealing and storing at a low temperature and adding required amount of CBD egg butter according to skin care products set by different functions and stirring to prepare skin care product for specific medical skin care purposes.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing skin care products with cannabidiol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical and contributes to popularization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing skin care products with cannabidiol comprises (i) adding tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) less than 0.3% of industrial Cannabis leaves, stems, flowers, root or other crushed material or industrial Cannabis extract cannabidiol (CBD) to feed and feeding poultry with nutrients to produce poultry eggs in order to meet the national industrial Cannabis standard of tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) to obtains eggs enriched with CBD nutrients in egg yolk; (ii) breaking egg obtained in step (i), separating egg white and egg yolk in the egg by the egg white separation device and obtaining egg yolk liquid rich in CBD nutrients; (iii) subjecting egg yolk liquid obtained in step (ii) to microwave drying method or spray drying method or freeze drying method to obtain nutrient-rich egg yolk powder having a moisture content of less than 4%; (iv) obtaining egg butter of the CBD nutrient rich by subcritical or carbon dioxide supercritical method using egg yolk powder obtained in step (iii); (v) pasteurizing egg butter rich in CBD nutrient obtained in step (iv), sealing and storing at a low temperature; and (vi) adding required amount of CBD egg butter according to skin care products set by different functions and stirring at room temperature to prepare skin care product for specific medical skin care purposes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The birds are chicken or duck or goose.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The skin care product includes facial mask or serum or hand cream or eye cream or lip balm or repair liquid.

PN CN110387351-A
TI Isolating and culturing human retinal muller cells comprises aseptically removing normal eyeball, peeling off retinal tissue, cleaning with phosphate buffer saline containing double-antibody, culturing, purifying and observing.
AU INVENTOR U
AE JIANGSU CHI SCI CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019924701
AB    NOVELTY - Isolating and culturing human retinal muller cells comprises (a) aseptically removing the normal eyeball surgically, trimming and removing connective tissue and muscle, then peeling off the retinal tissue under a dissecting microscope, cleaning with phosphate buffer saline containing the double-antibody, soaking, (b) cutting retinal tissue, mixing with type I and type IV collagenase, (c) terminating the digestion with Dulbecco's modified eagle /F12 medium containing fetal bovine serum and double antibody, filtering, centrifuging, removing the supernatant, retaining the precipitate, (d) adding fetal calf serum, basic fibroblast growth factor, common insulin, penicillin and streptomycin to DMEM/F12 medium, (e) resuspending the pellet in complete medium and placing a poly-lysine-coated flask and culturing carbon dioxide culture tank, (f) inoculating, carrying out the first liquid exchange, (g) cell passaging and purifying and (h) observing and identifying cell morphology.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating and culturing human retinal muller cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, stable, highly-efficient; and provides good experiment material for further researching the retinopathy damage occurs mechanism.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Isolating and culturing human retinal muller cells comprises (a) aseptically removing the normal eyeball surgically by the cornea donor, trimming and removing the surrounding connective tissue and muscle, then peeling off the retinal tissue under a dissecting microscope, cleaning with phosphate buffer saline containing the double-antibody, soaking in a double anti-phosphate buffer saline buffer for 10 minutes, (b) cutting retinal tissue into 1 mmx 1 mm x 1 mm tissue fragments, mixing with type I and type IV collagenase, (c) terminating the digestion with Dulbecco's modified eagle /F12 medium containing fetal bovine serum and double antibody, filtering through a 200 mesh stainless steel mesh, centrifuging at 1200-1500 rotations per minute for 5 minutes, removing the supernatant, retaining the precipitate, (d) complete medium preparation by adding fetal calf serum, basic fibroblast growth factor, common insulin, penicillin and streptomycin to DMEM/F12 medium, (e) resuspending the pellet in complete medium and placing a poly-lysine-coated 25 cm2 flask and culturing at 37 degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide culture tank, (f) inoculating for 24 hours, carrying out the first liquid exchange, changing the liquid for every 2-3 days, (g) cell passaging and purifying and (h) observing and identifying cell morphology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The pre-cooled phosphate buffer saline buffer concentration in the step (a) is 0.01 M and the pH is 7.2-7.4. The concentrations of mixed type I and IV collagenase in the step (b) is 0.1%. The fetal bovine serum concentration in the step (c) is 10%, the concentration of the double antibody is penicillin 100 u/ml and the streptomycin is 100 mu g/ml. The medium in the step (d) is a DMEM/F12 culture comprises 10% fetal calf serum, 100 u/ml penicillin, 100 mu g/ml streptomycin, 5 ng/ml basic fibroblast growth factor, 5 mg/l ordinary insulin and pH value is 7.2-7.4. The culture flask in the step (e) is coated with 0.01% polylysine.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing honeysuckle extract by                (i) adopting supercritical fluid extraction                technology, placing honeysuckle flower in an                extraction unit at a pressure of 20-30 MPa for                0.5-1.5 hours, where the flow rate of carbon                dioxide is 25-45 kg/hour, concentrating the                extracting solution, (ii) dissolving starch in                honeysuckle clear paste, mixing, heating and adding                sodium chloride, mixing, and rapidly cooling by a                ultralow-temperature refrigerator, curing and                drying, cooling to -50 to -150 degrees C through a                ultralow-temperature freezing crusher, and crushing                into 80 mesh to prepare a honeysuckle extract, (or)                dissolving starch in honeysuckle clear paste,                stirring, and adding vitamin C, uniformly mixing at                35-45 degrees C through an ultralow-temperature                vacuum belt drier, setting the temperature of the                second zone at 30-40 degrees C, setting the                temperature of the third zone at 20-30 degrees C,                setting the condensation temperature at 20-25                degrees C, and crushing the sieve mesh by 80 mesh                to obtain honeysuckle extract.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing honeysuckle extract                involves (i) adopting supercritical fluid                extraction technology, placing honeysuckle flower                in an extraction unit at 35-45 degrees C in a                pressure of 20-30 MPa for 0.5-1.5 hours, where the                flow rate of carbon dioxide is 25-45 kg/hour,                concentrating the extracting solution, (ii)                dissolving starch in honeysuckle clear paste,                uniformly mixing, heating, melting, and adding                0.03-0.1% sodium chloride, uniformly mixing, and                rapidly cooling to -20 to -60 degrees C by an                ultralow-temperature refrigerator, fully curing and                drying, cooling to -50 to -150 degrees C through an                ultralow-temperature freezing crusher, and crushing                into 80 mesh powder to prepare a honeysuckle                extract, (or) dissolving starch in honeysuckle                clear paste, uniformly stirring, and adding                0.05-0.3% vitamin C, uniformly mixing at 35-45                degrees C through an ultralow-temperature vacuum                belt drier, setting the temperature of the second                zone at 30-40 degrees C, setting the temperature of                the third zone at 20-30 degrees C, setting the                condensation temperature at 20-25 degrees C, and                crushing the sieve mesh by 80 mesh to obtain the                honeysuckle extract. The method containing raw                material comprises 100-200 pts. wt. honeysuckle and                15-30 pts. wt. starch.

TI Preparing a bend-resistant foam ceramic involves mixing and stirring sawdust, mixed enzyme solution and water at a constant temperature, inactivating the enzyme, and then filtering and washing to obtain primarily treated sawdust.
AU GAO Y
AE GAO Y (GAOY-Individual)
GA 201969838N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a bend-resistant foam ceramic involves mixing and stirring 20-30 pts. wt. sawdust, 2-3 pts. wt. mixed enzyme solution and 30-50 pts. wt. water at a constant temperature, inactivating the enzyme, and then filtering and washing to obtain primarily treated sawdust. 20-30 pts. wt. Primarily treated sawdust, 2-3 pts. wt. catalyst, 3-5 pts. wt. modified additive, and 40-60 pts. wt. water are stirred and mixed, followed by introducing carbon dioxide, stirring and mixing, and then filtering, washing and drying to a water content of 5-8% to obtain secondary treated sawdust. The secondary treated sawdust is mixed with tetraethyl orthosilicate in a mass ratio of 1:10-1:20, filtered, and then treated at a high temperature to obtain modified sawdust.
   USE - Method for preparing a bend-resistant foam ceramic.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a bend-resistant foam ceramic, which has the characteristics of improving the porosity and improving the mechanical properties, and has broad prospects in the development of the building materials technology industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a bend-resistant foam ceramic involves mixing and stirring 20-30 pts. wt. sawdust, 2-3 pts. wt. mixed enzyme solution and 30-50 pts. wt. water at a constant temperature, inactivating the enzyme, and then filtering and washing to obtain primarily treated sawdust. 20-30 pts. wt. Primarily treated sawdust, 2-3 pts. wt. catalyst, 3-5 pts. wt. modified additive, and 40-60 pts. wt. water are stirred and mixed, followed by introducing carbon dioxide, stirring and mixing, and then filtering, washing and drying to a water content of 5-8% to obtain secondary treated sawdust. The secondary treated sawdust is mixed with tetraethyl orthosilicate in a mass ratio of 1:10-1:20, filtered, and then treated at a high temperature to obtain modified sawdust. 20-30 pts. wt. Silicon carbide powder, 20-30 pts. wt. modified sawdust, 2-3 pts. wt. calcium stearate, 5-8 pts. wt. petroleum coke, 10-20 pts. wt. graphene oxide, 5-8 pts. wt. modified additive, 5-8 pts. wt. tar, 10-20 pts. wt. phenolic resin, 10-20 pts. wt. solvent, and 2-3 pts. wt. isocyanate and stirred and mixed, and then subjected to injection molding, press molding, sintering and cooling to obtain bend-resistant foam ceramic.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sawdust is any one of poplar sawdust, peach sawdust, pine sawdust or birch sawdust. The tar is any one of low temperature coal tar or medium temperature coal tar. The phenolic resin is any one of a 3201 phenol resin or a 2123 phenol resin. The solvent is any one of acetone, xylene or ethanol. The isocyanate is any one of tolylene diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate or dicyclohexylmethane diisocyanate. Preferred Composition: The mixed enzyme solution comprises 1-2 pts. wt. cellulase, 1-2 pts. wt. ligninase, and 10-20 pts. wt. water. The catalyst is prepared by mixing sodium fluoride and nano iron powder in a mass ratio of 3:1-3:2. Preferred Process: The preparation process of the modified additive involves mixing (N-fluorenyl)dodecyl acrylamide with methoxypolyethylene glycol in a mass ratio of 1:1-2:1, adding para-dichlorobenzene at an amount of 0.1-0.2 times of methoxypolyethylene glycol and ferrocene at an amount of 0.07-0.10 times of methoxypolyethylene glycol, and then stir mixing. The preparation process of the modified additive involves mixing 10-20 pts. wt. sepiolite, 20-30 pts. wt. absolute ethanol and 5-8 pts. wt. low melting point alloy by ball milling, and then drying to obtain modified additive.
