TI Preparation of low water absorption crosslinked polyimide aerogel used in e.g. aviation application, involves dissolving aromatic dianhydride and aromatic diamine in strong polar solvent, adding adamantane cross-linking agent and reacting.
AU NIU Y
   MA Y
   ZHANG X
   PEI Y
   LI M
AE UNIV SHENYANG CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (UYHG-C)
GA 201931028K
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of low water absorption crosslinked polyimide aerogel involves gradually dissolving aromatic dianhydride and aromatic diamine in a molar ratio of (1.00-1.50):1 in a strong polar solvent, reacting at room temperature for 5-8 hours in a nitrogen atmosphere to obtain polyamic acid solution, adding adamantane cross-linking agent to polyamic acid solution, stirring slowly, leaving still the reaction at room temperature, adding dehydrating agent to carry out chemical imide process to obtain crosslinked polyimide wet gel, drying the polyimide wet gel by supercritical carbon dioxide drying, freeze drying, atmospheric drying and degassing drying. The aromatic dianhydride and aromatic diamine monomer are biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride, 3,3',4,4'-diphenyl ether tetracarboxylic dianhydride or 3,3',4,4'-benzophenonetetracarboxylic dianhydride, 2,2'-dimethyl-4,4'-diaminobiphenyl, 4,4'-diaminoanisole or 4,4'-diphenylmethanediamine.
   USE - Preparation of low water absorption crosslinked polyimide aerogel used in aviation, marine, electronic equipment and application with high requirements for water vapor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of low water absorption crosslinked polyimide aerogel with low water absorption rate of 1-20%, low dielectric constant of 2.4-2.8 and high thermal stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation of low water absorption crosslinked polyimide aerogel involves gradually dissolving aromatic dianhydride and aromatic diamine in a molar ratio of (1.00-1.50):1 in a strong polar solvent, reacting at room temperature for 5-8 hours in a nitrogen atmosphere to obtain polyamic acid solution, adding adamantane cross-linking agent to polyamic acid solution, stirring slowly, leaving still the reaction at room temperature, adding dehydrating agent to carry out chemical imide process to obtain crosslinked polyimide wet gel, drying the polyimide wet gel by supercritical carbon dioxide drying, freeze drying, atmospheric drying and degassing drying. The aromatic dianhydride and aromatic diamine monomer are biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride, 3,3',4,4'-diphenyl ether tetracarboxylic dianhydride or 3,3',4,4'-benzophenonetetracarboxylic dianhydride, 2,2'-dimethyl-4,4'-diaminobiphenyl, 4,4'-diaminoanisole or 4,4'-diphenylmethanediamine. The strong polar solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetyl or N-methylpyrrolidone. The dehydrating agent is acetic anhydride combined with tertiary amine such as pyridine, isoquinoline or triethylamine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adamantane crosslinking agent is 1,3,5,7-tetrakis(4-aminophenyl)adamantane, 1,3,5,7-tetrakis(3-aminophenyl)adamantane, and/or 1,3,5,7-tetrakis(2-aminophenyl)adamantane. The addition amount of crosslinking agent is 0.5-5% based on the molar amount of the amino group. Preferred Conditions: The room temperature leaving still reaction time is 36-72 hours.

PT P
PN CN109232339-A; CN109232339-B
TI Cleaning cogeneration of DL-methionine, DL-methionine hydroxy analogue and calcium salt thereof comprises e.g. combining calcium carbonate and DL-methionine hydroxy analogue to obtain DL-methionine hydroxy analogue calcium salt.
AU ZHOU R
   LIAO C
   PENG Q
AE LUFENG TIANBAO PHOSPHORUS CHEM CO LTD (LUFE-Non-standard)
   TIANBAO ANIMAL NUTRITION TECHNOLOGY CO (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201912652G
AB    NOVELTY - Cleaning cogeneration of DL-methionine, DL-methionine hydroxy analogue and calcium salt thereof comprises e.g. (i) taking 3-(methylthio)propionaldehyde and hydrogen cyanide, and synthesizing to obtain 2-hydroxy-4-(methylthio)butyronitrile i.e. cyanohydrin; (ii) subjecting cyanohydrin to hydration and hydrolysis, neutralizing, concentrating and separating to obtain DL-methionine hydroxy analogue and by-product ammonium salt; (iii) reacting cyanohydrin to obtain intermediate 5-( beta -(methylthio)ethyl)hydantoin, reacting, concentrating the filtrate, separating again to obtain solid DL-methionine and crystallized mother solution containing hydrolyzing auxiliary agent; and (iv) combining calcium carbonate and DL-methionine hydroxy analogue to obtain DL-methionine hydroxy analogue calcium salt, returning the crystallized mother solution containing hydrolyzing auxiliary agent to the solid DL-methionine hydrolyzing step for recycling as a hydrolyzing auxiliary agent.
   USE - The method is useful for cleaning cogeneration of DL-methionine, DL-methionine hydroxy analogue and calcium salt thereof.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cleaning cogeneration of DL-methionine, DL-methionine hydroxy analogue and calcium salt thereof comprises (i) taking 3-(methylthio)propionaldehyde and hydrogen cyanide, and synthesizing under the action of catalyst to obtain 2-hydroxy-4-(methylthio)butyronitrile i.e. cyanohydrin; (ii) sequentially subjecting cyanohydrin to hydration and hydrolysis under the action of acid, neutralizing the obtained reaction solution with ammonia water, concentrating and separating to obtain DL-methionine hydroxy analogue and by-product ammonium salt, where the DL-methionine hydroxy analogue is referred as liquid-met; (iii) reacting cyanohydrin with carbon dioxide, ammonia or ammonium bicarbonate to obtain intermediate 5-( beta -(methylthio)ethyl)hydantoin, reacting 5-( beta -(methylthio)ethyl)hydantoin with calcium hydroxide in the presence of hydrolyzing auxiliary agent to obtain calcium carbonate precipitate and DL-methionine salt solution, separating and removing calcium carbonate in the obtained hydrolyzate, concentrating the filtrate and passing into carbon dioxide crystal, separating again to obtain solid DL-methionine and crystallized mother solution containing hydrolyzing auxiliary agent, where the 5-( beta -(methylthio)ethyl)hydantoin is referred as hydantoin and solid DL-methionine is referred as solid-met; and (iv) combining calcium carbonate obtained in step (iii) and DL-methionine hydroxy analogue to obtain DL-methionine hydroxy analogue calcium salt, returning the crystallized mother solution containing hydrolyzing auxiliary agent to the solid DL-methionine hydrolyzing step for recycling as a hydrolyzing auxiliary agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the catalyst is used as a buffer solution comprising an aqueous solution of phosphoric acid and sodium phosphate or phosphoric acid and sodium hydroxide, succinic acid and sodium succinate or succinic acid and sodium hydroxide, acetic acid and sodium acetate or acetic acid and sodium hydroxide, and/or citric acid and sodium citrate or citric acid and sodium hydroxide. In the step (ii), the acid used for hydration and hydrolysis reaction is sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid or phosphoric acid. The hydrolyzing auxiliary agent comprises sodium hydroxide, sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate and/or potassium hydroxide. The molar ratio of the metal cation of hydrolyzing auxiliary agent and (hydantoin and solid-met) is 0.95-1.2:1. The step (iii) comprises reacting hydantoin with calcium hydroxide in the presence of a hydrolyzing auxiliary agent, where the mass ratio of calcium hydroxide and hydantoin is 0.8-1.2:1. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the separation operation of calcium carbonate and hydrolyzate is carried out by suction filtration, pressure filtration or centrifugation, where the solid DL-methionine crystals are separated from the mother liquor by suction filtration, pressure filtration or centrifugation. In the step (iv), the crystallized mother liquid containing the hydrolyzing auxiliary agent can be directly recycled to the hydrolysis step after decarburization heat treatment.

PN CN109007714-A
TI Mint flavor jelly comprises instant coffee powder,            mint, jujube, rhizoma polygonati, ginseng, goji,            glucose, carrageenan, locust bean gum and stevia.
AU XIAN H
   FENG W
   LUO Y
   LA Z
   CAO Y
   ZHENG L
AE GUANGDONG EFONG PHARM CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A45524
AB    NOVELTY - Mint flavor jelly comprises 100-150 pts. wt.                instant coffee powder, 30-60 pts. wt. mint, 20-40                pts. wt. jujube, 10-30 pts. wt. rhizoma polygonati,                10-30 pts. wt. ginseng, 10-30 pts. wt. goji, 80-150                pts. wt. glucose, 4-8 pts. wt. carrageenan, 3-6                pts. wt. locust bean gum and 0.2-0.6 pts. wt.                stevia.
   USE - Used as mint flavor jelly.
   ADVANTAGE - The mint flavor jelly combines mellow and mint                scent of coffee, stimulates taste buds and feeling                of comfortable feel, nourishes qi and blood, has                unique taste, flavor, rich aroma, anti-oxidation,                anti-fatigue effect and is refreshing and healthy                ready-to-eat food.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing mint flavor jelly comprising, (i) taking                mint, washing, drying at less than or equal to 50                degrees C, crushing into 40-60 mesh powder,                extracting with supercritical carbon dioxide to                obtain peppermint oil, storing peppermint oil with                cleaner, setting aside, washing jujube, drying,                cutting into 3-5 mm thick slices, steaming for                30-60 minutes with large heat to until jujube color                turns into reddish brown, washing rhizoma                polygonati, moistening, cutting into 2-4 mm thick                slices, washing ginseng, moistening, cutting into                1-2 mm flakes, mixing carrageenan, locust bean gum,                glucose and stevia in uniform mixture to obtain                gel-sugar mixture; (ii) taking mint and steamed                jujube after supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction, adding water to decoct once, adding 2-6                times amount of water, decocting for 20-40 minutes,                adding instant coffee powder and stirring until                fully dissolved, boiling continuously for 10- 30                minutes, filtering and collecting filtered                solution; (iii) Taking extracted mint and jujube                medicinal residue, adding rhizoma polygonati, goji,                ginseng, adding water to decoct twice, adding 6-10                times amount of water for first time, decocting for                1-3 hours, adding 4-8 times amount of water for                second time, decocting for 1-2 hours, filtering,                combining filtrate and concentrating at 70 degrees                C under reduced pressure to concentrated solution                with relative density of 1.06-1.08; (iv) taking                gel-sugar mixture, adding 20-40 times amount of                water, stirring to dissolve until dispersion is                uniform, to obtain the solution; (v) combining                filtered solution and concentrated solution to                obtain mixed solution; (vi) dissolving peppermint                oil obtained in ethanol to obtain ethanol mixed                solution; and (vii) adding gel-sugar mixture,                ethanol mixed solution, stirring quickly, mixing                uniformly, packing and sterilizing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The mint flavor jelly                  comprises 120-130 pts. wt. instant coffee powder,                  40-50 pts. wt. mint, 25-35pts. wt. jujube, 15-25                  pts. wt. rhizoma polygonati, 15-25 pts. wt.                  ginseng, 15-25 pts. wt. goji, 100-120 pts. wt.                  glucose, 5-7 pts. wt. carrageenan, 3-4 pts. wt.                  locust bean gum and 0.3-0.5 pts. wt. stevia.                  Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  carrageenan and locust bean gum is 1:1-2:1,                  preferred 2:1. Preferred Method: In the step (i),                  extracting supercritical carbon dioxide at                  extraction tank pressure of 20-25 MPa, separation                  I pressure of 6-10 MPa, separation II pressure of                  4-8 MPa, extraction temperature of 30-40 degrees                  C, separation I temperature of 40-55 degrees C,                  separation II temperature of 25-35 degrees C,                  carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-30 l/hour and                  extraction time of 1.5-3 hours and steaming                  jujube with boiling water which contains                  naturally refluxed jujube aromatic water.

TI Method for preparing polymer film coating            trivalent cation phosphate, involves mixing acetone and            dimethylformamide to prepare mixed solution and adding            coating agent of mixed solution, followed by ultrasonic            shaking and air-drying.
AU TONG Q
   YU X
   CHEN F
   MA S
   LIAO J
   LI Y
   XI Q
   ZU G
   WANG T
AE UNIV FUJIAN (UYFJ-C)
GA 201898148H
AB    NOVELTY - A polymer film coating trivalent cation                phosphate preparing method involves mixing acetone                and dimethylformamide to prepare a mixed solution,                adding coating agent of the mixed solution,                ultrasonic shaking to obtain a uniformly mixed                suspension, adding polyvinylidene fluoride                hexafluoropropylene of the mixed solution to the                suspension, adding polymethyl methacrylate of the                mixed solution, ultrasonic vibrating, stirring to                convert the solution in the reaction kettle into a                viscous liquid, flatting the base film, treating                the surface of the base film with a low temperature                plasma for 1 second to 5 minutes or treating the                surfaces of the base film for 1 second to 5 minutes                under an atmosphere of oxygen, carbon dioxide or                formaldehyde, coating a viscous liquid on the                surface of the treated base film, and vacuum-drying                or air-drying at any temperature in the temperature                range of 50-110 degrees C to obtain the finished                product.
   USE - Method for preparing polymer film coating                trivalent cation phosphate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing polymer film                coating trivalent cation phosphate with improved                coating membrane, anode, cathode, and electrolyte                and improved cycle performance of the                battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A polymer film coating trivalent cation                phosphate preparing method involves mixing acetone                and dimethylformamide in a ratio of volume ratio                (0.1-15):1 to prepare a mixed solution, adding                0.5-2.5 wt.% coating agent of the mixed solution,                ultrasonic shaking for 1-30 minutes to obtain a                uniformly mixed suspension, adding 1-5 wt.%                polyvinylidene fluoride hexafluoropropylene of the                mixed solution to the suspension, adding 0.5-2.5                wt.% polymethyl methacrylate of the mixed solution,                ultrasonic vibrating for 10-50 minutes, stirring at                50-90 degrees C for 8-12 hours to convert the                solution in the reaction kettle into a viscous                liquid, flatting the base film, treating the                surface of the base film with a low temperature                plasma for 1 second to 5 minutes or treating the                surfaces of the base film for 1 second to 5 minutes                under an atmosphere of oxygen, carbon dioxide or                formaldehyde, coating a viscous liquid on the                surface of the treated base film, and vacuum-drying                or air-drying at any temperature in the temperature                range of 50-110 degrees C to obtain the finished                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The phosphate                  trivalent cation is aluminum phosphate, strontium                  phosphate, iron phosphate, gallium phosphate and                  strontium phosphate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The base film is a                  polyethylene single layer film or a multilayer                  film containing a polypropylene layer.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of biodiesel includes mixing bagasse and corncob, crushing, mixing sieved granules and solvent A, standing, filtering, drying filter residue, mixing dried filter residue, sterile water and compound enzyme solution, centrifuging, adjusting pH, mixing peanuts and impregnation liquid, soaking, filtering, drying, spraying, germinating, mixing germinated peanuts and solvent B, chopping, crushing, homogenizing, washing, cutting, inoculating activation medium, incubating, inoculating hyphae in seed culture medium, culturing to obtain seed culture solution, mixing seed culture solution, casein, germinated peanut homogenate, spare sugar cane treatment solution, ammonium sulfate, potato, vitamin B6 and water, shaking, fermenting, culturing to obtain fermented product, centrifuging ferment, collecting centrifuge, drying to obtain base material, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain bio-fat, mixing bio-oil, methanol and sodium hydroxide, standing and drying.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of biodiesel comprises mixing bagasse and corncob at 50-70 degrees C, crushing, mixing sieved granules and solvent A at 1:10-15, standing, filtering, drying filter residue at 32-40 degrees C, mixing dried filter residue, sterile water and compound enzyme solution at 1:8-12:0.01, centrifuging, adjusting pH to obtain bagasse treatment liquid, mixing peanuts and impregnation liquid at 1:10-15, soaking, filtering, drying filter residue, spraying filter residue, germinating peanuts with nutrient solution, mixing germinated peanuts and solvent B, chopping, crushing, homogenizing peanuts, washing surface with ethanol solution, cutting wire, inoculating activation medium, incubating at 25-28 degrees C for 3-5 days to obtain activator, inoculating hyphae in seed culture medium, culturing at 25-30 degrees C for 5-7 days to obtain seed culture solution, mixing 20-40 pts. wt. seed culture solution, 4-8 pts. wt. casein, 10-15 pts. wt. germinated peanut homogenate, 15-25 pts. wt. spare sugar cane treatment solution, 0.3-0.8 pt. wt. ammonium sulfate, 10-15 pt. wt. potato, 0.1-0.4 pt. wt. vitamin B6 and 50-70 pt. wt. water, shaking, fermenting, culturing to obtain fermented product, centrifuging ferment, collecting centrifuge, drying to obtain base material, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain bio-fat, mixing bio-oil, methanol and sodium hydroxide at 3:15-20:0.1, standing to obtain reactants and drying reactants.

PT P
PN CN108823253-A
TI Producing butanol by fermenting candied plant wastewater using Clostridium acetobutylicum involves using P2-PGY medium, and weighing glucose is add to P2-PGY medium to prepare culture solution, and culture solution is extinguished.
AU YE Z
   SONG J
   ZHUO S
   SONG X
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 201891884P
AB    NOVELTY - Producing butanol by fermenting candied plant wastewater using Clostridium acetobutylicum involves using P2+PGY medium, and weighing 5 pts. wt. glucose is add to 100 pts. wt. P2+PGY medium to prepare a culture solution. The culture solution is extinguished by high pressure steam, sterilizing by ultraviolet (UV) lamp, and aspirating the culture solution with a sterile pipette to dissolve the freeze-dried Clostridium acetobutylicum to get Clostridium acetobutylicum seed solution. The sterile syringe to absorb the bacterial liquid, add it to the fermentation flask containing the sterile culture solution, pass nitrogen for 3 minutes, connect the exhaust device, discharge the hydrogen and carbon dioxide generated during the fermentation process, and maintain the anaerobic process during the culture.
   USE - Method for producing butanol by fermenting candied plant wastewater using Clostridium acetobutylicum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce butanol by fermenting candied plant wastewater using Clostridium acetobutylicum, which has reduced aeration pollution to the environment, effectively solves preserved plant wastewater due to low pH value and high salinity, high molecular mass organic pollutant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The state is placed in a constant temperature air bath shock box, which is oscillated and anaerobic at 37 degrees C for 2-3 days of fermentation, it is observed that there is floating matter in the upper portion of the fermentation liquid, and there is a cloud band in the lower portion, indicating good growth as a seed liquid. The weight 10 pts. wt. candied plant wastewater is uniformly mixed with 90 pts. wt. P2+PGY medium prepared, and pH is adjusted to 5.5 with sodium hydroxide (NaOH) to obtain preserves. The plant wastewater culture solution is sterilized by high pressure steam sterilization pot and ultraviolet lamp irradiation. The seed liquid obtained is aspirated by a sterile syringe, and the 10 wt.% inoculum amount, and added to a fermentation flask containing the candied plant wastewater culture liquid, and nitrogen gas is introduced for 3 minutes. The exhaust device is connected to discharge the fermentation process, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and maintain the anaerobic state during the culture process, placed in a constant temperature air bath shock box, convoluted, anaerobic fermentation at 37 degrees C, fermentation 2-3 days, to obtain product butanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The P2+PGY medium comprises 0.5 pts. wt. potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 0.5 pts. wt. potassium hydrogen phosphate, 2.2 pts. wt. ammonium acetate, 0.01 pts. wt. manganese(II) sulfate, 0.2 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 0.01 pts. wt. ferrous sulfate heptahydrate, 0.01 pts. wt. sodium chloride, 0.001 pts. wt. p-aminobenzoic acid, 0.001 pts. wt. vitamin B1, 0.001 pts. wt. biotin, 0.001 pts. wt. yeast extract, 3 pts. wt. peptone, 21.325 pts. wt. 2-(N-morpholine)ethanesulfonic acid monohydrate and 1000 pts. wt. deionized water. Preferred Conditions: The autoclave sterilization is sterilized at 121 degrees C for 15 minutes, and the ultraviolet lamp irradiation sterilization time is 30 minutes. The freeze-drying the acetone butanol Clostridium powder is obtained by wiping the surface of the freeze-dried tube of the Clostridium acetobutylicum strain with alcohol cotton for disinfection, and uniformly heating the top of the tube on the outer flame of the alcohol lamp. The sterile water droplets are drain at the nozzle, break it and break the nozzle with tweezers. The rotational speed of the gyroscopic oscillation is 2 revolution per minute.

PN CN108586770-A; CN108586770-B
TI Cleansing and separating lignin and hemicellulose from plant biomass comprises e.g. dipping pulverized plant powder into microwave digestion solution, adding into microwave digestion tank, and adding into microwave digestion device.
AU HAN G
   JIANG W
   SONG Y
   ZHANG Y
   ZHOU C
AE UNIV QINGDAO (UNQD-C)
GA 201879533R
AB    NOVELTY - Cleansing and separating lignin and hemicellulose from plant biomass comprises e.g. (1) dipping pulverized plant powder into microwave digestion solution, adding into microwave digestion tank, adding into microwave digestion device, setting the microwave digestion program, heating, slowly cooling, taking out powder, filtering powder to obtain filtrate, collecting the filtrate and solution together in reaction kettle, and recording it as first solution; and (2) treating above powder by high pressure carbon dioxide to obtain intermediate powder and second solution; adding the intermediate powder into bleaching tank, opening the inlet valve, injecting the bleaching solution to bath; heating the bleaching solution in bleaching tank; discharging the liquid in the degumming groove of the bleaching tank, and starting water inlet valve, spraying washing to obtain pure plant cellulose; where the bleaching solution comprises sodium percarbonate, hydrogen peroxide and water.
   USE - The method is useful for cleansing and separating lignin and hemicellulose from plant biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cleansing and separating lignin and hemicellulose from plant biomass comprises (1) dipping pulverized plant powder into microwave digestion solution, adding into microwave digestion tank, adding into microwave digestion device, setting the microwave digestion program, heating to 120 degrees C for 15 minutes, heating for 5-20 minutes, slowly cooling, taking out powder at 70 degrees C, filtering powder by using 10 times methanol to obtain filtrate, collecting the filtrate and solution together in reaction kettle, and recording it as first solution; where the mass ratio of the above plant powder and the components of microwave digestion solution is plant powder, methanol, glycerol, and concentrated sulfuric acid is 2:3:6:0.14; and (2) treating above powder by high pressure carbon dioxide to obtain intermediate powder and second solution; adding the intermediate powder into bleaching tank, opening the inlet valve, injecting the bleaching solution to bath in the ratio of 1:10; heating the bleaching solution in bleaching tank to 70-90 degrees C; discharging the liquid in the degumming groove of the bleaching tank, and starting water inlet valve, spraying washing to obtain pure plant cellulose; where the bleaching solution comprises 4-6 wt.% sodium percarbonate, 2-3 wt.% hydrogen peroxide and water (remaining amount).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, the above high pressure carbon dioxide treatment comprises soaking microwave-digested powder in water, where the solid-liquid ratio is 1:10, placing in high pressure reactor, passing through carbon dioxide to pressure of 5 MPa, heating the reaction kettle to 180-220 degrees C for 10-30 minutes, slowly cooling to below 70 degrees C, taking out the powder, filtering, collecting the filtrate and aqueous solution together in reaction kettle, and recording it as second solution. In the method, purifying the above first solution by distillation to obtain lignin. In the method, purifying the above second solution by distillation to obtain oligomerized polysaccharide. In the method, before the step (1), crushing phloem, stem and leaves of plant material, passing through 60 mesh sieves to obtain pulverized plant powder.

PN CN108455588-A
TI High wearable ceramic material is prepared by dispersing graphite oxide solution, adding ferric chloride solution, stirring, adding sulfuric acid, heating, reacting, setting into freezing centrifuge and removing supernatant liquid.
AU PAN W
   HUANG L
AE FOSHAN YINNUOWEI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201869170M
AB    NOVELTY - A high wearable ceramic material is prepared by ultrasonically dispersing graphite oxide solution, adding ferric chloride solution, magnetically stirring on magnetic stirrer, adding sulfuric acid, heating, continuously reacting, setting into high speed freezing centrifuge, removing supernatant liquid, washing precipitate using deionized water, oven drying to obtain catalyst composite powder, placing under argon atmosphere, adjusting temperature, introducing hydrogen, introducing carbon dioxide gas to react, vapor depositing graphene surface with carbon nanotubes, cooling in argon atmosphere, taking out to obtain second composite powder, calcining with far infrared ceramic powder in muffle furnace, and milling. The volume ratio of graphene oxide dispersion to ferric chloride solution is 80-85:1.
   USE - High wearable ceramic material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has excellent performance, protection layer on surface and good abrasion resistance and film-forming property, reduces abrasion, and prolongs glossiness and color.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A high wearable ceramic material is prepared by ultrasonically dispersing 8-10 g/L graphite oxide solution at 28 plus minus 2 degrees C for 5-6.5 hours to prepare graphene oxide dispersion, adding 10 mg/L ferric chloride solution to obtain mixed solution, magnetically stirring on magnetic stirrer at 50 plus minus 5 degrees C for 40 minutes, adding 0.05 M sulfuric acid at speed of 0.5 ml/second, heating to 70 degrees C, continuously reacting for 12 hours, setting into high speed freezing centrifuge at speed of 1000 revolutions/minute (rpm), removing supernatant liquid, washing precipitate using deionized water 3 times, oven drying at 150 degrees C for 8 hours to obtain catalyst composite powder, placing under argon atmosphere, adjusting temperature to 825 degrees C, introducing hydrogen for 60-70 minutes, introducing carbon dioxide gas to react for 30 minutes, vapor depositing graphene surface with carbon nanotubes, cooling after 1 hour in argon atmosphere to 27 plus minus 2 degrees C, taking out to obtain second composite powder, calcining with far infrared ceramic powder in muffle furnace at 50-100 degrees C for 2 hours, 100-500 degrees C for 4 hours and 500-1200 degrees C for 2 hours, and milling. The volume ratio of graphene oxide dispersion to ferric chloride solution is 80-85:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The grain diameter of far infrared ceramic powder is 10-35 mu m. Preferred Method: Calcining is done with second composite powder, far infrared ceramic powder and mica powder at mass ratio of 5:32 for 8 hours.

PN CN108455602-A
TI Preparing carbon material from oak waste wood chips comprises e.g. crushing oak wood chips, drying, soaking, washing, filtering, drying, adding cellulose acetate, heating, carbonizing, cooling, soaking, filtering, washing and drying.
AU YANG J
AE GUIGANG WANSHENG WOOD IND CO LTD (GUIG-Non-standard)
GA 2018691708
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon material from oak waste wood chips comprises e.g. crushing oak wood chips, adding into sawdust dryer, drying, soaking in soaking agent, washing, filtering, drying to obtain pre-treated wood, adding cellulose acetate into pre-processed wood chips, heating in the transverse program tubular furnace, filling mixed atmosphere and raising temperature to 250-280 degrees C, maintaining the temperature, atmosphere change, carbonizing for 1-2 hours to obtain pre-processed carbon, placing the pre-processed carbon in 95-98 degrees C molten coal tar pitch, soaking, taking out and adding into carbonization furnace, inletting nitrogen atmosphere, carbon dioxide atmosphere, carbonizing at temperature of 280-290 degrees C for 2-3 hours, cooling, taking out, adding into sodium hydroxide solution and soaking, filtering, washing with clean water, and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon material from oak waste wood chips.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon material is safe; has high porosity, good absorption and no harmful substance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon material from oak waste wood chips comprises (1) adding the oak wood chips into crusher for crushing, adding into sawdust dryer, drying under 105-110 degrees C condition for 10-12 hours, soaking in soaking agent for 12-15 hours, washing using de-ionized water until it is neutral, filtering, drying at 105-110 degrees C for 15-18 hours to obtain pre-treated wood, where the soaking agent is mixed solution of 1.5-2 wt.% phosphoric acid solution and 3.2-4.5 wt.% potassium carbonate solution in equal volume, (2) adding cellulose acetate into pre-processed wood chips, heating in the transverse program tubular furnace, filling mixed atmosphere and raising temperature to 250-280 degrees C, maintaining the temperature, atmosphere change, carbonizing for 1-2 hours to obtain pre-processed carbon, and (3) placing the pre-processed carbon in 95-98 degrees C molten coal tar pitch, soaking at constant temperature for 3-5 hours, taking out and adding into carbonization furnace, inletting nitrogen atmosphere, carbon dioxide atmosphere, carbonizing at temperature of 280-290 degrees C for 2-3 hours, cooling, taking out, adding into sodium hydroxide solution and soaking for 0.5-1 hour, filtering, washing with clean water, and drying to obtain finished product carbon material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dried oak wood chips grain diameter in the step (1) after crushing is less than 1 mm. The use amount of the cellulose acetate in the step (2) is 2.3-2.8 wt.% the pre-dust. The mixed gas in the step (2) includes 200-220 ml/minute, preferably 210 ml/minute volume flow nitrogen atmosphere, 30-40 ml/minute, preferably 35 ml/minute volume flow carbon dioxide atmosphere, and 120-150 ml/minute, preferably 135 ml/minute flow rate ammonia gas atmosphere. The mass concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution in the step (3) is 0.3-0.5 wt.%. Preferred Method: the volume flow of nitrogen atmosphere and carbon dioxide atmosphere in the step (3) is 100-150 ml/minute and 10-20 ml/minute.

PN CN108410614-A
TI Brewing brandy useful for e.g. beautifying, lowering cholesterol, and inhibiting cancer comprises e.g. providing fresh and ripe grapes, washing, draining, steaming, beating into fruit pulp, lowering temperature, mixing koji and stirring.
AU YAO W
AE YAO W (YAOW-Individual)
GA 201866545C
AB    NOVELTY - Brewing brandy using grapes comprises e.g. providing fresh and ripe grapes, washing, draining, steaming for 25-30 minutes, sterilizing, beating to obtain fruit pulp, lowering pulp temperature to 30 degrees C, mixing koji, stirring, sealing port, maintaining temperature at 25-30 degrees C, saccharifying for 2 days to obtain wine material, adjusting concentration of the sugar in wine material to 15-25%, adjusting pH to 4-6, adding wine making starter and rehydration high temperature resistant active dry yeast, stirring, sealing tank port, releasing carbon dioxide at regular intervals, controlling temperature at 25-30 degrees C, stirring for 2 times every day, fermenting for 8-12 days until residual sugar content is 0.3%, pouring obtained fermented wine into distillation kettle, distilling directly to obtain low-grade brandy, pouring low-grade brandy in distillation kettle, performing slow distillation, maintaining boiling point to 65 degrees C for 20 minutes, and removing methanol.
   USE - The brandy is useful for beautifying, lowering cholesterol, inhibiting cancer cells and softening blood vessels.
   ADVANTAGE - The brandy: is clear, transparent, delicious, sweet, and rich in vitamins, amino acids, dietary fiber; and has grape and oak aroma, good health care effect, and long lasting taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Brewing brandy using grapes comprises (i) providing fresh and ripe grapes, washing, draining, steaming for 25-30 minutes, sterilizing, and beating to obtain fruit pulp, (ii) lowering pulp temperature to 30 degrees C, mixing koji, stirring, sealing port, maintaining temperature at 25-30 degrees C, and saccharifying for 2 days to obtain wine material, (iii) adjusting concentration of the sugar in wine material to 15-25%, adjusting pH to 4-6, adding wine making starter and rehydration high temperature resistant active dry yeast, stirring, sealing tank port, releasing carbon dioxide at regular intervals, controlling temperature at 25-30 degrees C, stirring for 2 times every day, and fermenting for 8-12 days until residual sugar content is 0.3%, (iv) pouring obtained fermented wine into distillation kettle, distilling directly to obtain low-grade brandy, pouring low-grade brandy in distillation kettle, performing slow distillation, maintaining boiling point to 65 degrees C for 20 minutes, removing methanol, raising boiling point to obtain 28-66% brandy, and (v) placing brandy into the oak barrel, storing, freezing, filtering, and bottling to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the amount of koji is 0.3-0.9% weight of pulp.
   ACTIVITY - Antilipemic; Cytostatic; Vasotropic.

PN CN108004213-A; CN108004213-B
TI Amplifying cytokine-induced killer cell comprises adding activator into culture flask, adding cytokine-induced killer cell activator into plasma-containing basal medium, adding expansion medium and incubating.
AU DUAN H
AE BEIJING HUIZHI CHIKANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   BEIJING HUIZHI CHIKANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018379119
AB    NOVELTY - Amplifying cytokine-induced killer cell comprises (i) preparing activator by dissolving cluster of differentiation-3 monoclonal antibody and interferon gamma in phosphate-buffered saline solution, and adding activator into a culture flask to cover the bottom of the flask, incubating to obtain activated flask, (ii) preparing cytokine-induced killer cell activator by dissolving interleukin-2, interleukin-1 alpha and interferon gamma in phosphate-buffered saline solution, and adding cytokine-induced killer cell activator into 49 ml plasma-containing basal medium to obtain activation medium, (iii) re-suspending mononuclear cells in the activation medium, inoculating in activated flasks, and incubating to obtain cytokine-induced killer cells, and (iv) discarding the activation medium, adding expansion medium, and incubating, where the expansion medium is a basal medium containing interleukin-2, ascorbic acid, vitamin-E, human plasma albumin, and insulin.
   USE - The method is useful for amplifying cytokine-induced killer cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method shortens the culture period and amplifies rapidly. The kit is convenient and simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Amplifying cytokine-induced killer cell comprises (i) preparing activator by dissolving cluster of differentiation-3 monoclonal antibody and interferon gamma in phosphate-buffered saline solution, and adding activator into a culture flask to cover the bottom of the flask, incubating to obtain activated flask, (ii) preparing cytokine-induced killer cell activator by dissolving interleukin-2, interleukin-1 alpha and interferon gamma in phosphate-buffered saline solution, and adding cytokine-induced killer cell activator into 49 ml plasma-containing basal medium to obtain activation medium, (iii) re-suspending mononuclear cells in the activation medium, inoculating in activated flasks, and incubating at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 48-96 hours to obtain cytokine-induced killer cells, and (iv) discarding the activation medium, adding expansion medium, and incubating at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator to the required amount, where the expansion medium is a basal medium containing interleukin-2, ascorbic acid, vitamin-E, human plasma albumin, and insulin. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for kit, comprising activator, cytokine-induced killer cell activator and cytokine-induced killer cell proliferation agent, where the activator is prepared by dissolving cluster of differentiation-3 monoclonal antibody and interferon gamma in phosphate-buffered saline solution, and cytokine-induced killer cell proliferation agent contains interleukin-2, ascorbic acid, vitamin-E, human plasma albumin, and insulin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the concentration of activated cluster of differentiation-3 monoclonal antibody is 30-100 mu ml, preferably 50 mu ml, interferon gamma is 1000-3000 U/ml, preferably 2000 U/ml. In the step (ii), the concentration of interleukin-2 in cytokine-induced killer cell activator is 20000-100000 U/ml, preferably 50000 U/ml, interleukin-1 alpha is 20000-100000 U/ml, preferably 50000 U/ml, and the interferon gamma is 50000-150000 U/ml, preferably 100000 U/ml. In the step (iv), the concentration of interleukin-2 in the amplification medium is 500-1000 U/l, preferably 700 U/l, ascorbic acid is 100-400 mg/l, preferably 200 mg/l, vitamin-E is 300-800 mg/l, preferably 500 mg/l, human plasma albumin is 3-8 g/l, preferably 5 g/l, and insulin is 5-20 ng/l, preferably 10 ng/l. The basic culture medium is RPMI-1640 basic culture medium. In the step (iii), the plasma is inactivated autologous plasma, and the plasma content is 10% of the volume of the activation medium. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the incubation temperature is 37 degrees C, and the time is 1-18 hours. In the step (iv), the amplification medium is replaced every 1-3 days when cytokine-induced killer cells expands.

PT P
PN CN107970490-A; CN107970490-B
TI Degradable composite material for bone wound healing comprises polylactic acid injection molded component with mesh structure as substrate, filled with beta-tricalcium phosphate dispersed in drug microcapsule particles.
AU LUAN F
AE UNIV CHONGQING MEDICAL YONGCHUAN HOSPITA (UCQM-C)
   UNIV CHONGQING MEDICAL YONGCHUAN HOSPITA (UCQM-C)
GA 201836615H
AB    NOVELTY - A degradable composite material for bone wound healing comprises polylactic acid injection molded component with mesh structure as substrate, where mesh on substrate is filled with beta -tricalcium phosphate dispersed in drug microcapsule particles. Substrate surface is modified with type-II collagen protein coating, and substrate is formed from micropore foaming material using supercritical carbon dioxide as solvent by millipore foam molding technology.
   USE - A degradable composite material for bone wound healing (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material has high mechanical strength and good biocompatibility. As the polylactic acid plate is degraded, the fixing force weakens slowly and can prevent secondary bone trauma, and at the same time, the surface can undergo selective chemical reaction with the physiological environment. The material promotes adhesion and proliferation of cells, induces and promotes growth of new bone tissue on the surface, forms a strong chemical bond with the bone at the interface, and grows new bone cells into the mesh structure on the matrix of the injection molded component and is environment-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of degradable composite material which involves pouring degradable polymer material polylactic acid into a mold, performing millipore foam molding technology using supercritical carbon dioxide as solvent for injection molding to obtain polylactic acid injection molded component, using laser drilling technique to drill surface of polylactic acid injection molded component to obtain polylactic acid injection molded component having a mesh structure, dispersing anti-inflammatory drug microcapsule particles in beta -tricalcium phosphate, filling mesh of polylactic acid injection molded components via sol-gel method, drying to obtain skeleton unit, immersing skeleton unit in 30% hydrogen peroxide solution, irradiating light at 50 degrees C under 250 W UV lamp for 40 minutes, immersing and containing photooxidized skeleton unit under nitrogen protection, initiating graft polymerization of methacrylic acid on the surface of polylactic acid injection molded component in 0.0015 mol/L Fe2+ aqueous solution of methacrylic acid to obtain modified skeleton unit, washing with large amount of deionized water, immersing skeleton unit in buffer containing 10 mg/ml 1-ethyl-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)carbodiimide hydrochloride, reacting at 0-4 degrees C for 4 hours, washing skeleton units repeatedly with deionized water, immersing in 4 mg/ml type-II collagen in 0.3% aqueous acetic acid, reacting at 0-4 degrees C for 24 hours, removing skeleton units, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Processes: Polylactic acid injection molded component is immersed in 4 mg/ml 0.3% acetic acid aqueous solution of type-II collagen, reacting, removing skeleton units, immersing again in 0.3% acetic acid solution for 12 hours, immersing in deionized water for 12 hours, and drying. Preferred Component: Buffer is 1-ethyl-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)carbodiimide hydrochloride with pH of 6.8-7.3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The drug is aspirin, metamizole, acetaminophen, indomethacin, piroxicam, and/or ketoralac. Wall material of the drug microcapsule particles is carboxymethyl chitosan, silk protein, polymethyl methacrylate, and/or sodium alginate.
   ACTIVITY - Vulnerary; Osteopathic.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); ; A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. TI System for separating components of natural gas            product, comprises first separation column to receive            natural gas product and to provide first stage            components that comprise first component gas and second            component gas.
AU CARTELLIERI A
   BAECKER B
   AZIZI S
AE BAKER HUGHES OILFIELD OPERATIONS INC (BAKO-C)
   BAKER HUGHES OILFIELD OPERATIONS INC (BAKO-C)
GA 2022C9237S
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a first separation column to                receive a natural gas product and to provide first                stage components that comprise a first component                gas and a second component gas. A gas converter                receives the first stage components and provide                second stage components that comprise the first                component gas. A second separation column receives                the second stage components to provide third stage                components, where the third component gas is formed                by conversion of the second component gas. A                hydrogen source provides hydrogen gas to the gas                converter to support conversion of carbon dioxide                of the first stage components to the third                component gas.
   USE - System for separating components of natural                gas product.
   ADVANTAGE - The system enables a spectrometer or detector                to provide spectral peaks at different times based                in part on components separated in one or more of a                first separation column, a gas converter, and a                second separation column of a fast in-field                chromatography system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for separating components of a natural gas product,                which involves receiving the natural gas product,                receiving first stage components from the first                separation column, receiving second stage                components comprising the first component gas and a                third component gas, and providing third stage                components comprising the first component gas, the                third component gas, and additional carbon-based                components.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The second component                  gas is carbon dioxide, ammonium, or hydrogen                  sulfide. The system also comprises a hydrogen                  source for providing hydrogen gas to the gas                  converter to support conversion of the carbon                  dioxide of the first stage components to the                  third component gas. The third component gas is                  methane, and an oxygen source for providing                  oxygen gas to the gas converter to support                  conversion of ammonium of the first stage                  components to the third component gas. The third                  component gas is nitrogen dioxide, or an oxygen                  source for providing oxygen gas to the gas                  converter to support conversion of the hydrogen                  sulfide. The system also comprises a combustion                  chamber to chemically reduce the copper                  oxide-based catalyst to be regenerated                  periodically by reaction in presence of copper                  oxide-based catalyst.

AU XU W
   CHEN J
   TONG S
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 202268629M

The satellite remote sensing product data is obtained. A time series of terrestrial water storage within the representative area is reconstructed, and the groundwater variation is calculated. AU QIU S
   DING H
   SONG L
   HU Y
   WANG X
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 202265371U
AB    NOVELTY - Bio-based non-isocyanate polyurethane i.e.                2-hydroxybutyl formate compound of formula (I) is                new.
   USE - Used as bio-based non-isocyanate                polyurethane.
   ADVANTAGE - (I) has excellent biological degradability and                high strength, self-repairing and processing                performance; solves the problems of high toxicity                of existing polyurethane raw materials and                dependence on petrochemical resources, meets the                requirements of sustainable development.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Bio-based non-isocyanate polyurethane i.e.                2-hydroxybutyl formate compound of formula (I) is                new.
   R1 = ethane-1,2-diyl, propane-1,3-diyl,                (oxybis(ethane-2,1-diyl)) or butane-1,4-diyl;
   R2 = 3-methylbutan-2-one, octan-2-one,                1,1'-(tetrahydrofuran-2,5-diyl)bis(hexan-1-one), or                1,1'-(tetrahydrofuran-2,5-diyl)bis(pentan-1-one);
   R3 = ethane-1,2-diyl, butane-1,4-diyl,                pentane-1,5-diyl, hexane-1,6-diyl,                (azanediylbis(ethane-2,1-diyl)), or                ((ethane-1,2-diylbis(azanediyl))bis(ethane-2,1-diyl));                and
   R4 = 4-methyl-1,3-dioxolan-2-one moiety of                formula (II) or -NH-R3-NH2;
   x and y = 3-50; and
   z = 1-100.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises (i) reacting bio-based polyester                  diol and epichlorohydrin under the action of a                  catalyst to obtain bio-based polyester diglycidyl                  ether intermediate, (ii) placing the bio-based                  polyester diglycidyl ether intermediate in a                  carbon dioxide atmosphere, and reacting under the                  action of a catalyst to obtain bio-based                  polyester bicyclic carbonate intermediate, and                  (iii) reacting the bio-based polyester bicyclic                  carbonate intermediate with the amine compound to                  obtain final product.Preferred Method: The bio-based polyester                  diol is a hydroxy-terminated polylactic acid                  obtained by polymerization of monomers,                  hydroxy-terminated poly(epsilon-caprolactone),                  hydroxy-terminated polyethylene                  furandicarboxylate and/or hydroxy-terminated                  polybutylene furandicarboxylate, the monomer                  comprises ethylene glycol, propylene glycol,                  butylene glycol and/or diethylene glycol, and the                  degree of polymerization of the bio-based                  polyester diol is 3-50. In step (i), the reaction                  temperature is 60-150degrees Celsius, and the                  reaction time is 2-12 hours. In step (ii), the                  pressure of the carbon dioxide atmosphere is                  0.1-10 MPa, the temperature of the reaction is                  60-150degrees Celsius, and the reaction time is                  2-12 hours, the molar ratio between bio-based                  polyester diglycidyl ether intermediate and the                  catalyst is 1:0.01-0.1. In step (iii), the                  temperature of the reaction is 60-150degrees                  Celsius, and the reaction time is 4-72                  hours.Preferred Components: In step (i), the                  catalyst comprises tetrabutylammonium bromide,                  tetrabutylammonium iodide, tetrabutylammonium                  chloride, hexadecyltrimethylammonium chloride,                  tetramethylammonium bicarbonate, tin(IV) chloride                  and/or boron trifluoride, the molar ratio between                  bio-based polyester diol and epichlorohydrin is                  1:2-100, the molar ratio between bio-based                  polyester diol and the catalyst is 1:0.01-0.10.                  In step (ii), the catalyst comprises                  tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium                  iodide, tetrabutylammonium chloride,                  hexadecyltrimethylammonium chloride,                  tetramethylammonium bicarbonate, potassium                  bromide, potassium iodide, sodium bromide and/or                  sodium iodide. In step (iii), the molar ratio of                  the bio-based polyester bicyclic carbonate                  intermediate to the amine compound is 1:0.8-1.2,                  the amine compound comprises ethylenediamine,                  butanediamine, pentamethylenediamine,                  hexamethylenediamine, diethylenetriamine,                  triethylenetetramine, montanediamine and/or                  isophoronediamine.

   HUANG H

   CAO L

AE UNIV JIANGXI NORMAL (UYJX-C)
GA 2022541610
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of ultrafine ruthenium diphosphide nano-particle electrocatalyst involves: (i) adding 50-200 mg carbon nano-tube and 20-100 mg ruthenium salt i.e. ruthenium nitrate salt, ruthenium acetate salt, ruthenium acetylacetonate salt or ruthenium chloride salt into 80 mL ultra-dry methanol solvent, and uniformly mixing to obtain solution-A; (ii) dissolving hexachlorotriphosphazene and 4,4'-dihydroxydiphenyl sulfone with molar ratio of 2:3 into 20 mL ultra-dry methanol to obtain solution-B, adding solution-B into solution-A to obtain solution-C; (iii) adding 1-2 mL triethylamine to solution-C, and reacting to obtain ruthenium-loaded polymer and carbon nano-tube composite precursor; and (iv) pyrolyzing ruthenium-loaded polymer and carbon nano-tube composite precursor under argon or nitrogen gas atmosphere.

AU LIU Z
   LI L

   MENG L
   ZHANG H
   HU Z
   MIAO X
AE HUNAN CHANGYUAN LICO CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
   HUNAN CHANGYUAN LICO NEW ENERGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
   JINCHI ENERGY MATERIALS CO LTD (JINC-Non-standard)
GA 202232130V
AB    NOVELTY - The synthesis of spherical cobalt carbonate with large particle size involves preparing cobalt sulfate solution and ammonium bicarbonate solution, preparing seed crystals by using ammoniumbicarbons solution as the bottom liquid of the reaction kettle, using water to adjust the size of the seed crystals, adjusting the pH value of the reactor to 7.6-7.8, adding cobalt sulfurate solution to the reaction system, adjusting in the reaction initial 4-10 hours, (ix) controlling the pH of reaction system every 2-5 hours to reduce 0.1 until the pH reaches 7.2-7:3, and maintaining the pH until the particle size of reaction slurry reaches the target value, and filtering, washing, and drying to obtain spherical cobald carbonate.

The method solves the cracking problem of secondary particles in the calcining process. PN CN113999424-A
TI Preparation of polypropylene foaming material involves forming foaming master batch using high-melting-point random copolymer, low-melting-point random copolymer and nucleating agent, forming polypropylene foaming beads, pre-pressing, further prepressing expanded beads under preset conditions.
AU ZHOU Z
   GAO H
   ZHU M
   ZENG J
   HE R
   LIU H
AE WUXI HUITONG LIGHTWEIGHT MATERIALS CO (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 202226073M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polypropylene foaming material involves:
1) selecting high-melting-point random copolymerized polypropylene A and low-melting-point random copolymerized polypropylene B as raw materials; 2) adding inorganic nucleating agent into raw materials of preset proportion, performing co-extrusion granulation to form foaming master batch, polypropylene A is used as foaming master batch core layer and polypropylene B is used as foaming master batch skin layer; 3) foaming master batch in a reaction kettle, into polypropylene foaming beads by using supercritical carbon dioxide as foaming agent; 4) pre-pressing polypropylene foaming beads; 5) subjecting polypropylene expanded beads to prepressing treatment for molding, calculating the internal pressure of polypropylene expanded beads and adjusting the proportion of the random copolymer polypropylene A, random copolymer polypropylene B and inorganic nucleating agent, based on internal pressure value and molding process conditions.
   USE - The method is used for preparing polypropylene foaming material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method of preparing polypropylene foaming material, enables to avoid the phenomenon of 'flash' occurring at the edge of a workpiece, and tape casting.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows graph of the molding parameters and calculated values of the internal pressure of polypropylene expanded beads. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The random copolymerized polypropylene A used in preparation of polypropylene foaming material, has melting point 150-155degrees Celsius and melt index 2-3 g/10 minutes. The random copolymerized polypropylene B has melting point 125-130degrees Celsius and melt index 7-9 g/10 minutes.Preferred Components: The weight ratio of random copolymerized polypropylene A and random copolymerized polypropylene B used, is (10-95): (90-5), preferably (80-95): (20-5). The inorganic nucleating agent used in step 2 of the preparation process, is talcum powder, calcium carbonate, silicon dioxide, zinc borate, titanium dioxide and/or aluminum oxide. The inorganic nucleating agent has a random sheet structure and a specification 5-8 mum. The amount of nucleating agent used is 3.5-5 wt.%.Preferred Method: The internal pressure of the polypropylene foam beads subjected to pre-pressing in step 5, is calculated by:1) preparing nylon mesh bag and balance, weighing the nylon mesh bag using the balance and recording as m1; 2) taking preset amount of foaming beads in the nylon mesh bag and sealing it; 3) placing the nylon mesh bag in a prepressing tank, followed by performing prepressing based on a preset prepressing program; 4) removing the nylon mesh bag immediately after pre-pressing, weighing and recording the weight as m2; 5) Placing the weighed nylon mesh bag in an oven, baking for 2 hours until the weight of the mesh bag is constant, and recording the weight as m3; 6) testing the bulk density rho of the expanded beads in the mesh bag; 7) calculating the internal pressure of the polypropylene expanded beads based on preset formula, the unit of P is bar, unit of rho is g/l, m1, m2 ,m3, unit of (a) is g, T is the ambient temperature on the day of the test, and temperature of the compressed air in pre-pressing tank is the same as the ambient temperature.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel-nitrogen-carbon composite                catalytic material based on biomass comprises                washing the biomass material with deionized water,                oven drying at 70-90degrees Celsius for 8-16 hours,                using the dried and pulverized biomass material as                the carbon source, mixing carbon source, nitrogen                source and metal salt, mixing and placing in the                ball mill pot of zirconia material, ball milling                for 30-60 minutes to obtain the powder; where the                biomass material is corn stalk, sweet potato leaf                or Ginkgo biloba; (ii) adding the ball-milled                powder in a tube furnace, and under argon                protection, heating to 450-500degrees Celsius, and                calcining to obtain a pre-product of biomass-based                nickel-nitrogen-carbon composite catalytic                material; adding the catalytic material into                hydrochloric acid, refluxing and heating, washing                with deionized water until neutral, oven drying,                adding hydrofluoric acid, stirring and immersing,                washing and oven drying.

AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 2021C0630Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves uniformly mixing urea and sodium citrate at a ratio of 10:1, and carrying out pyrolysis at high temperature to prepare graphite phase carbon nitrate. The graphite stage carbon nitrite is ball milled, and graphite surface carbon nitric oxide nanosheet is prepared. The nano-sheet and pyrrole are ultrasonically polymerized, and high-temperature carbonization is carried out to obtain carbon-doped graphite product with high crystallinity. The carbon-deposited graphite content carbon dioxide is mixed with chloroplatinic acid aqueous solution. The freezing and drying are perfromed to obtain the irradiation modified tubular graphite-phase carbon nitride sample.
   USE - Method for preparing hollow tubular graphite phase carbon nitride/platinum nanoparticle composite material by gamma irradiation.
   ADVANTAGE - The process has simple operation, easy control, good repeatability, which is good for large-scale production of industrialization. The process does not need template and toxic reagent. The process has a regular tubular structure, and successfully prepares the carbon tubular material conjugated with the crystalline carbon, optimizes the specific surface area and the charge transmission capability of the carbon-nitride, and enhances the oxygen reduction activity. The process is simple and convenient, and environment-friendly without pollution. The process can be produced in large scale, and has universality.

TI Separating and recycling ammonia gas of            sulfaamidine alkaline solution comprises e.g.            subjecting mixed gas to selective ammonia absorption,            heat exchange, rectifying ammonia-containing solution,            condensing tower top product and refluxing.
AU QIAN W
   LIU Y
AE HUNAN WUGAN PHARM CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2021B2298N
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and recycling ammonia gas of                sulfaamidine alkaline solution comprises (a)                subjecting mixed gas to selective ammonia                absorption under 9-11 barg pressure, vacuum                extracting, heat exchange to obtain                ammonia-containing solution, where mixed gas is gas                phase product separated by gas and liquid after                reaction of sulfamate alkaline solution, selective                ammonia is absorbed in ammonia absorption tower,                tower plate number of ammonia absorption tower is                5-10, temperature of tower top is 50-60 degrees C                and temperature of tower bottom is 160-170 degrees                C, air inlet flow rate of reaction product is 1-1.5                t/hours and liquid inlet flow rate of absorption                liquid 4-5 t/hours, (b) rectifying                ammonia-containing solution to obtain tower top                product and tower bottom product, (c) condensing                tower top product, refluxing to obtain gas phase                product and liquid phase product, and (d)                controlling reflux ratio of liquid phase product,                making it partially reflux to rectify, rest part to                form ammonia water.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and                recycling ammonia gas of sulfaamidine alkaline                solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: achieves separation of ammonia and                carbon dioxide; is economical and environmentally                friendly; and ammonia can be used to prepare                ammonia.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (a), temperature                  of absorption liquid in ammonia absorption tower                  is 30-50 degrees C, pressure is 10 barg. In step                  (b), rectification is carried out by a                  rectification tower, and number of trays of                  rectification tower is 18-22. temperature at top                  of tower is 85-90 degrees C and temperature at                  bottom of tower is 91-100 degrees C, inlet flow                  rate of rectification tower is 5.5-6 t/hours and                  outlet flow rate at bottom of tower is 5.0-5.4                  t/hours. In step (d), reflux ratio is 1:8.5-9.5.                  In step (c), part of bottom product is also                  refluxed to rectification tower, and reflux ratio                  of bottom product is 1:5-7.

AE UNIV NANCHANG HANGKONG (UYNH-C)
GA 2021968189
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing an alkali metal modified                metal oxide catalyst involves (a) adding the alkali                metal salt solution to the water-soluble metal salt                solution for co-precipitation reaction to obtain a                metal-based co-precipitate, where the water-soluble                metal salt in the water-soluble metal salt solution                comprises a water-soluble cobalt salt or a                water-soluble manganese salt, and (b) calcining the                metal-based coprecipitate obtained in the step (a)                to obtain the product.
   USE - Method used for preparing alkali metal                modified metal oxide catalyst used in catalytic                oxidation of volatile organic pollutant (all                claimed).

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The calcination in the                  step (b) is carried out at 300-500 degrees C for                  2-4 hours. The water-soluble cobalt salt comprises                  cobalt(II) chloride hexasulfate, cobalt sulfate,                  cobalt nitrate or cobalt acetate. The                  water-soluble manganese salt comprises manganese                  chloride, manganese nitrate or manganese acetate.                  The concentration of alkali metal ions in the                  alkali metal salt solution in the step (a) is                  0.75-0.85 mol/l. The concentration of metal ions                  in the water-soluble metal salt solution in the                  step (a) is 0.45-0.65 mol/l. The volume ratio of                  the alkali metal salt solution and the                  water-soluble metal salt solution in the step (a)                  is 1-2:10-15.

   SHI Z
   LI M
   TANG T
   QIU J
   JIANG Z
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 202190077S
AB    NOVELTY - High-flame retardant polypropylene foaming beads comprises 10-50 wt.% expanded graphite, 5-20 wt.% one-dimensional rod-shaped material, 10-30 wt.% coated red phosphorus and 20-50 wt.% ammonium polyphosphate. The one-dimensional rod-shaped material is single-wall carbon nano tube, multi-wall carbon nano tube, chopped carbon fiber, chopped glass fiber, chopped steel fiber, and chopped copper fiber.
   USE - The high-flame retardant polypropylene foaming beads is useful in preparation of foamed materials (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The foaming beads and material has short damage length, ignition time is not reached or just reaches the 25 mm marked line, and there is no dropping phenomenon in the combustion process, and can keep the initial shape.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the foaming beads, comprising (i) mixing uniformly polypropylene, flame retardant, nucleating agent and other auxiliary agent, melting by an extruder, extruding, drawing, and cutting to obtain flame-retardant polypropylene particles, and (ii) adding the flame retardant polypropylene particles into a reaction kettle, introducing carbon-dioxide, heating and pressurizing, keeping the temperature and pressure, releasing pressure; and
   (2) use of high flame-retardant polypropylene foamed beads in the preparation of foamed materials, comprising placing the high-retardant polypropylene foam beads in mold, and molding to obtain foamed forming body.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The diameter of the one-dimensional rod-shaped material is 10 nm-100 mu m, the length is 1 mu m-1 mm, and the aspect ratio is greater than or equal to 5. The polypropylene resin is propylene homopolymer, ethylene-propylene random copolymer, propylene-butene-1 random copolymer, and/or ethylene-propylene-butene-1 random copolymer. The nucleating agent is silicon dioxide, zinc borate, kaolin, and/or talc. The other auxiliary agent is compatibilizer, antioxidant, anti-ultraviolet agent, and/or antistatic agent. The compatibilizer is polypropylene-graft-maleic anhydride, polyethylene-octene elastomer, polyethylene-graft-maleic anhydride, and poly(ethylene-co-vinyl acetate). The antioxidant is phosphorus antioxidants, hindered phenol antioxidants, and/or composite antioxidants. The anti-ultraviolet agent is an ultraviolet absorber. The antistatic agent is a mixture of low-molecular-weight antistatic agent and/or high-molecular-weight antistatic agent. The obtained high flame-retardant polypropylene foamed beads are subjected to secondary foaming.

PN WO2021118473-A1
TI Generating induced pluripotent stem cells-derived mesenchymal stem cell used in manufacture of cell therapy products, by dissociating iPSC into single cell suspension, resuspending in mesenchymal stem cell culture medium and culturing cells.
AU COLLIER P M
   VERMA R
   DMITRIJEVS S
AE COLLIER P M (COLL-Individual)
   VERMA R (VERM-Individual)
   DMITRIJEVS S (DMIT-Individual)
GA 202166057V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for generation of induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSC)-derived mesenchymal stem cell (MSC), involves dissociating iPSC into a single cell suspension, resuspending the single cell suspension in a mesenchymal stem cell culture medium to produce outgrowths of heterogeneous cells, plating the heterogeneous cells in a multi-well culture dish without a feeder layer, incubating at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator, and culturing the cells under conditions conducive to proliferation and expansion of the cells.
   USE - The method is useful for generation of iPSC-derived MSC useful in manufacture of cell therapy products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces robust and pure MSCs, enables to form a single master cell bank of iPSCs derived from a single donor to manufacture a needed population of MSCs, and achieves economic manufacture of cell therapy products, including MSCs, at a commercial scale. The method utilizes iPSCs as starting material, due to their ability to proliferate indefinitely and create an essentially infinite amount that is differentiated into extremely large quantities of MSCs. The method eliminates need to constantly seek out fresh donors to meet manufacturing demands. The MSCs are immune-privileged that they can generally be infused or injected into recipients without provoking a dramatic immune response.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step of culturing involves replacing the medium with a fresh pre-warmed mesenchymal stem cell culture, after 24 hours and incubating overnight, replacing the medium with a fresh pre-warmed mesenchymal stem cell culture, after 48 hours and incubating overnight, replacing the medium with a fresh pre-warmed mesenchymal stem cell culture, after 72 hours and incubating overnight, incubating the cells for about 120 hours and passaged to T25flask with fresh pre-warmed MSC medium, and growing the cells for 5 days to reach 90% confluency and passaging to T75flask. The iPSC are dissociated in an 80% confluent flask of iPSC with TrypLE (RTM: Animal origin-free, recombinant enzyme) express. The multi-well culture dish is a 6-well dish. The cells are plated in 1 well of 6-well dish with a total volume of 2ml of MSC medium, without the feeder layer.
   ACTIVITY - None given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Cell therapy. Test details are described but no results given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN112851724-A; CN112851724-B; WO2022174520-A1
TI Preparing vegetarian D-glucosamine hydrochloride            comprises adding the citric acid waste residue to            reactor, sealing the reactor, adding activated carbon            to the aqueous solution, heating, and decolorizing and            filtering solid-liquid mixture.
AU CHEN Y
   ZHAN J
   WANG S
AE JIANGSU AOXIN BIOENG CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU AOXIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202162201Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing vegetarian D-glucosamine                hydrochloride comprises (i) adding citric acid                waste residue to reactor, sealing, stirring, and                passing gas into reactor, dispersing materials in                the gas, introducing mixture of nitrogen, argon,                helium, and carbon dioxide gases; (ii) passing                1-10% hydrogen chloride at -20 degrees C to 30                degrees C, (iii) separating reaction materials into                gas and liquid, and using recovered gas to obtain                solids, (iv) washing solid twice with water,                centrifuging, collecting aqueous solution, and                mixing; (v) adding activated carbon to the aqueous                solution, heating, and decolorizing at 55-70                degrees C for 40-80 minutes, filtering to remove                solid activated carbon, and washing with water, and                mixing filtrate and washing liquid, (vi)                concentrating solution under reduced pressure at                55-70 degrees C and a vacuum of not greater than                0.08 MPa, producing white solid in the material                liquid, (vii) filtering solid-liquid mixture, using                filtrate for next batch of water washing, and                drying solid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing vegetarian                D-glucosamine hydrochloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes easily available raw                materials, unrestricted production resources,                economical, high product quality, stable product                quality, saves the cost of environmental governance                and has better social and economic benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pressure in the                  reactor is 0.1-10 MPa. In the step (i), the                  pressure in the reactor is 0.3-2.5MPa, more                  preferably 0.5-1.5 MPa; keeping for a period of                  time of 0.5-2 hours. In the step (ii), the                  temperature in the reactor is -10-10 degrees C.                  The reaction time described in step (ii) is 2-4                  hours. In the step (v), the decoloring                  temperature is 60-65 degrees C; the added active                  carbon is 1 % of the mass of the citric acid                  waste residue. In the step (v), the decoloring                  time is 55-65 minutes. In the step (vi), the                  concentration temperature is 60-65 degrees C and                  the vacuum is not greater than 0.09 MPa. In the                  step (iv), washed with water and citric acid in                  the amount of slag is 0.5 timesx 3. Preferred                  Components: In the step (ii), the mass of                  hydrogen chloride described is 2-8 wt.% of the                  material, more preferably 4-5 wt.%.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E13 (Heterocyclics.)
MC D05-C; E05-S; E10-A07A; E11-W; E31-B03D

GA 202150501E

An airtight member (120) is inserted between the second gland and the knife disk.
   USE - Safety connecting device for semiconductor equipment gas line.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a safety gas recovery apparatus.
