PN CN112778533-A; CN112778533-B
TI New porphyrin-based porous organic polymer useful            as catalyst in catalytic synthesis of cyclic            carbonate.
AU LUO R
   LIU X
AE UNIV GUANGDONG TECHNOLOGY (UGTE-C)
GA 202154983B
AB    NOVELTY - Porphyrin-based porous organic polymer (I), is                new. The porphyrin groups are connected through a                biaryl group, and the chemical structure of the                biaryl group is centrosymmetric.
   USE - The porphyrin-based porous organic polymer is                useful as catalyst in catalytic synthesis of cyclic                carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The obtained product: is easy to separate; and                can be effectively reused many times. The catalytic                reaction: does not need to add any solvent; and is                environmentally-friendly and suitable for                industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Porphyrin-based porous organic polymer of                formula (R-(R1)m) (I), is new. The porphyrin groups                are connected through a biaryl group, and the                chemical structure of the biaryl group is                centrosymmetric.
   R1 = porphyrin-metal complex of formula                (II);
   m = 2, 3, 4; either
   m = 2;
   R = benzene-1,4-diyl, pyridinium-based                moieties of formulae (II)-(IV); or
   m = 3;
   R = benzene-1,3,5-triyl,                tetrakis(phenyl)methane-4,4',4'',4'''-tetrayl, or                3,3'-(5-(3-methyl-1H-imidazol-3-ium-1-yl)-1,3-phenylene)bis(1-methyl-1H-imidazol-3-ium)                moiety of formula (V) or tetrakis(phenyl)methane-                moiety of formula (VI);
   M = Zn, Al, Mg, Co or Cu; and
   X = Br, Cl or I.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) synthesizing cyclic carbonate, comprising                catalytic synthesizing cyclic carbonate using                epoxide and carbon dioxide as raw materials and                using porphyrin-based porous organic polymer as                catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I) comprises (i) heating monomer compound and                  linker compound and reacting in organic solvent                  to obtain intermediate compound, (ii) dropping                  organic solution of pyrrole into the organic                  solution of the intermediate compound to perform                  first reflux reaction to obtain porphyrin                  polymer, and (iii) adding porphyrin polymer and                  acetate dihydrate of formula (M(OAc)2.2H2O) into                  the organic solution to perform second reflux                  reaction to obtain final product, where the                  monomer compound is                  1,4-bis(1H-imidazol-1-yl)benzene,                  1,3,5-tris(1H-imidazol-1-yl)benzene,                  tetrakis(4-(1H-imidazol-1-yl)phenyl)methane,                  pyrrole or bipyrrole, the linker compound is                  4-(bromomethyl)benzaldehyde,                  4-(chloromethyl)benzaldehyde or                  4-(iodomethyl)benzaldehyde. Preferred Method: The                  heating reaction temperature is 80 degrees C, and                  the time is 24 hours; the first reflux reaction                  temperature is 120-150 degrees C, and the time is                  24 hours; and the second reflux reaction                  temperature is 160 degrees C and the time is 12                  hours. The pressure of the catalysis is 0.1-3                  MPa, the temperature is 25-120 degrees C, and the                  time is 1-24 hours. Preferred Components: The                  amount of the catalyst is 0.05-2.50 mol.% of the                  epoxide, and the concentration of the carbon                  dioxide is 15-100%. The epoxide is                  7-oxabicyclo(4.1.0)heptane or of formulae                  (VII)-(VIII).R2 = H, halo, alkyl, unsaturated ether bond                  or phenoxy; andR3 = H, halo, alkyl or alkoxy.

   PAKRDAEWON
   WOO K D
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)

GA 2020B8321U
AB    NOVELTY - Novel zinc metal organic framework compound comprises metal organic framework compound which is synthesized by using zinc hydrate as metal source, adenine as organic linking agent, and 4,4'-stilbenedicarboxylic acid as structural linking agent.
   USE - The compound is useful for producing 5-membered ring carbonate compound (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound has regular structure, large surface area, stable structure, excellent reactivity and stability compared to conventionally known zinc-containing metal-organic framework catalysts, can be produced under low pressure and low temperature conditions in short reaction time, synthesizes 5-membered cyclic carbonate compound in high yield, and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing novel zinc metal organic framework compound comprising synthesizing zinc containing metal organic framework compound formed by stilbenedicarboxylic acid-based repeating structural unit of formula ((Zn2(H2O)(stdb)2(5HAde)(9HAde)2)n) (I) through synthetic reaction, and preparing reactant mixture by mixing zinc hydrate as metal source, adenine as organic linking agent, 4,4'-stilbenedicarboxylic acid as structural linking agent, and solvent; and
   (2) producing 5-membered ring carbonate compound comprising carbonylation by reacting novel zinc metal organic framework compound containing (I) as catalyst with carbon dioxide and epoxy compound.
   tdb = stilbenedicarboxylic acid;
   HAde = adenine; and
   n = 1-10.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: (I) is repeated to form three-dimensional network structure. The zinc hydrate includes zinc nitrate hydrate, zinc acetate hydrate, and/or zinc acetylacetonate hydrate. Preferred Method: The synthesis method is carried out by using solvent heat synthesis method, microwave synthesis method, and/or ultrasonic chemical synthesis method. The carbonylation reaction is carried out at 80-150 degrees C, carbon dioxide pressure of 0.8-1.6 MPa for 4-20 hours. In the carbonylation reaction, the epoxy compound and the zinc-containing metal-organic framework catalyst are added in molar ratio of 100:0.5-100:5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The epoxy compound includes propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, allyl glycidyl ether, phenyl glycidyl ether and/or vinylcyclohexene oxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   AN S
AE UNIV SHANGHAI JIAOTONG (USJT-C)
GA 202099551T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing nano-bubble                generating device with an inorganic crystal                nano-porous membrane, after filtering the                pressurized gas. The inorganic crystalline                nanoporous membrane is cut to produce 107-109                nanobubbles with a uniform and controllable                particle size of 100 nm or less per mL. The                titanium dioxide nanoporous membrane is a                hydrophobic membrane. The gas source is air,                oxygen, nitrogen, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, ozone,                helium or argon.
   USE - Method for generating nano-bubbles with                uniform and controllable particle size.
   ADVANTAGE - The size of the bubbles produced by the method                is uniform and controllable, stable below 100nm,                and contains a large amount of nanobubbles and low                energy consumption. The particle size and density                of the nanobubbles are controlled by changing the                membrane pore size and air intake time, which has                been verified by experiments to achieve good                results. The nano-bubble generating device is                provided with an inorganic crystal nano-porous                membrane, which can directly cut pressurized gas                through nano-holes in the aqueous solution to                generate nano-bubbles, avoiding a backflow system                and reducing the cost of nano-bubble                generation.

PN CN111732463-A
TI Biological carbon useful in treating sludge comprises iron powder and sludge.
AU JING Y
   LIU H
   LI F
   YUE P
   GU X
   BAI Y
AE DATANG ENVIRONMENT IND GROUP CO LTD (CDAT-C)
GA 2020993127
AB    NOVELTY - Biological carbon comprises 1-2 pts. wt. iron powder and 50-200 pts. wt. sludge.
   USE - The biological carbon is useful in treating sludge (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The biological carbon: reduces activity of heavy metals, polychlorinated biphenyls, polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons, halogenated hydrocarbons and other organic pollutants in the sludge; and reduces odor produced by the sludge fermentation process; solves problem that the current aerobic composting method is used to treat the sludge, it is difficult to effectively remove the heavy metals and organic pollutants in the sludge, and a large amount of odor will be generated during the fermentation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing biological carbon, comprising (i) mixing iron powder with sludge, (ii) under the atmosphere of absolute oxygen and containing hydrogen, heating and decomposing the sludge added with iron powder, obtaining zero-valent iron sludge biological carbon, and (iii) heating the zero-valent iron sludge biological carbon, introducing active gas into the zero-valent iron sludge biological carbon to modify to obtain the biological carbon, where the active gas is water vapor and/or carbon dioxide; and
   (2) processing sludge by biological carbon, comprising uniformly mixing the biological carbon and the sludge to be treated according to mass ratio of 1:(15-20), fermenting to obtain the standard sludge, and/or adding biological carbon to the new sludge to be processed or to part of the standard sludge, and carrying out sludge treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The iron powder is obtained by pulverizing iron-containing waste and sieving through a 100-200 mesh sieve, the organic matter content of the sludge is 50-80 wt.%, and the weight content of nitrogen and phosphorus is not greater than 5%. Preferred Method: The heating and decomposing is carried out at 400-600 degrees C for 2-4 hours. The step (iii) specifically comprises heating the zero-valent iron sludge biological carbon to 750-850 degrees C, introducing water vapor into the zero-valent iron sludge biological carbon to modify to obtain the biological carbon. In the sludge process, taking part of the standard sludge as clinker, uniformly mixing the biological carbon, clinker and new sludge to be treated according to mass ratio of 1:(5-15):(15: 20), and then fermenting again. The fermentation is carried out at 50-65 degrees C for 15-20 days.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. PN WO2020161488-A1
TI Producing monomers from thermoplastic polymer, and/or producing olefins, and/or depolymerizing thermoplastic polymer, and/or recycling a thermoplastic polymer by exposing solid composition comprising thermoplastic polymer to radiation.
AU XIAO T
   EDWARDS P P
   JIE X
AE UNIV OXFORD INNOVATION LTD (UYOX-C)
GA 202076679A
AB    NOVELTY - A process for (i) producing monomers from thermoplastic polymer; and/or (ii) producing olefins; and/or (iii) depolymerization of thermoplastic polymer; and/or (iv) recycling a thermoplastic polymer which involves exposing a solid composition comprising a thermoplastic polymer to electromagnetic radiation in presence of solid catalyst, where the solid catalyst comprises a group 6 or group 4 transition metal.
   USE - Production of monomers from thermoplastic polymer, and/or production of olefins, and/or depolymerization of thermoplastic polymer, and/or recycling a thermoplastic polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The thermoplastic polymers present in waste plastic are converted into monomers. The method allows the production of highly pure monomers with minimal by-products, such as carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) solid composition comprising a solid catalyst in mixture with a thermoplastic polymer, where the solid catalyst comprises a group 6 or group 4 transition metal;
   (2) use of solid composition for (i) producing monomers from thermoplastic polymer; and/or (ii) producing olefins; and/or (iii) depolymerization of thermoplastic polymer; and/or (iv) recycling a thermoplastic polymer; and
   (3) preparation of solid composition which involves providing (i) thermoplastic polymer and (ii) solid catalyst comprising a group 6 or group 4 transition metal, and putting (i) and (ii) into mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solid catalyst comprises a transition metal selected from chromium, molybdenum, tungsten, titanium and zirconium, preferably chromium, molybdenum and tungsten. The transition metal is in the form of a transition metal carbide or oxide, preferably transition metal carbide. Solid catalyst comprises chromium carbide (Cr3C2). Solid catalyst further comprises a promotor, suitably selected from an alkali metal, carbon or aluminum.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Weight ratio of thermoplastic polymer to solid catalyst is 0.2:1-3:1, preferably 0.5:1-2:1. Thermoplastic polymer and solid catalyst are in mixture, suitably intimate mixture. Solid composition comprises thermoplastic polymer and the solid catalyst in mixture, suitably intimate admixture. Thermoplastic polymer comprises greater than or equal to 1 thermoplastic polymers selected from polyethylene, polypropylene and polystyrene. Monomers produced are greater than or equal to 1 of ethylene, propylene and styrene. Preferred Process: Electromagnetic radiation is microwave radiation. Exposure to electromagnetic radiation is conducted in an inert atmosphere. Preferred Parameters: The solid composition is in pelletized or extruded form.

PN CN111454433-A; CN111454433-B
TI New bifunctional oligomeric metal porphyrin complex is prepared by polymerizing bicyclo heptenyl ethanone compound and porphyrin compound by using Grubbs catalyst to obtain compound.
AU ZHUO C
   WANG X
   WANG F
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 2020727175
AB    NOVELTY - Bifunctional oligomeric metal porphyrin complex (I) is new.
   USE - Bifunctional oligomeric metal porphyrin complex.
   ADVANTAGE - The bifunctional oligomeric metal porphyrin complex has excellent catalytic performance, high catalytic activity and high selectivity to prepare polycarbonate, and thus achieved new breakthrough in the catalytic performance of metalloporphyrin complexes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Bifunctional oligomeric metal porphyrin complex of general formula (I) is new.
   x and y=1-25;
   Ra'=a quaternary ammonium salt functional group and a quaternary phosphonium salt functional group;
   Rb'=a metalloporphyrin complex having group of formula (II);
   M=a metal element;
   X=independently selected from halogen, -NO3, CH3COO-, CCl3COO-, CF3COO-, ClO4-, BF4-, BPh4-, -CN, -N3, p-toluic acid group, p-toluene sulfonic acid group, o-nitrophenoloxy, p-nitrophenoloxy, m-nitrophenoloxy, 2,4-dinitrophenoloxy, 3,5-dinitrophenoloxy, 2,4,6-trinitrophenoloxy, 3,5-dichlorophenoloxy, 3,5-difluorophenoloxy, 3,5-di-trifluoromethylphenoloxy or pentafluorophenoloxy; and
   R1-R19=independently selected from H, halogen, 1-5C aliphatic groups, substituted 1-5C aliphatic groups, aryl groups with 1-3 benzene rings, or aryl groups with 1-3 substituted benzene rings.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing bifunctional oligomeric metal porphyrin complex; and
   (2) a method for preparing polycarbonate, which involves copolymerizing carbon dioxide and epoxide in presence of metalloporphyrin complex or metalloporphyrin complex as a catalyst to obtain polycarbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The bifunctional oligomeric metal porphyrin complex is prepared by polymerizing 1-(bicyclo(2.2.1)hept-5-en-2-yl)ethanone compound of formula (III) and porphyrin compound of general formula (IV) by using Grubbs III catalyst to obtain compound of formula (V). The obtained compound is reacted with a metal salt compound to obtain the compound of formula (VI), which is reacted with tertiary amine compounds to obtain the bifunctional oligomeric metal porphyrin complex. The tertiary amine compound is trimethylamine, tributylamine and trihexylamine. The metal salt compound is selected from magnesium, aluminum, zinc, chromium, manganese, iron, cobalt, titanium, yttrium, nickel or ruthenium. The metalloporphyrin complex and the epoxide are provided in a molar ratio of 1:2000-500000. The epoxide comprises ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, 1,2-butylene oxide, cyclohexane oxide, cyclopentene oxide, epichlorohydrin, glycidyl methacrylate, methyl glycidyl ether, phenyl glycidyl ether and styrene alkylene oxide.
   Preferred Definitions:
   Ra'=a quaternary ammonium salt functional group having a structure of formula (XII) and a quaternary phosphonium salt functional group having a structure of formula (XIII);
   n, m and k=1-6;
   Y1-Y2=independently selected from halogen anions, -NO3, CH3COO-, CCl3COO-, CF3COO-, ClO4-, BF4-, p-toluenesulfonic acid group, p-toluene sulfonic acid group, o-nitrophenoloxy, p-nitrophenoloxy, m-nitrophenoloxy, 2,4-dinitrophenoloxy, 3,5-dinitrophenoloxy, 2,4,6-trinitrophenoloxy, 3,5-dichlorophenoloxy, 3,5-difluorophenoloxy, 3,5-di-trifluoromethylphenoloxy or pentafluorophenoloxy; and
   M=selected from magnesium, aluminum, zinc, chromium, manganese, iron, cobalt, titanium, yttrium, nickel or ruthenium.
   .
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 06 Bifunctional oligomeric metal porphyrin complexes are specifically claimed, e.g. group of formulae (VII)-(XI)..

AU DOU Y

AE DOU Y (DOUY-Individual)
GA 202063652D
AB    NOVELTY - Sewage treatment system, comprises an integrated pre-processing device, an RHS hydrolysis reaction precipitator, a RH-MBR reactor and a clear water tank connected in sequence, and the treatment process of sewage treatment system comprising (1) pumping the domestic sewage into the RHS hydrolysis reaction precipitator after degreased, decontaminating and sedimenting by the integrated pretreatment device; (2) hydrolyzing and acidifying the wastewater by RHS hydrolytic reaction precipitator, transforming difficult biochemical substances into easily biochemical substances, degrading large molecular substances into small molecular substances, and degrading organic substances into various organic acids; (3) discharging hydrolyzed and acidified water into the RHMBR reactor under the action of aerobic bacteria, degrading biochemical oxygen demand (BOD5) in the sewage into carbon dioxide and water; and (4) after treated by RH-MBR reactor, reusing or discharging the sewage in clear water tank.
   USE - Used as a sewage treatment system.
   ADVANTAGE - The sewage treatment system: has a high degree of integration and is suitable for sewage treatment projects with a small site area; after treatment, the sewage has chemical oxygen demand, five-day biochemical oxygen demand, solid suspended solids, bacterial population, and feces; and indicators including large intestine and pH are better than the current national standards.

PN CN111296746-A
TI Making cold noodle gluten comprises taking e.g.            flour, kneading, covering with plastic wrap, rolling,            washing, making vermicelli and gluten, sealed packaging            box, refrigerating, opening the packaging box, adding            sauce and mixing.
AU HAO Y
AE SHANGHAI AIYITE CATERING CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020582341
AB    NOVELTY - Making cold noodle gluten comprises (1) taking                500-600 pts. wt. flour, 250-300 pts. wt. warm                water, 3-15 pts. wt. salt, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. yeast,                15-50 pts. wt. flavoring agent and 20-30 pts. wt.                auxiliary materials, (2) adding warm water and salt                to the flour to the proportion, kneading to obtain                the dough, (3) covering with plastic wrap and damp                cloth for 30-50 minutes, then continue kneading the                dough for 5-10 minutes, adding the dough to the                cold water, after the dough has not reached the                cold water level, rolling and kneading the dough,                changing the water and continuing to knead, washing                the dough for 15-20 minutes each time and repeating                for 3-5 times, (4) making vermicelli and gluten,                (5) adding cold noodle and gluten into the sealed                packaging box and simultaneously fill with carbon                dioxide gas to seal, refrigerating at -18-0 degrees                C for 10-20 hours and opening the packaging box,                adding sauce and auxiliary materials and mixing                well.
   USE - The method is useful for making cold noodle                gluten.
   ADVANTAGE - The gluten: improves the low-temperature                storage time of the cold skin; ensure the                nutrients; and has better taste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The flavoring agent                  comprises 3-15 pts. wt. vinegar, preferably 5                  pts. wt., 1-10 pts. wt. spicy oil, preferably 3                  pts. wt., 1-3 pts. wt. sesame oil, preferably 1                  pts. wt., 3-13 pts. wt. fresh soy sauce,                  preferably 5 pts. wt., 1-3 pts. wt. sugar                  preferably 1 pts. wt., 1-3 pts. wt. salt                  preferably 1 pts. wt., 1-3 pts. wt. garlic paste,                  preferably 1 pts. wt. and 2 pts. wt. chicken                  flavor. The auxiliary material is peanuts,                  shredded cucumber, shredded carrots, bean                  sprouts, coriander and spring onion. The main                  components of the carbon dioxide composite gas                  charged are 40-60% nitrogen and 60-40% carbon                  dioxide. The flour is added with 3-5 pts. wt.                  chitooligosaccharide and 3-8 parts of                  chitosan.

PN CN111070814-A; CN111070814-B
TI Sandwich structure metal material useful for in-situ protective coating of magnesium alloy, comprises three material layered sandwich structure, where one layer is magnesium-zinc and middle layer is quasicrystalline phase.
AU LIU K
   GUO C
   DU W
   LI S
   WANG C
   DU X
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 202038072L
AB    NOVELTY - Sandwich structure metal material comprises three material layered sandwich structure, where one layer is magnesium-zinc, the middle layer is quasicrystalline I phase, and the other layer is long-period stacking-order phase. The three different types of metal compound forms a sandwich structure of metal material.
   USE - The sandwich structure metal material is useful for in-situ protective coating of magnesium alloy (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the sandwich structure metal material, comprising (i) preheating magnesium-rare earth intermediate alloy in a crucible at 200-220 degrees C, passing sulfur hexafluoride/carbon dioxide mixed gas to increase the temperature, melting at 720-820 degrees C for 15-25 minutes, then stirring and removing scales and other residues on the surface of the alloy liquid, (ii) cooling the melting temperature to 700-780 degrees C, adding magnesium ingot, holding for 10-20 minutes, then stirring and removing slag, (iii) lowering the temperature to 700-760 degrees C, adding zinc, stirring and removing slag, (iv) increasing the heating current, raising the temperature to 760-800 degrees C, stirring alloy liquid and removing slag, and (v) preserving heat of alloy liquid for more than half an hour, cooling to 720-780 degrees C, pouring into a mold and allowing to stand.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sandwich structure metal material comprises 15-30 wt.% rare-earth, 10-25 wt.% zinc and magnesium (balance amount). Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the temperature of the alloy liquid is not greater than melting temperature of the master alloy during adding magnesium ingot. In the step (iii), the temperature of the magnesium ingot is not greater than temperature during adding zinc.

PN CN111011478-A
TI Preserving tender fresh garlic comprises harvesting fresh garlic, selecting, trimming, removing outer layer, adding fresh garlic, soaking garlic, sterilizing and color-protecting, draining water and storing.
AU ZHANG L
   SHI J
   WANG H
   HAN H
   SHEN Y
AE XUZHOU LIMING FOOD CO LTD (XUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202034123Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving tender fresh garlic comprises (a) harvesting fresh garlic in time for 15 -20 days, when the leaves turn 1/2-2/3 yellow and the bulbs are enlarged but has not spread during harvesting period, selecting fresh garlic clove with uniform size, no plant diseases and insect pests and no garlic mechanical injury, (b) manually trimming the roots, removing on a day, leaving 1-3 cm base leaves on garlic head, removing outer layer of old skin with soil, leaving 2-3 layers of garlic fresh carpel while removing mechanically damaged and diseased garlic, (c) adding fresh garlic after cleaning into cold storage sterilizing to make it quickly cooling to 0 degrees C for 12 hours, (d) soaking the garlic into heat temperature in fresh sterilizing liquid, maintaining garlic at 0 degrees C and (e) sterilizing the fresh garlic and color-protecting and draining water in the shade, storing at -1 to -2 degrees C having a humidity of 92-94%, oxygen content of 3.5-4% with an ambient carbon dioxide content of 17-19%.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving tender fresh garlic.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is safe and fresh; slows the water loss of fresh garlic and maintains a high hardness; inhibits the respiration of fresh garlic; and has a significant freshness preservation effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The process of preserving tender fresh garlic comprises soaking for 10-12 minutes, extracting with deionized hydrothermal reflux for 3 times for 2 hours each time and centrifuging at 4500 revolutions/minute for 15-20 minutes, allowing to stand in alcohol for 12 hours and extracting 1 kg celery leaves with 18-22 l deionized hydrothermal reflux. The process of preparing celery leaf extract comprises cleaning the celery leaf, drying, crushing, performing hot reflux extraction with deionized water, filtering, vacuum extracting and concentrating the filtrate, centrifuging and collecting the supernatant, adding 6 times volume of absolute ethanol, uniformly mixing, allowing to stand for alcohol precipitation, collecting precipitate, redissolving with deionized water, precipitation, collecting precipitate, washing with absolute ethanol, reconstituting with deionized water, redissolving using Sevag method and freeze-drying. Preferred Components: The preserving sterilizing liquid is made up of water, citric acid, chitooligosaccharide and celery leaf extract in a mass ratio of 100:0.25-0.35:0.07-0.11:17-19.

TI Performing molding process for sand mold, involves mixing dried sand mold material uniformly with binder to make molds according to portion drawings, and uniformly mixing sand mold and binder are put into lower mold and upper mold.
AU LI S
AE NINGGUO ZHUFENG STEEL BALL CASTING CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2020298278
AB    NOVELTY - Performing molding process involves mixing dried sand mold material uniformly with the binder to make molds according to portion drawings. The uniformly mixed sand mold and binder are put into the lower mold and upper mold of the mold, then put the core in the mold and close the mold. The carbon dioxide gas is injected into the mold after mold clamping to solidify the binder to obtain a sand mold blank. The flame-roasting is performed on inner surface of the sand mold blank to obtain a sintered sand mold. The sintered sand mold is heat-treated at 230-270 degrees C for 80-150 second to obtain a sand mold.
   USE - Method for performing molding process for sand mold.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform molding process which is helps to improves the collapsibility of the water glass, avoids the problem that the sand material after use has agglomeration, high strength and cannot be recycled, which ensures the cleanness of the surface of the casting and facilitates subsequent processing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for forming a sand mold, which involves dissolving 3-8 pts. wt. gelatin in 40-50 pts. wt. water glass at 60 degrees C, after cooling to obtain modified gelatin modified water glass.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The particle diameter range of the molding sand material is 100-200 mesh. The density of the water glass is 1.35-1.45 gram per cubic centimetre. The added amount of the binder is 2-4 wt.% sand material. The flame smoked used as an open flame burned by liquid paraffin, and the inner surface of the sand mold blank is blackened. The forming layer thickness of the sand mold blank is 5-15mm. Preferred Compositions: The binder includes 80-90 pts. wt. modified water glass and 3-5 pts. wt. sodium fatty acid. Preferred Components: The modified water glass is gelatin modified water glass. The molding sand material includes one or more of silica sand and baozhu sand. Preferred Process: The sand type material is dried by an air dryer, and the drying temperature is 80 degrees C.

PN CN110922189-A
TI Preparing silicon carbide microwave heating medium kiln furniture useful for appliances, devices and accessories that use microwave heating, and containers or structural parts for holding materials in microwave oven chamber.
AU ZHU Z
   LI R
   LI T
AE JIANGXI TOP NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202025907Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silicon carbide microwave heating medium kiln furniture using powder quartz comprises (i) mixing raw materials with powder quartz and carbon powder at molar ratio of 0.7:1, mechanical mixing and stirring, placing in sintering furnace for heating, heating to 1800-1900 degrees C, heat preserving, sintering to produce cubic silicon carbide powder, showing gray black, (ii) heating silicon carbide powder to oxidize unreacted carbon powder into carbon dioxide to obtain light green cubic silicon carbide powder, (iii) mixing cubic silicon carbide powder collected with specific amount of absolute ethanol to form mud, stirring, then squeezing mixed mud into silicon carbide microwave heating medium kiln body through hydraulic press, (iv) drying silicon carbide microwave heating medium kiln body, (v) placing dried silicon carbide microwave heating medium kiln body blank into sintering furnace for sintering to obtain silicon carbide microwave heating medium kiln furniture.
   USE - The silicon carbide microwave heating medium kiln furniture is useful for appliances, devices and accessories that use microwave heating, containers or structural parts for holding materials in microwave oven chamber, pipes, heat exchange tubes or water tanks connecting inside and outside of microwave oven, and as heating medium of microwave boiler system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The kiln furniture improves conversion efficacy of microwave energy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The shape of microwave heating medium kiln furniture is pot-shaped, basin-shaped, box-shaped, single-walled tubular, or honeycomb tubular, and also includes box shape of import and export, crucible, tray, partition/pad plate. The microwave heating medium kiln is honeycomb silicon carbide tube, the honeycomb silicon carbide tube is used in the microwave heating cavity, and the two ends of the tube mouth are connected with water pipes, after the microwave oven is started, the silicon carbide tube in the microwave heating chamber will quickly heat up, and then heat the water flowing through the tube. The microwave heating medium kiln is silicon carbide basin or a silicon carbide pot, the silicon carbide basin or the silicon carbide pot contains the heated objects and is placed in the microwave heating cavity of the microwave oven, after the microwave oven is started, the silicon carbide pot or silicon carbide pot in the microwave heating chamber will be quickly heated, and then the contents in the silicon carbide pot or silicon carbide pot will be heated. Preferred Method: In the step (v), the temperature rise curve includes after heating up to 400-500 degrees C, controlling the temperature for 3 hours, performing liposuction, raising temperature to 800-900 degrees C for 2 hours, heating up to 1800-1900 degrees C, and controlling temperature for not less than 10 hours. In the step (i), the stirring speed of mechanical stirring and mixing is not less than 100 revolutions/minute, and the stirring time is not less than 10 hours. In the step (iii), mixing cubic silicon carbide powder and absolute ethanol into sludge at a ratio of 100 g:10 ml, stirring by a wet mill, where the stirring speed is not less than 30 revolutions/minute, and the stirring time is not less than 10 hours. In the step (iv), carrying out drying in microwave tunnel kiln at drying temperature of 180 degrees C and drying time of 0.5 hours.

PN CN110868804-A
TI Method for manufacturing of printed wiring board for portable card type equipment, involves positioning and moving forward substrate on laser tabletop according to positioning holes to perform laser drilling to obtain desired vias.
AU SHI L
   BAI Y
   YUAN C
   ZHENG Z
   XU L
   KE X
AE QUZHOU SHUNLUO CIRCUIT BOARD CO LTD (QUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202020773B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves drilling mechanically the positioning holes on a substrate. The surface copper of the substrate is thinned. An oxidation treatment such as a black treatment or a browning treatment is performed on the thinned copper surface, so that the copper surface is in a dark and rough state. The substrate on a laser tabletop is positioned and moved forward according to the positioning holes to perform the laser drilling using a carbon dioxide laser drilling machine to obtain the desired vias. The positioning hole is punched in a fixed position of the substrate, so that the substrate is positioned at a fixed position of the laser tabletop. The thickness of the thinned copper is less than or equal to 9 microns.
   USE - Method for manufacturing of printed wiring board for portable card type equipment such as transflash (TF) board or U disk.
   ADVANTAGE - The drill pin is not needed and the difficulty in making the drill pin is eliminated since the hole diameter to be drilled is reduced. The change of the drill pin causing a problem in the drilling effect is greatly reduced. The TF board substrate is subjected to copper reduction treatment and copper surface oxidation treatment to ensure the laser drilling effect.

PN CN110272932-A
TI Preparing Ganoderma lucidum spore powder            polysaccharide-peptide, comprises e.g. performing            low-temperature physical breaking processing of            Ganoderma lucidum spore powder and mixing degreased            broken Ganoderma lucidum spore powder-pure water.
AU XU K
   ZHOU J
   TAN Y
   HU Y
   XU W
AE KAIPING HEALTHWISE HEALTH CO LTD (KAIP-Non-standard)
GA 201982543X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Ganoderma lucidum spore powder                polysaccharide-peptide, comprises (a) carrying out                low-temperature physical breaking processing of                Ganoderma lucidum spore powder, until the wall                breaking rate reaches more than 95%; (b) extracting                the Ganoderma lucidum spore powder after breaking                by a supercritical carbon dioxide extracting                device, separating twice and collecting oil and                degreased broken Ganoderma lucidum spore powder;                (v) mixing the degreased broken Ganoderma lucidum                spore powder with pure water, adjusting pH to 5-10                using alkali solution and carrying out enzymolysis                in presence of an enzyme; (d) heating enzymolysis                liquid to 90-100 degrees C under stirring                conditions, maintaining for 1-2 hours to obtain an                extracting liquid, separating and collecting the                clear filtrate; (e) concentrating the filtrate                until the solid content is 35-55%; (f) subjecting                the extracting liquid to vacuum drying; and (f)                crushing and sieving by 60-80-mesh sieve.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Ganoderma                lucidum spore powder polysaccharide-peptide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, environmentally-friendly                and convenient for industrial production; has                shorten treatment time; performs enzymatic                hydrolysis which protects glycopeptide structure                and is easy to filter; prevents use of organic                solvent avoiding generation of residues, and                glycopeptide yield can reach up to 9.50% and                polysaccharide peptide content of the sample is                36.1%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The low temperature                  physical wall breaking is performed by a                  vibrating superfine pulverizer under wall                  breaking time of 25-35 minutes. The pumping                  frequency of the supercritical carbon dioxide                  extracting device is 25-30 Hz, and the flow rate                  of supercritical carbon dioxide is greater than                  or equal to 120 l/hour. The extraction pressure                  is 18-25 MPa, the extraction temperature is 40-50                  degrees C and the extraction time is 4-5 hours.                  The primary separation pressure is 8-10 MPa with                  separation temperature of 40-50 degrees C and the                  secondary separation pressure is 5-6 MPa with                  separation temperature of 28-35 degrees C. The                  alkali solution is sodium hydroxide, potassium                  hydroxide, calcium hydroxide or aqueous ammonia.                  The pH is adjusted to 7-8.5 using alkali                  solution. The enzyme is cellulase, a complex                  protease, an alkaline protease or a neutral                  protease. The enzyme is added in an amount of                  0.1-2 wt.% based on the weight of the degreased                  broken Ganoderma lucidum spore powder. The                  enzymatic hydrolysis is carried out at 40-55                  degrees C for 2-12 hours. The method comprises                  carrying out ultrasonic treatment during                  enzymatic hydrolysis, under ultrasonic frequency                  of 20-40 KHz for 4-6 hours. The concentration                  process is carried out using a double-effect                  vacuum energy-saving concentration device having                  vacuum of -0.66 Mpa and the temperature is 70-80                  degrees C and the second-effect vacuum is -0.88                  MPa and the temperature is 50-60 degrees C.

AE XINXIANG GUANGZHOU HYDROGEN TECHNOLOGY (XINX-Non-standard)
GA 2019780100
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting (S1) a carbon paper blank density of 10-20 g/m2, and impregnated in a resin solution of a phenolic resin, a furan resin ester, a polyvinylpyrrolidone, or a polyimide having a concentration ranging from 0.1 to 0.2 g/ml, and then baking in an oven at 80 to 160 degrees C. The yttrium aluminum garnet (YAG) or carbon dioxide (CO2) laser is used (S2). The laser sets the power density, and irradiation time. The carbon paper is heated (S3) at room temperature to 10 degrees C /min from room temperature to 1600 degrees C for 120 min, after the furnace is turned off the furnace temperature is naturally cooled to take out at 300 degrees C or less to obtain finished carbon paper. The protective atmosphere is nitrogen, argon and helium.

TI Method for preparing flexible self-supporting            lithium-sulfur battery anode for lithium-sulfur            battery, involves precarbonizing natural fiber and            calcining, followed by loading elemental sulfur in            carbon carrier and performing heat treatment.
AU SONG H
   CHEN X
   LI F
   YAN C
   ZHENG G
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201961071Y
AB    NOVELTY - A flexible self-supporting lithium-sulfur                battery anode preparing method involves                pre-carbonizing a natural fiber, calcining in a                protective atmosphere to obtain a carbonized                material, activating and/or nitriding the                carbonized material to obtain a carbon support,                loading elemental sulfur in a carbon carrier and                performing a heat treatment under vacuum to obtain                the finished product, where the temperature of the                pre-carbonization is 150-300 degrees C, and the                calcination temperature is 500-1200 degrees C, the                activation is a carbon dioxide gas activation, a                potassium hydroxide activation, a zinc chloride                activation or a water vapor activation, and the                nitriding treatment is an ammonia water assisted                hydrothermal treatment or an ammonia gas                calcination treatment, and the temperature of the                heat treatment is from 120-350 degrees C.
   USE - Method for preparing flexible self-supporting                lithium-sulfur battery anode for lithium-sulfur                battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing flexible                self-supporting lithium-sulfur battery anode with                high mass load, inhibited volume expansion problem                appearing in the cyclic process of the                lithium-sulfur battery, and in simple,                environment-friendly and cost effective                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                flexible self-supporting lithium-sulfur battery                anode prepared by the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The protective                  atmosphere is argon or nitrogen.    TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The natural fiber                  comprises natural plant fiber and natural animal                  fiber, where the natural plant fiber comprises                  cotton, kapok, flax and palm fruit fiber, and the                  natural animal fiber comprises wool, rabbit,                  mohair and silk.

PN WO2019115871-A1; FI201706100-A; EP3723901-A1; FI128921-B1
TI Treating side water fraction used in e.g. aqueous-phase reforming, involves reforming side water fraction comprising undesired compound(s) using nickel/nickel aluminate catalyst to convert organic compound to hydrogen.
AU KIHLMAN J
   CORONADO I
AE VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
   VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
GA 201951840W
AB    NOVELTY - Treating side water fraction (1) comprising at least one undesired compound formed by thermochemical treatment of biomass, involves reforming (2) the fraction to convert organic compound to at least hydrogen (5) and optionally also other compounds (6) chosen from organic compounds, hydrocarbons, carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide. The reformation is catalyzed by nickel/nickel aluminate catalyst (3).
   USE - Treating side water fraction used in aqueous-phase reforming, production of bio-fuels, after Fischer-Tropsch (FT) reaction, after pyrolysis and/or its post-process, and treatment of water fraction comprising organic compounds (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures effective treatment of different water based streams. High conversion rates and hydrogen selectivity are obtained by reforming treatment. The catalyst used is less expensive. The method enables industrially applicable, simple and affordable way to treat water based streams, provides environmentally friendly bio-fuel production process and decreases the amount of wastewater for disposal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for treating side water fraction, which comprises at least one reforming treatment device (12), and the reforming catalyst arranged inside the device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of an apparatus for treating side water fraction.
   Side water fraction (1)
   Reforming treatment (2)
   Nickel/nickel aluminate catalyst (3)
   Hydrogen (5)
   Other compounds (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The reforming treatment device is an aqueous-phase reforming (APR) reactor. Preferred Conditions: The reforming treatment (2) is carried out at a temperature of 200-250 degrees C, under pressure 30-34bar.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The nickel/nickel aluminate catalyst is a nickel/nickel aluminate spinel washcoat catalyst formed by arranging thin washcoat layer on the substrate. Preferred Composition: The nickel content of the nickel/nickel aluminate catalyst (3) is 8-15%wt. The side water fraction comprises 1-40%/volume organic compounds. Preferred Process: The nickel/nickel aluminate catalyst (3) is prepared by impregnating nickel precursors into a boehmite carrier material to form a catalytic material which is dried and calcined, and a slurry comprising at least a catalytic material is prepared and stirred and the substrate is washcoated with slurry and is subjected to thermal treatment.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The biomass is lignocellulosic based biomass. Preferred Process: The biomass is treated by at least one process chosen from gasification, Fischer-Tropsch (FT) reaction of syngas, pyrolysis and/or bio-oil refining. Hydrogen (5) may be recirculated to thermochemical treatment to adjust hydrogen/cobalt ratio or to improve hydrotreatment.

PN CN109810254-A
TI New ion-crosslinked            polyetheretherketone-polyethersulfone polymer useful in            preparing film for salt difference power            generation.
AU WEN L
   DONG T
   HUANG X
   KONG X
   JIANG L
AE BEIJING SAITE CHAORUN INTERFACE TECHNOLO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019488046
AB    NOVELTY - Ion-crosslinked                polyetheretherketone-polyethersulfone polymer (I)                is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in preparing film for salt                difference power generation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The film has excellent chemical stability and                power density exceeds the commercial Nafion 112                film at different salt concentration                multiples.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ion-crosslinked                polyetheretherketone-polyethersulfone polymer of                formula (I) is new.
   A1 = e.g. benzophenone-4,4'-diyl,                sulfonyldibenzene-4,4'-diyl, benzonitrile-2,6-diyl,                1-chlorobenzene-2,4-diyl;
   A2 and A7 = benzophenone-4,4'-diyl,                sulfonyldibenzene-4,4'-diyl, benzene-1,4-diyl,                bipheyl-4,4'-diyl, biphenyl-3,3'-diyl,                2,5-diphenylbenzene-1,4-diyl, or                benzene-1,2,3,4,5-pentayl;
   A3 =                N3,N3,N3',N3'-tetramethylbiphenyl-3,3'-diamine-4,4'-diyl,                pentane-2,2-diyldibenzene-4,4'-diyl,                2-methoxybenzene-1,4-diyl, biphenyl moiety of                formula (II), sulfonyldibenzene moiety of formula                (III);
   R = H or -OCH2-;
   A4 = e.g. sulfur trioxide containing moiety of                formula (IV), carbon dioxide containing moiety of                formula (V) or 1H-imidazole moiety of formula (VI)                ;
   L = 1-12;
   r = 1-16;
   A5 = benzophenone-4,4'-diyl,                sulfonyldibenzene-4,4'-diyl, benzonitrile-2,6-diyl,                1-chlorobenzene-2,4-diyl;
   A6 = biphenyl moiety of formula (II);                and
   m, n, p, q = 5-50 (length of the hydrophilic                and hydrophobic portion).
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) film obtained by (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises carrying out ion-crosslinking                  polymerization of side chain type poly(ether                  ether ketone) and a side chain type polyether                  sulfone. Preferred Method: The preparation of                  side chain poly(ether ether ketone) comprises (i)                  taking A1 , A2 and A3 as precursors, and then                  carrying out polycondensation reaction to obtain                  polymer A with methoxy group, (ii) demethylating                  the polymer A to obtain polymer B having phenolic                  hydroxy group, (iii) reacting polymer B with a                  sulfonic acid or carboxylic acid-containing                  monomer at 25-110 degrees C to obtain polymer C                  having an acidic group i.e. a side chain type                  poly(ether ether ketone). In step (ii), polymer A                  is demethylated by boron tribromide. The                  preparation of side chain poly(ether ether                  ketone) comprises (i) taking A5, A6 and A7 as                  precursors, and then carrying out                  polycondensation reaction to obtain polymer D                  with methoxy group, (ii) reacting polymer D with                  a bromide ion-containing monomer under a strong                  oxidizing agent to obtain a polymer E, and (iii)                  reacting polymer E with an imidazole salt in an                  organic solvent to obtain imidazole                  group-containing polymer F i.e. side chain type                  polyethersulfone. The reaction temperature of the                  polycondensation reaction is 135-170 degrees C,                  and the reaction time is 1.5-4 hours. The mixed                  mass ratio between side chain type poly(ether                  ether ketone) and side chain type                  polyethersulfone is 5-20 wt.%; the ion                  crosslinking polymerization temperature is 35-75                  degrees C. Preferred Components: In step (ii),                  the strong oxidizing agent is                  azobisisobutyronitrile, potassium superoxide or                  sodium superoxide.

PN CN109706115-A
TI Constructing mouse bone marrow mesenchymal stem            cell line, comprises e.g. adding cell culture medium            into fetal bovine serum, penicillin and streptomycin,            adding into bone marrow, culturing, adhering and            digesting.
AU HU Y
   XU Y
AE UNIV CHINA MEDICAL (UYMC-C)
GA 201942661Q
AB    NOVELTY - Constructing mouse bone marrow mesenchymal                stem cell line, comprises (i) adding the total                volume of the cell culture medium into 20% fetal                bovine serum, 100 U/ml penicillin, 100 mu g/ml                streptomycin and 1% glutamine, and storing at 4                degrees C, (ii) adding step (i) into the bone                marrow of the mouse to prepare a cell culture                solution to obtain a suspension cell liquid, and                culturing the suspension cell liquid at 37 degrees                C, under mixture gas containing 5% carbon dioxide                and 5% oxygen for 4 days, discarding the culture                solution, and adding the cell culture solution                prepared in the step (i) to continue the culture,                and (iii) adhering the primary cultured cells to                the 10th day, digesting the cells with 0.25%                trypsin, and then the cell culture solution                prepared in step (i) is used for the first time                with 1:1 cells, subculturing, followed by passage                1:2 or 1:3 every 5-7 days until the 20th                generation, and establishing the mouse bone                marrow-derived mesenchymal stem cell line                successfully.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing mouse                bone marrow mesenchymal stem cell line.
   ADVANTAGE - The cell line is fibroblast. The method is                simple and has high in reproducibility and promotes                practical application of mesenchymal stem cell                tissue engineering.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The method further                  comprises the DMEM culture solution has a glucose                  content of 1500 mg/l, and the fetal calf serum is                  a secondary filtered fetal bovine serum. The cell                  culture incubator contains 5% oxygen and the                  culture medium is changed for the first time                  after 4 days. Preferred Method: The method                  comprises taking the mouse for 6-8 weeks old                  mice, and sacrificing by cervical dislocation,                  spraying the abdomen of the mouse with 75%                  alcohol, and taking out the bilateral leg bones                  and placing in a centrifuge tube containing 75%                  alcohol for 5 minutes, soaking, wiping the leg                  bones with sterile gauze, carefully removing the                  muscle tissue adhering to the bone, transferring                  into a 60 mm diameter sterile culture dish                  containing 5 ml of DPBS, cutting off the ends of                  the leg bones, rinsing the bone marrow with DPBS,                  and repeatedly blowing, disrupting the cells,                  allowing to stand for 3 minutes, filtering                  through a 70 mu m sterile filter into a 50 ml                  centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 300 g for 10                  minutes, and discarding the supernatant. The                  method comprises cutting the two ends of the leg                  bone, sucking DPBS by a syringe, and washing out                  the bone marrow from the femur or the tibia until                  the bone is white.

PN CN109608151-A; CN109608151-B
TI Preparing high-strength carbide material by steel slag micro mist comprises e.g. drying the steel slag, grinding the dried steel slag, grading the gypsum, mixing steel slag and gypsum and adding to the mixed dry material.
AU XING Y
   WANG X
   LI J
   ZHANG S
   NI W
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 201935820S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-strength carbide material by steel slag micro mist comprises (a) drying the steel slag to a moisture content of 0-1%, grinding the dried steel slag and grading the gypsum to a moisture content of 0-1% and grinding the gypsum after grinding to a ratio surface area of 400-1000 kg/m2; (b) mixing steel slag and gypsum prepared in to 70-100 wt.% of steel slag and 0-30 wt.% of gypsum, adding total dry mass of 5-25 wt.% to the mixed dry material, adding water and stirring for 90-180 seconds to obtain a mixed wet powder; (c) taking mixed wet powder and adding it into a molding die for press molding and demolding;and (d) placing test piece removed after the molding in a constant temperature and humidity carbonization environment for carbonization curing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-strength carbide material by steel slag micro mist.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can overcomes the problem of low comprehensive utilization rate of steel slag, improve absorption of carbon dioxide in industrial waste gas, alleviate the greenhouse effect to a certain extent, prepare product having excellent early strength and stability; and is environmentally-friendly and economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a) steel slag comprises electric furnace steel slag, converter steel slag or open hearth steel slag, where the calcium sulfide content in the steel slag is 30% or more. In the step (d) carbonized product has a carbonization strength of 50 MPa or more per day. Preferred Method: In the step (a) steel slag is ground and graded, where dried steel slag is respectively ground to a specific surface area of 250-400 kg/m2 and 500-800 kg/m2 and steel slag mixture is prepared by mixing the specific surface area of 250-400 kg/m2 and 500-800 kg/m2 in a mass ratio of 1:2-1:0.5. In the step (a) gypsum is a desulfurized gypsum produced by a power plant or a steel plant or natural gypsum. In the step (c) molding pressure in the press forming is 2-35 MPa and the dwell time is not less than 1 minute. In the step (d) curing temperature of carbonization is 20-40 degrees C, relative humidity of curing is 60-100%, volumetric concentration of carbon dioxide in the carbonized environment is 17-23% and curing time is not less than 2 hours.

An                electrolytic solution comprising a precursor of the                metal oxide and a nitrate is electrochemically                deposited on the metal oxide on the set of vertical                aligned carbon nanotubes, where the deposition is                carried out by chronopotentiometry technique.
   USE - Method for preparation of an electrode such as                positive or negative electrode of a device for                storing and recovering electricity such as super                capacitor or battery, electrode for photovoltaic                device, in materials for storing carbon dioxide or                as electrode for electrochemical sensors (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrolytic solution comprising the                precursor of the metal oxide and the nitrate is                electrochemically deposited on the metal oxide on                the set of vertical aligned carbon nanotubes, thus                ensuring better performance of the electrode                irrespective of the chemical nature of the metal                support.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                electrode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a photograph of a part of a                substrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The metal oxide includes ruthenium oxide,                  titanium oxide, manganese oxide, copper oxide,                  nickel oxide, cobalt oxide, iron oxide, chromium                  oxide and vanadium oxide. The precursor of the                  metal oxide includes titanium nitrate, manganese                  nitrate, copper nitrate, nickel nitrate, iron                  nitrate, titanium chloride, manganese chloride,                  cuprous chloride, nickel chloride, cobalt                  chloride, and iron chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - The metal support includes titanium, nickel,                  aluminum, copper, chromium, tantalum, platinum,                  gold, silver or stainless steel. The precursor of                  the metal oxide includes vanadium chromium, iron,                  cobalt, nickel, copper, manganese, titanium and                  ruthenium halide.

PT P
PN CN109134910-A; CN109134910-B
TI Preparing high temperature resistant, oil resistant ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber for automobile rubber pipe, involves adding zinc oxide crystal whisker into graphene hydrogel, stirring and dispersing to freeze and drying.
AU CAI J
   WANG Y
   WANG J
   QIU S
   BAO W
AE CHENGDU SHUILONGTOU CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO (CHEN-Non-standard)
   ZHEJIANG FOMAY IND MACHINERY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201905490C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high temperature resistant, oil resistant ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber involves adding zinc oxide crystal whisker into graphene hydrogel, stirring and dispersing uniformly to freeze and drying, rolling resulting aerogel and crushing to obtain graphene micro sheet and inserting zinc oxide whiskers three-dimensional sponge filler. The 10% potassium bromide solution is added with 40% sulfuric acid solution, and immersed ethylene propylene diene rubber in mixed solution for 5-10 hours, plasticized for 2-5 minutes and transferred to mill. The three dimensional sponge filler, accelerator, crosslinking agent, antioxidant, scorch retarder, solubilizer, xylene are added, mixed uniformly and discharged to obtain composite gel material. The prepared composite rubber material is extruded by extruder, cooled and formed to obtain rubber tube.
   USE - Method for preparing high temperature resistant, oil resistant ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber for automobile rubber pipe (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare high temperature resistant, oil resistant ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber that improves plasma polymerization property high temperature capability and oil resistance capability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The mixed gas of acetylene and carbon dioxide/hydrogen are put in rubber pipe, adopted inner outer rubber tube surface plasma treatment, surface polymerization and formed hydrophilic layer to obtain high temperature resistant, oil resistant ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber for automobile rubber pipe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The amount of graphene microchip is 40-60 pts. wt. The amount of zinc oxide crystal whisker is 40-60 pts. wt. The amount of acetylene is 20-30 vol.%. The amount of carbon dioxide is 40-60 vol.%. The amount of hydrogen is 20-30 vol.%. Preferred Compositions: The raw material comprises 15-20 pts. wt.potassium bromide solution, 25-52 pts. wt. sulfuric acid solution, 20-30 pts. wt. ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber, 5-10 pts. wt. three-dimensional sponge filler, 0.5-1 pts. wt. accelerator, 1-2 pts. wt. crosslinking agent, 0.5-1 pts. wt. antiager, 0.5-1 pts. wt. anti-scorching agent, 2.5-5 pts. wt. solubilizer, and 3-5 pts. wt. xylene. Preferred Components: The accelerant is 2-mercapto benzothiazole, N-cyclohexyl-2-benzothiazole sulfenamide, N-tert-butyl-2-benzothiazole sulfenamide, and dibutyl dithiocarbamate. The crosslinking agent is magnesium oxide, zinc oxide and lead oxide. The anti-aging agent is N-phenyl-beta -naphthylamine, 2-mercapto-benzimidazole, N, N '- di (beta-naphthyl)-p-phenylenediamine, and N-(1, 3-dimethyl)-butyl-N'- phenyl para-phenylenediamine. The scorch retarder is N-cyclohexyl-thiophthalimide and N-nitroso diphenylamine. The solubilizing agent is polyoxyethylene castor oil,fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether and polyglycerolfatty acid ester.

AU WANG J
   YANG W
   WAN G
   LIU C
   LIU S
   LIN D
   WANG D
   DAI Z
   DU J
   WANG C
   GUO K
AE QINGDAO JIFA GROUP CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201894309X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves loading to-be-whitened terylene (RTM: Synthetic polyester fiber) yarn into a dyeing kettle of a cone yarn anhydrous dyeing device. The to-be-whitened terylene (RTM: Synthetic polyester fiber) yarn is sleeved in a yarn shaft. Terylene (RTM: Synthetic polyester fiber) whitening agent is added to dye in the dyeing kettle. A booster pump system of the cone yarn anhydrous dyeing device is started to input proper amount of carbon dioxide. A heating sleeve and a heat tracing circuit are started. A circulating pump is started when temperature and pressure in the dyeing kettle reaches technical parameter. The whitening agent is gradually dissolved to diffuse and whiten the yarn in the dyeing kettle. The circulating pump is closed to open a separating valve according to process time. Liquid recycling and separating processes are performed. The dyeing kettle is opened to take out whitened terylene (RTM: Synthetic polyester fiber) yarn.
   USE - Method for whitening terylene (RTM: Synthetic polyester fiber) yarn by utilizing a cone yarn anhydrous dyeing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving terylene (RTM: Synthetic polyester fiber) yarn production efficiency, reducing whitening error, and increasing terylene (RTM: Synthetic polyester fiber) yarn whitening effect.

AE UNIV SHANDONG TECHNOLOGY (SDUT-C)
GA 201892745P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing catalytic oxidation auxiliary agent in the high-salt and high-COD organic wastewater in advance, and preheating into a fixed bed reactor to oxidize with an oxidant. The organic matter in the wastewater is oxidized to water and carbon dioxide. The reaction solution is desalted by flash evaporation and concentration by evaporation after the reaction. The catalytic oxidation aid is a soluble ferrous salt, and the fixed bed reactor is filled with a SBA-15 supported vanadium pentoxide porous solid catalyst. The salt in the high-salt and high-COD organic wastewater is potassium chloride, sodium chloride or calcium chloride.
   USE - USE Method for treating high-salt and high chemical oxygen demand (COD) organic wastewater.

PT P
PN JP2018162370-A
TI Manufacture of polypropylene resin foaming particles for manufacturing molded article, involves dispersing polypropylene resin particles containing flame retardant and carbon black in aqueous dispersion medium within airtight container.
AU ITOI A
AE KANEKA CORP (KANF-C)
GA 2018805832
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of polypropylene resin foaming particles involves manufacturing polypropylene resin foaming particles having an expansion ratio of 13-28 times during foaming process by dispersing polypropylene resin particles comprising a steric hindrance amine ether-type flame retardant, a phosphorus antioxidant, a phenolic antioxidant, and 2-5 pts. wt. carbon black having a primary particle size of 20-50 nm with respect to 100 pts. wt. polypropylene resin in an aqueous dispersion medium within an airtight container, impregnating carbon dioxide as a foaming agent, and discharging the polypropylene resin particles heated at a temperature higher than the softening temperature of polypropylene resin particles to an atmosphere of 80-110 degrees at a pressure area lower than the internal pressure of the airtight container.
   USE - Manufacture of polypropylene resin foaming particles used for manufacturing molded article (claimed). Uses include but are not limited to thermal insulation material, buffer packing material, motor vehicle upholstery material, and core material for motor vehicle bumper.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of polypropylene resin foaming particles, providing molded article with excellent chemical resistance, flame resistance, discoloration resistance, external appearance, and heat resistance, and high compressive strength and dimensional accuracy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of polypropylene resin foaming molded article.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polypropylene resin particles contain 0.01-0.5 pt. wt. steric hindrance amine ether-type flame retardant, 0.01-0.5 pt. wt. phosphorus antioxidant, and 0.01-1 pt. wt. phenolic antioxidant, with respect to 100 pts. wt. polypropylene resin, and further contain 0.01-10 pts. wt. hydrophilic compound with respect to 100 pts. wt. polypropylene resin.
DC A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.); A95 (Transport - including vehicle parts, tyres and armaments.)

PT P
PN JP2018162371-A
TI Manufacturing polypropylene resin foaming particle for manufacturing polypropylene resin foaming molding by foaming polypropylene, and dispersing polypropylene-resin particle to carbon black and cell nucleating agent to dispersion medium.
AU ITOI A
AE KANEKA CORP (KANF-C)
GA 2018805831
AB    NOVELTY - A polypropylene resin foaming particle is manufactured by foaming polypropylene having expansion ratio of 13-28 times, dispersing 0.01-0.25 pt. wt. polypropylene-resin particle to 2-5 pts. wt. carbon black with primary particle size of less than or equal to 50 nm, and cell nucleating agent with size of greater than or equal to 0 nm to water-type dispersion medium within airtight container with respect to 100 pts. wt. polypropylene resins, impregnating carbon dioxide as foaming agent, obtaining polypropylene resin foaming particle at a pressure area lower than the internal pressure of airtight container, and discharging polypropylene-resin particle heated to temperature more than the softening temperature of polypropylene-resin particle to atmosphere at 80-110 times.
   USE - Manufacture of polypropylene resin foaming particle for manufacturing polypropylene resin foaming molding (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Blackness of molded object is high, color is uniform, and in-mold-foam molding with excellent external appearance can be obtained.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacturing polypropylene resin foaming molding which involves filling the polypropylene resin foaming particle with foaming particle in molding space which consists of 2 metal molds that cannot be sealed but can be closed down, and heating foaming particle with water vapor through water vapor hole arranged at metal mold.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: Primary particle size of carbon black is 10-50 nm. Water vapor hole includes drill holw with diameter of 0.5-2 mm. Preferred Components: Cell nucleating agent is silicate compound.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: Amount of hydrophilic compound is 0.01-10 pts. wt. with respect to 100 pts. wt. polypropylene resin.

TI Method for processing liquid sample e.g.            biological sample e.g. cell culture sample or chemical            sample located in container, involves dispensing fluid            directly into liquid sample through fluid line.
AU SCHONDUBE J
   CHENGHAN T
   GROSS A
   ZIMMERMANN S
   KOLTAY P
   SCHOENDUBE J
   CAI C
   TSAI C
AE CYTENA GMBH (CYTE-Non-standard)
   UNIV FREIBURG ALBERT-LUDWIGS (UYFR-C)
   CYTENA GMBH (CYTE-Non-standard)
   CYTENA GMBH (CYTE-Non-standard)
   CYTENA GMBH (CYTE-Non-standard)
   UNIV FREIBURG ALBERT-LUDWIGS (UYFR-C)
   CYTENA BIOPROCESS SOLUTIONS CO LTD (CYTE-Non-standard)
   CYTENA BIOPROCESS SOLUTIONS CO LTD (CYTE-Non-standard)
   CYTENA BIOPROCESS SOLUTIONS CO LTD (CYTE-Non-standard)
GA 201881975F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves attaching an attachment                device to a container (2) such that a fluid line                (4) is projected into a liquid sample (3). The                fluid is dispensed directly into the liquid sample                through the fluid line. A portion of the liquid                sample is absorbed into the fluid line. The suction                and dispensing are carried out alternately to mix                the liquid sample. The dispensed fluid is a gas                such as oxygen or carbon dioxide. A gas bubble                diameter is increased and decreased to mix the                liquid sample. The mixing of the liquid sample is                realized by reciprocal pumping.
   USE - Method for processing liquid sample such as                biological sample e.g. cell culture sample or                chemical sample located in container.
   ADVANTAGE - The cell culture stability and higher cell                density are achieved where the cell cultures are                cultured under static conditions, since ideal                production conditions are realized from the start                so that rapid growth of cells is realized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) an attachment device for processing liquid                sample located in container; and
   (2) an attachment device and container                integrated apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                attachment device and container integrated                apparatus.
   Container (2)
   Liquid sample (3)
   Fluid line (4)
   Cover (5)
   Filter (6)

AU YOON J H

   XILIN H
   TAOSHENG W
   JINDI H
   XU Y
   LI L
   HUANG Q
   WANG T
   HUANG J
AE UNIV JIANGXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJL-C)
   UNIV JIANGXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJL-C)
GA 201810134Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of aluminum-scandium master alloy by molten salt electrolysis with a chloride salt oxide system involves adding calcium oxide or magnesium oxide into chloride molten salt electrolyte composed of sodium chloride-calcium chloride or potassium chloride-calcium chloride, and adding scandium oxide to form chloride-oxide mixed molten salt electrolyte, placing the electrolyte in an electrolytic cell, placing aluminum cathode and graphite anode, connecting to direct current power supply, carrying out electrolysis, and depositing aluminum-scandium master alloy at the cathode.
   USE - Preparation of aluminum-scandium master alloy by molten salt electrolysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is simple, easy, continuous and economical, reduces environmental pollution and produces master alloy with high purity without the addition of a reducing agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of aluminum-scandium master alloy by molten salt electrolysis with a chloride salt oxide system involves adding calcium oxide or magnesium oxide into chloride molten salt electrolyte composed of sodium chloride-calcium chloride or potassium chloride-calcium chloride, and adding scandium oxide to form chloride-oxide mixed molten salt electrolyte, placing the electrolyte in an electrolytic cell, placing aluminum cathode and graphite anode, connecting to direct current power supply, carrying out electrolysis, depositing aluminum-scandium master alloy at the bottom of the cathode, emitting carbon dioxide at the anode without other harmful gases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mol ratio of sodium chloride:calcium chloride is 0.35-0.85, and the mol ratio of potassium chloride: calcium chloride is 0.35-3:1. The addition amount of calcium oxide or magnesium oxide is 5-10% of the total weight of the molten salt electrolyte and the addition amount of scandium oxide is 2-8% of the total weight of the electrolyte.
